###Chapter 1

"You're already married, how can you register again!"

"Don't you know bigamy is a crime?"

…

Evelyn Ross walked out of City Hall in shock.

She held a freshly printed marriage certificate in her hand.

The man who accompanied her looked at her with pity. "Miss Ross, you're already married. Why did you pay me to fake a marriage with you?"

He threw out a final remark before hurrying away. "The deposit is non-refundable."

Evelyn pressed her lips together tightly.

She had never even been in a relationship. How could she possibly be married already?!

She looked down at the printed document.

The girl in the photo had a teardrop mole near the corner of her eye. It was definitely her.

As for the man beside her…

His features were sharp and defined.

He had a tall nose bridge and thin lips that held a faint, almost imperceptible smile.

Even in black and white, his powerful presence was undeniable.

The name field read: Alexander Vance.

She didn't know this man at all!

What was going on?!

Evelyn took a picture of the certificate with her phone.

She opened WhatsApp and sent it to a contact with a black avatar. [Find out who this is for me.]

The reply was instant. [Received.]

Evelyn pushed down her confusion.

She got on her old scooter and drove into an upscale gated community.

Today was her sister Isabella Ashcroft's special day.

Her fiancé was coming to present the dowry.

The Ashcroft house was decorated with lanterns and streamers.

Servants bustled about, busy with preparations.

Evelyn parked her scooter in a corner.

She overheard temporary workers and maids gossiping behind her.

"Who is she? She's so beautiful!"

"Shh, she's the unacknowledged love child."

"Her mother is the mistress. She showed up here pregnant and made a scene, causing the Mrs. to go into early labor. They gave birth on the same day. That old woman has no shame. She clung to the family and refused to leave."

"Miss Ross knows her place, though. She moved out in middle school and hasn't been back for years. I wonder what brings her here today…"

Evelyn pretended not to hear and walked into the living room.

Her mother, Linda Lowell, was waiting by the entrance.

The still attractive woman anxiously pulled her upstairs. "Come with me to see your sister first. Did you get the marriage certificate?"

Evelyn's voice was light. "I got it."

Even though the groom was different, it still counted, right?

"Good. Remember your place. Julian Evans is your sister's fiancé. A top-tier family like that is beyond someone like you. Only your sister is worthy of him!"

A hint of mockery flashed in Evelyn's eyes.

Julian was the eldest grandson of the prestigious Evans family.

He had pursued her for four years in college.

But on graduation day, he proposed to Isabella…

When Linda found out, she demanded Evelyn marry someone immediately.

To sever any possibility with Julian.

It had always been like this…

Whenever her interests conflicted with Isabella's, Linda demanded she yield unconditionally.

Because she was a love child. Her very existence was a sin.

When she was young, she was brainwashed into thinking this was normal.

But she had long since woken up.

Evelyn's expression was serious. "We agreed this is the last time."

Linda was the one who made the mistake.

She was the one who clung to the Ashcroft family to see William every day.

She was the one who wanted to curry favor with Isabella.

Evelyn wouldn't sacrifice her life for Linda's choices.

This was her chance to repay the debt of birth and cut ties.

Linda sounded impatient. "I know."

They arrived at Isabella's room.

The pretty girl sat on a sofa in an elaborate gown, selecting jewelry.

The room was filled with glittering treasures.

Evelyn, in her simple clothes, stood straight-backed.

Isabella noticed her. "Evelyn, what are you doing here?"

Before Evelyn could speak, Linda cut in. "Isabella, Evelyn got married today."

Isabella feigned surprise. "So soon? Who is the man? Is he better than Julian?"

Linda sneered. "Of course not! No one in San Francisco is more prestigious than Mr. Evans! What kind of good family would want her? She married some destitute loser. She didn't even dare bring him here, afraid he'd dirty your eyes!"

Isabella's voice held a hint of jealousy. "How could that be? Evelyn is so beautiful. Otherwise, Julian wouldn't have chased her for four years."

"What use is beauty? Trash belongs with trash. Only lowlifes would marry someone of her status. Mr. Evans just saw her as a toy to play with. Only someone of your status is worthy of him…"

Evelyn frowned.

The man in the photo didn't look or carry himself like a destitute loser.

But she couldn't be bothered to argue.

Isabella finished selecting her jewelry.

She tried to put on her high heels but found her gown too tight to bend over.

Isabella smiled sweetly and looked at Evelyn.

Linda immediately shoved Evelyn. "Useless thing, where are your manners? Your sister can't manage. Help her put her shoes on!"

Evelyn remained silent.

Again.

Did Linda really think she was still that ignorant little girl?

Her gaze was cool. "You can help her yourself."

"Evelyn, what attitude is that? You think you can rebel just because you're married? Your husband is just a freeloader. You'll still need to rely on the Ashcroft family!"

Linda's voice rose. "If you don't get on your sister's good side now, you'll beg for her help someday! The Ashcrofts raised you. You should serve them like a slave!"

A tall figure appeared at the doorway.

It was her father, William Ashcroft.

The man frowned. "An important guest is arriving soon. What is all this noise?"

Isabella stayed silent, playing innocent.

Linda began to cry. "It's this wretched girl. Now that she's married, she looks down on me…"

William turned to Evelyn. "You got married? Why didn't you let the family introduce someone? Where's the marriage certificate? Let me see…"

Faced with her distant father's concern, Evelyn paused, then pulled the printout from her bag.

Linda snatched it away. "Let me see what your useless husband is called!"

Isabella asked curiously, "Dad, who is coming? You seem so nervous."

William thought of that person and felt honored.

He excitedly said a name.

"Alexander Vance."

Evelyn froze completely.

…Who?

###Chapter 2

Isabella looked puzzled. "Who is Alexander Vance? Is he important?"

Evelyn pricked up her ears.

She had been around San Francisco for years, but had never heard that name.

William explained, "He keeps a very low profile. I've never met him myself. He's Julian's uncle. Only twenty-eight, but he's already the true head of the Vance family!"

Linda's eyes lit up. "So he'd be a better match for Isabella than Julian?"

The head of the family was far better than some eldest grandson!

William shot her a warning look. "Don't say such things. Mr. Vance is already married!"

Evelyn narrowed her eyes.

Married...

If he was really the man on the marriage certificate, then he should know the truth.

Linda sighed with regret. "Who is his wife? She must be even luckier than our Isabella."

Evelyn watched William intently.

William shook his head. "No one knows. They say neither of them are social people."

He frowned in thought. "Strange. Why would he suddenly come today..."

The Vances were one of San Francisco's top elite families. Their patriarch held a position of great respect.

The Ashcrofts were only upper-middle class. This marriage was already a step up for them. The patriarch attending the wedding was one thing, but why would he personally come for the dowry discussion?

Linda, however, was smug. "It must be because Isabella is so exceptional! She's caught the attention of the Vance family! Isabella, that necklace isn't grand enough. With elders present today, you need something that shows your status!"

She shoved the printed paper back into Evelyn's hand, eager to pull Isabella away to choose jewelry.

She was more enthusiastic than Sophia, Isabella's own mother.

Evelyn let out a derisive laugh.

"Sir, the Vances have arrived."

The butler reminded William to go downstairs. Passing Evelyn, he said offhandedly, "Since you're here, you might as well stay for a celebratory drink."

Evelyn nodded in agreement.

She was going to stay and meet Alexander Vance!

In the room, Linda helped Isabella put on her jewelry.

Looking at her radiant, beautiful daughter, a flash of triumph shone in her eyes.

Over twenty years ago, if Sophia Ashcroft hadn't interfered, she would have been the one to marry William!

She hated Sophia. She deliberately gave birth on the same day and switched the babies at the hospital.

Now her daughter was marrying into glory, and Sophia had to provide a lavish dowry!

While Evelyn could only marry some lowlife as the illegitimate love child!!

The tables had turned. This was what Sophia owed her!

Downstairs.

Evelyn leaned in the shadows of the staircase, watching the entrance intently.

After some time, Sophia was helped downstairs by a maid. She wore a purple cheongsam, her figure slender, exuding a strong scholarly air.

The maid whispered, "Ma'am, you're not well. You should rest."

Sophia shook her head. "No... It's Isabella's big day... I can't miss it..."

They didn't notice Evelyn and walked towards the entrance.

Evelyn gazed at Sophia's back, her eyes full of affection.

The irony was that Sophia, the one who should hate her, was the only person in the Ashcroft family who had ever shown her warmth.

Linda was never much of a mother. She often forgot to feed her.

As a young child, Evelyn was skin and bones. She learned to scavenge for food from trash cans as soon as she could walk.

Once, Sophia saw her. After that, she started leaving food in the garden every day.

She did this for twelve years.

If not for Sophia, Evelyn would have starved to death.

Hearing Sophia's cough, Evelyn frowned with worry.

Then there was movement at the door. The Vances had arrived!

William and Sophia greeted them at the entrance. After exchanging pleasantries, they stepped aside, and a large group filed in.

Evelyn's eyes immediately found Alexander Vance.

He wore a black custom-tailored suit, walking at the forefront like the center of attention. His face was more defined than in the photo, with sharp contours and a clear jawline.

The man's eyes were deep, his lips thin and cool. Every movement exuded an air of nobility.

Sensing her gaze, the man suddenly looked over.

Their eyes met.

His gaze was sharp, making Evelyn's heart skip a beat. She wanted to study him further, but he had already looked away.

Evelyn was a bit confused.

That reaction... did he know her or not?

William smiled obsequiously. "Mr. Vance, didn't your wife come with you?"

Evelyn felt Alexander's gaze sweep over her again before he replied coolly, "She's unavailable."

They chatted as they moved towards the drawing room.

Julian Evans, who had pursued her for four years, followed behind. The suit made him seem more mature than he was in college. He didn't see Evelyn, distractedly whispering to Isabella.

Everyone crowded around Alexander as he took a seat, discussing the wedding arrangements.

Evelyn stepped out of the shadows.

She stood quietly, observing the lively scene in the drawing room.

Suddenly, her arm was grabbed. Linda hissed in a low voice, "Evelyn, what are you still doing here? Still can't let go of Julian? I'm telling you, he's your sister's fiancé now!"

Evelyn shook off her hand, sneering. "Relax, I have no interest in being a mistress. Mr. Ashcroft asked me to stay for a drink."

Since she was old enough to understand, she had only ever called William "Mr. Ashcroft."

Linda was furious. "That was just politeness! Can't you take a hint? Know your place! Even I can't sit at the table on an occasion like this without embarrassing everyone. How can an illegitimate love child like you? Get out of here now!"

...This was endless!

Evelyn frowned impatiently, about to retort, when she saw Alexander stand up. He gestured to his phone and walked towards the balcony to take a call.

Evelyn's eyes lit up. "Fine, I'm leaving."

After brushing off Linda, she walked out of the drawing room but didn't leave the house. Instead, she turned towards the balcony.

The first-floor balcony connected to a small garden.

Evelyn had just approached when the man on the phone ended his call and his cold, stern gaze swept over her.

Evelyn paused.

Meeting his dangerous eyes, she suddenly curved her lips into a smile and called out carefully, "Husband?"

###Chapter 3

A glass door muffled the lively chatter from the drawing room.

Evelyn Ross stared directly at Alexander Vance.

Hearing that form of address, the man’s entire demeanor turned icy.
His eyes darkened, radiating a chilling coldness.
His expression remained blank as he turned to leave.

Evelyn quickly stepped in front of him, blocking his path.

Alexander frowned.
"Move aside."

His voice was deep, each word laced with an aristocratic chill.
It made her heart itch strangely.

Evelyn sensed something was off.
"You don't know me?"

Alexander looked down at her.
"Should I know you?"

He had felt a pair of eyes on him since entering the Ashcroft residence.
The gaze was bold, unlike the fawning looks from others.
He had glanced over a couple of times.
The girl was beautiful, with fair skin and bright eyes.
A teardrop mole near her eye added a touch of allure.
She seemed docile on the surface, but an untamed spirit lurked beneath.
Even after he noticed her staring, she didn't look away.
She met his gaze directly.
He had thought she was different.
But she was even bolder, addressing him as 'husband' right away...

Impatience flashed in Alexander's eyes.
"Miss, I am a married man. Please show some respect."

Evelyn was stunned.

He didn't know her, yet claimed to be married?
Did City Hall make a mistake?

She asked, "Who is your wife?"

"That is none of your concern."

The four words were frigid.

Evelyn pulled out a copy of her marriage certificate.
"Mr. Vance, is this you?"

Alexander looked at the document.
His eyes fell on the woman's name: Evelyn Ross.
He glanced up, his expression mocking.
"Miss Ashcroft, if you're going to forge a document, at least do it professionally."

He stepped around her and walked straight toward the parking lot.

Evelyn tried to follow, but two bodyguards in black suits stopped her.
She called after his retreating back, "The certificate is real! You can check at City Hall..."

Alexander didn't look back.
He got into his car and drove away.

His private assistant returned to the drawing room and encountered Isabella Ashcroft.

Isabella had just witnessed Evelyn pestering Alexander, but hadn't heard their conversation.
Seeing Alexander leave and Evelyn chase after him on her scooter, she immediately asked, "Why did Mr. Vance leave? Did someone offend him?"

The assistant smiled politely.
"Mr. Vance had urgent business to attend to. Please convey his apologies to the elders."

Since his boss hadn't ordered the girl to be taught a lesson, he was letting it slide.

Isabella nodded and politely saw the assistant out.

The wedding date was settled.
The Vance family left after lunch.

Once the guests were gone, William Ashcroft expressed concern.
"Why did Mr. Vance leave early? Was our hospitality lacking?"

Isabella thought of Julian Evans's distracted behavior all day, his eyes constantly searching the room.
She also recalled Evelyn's face.
Her fists clenched.
Her eyes shifted nervously.
"Father, I saw Evelyn harassing Mr. Vance. She angered him so much he left. Mr. Vance even left a message..."

"What message?"

"He said you should discipline your daughter properly." Isabella bit her lip.
"With Evelyn acting like this, the Vances will think we have poor family morals."

William's face darkened with anger.

Evelyn rode her electric scooter out of the villa district, but had already lost sight of him.

She was feeling frustrated when her phone rang.

It was James Klein, her subordinate.
"Boss, a lot of people are investigating Dr. Nan."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.
"Did we get exposed?"

"Of course not! Who would guess the brilliant mind who cracked hydrogen fuel is a freshly graduated young woman..."

"Get to the point," Evelyn interrupted him.

"Right, right. We found information on Alexander Vance! Second son of the Vance family. Rumored to have a violent temper. Sent abroad as a child. Everyone thought the family business would go to his older brother. But he returned last week unexpectedly. Used ruthless methods to force the old man to step down. Now he controls the Vance Group."

James was curious.
"I thought you found someone easy to control for a marriage of convenience? How did the groom turn into this cold-faced king of hell? Boss, an unstable marital status could affect the company's IPO..."

Evelyn frowned.
"Find his contact information and schedule. I need to speak with him again."

She had agreed to Linda Lowell's demand for an immediate marriage only because having a married status as the company's legal representative was beneficial for the IPO.
Now she was inexplicably married, entangled in some unknown scheme.
Alexander's identity was complicated.
It was best to get a divorce quickly to avoid further trouble.

She ended the call and rubbed her temples.

This was troublesome.
Alexander traveled with bodyguards.
Getting close to him wouldn't be easy.
She shouldn't have called him 'husband' today and angered him...

She sighed, started her beat-up scooter, and slowly headed home.

Leaving the city center, she returned to the old urban village in the suburbs.
She had rented this old place when she moved out of the Ashcroft home in middle school, back when she had no money.
She had grown accustomed to it and never moved.

As she turned the corner toward home, an elderly woman suddenly darted into the road!

Evelyn slammed on the brakes, nearly hitting her.

The old woman didn't seem like a scam artist.
She was dressed well, unlike an ordinary person.
A placard hung around her neck with a contact number and the words: Reward if found.

...Alzheimer's.
Someone's grandmother was lost.

Evelyn took out her phone and entered the number.

The dazed old woman suddenly grabbed her wrist.
Her cloudy eyes lit up.
"Granddaughter-in-law! You are my granddaughter-in-law!"

Evelyn gave a wry smile.

Single for 22 years, and now she had another husband?
Was City Hall handing them out wholesale today?

She found it amusing and asked casually, "Grandmother, who is your grandson?"

The old woman frowned in thought.
What was her grandson's name...?
Oh, Alexander Vance!!
###Chapter 4

Granddaughter-in-law!

The old lady strained to remember.
"He's called... Vance... Vance something..."
The name was on the tip of her tongue, but she just couldn't get it out.
The elderly woman grew frustrated, slapping her thigh in agitation.

Evelyn Ross spoke softly to soothe her.
"Don't rush. Take your time."
She took out her phone and dialed a number.

At that exact moment, on a nearby street.
The atmosphere inside the Bentley was oppressive.
Alexander Vance's face was like dark water.
Thomas Watson reported nervously, "Mrs. Vance gave the bodyguards the slip and ran off by herself..."
A chill emanated from the man.
Surveillance showed the elderly woman had taken a bus to the suburbs.
This area had sparse camera coverage, making the search difficult.

The abrupt ring of a phone broke the silence.
Alexander pressed the answer button.
A lazy female voice came through the receiver. "Hello. Your elderly relative is with me."
The temperature in the car seemed to drop instantly.
Thomas immediately began tracing the signal source.
Alexander's voice was icy. "Name your price."
"...Just kidding," the girl said cheerfully. "Remember to keep a better eye on her."
She gave an address and hung up.

Thomas let out a long sigh of relief.
That good Samaritan really knew how to joke!
A flicker of recognition passed through Alexander's eyes.
That voice... where had he heard it before?

Five minutes later, they arrived at the location.
They found the old lady waiting with a police officer.
Alexander stepped forward. "Grandmother, what are you doing all the way out here?"
The old woman whispered mysteriously, "Looking for my granddaughter-in-law! She lives around here!"
Alexander sighed helplessly. "You're confused again..."
"I really saw her!" the old lady complained. "That girl handed me over to the police and slipped away... Let me borrow your phone."
She took the phone and carefully noted down the number that had just called.
Her little notebook finally had the granddaughter-in-law's contact information!

Evelyn hated dealing with grateful scenes.
Seeing a patrol officer, she promptly handed over the elderly woman and left.
Early the next morning, her advisor called, urging her to come in.
Evelyn rode her scooter to the university.

Inside Professor Lowell's office, Isabella Ashcroft and Linda Lowell were already waiting.
Evelyn narrowed her peach-blossom eyes.
She and Isabella attended the same university but had vastly different experiences.
Isabella had gloriously tested into San Francisco University.
Evelyn had deliberately scored low to enter the obscure Energy and Power Engineering major, aiming to manage her company.
Then, new energy suddenly became a hot field.
Isabella immediately transferred departments, becoming her classmate once again.
Linda's presence was a surprise.

Professor Lowell looked grave. "Evelyn, your guaranteed graduate admission has been revoked."
Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "On what grounds?"
"Your mother reported you for misconduct," Professor Lowell said, frowning. "Quickly apologize to your mother. Don't ruin your future!"
Isabella spoke up gently. "Professor Lowell, Mom only wants what's best for Evelyn."
She turned to Evelyn. "You offended Mr. Vance. He said he wants you to disappear from San Francisco."
Evelyn was taken aback.
Alexander didn't seem the petty type.
Isabella, however... was an expert at spinning tales.

Isabella held out a plane ticket. "Dad wants you to lay low in Argentina."
Evelyn glanced at the destination.
The other side of the planet.
The Ashcrofts were clearly afraid she might return.
She pushed the ticket away. "Not necessary."
Isabella then produced a bank card. "This fifty thousand is what I've saved. You can use it for now..."
Linda snatched the card back. "Isabella, are you stupid? The Ashcrofts paying for the ticket is already more than enough!"
She commanded Evelyn, "Withdraw from school and go abroad immediately!"
Evelyn met her gaze coldly. "What gives you the right to decide for me?"
"The fact that I'm your mother! Do you think you can even graduate? Getting a job and earning money is what you should be doing!"
Professor Lowell quickly interjected, "Mrs. Lowell, you're mistaken! Evelyn's performance in her major is exceptional..."
Linda cut him off directly. "Professor, don't cover for her! The only reason she wants a graduate degree is to compete with Isabella! She should look in the mirror and see if she's even worthy!"
The crude language left the professor speechless.

Isabella smiled, smoothing things over. "I was admitted through a special recruitment channel."
Professor Lowell was curious. "Which professor recruited you specially?"
Isabella pretended to be modest. "It was Dr. Nan. He just patented a new hydrogen fuel."
Evelyn's head snapped up. "You said who?"
###Chapter 5

Dr. Nan?

When had she ever said she would specially recruit Isabella?

Professor Lowell said in surprise, "You know Dr. Nan?"

Isabella smiled gracefully. "It's quite a coincidence, actually. My mother sponsored Dr. Nan's education years ago. After he achieved success, he sought out our family to repay the kindness. He's provided technical support for our family company all these years. He's always been very accommodating to my requests."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

She had contacted Sophia Ashcroft using the "Dr. Nan" identity to repay her for raising her.

When had she ever been accommodating to Isabella's requests?

She was really good at taking credit.

Professor Lowell, however, believed her. "Which university does Dr. Nan work for now?"

Isabella replied, "I've promised the Dean I would invite Dr. Nan to teach here."

"Excellent!" Professor Lowell beamed. He looked at Evelyn. "Evelyn, your research aligns perfectly with Dr. Nan's. I'll introduce you when he arrives. If Dr. Nan speaks for you, there might still be a chance to salvage your graduate position!"

Isabella feigned concern. "Professor, is that feasible? The Vance family is, after all, the wealthiest in San Francisco. They provide substantial research funding to the university every year..."

Professor Lowell waved it off. "Dr. Nan is highly sought after now. I heard Cambridge and Oxford have both extended invitations. Numerous companies are competing to invest in his work. If he intervenes, the university will undoubtedly choose him!"

Isabella sighed. "But Dr. Nan is only helping out of respect for my mother. Evelyn is my father's... love child. Her position is opposed to my mother's... Evelyn, perhaps I could ask Dr. Nan for you?"

Evelyn declined. "That won't be necessary."

Isabella's performance was utterly ridiculous.

A faint smile touched Evelyn's lips as she addressed Professor Lowell. "Professor, it's really not worth the trouble. You know I never planned on graduate school anyway."

Professor Lowell's expression was full of regret.

He was the one who had discovered Evelyn's talent and insisted she pursue the graduate position.

He never expected things to turn out this way.

His eyes reddened slightly. "Then let me write you a letter of recommendation for a job."

Linda scoffed. "Don't waste your effort! After offending Mr. Vance, she thinks she can find a job in San Francisco? She's delusional!"

Professor Lowell retorted angrily, "I refuse to believe anyone holds absolute power in this city! If she can't find a job, she can be my teaching assistant! I insist on having her!"

Warmth spread through Evelyn's heart. "Professor, it's just a misunderstanding between Mr. Vance and me. Once it's cleared up, everything will be fine. Please don't worry."

Professor Lowell sighed. "If it gets resolved, come back. You'll still be my student."

Evelyn's voice softened. "Alright. I'll definitely come back."

Teaching at San Francisco University wouldn't be a bad option.

Isabella clenched her fingers.

She didn't understand. She was the top student. Why did Professor Lowell favor the utterly ordinary Evelyn?

Was the old fool also bewitched by her face?

Furious, Isabella glanced at Linda. "Evelyn, are you still planning to pester Mr. Vance? He's married. Your behavior is truly inappropriate..."

Linda predictably exploded in rage.

Slap!

The sound of the slap echoed through the office.

Evelyn's cheek burned with a sharp, stinging pain.

She hadn't expected Linda to hit her right in front of Professor Lowell!

Linda showed no remorse. She pointed a finger and yelled, "You shameless brat! Has a few years away made you forget the rules at home? Apologize to your sister this instant! Promise you will never see the Vances again!"

Evelyn's eyes turned completely cold.

It seemed her words about it being "the last time" the other day had made no impression.

Linda still saw her as that meek, pathetic pushover.

Since reason didn't work, she couldn't blame her for not holding back...

Isabella secretly gloated.

When they were young, Evelyn was subservient to her, never fighting back.

After they reunited in university, Evelyn actually dared to stand tall.

A few years without discipline, and she forgot her place!

Today, Linda would teach her a lesson.

Isabella was still thinking this when she saw Evelyn walk over. She assumed she was coming to apologize.

Isabella's lips had just begun to curve into a smile—

Evelyn suddenly raised her hand and slapped Isabella hard across the face, twice!

Slap! Slap!

Isabella was stunned by the blows.

Linda roared and charged forward. "Evelyn, have you lost your mind!"

Evelyn whirled around!

Her gaze was sinister, like a vengeful spirit. A chilling aura of menace radiated from her.

Linda was frightened into stillness. "You... what do you think you're doing? I'm your mother! How dare you raise your hand against me? You unfilial beast!"

"You failed as a mother. Don't blame me for failing as a daughter!"

Evelyn's voice was icy. "Stay out of my life. Whatever you do to me, I will return it to Isabella twofold!"

She turned and bowed deeply to Professor Lowell. "Thank you for your guidance these past four years. I will be back."

With that, she turned and left.

It wasn't until her figure disappeared that Linda snapped out of it. She rushed to Isabella. "Isabella, are you alright? That wretch actually dared to hit you! The world is upside down!"

Isabella touched her swollen cheek, trembling with rage. "Where does she get the nerve?"

Linda was stunned. "Could Mr. Vance actually be interested in her? Impossible. She's married. Why would Mr. Vance want used goods..."

Isabella had the same thought.

But thinking of Evelyn's face, she felt a flicker of doubt...

Professor Lowell was shocked by the spectacle.

He looked at Linda in disbelief, listening to the vile language. He couldn't help but wonder:

Was this really Evelyn's mother? Not Isabella's?

Perhaps it was his imagination, but Professor Lowell suddenly thought the two looked somewhat alike...

Evelyn had just left the campus when James called.

"Boss, it's all clear now! I know why Alexander Vance claims to be married but doesn't recognize you!"
###Chapter 6

The school gate was bustling with activity.

Groups of energetic students walked by in twos and threes.

Evelyn Ross stood by the roadside, pushing her scooter.

She pressed her tongue against her swollen lip, her voice slightly hoarse. "Go on."

"The Vance Group appears prosperous, but the internal power struggles are vicious. The old patriarch favors the eldest son and is determined to pass the family business to him. Alexander Vance, as the youngest son, has faced significant suppression over the years."

"A few years back, the patriarch forced several arranged marriage candidates on Alexander. It was quite the scandal. Eventually, Alexander publicly declared he had already married a girl from an ordinary family, which finally quieted the rumors."

"The interesting part is, this so-called wife has never made a public appearance. She never attends any Vance family gatherings."

"So the truth is…"

James Klein deliberately dragged out his words, trying to build suspense.

Evelyn cut him off directly. "Understood. His schedule and contact info?"

James was left speechless, replying sullenly, "Schedule's in your email. Can't get his private number. Someone at his level has extremely tight security."

Evelyn remained calm. "Doesn't matter. I'll intercept him."

James immediately perked up. "He has plenty of bodyguards. Planning a direct approach or something more subtle?"

"Need to keep a low profile recently." Evelyn gave a lazy, roguish smile. "Besides, I'm a woman. A direct confrontation isn't suitable."

James was left utterly speechless.

The Vance Group tower soared into the clouds, dominating the most prosperous area of downtown.

Evelyn changed into a delivery uniform and entered the lobby carrying a cardboard box.

She showed her work ID to the receptionist. "A package for Mr. Vance. Requires his personal signature."

After the receptionist confirmed with the secretary's office, she was allowed upstairs.

The elevator went straight to the eighty-eighth floor.

As the doors opened, the view expanded dramatically.

The entire floor was the secretarial workspace, with over a hundred elite employees serving Alexander Vance exclusively.

Evelyn followed the guiding secretary towards the CEO's office.

After knocking, a deep male voice came from inside. "Enter."

Just as Evelyn was about to relax, a tall, lean figure stopped her.

Thomas Watson, the special assistant, scrutinized her with a furrowed brow. "Miss Ross? Why is it you?"

Yesterday she was pestering the boss at the Ashcroft residence, and today she dares to impersonate a delivery person to get up here?

Thomas looked displeased, signaling for security. "How did they let just anyone in? Escort her out immediately!"

Evelyn didn't change her expression. "Does the Vance Group look down on delivery personnel?"

Thomas sneered. "Don't turn this around. We respect all professions, but are you a real delivery person?"

"I am."

"Your work ID?"

Evelyn calmly handed over her credentials.

Thomas glanced at the registration date and was stunned. "...Eight years ago?"

She really was a delivery person?

"Can't I work part-time while studying?" Evelyn's tone was lazy as she called towards the office door. "Mr. Vance, can you sign for this now?"

A steady response came from inside the room. "Come in."

Evelyn shot Thomas a challenging look and walked past him into the office.

The office was decorated in a cool, minimalist luxury style. The black, white, and grey color scheme exuded an air of detachment.

Alexander Vance sat behind a large desk, the sleeves of his black shirt rolled up to reveal lean forearms. His well-defined hand held a pen as he reviewed documents.

He finished signing the last one before looking up. His dark eyes were unfathomable.

Evelyn pointed to the delivery slip. "Sign here."

The girl's fingers were pale and slender, their tips calloused yet柔韧有力.

Like herself, seemingly delicate yet upright, full of pride.

Alexander's gaze swept over her swollen lip. He picked up the pen to sign.

Evelyn suddenly spoke. "Mr. Vance, you're not actually married, are you?"

The pen tip stopped abruptly.

The man looked up, his gaze sharp as a blade locking onto her. An intangible pressure filled the room.

Evelyn was sure she had guessed correctly.

City Hall registrations require handwritten information. Mistakes are impossible.

Alexander claimed to be married publicly but didn't recognize her.

Combined with James's intel…

The truth could only be that Alexander fabricated a wife to deal with the marriage pressure.

He had never been to City Hall, so naturally, he was unaware they were legally married.

Evelyn said seriously, "Mr. Vance, I know this sounds absurd, but we are actually legally married."

Alexander slowly leaned back in his chair, a hint of amusement at his lips. "Miss Ross, don't waste your effort. Even if I were unmarried, I wouldn't marry you just to spite Julian Evans."

Evelyn was slightly taken aback.

He knew about her history with Julian Evans and thought she was approaching him for revenge?

Evelyn explained, "My finding you has nothing to do with Julian Evans. I want to…"

"I have no interest in hearing about your romantic entanglements."

Alexander cut her off, finished signing neatly, and handed back the delivery slip. "Stop harassing me. Or face the consequences."

Evelyn felt a surge of anger. "Haven't you already been unfriendly? Threatening to make me disappear from San Francisco!"

Alexander frowned. "When did I…"

Before he could finish, his专属铃声 rang suddenly.

It was a call from his grandmother's caregiver.

He answered immediately. The voice on the other end was urgent. "Mr. Vance, Mrs. Eleanor is missing again!"

Alexander stood up abruptly and strode quickly towards the door.

Evelyn wanted to chase after him and explain, but Thomas blocked her. "Miss Ross, know your limits."

Evelyn was helpless.

She was "escorted" out of the Vance Group building and walked home slowly.

Just as she reached the alley entrance, she sensed something and turned around.

The elderly woman she had met last time was sneakily following her.

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

Before she could speak, the small old woman grabbed her wrist.

The old woman's eyes were bright, her tone both aggrieved and determined.

"Granddaughter-in-law, you're not getting away from me this time!"
###Chapter 7

Alexander Vance offered too much.

Evelyn Ross's rented apartment was small.

But she kept it clean and tidy.

The old lady sat at the dining table.

She had already drunk three glasses of water.

Evelyn looked at her. "I'm really not your granddaughter-in-law."

"You are."

The old lady was stubborn.

She poured another glass of water.

Evelyn took out her phone.

She dialed the number from last time.

The call was answered quickly. "Hello."

The man's voice sounded somewhat familiar.

Before Evelyn could speak.

The old lady snatched the phone away.

Alexander Vance was searching the area with his team.

His expression was calm.

But he was anxious inside.

His grandmother was old.

Her health was poor.

She could have an emergency at any time.

He answered his phone immediately when it rang.

He heard his grandmother's strong, clear voice.

"You brat, don't come looking for me. I'm with my granddaughter-in-law."

Granddaughter-in-law...

That girl again.

Alexander asked sternly, "Where are you?"

"I'm not telling you."

"I'll find you even if you don't tell me."

"...Don't look for me! Don't track me!"

Alexander rubbed his temples.

He looked at the doctor. "Can we bring her back by force?"

The doctor shook his head. "We mustn't upset her. It's best to humor her. That girl doesn't seem like a bad person..."

Alexander pressed his lips together.

He spoke gently into the phone. "Grandma, let me bring you your medicine."

"No need. I have my medicine with me. You behave yourself and wait. I'm bringing my granddaughter-in-law home!"

The old lady hung up.

She returned the phone to Evelyn. "All settled, granddaughter-in-law!"

Evelyn was speechless.

Was this family completely irresponsible?

Just dumping their elderly relative on her?

She was about to call back.

A WhatsApp notification sounded.

Someone wanted to add her as a contact.

The note said: The old lady's grandson.

Evelyn accepted the request.

She noted his name: Grandson.

He sent a message. [Please look after my grandmother for a few days. She must not be upset.]

Evelyn smirked and typed. "Not convenient. This isn't a..."

She hadn't finished typing.

A sound came from the kitchen.

She walked over.

The old lady was boiling eggs.

Evelyn asked, "Are you hungry? Just eggs aren't enough."

"No." The old lady turned around, smiling. "Granddaughter-in-law, use a warm egg on your face. It reduces swelling."

Evelyn froze.

She hadn't even noticed.

Since her mother hit her.

She had been feeling detached.

She had shut herself off.

The old lady's words softened her heart.

She pursed her lips and looked at her phone.

A transfer notification appeared on the chat screen.

Grandson had transferred $30,000 to her.

Grandson: [This is for one week's expenses. Let me know if it's not enough.]

Evelyn deleted her previous text.

She typed a new reply. [Fine.]

How could her heart soften?

It must be because the money was too much!

The Ashcroft residence.

Isabella Ashcroft's cheek was red and swollen.

She sat on the sofa crying.

Linda Lowell hung her head. "Isabella, that little wretch is just jealous of you. Wait until your father comes home to teach her a lesson!"

Sophia Ashcroft came downstairs.

Her voice was weak but firm. "Evelyn isn't that kind of person. You must have provoked her..."

Isabella clenched her fingers. "It's my fault. I shouldn't have agreed to Julian. She likes Julian so much, that's why she went after Mr. Vance..."

Sophia's face was pale. "Evelyn understood manners when she was little."

Linda said bitterly, "She learned bad ways outside! She hit Isabella today, and she even wanted to hit me!"

Sophia still didn't believe it.

Isabella's eyes flickered. "Mom, we haven't cared for her enough..."

Linda was reminded. "She never comes home! You were so good to her before, but she never comes to see you. Such an ungrateful person!"

Sophia was stunned.

She had raised Evelyn herself.

She had feelings for the girl.

When the child left back then.

She had said to come back and visit when she had time.

But Evelyn never returned.

Had that child really become so cold?

Isabella saw her hesitation.

She changed the topic with satisfaction. "Mom, can you ask Dr. Nan to be a mentor at Stanford?"

Sophia refused immediately. "We can't use a favor to make demands. Dr. Nan has already done enough for the company!"

Isabella smiled. "Mom, that's not what I mean. Stanford is the best university. Maybe Dr. Nan would want to go himself? We can help make the connection. It would be a way to repay him."

Sophia thought it made sense.

She took out her phone. "I'll ask him."

Isabella moved closer. "Dr. Nan has helped us so much. The debt is already repaid. We should host a dinner for him. It would be better to ask him in person."

Dr. Nan never refused any of Sophia's requests.

If he came.

She could ask to be his graduate student.

He definitely wouldn't refuse.

Sophia was persuaded.

She sent a message to Dr. Nan. [Xiao Nan, do you have time to come over for a visit?]

Evelyn saw the message.

Her heart tightened.

Why did Sophia want to meet so suddenly?

Was her health worse?

Thinking of her coughing...

Evelyn stood up and walked out. [I'm free. Should I come now?]

###Chapter 8

Evelyn clenched her jaw.

Her heart burned with urgency.

Just as she was about to leave, a WhatsApp message arrived from Sophia Ashcroft: ["Not now. Are you working this Saturday? I'd like to invite you for a simple meal."]

Evelyn realized she was being too hasty.

Dinner at the Ashcroft residence?

A bitter smile touched her lips.

She had returned the first weekend after moving out a decade ago.

Through the glass window, she saw Sophia, Isabella, and William Ashcroft laughing and talking together.

Sophia's face held a radiant smile Evelyn had never seen before.

Linda Lowell had said then, "See? Without you, they are a real family of three. If you truly care for Sophia, don't disturb her again."

Evelyn left quietly.

Since then, she only bought a small gift for Sophia's birthday each year, leaving it at the doorstep...

After all these years, it was time to meet.

Evelyn replied: ["I should be the one treating you. Saturday at 6 PM. It's a date."]

She sent the restaurant location.

Meeting at the Ashcroft home would inevitably lead to arguments. A public place was quieter.

She could talk properly with Sophia and check on her health...

Sophia replied: ["Good. It's a date."]

Evelyn exited the chat and saw a message from "Grandson":

["The elderly lady is old and impatient. She requires patience."]

["The elderly lady suffers from insomnia. She has difficulty falling asleep at night."]

["The blue pills, twice a day, morning and evening..."]

Five consecutive instructions ended with:

["These are the family doctor's orders. Thank you. Please contact me immediately if Grandma shows any discomfort."]

Evelyn quietly entered the second bedroom and recorded a short video for "Grandson."

Two discreet cars were parked on the street nearby.

The lead luxury van was fully equipped, resembling a small suite.

Alexander Vance, in a black suit, sat on the sofa reviewing documents.

The family doctor sat in a corner, his expression grave.

The elderly lady had moved locations suddenly. She definitely wouldn't sleep tonight.

Her body was weak. Insomnia could lead to arrhythmia. The slightest misstep could be life-threatening.

The following car was equipped with instruments, ready for emergency resuscitation.

As the doctor pondered this, he heard Alexander's WhatsApp notification.

Alexander picked up his phone. His perpetually icy expression showed a trace of... surprise?

The doctor urgently asked, "Has something happened to the elderly lady?"

Alexander pressed his lips together and handed over the phone.

The video showed the elderly lady sprawled comfortably on a small floral-patterned bedsheet, sleeping soundly with light snores!

It was only 9 PM!

Usually, they were grateful if she fell asleep before 1 AM!

The doctor's face was full of astonishment. "This girl is very special to the elderly lady. If she could stay with her, the elderly lady's health would certainly improve!"

Sleep was crucial for her recovery.

Alexander's jaw tightened, his gaze deep and thoughtful.

The next day.

Before leaving, Evelyn addressed the elderly lady, who now had a healthy glow. "I've asked James to come keep you company. He'll be here soon."

"Okay," the little old lady nodded obediently. "Where are you going, granddaughter-in-law?"

"To see someone."

"Who? Is it necessary?"

"Yes."

If she didn't go to see Alexander Vance, he would discover they were married during the marriage registration at City Hall.

Her company needed to go public. Evelyn urgently needed a divorce.

The little old lady waved her hand grandly. "Then I'll have my grandson arrange a meeting for you! My grandson has a lot of influence!"

Evelyn smiled. "Grandma, I'm afraid your grandson probably can't manage it."

The Vance Group was the wealthiest family in San Francisco. However well-off the little old lady's family might be, could they compare to the Vances?

She rode her scooter to the Vance Group building.

Alexander Vance's schedule was monotonous. He had no leisure activities. He was either working or on his way to work.

Evelyn approached the reception desk. Before she could speak, the receptionist said, "You again? Mr. Watson specifically said Mr. Vance has no packages today. You are not allowed upstairs!"

Evelyn said, "I'm not delivering a package. I—"

The receptionist interrupted impatiently, "Do you have an appointment? No appointment, no entry!"

Evelyn was about to speak when the receptionist's eyes lit up, her face breaking into an ingratiating smile. "Miss Ashcroft? You're here!"

Evelyn frowned and turned to see Isabella Ashcroft.

Isabella, graceful and poised, smiled at the receptionist. "I'm here to see Julian."

She glanced at Evelyn and added, "But I forgot to make an appointment..."

"Miss Ashcroft, you're joking. You don't need an appointment! Young Master Evans will be delighted to see you!"

The receptionist swiped her card to open the access gate. "Please, go right in."

Isabella looked at Evelyn and sighed. "Evelyn, the Vance Group isn't a place you can just enter. If you want to pester Mr. Vance, don't make things difficult for the receptionist here..."

Evelyn: ?

When had she made things difficult for the receptionist?

The receptionist frowned.

Mr. Watson had only said not to let this girl upstairs, without explaining why.

So that was the reason?

The receptionist's face showed disdain and annoyance. "Some people really don't know their place. Thinking they can climb the social ladder just because of their looks? Look around. This is the Vance Group. Please step away and don't interrupt my work, or I'll call security!"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. She had intended to explain, but seeing the receptionist's snobbish attitude, she smiled slightly. "Very well. You're the one not letting me upstairs."

Meanwhile, upstairs.

Alexander finished reviewing the urgent documents and took out his phone. He saw the pinned contact: "IronDeficient."

It was the girl's WhatsApp name. A bit strange.

He messaged: ["Hello. How is Grandma today?"]

The reply came quickly: ["She was fine when I left."]

Alexander frowned. ["Left for work?"]

"IronDeficient": ["You could say that."]

Alexander's expression darkened. She left Grandma alone at home?

But she wasn't a hired caregiver. She had no obligation to provide round-the-clock care.

He was the one asking for a favor...

After a moment's thought, Alexander typed: ["Where are you? I'll come find you. We need to discuss Grandma's situation."]

"IronDeficient" immediately sent an address.

Alexander's gaze paused when he saw it.

Wasn't this... the entrance to the Vance Group building?

He stood up and headed downstairs.
###Chapter 9

The receptionist personally escorted Isabella Ashcroft into the elevator.

A cold smile curved Isabella's lips.

Did Evelyn really think her pretty face could help her climb the social ladder?

How naive.

Marriage among the elite was all about matching status and mutual benefit.

No matter how much Julian Evans liked her, he would ultimately choose a union that brought advantages.

Resources like Dr. Nan were something Evelyn could never hope to access.

She didn't care.

All she wanted was the position of Mrs. Evans.

Downstairs in the lobby.

Evelyn was contacting the "grandson."

The old man was staying at her place. She needed to clarify responsibility with his family.

Evelyn leaned back lazily against the sofa. "On what grounds?"

The receptionist lifted her chin. "You're interfering with my work."

Evelyn chuckled lightly. "If you're so easily distracted, perhaps you should consider a different job."

The receptionist's face flushed red. She ordered the guard, "Throw her out!"

As the guard moved to act, the executive elevator doors suddenly opened.

Alexander Vance stepped out, followed by Thomas Watson.

The man's gaze swept across the lobby, as if searching for something.

Thomas strode over. "What's going on here?"

The receptionist spoke first, accusingly. "Mr. Watson, she insists on seeing Mr. Vance and refuses to leave!"

Evelyn stated calmly, "I'm waiting for someone."

The receptionist scoffed. "See, Mr. Watson? She's still making excuses!"

Thomas frowned at Evelyn. "Miss Ross, have some self-respect."

He then turned to the receptionist. "Has the maintenance technician arrived? The penthouse air conditioning urgently needs repair."

The receptionist looked blank. "I haven't seen any technician…"

Evelyn spoke up. "I'm here."

She held up her work ID. "The receptionist wouldn't let me upstairs."

Thomas was stunned. "You're the technician?"

The ID's issue date showed it was from nine years ago.

Thomas looked incredulous. "You're still doing part-time work?"

Evelyn didn't answer. Her gaze shifted to Alexander. "Mr. Vance, your receptionist not only blocked my way but also tried to have me physically removed."

Alexander's cold eyes swept toward the receptionist. "You're fired."

The receptionist's face turned deathly pale. She didn't dare to argue.

Alexander looked at Evelyn, his expression detached. "Satisfied?"

Evelyn stood up. "Mr. Vance, have you checked the records at City Hall?"

Alexander ignored her. He instructed Thomas, "Take her upstairs. I don't want to see her again."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

Thomas grabbed her arm. "Miss Ross, come with me. Even if you went through all this trouble to get up there, the boss wouldn't give you a second glance."

Alexander took out his phone and placed a voice call to "Wuxing Que Tie."

Evelyn's phone rang at the exact same moment.
###Chapter 10

Her phone rang abruptly.

The call hadn't connected.

Top floor.

Evelyn was on the phone with James Klein while typing a reply.

With his number, she could remind him about City Hall anytime.

Divorce paperwork required contact.

A notification chimed.

"Grandson" sent a WhatsApp: [Reach out whenever you're free.]

The air conditioner was fixed.

His attitude softened slightly.

To prevent her from bothering the boss again.

He didn't take her words seriously.

Returning to the top floor.

Alexander had a lunch appointment at noon.

Thomas accompanied him throughout.

Thomas warily scanned the servers.

He didn't see Evelyn.

He felt he was being too sensitive.

How could one person hold three jobs simultaneously?

Evelyn stood at the door wearing a chef's hat.

Five work IDs accidentally fell out.

Then he heard her say calmly, "I didn't bring them all."

Thomas was speechless.

Alexander's face grew colder.

Everyone at the table was watching her.

Alexander said to Thomas in a low voice, "Find out who leaked my schedule."

Evelyn's heart leapt.

She thought he had agreed.

Then she heard the rest.

Thomas stepped forward. "Miss Ross, please leave."

Why didn't he believe her?

Her patience was wearing thin.

She didn't leave.

She waited outside.

She hadn't been treated like this in two years.

The more she thought about it, the angrier she became.

She took out her phone to vent.

She scrolled through WhatsApp but couldn't find the right person.

She simply posted on her feed.

Inside the private room.

Alexander dealt with the flattery.

Bored, he scrolled through his phone.

He saw a post from "MetalSeeker": [Don't get angry. Be patient with grandsons.]

Alexander was taken aback.

He sent a message: [You have a grandson?]

"MetalSeeker": [?]

"MetalSeeker": [That's an insult! Some fool doesn't understand words!]

Alexander: "..."

"MetalSeeker": [If you can insult, insult more.]

Alexander's lips curved. [We only insult people. We don't insult him.]

"MetalSeeker": [Seeing you know how to talk, I'll add something extra for the Dowager tonight. Any dietary restrictions?]

Alexander: [Less sweet and spicy. Anything else is fine.]

"MetalSeeker": [Okay.]

The conversation made him feel comfortable.

He suddenly asked: [Can I visit Grandma tonight?]

"MetalSeeker": [You're welcome anytime.]

She sent the specific address.
###Chapter 11

Alexander Vance noted the address and replied: ["Apologies for the disturbance."]

He needed to bring a doctor to check on his grandmother tonight. Otherwise, he wouldn't feel at ease.

"Iron-Deficient" did not reply again.

She seemed to dislike pleasantries.

Alexander quietly noted this habit of hers.

"Mr. Vance, chatting with Mrs. Vance?"

Someone teased him from the side. "You look so happy."

The smile vanished from Alexander's lips.

He put away his phone and looked at the speaker.

Since he took over the Vance Group, Mrs. Vance had never made a public appearance. Some had begun to doubt the marriage's authenticity.

He did not deny it.

The man took his silence as confirmation and fawned, "Mr. Vance is so devoted. That head chef was so beautiful, yet you didn't even glance her way. A true model husband!"

Others chimed in. "We're all curious about what Mrs. Vance is like, to have won you over... Mrs. Eleanor must be very pleased with her granddaughter-in-law?"

Everyone knew Alexander had a cold relationship with the Vance family. He only cared about Mrs. Eleanor.

Alexander lowered his gaze, his voice flat. "Grandmother likes her very much."

Outside the door.

Evelyn Ross was waiting idly when she heard a familiar voice.

"Evelyn Ross!"

She turned and met a handsome but grim face.

Julian Evans wore a black suit. His usually gentle expression was now icy. "I knew you'd be here."

Evelyn pressed her lips together.

In university, she was always alone.

Julian was warm and sunny. He stuck by her side. He was her only friend.

Their relationship was now awkward.

She hesitated. "Is something wrong?"

Julian sneered. "Isabella told me everything. Why did you hide it from me? I despise mistresses and love children the most. Yet I chased you for four years like a fool. Was it fun?"

A sharp pain shot through Evelyn's heart...

She never understood why Julian, who promised a surprise at graduation, proposed to Isabella instead.

So that was why.

His family was troubled. He had a love child for a sister.

Isabella merely revealed her identity and easily destroyed their four-year bond.

Evelyn took a step back, creating distance.

Julian closed in and demanded, "Don't you have anything to explain?"

Explain what?

Explain why she was a love child? Why she was born into the Lowell family?

Evelyn smirked mockingly. "No."

She turned to leave.

Since he looked down on her origins, then so be it if they couldn't be friends.

Her resolute attitude irritated Julian. Seeing her about to leave, a panic seized him. He grabbed her arm. "You can't leave!"

Evelyn looked back. "Is there anything else?"

Julian's face darkened, finding her cold and heartless.

Anger mixed with grievance welled up inside him.

He was the one who let go first. Why did it feel like he was being abandoned?

He mocked, "You're still working part-time here? Haven't found a job? Why not be my personal assistant?"

Evelyn shook him off and refused coldly. "I can't do that."

Julian persisted. "You won't have to do much. I have an apartment near the company. You can live there and take care of my daily needs. A monthly salary of five thousand USD. Is that enough?"

Evelyn's eyes turned icy. "You want me to be your mistress?"

Julian scoffed. "You must be good at that. Your mother was a mistress. You learned from the best. It's in your blood. I trust you're professional..."

Slap!

Evelyn slapped him across the face. "Julian Evans, that's enough!"

Julian touched his stinging cheek and反而 laughed. His eyes were obsessive and sinister.

"Evelyn Ross, mark my words. If I don't want you, no one in San Francisco will dare to have you! You won't find a job or a husband. You'll end up as a plaything. Since that's the case, why not choose me? At least, for old times' sake, I'll be gentle..."

"I'll give you time to think it over. Come find me when you've made up your mind. You have my number."

Julian finished and strode away.

Evelyn smiled bitterly at herself.

She turned around and saw Alexander Vance standing at the private room's entrance.

...

Alexander disliked socializing. He found an excuse to leave with Thomas Watson. He didn't expect to witness the scene in the hallway just after stepping out.

His gaze was deep, unreadable.

Thomas Watson clicked his tongue at Evelyn. "So this is why you didn't get a stable job after graduation? You've been doing part-time work? Julian Evans forced you into this?"

Evelyn: ?

Thomas sighed. "So pestering the boss was your last resort? No one in San Francisco dares to cross the Vance family..."

"......"

Evelyn narrowed her peach-blossom eyes. She didn't refute it.

Two days had passed. This was the first time they were speaking calmly!

Thomas felt sympathetic. "Boss, help her out? She's quite pitiful."

Evelyn saw a glimmer of hope.

Yes, yes, yes... Help the poor girl. Just go to City Hall and finalize the divorce! The company is waiting to go public!

But then she heard Alexander's cold voice. "Why should I upset Julian Evans for someone insignificant?"

He walked past Evelyn with long strides.

Pitiful?

The girl pretended to be docile, but her eyes held cunning and delight. Where was the pity? She was downright hateful!

Besides, she managed to get his itinerary. That showed she was shrewd and resourceful!

The hallway fell quiet.

Evelyn stood still, thinking Alexander was cold-blooded and heartless!

...Forget it. What that "grandson" said was right. She shouldn't waste her anger on people.

Evelyn left the building. She rode her scooter slowly home.

There was an elderly person at home. She couldn't stay out too long. She would find him again tomorrow.

Evening.

Alexander got off work on time and arrived at the old residential area in the suburbs.

Carrying a fruit basket, he found the unit according to the WhatsApp address and knocked.

His grandmother's familiar voice came from inside. "Coming, coming~"
###Chapter 12

The door opened.

Alexander Vance's gaze fell on Eleanor Vance.

The elderly woman looked rosy-cheeked and full of energy.

He glanced around the room.

It was clean and bright, a cozy sixty-square-meter two-bedroom apartment.

Four light, nutritious dishes were arranged on the dining table.

The sound of running water came from the bathroom.

A blurred silhouette was visible through the frosted glass door.

The curves were graceful, the waist slender.

Alexander averted his eyes as if shocked.

The tips of his ears grew warm.

The family doctor followed behind him, about to enter.

Alexander stepped aside to block him. "Give me the equipment. Wait in the car."

The doctor nodded and left, gently closing the door.

Alexander quickly took the elderly woman's blood pressure and blood sugar.

All the readings were normal.

The old lady raised her eyebrows proudly. "My granddaughter-in-law takes excellent care of me!"

Alexander finally felt relieved.

His gaze unconsciously drifted toward the bathroom.

He packed up the equipment. "Since you're fine, I'll go now."

The old lady quickly grabbed his arm. "Won't you stay for dinner? My granddaughter-in-law is a wonderful cook!"

"...It wouldn't be proper."

Alexander set down the monitor. "Keep this here."

The old lady's eyes twinkled. "Take it away. My granddaughter-in-law doesn't know how to use it. I don't want anyone else checking on me. I only want you to come every day!"

The sound of water abruptly stopped.

Alexander immediately stood up and grabbed his medical kit. "I'll come back tomorrow."

"Promise me!"

"...Yes."

The moment the door closed, the bathroom door opened.

Evelyn Ross stepped out fully dressed, drying her hair. "Has your grandson left?"

"He said it wouldn't be proper... That boy is just too serious. Granddaughter-in-law, do you find him boring? He can change..."

"I really am not your granddaughter-in-law..."

"You are!"

Evelyn shook her head helplessly.

Over the past two days, she had found the old lady gentle and easy to get along with, except on this particular matter.

She dried her hair and had dinner with the old lady.

At nine o'clock sharp, she lit some lavender essential oil.

The old lady soon fell asleep.

Evelyn dutifully sent a video to the "grandson."

He replied quickly: [Grandma looks well. Thank you.]

Evelyn: [It's my pleasure.]

"Grandson": [Do you need help with what happened yesterday?]

Evelyn knew he meant the social media post. She smiled slightly: [No need.]

Inside the limousine, Alexander looked at the reply, his stern expression softening.

This girl didn't know his identity, yet she took good care of his grandmother without asking for anything in return.

She was far better than that Evelyn Ross, who had no connection to him yet persistently pursued him.

He replied: [I owe you a favor. If you need anything in the future, just ask.]

Evelyn didn't take it seriously.

The only thing she needed right now was a divorce, which required Alexander Vance's cooperation. The "grandson" couldn't help with that.

The next morning.

Evelyn took the old lady to a luxury mall.

The grandson provided ample living expenses, so she saw no reason to hold back.

As soon as they entered the store, the old lady began browsing with familiarity.

Eleanor Vance often wore this brand. Each season's new collection was sent directly to the Vance residence for her to choose from.

This was her first time visiting a boutique, and she found it novel.

Not far away.

Isabella Ashcroft held William Ashcroft's arm and said softly, "Dad, you're so busy. You didn't have to come shopping with me..."

William smiled dotingly. "Tonight is your first formal visit to the Vance family. We mustn't be careless with the gifts for the elders, especially Mrs. Vance..."

Isabella was curious. "That great-grandmother is eighty-seven years old. Why does the Vance family value her so much?"

William lowered his voice. "The eldest branch of the Vance family and Alexander Vance are fighting openly and covertly for the position of head. Mrs. Vance holds twenty percent of the shares. If you can win her favor, your status in the Vance family will be secure!"

Isabella immediately asked, "What does Mrs. Vance like?"

William recalled the Mrs. Vance he had met two years ago and said slowly, "She has Alzheimer's and can be a bit eccentric, but she particularly likes H's clothing. Just cater to her preferences. Even if you can't make her like you, be careful not to annoy her..."

Isabella nodded solemnly.

They entered the H store.

William waited in the lounge area while Isabella browsed for gifts alone.

Passing by the fitting area, she suddenly caught sight of Evelyn Ross and an old lady.

The old lady emerged from the fitting room and asked with a smile, "How does this look, granddaughter-in-law?"

Granddaughter-in-law?

This old woman was that ruffian's grandmother?

Isabella's lips curled into a sneer.

She saw Evelyn shake her head.

The old lady tried on another outfit, and Evelyn still shook her head.

The old lady sighed. "Don't any of these clothes look good?"

Isabella couldn't resist approaching. "Evelyn, what a coincidence. Is this your husband's grandmother?"

Evelyn ignored her with a cold expression.

Isabella turned to the old lady. "Hello, Grandma. You haven't bought clothes from this brand before, have you? The clothes here are very selective. They don't really suit ordinary people..."

The old lady immediately widened her eyes. "Who are you saying has no style? How rude!"

Evelyn quickly supported the old lady, afraid she might become upset.

Isabella continued, "Evelyn, it would be more suitable to take your grandmother to a wholesale market. The prices are better there..."

"Shut up!"

Evelyn snapped sharply.

The commotion attracted William.

He hurried over. "What's going on?"

Isabella said pitifully, "Dad, Evelyn has been having her grandmother try on lots of clothes but isn't buying anything... I was just making a friendly suggestion..."

William frowned at Evelyn.

But when his eyes fell on the old lady beside her, his expression changed dramatically.

Mrs. Vance?!
###Chapter 13

The old woman was nearly ninety.

Her skin was so loose its original shape was lost.

Her thin, withered hand pressed against her chest.

Her breathing was so rapid it seemed she might stop at any moment.

William Ashcroft felt he had overthought it.

How could someone of Eleanor Vance's status come to a place like this herself…

He was afraid Isabella might be scammed by the elderly woman.

He scowled at Evelyn Ross. "Your sister meant well. Was such a big reaction necessary? Making a scene here is too embarrassing."

The little old lady immediately looked at Evelyn. "Granddaughter-in-law, is this your father?"

Evelyn lowered her eyes.

The term "father" felt very distant to her.

As a child in the Ashcroft home, Linda Lowell never let her get close to William.

She could only hide in the shadows and secretly watch him.

Watch him spin Isabella around in circles.

Watch him buy toys for Isabella.

Back then, she thought her father's figure was as tall and imposing as a mountain.

On nights when Linda hit her, scolded her, or left her hungry, she always fantasized that "Dad" would suddenly appear.

And take her away from that home.

It never happened.

Not even once.

He never showed any concern for her.

When they occasionally crossed paths, he would only say, "Listen to your mother."

The atmosphere in the Ashcroft family was strange.

Everyone had accepted Linda.

But no one was willing to accept little her.

Evelyn pressed her lips together and did not answer the old woman's question.

She picked up the few outfits she had tried on earlier.

She selected the one that was relatively suitable and handed it to the shop assistant.

She prepared to pay and leave.

Seeing this, William spoke up. "I know you're struggling. I'll pay for this outfit. Consider it a gift for your husband's grandmother."

Isabella suddenly interjected. "Dad, I looked earlier. This one is the most suitable for a gift…"

The shop assistant immediately smiled and chimed in. "The young lady has excellent taste. This is pure hand embroidery. The only one in San Francisco. Perhaps you'd like to look at something else?"

William's eyes lit up. "This one it is!"

He turned to Evelyn. "Let your sister have this outfit. You choose another one."

Evelyn's peach-blossom eyes showed no emotion. "Why should I?"

"Your sister is visiting the Vance family! This is a gift prepared for Eleanor Vance!"

"So I have to give it up?"

William scolded sternly. "What does it matter what your husband's grandmother wears? Could she possibly be more important than Eleanor Vance?"

Evelyn's attitude was firm. "No."

"Can't you be a little more sensible? Must you always compete with your sister?"

Evelyn found it ridiculous.

She had seen the outfit first.

How did it become her competing with Isabella?

William spoke again. "Fine. The outfit costs fifty thousand. I'll give you two hundred thousand. Consider it supplementing your dowry."

Isabella pretended to advise. "Evelyn, you've never had a job. You should agree quickly. Don't let pride make you miss this money and regret it later."

William issued a final threat. "Think carefully. If you insist on having this, I won't pay for it."

A true veteran of the business world.

Using both coercion and temptation just to buy an outfit for Isabella.

What a good father…

Evelyn couldn't be bothered to argue further.

She was about to take out her bank card.

The little old lady suddenly pressed her hand down. "Granddaughter-in-law, I don't want this one. It's ugly."

Evelyn looked at her. "Grandmother, the others are even less suitable."

The little old lady was stubborn. "Then we still shouldn't settle."

She had already bought all the suitable clothes for this season!

Everything left in the store was what she had picked over.

How could she possibly want any of it?

Listening to their conversation, the granddaughter-in-law's sister was buying this outfit to give to Eleanor Vance?

Eleanor Vance… that name sounded somewhat familiar…

Who was she again?

The little old lady felt a bit confused.

Evelyn didn't dare insist and went along with her. "Then we won't buy it."

William was finally satisfied. "If you had been this sensible from the start, it would have been over. If you can't afford it in the future, don't come into stores like this. Save yourself from embarrassing the Ashcroft family!"

Isabella sneered.

Putting on such an act with the old lady earlier, pretending it was real.

Turns out she still couldn't afford to buy it!

She asked the shop assistant to pack the outfit.

She smiled at Evelyn. "Thank you for giving it to me. After I marry into the Vance family, I'll arrange jobs for you and your husband."

Evelyn completely ignored her.

She prepared to leave with the old lady.

"Wait!"

William stopped her and handed her a check. "Take the two hundred thousand. I said I'd give you a dowry. Use it to subsidize your living."

Evelyn was slightly stunned. "It's not necessary."

"Think it's too little? The Ashcroft company belongs to Isabella's mother. It will only be left to her in the future. This is the most I can give you!"

William said condescendingly. "Take the money. Set up a small stall and live your life. Be content. Don't covet things that don't belong to you!"

For a moment, Evelyn had really thought William felt some fatherly love for her.

Now she just felt讽刺. "What things don't belong to me?"

"For example, shopping in malls like this. And also… seducing Mr. Vance!"

William warned. "Don't bring down the Ashcroft family with your own self-destruction! Today the shop assistant was afraid the old lady would cause trouble, so she didn't pursue it. Angering Mr. Vance isn't something that can be solved by acting shamelessly!"

Evelyn's heart turned completely cold. "Don't worry. The Ashcroft family and I have been unrelated for a long time!"

She left without looking back.

After they had walked some distance, the little old lady held her hand. "Granddaughter-in-law, your father and sister are too terrible. From now on, I'll have my grandson protect you. Let's see who dares to look down on you then!"

Evelyn felt a warmth in her heart. "Okay."

The little old lady said stubbornly. "Let them give that ugly outfit to their so-called Eleanor Vance. I don't want it!"

As soon as the words left her mouth, the old lady's phone rang.

She answered it.

A man's voice came from the other end. "Mom, come back with Alexander tonight. Julian's fiancée is visiting for the first time. It's time for the family to meet her."

The little old lady was stunned.

The haze that had been clouding her mind was suddenly pulled away!

In a flash of insight, she remembered who she was.

So she was that unlucky Eleanor Vance!

Thinking back to just being despised and mocked…

The little old lady immediately gritted her teeth and said, "I will definitely be back!"

She hung up the phone.

She sent Alexander Vance a voice message. "Grandson, I've been bullied! You must come back tonight and stand up for me!"

After sending the message, the little old lady mysteriously pulled Evelyn closer. "Granddaughter-in-law, I remembered my grandson's name!"
###Chapter 14

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.
"What is his name?"

She was genuinely curious about this "grandson's" identity.

She had met many corporate executives.
Perhaps they had crossed paths before.

The little old woman beckoned her mysteriously.

Evelyn leaned in cooperatively.

The old lady lowered her voice.
"I'll only tell you. Don't tell anyone else!"

"Okay."

The little old woman cleared her throat.
"His childhood name is Doggie."

Evelyn fell silent.

The old lady explained.
"My grandson was born in the Year of the Dog."
"He was as skinny as a kitten when he was born."
"I gave him that name. A humble name helps a child survive!"

"..."

Evelyn suppressed her laughter.

From the old lady's description, her grandson was at least a company CEO.

She wondered what his elite subordinates would think upon hearing the name "Doggie."

She was about to ask for his formal name when the old lady grabbed her hand.
"Granddaughter-in-law, the clothes here are too ordinary."
"Come home with me tonight to pick a few pieces?"

Evelyn recalled the voice message the old lady had sent earlier.
She knew her grandson would be home too.

They had already arranged to meet.
She nodded.
"Okay."

After a simple lunch outside, Evelyn planned to take the old lady home to rest.
She intended to intercept Alexander Vance herself.

But the old lady tugged at her sleeve.
"I'm not tired."
"I'll accompany you to work."
"We can go home together after you finish."

She looked afraid Evelyn would change her mind.

The older she got, the more she resembled a child...

Evelyn reluctantly agreed.

They arrived at a charity gala.

Evelyn settled the old lady in the lounge.
She then entered the venue with her press pass.

Alexander Vance was scheduled to deliver a speech on behalf of the Vance Group.

She deliberately chose a seat in the center of the front row.
She wanted to ensure he would see her immediately.

She had also arranged with the organizers to ask a question later.

In front of everyone, she would remind Alexander Vance to verify their marital status at City Hall!

Thinking of his potential expression, Evelyn's lips curved into a smile.

The event began.
Soon it was the Vance Group's segment.

Evelyn watched with anticipation.
But she saw Thomas Watson take the stage instead.

Thomas shot her a meaningful glance.
"My apologies. Mr. Vance has urgent matters to attend to."
"But the Vance Group's charitable commitments remain unchanged..."

Evelyn's smile froze.

She checked her phone.
She saw a message from James.
[Boss, Alexander Vance's entire schedule has been changed last minute!]

...This man was truly something!

It probably wasn't directed at her personally.
In Alexander Vance's eyes, she was insignificant.

He likely discovered his schedule was leaked and changed it for security reasons.

Tsk.
Who was he guarding against?

...

Evelyn completed her interview duties and handed her equipment to a colleague.

She picked up the old lady from the lounge and headed to the next location.

It was an automobile factory owned by the Vance Group.

Alexander Vance could cancel other appointments.
But a基层巡视 like this involved extensive preparation by the factory.
He wouldn't cancel it last minute.

Sure enough, after waiting briefly at a café outside the factory, they saw a black Bentley enter the premises.

Evelyn asked the café staff to look after the old lady.
She approached the main gate alone.

She showed her temporary worker pass.
The guard shook his head.
"Sorry. Mr. Watson specifically ordered no outsiders allowed today."

"..."

Evelyn had expected this.

Thomas was pulling out all the stops to prevent her from meeting Alexander Vance...

She smiled faintly and produced another pass.

The guard immediately straightened up upon seeing it.
He respectfully stepped aside.
"Please proceed."

Evelyn retrieved her pass and calmly entered the factory.

She found Alexander Vance quickly.

The man was inspecting the workshop accompanied by the factory manager.
All regular workers had been cleared out.
Bodyguards stood watch at the entrance, making approach impossible.

Evelyn could only watch from afar through a glass window.

The man wore a tailored suit.
His noble demeanor starkly contrasted with the cold machinery.

He listened intently to reports.
His occasional comments drew visible admiration from the factory manager.

In mere moments, this newly appointed leader had earned everyone's respect.

Suddenly, he turned as if sensing something.
His eyes met Evelyn's.

Evelyn waved at him.

Alexander Vance frowned and looked away.

Thomas walked quickly to the window.
He pulled down the blinds with a sharp "swish."

Evelyn was speechless.

She was once again "escorted" out by bodyguards.

Thomas stood at the entrance reprimanding the guard.
"Didn't I say no one gets in?"

The guard whispered in defense.
"But she's not ordinary. She..."

Thomas coldly interrupted.
"Indeed, not ordinary."

After all, what normal person worked so many jobs simultaneously!

Thomas looked at Evelyn with disgust.
"Miss Ross, the boss has already shown you courtesy."
"Please know your limits."
"If you continue harassing him, we will involve the police!"

Evelyn sighed.
"If you just verify at City Hall, you'll know I'm telling the truth."
"Why won't you believe me?"

Thomas scoffed.
"Both parties must be present to get married here."
"Your lies are full of holes."
"Only someone insane would believe such nonsense!"

"..."

Evelyn was utterly exasperated.

She spread her hands.
"Then I'll have to keep trying to see him."

Thomas sneered.
"Do you really think you can see the boss whenever you want?"
"If you succeed again, I don't deserve to be his assistant!"

Evelyn said nothing more and turned to leave.

It seemed she would need to use special methods tomorrow.

After picking up the old lady, Evelyn messaged the "grandson."
[The old lady wants to go home to get some clothes. Please send me the address.]

The "grandson" quickly sent his location.

He added another message.
[I'm almost done. Where are you? I'll pick you up.]

Evelyn opened the location and froze.

Beverly Hills... Wasn't this the Vance family estate?!
###Chapter 15

To the Vance Estate!

Beverly Hills was a legend in San Francisco.

It occupied the mountainside, sprawling over three thousand acres.

The Vance family estate alone claimed more than two thousand of those acres.

Dozens of other villas were scattered around the Vance property like stars.

It was the most exclusive enclave for the wealthy in the city.

Evelyn Ross had assumed the little old lady lived in one of the smaller surrounding villas.

She hadn't dared to imagine the Vance mansion itself.

She replied to the message: [No need. We are already in the car.]

The little old lady looked tired and fell asleep as soon as she got in the car.

The taxi wound its way up the wide, curving mountain road.

It entered Beverly Hills and dropped them off at the pin location before leaving.

Evelyn looked up at the magnificent, ornately carved black gate before her.

She hesitated. "Grandma, is this your home?"

The old lady was bleary-eyed and looked confused. "Granddaughter-in-law, I can't remember."

Evelyn sighed.

She texted the "grandson": [I think we might be lost.]

Grandson: [Wait right there. I'm coming.]
