When a car approached, Evelyn noticed the nearest house was a few minutes' drive away.

They really couldn't wander off.

She helped the old lady sit on a nearby stone bench.

Suddenly, a BMW X7 pulled up in front of them.

Isabella Ashcroft and Linda Lowell rushed out from the back seat.

Linda frowned deeply, her voice sharp. "Evelyn Ross? It is you! What are you doing here?"

She had come to the Vance estate today as a maid, accompanying Isabella for a visit.

She had seen a girl loitering near the Vance gate from afar and couldn't believe it was actually her!

Isabella stared at the tightly shut Vance gate. "Evelyn, are you here to bother Mr. Vance again?"

Linda immediately started yelling. "You shameless wretch! Don't you know what day it is? Get out of here now! If you dare ruin Isabella's family recognition, I won't let you off!"

Evelyn was sick of them.

But with the old lady present, she didn't want a confrontation.

She suppressed her impatience and explained, "I'm taking an elderly lady home. We took a wrong turn."

Isabella blurted out, "Isn't this your husband's grandmother? How could she possibly live here?"

Evelyn retorted, "Why couldn't she? Do you own this neighborhood?"

Isabella was speechless.

Linda cursed angrily, "Stop pretending! How could that pauper husband's grandmother afford to live here?"

She turned directly to the old lady. "You old hag, tell me then, what's your house number?"

Evelyn frowned and was about to speak.

But the old lady stood up. "I remember now! I live right here! Granddaughter-in-law, let's go home!"

She pulled Evelyn toward the grand Vance gate.

They were about a hundred yards from the gate.

After just two steps, Isabella spoke. "Evelyn, after all your excuses, you're still using the old lady as a reason to go to the Vance house?"

Evelyn stopped in her tracks and stared ahead.

The Vance estate?

Although she had secretly wondered when she saw the impressive gate...

She couldn't connect the polite "grandson" on WhatsApp with the cold, ruthless Alexander Vance...

Maybe the old lady was mistaken?

She was about to confirm.

But Linda rushed over and made a grab for the old lady. "You old fool! Spreading lies to beg at the Vance doorstep! How shameless! I won't let you cause trouble at the Vance house!"

Evelyn was alarmed.

She shielded the old lady with one hand and grabbed Linda with the other, shoving her away forcefully.

Her voice was stern. "What are you doing?"

The old lady was frail and couldn't handle being pushed!

Linda stumbled back, steadying herself before screaming, "Ungrateful brat! You hit your own mother for this old hag! I'll call the police! I'll report you for filial impiety!"

Evelyn ignored her.

But the old lady retorted, "My granddaughter-in-law is not impious! You're lying!"

Linda sneered. "You old thing, how poor are you that you have to pimp out your granddaughter-in-law to other men? Huh? Does she give you a cut? Or is that your family business? You serviced plenty of men in your youth too, didn't you? You lecherous old—"

"Shut up!!" Evelyn shouted.

She quickly looked at the old lady.

The old lady, who had never been insulted like this, was trembling with rage.

Her hand pressed against her chest.

Her body shook uncontrollably.

She began to slump toward the ground.

Evelyn hurriedly caught her. "Grandma! What's wrong?"

The old lady took deep breaths, unable to speak.

Evelyn immediately reached into the old lady's pocket for the emergency heart pills.

She opened the bottle cap only to find it empty!!

Her pupils contracted.

The old woman's body was too weak for CPR.

Medicine was best.

She looked sharply at the grand gate.

The Vance house must have medicine!

Even if the old lady wasn't a Vance, she was a neighbor.

They wouldn't refuse to save a life!

Evelyn picked up the old lady. "Grandma, don't be afraid. I'll take you to get medicine!"

But Isabella grabbed her arm forcefully. "Evelyn, don't do this. Playing the elderly card when buying clothes was bad enough, but how can you try to scam the Vances? They won't let you in..."

She looked at the old lady, her eyes dark.

The old woman looked terrible, like she really might not make it.

If she died just like that, Evelyn's husband would surely hate her!

Let's see how she seduces men then!

Evelyn was furious.

Rage boiled in her peach-blossom eyes.

She lifted her leg to kick Isabella.

"Get lost!" Her voice was hoarse.

The next moment, her leg was tightly hugged.

Linda sat on the ground, clutching her legs without any dignity.

"You think you're the only ones who can fake an accident? I can do it too! I'm dying! I'm being beaten to death by my own daughter!"

"My chest hurts so much! I can't take it!"

"I'd rather die today than let this old hag scam the Vances!"

Evelyn trembled all over, her expression grim.

She kicked Linda away and elbowed Isabella in the stomach.

She strode forward!

Isabella bent over, sweating from the pain.

Linda, ignoring the pain, rushed up again to hug her legs, making a scene.

"Help! Murder!"

Evelyn, mindful of the old lady in her arms, couldn't break free immediately!

Just then!

A black Bentley slowly drove up.

Alexander Vance got out of the car, his voice sharp. "What is going on here?"

Isabella quickly spoke. "Mr. Vance, it's Evelyn. She insists on bringing a dying old woman to scam the Vances. It's my fault for not stopping her..."

Hearing this, Evelyn slowly turned around.

Seeing the grandmother in her arms, Alexander's pupils constricted violently.
###Chapter 16

The elderly woman in Evelyn's arms was deathly pale.

Her arm hung limp.

Alexander Vance charged forward.

His face was stoic, but a storm raged in his eyes.

Eleanor was his last source of warmth in this world.

Her health had been failing for years.

Doctors had hinted she might not make it through this year.

He could not lose her.

Evelyn spoke quickly as he approached. "Mrs. Vance had a heart attack."

"The emergency medicine is gone. She needs more immediately."

She paused, then added. "She will be fine if treated in time."

Alexander's lips pressed into a tight line.

He took his grandmother and rushed into the Vance mansion without looking back.

Evelyn remained outside.

Linda still clung desperately to her leg.

Isabella watched Alexander's retreating figure, a strange unease in her heart. "Was that really Mrs. Vance?"

"How could Mrs. Vance be with Evelyn? She's the grandmother of Evelyn's husband!"

Linda finally let go.

She stood up, brushing dust off her clothes. "Mr. Vance was just worried about a death on their property."

"A scandal about the Vances refusing to help would look bad…"

She shot a venomous glare at Evelyn. "She knew that. That's why she dared to bring that old woman here."

Isabella frowned in thought.

It made sense.

It was impossible for Evelyn's lowlife husband to be Alexander Vance.

She was overthinking…

The main gate of the Vance estate swung open.

Julian Evans stepped out.

Isabella's eyes lit up.

She smiled sweetly. "Julian, I told you to wait at the parking lot. Why did you come to the gate?"

Julian looked annoyed.

Isabella had texted she was almost here.

She had too many gifts and begged him to come help.

He had waited in the parking lot for a long time.

He finally came out to check.

His eyes fell on Evelyn.

He stopped walking.

His impatience vanished, replaced by a gentle expression.

He spoke softly to Isabella. "It's your first time here. I was worried you'd get lost."

Isabella ran over and linked her arm with his, cooing. "You're so sweet, Julian~"

She probed carefully. "Is Mrs. Vance home today?"

"Yes."

"Has she been home all day?"

"Yes. Great-grandmother is unwell. She rests at home all year."

Mrs. Vance lived in a separate wing.

She disliked disturbances.

Julian answered absently.

Isabella felt completely relieved.

Julian's gaze swept over Evelyn brazenly.

He scrutinized every tiny expression on her face.

He searched for jealousy, envy, or resentment.

He found nothing.

His face darkened.

Evelyn was worried about the old lady.

She paid no attention to their performance.

She was now almost certain the "grandson" was Alexander Vance.

She looked at the gate, wondering if she would be stopped if she tried to enter now.

A shadow fell over her.

Julian blocked her path.

He said coldly, "Evelyn, here to see me? Finally came to your senses?"

Evelyn: ?

Julian sneered, his words cutting. "A call would have sufficed if you'd thought it over."

"Who said you could come to the Vance estate? Do you think someone like you, a love child, is allowed through these gates?"

"…"

Evelyn pressed her lips together and stayed silent.

Her stubborn expression softened Julian's heart.

"If you really want to see inside, it might be arranged…"

Isabella cut him off. "You misunderstood, Julian."

"Evelyn is waiting for her husband's grandmother. The old woman had a heart attack just now."

"Mr. Vance carried her inside for emergency treatment…"

Julian froze. "…Husband?"

Isabella smiled. "I never got the chance to tell you. Evelyn is married…"

Julian's eyes reddened instantly.

He gritted his teeth. "Evelyn, who is that man?!"

He sounded like a man catching his wife cheating.

Evelyn's eyes held mockery. "Julian, who my husband is is none of your business."

Julian raised his voice sharply. "How is it none of my business? I'd like to see who dared marry you without my permission!"

Isabella quickly added fuel. "According to Evelyn's mother, he's some lowlife."

Julian clenched his fists, lashing out recklessly. "You'd degrade yourself like this? A love child and a lowlife. A perfect match!"

Isabella continued fanning the flames. "Evelyn probably isn't satisfied either. That's why she's always pestering Mr. Vance…"

Evelyn let out a cold, derisive laugh. "Isabella, you call yourself a lady from a good family."

"Yet your mind is so filthy. Is there nothing else in there?"

Isabella's face stiffened.

Her fingers tightened.

She sighed. "Evelyn, I know you're trying to get back at me and Julian."

"But Mr. Vance is already married. You shouldn't try to break up someone's marriage…"

Julian exploded in rage.

He had been waiting for Evelyn's call these past two days.

He thought if she begged for mercy, he might let her off.

So she had already found her next target?!

He was furious. "No wonder your husband's grandmother had a heart attack at the Vance gates!"

"You came to see my uncle, didn't you? Evelyn, have you no shame?!"

He trembled with anger.

He pointed at Evelyn and ordered the guard. "See this face? Remember it. Don't let her set foot inside the Vance estate!"

Isabella lowered her head, hiding a smug smile.

She could almost call the shots at the Vance gates…

The next moment, the Vance housekeeper emerged from inside.

Her gaze swept over the group and settled on Evelyn.

"Miss Evelyn Ross, sir requests your presence inside."

Isabella was stunned.

Julian was dumbfounded.

Evelyn looked up.

She glanced at the guard with a faint, mocking smile. "So, can I go in now?"

The guard cautiously peeked at Julian.

Then he opened the gate without hesitation.

He knew very well who was in charge at the Vances…

……

The Vance estate was vast.

A golf cart transported Evelyn to the main residence's entrance.

The housekeeper was respectful. "Mrs. Vance is stable now."

"She and sir are waiting for you in the drawing room."

Evelyn felt slightly relieved.

As she got off the cart, she saw the Ashcroft's BMW X7 parked not far away in the lot.

Isabella, Linda, and Julian, led by him, marched over with aggressive air.

They met Evelyn at the entrance.

Linda glared at her angrily. "Mr. Vance must have called you here to collect your grandmother."

"Take her and get out. Don't let your filthy kind dirty the Vance carpets!"

As she spoke, they entered the drawing room.

They immediately saw Mrs. Vance seated in the host's chair.

The old lady glared at them furiously.

She said sharply, "You wicked woman! Who are you calling filthy?!"
###Chapter 17

The elderly woman had changed into an exquisite qipao.

Her face was still pale.

Alexander Vance sat expressionlessly on her left.

On her right sat his father, Robert Vance, in his sixties.

On the side sofa were Alexander's elder brother and sister-in-law, Julian Evans' parents.

No one introduced the elderly woman's identity.

But only Eleanor Vance, the matriarch of the Vance family, could sit in the main seat.

Linda Lowell froze on the spot.

"You... you..."

She stammered for a long time, unable to form a complete sentence.

Isabella Ashcroft's legs went weak, and she nearly collapsed.

Julian caught her.

"What's wrong?" he asked, puzzled.

Isabella's hands trembled.

Every member of the Vance family required her careful flattery.

She had offended the matriarch before even entering the family!

She forced a bitter smile.

"Evelyn, why did you lie to us? You made us disrespect Mrs. Vance..."

Evelyn Ross immediately felt several suspicious gazes on her.

Linda snapped back to reality.

"You ungrateful girl! How dare you deceive Mrs. Vance! Kneel down and apologize now!"

Evelyn's eyes turned cold.

Trying to shift the blame? Not so easy.

Crack!

The elderly woman's teacup smashed into Linda.

Scalding tea splashed all over her.

Linda didn't dare to cry out.

"Rubbish!" Eleanor Vance roared. "She is my granddaughter-in-law!"

Linda endured the pain and shouted, "Mrs. Vance, you've been deceived! Her husband really is a lowlife thug!"

"Enough arguing."

Julian's mother spoke up.

"Alexander, is she really the wife you've been hiding?"

Evelyn looked at Alexander Vance.

She heard his icy reply.

"No."

What an ungrateful scoundrel!

Anger flashed in Evelyn's eyes.

Isabella breathed a sigh of relief, her face brightening.

Linda sneered.

"I told you it was Evelyn's doing!"

Evelyn was about to retort.

The man's deep voice rang out again.

"Grandmother mistook her identity. I asked Miss Ross to help take care of her."

Evelyn fell silent.

She had misjudged her "grandson."

Everyone was stunned.

No one had expected this turn of events.

Isabella lowered her head.

"Evelyn, why didn't you say so earlier? You made us misunderstand."

"Miss Isabella Ashcroft."

Alexander leaned back on the sofa, his presence imposing.

"Shifting blame when things go wrong. Is this how the Ashcroft family raises their daughters?"

Eleanor Vance was jolted to realization.

She flew into a rage.

"You think I'm a senile old fool?"

"You made a mistake, showed no remorse, and even framed someone else. How shameless!"

Eleanor turned to Julian.

"Call off the engagement! I do not accept this great-granddaughter-in-law!"

Isabella trembled from head to toe.

She couldn't lose the marriage she had worked so hard to secure!

She looked at Julian.

But he was staring blankly at Evelyn, still processing the news of her marriage.

Isabella clenched her fingers.

She walked over to Julian, tears falling.

"Julian, it's my fault... Even if the engagement is called off, I will still introduce you to that person as promised."

Julian snapped back to reality.

Isabella was still going to help him meet Dr. Nan...

Dr. Nan was pivotal in the new energy field!

He hurriedly said to Eleanor, "Great-grandmother, Isabella didn't know. Please forgive her. Both families have already exchanged the dowry. It wouldn't be good to call off the engagement now."

Eleanor gave him a deep look.

"You're grown up now. I can't control you anymore. Do as you please."

The matriarch understood her boundaries.

Her great-grandson's matters were for his parents to decide.

But...

Julian's mother smiled.

"Grandmother, haven't you forgotten the meeting gift?"

Eleanor lowered her eyelids.

"I'll transfer $2,500 to Julian."

Julian's mother's face stiffened.

"Didn't you say before you'd give two percent of the shares? I was worried they might be short on funds..."

The wealthiest person in the Vance family was Eleanor.

Born into a prominent New York family, she had a lavish dowry and controlled twenty percent of the Vance shares...

Julian's mother had long planned to secure a large sum through this wedding.

Something originally agreed upon, but the matriarch had changed her mind!

Eleanor smiled faintly.

"I am not satisfied with this great-granddaughter-in-law. I won't give it!"

Isabella's face turned deathly pale.

She felt utterly humiliated.

The Vance household had many servants.

Once these words spread, all of San Francisco would know the matriarch despised her!

High society valued face above all.

The matriarch had trampled her dignity underfoot...

The elderly woman paid no mind.

She took Evelyn's hand and led Alexander back to her own courtyard.

Claiming a urgent need for the restroom, she told Alexander to "properly" entertain his granddaughter-in-law.

Then she hurriedly left with her attendants.

In the blink of an eye, only the two of them remained in the courtyard.

The night was deep.

Evelyn stood side by side with Alexander Vance.

She noticed the man was a full head taller than her, about six foot four.

Didn't the old lady say he had a poor appetite?

As she pondered, Alexander suddenly looked at her, frowning deeply.

"Lacking iron?"

Evelyn met his gaze seriously.

"Doggy?"

Alexander's face darkened.

Grandmother had even told her that!

She was definitely up to something!

Grandmother latching onto her might have been accidental.

But this woman taking Grandmother in... perhaps it was because Grandmother revealed her identity.

Evelyn hadn't forgotten her main purpose.

"Remember you said you'd help? Can you come with me to City Hall to check my marital status?"

Alexander's expression turned grim.

He said sharply, "Fine! If it's not true, don't ever use Grandmother to pester me again!"

###Chapter 18

The elderly woman poked her head out the window. "Don't you dare bully my granddaughter-in-law, you rascal!"

Alexander Vance stiffened. "I wasn't."

Grandma Eleanor eyed Evelyn Ross suspiciously. "Did he really not bully you, dear?"

Evelyn glanced at the man, one eyebrow raised.

The tense line of his jaw betrayed his nervousness.

She smiled slightly. "He really didn't."

The man let out a barely audible sigh of relief.

Grandma beamed. "Good. Dinner is ready. Come inside."

The meal consisted of four simple dishes and a soup.

Grandma had little appetite. She set down her chopsticks after just a few bites.

She wearily took Evelyn's hand. "Let's go home, dear."

Evelyn spoke softly. "You should rest here tonight."

The small courtyard was fully equipped, more like a professional convalescent home.

But Grandma held her hand tightly. "Will you stay with me?"

Evelyn looked at Alexander.

She was truly worried about the old woman, but remembered his earlier suspicions...

Alexander was silent for a moment. "Fine."

His glance held a clear warning.

It plainly said this was only for Grandma's sake.

Evelyn rolled her eyes internally.

Grandma immediately brightened. "Wherever my granddaughter-in-law is, I'll be."

After dinner, Evelyn helped Grandma to her room to rest.

The elderly woman was exhausted. She fell into a deep sleep still holding Evelyn's hand.

But her sleep was restless.

The moment Evelyn tried to pull her hand away, Grandma murmured in her dreams. "Granddaughter-in-law..."

Evelyn had no choice but to remain seated by the bed.

Outside the window.

Alexander's expression was grave. "What's wrong with Grandma?"

The family doctor sighed. "It's unclear why Mrs. Vance has fixated on this young lady, but her anxiety is evident... She may feel her time is short and wants to ensure you have a companion."

Alexander paused.

Dark emotions churned in his eyes before settling into stillness.

He stood under the moonlight, seeming to merge with the night.

After a long moment, he suddenly turned and strode toward the main house.

—

Isabella Ashcroft endured the meal feeling intensely uncomfortable.

Her mistake had cost the eldest branch two percent of their shares.

Julian's mother, Margaret Evans, didn't hold back her scorn. "Even a love child like Evelyn Ross can please the old lady. Why can't you?"

Isabella bowed her head apologetically. "I misspoke, Auntie."

Margaret sneered. "I don't care how you do it. Get those shares back! Otherwise, forget about joining the Evans family!"

Isabella was trapped and couldn't explain.

She barely made it to the end of the dinner and was about to leave.

Alexander returned.

The man's tall frame carried an overwhelming presence.

His gaze swept over the dining table, his expression turning colder.

Grandma hadn't eaten anything, yet these people were feasting here?

Richard Evans frowned. "Is there something you need, Alexander?"

Alexander looked directly at Isabella. "Since the engagement isn't called off, you're considered part of the Evans family."

Everyone looked confused.

"Those in the Evans family must follow its rules. Disrespecting an elder warrants a night kneeling at the Family Shrine."

Two bodyguards stepped forward. "Miss Ashcroft, this way."

Isabella was completely stunned.

Kneel all night?

She looked at Julian, but he didn't dare say a word.

Gritting her teeth, she followed the bodyguards out.

As she left, she heard Julian ask, "Uncle, where is Evelyn Ross?"

The man's voice was cold. "She is my guest. She stays here tonight."

…

Isabella was led outside the Family Shrine.

The bodyguard stated impassively, "As you are not yet formally part of the family, Miss Ashcroft, you cannot enter the shrine. You will kneel outside."

The brick pavement was icy and hard.

Isabella shivered from the cold, her knees aching.

She thought about slacking off, but the two bodyguards watched her like statues.

Thinking of Evelyn sleeping comfortably somewhere...

Isabella seethed with hatred.

But it would all be worth it once she secured Dr. Nan.

—

Evelyn woke to find herself in bed.

A gray blanket was draped over her, clashing with the room's decor.

She didn't think much of it. After washing up, she joined Grandma for breakfast.

Fried sugar cakes were on the table.

Grandma's eyes lit up, and she reached for them.

Alexander frowned.

Grandma had high blood sugar. She shouldn't eat sweets.

He was considering how to stop her when Evelyn spoke calmly. "Grandma, you can't have that."

The usually stubborn and willful old lady obediently turned and placed the sugar cake in Evelyn's bowl.

She smiled ingratiatingly. "You have it, dear."

Alexander stared.

He looked deeply at Evelyn.

Evelyn ate the sugar cake calmly and addressed Alexander. "Remember to bring your ID and household register when we go to City Hall later."

Grandma was curious. "What are you going to City Hall for?"

Evelyn answered offhandedly. "Our Marriage Certificate is lost. We need a replacement."

Getting a divorce did require the Marriage Certificate.

To her surprise, Grandma immediately said, "It's not lost!"

She hurried into the bedroom and came out with two red booklets.

They were their Marriage Certificates.
###Chapter 19

She Had Always Known.

Evelyn stared at the red booklet in her hand.

She suddenly remembered the first time she met the old lady.

The lady had called her granddaughter-in-law.

So the old lady had known they were married all along.

She immediately pressed for answers. "Grandma, how did you get this?"

Eleanor Vance looked confused. "I... I can't remember."

Evelyn asked another question. "How did Mr. Vance and I get married?"

Eleanor continued to shake her head. "I don't remember."

Evelyn grew anxious. "Grandma, please try to think harder..."

"Enough!"

Alexander Vance's cold voice cut through the air.

His tall frame stepped between them.

His gaze was sharp. "Miss Ross, is that certificate real?"

"Of course."

Alexander's eyes darkened. "Next, you're going to say we don't need to go to City Hall."

So that was her plan.

First, suggest going to City Hall to set the stage.

Then have his grandmother produce a fake document.

If he hadn't seen how obedient his grandmother was to her, he might have believed it.

Evelyn felt the chill emanating from him and found it baffling. "We don't have to go today."

Divorce wasn't a simple trip to City Hall.

Two years of marriage involved asset division.

They needed to sign a divorce agreement first.

"No."

Alexander stared at her intently.

The girl merely raised a brow. "Suit yourself."

Was she that good of an actress, or was the certificate real?

Alexander suddenly felt uncertain.

He picked up the marriage certificate and strode out first. "I'll wait for you in the parking lot."

Evelyn gave Eleanor a reassuring smile and quickly followed.

The man walked fast.

She almost had to jog to keep up.

They reached the parking lot.

Linda Lowell was there supporting Isabella Ashcroft.

Isabella looked pale.

Her legs trembled.

She kept sneezing.

Linda's eyes lit up when she saw Evelyn. "Evelyn!"

She bypassed Alexander and walked over.

She spoke with a sense of entitlement. "You must help your sister!"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

She didn't understand why these two were here so early in the morning.

Did they not leave last night either?

She remained silent.

Linda chattered on incessantly.

"Evelyn, the Ashcrofts raised you despite your background. You should be grateful."

"Your sister has been so good to you. She took you to school and shared her clothes with you."

"You were weak at birth. You survived by taking your sister's milk."

"The Ashcrofts took us in because you and Isabella were born on the same day."

"You owe Isabella. You must be good to her for life."

Evelyn hadn't heard these words in ten years.

Thinking about how she believed these lies as a child.

She had worked like a slave for the Ashcrofts.

It was laughable.

The Ashcrofts did raise her.

But parents are supposed to provide for their children.

She went to school with Isabella.

But she had to carry Isabella's backpack and be her follower.

If Isabella felt slightly unwell, Evelyn would be beaten.

Her test scores could never surpass Isabella's.

She always had to be last.

She always wore Isabella's hand-me-downs.

They were always too short.

When Mrs. Ashcroft bought a new dress, Evelyn would wear it.

But it would immediately be taken off and given to Isabella.

As for being weak...

What baby wouldn't be weak after starving for four or five days?

...

The mockery in Evelyn's eyes grew deeper.

Linda remained oblivious and continued.

"Now is the time for repayment. Mrs. Vance listens to you. Tell her to return the shares to your sister!"

Evelyn lowered her eyes and smiled lightly. "You're right."

Linda looked pleased, thinking she had convinced her.

But the girl said softly, "I'm just a love child. Why would Mrs. Vance listen to me?"

Linda was stunned and wanted to say more.

But Evelyn had already strode past her.

She got directly into Alexander's car. "Let's go."

Alexander was speechless.

He looked at the girl in the back seat.

He wanted to say the driver hadn't arrived yet.

But he saw Linda trying to catch up...

Alexander got into the driver's seat.

He closed the door, fastened his seatbelt, and drove off.

The black Bentley drove smoothly.

Alexander glanced in the rearview mirror.

The girl was looking out the window.

Her profile was exquisite but aloof.

He looked away and continued driving.

The warning he intended to give remained unspoken.

They soon arrived at City Hall.

The car had just stopped when Thomas Watson knocked on the window.

Alexander rolled down the window.

Thomas said urgently, "Boss, the driver said you drove off alone. I rushed over. Is something wrong?"

"No."

Alexander was as concise as ever.

Thomas looked at City Hall. "Why are we here? Did Evelyn pester you into paranoia? Don't worry, I said she wouldn't see you again, or I would..."

"Eat shit on a live stream?"

A lazy female voice interrupted him.

Thomas turned stiffly.

He saw Evelyn in the back seat smiling faintly. "What's your live stream channel?"

...

Thomas was completely dumbfounded.

The three entered City Hall and approached a service window.

Thomas asked first, "Excuse me, can you get a marriage certificate without both parties present?"

The staff replied, "Definitely not."

"See?" Thomas looked at Evelyn mockingly.

He glanced at the marriage certificate in her hand and sneered. "You even prepared a fake one? I'm calling the police! I'll report you for forgery and harassment!"

Alexander handed the marriage certificate over. "Could you please check if this is authentic?"
###Chapter 20

The clerk took the marriage certificate, his eyes filled with doubt.

He looked down and entered the information into the computer.

Alexander Vance pressed his thin lips together tightly.

He felt his own behavior was absurd.

He was absolutely certain he was unmarried, yet he had inexplicably come to City Hall.

He had even canceled an important meeting.

The clerk soon looked up. "The document is authentic."

Alexander was stunned.

Thomas blurted out, "Impossible! Your system must have an error!"

The clerk was displeased. "Marriage is a serious matter. Both parties must verify their identities and sign affidavits!"

Affidavits...

Evelyn spoke up. "Can we see the affidavits?"

"Please retrieve the affidavits."

Alexander spoke at the same time.

Their eyes met for a moment, then quickly looked away.

The clerk stood up. "You two are really in sync. I'll go find them."

He returned quickly and handed them two affidavits.

Evelyn saw her own handwriting on one.

Thomas also exclaimed, "Boss, this is your handwriting!"

Evelyn looked toward the surveillance cameras. "Can we retrieve the footage from the registration day?"

The clerk shook his head. "Footage is only kept for six months."

...

The clerk repeatedly assured them the process was compliant.

The three of them walked out of City Hall.

Thomas asked blankly, "Boss, what do we do now?"

Alexander's eyes were deep and thoughtful.

Evelyn's peach blossom eyes lifted slightly.

"Divorce."

"Divorce."

They spoke in unison again.

Thomas was stunned. "Now? Do we need to negotiate terms?"

"My lawyer will contact you."

"My lawyer will handle the negotiations."

Alexander and Evelyn were synchronized once more.

They looked at each other.

Evelyn gave a light laugh. "How about we each draft an agreement? Our lawyers can communicate if there are issues?"

Thomas sneered. "Can you even afford a lawyer?"

Alexander, however, nodded in agreement.

Evelyn ignored Thomas and took out her phone. "Mr. Vance, let's connect on WhatsApp for easier communication."

Alexander took out his phone and showed his QR code.

Evelyn scanned it. The interface popped up with a contact note: Grandson.

Alexander's face instantly darkened.

Evelyn: "..."

She had forgotten she already had him on WhatsApp!

She blocked her phone screen and quickly changed the note to "Dog Boy."

Then she tossed out a "See you later," hailed a taxi, and left.

Thomas looked at Alexander. "Boss, you really are married, aren't you?"

Their synchronization just now was startling!

Alexander gave him a cold look. "Find the best divorce lawyer. Draft an agreement to protect the assets."

Thomas immediately nodded. "Yes!"

Then he had a sudden realization. "I get it. The marriage must have been that woman's scheme. Now that she can't have you, she wants money! No wonder she can afford a lawyer. A divorce settlement could net her a hefty legal fee!"

Thomas gritted his teeth in anger.

"Don't worry, we won't let her succeed!"

...

"I absolutely cannot let him take a single cent from me."

In the taxi, Evelyn called James. "Find me the best divorce lawyer."

"No problem." James was curious. "Boss, what's this about getting married? Did you and Alexander Vance both get amnesia?"

Evelyn said flatly, "Have you been reading too many novels?"

James: "This is too bizarre! Maybe City Hall will issue me a wife someday too."

Evelyn was silent for a moment. "There's one person who must know the truth."

"Who?"

"Eleanor Vance."

"... But her memory isn't clear, is it?"

"How is the progress on the clinical drug for Alzheimer's?" Evelyn frowned. "We have to speed it up. Otherwise, it will be too late."

"Too late for what?"

Evelyn lowered her eyes and didn't answer.

She arrived home.

Opening the door, the six-hundred-square-foot two-bedroom apartment felt empty.

The little old lady came in from the hallway carrying a suitcase. "Granddaughter-in-law, you're back! I've been waiting for you. I brought so many clothes~"

Evelyn was slightly taken aback, then suddenly smiled.

That same day, the most famous divorce law firm in San Francisco received two clients.

Both demanded "not a single cent of the assets be shared."

The terms were extremely harsh.

A junior lawyer complained, "I've never seen anything so ruthless. One male client, one female client... When it comes to being cutthroat, there's no gender divide!"

The lawyer was matter-of-fact. "One job, double the pay! Get the drafts ready to send to the clients!"

Top floor of the Vance Group.

Alexander had been busy since returning from City Hall.

He received a call from home saying his grandmother had gone to find her "granddaughter-in-law" again. It was already evening.

His grandmother needed daily health check-ups to monitor her condition...

He frowned, instinctively opened WhatsApp, found "Iron-Deficient," but didn't know what to say.

Ever since he found out "Iron-Deficient" was Evelyn, he felt awkward.

He scrolled through their chat history.

They had chatted like old friends before.

Especially when he helped her curse someone out... Wait, that day at the restaurant, the person she was cursing was probably him?!

Alexander's face darkened again.

His phone vibrated. "Iron-Deficient" sent the divorce agreement: [Mr. Vance, the asset division is my bottom line. Please review if it's acceptable.]

Asset division...

Did this woman really marry him for money?

Alexander had just opened the document when Thomas burst into the office:

"Boss, I found out about the marriage!"
###Chapter 21

Before Thomas could finish, Alexander opened the divorce agreement sent by Evelyn.

His eyes scanned the document, pausing at the asset division section.

His brow lifted slightly.

Thomas leaned in and snorted. "She actually specified you can't claim any of her assets? What assets? Is she worried you'll go after her credit line?"

Alexander looked up. "What did you find?"

"Boss, we discovered she only learned about the marriage when she went to get married a few days ago. This agreement seems genuine. She wants out quickly."

Thomas paused. "But that makes it more suspicious. Her records after leaving the Ashcroft family are mostly erased. Why would a normal person hide like that?"

Alexander tapped the desk. "So you think she's involved?"

"Definitely. But her motive... that will take more time."

Alexander spoke calmly. "Anything else?"

Thomas scratched his head. "The incident two years ago is hard to trace. We were moving constantly back then. The only lead might be if the old lady recovers her memory..."

Alexander shot him a look.

Thomas immediately bowed his head. "We failed you. We'll keep investigating."

Alexander printed two copies and stood. "Any contact with Researcher Nora?"

Rumor said Nora's Alzheimer's treatment was entering clinical trials. A breakthrough drug was imminent.

Alexander wanted it for his grandmother immediately.

Thomas lowered his head further. "Nora is elusive. Every biotech firm is watching. No one can find her..."

Feeling the chill from his boss, he quickly added, "I'll find her, boss. I promise!"

...

Evelyn had just finished dinner with Eleanor when her phone buzzed.

A message from Alexander: "Downstairs. Now."

She excused herself to take out the trash and walked to the black Bentley.

He handed her the agreement. "No objections. Sign."

He watched closely, expecting hesitation.

Instead, she signed quickly and looked up. "City Hall tomorrow?"

His eyes darkened. "In a hurry?"

She nodded. "Very."

Her company's IPO couldn't wait.

He studied her. "Fine."

She added, "Nine AM. Bring all documents. City Hall."

He was about to agree when his expression changed.

Eleanor stood outside the car, pale and trembling.

She clutched her chest. "You're getting divorced?"

Before anyone could answer, she collapsed.

"Grandmother!"

...

Outside the VIP suite at First Hospital.

The doctor was stern. "She's stable, but no more stress. Absolutely no upsetting her."

Evelyn remembered the frantic抢救 scene and shuddered.

She glanced at Alexander.

He appeared calm and composed.

But when the doctor said "stable," he loosened his tie, sat slowly, and his clasped hands trembled slightly.

A nurse called out from the room. "She's awake!"

Alexander rushed in.

Evelyn followed. The old woman's eyes were open.

She looked at Evelyn, voice hoarse. "Granddaughter-in-law, I dreamed you were divorcing... It wasn't real, was it?"

Evelyn stayed silent, but Alexander spoke. "It wasn't real."

"So no divorce?"

"No divorce."

"Give me your household register and marriage certificate."

"Okay."

Alexander agreed to everything.

The old woman looked at Evelyn. "Granddaughter-in-law, is he lying?"

Evelyn thought of the doctor's warning and whispered, "No."

Only then did Eleanor relax.

She needed to stay overnight for observation.

Evelyn and Alexander remained in the room.

Night deepened.

Evelyn curled on the sofa, checking her phone.

She was drafting a clinical report on an Alzheimer's drug. The document was in English. One technical problem remained unsolved.

It required extensive biomedical literature.

As she pondered, a new message arrived.

"Doggy": ["After grandmother recovers, then we divorce."]

She couldn't refuse.

The agreement was signed. Assets were clear. Her marital status didn't affect the IPO. Timing wasn't crucial.

Evelyn: ["Agreed."]

Alexander stared at the single word reply, eyes narrowing. ["Any other conditions?"]

Evelyn: ["Once she's fully recovered, we finalize it quickly."]

He frowned, suddenly finding her utterly inscrutable.

In the bed, the old woman glanced between Evelyn and Alexander.

They barely spoke.

This wouldn't do.

She suddenly declared, "Boy, I'm getting our granddaughter-in-law a job at Vance Group!"

Daily commutes together. Constant proximity. Feelings would develop naturally.

Alexander paused. "Fine."

He was unusually agreeable tonight.

She pondered. "What position should it be?"

An idea struck. "Granddaughter-in-law, start tomorrow with him!"

Evelyn blinked her peach-blossom eyes. "Okay."

It occurred to her—Vance Group's database might have the literature she needed!

Alexander had a meeting and left with Thomas.

In the hallway, Thomas couldn't hold back. "No wonder she signed so fast! She planned this! Let the old lady see on purpose. This divorce won't happen!"

He looked at Alexander. "Boss, she even seized a chance to join Vance Group. She's scheming! Why agree?"

"Keep your enemies closer. How else do we learn her game?"

His eyes glinted sharply.

For his grandmother, divorce was off the table.

As for the woman's innocence... Her actions at the company would reveal all!

Back in the room, Evelyn lay on the sofa.

She heard the old woman whisper, "Granddaughter-in-law, I'll tell you a secret..."

Evelyn leaned in immediately. "What secret?"

Had she remembered something else?
###Chapter 22

Evelyn's heart raced.

She desperately hoped the old lady could remember something important.

Something about how they first met.

Something about why she and Alexander were married.

"Grandma, what is the secret?"

Evelyn asked softly.

The old lady lowered her voice mysteriously. "That brat has a fatal weakness. I'll tell you now. If he dares to bully you, use this against him!"

Evelyn fell silent.

She didn't actually want to pry into others' privacy.

But the old lady was enthusiastic. "Don't be fooled by his cold face all day. He's actually afraid of cats."

Evelyn was stunned.

Deep in her memory, a little boy was also afraid of cats...

But Alexander, such a tall and powerful man, was afraid of cats?

The old lady asked again, "Granddaughter-in-law, do you like cats or dogs?"

"Dogs."

She used to like cats, but that boy didn't, so she changed her preference.

Evelyn quickly understood the old lady's intention.

The next day, on the top floor of the Vance Group.

"Woof! Woof!" A little Corgi wagged its tail and rubbed against Evelyn's leg.

Thomas looked helpless. "The job Madam arranged for you, Miss Evelyn, is to take care of this little dog."

Alexander frowned.

The old lady was getting older and acting willfully, which was understandable.

But bringing a dog to work... that was just absurd!

He ordered coldly, "Take her... and the dog to report to the New Energy R&D Department, Group One."

This had been his plan for a while.

The New Energy R&D Department wasn't involved in core business, but it concerned the group's future. It was perfect for testing her background.

Thomas immediately agreed.

Evelyn indifferently picked up the little dog and asked casually, "What's its name?"

Thomas: "It doesn't have one yet. Madam said you should decide."

Evelyn glanced at Alexander and suddenly smiled. "Then call it Mimi."

"Name a dog Mimi?"

"Is that not allowed?"

"...It's fine."

…

The New Energy R&D Department was located on the 38th floor of the Vance Group.

Inside the elevator.

Thomas explained, "Miss Evelyn, most of the R&D staff at Vance Group have PhDs. The lowest qualification is a master's degree from a prestigious university. You are the first undergraduate! It will be good for you to learn from the seniors here. This was specially arranged by the boss. Of course, you don't need to be too grateful. It's the boss's way of thanking you for making Madam happy~"

Evelyn: "..." It really wasn't necessary.

"Also, the boss doesn't want your relationship to be public. It avoids trouble later when you divorce."

Evelyn lazily looked up. "I was thinking the same thing."

Thomas: ?

This woman dared to look down on the boss?

He was about to say more when the elevator arrived.

The New Energy R&D Department was divided into two groups, one on each side of a central common area.

As soon as Evelyn stepped out of the elevator, she heard some male colleagues gossiping.

"I heard a beautiful intern is joining Group One today!"

"Finally, a girl in our monastery! This is great!"

Someone from Group Two sneered. "Group Two is about to hire Dr. Nan as a technical consultant. What's a mere undergraduate intern? Can a pretty face put food on the table?"

The Group One staff were about to retort when a cold voice cut in. "What's with all this chattering? A bunch of grown men. Don't you have work to do?"

The speaker was Jason Lawrence, the leader of Group One. He was in his late twenties, tall with sharp features, radiating arrogance.

Everyone immediately scattered.

He looked at Evelyn impatiently. "Thomas, an undergraduate with zero experience? And you dare to dump her on me?"

Thomas smiled apologetically. "It's the boss's order."

Jason was still displeased. He looked down at Evelyn. "I don't care what connections you have. In my group, you follow my rules! No bringing dogs to work next time!"

Evelyn couldn't be bothered to explain. "Fine."

Jason pointed her to an empty office.

After she entered, he said to Thomas, "Thomas, this is the last time. Of course, if my cousin can get Dr. Nan to come, you can dump as many connected people here as you want!"

As a relative of the Vance family, he got into the company on merit alone.

He hated nepotism the most!

…

Evelyn found her first day at a desk job novel.

No one assigned her any work, so she enjoyed the leisure.

She played with "Mimi" in the office for a while. The little thing soon curled up in a corner and fell asleep.

She got up quietly, closed the door, and planned to go to the Vance R&D database.

Only by developing a breakthrough treatment for Alzheimer's, helping the old lady recover, could she uncover the truth and solve the mystery of her marriage to Alexander!

Evelyn found a colleague who looked friendly and asked, "Excuse me, how do I get to the database?"

The male colleague blushed and was about to give directions when a cold voice interrupted. "Why do you need to go to the database?"

Evelyn turned. It was Jason.

She replied, "I want to learn."

Jason sneered. "You? What qualifications does an undergraduate have to enter the database? It contains cutting-edge international technology. Master the group's basic theories first!"

Evelyn: "…"

She took out her phone, ready to contact Alexander directly. Only a fool wouldn't use their privileges!

Before she could send the message, Jason snorted. "It's no use calling anyone. I'm not afraid of connected people! Stay put in my group. Don't put on airs and annoy people!"

The friendly male colleague suddenly spoke up. "Team Leader, she graduated from San Francisco University. Her major grades were very good…"

Jason immediately cut him off. "What use is book knowledge? Every new graduate starts from zero!"

He randomly picked up a stack of documents and threw them to Evelyn. "This is our group's new energy project. Ask her if she can understand it? Newcomers should know their place!"

Evelyn lowered her head and quickly skimmed through them…

Jason couldn't help but mock her. "Flipping through it so fast, you think you're Dr. Nan? If you can't understand it, get back to your office and stay there!"

It was said Dr. Nan had a photographic memory.

Evelyn looked up and retorted coldly, "Who says I can't understand it?"
###Chapter 23

Jason Lawrence froze. "You can understand this?"

Evelyn Ross was about to answer when the quiet male colleague next to her spoke. "Team Lead, she really can."

Jason frowned. "Do you two know each other?"

The colleague smiled shyly. "I'm Professor Lowell's graduate student at San Francisco University. Last year, when I was working on a project with him, she was also on the team..."

Evelyn studied him, then recognition dawned. "Daniel Clementson? Senior Daniel?"

Daniel nodded and explained to Jason. "She was the only undergraduate Professor Lowell ever made an exception to bring onto a project. Her reading speed is incredible, and she's very familiar with these basic frameworks."

Jason's face remained stern. "Understanding basics is nothing special. If she's so capable, let her solve the core technical challenges!"

Daniel quickly tried to smooth things over. "Team Lead, that's asking too much."

Jason snorted. "If you don't have the skill, just stay in your lane. Going to the archives to look up basic material? How embarrassing!"

He turned and walked away.

Daniel gave Evelyn an apologetic look. "The Team Lead has been struggling with a technical issue for days. His temper is short, but he's not a bad person. Please don't take it to heart."

Evelyn wasn't bothered.

She came to Vance Group to research. After asking for directions to the archives, she headed straight there.

The Vance Group archives occupied the entire 60th floor. It was a massive internal library, accessible only to employees.

It housed precious documents from various industries. Many rare scientific research materials, difficult to obtain elsewhere, could be found here.

Evelyn's heart raced. She had a feeling she would find what she wanted here.

…

Top-floor office.

Alexander Vance was reviewing documents when his phone chimed.

Eleanor Vance had sent a WhatsApp message. [Remember to have lunch with your wife at noon. Send me a cute couple photo.]

Alexander frowned.

He was about to make an excuse about a meeting when the chat window flickered. His grandmother's display name changed to "Fragile Grandma Who Can't Handle Stress".

Alexander stared at the new nickname for a long moment. Finally, he sighed and pressed the call button.

Thomas Watson entered. "Boss?"

"Prepare two lunches and bring them up."

"Yes, sir."

Alexander messaged "Ferrum" on WhatsApp, asking her to come to the top floor for lunch.

But the lunch sat on the table for a long time with no reply.

Alexander stood up. "Where is she?"

Thomas replied, "Miss Evelyn is in the archives."

Alexander took the elevator to the 60th floor. He weaved through the bookshelves, searching for her.

Soon, he found her... and Julian Evans standing right in front of her.

…

Evelyn was completely immersed in the books. She didn't notice her phone vibrating.

This archive contained the most cutting-edge technical literature. It was a treasure trove.

She lingered among the shelves, absorbed. Stepping back, she accidentally bumped into someone.

"Sorry."

She turned and met Julian's complicated gaze.

Julian stared at her intently. "Evelyn Ross, you're sucking up to Great-Grandma. It's not to get close to Uncle, is it?"

Evelyn's expression turned cold. "None of your business."

Julian pressed on. "Uncle never gets close to women. Don't think your pretty face will attract him. He's not that shallow! He just gave you some meaningless job to keep you quiet, right?"

Evelyn scoffed. "Are you saying you chased me for so long back then because you are shallow?"

Julian was speechless.

Evelyn looked calmly at the man before her.

He was once her only friend in university.

She had seriously considered his pursuit. She had even looked forward to the surprise he promised at graduation.

She never thought it would come to this.

She sighed softly. "Julian Evans, can't we just part amicably?"

"No!"

Julian's eyes reddened. "I know you hate me for proposing to Isabella Ashcroft. But elite marriages involve too many factors! I fought for you. My mother even agreed to me dating a commoner. But your status... I could never marry a love child! How would I hold my head up in the Vance family? I'd be the laughingstock of all San Francisco!"

Evelyn watched him.

She used to feel insecure about her status as a child.

But the day she left the Ashcroft family, she understood. Self-respect is what matters.

She looked away. "Thanks for not marrying me."

She tried to leave, but Julian blocked her path. "I heard Jason Lawrence is giving you a hard time in R&D Team One. I can transfer you to Team Two. I'll have my best person mentor you... With your intelligence, you'll be independent soon. You won't have to work odd jobs everywhere..."

Evelyn cut him off. "What's the condition?"

"Divorce him. Be with me."

Julian couldn't stand the thought of another man having her!

Just thinking about it drove him insane!

Evelyn said coldly, "You're not worthy!"

"Why? Do you love that thug so much? Want to bet? If I offer him money, or have someone scare him, he'll abandon you immediately! What does he have that I don't?"

Julian was furious. He grabbed Evelyn's wrist and pinned her against a bookshelf.

His eyes were bloodshot. "Or are all women like you? Once you sleep with someone, you can't let go? Then let's see who's better, him or me!"

He leaned in, trying to force a kiss.

At the end of the aisle, Alexander watched everything.

Seeing Julian's失控 behavior, his face darkened. He was about to step forward when—

The slender figure suddenly raised her knee, striking Julian hard between the legs!

Julian grunted in pain and stumbled back. She didn't hesitate and slapped him across the face!

"Slap!"

Evelyn was still angry. She glared coldly at Julian.

"I didn't want to crush you. But since you insist on comparing, I'll tell you. My husband is taller than you, handsomer than you, and his stamina is infinitely better than yours. Unlike you, who can't even push me away. Maybe you should hit the gym more."

Evelyn turned to leave but collided with Alexander's gaze from around the corner.

Evelyn was speechless.

Alexander was speechless.

Julian was bent over in pain. He shouted furiously, "What good is his stamina? I can make your life hell at the company anytime! Your husband can't do anything about it!"

Before he finished, a deep male voice rang out. "Who says he can't?"
###Chapter 24

Great stamina!

Several pairs of eyes turned toward the speaker.

Jason Lawrence stood there.

He had heavy dark circles under his eyes and an impatient expression.

Julian Evans was stunned. "You're her husband?"

"Of course not." Jason scoffed. "If her husband knew you were bullying her here, he would have already taken action! How could he just stand by and watch?"

Julian fell silent.

He stood up, enduring the pain, his gaze cold. "I want to transfer her to my team."

"No." Jason's tone was firm. "She's my person. You don't get to interfere."

He was still his usual arrogant self, but Evelyn found him surprisingly agreeable at this moment.

Julian was puzzled. "Don't you hate people who get by on connections the most?"

Jason sneered. "What right do you have to call her that? You, an undergraduate, became a team leader. Aren't you the biggest connection case of all?"

Julian's face darkened. "I am the eldest grandson of the Vance family!"

"Oh, the number one connection case at Vance Group."

"..."

Julian took a deep breath and suddenly said, "Dr. Nan is about to join my team soon. Jason, don't you admire him? If you stay out of this, I can introduce you."

Jason fell silent.

Who in the field of new energy research didn't admire Dr. Nan?

Jason had studied every one of Dr. Nan's papers repeatedly, each time feeling more amazed.

He had always wanted to find a way to meet Dr. Nan.

Julian was actually using this to threaten him...

Jason hesitated for a second out of respect for Dr. Nan, then said resolutely, "No!"

He looked at Evelyn. "Stop embarrassing yourself here. Let's go!"

Evelyn glanced at the spot where Alexander Vance had been standing and realized the man had left at some point.

She secretly breathed a sigh of relief.

Thank goodness he was gone. Otherwise, that "great stamina" comment would have been way too awkward.

She quietly followed Jason, and the two left the archives.

Jason kept a stern face. "Because of you, I missed my chance to meet Dr. Nan!"

Evelyn, however, smiled slightly. "There will be other chances."

Jason scolded her again. "You're an undergraduate. Shouldn't you be staying in the office instead of coming here to humiliate yourself?"

Evelyn didn't argue.

Daniel was right. The team leader had a sharp tongue but a kind heart.

Julian, left behind, looked furious.

He clenched his fists tightly.

One day, he would make Evelyn beg him on her knees!

Once he had recovered, he called Isabella. "Didn't you say you were having lunch with Dr. Nan tomorrow afternoon? Take me with you!"

…

Evelyn and Jason returned to Team One. Only then did she see the WhatsApp message.

Using lunch as an excuse, she went to the top-floor office.

Alexander was wearing a white shirt and black suit, looking refined and noble. Without looking up, he pointed to the dining table. "You can start first."

Evelyn felt a little guilty.

He seemed like this... He probably hadn't heard that comment, right?

Just as she was thinking that, she heard the sound of a photo being taken.

Alexander calmly finished taking the picture and sent it to his "frail grandmother who couldn't handle any shocks." Then he walked over.

Evelyn knew what the photo was for and didn't ask. She just looked at the dining table, making small talk. "Your lunch looks very lavish, Mr. Vance."

"Hmm."

The man took off his jacket, rolled up his shirt sleeves, and sat down opposite her. He elegantly picked up his chopsticks. "Good nutrition leads to good stamina."

Evelyn:!!!

Her cheeks felt warm. Her gaze swept over his powerful forearms. She immediately packed a portion of the food.

"Mr. Vance, I just remembered I haven't fed my cat yet. I should go."

She picked up the food container. "Thank you for lunch."

Alexander's expression froze.

Evelyn left in a good mood.

…

R&D Team One.

Jason ate his takeout without tasting it, replaying the earlier scene in his mind.

He had thought that undergraduate was overly ambitious. Turns out, she had been wronged by Julian and just wanted to work hard to succeed?

Thinking about Julian's demands, they were truly insulting.

No wonder she was so desperate to learn.

Unfortunately, haste makes waste. Without a solid foundation, no amount of advanced materials would make sense.

He stood up and walked over to Daniel. He said quietly, "Give her some work this afternoon. Guide her a little."

Daniel was startled. "Ah, okay."

Jason looked embarrassed. He said fiercely, "So she doesn't run around causing trouble!"

Daniel: "..."

So when Evelyn tried to go to the archives that afternoon, Daniel stopped her. "The team leader said we don't keep idle people here. You need to finish some work first."

He assigned her some basic organizational tasks.

Without a clear research framework, organizing was tedious.

But a newcomer could learn a lot from it.

Daniel thought it would take at least a month to figure it all out.

Unexpectedly, Evelyn came to hand in her work just half an hour later.

Daniel: "..."

He checked it and found she had done it even better than they could!

Evelyn asked humbly, "Is there any other work? If not, I'll go to the archives."

Daniel wasn't sure what to assign her next.

He still had a lot he hadn't figured out himself.

Gritting his teeth, he handed over the task he was currently working on.

He had been stuck on this module for a week. It was a bit difficult... but this junior seemed to have a solid foundation?

Let her hit a wall, and she'll settle down and learn properly.

An hour later, Evelyn came to hand in her work again.

"Finished?"

Daniel was dumbfounded. Looking at the email on his computer, he realized she had easily solved the technical problem that had stumped him!

Daniel went to find Jason.

Jason pondered. "You're overcomplicating it. She just got lucky and solved it another way. Give her something even more difficult."

Daniel: "...Anything more difficult would be a core problem!"

This particular problem had stumped Jason for a month!

Jason: "Give it to her!"

It was just to keep her from running around anyway. Let her see what a real research problem looks like!

So Daniel handed the core problem that had stumped the entire Team One to Evelyn...
###Chapter 25

The technical core issue from R&D Team One was indeed challenging.

Evelyn spent two full hours resolving it.

She emailed the solution to Daniel and went to find him.

Daniel was packing his things, a relaxed smile on his face. "Time to clock out!"

Friday evening was the most anticipated moment for every office worker.

Finally, the weekend rest was within reach.

Daniel looked up and saw Evelyn. "How's the work coming along, junior?"

Evelyn was about to answer.

"Actually, no rush!" Daniel cut her off excitedly. "Just enjoy the weekend first. We can deal with anything next week."

Evelyn quietly swallowed her words.

She didn't work a regular office job often, but she knew discussing work right before Friday下班 wasn't appropriate.

Better not disturb her senior's weekend.

The task she completed was probably just a minor issue for the R&D department.

Daniel slung his bag over his shoulder and headed out. He paused beside Evelyn. "Don't be discouraged, junior. You're already very impressive."

Evelyn was a little puzzled.

She hadn't needed to do this work originally. She only did it because Jason had helped her today.

Since the work was done, she could head to the archives when she came in next week.

Thinking this, Evelyn returned to the office, picked up her "kitten," and left the company.

...

Top floor.

Alexander finished the last document and checked the time. He prepared to go home.

Thomas asked, "Boss, which residence?"

Alexander paused, realizing he hadn't asked where his grandmother was.

He called Eleanor. "Where are you... whose house?"

Eleanor's answer was unexpected. "At the hospital."

Alexander frowned. "Didn't the doctor say you could be discharged?"

"My chest started hurting again this afternoon. I want to stay a few more days. Are you coming to keep me company tonight, youguy?"

"..."

"It's fine if you don't. This old lady is all alone. My son doesn't care, my grandson doesn't love me. I'll just lie here pitifully in the hospital. No one to even fetch me a glass of water in the middle of the night, sigh~"

"...I'm on my way."

"That's more like it. Oh, and I already spoke with my granddaughter-in-law. She's coming to keep me company too. Come together, okay~"

After hanging up, Alexander rubbed his temples.

He understood his grandmother's little scheme. Whether she returned to the Vance estate or went to Evelyn's place, they would be separated at night. Staying at the hospital for company meant more time together.

But she had a boyfriend...

Alexander pressed his lips together, wanting to maintain distance.

But thinking of his grandmother's health, he suppressed his displeasure and sent her a WhatsApp: [I'll pick you up after work to go to the hospital.]

The reply came quickly: [No need. I'm already in a taxi on the way.]

She knew how to avoid suspicion.

She didn't presumptuously cling to him just because his grandmother favored her...

Alexander should have been pleased, but his inexplicable displeasure deepened.

...

Evelyn hadn't overthought it.

She just didn't want to be overly involved with Alexander before the truth about their marriage was cleared up.

This bizarre marriage was undoubtedly trouble stirred up by Alexander's side. Who would pay attention to an insignificant person like her?

Holding her little Shiba Inu, she walked straight into the inpatient building towards the VIP suites.

Not far away.

Sophia had just finished her follow-up examination.

Isabella supported her arm. Both watched Evelyn's retreating figure.

Sophia said hesitantly, "Was that Evelyn? Why is she at the hospital? Is she sick?"

Isabella felt a surge of anger.

Eleanor's hospitalization couldn't be hidden from the Vance family.

But the old woman was stubborn and refused all visitors. Even Isabella had been turned away at the door today without seeing her.

Evelyn coming now was definitely to accompany that old thing!

Her eyes flickered. "She must be visiting Mrs. Vance... Evelyn is so filial."

Hearing this, the nanny Nancy was displeased. She frowned, "Filial? I haven't seen her come visit Madam!"

Nancy was in her fifties, over a decade older than Sophia. She had taken care of Sophia personally for years, never marrying.

Sophia treated her like an older sister. Nancy held a high position in the Ashcroft family, and even Isabella was polite to her.

Sophia looked wistful. "Back at the Ashcroft residence, Evelyn had a hard life..."

Nancy immediately said, "Madam, you still speak up for her! Although I can't stand Linda's petty ways, she was right about one thing. Miss Evelyn is an ungrateful person! It's a waste that you often think of her and worry about her! She's probably forgotten all about you!"

Isabella added, "I heard Mr. Vance found her a job at the Vance Group... so she's repaying Mrs. Vance now?"

Nancy scoffed. "Madam raised her! When Linda neglected her as a child, it was Madam who fed her. Later, Madam sent her to school. The grace of upbringing is greater than heaven, yet I don't see her repaying Madam!"

Sophia stopped them. "Alright, that's enough."

But Isabella continued, "Mom has a kind heart. She never asked for回报 when helping others back then. But some people repay a drop of water with a flowing spring. Like Dr. Nan. Mom only sponsored a little money for his education. How much has he helped our family over the years? Three years ago, when the company was almost bankrupt, Dr. Nan directly gave us a technical patent that turned the crisis around..."

Nancy nodded. "Compared to that, Dr. Nan is much better than Evelyn! I wonder what Dr. Nan looks like. But one's heart is reflected in their face. He must be impressive!"

Isabella pretended curiosity. "I want to know what kind of person Dr. Nan is too, Mom. Take me with you tomorrow!"

Sophia tapped her nose with a smile. "Fine, I'll take you."

As they spoke, they went downstairs to the parking lot and left.

This scene was witnessed by Evelyn, who had just entered the VIP suite and sat on the couch by the bed.

Her gaze followed Sophia.

Sophia's face was unnaturally pale. Her figure was thin but upright, carrying a unique grace.

Isabella was saying something to her. Sophia nodded helplessly.

The mother and daughter were intimate.

Evelyn reluctantly withdrew her gaze only after the car drove away.

Turning back, she found the little old lady standing behind her. "Granddaughter-in-law, who was that? She looks very familiar."

Evelyn's expression softened. She was about to speak.

"You look so much alike..." The little old lady's eyes lit up. "I remember! She's your mother!"
###Chapter 26

She is your mother!

Similar?

Evelyn Ross was momentarily stunned.

She and Sophia Ashcroft both had oval faces, but their features were completely different.

Sophia had sharp, phoenix-like eyes, while Evelyn's were captivating peach blossom eyes.

If there was any resemblance, it was their similarly cool, detached aura.

...That made sense.

After leaving the Ashcroft family, whenever she faced difficulties, she would wonder what Sophia would do.

Over time, she had unconsciously adopted some of Sophia's demeanor.

Evelyn looked at the elderly lady. "Grandma, she is not my mother."

The old lady stubbornly shook her head. "She is! How can you not acknowledge your own mother, granddaughter-in-law?"

Evelyn could only smile helplessly.

Grandma Eleanor was confused again.

Fortunately, Alexander Vance appeared at the door with their dinner, successfully diverting the old lady's attention.

The meal was lavish.

There were light side dishes paired with exquisite porridge, along with three servings of fresh fruit.

Grandma Eleanor suddenly frowned. "Granddaughter-in-law can't eat mango! She's allergic!"

Evelyn's head snapped up.

She looked at the old lady in surprise. "Grandma, how did you know that?"

She used to be allergic to mangoes, but the allergy disappeared two years ago after her constitution improved.

She had never mentioned this to Grandma Eleanor!

The old lady smiled. "You told me before. You said you break out in red rashes all over your face if you eat mango..."

Evelyn's expression turned serious. "When was 'before'?"

Grandma Eleanor looked confused. "Before the wedding, I think... Yes, you told me before the wedding!"

But Evelyn was certain she hadn't even known the old lady back then.

Her memory had never had any gaps.

She pressed urgently, "What else do you remember? Like how Mr. Vance and I got our marriage certificate?"

The old lady shook her head. "I can't remember..."

It seemed specific triggers were needed for Grandma Eleanor to recover fragments of her memory.

As Evelyn was lost in thought, she suddenly felt an intense gaze.

Turning her head, she met Alexander Vance's deep, penetrating eyes.

The man's expression was icy cold. He had clearly understood the old lady's words and was suspecting her of having ulterior motives again!

It was utterly impossible to defend herself.

Evelyn picked up the Shiba Inu. "Grandma, you two eat first. I'll take the dog for a walk."

She walked out of the hospital room and stopped at the end of the corridor.

Leaning against the wall with the dog in her arms, her peach blossom eyes glanced toward the room.

Sure enough, a tall figure soon followed her out.

Alexander Vance approached slowly, his deep voice resonating. "Did you have something to say, Miss Ross?"

Evelyn pressed her lips together. "I thought you might require an explanation."

"And what explanation did you wish to provide, Miss Ross?"

Evelyn said earnestly, "I genuinely have no knowledge of what Grandma mentioned about before the wedding."

Even she found her own words unbelievable. A mango allergy was private information the old lady couldn't possibly fabricate.

She expected the man to question her, but he only responded indifferently, "Oh."

Evelyn was taken aback. "You believe me, Mr. Vance?"

"Not sure."

Evelyn: ?

Alexander suddenly took a step closer.

The height difference meant she was enveloped in his shadow.

The man spoke slowly. "Miss Ross, whatever your goals might be, as long as you keep Grandma happy, I will ensure your safety."

"..."

Evelyn instantly understood his stance.

He didn't care about the truth. He wasn't concerned about whether she was deceiving him.

He was powerful enough to control the city of San Francisco, unafraid of any schemes...

His only concern was Grandma Eleanor.

Even if she truly had ulterior motives, as long as the old lady was happy, he would turn a blind eye...

"Of course."

The man's demeanor suddenly turned sharp and imposing. "But if you harm Grandma, I will make sure you cannot take a single step freely in San Francisco."

Evelyn laughed coldly. "Haven't you already done that, Mr. Vance?"

Alexander frowned and was about to speak when a hoarse voice interrupted.

"Evelyn Ross?"

Evelyn turned to see Professor Lowell.

He seemed to have a cold, his voice nasal, as he hurried over. "I was just about to ask how your job search is going? Have you cleared up the misunderstanding with Mr. Vance? Mr. Vance is so ungentlemanly, holding a grudge against a young woman. Revoking your guaranteed graduate spot was bad enough, but he also threatened to make your life in San Francisco impossible?"

Alexander Vance: ??

Evelyn glanced at Alexander with a mocking smile. "Don't worry, Professor. The misunderstanding has been resolved. Mr. Vance is very kind. He arranged a job for me and even promised to protect me~"

Her sarcastic tone made Alexander's face darken instantly.

Professor Lowell sighed in relief, finally noticing Alexander. Seeing them standing close together, he recalled the conversation with Isabella and her mother last time and instinctively asked, "Is this your... hoodlum husband?"

Alexander Vance: "..." His face turned as dark as a pot.

Evelyn's lips curved upward. "You could say that."

Professor Lowell looked at Alexander with grave concern. "Young man, you're strong and handsome. You should find a proper job and work hard!"

"..."

...

Ashcroft Family Dining Room.

The lunch atmosphere was heavy.

Sophia Ashcroft had little appetite and looked unwell.

William Ashcroft immediately asked with concern, "Sophia, what's wrong? Are you feeling unwell?"

Sophia waved her hand dismissively.

Isabella Ashcroft said softly, "Dad, we ran into Evelyn at the hospital today. Mom might be worried about her."

William frowned. "Why worry about her?"

Isabella sighed lightly. "She must hate me for processing her withdrawal and forcing her to study abroad... I'm afraid she might say something inappropriate in front of Mrs. Vance. Mr. Vance might take his anger out on me..."

William snorted coldly. "She's the one who offended Mr. Vance. What does it have to do with you? Even if she was lucky enough to save Mrs. Vance, that's between them. Speaking of which, Mr. Vance specifically instructed us to raise our daughter properly!"

Isabella's face paled.

That incident where she falsely relayed Mr. Vance's instructions... Surely it was all in the past now?

She had always been acutely aware that her father, William Ashcroft, was only good to her because she was Sophia Ashcroft's daughter!

If he found out she had displeased Mr. Vance... The consequences would be unthinkable!

Just then, William's phone suddenly rang.

He answered the call. "Assistant Watson?! What brings your call? Does Mr. Vance have any instructions?"

Isabella's heart tightened upon hearing this!!

Please don't let it be what she feared! She was utterly panicked!
###Chapter 27

Thomas Watson's voice came through the phone.
"Mr. Ashcroft, the boss wants to ask Miss Isabella when exactly he said he wanted Evelyn Ross to disappear from San Francisco?"
"He himself has no memory of saying such a thing."

William Ashcroft froze, staring at Isabella in disbelief.

Isabella's face instantly turned deathly pale.

William spent a long time apologizing profusely into the phone.
He was only met with a final, cold remark.
"The Ashcroft family should really learn to discipline their daughter properly."
After the call ended, he glared furiously at Isabella.

Isabella bowed her head, silent tears falling.
"I saw Evelyn pestering Mr. Vance. I was afraid she would ruin my engagement with Julian."
"I was even more afraid the Vance family would look down on me… Dad, I didn't mean to."

William was furious.
"No matter what, you cannot falsely use Mr. Vance's name!"

Isabella clenched her fists, her eyes shifting nervously.
She sobbed.
"Dad, Evelyn is still your daughter. I was afraid you wouldn't have the heart to scold her…"

"Foolish!"

William took the bait, his anger redirected.
"How many times must I say it? I only have one daughter, and that's you!"
"What is Evelyn Ross? How could she ever compare to you?"

Sophia Ashcroft spoke up disapprovingly.
"William, don't say that. Evelyn is still your daughter!"

William immediately turned to her.
"Sophia, after all these years, don't you understand me?"
"That year, I was tricked by Linda Lowell. I wronged you and our daughter."
"I will never acknowledge her or her child!"
"In my heart, only you and our daughter are my family."

Linda Lowell, standing to the side disguised as a maid, clenched her fists in bitter resentment.

She looked at William with pained eyes.
This was the man she deeply loved, her childhood friend from next door.
Yet his eyes and heart only held Nán Jìngshū!

That year, after she became pregnant, she went to him with the positive test.
She said she was willing to be his mistress.
He mercilessly threw her out, saying he would never betray his wife…

Left with no options, Linda could only go to Sophia.
She threatened suicide to secure a place in the Ashcroft household.

All these years, William never showed her any kindness.
He treated Evelyn as if she were invisible…

Fortunately… she had switched the two babies at birth!

Linda lowered her head, forcing down a smirk.
William would never know.
The daughter he constantly claimed was his and Sophia's, the one he doted on all these years, Isabella, was actually her daughter!

As for Evelyn… she deserved the neglect! She deserved to be suppressed!

Sophia, however, shook her head stubbornly.
"William, Isabella was wrong. She lied and made us misunderstand Evelyn."
"Our family should apologize to Evelyn."

William frowned.
"Apologize? How could you, my wife, and my daughter apologize to Linda Lowell's child?"

Linda also quickly interjected.
"No need, no need. The Ashcroft family raised her. That's a tremendous grace in itself!"
"Besides, she was shamelessly chasing Mr. Vance. She was in the wrong."
"The Ashcroft family teaching her a lesson is only right. It's her good fortune…"

Sophia looked at them and sighed softly, offering no further argument.

After dinner, when Isabella escorted her back to her room, Sophia looked at her.
"If you have time tomorrow, I will take you to apologize to Evelyn in person."

Isabella's face stiffened. She thought she'd escaped her father, but not her mother…
She said with feigned grievance, "Okay."

Seeing her like this, Sophia sighed again.
"Didn't you want to bring Julian to meet Dr. Nan? We can go together tomorrow."

Isabella's eyes immediately lit up. She linked her arm with Sophia's.
"Okay, Mom. You're the best to me."

After offending Eleanor Vance, she had lost two percent of the shares in Vance Group.
She urgently needed to prove her worth in front of the Vance family, terrified Julian would call off the engagement.

Sophia patted her head helplessly.

After Isabella left happily, Sophia looked at Nancy.
"Nancy, my health has been poor these years. I haven't had the energy to properly guide Isabella. I've failed as a mother… cough cough cough…"

Nancy immediately said, "Madam, you're overthinking it! Miss Isabella is wonderful, filial and considerate."
"Even if she tells a lie occasionally, she can change later. It's not a major flaw."

Sophia replied, "Evelyn never lies."

She was wracked by another bout of violent coughing.

Nancy quickly helped her to the bed.
"You, Madam, think too much… that's why your illness hasn't improved all these years…"

A bitter expression crossed Sophia's features.

…

The next day.

Evelyn Ross woke up feeling slightly nervous.
It had been ten years since she last saw Sophia Ashcroft.
Would she be happy or angry to see her?

Around noon, she decided to go home to shower and change clothes.

The little old lady asked Alexander Vance to take her.

They went downstairs, and Thomas Watson approached.
"Boss."

Alexander nodded. Thomas handed a piece of paper to Evelyn.
"Miss Ross, please take a look."

Evelyn took it. It listed a row of renowned domestic professors specializing in new energy!
She looked at Thomas in confusion.
"What is this?"

Thomas lifted his chin.
"These are all professors more famous than Professor Lowell."
"You can choose one for your postgraduate studies."
"It's the boss's compensation for your revoked guaranteed admission."
"Miss Ross, meeting our boss, you've really turned misfortune into fortune."

Evelyn's mouth twitched.
"No, thank you."

Most of these professors had emailed her, seeking her professional advice.

"Then you want to continue with Professor Lowell for your postgraduate studies?"

"No."
