Thomas frowned.
"That's difficult. Dr. Nan is extremely sought-after right now."
"I heard several foreign universities have invited him."
"Miss Ross, that kind of genius professor only accepts genius students."
"I'm afraid you wouldn't catch his eye."
"Don't aim too high. Pick one of these professors first."
"You can always try for Dr. Nan again after your Master's?"

"…"

Evelyn gave him a look as if he were an idiot.
She spoke directly to Alexander.
"Mr. Vance, there's no need to see me off. I'll take a taxi back."

She left the hospital, hailed a cab, and left.

Thomas scratched his head.
"I heard Isabella wants to study under Dr. Nan."
"Is Miss Ross just trying to not lose to her? Insisting on Dr. Nan?"
"Isn't that overestimating herself?"

Alexander watched the direction she left, his handsome face expressionless, his deep eyes unfathomable.
He suddenly said, "Help her contact Dr. Nan."

"What?" Thomas was stunned.
Why was the boss being so nice to her!

Was he bewitched by her face?

…

Sophia brought Isabella and Julian Evans to the private dining room ten minutes early.

After getting out of the car, Julian looked around.
"Why meet here?"

Isabella was puzzled.
"Julian, what's wrong?"

Julian pressed his lips together.
"Nothing."

Isabella looked at Sophia.
"Mom, is Dr. Nan here yet?"

Sophia replied, "She's already in the private room."
###Chapter 28

The three of them entered the restaurant.

Julian Evans scanned the room but didn't find the person he was looking for.

He collected his thoughts and looked at Isabella. "Is Dr. Nan really coming to Vance Group?"

Isabella glanced at Sophia and lowered her voice. "Julian, go in and talk to Dr. Nan first. With enough sincerity, Dr. Nan will definitely be interested."

She turned to Sophia. "Mom, my bra strap is loose. Come help me fix it."

Sophia followed her toward the restroom.

Julian walked alone to the private room door.

His first assignment at Vance Group was in the R&D department. If he could persuade Dr. Nan to join, it would greatly boost his standing in the company.

He straightened his collar and pushed the door open.

A slender back came into view. A woman was facing away from the door, preparing tea.

The renowned Dr. Nan was a young woman?

Julian suppressed his surprise and spoke respectfully. "Dr. Nan, I'm Julian Evans from Vance Group. I've long admired your work."

The woman turned around slowly.

The moment he saw her face, Julian's pupils constricted sharply. "Evelyn Ross? What are you doing here?"

Evelyn had just poured tea for Sophia when she heard the door open.

Seeing Julian, she raised an eyebrow. "I should be asking you that."

She had clearly arranged to meet only Sophia...

Julian's face darkened. "I'm not here for you."

He looked around the room. "Where is Dr. Nan?"

Evelyn narrowed her eyes. "Who?"

Julian said impatiently, "The guest reserved this private room. Where did they go?"

Evelyn remained silent.

Footsteps sounded at the door as Sophia and Isabella entered.

Seeing Evelyn, they both stopped in their tracks.

Isabella's face instantly turned ugly.

Sophia's eyes reddened slightly. "Is that... Evelyn?"

Evelyn had left the Ashcroft family ten years ago. Sophia hadn't seen her since.

That little girl had now grown into an elegant young woman.

Warmth bloomed in Evelyn's chest.

Seeing her, Sophia showed neither awkwardness nor distance, only pure joy.

Perhaps she shouldn't have deliberately avoided her all these years.

She suppressed the moisture in her eyes and stepped forward with a smile. "How have you been, Madam?"

"Well... cough... I've been well." Sophia sat in the main seat, holding Evelyn's hand tightly. "I heard you got married?"

Evelyn nodded gently, saying no more.

Sophia looked wistful. She wanted to ask about her husband, but remembered William's attitude yesterday and swallowed her words.

A dark glint flashed in Isabella's eyes. "Evelyn, what are you doing here?"

Julian sneered. "She works here."

"Mom, let's focus on business first. We have an important guest today."

Isabella interrupted, turning to Evelyn. "We should invite you to join us, but since you're working, it might not be appropriate. We'll leave a generous tip later to help you out."

"Enough!" Sophia scolded Isabella, quickly looking at Evelyn.

Both were William's daughters, yet one was高高在上 while the other was a waitress. This must be painful for Evelyn.

Unexpectedly, Evelyn remained calm, seemingly unbothered by the status difference.

Sophia's eyes showed approval.

She said directly, "Evelyn, we're meeting Dr. Nan today. You study new energy too. Meeting them would be good for you!"

Isabella clenched her fist.

Dr. Nan was her bargaining chip. Why should she be introduced to Evelyn?

What if Dr. Nan was also captivated by that pretty face...

Her gaze swept across the table, settling on the cup of scalding tea.

She picked up the teacup and walked to Evelyn's side. "Evelyn, Mom wants me to apologize. Let me offer you tea instead of wine."

She loosened her fingers, splashing the scalding tea directly at Evelyn—

Evelyn had been prepared.

Isabella had always tried to outdo her since childhood. A sincere apology was unlikely.

Seeing the tea coming, she sidestepped, her elbow seemingly accidentally bumping the cup—

"Ah!"

The tea splashed onto Isabella's chest, making her scream.

Isabella endured the pain,委屈地说, "Evelyn, I know you dislike me, but how could you do this?"

Evelyn lowered her eyes, looking innocent. "Sorry, I wasn't paying attention."

Isabella's eyes welled with tears, on the verge of crying.

Julian怒said, "Evelyn, is this how you work as a waitress? Clumsy and hurting guests! Call your manager. I want to complain about you!"

Sophia hurriedly stopped him. "Enough, we're family..."

Isabella's eyes flickered. "Evelyn might have gotten too excited hearing about meeting Dr. Nan..."

Knowing Dr. Nan... she would be harder to control!

This thought flashed through Julian's mind. He immediately said, "Aunt Sophia, Isabella is my fiancée. I can't let her be wronged."

He glared at Evelyn. "Someone like you deserves to meet Dr. Nan? Get out now, or I'll make you leave!"

Sophia frowned, about to speak, but Isabella挽住her arm. "Mom, Julian is angry. Let Evelyn leave first to avoid more conflict."

Sophia sighed. "Let me send a message to ask."

"No need."

Evelyn smiled faintly. "They're already here."
###Chapter 29

They are mother and daughter.

These words made all three people in the room look at her simultaneously.

Evelyn saw she was coughing severely and quickly handed her a cup of warm water.

…

Alexander had dinner with his grandmother and was handling documents outside the suite.

Sitting for a long time made him somewhat restless. He stood up and walked to the window.

He suddenly caught sight of a familiar figure downstairs.

A group of people were hurriedly escorting a hospital bed towards the inpatient ward.

Evelyn followed far behind, like a discarded little fox.

Alexander raised an eyebrow and stood up to walk out.

In the lobby on the first floor, Isabella and Julian were handling the admission procedures.

The doctor apologized. "All beds are full now. We can only temporarily place her in the hallway. We'll move her to a room as soon as one becomes available."

Julian pressed further. "Are there no VIP suites available? Money is not an issue."

Isabella's eyes welled up with tears. "Julian, what should we do?"

Julian walked aside to make phone calls.

His gaze shifted to Evelyn.

The girl's face was calm, but her eyes were fixed intently on Mrs. Ashcroft on the hospital bed. She was小心翼翼, as if afraid of disturbing the patient.

"...Yes."

…

Evelyn stayed by the hospital bed. Seeing Julian make call after call with a grim expression, she knew she couldn't rely on him.

Mrs. Ashcroft was not in critical condition. She was just weak from long-term insomnia and needed quiet rest. The hallway was too noisy!

She had no acquaintances at San Francisco General... Would she have to ask that person for help?

The front desk of the inpatient ward received a call and quickly waved. "A VIP suite just became available!"

Isabella said joyfully, "Julian, you did it!"

Julian looked puzzled.

His friends had all said they needed time earlier. Could someone have actually helped just in time?

He didn't think too much about it and tacitly accepted the credit.

Everyone escorted Mrs. Ashcroft into the room.

Evelyn followed silently. Mrs. Ashcroft on the bed was much thinner than ten years ago, her face pale...

She wanted to follow into the room but was stopped by Isabella at the door. "Evelyn, my mother needs rest. Please leave."

Slam!

The door closed shut.

Evelyn stood frozen in place.

Though worried about Mrs. Ashcroft's condition, she understood she had no right to step through that door.

…

Evelyn decided to wait until Mrs. Ashcroft woke up before leaving, so she sat down on a bench in the hallway.

Soon, hurried footsteps approached. William rushed into the room, his face full of anxiety.

After confirming his wife was okay, he pulled Isabella out into the hallway.

William frowned and asked, "What happened? How did she suddenly collapse?"

Isabella looked at Evelyn sitting nearby and said directly, "Evelyn insisted on knowing about Dr. Nan. She argued with Julian and upset Mother..."

William immediately glared at Evelyn. "You孽种!"

He raised his hand to strike her!

...Perhaps she really shouldn't have appeared before Mrs. Ashcroft!

Slap!

The expected pain did not come. She opened her eyes to see a tall figure blocking the way.

Alexander stood there in his impeccable suit, god-like.

He was there, holding up the sky for her.

She was awake!

William and Isabella hurried into the room.

In the photo were her and Mrs. Ashcroft!!
###Chapter 30

Nora's Miracle Cure!

Evelyn stared at the photo.

In it, she and Sophia stood by the sea.

Both wore white chiffon dresses.

She was in front, Sophia behind her.

They were smiling brightly and warmly.

They looked like a close mother and daughter pair.

But she never owned a white chiffon dress...

She hadn't seen Sophia since leaving the Ashcroft family in middle school.

How could there be a photo together?

She took the old lady's phone.

She zoomed in on the photo for a closer look.

She quickly found the flaw!

The photo was a composite.

The technique was crude and clumsy.

It looked like a random mother-daughter photo was taken from online.

Her and Sophia's faces were photoshopped onto it...

She looked at the old lady. "When was this sent to you?"

The old lady shook her head blankly. "I can't remember."

"How was it sent? Email? Or text message?"

The old lady continued shaking her head. "I can't recall."

Evelyn sighed.

The old lady occasionally said shocking things.

But she could never explain the context.

It was frustrating.

But the old lady was unusually firm. "Granddaughter-in-law, she is your mother. You can't ignore your mother..."

If only it were possible.

How she wished Sophia was truly her mother...

Evelyn lowered her gaze, hiding the bitterness in her eyes. "...I know."

She escorted the old lady back to her room.

Alexander Vance stood in the hallway and did not enter.

Thomas Watson approached quickly. "Boss, it's confirmed. Miss Ross was working a part-time job at that private dining restaurant. She happened to run into the Ashcroft family..."

She went home, showered, and changed clothes.

He thought she was going on a date with her boyfriend!

The corner of Alexander's lips lifted almost imperceptibly.

His voice was low. "Does the Vance Group not pay her enough?"

Why else would she take an outside job!

Thomas replied, "It's quite substantial. It was arranged by the Madam herself! Perhaps Jason Lawrence gave her work that was too difficult? She's afraid of being fired?"

Evelyn's movements within the company were all under Thomas's surveillance.

"Never showed up in the end..."

...

She spoke with a light cough. "Isabella, let Julian go back first."

Julian wasn't staying out of concern for Sophia.

Only by bringing Dr. Nan on board can I achieve something for the department and secure my position. So Dr. Nan is crucial to me. You must persuade him."

It might agitate Sophia and worsen her condition.

"I already said today wasn't Evelyn's fault. I haven't slept well for days. I was just too tired..."

Sophia chided him gently.

She took Evelyn's hand. "Good child, were you frightened today?"

Though Sophia was thin and had a cool demeanor, her palms were warm.

Evelyn smiled. "No."

Sophia leaned against the hospital bed, her expression kind. "And that husband of yours. Your mother says he's a ruffian, but I don't believe it. Evelyn has always had good judgment and strong opinions. Anyone you chose must have their merits. Bring him to see me someday."

Evelyn's nose tingled with emotion. "...Okay."

From the day she got the marriage certificate, everyone had given her scorn and humiliation.

Only with Sophia did she receive genuine care.

Sophia wanted to say more but started coughing violently again.

William said urgently, "Alright, that's enough. Rest properly."

Evelyn stood up.

She pulled a plastic medicine bottle from her pocket and placed it in Sophia's hand. "This is... a cough medicine I asked someone to get for you. Take one pill when you can't sleep at night."

Evelyn didn't want to disturb Sophia's rest and took her leave.

He casually threw the bottle into the trash. "I heard Nora's developed a special cough medicine that's entered clinical trials. I'll pull some strings to get it for you!"

Sophia could only nod.

Late at night, everything was quiet.

Sophia suddenly opened her eyes.

Another fit of violent coughing seized her.

She was exhausted to the extreme but simply couldn't sleep.

She turned on her side, trying to suppress the itch in her throat.

She didn't want to wake William sleeping in the outer room.

No one knew how desperate she truly felt.

Neurogenic chronic cough was incurable...

Her condition was worsening.

The anxiety from lack of sleep had even made her contemplate ending it all.

If not for her strong will, she might have already...

But she didn't know how much longer she could hold on...

Lately, she'd felt her life fading away more and more.

Perhaps she wouldn't live to see her daughter get married?

Sophia thought pessimistically.

Her eyes fell on the medicine bottle in the trash.

Almost as if possessed, she picked it up.

She shook out a white pill and placed it in her mouth...
###Chapter 31

Sophia Ashcroft swallowed the white pill.

A cool sensation instantly spread through her mouth.

The itch in her throat was gently soothed.

She swallowed it without water.

"Cough, cough…"

Another fit of violent coughing wracked her.

She shook her head with a bitter smile.

For a fleeting moment, she had actually thought the medicine was working.

...Wishful thinking.

Sophia lay back on the bed and closed her eyes.

This was an old ailment, over twenty years now.

A nervous cough.

She had seen countless specialists and taken countless medications.

None of it worked.

...

"Ash? Wake up!"

That wasn't electric light.

It was sunlight!

How was that possible!

It was the medicine Evelyn gave her!

Nanny Nancy glanced at Isabella. "It was a potent sleeping aid Young Master Julian Evans procured. He added it to your medicine. They didn't tell you, afraid you'd worry and not sleep. Who knew it would work so well!"

Was that it?

But a sleeping aid only helps with sleep.

Today, her throat felt noticeably more comfortable.

Her gaze fell on the plastic bottle by her pillow.

She would test it again tonight.

Take just the sleeping aid, not this.

See what the effect was.

Sophia remained silent.

William looked at Isabella, pleased. "That boy Julian is truly commendable. I heard the flu is rampant, the inpatient wards are completely full. There was no chance of getting a room yesterday. He pulled strings to arrange this. We owe him a lot this time."

She saw the expected struggle on her mother's face.

Sophia always disliked calling in favors.

But she hated being indebted even more.

After a moment's hesitation, Sophia finally said, "I'll ask Dr. Nan. But if he refuses, that's it."

Isabella beamed. "Okay."

In her experience, Dr. Nan had never refused Sophia.

Sophia took out her phone, found Dr. Nan's WhatsApp, and carefully composed a message: [Dr. Nan, are you free? I need to ask a favor.]

Evelyn Ross received the message just after returning from her walk with Eleanor Vance.

She frowned.

What did Sophia want?

Was there a problem with yesterday's medicine?

Or some adverse reaction?

That drug was still in clinical trials.

She had intended to wait until the trials concluded before giving it to Sophia.

But seeing Sophia so ill, she had provided it early.

A wave of anxiety hit her.

She asked the nurse to take the老夫人 back to her room and hurried straight to Sophia's suite.

But the familiar figure wasn't there.

His eyes darkened. "Where is Ms. Ross?"

The老夫人 said, "She went to her mother's room."

Alexander raised an eyebrow. "Is something wrong with Mrs. Ashcroft?"

The老夫人 grew worried. "My granddaughter-in-law ran there just now. You brat, go check on them!"

Thomas Watson hurried to follow.

...

Evelyn quickly entered the ward.

She found it crowded.

Julian Evans was there.

Linda Lowell was also present.

Someone had said something amusing, and everyone was laughing.

Standing in the doorway, Evelyn felt out of place.

Sophia seemed fine.

Sophia wanted to call out to her.

But with the room full of people, it felt inappropriate.

She would find a chance to meet alone later.

Evelyn said nothing, turning to leave.

Linda grabbed her arm. "I hear you spend all day currying favor with Mrs. Vance, even handling her waste. Do you really think she sees you as a granddaughter? Dream on! You're just a plaything to them!"

Isabella quickly interjected, "Don't say that, Mrs. Vance values Evelyn highly..."

Linda's voice grew louder. "What kind of value is that? The way Young Master Julian treats Isabella is true regard!"

She looked ingratiatingly at Julian. "I heard the wards were completely full yesterday, many important people couldn't get a room. It was all thanks to Young Master Julian's help that Madam got this VIP suite!"

Julian glanced at Evelyn, offering a modest smile. "My uncle didn't know about this, otherwise he would have helped. I merely did my small part."

"Your 'small part' was a huge help!"

Linda turned her scorn back to Evelyn. "Even if Mr. Vance knew, he wouldn't care about you. No matter how much you fawn, he'd only see you as some stray..."

Evelyn clenched her fist.

She didn't want to argue here and disturb Sophia's rest.

Alexander Vance, who had just arrived outside the door, heard every word clearly.

His face turned cold. He glanced at Thomas.

Thomas immediately understood, cleared his throat, and entered the room. "Mrs. Ashcroft, I'm here on Mr. Vance's behalf to see how you are. Is this suite to your satisfaction?"
###Chapter 32

The room fell silent.

Everyone looked at Thomas Watson.

Then they turned to Julian Evans.

Julian looked stunned.

Did his uncle arrange the private room?

Isabella Ashcroft felt utterly embarrassed.

She clenched her fists, seeing Julian as less perfect for the first time.

Sophia Ashcroft broke the silence. "Please thank Mr. Vance for me. I will visit Mrs. Eleanor Vance when I feel better."

Thomas nodded. "As long as you are comfortable, Ms. Evelyn is happy. That brings joy to Mrs. Vance too."

His words clearly elevated Evelyn's status.

The others felt a weight in their hearts.

Only Sophia was genuinely pleased.

Knowing Evelyn had someone looking out for her put her mind at ease.

Evelyn was slightly surprised.

She hadn't expected Alexander Vance to help.

She glanced at Linda, a faint, mocking smile on her lips.

Linda was provoked. "What are you so smug about? Think you're special with a powerful backer?"

"Enough!" William Ashcroft snapped. "My wife needs rest. Go home. Now!"

Linda pleaded, "William, I—"

"Get out!"

Linda shot a hateful look at Evelyn before storming off.

Evelyn turned to Sophia. "Did you sleep well last night, Madam?"

Sophia tightened her grip on the hidden medicine bottle. "Very well. I haven't slept that soundly in years."

"Good."

Evelyn offered no further explanation. She nodded and prepared to leave.

She and Thomas exited the room, finding Alexander waiting in the hallway.

The man stood tall in his tailored suit, leaning against the wall, focused on his phone.

His tousled hair partly obscured his sharp, handsome features.

Hearing them, he looked up. His deep eyes met hers. "Grandmother was concerned. She asked me to check."

His voice was low and pleasant.

A warmth spread through Evelyn's heart. "Thank you."

Her peach-blossom eyes were clear, her smile radiant.

Alexander was momentarily captivated.

The door opened again. Julian and Isabella stepped out.

Seeing Alexander and Evelyn together, Isabella felt threatened.

Julian's face darkened. "Uncle, what are you doing here?"

Alexander's tone was cool. "Do I need to report my movements to you?"

An invisible pressure filled the air.

Julian's palms grew damp. "That's not what I meant."

He glared at Evelyn.

The girl was breathtakingly beautiful.

Standing next to Alexander, they looked perfectly matched.

Jealousy churned inside Julian. "Ms. Evelyn, you've been at the hospital constantly. Doesn't your husband mind?"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow, glancing at Alexander. "I believe he doesn't."

Julian's expression soured.

He was about to say more when Alexander spoke. "Heading back? Grandmother is waiting."

Back...

Evelyn paused.

It had been a long time since she had a place to return to.

She had no home.

She lowered her gaze with a faint smile. "Let's go back."

They turned and walked away.

Julian watched Evelyn's retreating figure, his eyes dark and intense.

In that moment, he wanted to throw caution to the wind and reclaim her.

Isabella clung to his arm. "Julian, I will help you secure Dr. Nan..."

Julian snapped back to reality. "Good."

"I'll walk you downstairs."

"Alright."

After seeing Julian off, Isabella felt a deeper sense of crisis.

She returned to the room. Seeing Sophia up and moving, her eyes flickered.

She went to tidy the bed and suddenly gasped. "Mom, why is there a medicine bottle here?"

William frowned. "That unregulated drug from Evelyn? Sophia, why did you bring it back?"

Sophia explained, "I took one last night. It seemed effective..."

"Impossible! The sleeping pill worked!"

William snatched the bottle. "Taking random medicine could harm your health!"

Sophia urged, "Don't throw it away. I won't take it tonight. Let's see which medicine actually works."

William sighed. "...Fine."

Night fell.

Nanny Nancy held the sleeping pill like it was a miracle cure.

She watched Sophia take it, turned off the light, and tiptoed out.

The room grew quiet.

Half an hour passed with no sound from inside.

Nancy was thrilled. "Young Master Julian's medicine saved Madam! She hasn't slept through the night in over twenty years..."

Isabella feigned modesty. "Please, don't mention it. The room wasn't arranged by Julian. It was so embarrassing today..."

William stated, "The medicine is more important than the room! Julian helped greatly. He didn't show off like Evelyn did."

He glanced disdainfully at Evelyn's medicine. "Nanny Nancy, dispose of this questionable drug immediately!"

As soon as he finished speaking, violent coughing erupted from the room.
###Chapter 33 
William Ashcroft halted mid-step.

He exchanged a look with Nancy.

Their expressions were filled with shock and suspicion.

They rushed into the hospital room.

A typical symptom of nervous cough is worsening at night.

Nancy flipped the switch.

The lights came on.

Sophia Ashcroft was half-propped against the headboard, covering her mouth as she coughed violently.

Her face was flushed, and she was breathing rapidly.

Her thin frame trembled with each cough, a heartbreaking sight.

William held her shoulders. "Sophia, how are you feeling?"

"It's the medicine... cough..." she managed with difficulty.

William looked confused.

But Nancy understood instantly and dashed out of the room.

She returned with the bottle Evelyn had given her. "Ma'am, the medicine is here!"

Sophia nodded.

Nancy hurriedly shook out a pill and was about to hand it over when Isabella stopped her.

Isabella frowned deeply. "Mom's cough suddenly got worse. What if it's because of this medicine? We shouldn't risk giving her something of unknown origin..."

Her intuition told her the medicine might work.

She couldn't let that happen.

She looked at William.

When someone is biased against another, everything that person does is wrong.

Evelyn was Linda's daughter. That was William's biggest bias.

William flew into a rage. "Evelyn is truly useless! I always said she couldn't be trusted. After all your kindness, Sophia, she tries to harm you with medicine!"

Isabella secretly rejoiced.

But Sophia, between coughs, reached out to Nancy. "Give me the pill! Cough..."

Nancy, loyal to her mistress, immediately handed over the pill.

Sophia quickly swallowed it...

"Sophia!"

William cried out, trying to stop her, but was astonished to find—Sophia's coughing had stopped!

She caught her breath, exhaled deeply, and stated with certainty, "Evelyn's medicine worked."

"..."

After Sophia fell into a peaceful sleep, they moved to the outer room.

Isabella was pale, biting her lip. "Dad, could Evelyn really have gotten the special medicine?"

"She doesn't have that kind of connections. Mr. Vance must have gotten it for her."

William frowned. "It seems Old Mrs. Vance really values her..."

Isabella immediately clenched her fingers.

...

Inside the VIP suite.

"What did you have for lunch today?"

The little old lady thought hard. "Porridge? No... noodles! Right, you brat?"

Alexander Vance smiled and nodded. "Right."

The little old lady said triumphantly, "See? My mind is perfectly sharp!"

Alexander remained silent.

A closer look would reveal his smile didn't reach his eyes.

They had actually had rice for lunch. Grandma was misremembering again.

Her condition seemed to be worsening. Her memory was starting to fail...

Then Evelyn spoke lazily from her spot. "Grandma, what color was Mr. Vance's tie yesterday?"

The little old lady answered immediately. "Purple! The brat is so particular. He even matched it with diamond cufflinks."

"And the day before?"

"Blue! I bought him that blue tie myself~"

Evelyn smiled faintly, giving Alexander a meaningful look. "Grandma remembers things she cares about very clearly."

Alexander looked up at her.

The girl was sunk deep into a beanbag chair, looking completely relaxed.

She was fiddling with her phone, her dark hair cascading loosely, making her skin appear even paler. She seemed bathed in a soft light.

Her words just now were meant to comfort him.

His anxious heart gradually calmed...

The little old lady suddenly laughed. "Granddaughter-in-law, was I right about everything?"

Evelyn said, "Yes."

The little old lady looked at Alexander. "Brat, see? Your wife remembers what you wear every day so clearly! You're on her mind!"

Evelyn:!!

She just had a photographic memory, it wasn't intentional!

She wanted to explain, but saw Alexander staring at his computer, seemingly uninterested.

He probably didn't take Grandma's words seriously.

No need to explain then, might make it worse.

Evelyn didn't notice the slight reddening of the man's ears. She turned her attention back to her work on the phone.

The Alzheimer's project had hit a bottleneck in its final stages. She had no leads.

She stood up. "I'm going out for a walk."

While walking her Shiba Inu "Kitten" downstairs, someone tapped her shoulder.

Turning around, she saw a sleazy-looking middle-aged man grinning at her. "Long time no see, my dear niece!"

Evelyn's face instantly turned cold.

This was Linda's brother, her uncle Leo Lowell.

He was idle and addicted to gambling.

When he spoke, a foul smell emanated from his mouth. His yellowed teeth were disgusting.

Evelyn took a step back. "What are you doing here?"

Leo rubbed his hands together, leering. "Heard my dear niece has hooked the Vance family? Uncle's a bit short on cash lately. Could you lend me some?"

Evelyn scoffed.

Linda was a classic enabler. She clung to the Ashcrofts, giving all her living expenses to this brother.

After Evelyn left the Ashcroft home, Leo had shamelessly come to her for money multiple times.

Only after seeing she "truly had no money" did he finally stop.

Now he was back...

Evelyn said calmly, "I don't have any money."

Leo didn't believe her. "You're taking such good care of that old lady. Didn't she give you any money?"

Evelyn narrowed her eyes. "She hasn't given me any yet. Maybe she will later."

Leo immediately smiled. "You must lend some to Uncle then. If you give me enough, I might even tell you a secret!"

"Fine."

Leo didn't dare cause trouble here. The VIP suites housed the wealthy and powerful. Security guards patrolled nearby. He chuckled and left.

Evelyn snorted dismissively.

Just then, her phone rang. It was Sophia calling.

She answered immediately.

...

Isabella paced anxiously in her room.

Her standing in the Vance family depended entirely on Dr. Nan!

But a whole day had passed, and Dr. Nan hadn't replied to Sophia's message!

Julian had sent several more WhatsApp messages, constantly asking about Dr. Nan. She couldn't delay any longer...

Isabella suddenly stopped pacing and quietly entered the hospital room.

She took the phone from the sleeping Sophia's side and went out into the hallway.

Taking a deep breath, she opened the contacts, found "Little Nan," and directly dialed the number!

The phone rang three times before it was answered.

A cool voice came through. "Hello, Mrs. Ashcroft, is something wrong?"

Isabella froze upon hearing the voice. This voice...

"Evelyn?!"

###Chapter 34

Isabella's pupils contracted sharply.

She stared at her phone screen.

That voice was too familiar.

A moment of silence on the line was followed by an even colder tone. "Isabella, why do you have my mother's phone?"

It was really her!

A chill ran down Isabella's spine.

This was impossible…

A sudden thought struck her. "Is this your number?"

Evelyn's voice was flat. "Why ask such a pointless question?"

Isabella finally understood.

She had almost forgotten that Evelyn's name also contained the character "Nan."

Mother occasionally called her "Little Nan" affectionately.

Isabella let out a long sigh of relief.

Then, anger surged within her. "You're such a bootlicker. To please my mother, you even used her surname. Are you worthy of being called 'Nan'?"

Downstairs, Evelyn lowered her eyes.

She had chosen this name herself in elementary school.

This "Nan" did indeed come from Sophia's surname.

Back then, she was just a child who simply liked the character.

She never considered how Mrs. Ashcroft might feel.

The daughter of a mistress, using the lawful wife's surname.

She would never know how Mrs. Ashcroft felt at that time.

Her voice remained calm. "Did you call just to say this?"

"Of course not!" Isabella dropped all pretense, her tone venomous. "Let me tell you, don't think cozying up to Eleanor Vance will get you anywhere. You're just a plaything at best! Mr. Vance is a married man! I will marry Julian and become the rightful young mistress of the Vance family!"

Evelyn chuckled lightly. "Congratulations, then?"

Isabella felt like she had punched cotton.

She gritted her teeth. "High society isn't just about feelings! I hold the ultimate trump card, Dr. Nan. I will never lose!"

The call ended.

Evelyn shook her head with a wry smile.

Since when had she become Isabella's trump card?

As she pondered, another WhatsApp voice call came through.

This time, it was from Mrs. Ashcroft using the "Dr. Nan" account.

Evelyn tapped the screen and answered.

She lowered her voice. "Hello."

A voice-altered greeting was sent.

Leo Lowell's appearance had taught her that staying low-key was the wise choice.

It avoided being pestered by those peculiar relatives.

Isabella's tone was respectful. "Hello, Dr. Nan. Sorry to disturb you. I'm Isabella Ashcroft, Sophia Ashcroft's daughter. The Vance Group would like to invite you to join the R&D team. My mother also agrees. May I ask if you…"

"Not interested." Evelyn coldly cut her off.

Isabella tried to persuade her, but Evelyn spoke first. "I am only responsible for Mrs. Ashcroft's company."

She hung up immediately.

…

Monday morning.

Evelyn left the Shiba Inu puppy with Eleanor Vance and left for work on time.

Standing at the hospital entrance, hesitating between a taxi or the subway, the familiar Bentley slowly pulled up beside her.

The window rolled down, revealing Alexander Vance's sharp profile.

He looked straight ahead, his voice detached. "Get in."

Evelyn shook her head. "It's not convenient."

They weren't close enough for daily rides.

Alexander said flatly, "Grandmother asked me to drive you."

"You can tell her you did."

"She wants photo proof."

"…Fine."

Evelyn opened the rear door and sat beside Alexander.

Alexander raised his phone, aiming it at both of them.

Evelyn cooperatively flashed a peace sign, a bright smile on her face.

She tilted her head slightly, her hair brushing against Alexander's cheek.

Alexander stiffened, as if jolted by an electric current.

Thomas, in the passenger seat, looked puzzled.

When they left this morning, he didn't remember Mrs. Vance giving such instructions?

Maybe he missed it.

After the photo, Evelyn sat up straight and pulled out her phone to handle work.

Silence filled the rest of the ride.

The car entered the Vance Group's underground parking garage.

Alexander and Thomas took the CEO's private elevator.

Evelyn headed for the employee elevator and went to the R&D department.

The R&D management was in a meeting, the atmosphere tense.

The R&D General Manager announced personnel changes. "By company decision, Team Leader Julian Evans is promoted to Vice President! He will now oversee the R&D department's technical work!"

Someone asked, "Why so sudden?"

The General Manager explained. "VP Evans successfully invited Dr. Nan to provide technical support. This is a major event for R&D. While Dr. Nan won't be physically present, all technical issues can be communicated through VP Evans. Now, let's hear from VP Evans."

Julian stood up, his youthful gaze fixed directly on Jason Lawrence. "Both Team One and Team Two will now report to me. Team Leader Lawrence, you won't violate company policy, will you?"

Jason Lawrence, who had pulled an all-nighter working over the weekend, wore casual clothes and looked thoroughly irritated.

He sneered. "Company policy prohibits skipping levels in reporting or task assignment. My team members must be assigned through me!"

Julian's face darkened. "Team Leader Lawrence, do you even have the energy to manage Team One? Have you solved the core issue? R&D is one unit. Your delays affect the entire project's progress!"

Jason shoved his chair back and stood up. "You studied new energy too. You know the difficulty of R&D. Don't act like you could solve it!"

Julian replied unhurriedly. "I can't solve it, but I know someone who can. Should I ask Dr. Nan to help you?"

Jason scoffed. "Since when are you so helpful?"

Julian's eyes turned sinister. "We're all part of R&D. Of course, I'll look out for you. But to communicate with Dr. Nan, your team needs to assign a technical person to assist. How about that new intern?"

Jason had guessed his target was Evelyn!

He said coldly, "Not a chance! I'll manage on my own. Don't trouble yourself!"

Julian's face turned stormy. "Team Leader Lawrence, don't think Mr. Vance's backing lets you do as you please! Delaying R&D progress is your failure! I'm giving you two more days. If the problem isn't solved, prepare to resign or be demoted!"

Jason walked out. "We'll see if you have what it takes!"

He sounded confident in the office, but his expression darkened the moment he left.

He knew if Julian went through with this, even Alexander Vance would have difficulty protecting him.

Jason entered the Team One office and kicked a chair in frustration.

Daniel Clementson, who was late, slipped quietly to his workstation, afraid to provoke the irritable boss.

He turned on his computer and opened his email, noticing an unread message.

Huh?

An email from Evelyn Ross…

Did she actually solve the core problem?
###Chapter 35

Daniel stared at the screen.

Even Jason Lawrence couldn't solve this problem.

Could an undergraduate really handle it?

He clicked open the email.

The attachment was completely blank.

"Bang!"

Jason slammed the documents onto the desk.

"Meeting!"

Daniel glanced at the email.

Blank file.

She really hadn't solved it.

He stood up and followed.

The email attachment finally finished loading.

It sat quietly in the inbox.

The office atmosphere was tense.

Jason looked serious.

"If we don't solve this problem in two days,"

"I will resign."

The team members fell silent for a moment.

Then they all spoke up.

"Team leader, we'll give it our all!"

"We'll work through the night if we have to!"

After tasks were assigned,

Jason suddenly frowned.

"Where's Evelyn Ross?"

Daniel shook his head.

Someone answered, "She went to the resource library."

Jason's expression instantly darkened.

Team members began murmuring.

"Disappearing at a critical moment."

"Getting paid for doing nothing."

Daniel tried to explain.

"She just graduated. She can't understand this..."

Jason took a deep breath.

He thought Evelyn was being ungrateful.

......

Top-floor office.

Thomas reported to Alexander.

"She spent the whole morning in the library."

Alexander looked up.

"What was she reading?"

Thomas paused.

"I'll go check right away."

Alexander was already standing.

"No need."

He walked straight to the library.

He found her in the neuroscience section.

She was on tiptoe reaching for a book.

Her hoodie rode up.

Revealing a pale sliver of her waist.

Alexander's gaze darkened.

Evelyn got the book.

In her excitement, she forgot she was still on the ladder.

She missed a step.

And fell into a warm embrace.

Alexander caught her firmly.

His hand cupped her bottom.

It felt soft.

Evelyn's cheeks flushed instantly.

She quickly jumped down.

"Mr. Vance, thank you."

Alexander's hand felt empty.

His fingertips still tingled with warmth.

His Adam's apple moved.

"You're welcome."

The atmosphere was subtle.

Evelyn cleared her throat.

"I have work in R&D. I should go."

She hid the book.

And hurried away.

Her cheeks still felt warm.

......

Evelyn returned to Team One.

The atmosphere was clearly different.

Her colleagues ignored her.

She was confused.

She planned to go back to the library that afternoon.

Daniel stopped her.

"Don't go."

Evelyn was puzzled.

"My work is done."

Daniel frowned.

"You finished last week's tasks?"

"Yes, I did."

Daniel was stunned.
###Chapter 36

Daniel Clementson froze completely.

He stared blankly at Evelyn Ross.

Evelyn remained calm. "I sent it to your email last Friday."

Daniel swallowed hard. "That wasn't an empty email?"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

Daniel suddenly understood.

He rushed to his workstation and opened his email.

There it was. The attachment!

His fingers trembled as he clicked download.

New energy research requires both theory and practice.

Evelyn had completed the simulation portion for him last Friday.

He had been assisting in the lab all day today.

He never checked his email.

The download finished.

Daniel eagerly began reading.

The more he read, the more astonished he became.

The core technology application was extremely complex.

Some sections required repeated study to fully grasp.

Evelyn saw he was completely absorbed.

She turned and walked toward the elevator, heading for the 60th floor.

The elevator doors opened.

Several members from Team One entered.

"Beep beep beep—"

The overload alarm sounded.

Someone immediately looked at Evelyn. "You get out."

Their tone was impatient.

Evelyn looked up. "I wasn't the last one on."

The person snorted. "We're in a hurry. You're not busy. Just move aside, will you?"

Others quickly agreed.

"Exactly! You're just going to read books anyway!"

"Don't waste our time!"

"The team lead is waiting for us!"

Evelyn frowned.

She sensed clear hostility.

Though confused by the reason, she stepped out of the elevator.

Arguing was pointless.

The elevator doors closed.

Someone muttered quietly, "Was that too harsh?"

"Who told her to skip work but still want credit…"

Evelyn took another elevator to the 60th floor.

She hurried toward the archives section.

But her book was gone.

She paused, startled.

She searched the shelves frantically.

No one nearby had it either.

Archive books cannot be checked out.

Where did the book go?

A thought suddenly occurred to her.

Only one person in the entire company could have taken it.

The penthouse.

Alexander Vance examined the heavy volume in his hands.

It covered neurology.

He had some knowledge due to his grandmother's condition.

But this book was profoundly advanced.

Why would Evelyn be reading this?

Thomas Watson finished his report on the downstairs situation. "Sir, Evelyn is taking advantage of Mrs. Vance's favor! You arranged a professional department for her to learn, but she doesn't appreciate it at all!"

"She never came here to work in the first place."

Alexander's voice was cold as he set the book down.

Thomas was taken aback.

He felt his boss was defending her?

It must be a misunderstanding.

He asked, "Should I return the book?"

Alexander tapped the cover lightly.

He recalled the girl's eager expression when she saw the book.

He also remembered the firm feel of her in his arms that morning.

A sudden dryness touched his throat.

"Not yet."

Evelyn considered going to the penthouse to retrieve the book from Alexander.

But the memory of that morning's embrace made her hesitate, feeling unexpectedly shy.

She slowly returned to the R&D department.

The elevator doors opened.

She saw Jason Lawrence with Team One in the break area, drinking coffee.

Everyone looked exhausted.

Seeing Evelyn's relaxed appearance, someone couldn't hold back.

"Evelyn, this company isn't a place for you to wander around!"

"We're all working ourselves to the bone. How can you not even help?"

"Don't you know how much experience you gain participating in a project?"

"The project bonus is split equally. We haven't complained about your lack of skill, but you can't just collect a paycheck without contributing!"

"Even someone with connections should have limits!"

Evelyn stopped walking.

She asked seriously, "Is there something you need help with?"

"If you don't understand the tech, at least help with errands! Get coffee or make copies!"

Evelyn frowned. "I'm sorry. That's not within my job scope."

She had been working part-time since middle school.

She understood workplace rules.

The salary at Vance Group was good.

But she had completed three tasks last Friday.

She had already earned her keep.

She didn't know the minor issue she resolved last was the core problem stumping the entire team.

After speaking, she headed toward the office.

Her response angered her colleagues.

"No skills, but a real attitude!"

"Why did the company hire someone like this?"

Jason listened to the comments.

He watched Evelyn's indifferent demeanor.

His brow furrowed tightly.

He wanted to help Evelyn.

But her apparent lack of effort was truly disappointing.

He had stood up for her against Julian Evans.

How foolish!

Jason slammed his hand on the table and roared, "Evelyn! If you don't want to work, get out! Team One doesn't carry dead weight!"

Inside the office.

Daniel stared at the email on his computer with rapt fascination.

He finally understood it!

Daniel shot to his feet and burst out the door.

He excitedly grabbed Jason's arm!

###Chapter 37

Daniel shot up from his chair.

"I got it! It's really solved!"

His voice trembled with excitement.

Jason frowned at him. "What's wrong with you?"

Others in the lab shot disapproving looks his way.

"Daniel, where were you this afternoon?"

"We've been swamped with work, and you just disappeared."

"Don't tell me you actually solved that problem?"

Daniel nodded vigorously. "Yes! That exact problem!"

He pulled Jason over to the computer. "Team Lead, look! Our previous approach was wrong. It should actually be like this…"

He quickly explained the new solution.

Team One gradually gathered around them.

"So that's how it works!"

"Daniel, you're amazing!"

"The Team Lead doesn't have to resign now!"

…

Jason stared at the computer screen, then suddenly noticed the email sender.

He froze, looking at Daniel. "This is…"

"It was submitted by our junior, Evelyn Ross! She sent it to me last Friday, but I left work without checking it!"

The lab fell silent instantly.

All eyes turned to Evelyn.

It was unbelievable.

The problem that had stumped them for a month was easily solved by this young woman.

Last Friday… was her first day.

Evelyn stood quietly, her expression detached and cool.

A hint of surprise flickered in her peach-blossom eyes. "This is what you were all struggling with?"

Everyone: "…"

Why did they read "is that all?" on her face?

Someone swallowed hard. "You… aren't you an undergraduate?"

Evelyn responded seriously. "Ability isn't determined by degrees."

Everyone regarded her with newfound respect.

After a brief silence, someone apologized. "I'm sorry for what I said in the elevator."

"I was wrong too. I shouldn't have said you were slacking."

"I even said I'd teach you… really overstepped…"

"We were blind…"

Research scientists are straightforward.

Evelyn knew their earlier criticism came from frustration.

She responded mildly. "It's fine."

Jason walked over to her. Still proud, he extended his hand. "Welcome to Team One."

Before, she was just a connection. Now, he acknowledged her ability.

"Clap clap clap!"

Applause came from the doorway.

Everyone turned to see the R&D Manager and a gloomy Julian.

The manager praised, "The young are truly limitless! Ms. Ross's professional skills are outstanding!"

Julian stared at Evelyn, his expression complex.

He knew about her talent. His attraction wasn't just superficial. But he never expected her to solve Team One's core problem.

With proper training, someone like her could soar.

Her future achievements might even rival Dr. Nan's…

But that was the future. For now, Dr. Nan remained far beyond her reach.

Julian clenched his fist, his resolve hardening.

He needed to control R&D now, to secure his position at Vance Group. He couldn't wait for her to develop.

Besides, she was a love child…

Whether by status or ability, he could never marry her.

Suppressing his emotions, he coldly addressed Jason. "I was thinking, if Team Lead Jiang couldn't complete the task, I would ask Dr. Nan to advise…"

Team One's eyes widened instantly.

"Oh my god, Dr. Nan!"

"Being guided by Dr. Nan is my lifelong dream!"

"I'd give up dating forever just to meet Dr. Nan once!"

…

Jason's face stiffened. "This small problem isn't worth troubling Dr. Nan."

Julian insisted. "Dr. Nan is our technical support anyway. It's only right to help."

Evelyn frowned.

She clearly refused Isabella. How did she become Vance Group's technical support?

Julian's confidence meant Isabella had lied again.

Julian narrowed his eyes. "Team Lead Jiang, I just wanted a technically competent assistant, which is why I tried to transfer Evelyn. We're both key to R&D. No need for such tension. If you agree, I'll immediately invite Dr. Nan as technical support."

He dangled the bait, letting Team One know that handing over Evelyn meant access to Dr. Nan.

Dr. Nan's appeal to researchers was immense.

Team One would surely waver…

Evelyn's expression darkened. She found Julian despicable.

She looked at her colleagues, seeing their conflicted expressions. They understood Julian's implication.

Everyone exchanged glances, struggle written on their faces.

Evelyn's eyes dimmed.

She'd only been here a day and a half. Though she felt their goodwill last week, bonds were still shallow…

Julian saw his divisive tactic working and pressed further. "Team Lead Jiang, will you release her now? Evelyn has better prospects with me…"

Jason scowled, his tone firm. "No."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow, slightly surprised.

Julian smirked coldly. "The company believes in democracy. Team One's matters aren't yours alone to decide. If you won't release her, have you asked the team? Does Team One truly not need Dr. Nan?"

Team One fell silent.

Jason tensed, glaring at his team.

Evelyn suddenly laughed.

She intended to avoid trouble by hiding her identity, but now she didn't want to test human nature…

She met Julian's gaze directly, her voice cold. "You're using Dr. Nan as a bargaining chip. Did you ever ask if I agree?"
###Chapter 38

"What gives you the right?"

Julian Evans sneered.
"This is the Vance Group's decision, Evelyn Ross. You have no say."

"Does Dr. Nan agree?"

Evelyn's tone was calm, her eyes mocking.

Julian froze.
"Does Dr. Nan need to report to you?"

Could she actually know Dr. Nan?

Evelyn's lips curved slightly.
"I am—"

Before she could say "Dr. Nan," Daniel Clementson stepped forward, blocking her.
"We only take orders from our team leader!"

Evelyn's eyes widened in surprise as she looked at Daniel.

The rest of the team voiced their support.
"Yes, we all stand with our leader!"

Evelyn stared at them, unable to believe it.

Just half an hour ago, they had accused her of slacking off.

Now they were defending her without hesitation.

Julian was not only the VP of R&D but also the Vance family's heir.

Crossing him could ruin their careers at Vance Group.

Yet they didn't seem to care...

A lump formed in Evelyn's throat, warmth filling her eyes.

Julian's face darkened with suppressed rage.
"Quite confident, aren't you? If you don't need Dr. Nan, finishing the project should be easy. Submit the solution by Friday before leaving work."

The R&D project was split between two teams.
