She remained silent.

Alexander spoke lightly.

"You talk too much."

Jason was speechless.

He thought his cousin was acting very strange today.

Alexander looked at Evelyn.

"I'm heading back. Together?"

Evelyn immediately nodded.

"Okay. Meet you at the entrance."

Jason hurried to follow.

"I'll come too."

Inside the banquet hall.

Isabella saw Julian's ugly expression.

Her heart was uneasy.

Evelyn knew James Klein.

Did that mean she also knew Dr. Nan?

Julian walked up to James.

"Mr. Klein, does Evelyn Ross know Dr. Nan?"

Everyone pricked up their ears.

If James said yes.

They would definitely swarm Evelyn.

He cleared his throat lightly.

"How could that be?"

Isabella and Julian breathed a sigh of relief.

James saw Evelyn leaving.

He hurriedly made his excuses.

"I have matters to attend to. I must take my leave."

Everyone watched him chase after her.

They smiled with deep meaning.

Julian's face grew darker.

At the entrance.

James caught up to Evelyn.

"I'm going abroad tomorrow to discuss cooperation. I need to take the company seal and the patent documents."

Evelyn nodded.

James hurried away.

Jason looked at her strangely.

"Mr. Klein is going abroad. Why is he reporting to you?"

His eyes widened.

"Don't tell me…"

Evelyn was silent for a moment.

She sighed softly.

"Alright. I admit it."

"I am Dr. Nan."
###Chapter 44

Evelyn Ross's identity wasn't something that absolutely had to remain secret.

She never intended to hide it from Team One.

As long as it didn't become public knowledge and cause unnecessary trouble, it was fine.

So when Jason Lawrence grew suspicious, she simply admitted it.

Her words made Jason freeze.

He stared at Evelyn blankly.

Suddenly, he snorted with laughter. "You're pretty good at joking."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

Jason said, "I've never met Dr. Nan, but I know a top academic like that has to be at least forty. Don't try to fool me."

Evelyn was speechless. "I really am."

"Hah, you think you can bluff your way through like this?" Jason looked her up and down, frowning deeply. "You're not... having an affair with James Klein, are you?"

Evelyn sighed. "Your imagination is wild!"

What nonsense!

Jason advised her seriously. "Don't do that sort of thing. It won't look good if it gets out. I despise cheaters the most... We're all decent people. If you fall out of love, get a divorce first, then find someone else..."

Before he could finish, he felt a chill down his spine. A palpable, murderous aura washed over him!

He turned and saw Alexander Vance striding over, his eyes icy cold.

Jason was confused. "Cousin, did your business deal fall through today?"

Alexander withdrew his gaze. "So noisy."

Jason fell silent.

Evelyn noticed this fearless second-generation heir was only afraid of his cousin.

Just then, a bright yellow sports car pulled up in front of them. James Klein whistled. "Old... Evelyn, just got this car. Need a ride?"

He couldn't wait to show off to his boss.

He imagined her screaming in his car!

Evelyn understood his look and was about to refuse when Alexander suddenly spoke, his voice low. "Probably not on your way."

James laughed. "Wherever she's going is on my way!"

Evelyn sighed, noticing Jason's even more suspicious look.

She didn't notice Alexander's expression darkening further.

The man looked at her, his sharp brows slightly furrowed, and advised quietly. "You've been drinking. Don't get in a stranger's car."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow and said to James, "I'll ride with Mr. Vance."

"Fine."

James's head drooped, his enthusiasm gone. "I'll head off then."

The sports car roared away and disappeared.

Alexander's black Bentley slowly drove up.

The extended body was heavy and steady, much safer than that flimsy sports car.

Thomas Watson ran to open the door.

Alexander motioned for Evelyn to get in first, then followed closely.

When Jason tried to follow, he was stopped. "Don't you have your own car?"

Jason said, "I've been drinking."

"Oh." Alexander's expression was indifferent. "Then call a designated driver."

Jason was speechless.

What happened to not getting in cars after drinking?!

The door closed, and the Bentley drove away mercilessly, leaving Jason alone on the sidewalk.

The car drove smoothly.

Neon lights flashed outside, casting shifting shadows on Evelyn's profile, soft and hazy.

Alexander was momentarily captivated before speaking suddenly. "Ms. Ross, help me change the bandage."

Evelyn was startled. She looked up at him, surprised. "...Now?"

"Yes." Alexander nodded. "If I change it back at the hospital, Grandma will worry."

So that was it.

Evelyn secretly breathed a sigh of relief. "Okay."

The man took off his shirt and turned around.

Evelyn turned on the overhead light and looked at his back.

This was the second time she'd seen him shirtless. He looked slim in clothes, but his muscles were well-defined and powerful without them...

She collected her thoughts and focused on the wound. The blisters hadn't returned. A young body's recovery was strong.

She took out gauze and ointment, skillfully changed the dressing, and prepared to wrap the bandage.

Kneeling on the back seat, she reached her arms around Alexander's body. Just as she was about to pull back—

Screech!

The car suddenly changed lanes. Evelyn threw herself against Alexander's back, her cheek pressing against his shoulder.

Alexander braced himself to steady them.

But he felt the girl behind him holding him, her two small hands instinctively gripping his pectoral muscles, making him tense up instantly.

Then her soft lips lightly brushed over his shoulder...

The man's breath hitched. That patch of skin became hypersensitive, a tingling sensation spreading through his limbs...

Alexander's body stiffened, his mind blank.

Thinking she had pressed on his wound, Evelyn quickly steadied herself and pushed away from him, asking urgently, "Mr. Vance, does the wound hurt?"

Her voice brought Alexander back to his senses. His voice was hoarse. "It's fine."

Evelyn breathed a sigh of relief and finished wrapping the bandage.

Alexander quickly put his shirt back on and sat expressionlessly. The car felt cramped, stuffy, and unbearably hot.

Remembering those two hands, the lingering sensation on his shoulder... He loosened his tie.

As soon as the car stopped at the hospital, he pushed the door open and rushed out, heading upstairs without looking back.

Evelyn got out slowly. Watching his dark expression as he left, she couldn't help but ask Thomas, "Did the negotiation go badly tonight? He seems furious."

Thomas nearly rolled his eyes.

The acquisition tonight saved a hundred million under budget. The boss was very pleased!

But he didn't say anything, just jogged to catch up with Alexander.

Evelyn shook her head and was about to go upstairs when a dark figure suddenly darted in front of her, grinning slyly. "My dear niece!"

It was her uncle, Leo Lowell.

He looked at the Bentley, then at Alexander's retreating figure, his eyes darting around. "Mr. Vance really values you. Did he give you money?"

Evelyn smiled faintly. "Of course. Mrs. Eleanor Vance gave me some too."

Leo's eyes instantly lit up.

Evelyn said slowly, "By the way, about that truth you mentioned last time. Name your price."

Leo was taken aback.

He said earnestly, "I can only sell this truth once. I need a good price to tell you."

Evelyn looked down and operated her phone.

Soon, Leo's phone chimed. Twenty thousand dollars had been deposited into his bank account.

Leo was overjoyed.

Evelyn said, "This is a deposit. I have another two hundred thousand. Tell me the truth, and it's all yours. But I need to judge if it's worth that much first."

"It is! Absolutely! This concerns you!" Leo, afraid the money would vanish, said urgently, "I'll tell you right now!"
###Chapter 45

Evelyn's gaze was sharp.
"Speak."

Leo Lowell leaned closer, lowering his voice.
"This secret is worth two million. You're getting a bargain. The Ashcroft fortune is so vast..."

"Is it about the Ashcrofts?"

Evelyn's jaw tightened.

"Exactly."

Leo grinned.
"Actually, you are—"

His words were cut off by a sharp shout.
"Leo Lowell! Evelyn Ross! What are you doing?!"

Evelyn turned to see Isabella rushing over, her evening gown gathered in her hands.

Leo smiled cheekily.
"Just discussing business with my dear niece!"

Isabella clenched her fists.
"I already paid you!"

Leo shrugged.
"But I'm short on cash again~"

"...How much?"
Isabella took a deep breath.
"I'll give it to you!"

Leo raised an eyebrow.
"Your sister just offered two hundred thousand!"

Isabella's nails dug into her palms.

Leo tutted.
"Well, I suppose I'll make the deal with her then."

"I have it!"
Isabella spoke through gritted teeth.
"Go back now. I'll give you the money tomorrow."

"Deal."
Leo turned and ran, shouting back to Evelyn.
"Consider that twenty thousand a loan, dear niece..."

He fled quickly, afraid Evelyn would demand repayment.

...Scoundrel.

Evelyn wasn't worried.
She had countless ways to get that money back.

She turned and walked toward the inpatient building.

Behind her, Isabella's phone rang.
She answered, "Julian~"

Julian Evans asked, "Isabella, isn't Mrs. Ashcroft from New York? Could you ask if she knows the Roland family?"

"The Rolands?"

"Yes, the head is Ryan Roland. I just got word that my uncle wants to push for a Vance Group collaboration with them, but he hasn't found a way in yet."

Isabella understood immediately.
Julian wanted to use the Rolands to improve his standing at Vance Group.

She replied, "I'll ask my mother later."

Hanging up, Isabella took a calming breath.
Two hundred thousand was nothing if she could marry into the Vance family.

She needed to find a way to connect with the Rolands!

Mrs. Ashcroft was in a VIP suite on the second floor.
Eleanor Vance was on the third.
Evelyn and Isabella took the stairs together.

Just as they reached the second-floor landing, they overheard a mother and son talking in low voices.

The mother looked delighted.
"Your aunt just called. She found a wealthy girl for you... You need to go back for a blind date now!"

The son complained, "Mom, my wife will be fine after her surgery. Why would I go on a blind date?"

The mother grew anxious.
"The nurse said the surgery will cost fifty thousand, and there's recovery after. Where will we get that kind of money?"

The son hesitated.
"Let's sell the house. She bought it with her dowry."

The mother was exasperated.
"We can't sell the house! If she dies, you'll still have the house and can find someone better. If the house is gone, we have nothing! Two years of her youth—consider the house compensation!"

The son paused.
"That doesn't seem right. She cut ties with her family for me."

The mother sneered.
"Exactly because of that. She hasn't been in touch with them these two years. No one will back her up. Who cares if she lives or dies?"

She lowered her voice.
"Just say we have no money. Bring her home. She won't last three days. Bury her when she's gone, then marry the blind date girl... This one has wide hips, good for bearing children! Much better than her. A noble lady's airs but a servant's fate!"

The son narrowed his eyes, tempted.
"Then we won't treat her!"

Their voices were low, but the stairwell was quiet and echoed.
Evelyn heard every word.

She stopped walking.
She found the mother-in-law vicious and the husband even more disgusting.

She wondered which unfortunate girl had ended up with such trash.

As she thought this, she sensed something and looked down the hallway.

A pale, frail girl in a hospital gown stood in a doorway.

The mother and son noticed her too, their faces changing instantly.

The son spoke.
"Chloe..."

The girl named Chloe said weakly, "Call my dad. He'll pay for the surgery..."

The mother laughed scornfully.
"We already called. He hung up as soon as he saw it was my son's number. You cut ties. Why bother them now?"

Chloe leaned against the wall for support, trembling all over, barely able to speak.
She said to the son, "Then give me the phone. I'll call my brother..."

The son avoided her gaze.
"Stop making trouble. The doctor said no phones in your condition."

He decided firmly.
"Mom, go check her out. I'm taking her home."

Chloe tried to escape, but she was too weak and collapsed to the floor.
With her last bit of strength, she grabbed Isabella's ankle, begging faintly for help.
"Please... call my brother for me..."

Isabella, heading to the second floor, was closer to the hallway than Evelyn.
Instead of sympathy, she pulled her foot away with disgust!

She rolled her eyes in contempt.
"What are you doing? You cut off your family for a man. You deserve this for being lovesick. You brought this on yourself!"

Seeing she wouldn't interfere, the mother and son relaxed.

The son picked Chloe up.
"Chloe Roland, your parents don't want you anymore. I'm your only guardian now. You have to listen to me!"

Chloe felt despair...
She knew going home with them meant death...

But she didn't want to die.
She knew she was wrong.
She wanted to see her brother, her parents...

Tears of regret streamed down as Chloe closed her eyes.

Just as she thought her life was over, a cool voice spoke.
"Wait."

Chloe opened her eyes to see a beautiful figure emerge from the shadows of the stairwell.

The woman had a cold, stunning face and walked directly to her.
"Phone number."

Chloe burst into tears!

The mother yelled, "What are you doing? Mind your own business!"

The son added, "It's no use calling. No one will answer."

Evelyn kept her eyes on Chloe.
"The number."

Chloe weakly recited a string of digits.

Evelyn took out her phone and dialed.

The call was answered quickly.
A young man's cool voice came through.
"Hello, this is Ryan Roland."

Evelyn said immediately, "Chloe Roland is seriously ill. She needs surgery right away."

The voice on the other end became panicked.
The sound of hurried footsteps followed as the man asked anxiously, "Where is she?"

Evelyn gave him the hospital's name.

The man replied instantly, "I'm actually in San Francisco on business. I can be there in half an hour!"

"Okay."

Hanging up, Evelyn looked at the mother and son, her tone firm.
"No one takes her anywhere until her brother arrives."

Isabella snorted dismissively.
"Meddling in others' affairs. Be careful her family blames you when they get here!"
###Chapter 46

Chloe Roland's eyes were red-rimmed.

Her voice trembled. "My brother…"

"He said he'll be here in half an hour."

A sour, churning ache filled Chloe's chest.

She had met and fallen in love with her husband on their university campus.

At graduation, her parents and brother had firmly opposed the marriage.

Her husband had knelt, swearing vows of eternal love.

She had resolutely followed him to San Francisco.

For the past two years, her family had called countless times, urging her to come home.

She had stubbornly insisted on proving her choice was right.

Her parents fell silent. Her brother made one demand.

Conceal her family background. A two-year test.

She was the Roland family's precious daughter, cherished since childhood.

After moving to San Francisco, her mother-in-law taught her how to manage a household.

Every time she called her mother to say she had learned to cook and clean,

Her mother would choke up and cry.

She said Chloe had been pampered her whole life, not meant for such chores.

But Chloe accepted it willingly.

She just had to endure two years, and then her family would be reunited.

She never imagined she would nearly lose her life here.

All over fifty thousand dollars…

Only now did she have a sudden, painful awakening.

Her parents' words had been wise.

She had degraded herself!

Chloe forced her eyes wide open.

She needed to remember her savior's face!!

Her husband's grating voice cut through the air. "You think helping her will benefit you? Dream on! Her family is dirt poor. They won't pay even if they come! As for you, they'll just think you're a meddler!"

Rage made Chloe's vision darken.

She pleaded desperately. "My brother won't abandon me… Honey, my family has money… I'll give you the house, just let me go…"

"Your house?" Her husband's face twisted viciously. "That's marital property! Besides, you were the one who insisted on eloping. It must be because your family didn't love you, right? You haven't contacted them in two years. Why would your brother care about you now?"

She had insisted on eloping?

Blinding fury overwhelmed Chloe. She couldn't catch her breath and fell into complete unconsciousness!

Evelyn Ross saw her breathing stop and immediately stepped forward. "She needs resuscitation!"

"No resuscitation!" Her husband jumped back. "My wife said she didn't want to die ugly! She chose a natural death! No resuscitation! Mind your own business!"

Evelyn clenched her fists.

This man was utterly despicable!

Her mother-in-law rushed forward, blocking the way. "Everyone, be the judge! My daughter-in-law's last wish was to die at home! This person is stopping us!"

Her loud voice drew a crowd.

The mother-in-law wailed dramatically. "Daughter-in-law, your fate is so bitter! If not for this meddler, you wouldn't have to die in the hospital!"

Evelyn knew arguing would only waste precious time.

She charged at the husband, reaching for Chloe!

The husband tried to dodge, but Evelyn swiftly dislocated his arm.

Chloe fell into Evelyn's arms and was laid flat on the floor.

CPR began immediately!

The Head Nurse arrived at the commotion. "What's going on here?"

The mother-in-law sobbed. "Head Nurse, we've given up on resuscitation. This stranger is forcing her way…"

The husband, sweating from pain, cried out. "I'm her legal guardian! She said before she died she didn't want resuscitation! She wanted to die with dignity! Who does this woman think she is?"

The Head Nurse frowned, stepping closer. "Miss, you…"

Evelyn didn't stop her compressions. "She doesn't want to die. I contacted her brother. He's on his way."

The Head Nurse said immediately, "The brother is also family, a legal guardian. If he doesn't give up on resuscitation, it can continue!"

The husband and mother-in-law were stunned.

Isabella Ashcroft spoke softly. "Evelyn, don't force things. Even if she lives, the surgery is fifty thousand. The follow-up rehab is a bottomless pit. The deceased and her husband have given up. You had to call her brother. Every family has its difficulties. You can't morally blackmail others…"

The husband had a sudden realization and cried out. "Our family has no money! My brother-in-law's family has no money either! The surgery is fifty thousand! Monthly treatment after that is six thousand… My wife didn't want to burden us and her family… It's my fault! I'm useless! I can only watch my wife die! Waaa!"

The man's crying was moving.

Countless patients in the hospital chose to give up treatment.

The surrounding crowd began to criticize.

"This young girl is too naive. She doesn't understand life's hardships…"

"All passion, delaying the burial…"

"Isn't this desecration of a corpse?"

The mother-in-law shrieked. "Everyone, pull her away! Security! Someone is violating a corpse here!"

Security moved to step forward. Evelyn looked up sharply.

Her fierce gaze swept over the crowd. "You are all aiding murder!!"

Security paused.

The mother-in-law seized the chance, rushing up and grabbing Evelyn's hair. "Let go of my daughter-in-law! Don't touch her body!"

She absolutely could not let them save Chloe!

Scalp screaming in pain, Evelyn gritted her teeth. CPR was at a critical moment.

She endured, continuing the chest compressions.

The mother-in-law pinched her, leaving bruises, but Evelyn ignored it completely…

Isabella sneered.

Evelyn was truly foolish.

This mother and son were trouble. She had brought big trouble on herself!

Downstairs at the inpatient building, several luxury cars screeched to a halt.

Before they fully stopped, a tall figure leaped out.

Ryan Roland's expression was icy cold as he charged upstairs with black-suited bodyguards!
###Chapter 47

Ryan Roland rushed to the second floor.

He immediately saw his sister lying on the floor.

Chloe Roland's face was deathly pale.

Her eyes were tightly shut.

A girl knelt beside her, performing CPR.

Ryan's heart clenched.

This was a hospital.

Why weren't they using medical equipment?

The question flashed through his mind.

Then he saw an old woman trying to pull the rescuing girl away.

The old woman yanked the girl's hair.

Her voice was shrill. "Stop! My daughter-in-law has no money for treatment! Let her pass peacefully!"

Chloe's husband stood nearby.

He looked anguished. "The surgery costs five hundred thousand. Follow-up care is sixty thousand a month... I can't afford it... Her brother's family is struggling too..."

Ryan's face darkened instantly.

A terrifying aura radiated from him.

His sister had lived a life of luxury since childhood.

A single piece of her jewelry cost more than five hundred thousand.

Now this family was driving her to her death?

He strode toward Chloe.

The old woman noticed Chloe's fingers twitching.

She was clearly being revived.

Enraged, the old woman clawed at the girl's face.

Her sharp nails aimed with vicious intent.

Evelyn Ross felt Chloe's heartbeat return.

She also sensed the old woman's malice.

She turned her head sharply.

She was about to grab the old woman's wrist.

"Thud—"

A man suddenly appeared.

He kicked the old woman squarely in the chest.

She was sent flying.

She landed heavily on the floor.

Clutching her chest, she couldn't speak.

Her husband pointed at the man and yelled. "Who are you? Bullying us poor people? I'll call the police!"

Ryan's eyes were icy.

He kicked the husband away too.

He ordered his bodyguards. "Teach them a lesson. Call the police. Charge them with attempted murder."

He knelt beside Chloe.

His voice trembled. "Chloe, your brother is here..."

Evelyn spoke up. "Her heartbeat is back. She needs surgery immediately."

Ryan looked up. "Thank you."

He had already arranged for doctors.

He picked up Chloe and quickly left.

Police arrived soon after.

They confirmed the mother-in-law and husband were suspects in attempted murder.

Both were arrested.

Bystanders gave Evelyn thumbs-up.

Evelyn finished giving her statement and prepared to leave.

Isabella Ashcroft still stood not far away.

She frowned.

That "brother" had an imposing presence.

She should have made that call herself.

Evelyn stole all the credit!

Her tone was sarcastic. "Evelyn, your ambition is so transparent. You only saved her because you realized she was important. Too bad her brother didn't even acknowledge you!"

After the taunt, she turned toward Mrs. Ashcroft's room.

Evelyn watched her retreating figure.

She thought of how Isabella had twisted the truth earlier.

Now she only spoke of benefits.

Evelyn found her utterly malicious.

Of course Chloe's brother would prioritize saving his sister.

Evelyn hadn't saved her for a reward.

She touched her scalp.

She winced in pain.

The old woman had pulled too hard.

She smoothed her disheveled hair.

She sighed.

She texted Alexander Vance that she was going home.

…

Alexander waited in the hospital room.

The memory in the car still made his heart race.

He frowned, suppressing the flutter.

His eyes kept drifting to the door.

The girl hadn't returned.

Was she also embarrassed?

He frowned slightly.

Also?

Half an hour later, he received a WhatsApp.

Evelyn said she had something to do and was staying home.

Alexander felt relieved.

But then he frowned again.

What could she possibly have to do?

…

Evelyn took a taxi home.

She used a lot of conditioner after her shower.

Her hair finally became manageable.

Seeing the fallen strands, she felt a pang of regret.

She ate fried liver for dinner to replenish iron.

Then she opened her laptop to work overtime.

The night passed.

Evelyn arrived at the company early the next morning.

She ran into Jason Lawrence at the entrance.

His dark circles were lighter.

Seeing her get out of a taxi, he visibly relaxed.

"I kept thinking last night. Something felt off. My cousin sent you home. Do you live together? Good thing you didn't arrive together this morning."

Evelyn stayed silent.

They usually did arrive together.

But she didn't explain.

Jason glanced at her.

He said haughtily, "I know why my cousin looked so upset yesterday. He's trying to collaborate with the Roland family. But Ryan Roland doesn't get along with him. He's coming to the company today to discuss business. My cousin must have a headache."

Evelyn paused.

Ryan Roland? The brother of that girl from yesterday?

She didn't notice the company entrance.

Ryan Roland was following a secretary into Vance Group.

His sister's surgery had been very successful.

She woke up this morning and saw their parents.

The three of them embraced and cried.

His sister urgently asked about the savior's whereabouts.

The savior had already left.

Ryan promised to find the savior and thank her properly.

He had witnessed how desperately that girl fought.

The Roland family owed her a life.

But what did that girl do?

Where did she work?

As he pondered, he spotted Evelyn waiting for the elevator.
###Chapter 48

Ryan Roland paused mid-step.

Yesterday's brief glimpse at the hospital showed his savior with disheveled hair, her features blurred.

But this girl's eyes and brows resembled hers too closely!

Ryan quickened his pace, wanting to confirm.

The elevator doors opened at that moment.

Evelyn Ross and Jason Lawrence stepped inside.

He arrived just as the metal doors slid shut.

What a pity.

Ryan felt no urgency.

His savior had left contact information during last night's call.

After concluding negotiations with the Vance Group, he would dial that number.

Then he would discuss with his family how to properly express their gratitude.

...

Evelyn didn't notice Ryan.

She walked with Jason into the R&D Team One office.

The atmosphere felt heavy.

Team members frowned at their screens, sighing in frustration.

"The proposal is due Friday. Vice President Julian is clearly setting us up!"

"This is an impossible task."

"Even the team leader went home to rest yesterday. He's probably given up."

"What about Evelyn? Maybe she has a solution?"

Daniel Clementson immediately responded, "Don't put too much pressure on her. However talented she is, she can't compare to Dr. Nan. Age and experience matter..."

Everyone nodded in agreement.

Just then, Evelyn and Jason entered.

The team swarmed them, buzzing with questions.

"Team Leader, did you meet Dr. Nan at yesterday's gathering?"

Jason scoffed, "If Dr. Nan showed up, people would have devoured him on the spot!"

"What about James? Did he reveal Dr. Nan's current research focus?"

"Not exactly." Jason suddenly looked at Evelyn. "But Evelyn knows James well. She might ask him privately."

"What?! Evelyn knows James Klein? Then she indirectly knows Dr. Nan too?"

Dozens of eager eyes turned to Evelyn.

"Evelyn, if there's a chance, could you introduce us to Dr. Nan someday?"

Evelyn's peach-blossom eyes curved as she scanned their hopeful faces.

A slight smile touched her lips. "Sure. Maybe we can even work together someday."

The group burst into laughter.

"Working together is too much to hope for!"

"Just meeting Dr. Nan in person would be enough!"

"If we could get some guidance from him, it would be incredible!"

...

Amid the laughter, Jason's sharp command sent everyone back to their workstations.

Evelyn carried her laptop toward her private office.

Jason stopped her at the door. "Don't push yourself too hard. This project is beyond Team One's capabilities. Management knows this. Julian can't disappoint everyone."

Evelyn smiled reassuringly. "Don't worry. Trust me."

Julian stood outside the office, glaring at Evelyn.

The girl possessed stunning beauty, her smile radiating confidence.

Just like during university, as if no challenge could ever defeat her.

But why was she so confident?

No one except Dr. Nan could solve this project in such a short time!

He turned and walked to the break area, pulling out his phone to call Isabella.

"Can you get Dr. Nan's solution by tomorrow or not?"

Isabella's voice wavered, trying to stall. "Julian, Dr. Nan is abroad and extremely busy lately..."

Julian cut her off impatiently. "If we delay any longer, Evelyn's team might solve it!"

Isabella panicked. "I'll try to hurry him."

Julian narrowed his eyes. "Isabella, you'd better not be lying to me."

"Of course not! I'll contact Dr. Nan right now."

The call ended.

Isabella paced anxiously.

She had been using Sophia's email to contact Dr. Nan.

But the replies were cold, stating he was traveling and not to be disturbed!

She couldn't deliver the solution tomorrow.

If Julian discovered she had lost her value, he might call off the engagement...

This couldn't happen!

Her eyes hardened as she rushed into the hospital room to face Sophia.

"Mom, do you know anyone from the New York Roland family? Julian wants to make a connection."

Sophia's condition had stabilized, and she was due for discharge tomorrow.

She sat propped against the headboard, reading. Her fingers trembled slightly.

She looked up, expression neutral. "No, I don't."

A dark glint flashed in Isabella's eyes.

Sophia's reaction clearly indicated she did know them! But she refused to help!

How selfish! She didn't deserve to be a mother!

Unlike Linda, who had always indulged her since childhood!

If Sophia wouldn't help, what could she do?

Isabella was completely panicked.

...

Evelyn didn't tackle the project alone.

The entire R&D Team One collaborated closely.

By the end of the workday, the solution was largely formed, needing only final polishing.

Evelyn carried her laptop to the underground garage.

Alexander's Bentley waited in its usual spot, but he was nowhere to be seen.

The driver explained, "Mr. Vance has an important negotiation tonight. He asked me to take you home first."

Evelyn nodded. "Alright."

Alexander did not return that night.

The next day.

Evelyn entered the company, energized, carrying the meticulously prepared solution.

Just inside the R&D department, she saw Julian leaning against the break area counter, holding a coffee.

He was clearly waiting for her.

"Evelyn, a friendly reminder. Today is the final deadline. If you can't submit the solution by end of day, I win the bet! You'll be held accountable for your arrogance."

Evelyn sighed inwardly.

Did this man have nothing better to do?

She rolled her eyes and walked straight toward her office.

Julian followed closely.

"Being with me has its perks. I could at least introduce you to Dr. Nan..."

Evelyn laughed dismissively. "Not necessary."

Julian snapped, "Don't think just because you've latched onto James Klein you're untouchable! He sees you as a plaything! If he had any real feelings for you, he would have helped you solve this problem!"

"I didn't need help."

Julian sneered, "You didn't need it? Just because people call you a genius, you think you can rival Dr. Nan?!"

"My solution is already complete."

Evelyn stopped and turned, looking at him with an amused smile.

"The question is, Vice President Julian. Did you get Dr. Nan's solution?"
###Chapter 49

Julian Evans shot to his feet. "You finished? Impossible!"

As an energy major graduate, he knew exactly how complex this problem was.

Isabella had said Dr. Nan needed time. No solution had arrived yet, and he never doubted it.

How could Evelyn Ross possibly have solved it so quickly?

Evelyn narrowed her eyes. "I'll organize it and send it to you. Then you'll see. Julian Evans, remember your promise."

She turned and walked into the first team's office.

Daniel Clementson immediately approached her. "Junior, did he use Dr. Nan to pressure you again?"

Team members gathered around.

"Evelyn, ignore him!"

"You're already incredibly skilled for your age. Don't compare yourself to Dr. Nan."

"Yes, you're better than all of us!"

Jason Lawrence emerged from his office.

Dark circles under his eyes showed he had pulled another all-nighter.

He frowned at Evelyn. "Don't worry. I worked through the night and found a direction. I'll push harder today. At least I can provide a framework for the solution..."

He had also messaged his cousin about the R&D department's situation.

Regarding the VP targeting the first team, his cousin would handle it.

Evelyn felt warmed by their concern.

She smiled. "No need. I've already finished it."

Jason waved his hand. "Leave this to me... What did you say?"

His eyes widened. "You finished it?"

The team stared at her in shock. "Evelyn, did we hear that right? Or are we just tired and hearing things?"

Daniel gave a wry smile. "Junior, stop joking!"

Evelyn chuckled. "I'll send the email now. I'll cc everyone."

She carried her laptop into the office.

The first team stood frozen, exchanging bewildered glances.

Only when email alerts chimed on their computers did they return to their desks and open their inboxes.

Evelyn had indeed sent the email.

Everyone immediately downloaded the attachment. Upon opening it, they were all stunned.

The first team office fell completely silent.

Seeing everyone focused on studying the solution, Evelyn quietly left and went to the top floor.

Her work was done. She needed to find Alexander Vance to get that neurology book back.

She hadn't forgotten her promise to make medicine for Eleanor Vance.

In the top-floor office.

Alexander Vance was reviewing documents when Thomas Watson entered. "Sir, Miss Evelyn is here."

His hand stilled, the pen scratching a line across the paper.

A spot on his back seemed to remember a soft touch, growing warm.

He pressed his lips together, his voice low. "Send her in."

When Evelyn entered, Alexander was sitting quietly.

The man didn't look up, his tone gentle. "I know about the R&D department situation. It's fine if you can't provide a solution. Company policies will protect your rights."

Evelyn laughed softly. "I'm not here about R&D. I'm here because I missed you..."

Her throat suddenly felt itchy, and she paused.

Alexander's hand jerked, ruining another freshly printed document with his signature.

His Adam's apple bobbed as he slowly looked up. He saw Evelyn clear her throat and continue, "...missed you possibly giving me that book you borrowed from the library."

Alexander Vance: "..."

He secretly breathed a sigh of relief, but a faint, inexplicable disappointment lingered.

He casually picked up the book from his desk and handed it to Evelyn. As she took it, he said calmly, "Don't pause like that when you speak next time."

Evelyn happily accepted the book, not noticing the man's unusual behavior. "Oh, thanks."

She turned and left.

Only after the door closed did Alexander lower his gaze.

He let out a soft laugh.

The girl clearly hadn't given the kiss in the car a second thought, while he had been awkward about it for two days. Really... reading too much into it.

And he had almost forgotten. Evelyn had a boyfriend.

Alexander's expression cooled, returning to its usual composure.

...

With her R&D work complete, Evelyn fully relaxed, spending the entire day researching Alzheimer's.

As quitting time approached, the quiet first team office finally stirred.

The R&D General Manager and a scowling Julian Evans walked in.

Jason Lawrence had just finished verifying Evelyn's solution, finding it flawless. He said joyfully to the General Manager, "Our group really found a treasure this time. Evelyn's solution is absolutely on par with Dr. Nan's!"

The General Manager frowned. Julian sneered, "Yes, her solution is identical to Dr. Nan's!"

Jason didn't process it at first. "Hahaha, she's actually as amazing as Dr. Nan..."

He trailed off, suddenly freezing. "What do you mean?"

"I mean she stole Dr. Nan's solution!"

Julian finished, then scoffed at Evelyn. "I received Dr. Nan's solution this afternoon. It's almost exactly the same as yours! No wonder you were so calm. You'd already decided to steal it! Did you think sending the email earlier meant the solution was yours? Evelyn, you're so naive. Did you think changing a few details would fool us? How dare you steal from Dr. Nan?"

Jason frowned. "There must be some misunderstanding. Evelyn has been working with us all this time. The solution even includes our contributions..."

Though they only accounted for twenty percent of the entire solution.

Julian immediately retorted, "What misunderstanding could there be? Two identical solutions. Are you suggesting Dr. Nan stole from Evelyn? Do you think that's possible?"

One was a respected doctor, the other a recent college graduate. Who was more credible was obvious.

Jason was speechless.

Evelyn listened quietly to their exchange.

Dr. Nan couldn't have sent Julian an email. That left only one possibility...

Someone had disguised her solution as Dr. Nan's!

Was Julian trying to use this to suppress her and the first team?

How ridiculous.

Evelyn said calmly, "Is there no third possibility?"
###Chapter 50

The General Manager looked surprised.
"A third possibility?"

Julian Evans cut in sharply.
"Evelyn Ross, stop making excuses. Admit your mistake now and the punishment might be lighter."

Jason Lawrence pressed urgently.
"What exactly is going on?"

Evelyn Ross spoke calmly.
"There's another possibility. Julian Evans never received Dr. Nan's proposal. He is framing me."

Jason Lawrence immediately looked at Julian Evans.

Julian Evans scoffed.
"You're good at shifting blame! I have solid evidence!"

He showed a phone screenshot to Jason Lawrence.
"This is the proposal Dr. Nan sent to Isabella Ashcroft last night. The timestamp is earlier than your submission record! I also have a witness!"

Evelyn's eyes darkened slightly.

So it was Isabella Ashcroft's doing.

She remembered leaving her computer briefly in the hospital room yesterday while accompanying Sophia Ashcroft for her check-up.

Did Isabella manage to crack her password?

Her fingertips tightened slightly.

Julian Evans turned to the General Manager.
"This matter must be dealt with seriously! We should involve the police if necessary! If Dr. Nan finds out someone at Vance Group stole their proposal, they will never collaborate with us again!"

The General Manager waved his hands hastily.
"Let's not rush to call the police. The admin department has already clocked out. Submit the evidence to them on Monday, and we'll handle it then."

Julian Evans gave Evelyn a meaningful look.
"Think carefully over the next two days. If you show a good attitude in admitting your fault, I might treat this as a minor misunderstanding... If you remain obstinate, don't blame me for showing no mercy!"

He left with the General Manager.

Her team from Group One gathered around her.
"Evelyn, is there some misunderstanding? Did you ask James Klein for help?"

Evelyn felt a warmth in her heart.
"I will prove my innocence."

She had considered revealing her identity directly.

But remembering how Jason Lawrence didn't believe her last time...

Her patent certificate and the company seal were both overseas with James Klein.

He was returning next Monday.

A slight smile touched Evelyn's lips.

Everyone's reaction then would be quite interesting...

After work, she entered the underground parking garage.

She got on the Bentley, still not seeing Alexander Vance.

Thomas Watson sat in the passenger seat.
"The boss is in a meeting. He asked me to take you home first."

Evelyn said calmly.
"I can take a cab."

"It's the boss's order." Thomas Watson glanced at her.
"Miss Ross, the research community despises theft and plagiarism. Don't think you can do whatever you want just because you have Mrs. Eleanor Vance and the boss's support."

Evelyn put on her headphones, blocking out his voice.

Thomas Watson fell silent.

The car arrived at the hospital.

Evelyn walked upstairs and was stopped on the second floor by William Ashcroft.
"Sophia wants to see you."

She followed him into the hospital room.

Sophia Ashcroft was preparing for discharge. Nancy Ashcroft had already packed their belongings.

Sophia Ashcroft grabbed her hand.
"Evelyn, what's this about stealing a proposal? I don't believe you would do such a thing!"

William Ashcroft sneered.
"The evidence is solid! Young Master Julian is giving the Ashcroft family face by warning you! Sophia, you trust her too much! She has Linda Lowell's blood. Nothing good can come from that!"

Sophia Ashcroft roared.
"Be quiet! Evelyn, speak!"

The concern in Sophia Ashcroft's eyes warmed Evelyn's heart.

She was about to speak when Isabella Ashcroft said.
"Evelyn, I know you want to achieve great things at Vance Group to gain Mr. Vance's approval... But you can't steal my proposal! If you wanted it, I could have given it to you!"

Evelyn sneered.
"Who exactly stole whose proposal?"

Isabella Ashcroft said.
"Are you trying to make me take the blame for you? Or are you saying it's just a coincidence that your proposal matches Dr. Nan's?"

Evelyn said calmly.
"It's not a coincidence. You stole my proposal."

"Nonsense!" William Ashcroft grabbed a broom and swung it at her.
"How dare you do something wrong and then falsely accuse your sister! I'll teach you a lesson!"

Sophia Ashcroft stepped forward.
"William, calm down!"

William Ashcroft sneered.
"Sophia, listen to her! An undergraduate dares to claim Dr. Nan's proposal as her own? Preposterous! This wretched girl isn't worth your worry!"

Sophia Ashcroft said anxiously.
"But if Vance Group involves the police, she could go to jail!"

She took out her phone.
"It will be fine as long as Dr. Nan doesn't pursue it. I'll contact Dr. Nan right now."

The dial tone of a WhatsApp call echoed through the hospital room.
###Chapter 51

Evelyn Ross pulled out her phone.

She hadn't planned to expose Isabella Ashcroft in front of Sophia Ashcroft.

Even if the truth came out on Monday, it was better than a confrontation now.

She was afraid Sophia would be too shocked.

But since Sophia initiated the call, she didn't want to keep hiding it.

Before the call connected, Isabella rushed over and ended it. "Mom, don't!"

Sophia was stunned. "Why?"

Isabella bit her lip. "Julian said not to make it public. Handle it internally at the company. If Dr. Nan finds out, the Vance Group will have to punish severely to save face. It would be harder to show leniency."

Sophia hesitated. "Is that so?"

Isabella looked at Evelyn, her eyes shifting. "Evelyn, the evidence is clear. You should admit your mistake and write an apology. I won't press charges. I'll ask Mr. Vance to plead for leniency. They will go easy on you."

William Ashcroft immediately agreed. "Sophia, hear that? She'll be fine. You worry too much…"

Evelyn lowered her gaze.

Calling back now would reveal the truth.

But could Sophia handle it?

She was such a principled person. Her only demand of her children was integrity.

Could she accept that her daughter was the villain?

Evelyn was silent for a moment. She looked at Sophia. "Mrs. Ashcroft, I'm not that foolish. Failing a project is a matter of ability. There's no need to steal plans. Think about it. Did Dr. Nan really promise technical support?"

Sophia pressed her lips together.

Isabella spoke urgently. "I called him using Mom's phone. He already agreed."

Evelyn wanted to say more. William cut her off angrily. "Evelyn Ross, you refuse to repent! After all Sophia's concern for you! Isabella isn't even pursuing this, and you still accuse your sister?"

He pointed at the door. "Get out!"

Evelyn gave Sophia one last deep look, then turned and left.

Her words should give Sophia something to think about.

By Monday, she hoped Sophia wouldn't be too upset…

Isabella followed her out.

Her eyes were evasive. "Evelyn, the Vance Group doesn't want a scandal. Resign quietly. Let this go."

Evelyn laughed softly.

Isabella wanted to sweep it under the rug.

She said calmly, "You know who stole from whom."

Isabella lifted her chin triumphantly. "Of course you stole from me. The evidence is overwhelming. You can't deny it."

Evelyn narrowed her eyes. "Nothing is perfect. Do you really think there are no flaws?"

Isabella sneered. "You want to confront Dr. Nan? Don't be naive. Something this trivial would never reach him!"

The implication was clear. Dr. Nan knew nothing. No one would bring it to him.

Evelyn was stuck with the blame.

If she weren't Dr. Nan herself, she would truly be powerless.

Evelyn smiled. "Isabella, walk too many dark paths, and you'll meet a ghost."

She turned and walked away.

Isabella's smile vanished, replaced by pure malice.

…

Alexander Vance was in a meeting.

It ended at eight in the evening.

He left the conference room and saw Jason Lawrence lurking nearby.

Jason immediately approached. "Cousin, I need a favor."

Alexander kept walking toward the elevator. "Speak."

Jason glanced at the executives behind them. He lowered his voice and explained the R&D department situation. He pleaded, "...She just graduated. Maybe she took a wrong step trying to achieve something. No real damage was done. Can you go easy on her?"

Alexander pressed the button for the underground parking.

Jason continued. "Cousin, I've never asked you for anything. Evelyn is talented. I don't want to see her career end here…"

Alexander gave him a cold look. "You think she stole it?"

Jason was stunned.

The elevator arrived.

Alexander strode out and got into his car.

Thomas Watson watched his expression carefully. "Sir, you don't think she stole it? Did Dr. Nan steal her solution?"

Alexander glanced at him but didn't answer.

Thomas muttered, "You seem to trust her quite a bit."

Alexander leaned back in his seat.

It wasn't trust. It was her innate pride.

She wouldn't steal. She didn't need to steal.

Why was her solution the same as Dr. Nan's?

Perhaps because of… James?

He remembered Evelyn had a boyfriend. They were in a rush to get married…

Was that boyfriend James Klein?

The car arrived at the hospital.

Alexander went upstairs and ran into Evelyn.

The hallway was dimly lit. Patients in gowns looked pale and weary.

Only the girl had fair skin. Her casual walk exuded vitality. She was the only splash of color in the hospital.

Alexander's expression softened unconsciously.

They met outside Eleanor Vance's room.

Alexander didn't enter. He spoke lightly. "Do you need help?"

Evelyn paused. She smiled faintly. "No."

Alexander's voice turned cold. "You don't need my help. You'll ask James? I overstepped."

Evelyn nodded. "I do need his help. He can better prove my identity."

Alexander was taken aback. "Your identity?"
###Chapter 52

Alexander's eyes darkened.

What was her relationship with him?

James' girlfriend?

In a flash of insight, he pieced it together.

Evelyn couldn't have stolen the solution.

It was too brilliant. Jason had said only Dr. Nan could complete something like that so quickly.

So...

Her solution came from James.

But James hadn't informed Dr. Nan. Otherwise, Dr. Nan wouldn't have sent another copy to Julian, causing this misunderstanding.

The business world is a battlefield. Why would James hoard Dr. Nan's solution? Did Dr. Nan know? Would he really clear Evelyn's name?

Alexander asked in a low voice, "Are you sure he'll come?"

"Of course."

"You trust him that much?"

"Yes."

James never dared to disobey Evelyn's orders.

Her matter-of-fact attitude inexplicably irritated Alexander.

This woman was such a naive romantic.

He suddenly said coldly, "Miss Ross, don't forget we are still legally married. I don't want any rumors spreading."

Evelyn was stunned.

She looked at the man in surprise, not understanding why he brought this up now.

Alexander felt even more frustrated.

He loosened his tie, his voice deep. "Please maintain appropriate boundaries during our marriage."

He turned and walked into the hospital room.

Evelyn was completely bewildered.

Did his meeting not go well today? He was so moody and speaking nonsense.

She didn't overthink it and followed him in to stay with Eleanor.

In the outer living room.

Alexander instructed Thomas, "Investigate James Klein."

"Yes, sir."

Thomas was extremely efficient. Half an hour later, he presented the information. "Sir, I've sent it to your phone."

Alexander opened the file.

James Klein, 27, Cambridge graduate. Became Dr. Nan's agent three years ago. Managed the company steadily. Now preparing for IPO.

Alexander frowned. "That's it?"

Thomas was confused. "What else do you need, sir?"

Alexander said calmly, "His personal life."

"..."

Thomas was surprised but quickly reported. "He's generous. Changes girlfriends every three months. His exes speak well of him. Currently maintains ambiguous relationships with multiple women. Not a committed type."

Thinking of Evelyn's trust in him, Alexander smiled mockingly.

He took his phone and entered the hospital room, ready to expose the truth.

Upon entering, he saw his grandmother wearing reading glasses and flipping through a dictionary. The woman was squatting on the floor playing with the dog.

The Shiba Inu wagged its tail wildly.

She laughed brightly and beautifully. Pure and stunning, like the cleanest scenery, making him reluctant to ruin it.

Alexander paused.

Should he tell her this cruel truth?

He gripped his phone, hesitating.

"What's wrong?"

Eleanor suddenly looked up.

Alexander pressed his lips together and put away his phone. "Nothing."

Eleanor didn't believe him. "You've been staring at your wife for so long. Something must be wrong. Do you have something to say to her?"

Evelyn looked up at him, her gaze clear.

Alexander's ears warmed. He looked away and coughed lightly. "...Grandmother looks well. She can be discharged."

The previously energetic Eleanor immediately clutched her chest, weak. "You brat, my heart still hurts."

If she was discharged, how could she make her grandson and granddaughter-in-law live together?

She had already converted the room into two bedrooms. One for herself, one for her granddaughter-in-law. The outer living room for her grandson to sleep in.

It was a bit cramped, but they could see each other every day...

Once they developed feelings, she would take her granddaughter-in-law back to the Vance family!

Eleanor chuckled to herself, showing no sign of illness.

Alexander: "..."

Evelyn: "..."

The night passed quietly.

The next morning, Evelyn was studying neurology books when she received a call from her senior, Daniel. "Evelyn, come back to school quickly! Professor Lowell is in trouble!"

Evelyn frowned. "I'll be right there."

She stood up with a serious expression and hurried out.

She called a cab while heading downstairs.

Thomas's voice came from behind. "Miss Ross, sir asked me to drive you."

Evelyn didn't stop walking. "San Francisco University, thank you."

...

The car barely stopped when Evelyn rushed out, running straight to Professor Lowell's office.

The office door was wide open, crowded with students.

Evelyn squeezed through. Professor Lowell was red-faced, glaring at the Dean of the Energy School. He said sharply, "A student's academic record affects their entire life! How can you expel her just like that?! Last time, you bypassed me and revoked her guaranteed graduate admission based on character and background! Without a reasonable explanation today, don't think about leaving this door!"

Evelyn was slightly stunned.

She thought something had happened to Professor Lowell, but he was standing up for her.

Expulsion?

She looked at the dean. The beer-bellied dean said seriously, "I just received news. She stole Dr. Nan's solution and claimed it as her own! Such a student tarnishes the school's reputation and must be severely punished!"

Professor Lowell angrily retorted, "Impossible! Evelyn would never do such a thing!"

The dean sneered. "The internal news is absolutely true! The school is trying to hire Dr. Nan as a professor. How can we争取 Dr. Nan without punishing her? Do you know how many schools are competing for him?"

Professor Lowell suddenly understood. "So it's all for Dr. Nan! But I am her advisor. I won't sign, and you can't expel her! I will never sign this!"

The dean was furious. "Lowell, don't be unreasonable! What is one Evelyn worth? Even you, what do you have to compare with Dr. Nan?!"

Evelyn stepped forward. "Without my teacher, there would be no Dr. Nan!"
###Chapter 53

Evelyn spoke from the heart.

During her freshman year, she had found this major dull and tedious.

It wasn't until she attended Professor Lowell's class that she found her direction.

Professor Lowell was her guide.

Her words were firm and powerful, instantly silencing the office.

Both Professor Lowell and the dean turned to look at her.

The dean frowned deeply. "Nonsense!"

Just as Evelyn was about to reveal her identity, Daniel stepped in front of her. "She's right! Without pioneers like our professor, would Dr. Nan even exist?"

His voice boomed. "New energy only emerged in recent years, but our professor has researched this field for over two decades! Without his and other pioneers' foundational work, where would today's achievements be? Dean, your words were unfair! Even if Dr. Nan were here, he would respectfully call our professor a senior!"

The dean fell silent, glancing at the students by the door.

They were all graduate students mentored by Professor Lowell, each now looking indignant.

The dean cleared his throat. "My words were indeed inappropriate. But Dr. Nan's status in the industry is unshakable. That is undeniable. Dr. Nan alone can drive an entire R&D team and push the new energy field forward. So, Evelyn Ross, how could you steal his plans? It's absurd!"

Evelyn stated firmly, "I didn't."

The dean sneered. "The Vance Group has both witnesses and evidence. Your excuses are useless! Evelyn Ross, you came just in time, saving me the trouble of notifying you—you're expelled! I'll issue the announcement immediately. I won't let you tarnish the school's reputation!"

Professor Lowell was furious. "Dean, Evelyn has stated she didn't steal anything. The matter isn't fully investigated yet. Your reason for expelling her is invalid!"

The dean glanced at him. "This can't blow up. Otherwise, outsiders will think Dr. Nan can't tolerate a student. The reason for her expulsion is... disciplinary issues. Evelyn Ross, it has been verified that you were frequently late, left early, and skipped classes during your studies. Your usual performance scores are zero. Therefore, the school has decided to expel you!"

Evelyn was slightly taken aback.

They say a college experience isn't complete without skipping classes. Which college student hasn't skipped a few classes?

She did skip frequently. The专业课 content was too simple, so she simply didn't attend.

The teachers noticed she could score full marks on exams. Since usual performance didn't affect her final grades, they let it slide.

She never expected it would become the dean's weapon now...

Evelyn's eyes turned cold.

Professor Lowell trembled with anger. "You're utterly shameless! My stance remains: I won't sign this. Evelyn is my student. Without my signature, you can't expel her!"

The dean's face darkened. "Professor Lowell, you're truly stubborn. Since you won't cooperate with the school, I have no choice but to suspend you!"

Professor Lowell laughed in anger. "Suspend me then! Do you think I'm afraid?"

The dean smiled. "Professor Lowell, after your suspension, your six graduate students will have no advisor. What about their graduation defenses? Do you want them all to delay graduation?"

If graduate students fail their defenses, they must postpone graduation for a year.

Many unethical advisors deliberately hold students back, forcing them to delay year after year... In this situation, students are always the vulnerable party!

Professor Lowell froze. "You... you're despicable!"

The dean was using the graduate students to force him to sign...

The old professor, who had dedicated his life to research, didn't know how to curse. He could only stomp his feet in frustration.

His graduate students paled, looking at him anxiously.

The dean looked triumphant. "Professor Lowell, which is more important: one student or six? The choice is yours."

He pushed the expulsion form toward Professor Lowell. "Will you sign this or not?"

Professor Lowell clenched his fists.

He looked back at his graduate students...

Some had already secured jobs, waiting for their diplomas to start.

Some were engaged, planning weddings after graduation...

But he couldn't sign.

Black was black. White was white.

Just as he struggled to face his students, Evelyn walked to his side and whispered, "Are the school leaders here today?"

Professor Lowell smiled bitterly. "There's a conference in New York. The president and vice president are both there..."

Everyone who could restrain the dean was absent.

Evelyn lowered her gaze and suddenly chuckled. "Professor, go home and rest for a few days. We'll talk after they return..."

If she revealed her identity now, the dean could dismiss it as a "misunderstanding."

After all, no real harm was done.

But if the dean forced the old professor to suspend and targeted his graduate students... then revealing the truth would end the dean's career!!

Professor Lowell sighed deeply. He looked at his graduate students solemnly. "Don't worry. Even if suspended, I will appeal to the president and vice president when they return. I won't let any of you delay graduation."

Evelyn spoke. "Professor, consider it a short break. On Monday, I'll give you an answer."

The graduate students' expressions darkened.

They understood the professor's choice. He had always been fair and selfless.

But being implicated for no reason, with the instigator casually saying "take a break," left a bitter taste.

Daniel quickly pulled Evelyn aside. "Evelyn, if you don't know what to say, say less."

Evelyn: ?

She personally saw Professor Lowell home. On the way, he worried. "Evelyn, what will you do about the Vance Group? Dr. Nan's identity alone can crush you. Who will believe you? Sigh! A esteemed professor like him, why would he seize your plans?"

In Professor Lowell's view, if his genius student conflicted with Dr. Nan, Dr. Nan must be bullying the young and stealing Evelyn's research.

Unexpectedly, Evelyn smiled. "Professor, Dr. Nan didn't seize my plans."

Professor Lowell was stunned. "Then whose plans are they?"

"Mine."

Evelyn's tone was calm. "Or you could say they are Dr. Nan's."
###Chapter 54

Evelyn took Professor Lowell home and returned to the hospital.

She spent most of her time in Eleanor's room.

Alexander worked in the outer area.

Thomas stood nearby, updating him quietly. "Miss Ross was too aloof. The Campus Network is full of criticism."

He handed over his phone.

San Francisco University's Campus Network was flooded with posts:

—Why is Professor Lowell protecting such scum?

—She stole Dr. Nan's design and isn't expelled?

—Just a troublemaker!

—Don't call yourself shit by association.

…

Some criticized the theft, others her attitude:

—Professor Lowell sacrificed himself, and she didn't even thank him. Said he could just rest at home. So cold.

—Those grad students are unlucky to have a junior like her.

…

Alexander's expression darkened. He tossed the phone back. "Carlo Collodi said, when one dog barks at the village gate, the others follow without knowing why."

Thomas stayed silent.

His boss had a sharp tongue.

Calling all those students dogs.

He muttered, "Miss Ross would be upset if she saw this."

Alexander paused. "Does she check the Campus Network?"

Thomas almost rolled his eyes. "Boss, she just graduated. Students love campus gossip."

Alexander said flatly, "I don't."

Thomas sighed inwardly. Of course not. His boss was practically a recluse online.

He coughed. "Most young people are online these days."

Alexander glanced through the glass partition.

The weather had turned cooler. The girl wore a camel-colored sweater, lounging on the sofa with a book. She tucked a stray lock behind her ear, revealing a pale neck.

She read with intense focus.

Sunlight streamed from the window behind her, pooling at her feet. The Shiba Inu slept curled beside her.

A picture of serenity.

After a while, Evelyn set the book down and stretched. Her hand reached for her phone…

Alexander stood abruptly and pushed the door open.

Both women looked at him.

Eleanor adjusted her glasses. "What's wrong, dear?"

Alexander's gaze fixed on Evelyn. "Miss Ross, it's a nice day. Should we take Grandma for some sun?"

Evelyn glanced outside and nodded.

She gently nudged the dog awake and helped Eleanor up.

Killing two birds with one stone.

They found a bench in the hospital garden for Eleanor.

Evelyn stared at the lake, lost in thought.

Processing the neurology concepts she'd just read…

A deep voice sounded beside her. "Do you often go online, Miss Ross?"

Evelyn gave Alexander a puzzled look.

He wore a black shirt and tailored pants. The high-end suit emphasized his cool, noble demeanor.

He didn't look at her, his expression casual, as if making small talk.

Evelyn answered offhandedly, "Not really."

Alexander nodded.

Evelyn turned her attention to Eleanor.

After days together, she knew he was a man of few words. He probably wouldn't say more after this exchange…

Just as she thought so, he spoke again, slowly. "People are impulsive these days. Anonymity online amplifies malice. Don't pay attention to what's on the internet."

Evelyn was confused.

Her phone vibrated.

A message from Daniel: [Don't check the Campus Network, junior. The comments are nasty!]

Evelyn understood why Alexander had said that.

She set her phone down and smiled. "Don't worry, Mr. Vance. Online words can't hurt me."

Alexander paused, a flicker of discomfort crossing his face before it settled into calm. "Good. I just didn't want it affecting Grandma's mood."

Evelyn chuckled softly.

Her laughter seemed to unsettle him. He stood, his expression neutral. "I have work. I'll leave Grandma to you."

"Of course."

Back in the room, Alexander said, "Cancel my Monday morning meeting."

Thomas was surprised. "But that's a跨国 conference… Understood. Canceled."

He hesitated, studying Alexander.

Was his boss going to the admin department to defend Evelyn?

He stiffened.

Many already opposed Alexander's leadership of Vance Group. Showing favoritism now…

Thomas felt a surge of irritation.

Evelyn had caused a huge mess in just days!

Julian had secured his position in R&D thanks to Dr. Nan.

And she went and offended Dr. Nan…

She really knew how to make trouble for his boss!

If Alexander shielded her on Monday, he'd stop him even if it meant angering his boss. He couldn't let him make a mistake!

Evelyn noticed Thomas's strange look when she returned with Eleanor after their walk.

She paid it no mind.

Monday arrived quickly!

After breakfast, Evelyn prepared to leave for Vance Group. Her phone buzzed with a WhatsApp from James: [Boss, landed on time! Heading to Vance Group now!]

###Chapter 55

Evelyn Ross and Alexander Vance's Bentley stopped at the entrance of the Vance Group.

Alexander suddenly turned his head. "Will James be on time?"

Evelyn nodded. "He should be."

Alexander was silent for a moment. "I have some information about James here. I'll give it to you after today's matter is concluded."

He decided to tell her about James Klein's notorious playboy nature.

It was better to recognize it early than to fall deeply.

Evelyn glanced at him, slightly surprised. "Okay."

She pushed the car door open and walked toward the elevator bank.

Before she even got close, she overheard several colleagues whispering.

"Something big happened in R&D! Someone stole a project plan!"

"Who would be so bold? Do they want to keep their job?"

"I heard it's a new intern. Apparently, they have connections and were directly placed in Jason Lawrence's team…"

"What kind of connections?"

Someone secretly pointed upward. "Related to that person. That's why they're so brazen…"

"That person's style is so aggressive. All the executives have been avoiding him this past month. Surely he wouldn't openly protect someone like that?"

"I heard he was spoiled by the old lady since childhood. He was a little tyrant in San Francisco. Protecting an intern is nothing to him."

"The company has rules and regulations. Isn't he afraid of causing widespread anger by acting so recklessly?"

"Young and impulsive, he doesn't care about these things…"

"Isn't Julian Evans young? Look at the Dr. Nan he brought in! A top talent in the new energy industry! Compare that to the intern that person shoved in here. The difference is clear…"

"Absolutely. A tiger father doesn't produce a dog son. The elder Mr. Vance has always been strict with himself. Speaking of which, the elder Mr. Vance is down-to-earth. He worked his way up from the bottom…"

"That position should have been the elder Mr. Vance's. How did that person snatch it?"

"…"

Evelyn originally couldn't be bothered with such idle gossip.

Being at the center of the vortex, being discussed was inevitable.

But she didn't expect the topic to shift to Alexander Vance.

She narrowed her eyes.

Having navigated society since middle school, she was no workplace novice. She immediately realized someone was trying to use this incident to drag Alexander down!

She looked at the person leading the discussion… What a coincidence. She knew him.

Tyler Evans, a researcher from Team Two. Julian Evans's loyal lapdog.

Evelyn recalled the information James had found…

The eldest branch of the Vance family had always wanted to reclaim power.

Last time she went to the Vance family home, she could feel the undercurrents between the eldest branch and Alexander.

Julian was trying to use public opinion to build momentum. Even if he couldn't deal a heavy blow to Alexander, he wanted to weaken his authority within the company.

Evelyn let out a soft, derisive snort.

She took two steps forward and spoke calmly. "Discussing me is fine. But before discussing the boss, maybe check if there are any surveillance cameras around?"

Everyone looked up in alarm, scanning their surroundings.

They relaxed only after confirming there were no cameras, then realized they'd been tricked.

They all turned to look at Evelyn. "Who are you?"

Before Evelyn could answer, Tyler Evans spoke up. "Let me introduce everyone. This is the new intern in our R&D department."

Tyler gave a fake smile. "Miss Ross, as expected of someone from Mr. Vance. The evidence of stealing the plan is conclusive, yet you can remain so calm…"

Everyone immediately glared at her with anger.

Workers hated having their achievements stolen most of all.

But Evelyn's expression didn't change. She just raised an eyebrow. "Someone from Mr. Vance?"

Tyler immediately said, "Still trying to deny it? Assistant James personally took you to R&D. Our entire Team Two saw it…"

He feigned distress. "Even with Mr. Vance backing you, you can't steal Dr. Nan's plans! Do you know how important Dr. Nan is to R&D? How much favor did Vice President Julian Evans have to call in to get him here? If you drive him away, even Mr. Vance can't shoulder that responsibility!"

The people around chimed in agreement.

Evelyn suddenly laughed. "You're wrong. I'm not Mr. Vance's person. Mr. Vance has always been low-key. He didn't make a fuss about inviting me to the company…"

Tyler was stunned. "Where was I wrong…"

Just then, the elevator arrived, and everyone crowded in.

No one noticed Alexander Vance approaching in the distance with Thomas Watson,恰好 hearing the conversation.

Alexander's expression remained unchanged, but Thomas's face instantly darkened!

Sure enough, someone was trying to use this!

But not only did Evelyn not avoid the association, she actively dragged the boss into it?!

So foolish!

Thomas couldn't help but angrily say, "Boss, did you hear that? She's afraid people won't know she's your person! What good does that do her!"

He advised further, "Boss, you really can't get involved in this matter anymore! Now everyone knows her relationship with you. If you protect her now, in everyone's eyes, you'll be an unjust ruler who can't tell right from wrong!"

Alexander narrowed his eyes.

The man's cold, stern face showed no fluctuation. His deep eyes looked ahead as he spoke淡淡ly. "Only the weak are bound by public opinion."

He straightened his collar and strode toward the CEO's private elevator.

Thomas followed, hearing his resolute command. "To the Administrative Department."

"…"

He should have known the boss wouldn't change his mind once decided!

Thomas reluctantly pressed the button for the Administrative Department floor, sighing inwardly.

No wonder people compared.

R&D was the company's core, with many eyes on it.

This month, only two people had joined R&D.

One was Dr. Nan, thanks to Julian Evans's connections.

The other was Evelyn Ross, the boss's connection.

The contrast was too stark!

He took a deep breath.

If only Dr. Nan were the boss's person. This battle would be won beautifully!!

"Ding—"

The elevator arrived.

The trial regarding the stolen plans was now officially in session.
###Chapter 56

Evelyn Ross stepped out of the elevator.

She turned and headed toward Team One.

Tyler Evans's voice came from behind her. "Miss Ross, I don't know the nature of your relationship with Mr. Vance."

"But his position within the company is not secure."

"The future leadership is still uncertain."

"I advise you not to offend Vice President Evans."

He was Julian Evans's right-hand man.

After several interactions, he had already discerned Julian's intentions.

He lowered his voice. "Aligning with Vice President Evans would improve your situation significantly."

"At the very least, he has the means to resolve your current predicament."

Evelyn gave him a cool glance.

She said nothing and walked straight into the Team One office.

Monday morning should have been a typical rush to work.

Yet the entire Team One had already assembled.

Everyone gathered in the break area, their expressions grave as they looked at Jason Lawrence and Daniel Clementson.

Jason's face was tense, his eyes dark.

Seeing Evelyn enter, everyone stood up immediately, hesitating to speak.

Daniel quickly approached her. "Evelyn, about the university..."

Before he could finish, the R&D General Manager and Julian Evans appeared at the door.

"Jason Lawrence, Evelyn Ross, Administrative is waiting for us."

He looked at Evelyn. "Let's go."

Julian suddenly spoke. "I thought you would call me."

His tone was confident, not bothering to hide his words from the General Manager and Jason.

Evelyn narrowed her eyes but did not respond.

Julian continued. "Besides Administrative, there are two other guests today."

"The President of San Francisco University and the Dean of the Energy College."

"The Dean heard about you stealing Dr. Nan's proposal."

"He has decided to expel you. You were aware, correct?"

"I cannot stand by and watch the university's reputation be tarnished."

"The President and Dean are here today to confirm the facts."

"If the allegations are true, they will not be lenient!"

"You... truly refusing to yield until faced with undeniable proof!"

The four soon reached the Administrative Department.

Julian and the General Manager entered the conference room first.

Evelyn was about to follow when Jason suddenly patted her shoulder.

"I am your supervisor. Any problem is ultimately my responsibility."

"Even if Julian brings Dr. Nan here for a confrontation, I will stand in front of you."

After saying this, Jason wore the expression of a man marching to his doom.

He strode boldly into the conference room.

Evelyn was speechless.

She followed him inside, finding the conference room spacious.

About ten people were seated inside.

The Administrative staff looked serious and stern.

The University President and Dean sat on either side.

To Evelyn's surprise, Alexander Vance was also present in the observers' section.

The man wore a crisp suit, his shirt buttoned to the top with a matching tie.

He exuded an air of meticulousness and nobility.

Hearing the footsteps, he glanced over briefly.

His deep gaze lingered on Evelyn for a moment before moving away, as if she were a stranger.

Thomas Watson stood behind him, his face dark with anger.

He glared at Evelyn.

Evelyn smiled faintly at them, unbothered.

She understood perfectly well why the incredibly busy Alexander Vance was here.

It was because of her...

No, it was to prevent his grandmother from being upset.

She could imagine how the man would explain his presence.

The corner of her mouth lifted slightly as she walked calmly into the room.

Director Brown from Administrative spoke first. "Vice President Evans, you mentioned having witnesses and evidence?"

Evelyn also looked at Julian with curiosity.

She genuinely didn't know what this so-called evidence was.

Julian checked his phone. "They've just arrived."

As soon as he finished speaking, the door was knocked.

Tyler Evans led two people inside.

It was Isabella Ashcroft and Linda Lowell!

Evelyn's brow furrowed slightly.

Isabella looked composed.

She walked up to Julian and called out softly, "Julian."

Linda, however, seemed nervous and fidgety.

She glanced around anxiously, a flattering smile on her face.

Julian pointed at Linda. "This is my witness and evidence!"

"This is Evelyn Ross's mother."

Everyone was stunned, their eyes turning to Evelyn.

Jason nudged her. "Is that true?"

Evelyn clenched her jaw and did not answer.

Linda rushed to her. "Evelyn, Mom helping you steal the proposal has been exposed!"

"I didn't expect there to be a camera in the hospital room."

"It recorded everything. It's their evidence now!"

Isabella took out a USB drive and inserted it into the computer.

The screen displayed surveillance footage from Mrs. Ashcroft's hospital room.

Linda was seen sneaking into the room.

She secretly opened Isabella's laptop, inserted a USB drive, and performed some operations...

Isabella sighed. "I installed the camera only to record the doctors' medication times."

"I never expected to capture this."

"Evelyn, how could you let your mother steal for you?"

Linda quickly bowed her head in admission. "It was my fault. Evelyn knew nothing about this."

"I saw her working so hard and wanted to help her."

"This has nothing to do with her..."

"I'm sorry. She just graduated and wanted to make a good impression."

"As her mother, I couldn't bear to see her struggle."

She looked around the room.

Suddenly, she fell to her knees with a thud.

"Please, I beg you, don't punish Evelyn."

"Punish me instead. Don't call the police..."

"Give Evelyn another chance!"

Director Brown hurried forward to help her up. "Please, stand up first..."

Linda cried and shook her head. "I won't get up until the company forgives Evelyn..."

Sighs were heard around the room. "The heart of a parent is truly pitiable..."

Julian looked at Evelyn arrogantly. "The evidence is irrefutable. Do you have anything to say?"

Isabella watched the scene with amusement.

She knew the moment Linda appeared, this imperfect evidence became perfect.

How could there possibly be a mother in this world who would frame her own daughter?

If Evelyn denied it, people would only despise her more for shirking responsibility.

The Vance Group would never tolerate such an employee.

It was a checkmate.

All eyes were fixed on Evelyn.

Yet she remained calm, standing with her head slightly bowed.

She even took out her phone and glanced at it.

A WhatsApp notification sounded.

A message from James Klein: [Boss, I'm almost there!]
###Chapter 57

Evelyn Ross set down her phone.
She looked up at the group.

She should have been devastated by her own mother's betrayal.

For Isabella Ashcroft, Linda Lowell would do anything.

What had Isabella offered Linda?
What made her so fiercely loyal?

Any defense would be useless before James Klein arrived.

"My daughter just graduated university."

Finally, she bowed her head. "No matter what, I did it!"
"Arrest me. It's all my fault..."

Director Brown's eyes held pity.

Julian looked at Linda.
He should despise this mistress.
But seeing her sacrifice everything for her daughter moved him strangely.

"Are you really going to let your mother take the blame?"

"Do you have no heart?!"

"Nor will we keep an unfilial employee."

Evelyn had expected this.
Denial would only invite more suspicion.

She gave a low, quiet laugh.
She knew it wasn't over yet.

Isabella stepped forward hypocritically.
She sighed at Linda. "Aunt Linda, trying to take the blame is useless."

She crawled on her knees to Evelyn.

"You can find another job."
"If you don't admit it, you'll lose your student status."
"Your future will be ruined!"

She feigned anguish, squeezing out a few tears.
From an angle no one else could see, her eyes were fierce.

Evelyn looked down at her kneeling mother.
She found it utterly ridiculous.

Before, Linda had been direct and forceful with her.
Demanding she give way.
Now she had learned manipulative tactics?
Taught by Isabella, no doubt.

Linda continued her tearful plea. "I'll admit it for Evelyn!"
"I'm her mother!"
"I'll kowtow and apologize to everyone!"
"Please, give her a chance!"
"Please, don't let the university expel her..."

She kowtowed, her head hitting the floor with heavy thuds.
The sound was heartbreaking to the listeners.
What a devoted mother!

Director Brown rushed to help her up but couldn't budge her.
She yelled at Evelyn. "What are you just standing there for?"
"Your mother is so sincere and honest!"
"How did she raise a daughter like you?!"

In the stands, Vice President Clark of San Francisco University showed pity.

Dean Mitchell spoke directly. "Evelyn, won't you admit your mistake?"
"Are you trying to push your mother to her death?"
"How does our university have a student like you?"
"President, you see?"
"Professor Lowell insisted on protecting her."
"He's lost his mind!"
"That's why I suspended him!"

He had come today because Professor Lowell called.
The Dean wanted to expel a student and suspend a professor.
Expulsion was serious.
The Vice President thought it excessive and came at the professor's request.

But now, it seemed the Dean might be right?
He looked at Evelyn. "Student, just admit your mistake!"
"If this continues, not even Professor Lowell can protect you!"

"Everyone wants you to admit it."
"They'll give you a chance..."

A voice called out. "Mr. James Klein is here."
###Chapter 58

The meeting room fell silent.

Evelyn: "..."

Dean Mitchell immediately said, "Don't worry, we won't protect a student who stole Dr. Nan's plans. We will definitely expel her!"

Vice President Clark tightened his jaw, growing more displeased.

Not only had Vance Group not found Evelyn guilty yet, but even if she were, expelling a student required a school meeting discussion. How could he make such a promise outright?

It felt like he was trying to curry favor.

He had no integrity.
