Dean Mitchell found this strange, as if he was being criticized. "Evelyn's mistake is too severe..."

"Oh? What mistake did she make?"

"She stole Dr. Nan's plans..."

James retorted, "Do you have evidence?"

Dean Mitchell pointed at Linda. "This is her mother. She admitted it herself. There's also surveillance footage of her mother stealing the plans from Miss Isabella's computer..."

"Is that so? Is that what Miss Isabella said?"

Isabella had been trying to downplay her role in this, but now that James had named her, she had to step forward. "That's correct."

Linda immediately understood.

She rushed to James and knelt down, kowtowing. "Mr. Klein, please forgive Evelyn. She didn't mean it..."

Julian angrily said, "Evelyn, why are you so stubborn?"

Dean Mitchell added fuel to the fire. "Miss Evelyn, how can you say that? What mother would slander her own daughter? You're talking nonsense!"

"Is that so?"

James said with a mocking smile, "But as far as I know, Dr. Nan never gave Miss Isabella any plans! So how did this kind, pure mother manage to steal them?"
###Chapter 59

James Klein finished speaking.

The conference room fell into dead silence.

He only showed James respect because of Dr. Nan.

James's confidence, Evelyn's composure...

Could it be...?

His words resonated with many.

This was too much.

Not only shielding the thief but also letting her steal credit?

"Dr. Nan, do you find my performance as your assistant satisfactory?"
###Chapter 60
The conference room was so silent you could hear a pin drop.

Everyone stared at James Klein in shock.

Their eyes darted between Evelyn Ross and James.

Julian Evans abruptly stood up.
"James, what nonsense are you spouting? Dr. Nan's identity is no joking matter!"

James smiled faintly.

He retrieved a document from his bag.

The patent certificate clearly displayed Evelyn Ross's name.

The official seal was distinctly visible.

Julian's face instantly turned pale.

He looked at Evelyn in disbelief.

The treasure he had abandoned was the real one all along.

"What exactly is going on?!"

She never imagined Evelyn was Dr. Nan.

This realization felt like plunging into an icy abyss.

She lunged at Evelyn, claws out.
"You wretched bastard! How could you possibly be Dr. Nan? Your grades were always terrible..."

Evelyn nimbly sidestepped.

Her gaze was icy.
"You know exactly why my grades were bad, don't you?"

Linda froze.

"Remember my first perfect score? Who made me kneel all night? Who forced me to turn in blank exams? Who threatened to sell me if I did well?"

Evelyn's tone was calm.

Yet every word pierced the heart.

"Controlling my score was harder than getting a perfect one. Did you know that?"

The room erupted in murmurs.

Everyone looked at Linda with contempt.

Director Brown was dumbfounded.

He never expected the mother he pitied to be so vicious.

Alexander Vance slowly rose.

His lips were pressed tight, his gaze heavy on Evelyn.

The girl spoke lightly.

But he heard the blood and tears behind her words.

He recalled Thomas Watson's investigation file.

Left home in middle school.
Worked part-time while studying.
Barely got into San Francisco University.

She never applied for aid.
Supported herself through countless jobs.

He hadn't thought much of it then.

Now his heart felt carved with a knife.

Linda showed no remorse, instead screaming curses.
"You deceitful brat! How dare you trick me! I should have made you quit school and work!"

Her face was twisted, none of her earlier pitifulness remained.

Julian's face was dark.
"Explain this to me!"

Tears streamed down Isabella's face.
"Julian, I truly didn't know... It was Aunt Linda! She did everything!"

"Yes, it was all me! Isabella knew nothing!"

She smirked at Evelyn.
"I did it to get back at you for calling the cops on me! I stole the proposal. I faked the video. What can you do? I'm your mother!"

She acted completely unafraid.

Alexander spoke coldly.
"Jason Lawrence, take Dr. Nan back to R&D."

Evelyn paused slightly.

She understood Alexander was protecting her.

She gave a slight nod and followed Jason and James out.

In the elevator, Jason kept staring blankly at Evelyn.

Even back in the office, he hadn't snapped out of it.

The team immediately crowded around.

"Team Lead, what happened?"

"Is Evelyn alright?"

Daniel Clementson was frantic.
"Don't worry, we'll all go plead with Dr. Nan..."

Amid the chatter, Jason finally found his voice.

He pointed at Evelyn, stammering.
"You... you're really... Dr. Nan?!"
###Chapter 61

The entire R&D Team One was stunned.

Jason Lawrence's words made their brains short-circuit.

"Team Lead, are you running a fever?"

"Dr. Nan is here? Where?"

"Evelyn is Dr. Nan? Stop joking…"

"She's so young, how could she possibly…"

Voices of doubt rose one after another.

Evelyn Ross calmly looked at Jason Lawrence.

She extended her hand.

"Let me reintroduce myself. I am Dr. Nan."

The air instantly solidified.

Daniel Clementson was the first to jump up.

"Junior?! Say that again?!"

Everyone froze in place.

Evelyn's gaze swept over the group.

These past few days, she had felt a level of support she'd never experienced before.

Misunderstandings were quickly cleared up.

The subsequent unwavering support moved her even more.

The corners of her lips lifted slightly.

"Thank you all for your care. Feel free to come to me with any technical issues in the future."

Since her identity was exposed, she couldn't stay any longer.

She had obtained the medical materials. It was time to leave.

While everyone was still in shock, she took two steps back.

She turned and left with James Klein.

Jason instinctively took a step to follow.

Then he stopped abruptly.

He suddenly felt annoyed.

His reaction upon learning she was Dr. Nan had been terrible!

Would it leave a bad impression?

He turned back and saw his team members, each one dumbfounded.

Their expressions grew more exaggerated by the second.

It wasn't until Evelyn entered the elevator that everyone suddenly snapped out of it.

"Ahhh! Evelyn is Dr. Nan!"

"I worked with Dr. Nan!"

"She said we can ask her for help! We're practically friends!"

The group suddenly rushed towards Evelyn's workstation.

They scrambled to grab the things she left behind.

"Dr. Nan's pen!"

"Dr. Nan's draft paper!"

"Dr. Nan's paper cup…"

Team One instantly descended into madness.

Jason watched them and, for once, didn't scold anyone.

Compared to this crowd, he seemed perfectly normal.

…

Administrative Department conference room.

The moment Evelyn left, Alexander Vance gave a direct order.

"Call the police."

Linda Lowell's face instantly turned pale.

She had just been out of prison for two days!

Director Brown hesitated.

"Mr. Vance, calling the police could damage the company's reputation. We weren't planning to make this a big issue…"

Even if Evelyn's guilt was confirmed, it would have been handled internally.

Alexander shot her a cold glance.

Director Brown immediately lowered her head.

"Yes."

Linda hurriedly cried out.

"Mr. Vance, is it illegal for me to steal from my own daughter? The victim isn't even pressing charges, why call the police?"

Alexander's gaze was icy.

"You stole Vance Group's core technology. This involves theft of commercial secrets. She may not pursue it, but the company will."

Linda was stunned.

"Commercial secrets? Could I go to jail?"

Alexander's voice was low.

"According to Section 219 of the Criminal Code, serious cases carry a sentence of three to ten years."

Linda was dumbfounded.

"It's that serious?"

Director Brown gave Mr. Vance a strange look.

This incident hadn't caused any actual losses. Was Mr. Vance trying to scare her?

Alexander continued.

"If you were instructed or deceived, your sentence could be reduced. So, was someone诱导 you to steal?"

Everyone looked at Isabella Ashcroft.

They weren't fools.

How would a fifty-year-old, disconnected from society, understand new energy?

Clearly, Isabella was behind it.

Director Brown understood.

Any normal person would try to implicate Isabella to avoid blame…

But then she heard Linda shout.

"No! Isabella didn't know! I did it all! It has nothing to do with her!"

Isabella's eyes reddened as she questioned.

"Aunt Linda, if you hate Evelyn and want revenge, don't drag me into this!"

She lowered her head to wipe her tears, but a sharp glint flashed in her eyes.

She had prepared a backup plan.

She had only made suggestive remarks the entire time.

Linda didn't even know the whole story about her background…

She had simply picked Linda up from prison and "unintentionally" vented her frustrations on the way.

She detailed the bet between Evelyn and Julian Evans.

She ended apologetically.

"Aunt Linda, why am I telling you all this? I'm just upset. Dr. Nan isn't responding. Evelyn's proposal is ready… Julian might call off the engagement. Forget it, let's just accept our fate."

Linda immediately took it upon herself to steal the proposal.

She found a way to transfer it to Isabella's computer.

Isabella wasn't involved at all!

When the police arrived, as long as Linda didn't implicate her, she would be fine.

Sure enough, the police only took Linda away.

But Isabella didn't relax.

What she was truly worried about was Julian!

Julian's face was ashen.

Even though Isabella wasn't taken away, everyone understood what had happened.

The look Director Brown gave him was full of suspicion.

She thought he and Isabella were in cahoots…

He couldn't defend himself!

Furious, Julian turned and left.

Isabella hurriedly chased after him.

"Julian, let me explain…"

After they left, Alexander watched their retreating figures.

His gaze was profound.

Why was Linda so protective of Isabella?

He suddenly said to Thomas Watson.

"Isabella seems more like Linda's daughter."
###Chapter 62

Thomas Watson looked dazed, still in shock.

He didn't understand research, but he knew what Dr. Nan meant to the R&D team.

He picked up his phone to check the company's internal forum posts.

The situation had indeed reversed!

Those who had been cursing Alexander Vance for favoring the beautiful intern were now changing their tune:

— Shocked! Dr. Nan is the beautiful intern?

— So it was Mr. Vance who invited Dr. Nan to the company, not Vice President Julian Evans?

— Still calling him Vice President? Isn't he embarrassed? Didn't he get promoted to VP because he recommended Dr. Nan for technical support?

— Ahh, I just asked R&D Team One. Dr. Nan has already left. Why did Vice President Evans do this? If he hadn't slandered the intern, Dr. Nan would still be helping anonymously, right?

Thomas was very satisfied, almost wanting to kneel in admiration for Evelyn Ross.

Thomas:!!

Julian smiled. "I'm Julian Evans. I heard from my uncle that our families are collaborating."

"You... are Ryan Roland?"

She hadn't expected the girl's brother from the hospital to be Ryan Roland?!

Ryan didn't recognize her and asked politely, "And you are?"

He seemed approachable, but his words held distance.

Isabella's eyes flickered. She smiled. "Have you forgotten, Mr. Roland? San Francisco General Hospital. Your sister. The phone call..."

Ryan was stunned. "You're the one who called... our benefactor?"

Ryan finally looked at Julian seriously. "Young Mr. Evans, what a coincidence."

His gaze swept over Isabella. He suddenly said, "I'm discussing business with Mr. Vance. Would you care to join us, young Mr. Evans?"

Ryan knew about the Vance family situation and hadn't planned to get involved with the main branch.

But if Julian was the fiancé of his sister's benefactor... he had to help.

After all, the Roland family owed their benefactor a life!

Julian was flattered.

Without Dr. Nan, his position in R&D was awkward.

The Rolands were a powerful family from New York. Ryan personally inviting him to participate in the collaboration would significantly boost his standing in the company!

He glanced at Isabella and patted her hand meaningfully. "Isabella, I'll go handle this. I'll take you ring shopping tomorrow."

Isabella breathed a sigh of relief. "Okay."

She was determined to marry Julian!

After Ryan walked away, he took out his phone. He found the saved number of their benefactor and sent a text: [Miss Xu, when are you free? My sister and I would like to invite you and your fiancé for a casual meal.]

…

Evelyn's phone buzzed while she was being stopped in the lobby by the Vice President and Dean of San Francisco University.

She glanced at it and saw a message from a familiar number.

Sensitive to numbers, she quickly recalled it belonged to "Chloe's" brother.

Evelyn also wanted to know what happened after that day. She replied: [This weekend.]

After sending it, she realized something was wrong. Since when did she have a fiancé?

"Dr. Nan, you graduated from San Francisco University. You understand our teaching philosophy. We sincerely invite you to return as a faculty member..."

The Vice President was earnest. "Please consider it."

Evelyn looked up, tired of pleasantries. She said directly, "I was planning to accept, as Professor Lowell mentored me. But now he's suspended, and I heard his graduate students are being treated unfairly."

The Dean's face stiffened. "Dr. Nan, that's a misunderstanding... I didn't know you were Dr. Nan..."

The Vice President immediately cut him off. "Quiet! Even an ordinary student shouldn't be expelled without a formal verdict! Dr. Nan, we will deal strictly with those responsible. As for Professor Lowell... He has nurtured so many outstanding talents over the years. I think he is very suitable for the position of Dean of the New Energy Science Institute. What do you think?"

Evelyn was very satisfied!

She quickly reached an agreement with the Vice President.

Isabella arrived downstairs and saw this scene of harmonious conversation.

She clenched her fists tightly.

Watching the Vice President's respectful attitude toward Dr. Nan, intense jealousy surged within her!!

After some discussion, the Vice President and Dean left.

James Klein had gone to get the car. Evelyn waited by the entrance.

Isabella strode over, unable to hide her spite and disgust. "Evelyn, do you know why I hate you?"

Evelyn ignored her.

Isabella continued, "I heard from Aunt Linda that your computer password is my mother's birthday?"

Evelyn slowly lowered her gaze.

So Linda knew her so well. That's how she could easily access her computer.

Isabella stepped closer, her voice accusatory and venomous. "Don't you have your own mother? Why use my mother's birthday as your password? Don't think I don't know your little schemes toward my mother. Coveting someone else's mother, how disgusting are you?"

She said each word clearly. "Have you fantasized about taking my place? Dream on! My mother will always be mine. She has nothing to do with you! Stop your wishful thinking!!"

Evelyn stood frozen. A wave of panic suddenly washed over her.

Her dark, hidden thoughts were exposed. For a moment, she felt utterly ashamed.

…

Evelyn sat in James's sports car. The cold wind tousled her hair but couldn't disperse her thoughts.

She had always envied Isabella for having such a gentle, kind mother.

As a child, she无数次 dreamed of being Isabella. Mrs. Ashcroft combing her hair, telling stories. She nestled in Mrs. Ashcroft's arms, greedily clinging to her warmth...

She carefully restrained and suppressed her longing for Mrs. Ashcroft. After leaving the Ashcroft home, she never saw her again. Yet, like some obsessed person, she used those passwords.

Watching the roadside scenery recede, she suddenly smiled bitterly. She picked up her phone, opened the password change settings, and entered a new password: 1234.

When the screen showed the change was successful, she felt an emptiness in her heart.

She shouldn't crave Mrs. Ashcroft's motherly love. She didn't deserve to disrupt her life...

Her eyes stung slightly.

Evelyn tilted her head back slightly, hiding the moisture at the corners of her eyes.

Just then, James's phone rang.

He answered. A voice came through: "James, about that secret regarding the boss's uncle, Leo Lowell, that she wanted investigated... I found something!"
###Chapter 63

###Chapter 64

…

###Chapter 66

Eleanor Vance overheard their conversation and looked at them curiously.

Evelyn helped Eleanor sit down to eat.

The three ate in silence.

Eleanor drank half a bowl of porridge before putting down her chopsticks.
"Can you cancel the cooperation with the Rolands?"

Evelyn looked at Alexander.

The man kept his eyes lowered, his long lashes hiding his emotions, making him unreadable.

Thomas immediately stood up.
"Miss Ross, shall we check with the doctor about Mrs. Vance's condition?"

Evelyn understood this was a request to clear the room.

She picked up her half-eaten sandwich and was about to stand when Eleanor stopped her.
"My granddaughter-in-law doesn't need to leave. You should know about these matters eventually."

Thomas looked at Alexander.

The man frowned slightly but ultimately said nothing.

Thomas quietly exited the room, closing the door and standing guard outside.

Evelyn felt awkward, thinking she shouldn't be listening to family matters.

Eleanor spoke up.
"I've never told you about this rascal's mother, have I?"

Evelyn glanced cautiously at Alexander.

The man continued eating his bread slowly, not objecting.

She asked softly, "Is Mr. Vance's mother related to the Rolands?"

Eleanor sighed.
"Not to the Rolands, but to Mrs. Roland. This rascal's mother was Mrs. Roland's older sister."

She began recounting the past.
"Back then, the Vance family wanted a marriage alliance with the Sherwoods of New York. My useless son was divorced and in his forties when he married a Sherwood girl in her twenties. After she became pregnant, she discovered his ex-wife had borne him a son, secretly raised until his teens—this rascal's older brother."

Evelyn sorted out the relationships.

This Miss Sherwood was Alexander's mother.

Alexander's father, Robert Vance, had a son with his ex-wife: Richard Evans, Julian's father.

Richard and Alexander were half-brothers, which explained their hostile relationship.

Eleanor continued.
"The Sherwood girl marrying a man over a decade older was a business alliance from the start. She was furious upon discovering the deception. She had a fierce temperament and chose a cesarean section at seven months pregnant. She gave birth to this rascal, then divorced and returned to the Sherwoods. Since then, the Sherwoods held a grudge against the Vances, cutting off all contact."

Eleanor's expression darkened.

Evelyn looked at Alexander.

Whether his father had known was impossible to verify.

But how could his mother have a cesarean at seven months and abandon her child at the Vances?

Even if she wanted a divorce, couldn't she have waited two more months to give birth?

At seven months, he was already a living being!

Eleanor had mentioned Alexander weighed only three pounds two ounces at birth.
He spent three months in the ICU, covered in tubes, critically ill multiple times, nearly dying.
That was why he was given the humble nickname "Puppy"…

So that was why.

Remembering Alexander's words yesterday—"Some people don't deserve to be mothers. Just treat them as strangers"—Evelyn took a deep breath, her chest tight.

So Alexander was like her, abandoned by their birth mothers.

She looked at the man.
He still kept his eyes down, drinking milk, his handsome profile cold and hard as iron.
He seemed to be listening to someone else's story, detached yet fragile.

Evelyn's heart ached.
She suddenly wanted to hug him…

Eleanor sighed.
"I heard Mrs. Roland was very close to this rascal's birth mother.
That's why the Rolands have held a grudge against the Vances all these years…
Everyone has their own立场, and no one is wrong.
You and that Roland boy have never gotten along. Why force yourself? Is this cooperation absolutely necessary?"

Alexander set down his milk glass, elegantly wiped his mouth, and lifted his cool, distant eyes.
"The business world only cares about利益. Past grievances are irrelevant."

He stood up.
"Don't worry, Grandma. I won't be at a disadvantage."

…

Evelyn followed Alexander toward the rehabilitation department.

They were stopped at the entrance.

Ryan Roland's secretary looked apologetic.
"Mr. Vance, Miss Roland's condition is unstable. It's not convenient for visitors right now."

Thomas immediately flared up.
"Mr. Vance coming here is a courtesy to the Rolands. Do you really think we're afraid of you?"

The secretary hurriedly explained.
"She truly isn't well…"

Thomas wanted to say more, but Alexander stopped him.
"Let's go."

The three turned back.

Thomas was furious.
"This is too much! Julian Evans was allowed to visit yesterday, but they stop us today?
If this gets out, people will think they can look down on you, boss!"

Alexander said calmly,
"Ryan Roland isn't the type to posture. His sister probably really isn't well."

Thomas fell silent.

Alexander headed to the company, leaving with Thomas in the parking lot.

Evelyn sighed quietly and prepared to return to the old lady's room.

Her phone suddenly rang.

She picked it up and saw a text from Chloe Roland's brother.
["Miss Ross, can you come to the hospital? Chloe's condition is bad. She insists on seeing you."]

Evelyn was slightly stunned.

Chloe's brother… Was his name Ryan Roland?
###Chapter 67

Evelyn stared at her phone screen, a cold smirk playing on her lips.

Her memory had always been exceptional.

That day when she called Chloe's brother, he had introduced himself as Ryan Roland.

She hadn't thought much of it then, but now all the clues connected, revealing the truth.

The Mr. Roland collaborating with the Vance Group was indeed Ryan, and his sister was Chloe.

…The world was so small.

Her fingers lightly tapped the screen: [On my way.]

…

Inside Chloe's hospital room, the atmosphere was tense as a drawn bowstring.

Ryan looked at the reply on his phone, his expression darkening as he turned toward the bed.

Chloe was curled up under the white sheets, her face as pale as paper.

Mr. and Mrs. Roland were frantic with worry. "Chloe, how will your body recover without rehabilitation? Please be reasonable."

Chloe bit her lip firmly. "I won't get treatment unless I see Miss Evelyn!"

Ryan's brow furrowed deeply.

His sister had been overly protected since childhood, developing a willful temper.

Otherwise, she would never have eloped back then.

She was naive and pure-hearted. She had to be extremely careful about who she befriended.

Someone like her ex-husband could never be allowed to happen again.

Ryan was absolutely determined not to allow her to continue associating with that "Miss Evelyn."

Mr. Roland, his heart aching for his daughter, looked at his son. "Have you contacted Miss Evelyn?"

Forced to send the text, Ryan's face was grim. "I have."

Mr. Roland asked urgently, "Is she coming?"

Chloe's eyes instantly lit up with hope.

Ryan took a deep breath. "Why would she miss an opportunity to climb the social ladder? She said she's on her way!"

Chloe's spirits lifted immediately.

Mr. Roland sighed in relief. Seeing his son's displeased expression, he chastised his daughter first. "Chloe, you're being too willful! Your brother has never misjudged anyone. Would your family ever harm you?"

Mrs. Roland also spoke up. "Chloe, you've gone too far this time. The Vances and our family have a complicated history. If it weren't for this project requiring cooperation, we wouldn't want anything to do with them. The way the Vances treated your aunt back then… And now we have to help the main branch of the Vance family. What kind of situation is this?"

She paused. "No matter how flawed Alexander Vance is, he is your aunt's own son. Now, to repay a debt of gratitude, we're helping the main branch. Your brother is upset about it too. These debts of favor ultimately fall on him to repay!"

But Chloe remained firm. "Miss Evelyn isn't that kind of person! Maybe it was just a coincidence that Brother mentioned introducing a fiancé?"

Her parents and brother didn't understand.

The feeling of being saved at the brink of death was like a divine intervention.

She had been betrayed and was on the verge of collapse.

Her parents could offer companionship, but there was ultimately a generation gap.

She also had her own pride.

She didn't want her parents to see her vulnerability; it would only remind her of her past mistakes…

She needed to confide in someone, to share her pain with someone she trusted.

Right now, the only person she trusted completely was her savior.

Listening to her naive words, Ryan's frown deepened. "The business world is a battlefield. I've seen too many people. I can see right through her intentions. That calculating demeanor is utterly vulgar!"

Isabella's ambition had been practically written on her face that day.

Chloe became agitated. "You're wrong! My savior isn't that kind of person! Brother, I hate it when you speak ill of her!"

Mr. Roland hurriedly tried to mediate. "Alright, your brother only has your best interests at heart. Didn't we invite her here for you?"

Mrs. Roland also fell silent. Their daughter's health was the most important thing.

Forced by his parents, Ryan was filled with anger. He turned and strode out of the room.

Mrs. Roland called after him, "Where are you going?"

"Work."

Ryan left with large strides. If he stayed any longer, he feared he would be infuriated to death by his sister!

He had just intercepted Alexander Vance's visit. He needed to go explain in person.

That man was not someone to be trifled with.

The cooperation with the Vance Group was mutually beneficial. Ryan had only agreed because he insisted Julian Evans manage the project, and Alexander had conceded.

But Alexander would never agree to excessive demands.

The two were at odds due to past grievances, but they had a tacit understanding regarding the project.

His face somber, Ryan walked quickly toward the elevator.

"Ding."

The elevator doors opened, and a striking woman stepped out.

Despite his foul mood, Ryan couldn't help but glance at her twice.

This glance made him pause.

This girl… She looked somewhat familiar?

The night Chloe was saved, her savior's hair had been disheveled, obscuring her face. He hadn't gotten a clear look, only a vague impression.

But after Isabella claimed the credit, he had subconsciously superimposed that image onto Isabella.

Now, seeing Evelyn again, he only found her familiar and didn't think much of it.

They passed each other.

Ryan entered the elevator and went downstairs.

Evelyn glanced at him but said nothing.

Thinking of how he had helped Julian secure a position within the Vance Group, a feeling of dislike stirred within her.

Evelyn entered the hospital room.

Seeing her, Chloe immediately broke into a smile.

They chatted for a while. Finally, Chloe agreed to undergo her rehabilitation training.

Mr. and Mrs. Roland observed Evelyn from the side, whispering to each other.

"Miss Evelyn's eyes seem clear. She doesn't look like someone who would cling to the powerful and rich?"

"I think so too. Could Ryan have misjudged her?"

"Impossible. Our son has never misjudged anyone before. Perhaps she's just deeply hidden…"

Just then, Evelyn's phone rang.

It was a call from the police station. They informed her that Chloe's husband and mother-in-law were suspected of attempted murder and required her cooperation in giving a statement.

Evelyn responded succinctly, "Okay, I'll head over now."

At the same time.

Ryan, who had just left the hospital, also received a call from the police station. "Mr. Roland, we require your assistance with some procedures regarding your sister's case. Are you available to come in?"

Ryan's gaze turned icy. "I'm on my way."
###Chapter 68

Isabella's phone rang.

She glanced at the caller ID.

It was the police station.

As one of the witnesses in Chloe Roland's case, she had been prepared to cooperate with the investigation.

Especially after discovering the special status of Chloe's brother, she didn't dare to be negligent.

During her statement that day, she honestly disclosed every detail.

Now the police needed her to confirm her testimony again, and she was happy to comply.

After all, this could not only help testify for Chloe but also put Ryan Roland in her debt…

Thinking of this, a smile curled at the corner of Isabella's lips.

She answered the call cheerfully and readily agreed to the police's request.

After hanging up, she was about to leave when her phone rang again.

Seeing the name on the screen, Isabella's face instantly darkened.

It was Leo Lowell.

She took a deep breath and reluctantly answered. "What is it now?"

Leo's anxious voice came through. "Niece, you have to save me this time! If I don't pay up, they're going to kill me!"

Isabella gripped her phone tightly, her tone icy. "How much this time?"

"Five million."

"Five million?!" Isabella's voice shot up. "Are you insane?"

Although the Ashcroft family was well-off, since middle school, her monthly allowance had only been one hundred and fifty thousand.

The family's liquid assets totaled less than twenty million.

She had just given Leo two million a few days ago, and now he was asking for five million?

Leo was completely unfazed. "Niece, just tell me if you're giving it or not. If not, I'll have to go to Evelyn Ross…"

Isabella's pupils constricted.

"You have two days. If the money isn't in my account, don't blame me for revealing your and Evelyn's true backgrounds. After all, survival comes first, right?"

Leo hung up without waiting for a reply.

Furious, Isabella threw her phone to the ground!

She stood still for a long time, trying to calm down.

Five million was not a small amount. She could only ask her family for it.

At this thought, Isabella hesitated.

Although Evelyn hadn't exposed her for stealing the email, Mrs. Ashcroft clearly had her suspicions.

Since yesterday, Mrs. Ashcroft's attitude toward her had cooled significantly.

Isabella bit her lip and walked to Mrs. Ashcroft's room.

Just outside the door, she overheard a conversation.

Mrs. Ashcroft asked, "Where's Linda? I haven't seen her for days."

William Ashcroft replied, "Why bother about her? She probably went back to her brother's. It's best if that woman never comes back. I can't stand the sight of her."

Isabella's eyes flickered at the door.

She steeled herself, her eyes reddening, and walked into the room with a sob. "Mom, Dad."

Mrs. Ashcroft saw her and made to rise but settled back on the sofa.

William frowned. "Isabella, have you upset your mother again?"

Isabella lowered her head and cried. "Mom, I know I was wrong. I shouldn't have framed Evelyn. I just… I just saw how well you treated her, and I was jealous."

She cried with genuine emotion. "Evelyn is so accomplished now. She's Dr. Nan and helped arrange your hospital room. I'm not as good as her. I'm so afraid… afraid you'll like her more than me…"

Mrs. Ashcroft's expression softened.

She walked over to Isabella and sighed. "Silly child, why are you so narrow-minded? It's because of this that Evelyn never came home all these years. She never tried to take me away from you, and I could never like her more than you. Don't think like that anymore."

Isabella sniffled. "Really?"

Mrs. Ashcroft said helplessly, "You're all grown up. Would I lie to you?"

Isabella smiled through her tears. "Then say it, Mom. Say you love me the most."

Mrs. Ashcroft smiled indulgently. "Alright, I love you the most."

Isabella's smile became more genuine, but then Mrs. Ashcroft added, "But don't keep treating Evelyn as your rival anymore!"

Her smile froze, a shadow flashing in her eyes.

Isabella lowered her head in apology. "I won't. I really know I was wrong."

After placating Mrs. Ashcroft, Isabella finally said, "Mom, Julian said he's taking me to buy an engagement ring today. I want to get him a gift too."

Mrs. Ashcroft nodded. "That's the right thing to do. We can't always take advantage of others."

After Isabella started working, her monthly allowance increased to three hundred thousand.

She lived at home and had few expenses.

Mrs. Ashcroft thought she had at least ten million in savings and didn't catch the subtext.

Isabella looked embarrassed, bit her lip, and said directly, "My expenses have been high since I've been with Julian. I'm a bit short on cash right now…"

Mrs. Ashcroft finally understood. "I have a little over three hundred thousand left. I'll transfer it to you later."

Isabella was taken aback. "Only three hundred thousand?"

Mrs. Ashcroft explained, "I gave the rest to Evelyn. She said she wanted to buy stocks…"

Before Isabella could speak, William expressed his displeasure. "Buy stocks? The market is so bad right now. Who would enter at a time like this? This is nonsense! What if she loses it all?"

Mrs. Ashcroft waved her hand. "She said a friend gave her a tip. She won't lose. Even if she does, consider the money a gift to her. Over the years, she's generated hundreds of millions in profit for the company. Giving her over ten million is too little."

William frowned deeply, clearly very unhappy.

Isabella only felt panicked.

There was no way she could get five million in such a short time…

Distracted, she went downstairs and got into Julian's car.

Julian was in high spirits. "Isabella, my father was thrilled to hear you saved Mr. Roland's sister. He transferred me some money this morning to take you shopping for nice jewelry. It's a gift from him!"

Isabella guiltily averted her eyes. "Let's go to the police station first. I need to give my statement for Mr. Roland's sister's case."

Julian smiled. "Alright, I'll take you."

When they arrived at the police station, they happened to run into Ryan Roland in the parking lot.

Seeing Isabella, Ryan understood she was there to testify. His resentment toward her lessened. "Miss Ashcroft, thank you for coming."

Isabella smiled. "It's my pleasure."

After greeting each other, the three headed into the police station.

But at the entrance, they coincidentally met Evelyn, who was just leaving.

Evelyn had been the first to arrive at the police station.

She recounted the events of that day from beginning to end, confirmed her statement was accurate, and signed it.

An officer personally saw her out and was speaking to her. "Officer Ross, your quick action in saving the victim deserves an official commendation from our department. We will also issue a public notice to recognize your bravery."
###Chapter 69

Evelyn immediately shook her head upon hearing about the public announcement.
"No need for an announcement."

"It's necessary," the officer explained with a smile.
"Positive stories like this need publicity to encourage more people to act bravely."
"It's part of our job."

Evelyn nodded reluctantly.
"Alright."

They walked to the entrance and encountered Ryan Roland and Isabella Ashcroft.

They were explaining their purpose at the reception desk.
The officer there spotted Evelyn's companion and called out, "Officer Chen, these two are here about Chloe Roland's case."
"Please escort them inside."

Ryan and Isabella turned at the sound.

Isabella's eyes turned cold upon seeing Evelyn.

Ryan, however, caught the key word: also?
This woman was also here about his sister's case?

Evelyn gave them a brief, indifferent glance.
She had no interest in engaging and walked straight out.

Officer Chen addressed Ryan.
"Please wait a moment."
"I'll see this lady out and then take you in."

Ryan nodded, his gaze thoughtful as he looked at Isabella.
"Was this young lady also present when my sister had her accident?"

Julian Evans also turned to Isabella.

He already knew how Isabella and Ryan had met.
If Evelyn had also saved Chloe, how could he win Ryan over?

A cold glint flashed in Isabella's eyes, but she forced a bitter smile.
"Mr. Roland, that's my sister."
"We were indeed together at the time..."
"Miss Roland asked her for help first, but she has a cold personality."
"It wasn't intentional."
"You know how she is, Julian."

Julian frowned.
"She's always been heartless and avoids getting involved."

Isabella sighed.
"She even stopped me from calling for help."
"She said people who are lovesick deserve to die..."

She suddenly covered her mouth, feigning panic.
"Please don't take it to heart, Mr. Roland."
"I'm sure Miss Roland didn't mean it..."

Ryan's face darkened instantly.

He remembered Chloe waking up after surgery.
Her first words were, "Brother, should someone as foolish as me even be alive?"

He had thought his sister was heartbroken over love.
But she had been insulted by someone else?

Julian clenched his fists.
"She is terrifyingly rational."
"She never showed interest in any of her many suitors in college."
"In her eyes, love is probably just foolishness."

His chest felt tight.
Did she see him that way too?

Julian suddenly turned to Isabella.
"You go ahead with the statement."
"I'll wait outside."

He turned and hurried out after Evelyn.

Ryan watched him leave, deep in thought.

Isabella asked casually, "When are you returning to New York, Mr. Roland?"
"How is Miss Roland doing?"
"New York's medical facilities are better than San Francisco's, right?"

Ryan withdrew his gaze and replied calmly.
"We sign the contract today."
"I'll take Chloe back to New York for recovery in a day or two."
"I won't trouble you further, Miss Ashcroft."

Isabella was too calculating.
He wanted his sister as far away from her as possible.

Isabella breathed a silent sigh of relief.
As long as Ryan left San Francisco soon, he would never learn the truth.

Outside the police station.

Evelyn stood by the roadside waiting for her ride.

The light dimmed beside her as Julian's voice reached her.
"Evelyn, I never expected you to be Dr. Nan."
"But do you think talent alone guarantees success in business?"
"The business world relies on connections."
"Ability is secondary..."

Evelyn gave him a cold glance and ignored him.

Julian felt a pang in his chest.
In college, she was always this aloof with everyone else, but warm towards him.
Now that coldness was directed at him.

Anger and embarrassment surged within him.
"Evelyn, you're always so cold-hearted."
"No wonder you miss out on great opportunities!"

Evelyn was baffled.
What opportunity had she missed?

Her ride arrived.

She ignored Julian and got into the car, leaving without a word.

Julian watched the car disappear, knowing he had lost her for good.

But he didn't regret it.
Even if she was Dr. Nan, he could never marry a love child!

After mentally justifying his actions, Julian finally calmed down.

Isabella and Ryan emerged after giving their statements.
He immediately approached them.

Ryan said coolly, "Sorry for taking up your date time."

Isabella smiled.
"You're too kind, Mr. Roland."
"Where are you headed next?"

Ryan checked his watch.
"I have a signing appointment with Mr. Vance."
"Once the contract is finalized, our cooperation officially begins."

Julian quickly interjected.
"It's a pity my position isn't high enough."
"Otherwise, I would be the one signing with you today!"

Isabella smiled at Ryan.
"Hasn't Julian been promoted yet?"
"A Vice President's title isn't quite suitable for discussing cooperation with you, Mr. Roland."

Julian sighed.
"Uncle said he'd promote me after the contract is signed."
"But he's very busy."
"I'm afraid he might forget."

Isabella said to Ryan, "Mr. Roland, could you remind Mr. Vance during the signing?"

Ryan pressed his lips together.
A seasoned player in the business world, he understood their intention perfectly.

Julian had contributed nothing to this cooperation.
He was being pushed forward solely by Ryan's influence.
The original plan was for Julian to gradually rise to department head.

He hadn't expected them to be so impatient, trying to climb over him!

Ryan took a deep breath and gave Isabella a meaningful look.
"Fine."

After parting ways with them, Ryan headed to the Vance Group.

The cooperation between the two companies was a major event.
The signing ceremony was very formal.

In the spacious conference room, they sat on opposite sides of the table.

Alexander Vance signed the documents and handed them to Ryan, only to find Ryan's copy remained unsigned.

Alexander's gaze sharpened.
"Do you have questions about the contract, Mr. Roland?"

"The contract is fine," Ryan said calmly.
"But this project was supposed to be handled by young Mr. Evans."
"His position still seems to be Vice President?"

Alexander's expression turned cold.
"Are you interfering with Vance Group's personnel appointments?"

"Not at all."
"I just find young Mr. Evans quite agreeable."
"I trust you'll grant me this favor."

Ryan made his stance clear: no promotion, no signing.
###Chapter 70

It was her!

Thomas Watson's face was pale with anger.

How could Julian Evans become General Manager out of nowhere?

He had contributed nothing!

But Thomas knew this personnel change was insignificant to the Vance Group during this critical cooperation phase.

Under the gaze of all the executives, his boss couldn't refuse.

It was utterly humiliating!

What was Ryan Roland trying to do?

If the Sherwoods wanted to hold a grudge, they should target the main branch of the Vance family.

Why were they solely targeting his boss?

Alexander Vance's expression was unreadable.

He never showed his emotions, merely glancing coolly at Ryan. "If I refuse, will you terminate the contract, Mr. Roland?"

The meeting room fell silent instantly.

Alexander's demeanor remained unchanged.

While many Vance executives would pressure him, the Roland team was also present.

If the cooperation collapsed due to Ryan's unreasonable demands, Ryan would be equally responsible.

This was a test of patience.

Whoever yielded first would lose.

After a long moment, Alexander remained composed and unruffled.

Ryan finally softened his tone. "You misunderstand, Mr. Vance. This was merely a personal suggestion."

The cold aura around Alexander lessened slightly. "Sign first. Julian Evans will handle subsequent cooperation."

With actual achievements, Julian's promotion would be justified.

This was the concession Alexander offered Ryan.

Two major corporations clashed, each taking a step back.

Ryan nodded and signed.

The seals were affixed, making it official.

He stood and extended his hand. "A pleasant cooperation, Mr. Vance."

Alexander met his eyes coldly, his displeasure evident.

Ryan waved, dismissing everyone else.

Only the two of them remained in the meeting room.

He looked at Alexander. "This wasn't my aunt's intention. I owe Isabella Ashcroft a favor. I had to secure this for Julian."

Alexander stood. "No explanation is necessary. She has nothing to do with me."

Ryan frowned. "My sister was in danger at the hospital. Isabella protected her and notified me. A life-saving debt must be repaid."

Alexander's eyes turned colder. He left directly.

Thomas followed closely behind. "Sir, the celebration banquet is scheduled for tomorrow evening. Ryan Roland will attend. His parents are also in San Francisco and will be present."

A flicker of emotion passed through Alexander's eyes.

Ryan's mother... was that woman's sister. His biological aunt by blood.

Thomas asked further, "Shall your companion be chosen from the secretarial pool, or shall we invite Miss Ross?"

Alexander replied flatly, "She dislikes such occasions. It's unnecessary."

He meant he would attend without a female companion.

Thomas was stunned.

In the past, his boss would have scolded him for the impertinent question.

Now, he was considering Miss Ross's preferences?

Had his attitude changed?

Evelyn Ross noticed no change.

Alexander returned from work still grim-faced.

He didn't even glance at the Shiba Inu, "Kitty," who wagged its tail to greet him.

Evelyn kept her head down, focused on finalizing her drug research.

These past few days, she had thoroughly studied the neurology materials borrowed from the Vance Group.

A clinical drug for Alzheimer's was on the verge of a breakthrough.

Seeing Evelyn not going to work, Eleanor Vance grew anxious.

She had hoped her grandson would take his wife to build rapport—watch concerts or dine out...

But seeing his expression, she couldn't help but complain. "Must be work troubles."

She asked Thomas, "Encountered difficulties?"

Thomas seized the moment while Alexander was washing his hands. "Ryan Roland has been giving the boss a hard time. He even insisted on promoting young Mr. Evans..."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

Had Thomas forgotten that Eleanor was also Julian's great-grandmother?

He said it right to her face...

Unexpectedly, Eleanor pursed her lips. "What connection do the Rolands have with Julian Evans? It makes no sense! Doesn't Julian know the Rolands have old grievances with his uncle? Logically, the Rolands should resent the main branch!"

She stood firmly on Alexander's side without hesitation.

Thomas snorted coldly. "Who knows what's gotten into the Rolands? They ignore their actual relatives and side with the main branch!"

Eleanor pressed her lips together. "Didn't you visit the patient today? Didn't it help ease relations? Did Mrs. Roland show any reaction upon seeing her nephew?"

Thomas sighed. "Didn't see her. They said the patient's condition was poor."

Eleanor was displeased. "She should see him especially if her cousin is critically ill. It boils down to distance. With family, breaking the ice is what matters."

The elderly woman saw things clearly.

Her son had wronged Alexander's mother back then. The Sherwoods' resentment was understandable.

She used to resent the Sherwoods, thinking Alexander's mother was too cruel for the cesarean section that nearly cost him his life.

But now...

The Vance family lacked warmth.

Her health was declining.

If the Sherwoods could extend an olive branch, it might fill the void of family affection for her grandson...

She had hoped the cooperation would bring Alexander closer to the Rolands!

How did it turn out like this?

Eleanor worried deeply. "What do the Rolands truly want?"

Evelyn suddenly spoke. "Grandmother, perhaps I could make an introduction?"

After all, she was Chloe Roland's benefactor.

Mr. and Mrs. Roland were kind people.

Surely relations could be eased...

Alexander emerged from washing his hands just in time to hear this.

His gaze turned icy, then puzzled. "You know the Rolands?"

Evelyn nodded. "I helped with a minor matter."

A minor matter...

Alexander suddenly understood. "You saved his sister?"

Evelyn was surprised. "How did you know?"
###Chapter 71

Eleanor Vance’s eyes lit up as she listened to their conversation.
“Dear, is that Ms. Roland you mentioned visiting later today?”

Evelyn Ross had gone to see Chloe Roland after leaving the police station.
She didn’t return home until after Chloe finished her rehabilitation session.

She had briefly explained this to Eleanor.
She was surprised the elderly woman remembered it so clearly.

Evelyn studied her, noticing her condition hadn’t worsened recently.
In fact, there were signs of improvement.

The medication needed to be developed quickly.
It would be best if she could remember the truth about her marriage to Alexander Vance.

Alexander’s lips curved slightly as he listened.
A hint of mockery flashed in his eyes.

So Ryan Roland had mistaken someone else for her.

He suddenly spoke. “Ms. Ross, there’s a celebration banquet between Vance Group and the Rolands the day after tomorrow.”
“I need a plus-one. Are you interested?”

Evelyn didn’t look up. “Not interested.”

Alexander paused, his deep eyes flickering. “Didn’t you say you wanted to introduce the two families?”

Evelyn finally looked at him.
Her peach-blossom eyes shimmered with reluctant light. “Fine then.”

Eleanor looked delighted. “You’re willing to make peace with the Rolands?”

Alexander hummed indifferently and changed the subject. “What did you have for lunch today?”
“Rice, chicken soup, and some celery…”

Thomas Watson couldn’t help stealing a glance at his boss.
His boss had never shown any intention of reconciling with his biological mother.

This cooperation was strictly business.
That was why rumors of his discord with Ryan had spread.
In truth, Ryan had extended olive branches multiple times.
His boss never responded, clearly wanting no non-business interactions.

His earlier words were just to coax Ms. Ross into being his plus-one?

Thomas wore a thoughtful expression.

After dinner, Evelyn went to walk the dog.
She put a leash on the Shiba Inu and took it out.

Thomas happened to be going downstairs to retrieve documents and walked with her.

They walked side by side.
Thomas suddenly asked with a smile, “Ms. Ross, what kind of man do you like?”

Even though Ms. Ross had a boyfriend eager to marry, as long as she wasn’t married, his boss still had a chance!

He needed to gather information for his boss.
It would make it easier for his boss to steal her from that boyfriend!

Evelyn was slightly stunned by the question.

Having watched Linda Lowell’s love-struck persistence to stay with the Ashcrofts since childhood, she had always been averse to love and marriage.
She always rejected male advances.

She was afraid of losing herself if she fell in love.

She had never thought about what her future partner should be like…

But when Thomas asked that question, the first image that flashed in her mind was Alexander’s handsome face.

She subconsciously began speaking. “Handsome, six-foot-one, about a hundred and sixty-five pounds, deep and pleasant voice, thoughtful, especially good at scolding people…”

Thomas’s eyes grew brighter.
You might as well just say his social security number!

So Ms. Ross and the boss were mutually attracted?

Evelyn noticed his expression and instantly caught herself.
She abruptly changed course, the “terse and cold” on her tongue becoming: “…kind and warm, tells good jokes, a real sweetheart!”

Thomas: ???

A sweetheart? That was so not his boss!

He was gloomy all day, famously known as a human air conditioner!

Evelyn saw his expression change and breathed a sigh of relief, her cheeks slightly warm.
Annoyed by the slow dog, she bent down, picked it up, and quickly headed downstairs.

Thomas sighed, thinking his boss didn’t stand a chance.

He turned to go back to the hospital room and froze.

Alexander was standing right behind him!

The corridor lights were dim, casting half his face in shadow.
He looked somewhat lonely.

He had clearly heard the entire conversation between him and Ms. Ross!

Thomas coughed and scratched his head sheepishly. “Well, boss, you could try smiling more at Ms. Ross…”
“…Too much talk.”

Alexander chastised him, walked past, and quickly went downstairs.

It was dark outside.
Under the streetlights, the girl was squatting, petting the dog.

Her head was bowed.
Her expression towards the Shiba Inu was uncharacteristically gentle, lacking its usual sharpness.

Alexander walked over slowly until he stood before her.

The moment he approached, the girl looked up.

The corner of Alexander’s mouth twitched as he attempted what he thought was a gentle smile.

Then he saw Evelyn stand up and glance at him cautiously. “Mr. Vance, is there something wrong with your mouth?”

Alexander: “…”

He slowly retracted his smile.
Seeing the girl visibly relax, a few black lines seemed to form on his forehead.
He took a deep breath. “Grandmother asked me to accompany you on your walk.”
“Alright.”

The clumsy Shiba Inu ran towards the park, the two following closely behind.

A cold wind blew, making Evelyn shiver.

The temperature had dropped suddenly today, and she wasn’t prepared.

She was rubbing her arms, thinking it was about time to go back upstairs, when a large, warm coat was suddenly draped over her shoulders.

Evelyn’s body stiffened.
She slowly turned her head to see Alexander standing there in his shirt, looking away, his expression slightly awkward.

The corners of Evelyn’s eyes curved. “Thank you.”
“You’re welcome.” Alexander’s tone was flat. “I just don’t want you catching a cold and passing it to Grandmother.”

Evelyn chuckled silently.

This man’s mouth was the hardest part of him.

They were standing there awkwardly when a voice called out. “Mr. Vance?”

Evelyn turned and saw Ryan Roland standing in the park.
His face darkened when he saw her.

Evelyn: ?

Alexander frowned and stepped in front of Evelyn. “Can I help you?”
“A word in private.”

Ryan gave Evelyn another deep look before walking to the side.

He looked very upset.

He had just returned to the hospital room and made some discreet inquiries.
His sister was indeed deeply affected by that passerby’s comment during her first plea for help: “Go die, you love-struck fool.”
During her surgery, she had even lost the will to live because of it!

So he was furious!

Once Alexander approached, Ryan asked with a frown, “What is your relationship with this Ms. Ross?”
“That’s none of your business, Mr. Roland.”

Ryan took a deep breath. “Regardless, you are my aunt’s son. Seeing that you have an unusual relationship with her, I feel compelled to offer some advice.”
“You’d better open your eyes. This woman is no good!”
“Our families have no connection, Mr. Roland. There’s no need for you to concern yourself with my affairs.”

Alexander’s voice grew several degrees colder.
He enunciated each word clearly. “Besides, I believe it is you who needs to open your eyes.”

Ryan was taken aback. “What do you mean?”
###Chapter 72

Alexander Vance turned to leave.

Ryan Roland frowned. "Alexander Vance, don't be ungrateful..."

Before he could finish, Alexander spun around.

The man's eyes were sinister, his expression dark and menacing like a demon from hell.

Ryan's words caught in his throat.

Alexander stared at him, his voice low. "Mr. Roland, we are merely business partners."

He turned and walked away.

Ryan stood frozen, his face tense.

Throughout this collaboration, Alexander had always acted like a refined gentleman.

It made Ryan almost forget this man was once notorious for his ruthlessness.

That look just now made him realize the rumors were true.

Ryan watched him.

The man's menacing aura gradually dissipated as he approached Evelyn Ross.

Returning to her side, he became that normal, albeit somewhat aloof, person again.

...

Evelyn wore the man's jacket.

The suit's inner lining carried his body warmth, enveloping her completely.

A faint scent of vanilla reached her.

Her heart softened.

Seeing him wearing only a shirt, she picked up the Shiba Inu. "Let's go back."

"Okay."

The next morning.

Alexander went to work.

Evelyn sat on the sofa, basking in the sunlight.

She had finally completed the final stages of her Alzheimer's research.

After sending the email to the overseas base, she stood up and stretched.

Picking up her phone, she saw a message from Chloe Roland: [Are you free tomorrow night? Can you come keep me company?]

Evelyn replied: [I have to attend the Vance and Roland celebration banquet.]

Just after sending the message, James Klein called. "Boss, there's a problem with the project."

Evelyn's voice was calm. "Go on."

James said angrily, "Our hydrogen energy materials have always been supplied by the Roland family. Today, they suddenly stopped selling to us! We're short on raw materials, and finding another supplier last minute takes time... We signed contracts with clients. This will cause a breach!"

Evelyn frowned. "The New York Roland family?"

Her company was managed by James; she only handled the technology.

She didn't realize they also had dealings with the Roland family.

James replied, "Yes, them. The sales manager said someone higher up explicitly ordered not to sell to us!"

Evelyn lowered her gaze. "Understood. I'll look into it."

Hanging up, she headed towards the rehabilitation department.

She was friends with Chloe and had a good relationship with Mr. and Mrs. Roland.

She planned to ask them directly, to see if someone was causing trouble.

Just as she reached the inpatient department, she ran into Ryan Roland.

He seemed to have just returned and was about to go upstairs.

Evelyn quickened her pace. "Mr. Roland, hello."

Ryan stopped. His gentle expression turned cold upon seeing her.

Evelyn asked, puzzled, "I'm Dr. Nan. My company has been purchasing hydrogen energy materials from your company, but supply was suddenly cut off this month. Is there some misunderstanding?"

"There is no misunderstanding."

Ryan's expression was icy, his eyes filled with disgust. "I gave the order not to sell to you."

Evelyn was taken aback. "Why?"

Ryan frowned. "Have you forgotten what you said so quickly, Miss Ross?"

Evelyn was baffled.

Had she said something wrong while accompanying Chloe?

She had no recollection.

Besides, Chloe was just texting her...

Evelyn said directly, "Even if I said something wrong, Chloe probably doesn't mind. Considering I've helped Chloe before, could you please make an exception?"

The words "small favor" made Ryan think she meant testifying.

He scolded, "How dare you mention Chloe? Testifying is every citizen's duty!"

Evelyn's voice turned cold. "Mr. Roland, nothing is an absolute 'should'."

Ryan grew angrier. "You're right, Miss Ross. So the Roland family isn't obligated to sell materials to you either."

Evelyn took a deep breath.

During their first meeting, she thought Chloe's brother seemed nice.

She didn't expect him to be so cold and unforgiving.

She wanted to say more, but Ryan had already strode into the rehabilitation department.

His assistant blocked the entrance. "Miss, please don't cause a disturbance. You'll disturb the patients."

Furious, Evelyn turned and left.

Fine, she would talk to Mr. and Mrs. Roland at the banquet tomorrow!

Time quickly passed until the next evening.

The new project between Vance Group and the Roland family involved billions, worth celebrating.

They held a large celebration banquet at The Ritz-Carlton.

Many Vance Group employees attended, and some Roland family members flew in from New York.

At the hospital.

Chloe put on her evening gown and was carried to her wheelchair by Ryan.

Mr. Roland was amused. "Don't you hate these kinds of events the most? Why are you so insistent on going today?"

Mrs. Roland also tried to persuade her. "Don't go. I'll stay here with you."

Chloe looked listless, staring at her phone. "Since yesterday, my savior's replies have been distant. I asked her to keep me company, but she made excuses. I have to find her at the banquet! Let's go quickly."

Ryan wheeled her to the parking lot.

Just as he was about to help her into the car, a stretched Bentley drove past.

The Roland family turned and saw Evelyn and Alexander sitting in the back seat.

Chloe's eyes lit up. She quickly called out, "Savior!"
###Chapter 73

Chloe Roland finished speaking.

Her parents followed her gaze.

A delicate profile was faintly visible through the Bentley's window.

Mr. Roland squinted. "Is that Miss Xu?"

Mrs. Roland nodded. "I think so too."

Ryan Roland frowned slightly.

The only person who could be in Alexander Vance's car was Evelyn Ross.

He looked at Isabella Ashcroft. "You said that was your benefactor?"

"Of course it's my benefactor!" Chloe said eagerly. "Hurry and catch up! I want to enter with my benefactor!"

Ryan pressed his lips together.

Could it really be Isabella in that car?

The Roland motorcade accelerated, but only saw the Bentley's taillights disappear into a private entrance.

Chloe anxiously tapped her wheelchair armrest. "Why can't we go in?"

Ryan reassured her. "Don't worry. We'll see them at the party."

…

Penthouse suite at The Ritz-Carlton.

Evelyn followed Alexander into the room and suddenly paused.

This route didn't seem to lead to the ballroom. It felt more like...

"Where are we going?" she asked softly.

"The suite," Alexander replied calmly.

Evelyn's heart skipped a beat. "Why are we going to the suite?"

Thomas Watson answered eagerly. "Miss Ross, to change into your gown and get ready, of course! The hospital conditions were limited. The CEO specially prepared this suite."

Evelyn's ears grew warm.

So she had misunderstood.

Alexander glanced at her, a faint smile playing on his lips.

Evelyn looked away, her cheeks flushing.

She was being presumptuous.

They had agreed to divorce. How could she have such a misconception?

Thomas continued probing. "What did you think we were going to do, Miss Ross?"

Evelyn lowered her head to hide her embarrassment.

Alexander smoothly intervened. "Is the stylist here? How are the gown preparations?"

"Everything is ready. Miss Ross can choose from over a dozen evening gowns..."

Evelyn secretly breathed a sigh of relief.

Inside the presidential suite, the stylist asked respectfully, "Which gown do you prefer, Miss Ross?"

A dazzling array of gowns filled the room, every style imaginable.

Evelyn had never attended a formal gala and wasn't familiar with these things.

But she calmly countered, "What would you suggest?"

The stylist selected a dark green silk gown. "This one complements your气质 perfectly, Miss Ross. The elegant cut will surely stun everyone."

Evelyn nodded. "This one, then."

She went into the bedroom to change and emerged moments later.

Alexander looked up, his breath catching slightly.

The emerald silk perfectly outlined her graceful curves.

Her back was slender, her waist narrow, her collarbones delicate.

The skirt swayed gently with her steps, her black hair cascading over her shoulders.

The outfit should have been alluring, yet it carried a wild, untamed beauty.

Alexander's Adam's apple moved. He couldn't look away.

Evelyn felt her ears grow hot under his gaze but feigned composure. "What do you think, Mr. Vance?"

"Not bad."

Alexander took a sip of water to suppress the restlessness in his heart.

Evelyn walked toward the full-length mirror, but her high heel suddenly twisted.

Alexander swiftly steadied her by the waist.

The warmth of his palm seeped through the thin fabric, making her step back hastily.

The air suddenly grew charged with tension.

Evelyn kicked off the high heels. "I'm not used to these. Flats are more comfortable."

The stylist smiled. "Your height is perfect for flats, Miss Ross. And have you noticed? The color of your gown matches Mr. Vance's tie exactly. It's a perfect pair."

Evelyn remained silent.

She expected Alexander to deny it, but he never refuted the statement.

A subtle ripple stirred within her.

She wasn't oblivious. She could sense the change in the man's attitude.

In the past, she would have maintained her distance.

But now, a secret thread of joy unfurled within her.

Evelyn didn't want to dwell on it. She would let things take their natural course.

They entered the ballroom side by side.

In a corner, Chloe's eyes widened immediately. "My benefactor is here!"

Ryan followed her gaze, his eyes narrowing slightly.

At the entrance to the ballroom, a perfect couple walked in hand in hand.

###Chapter 74

San Francisco is the Vance Group's territory.

Alexander Vance had just stepped into the ballroom when executives immediately swarmed him.

He dealt with the crowd, but his gaze fell on Evelyn Ross.

She was like a nimble fish, slipping silently out of the crowd and hiding in the corner dessert area.

The woman picked up a piece of cake and took small bites.

She thought she was well hidden, unaware she had already attracted countless gazes.

Alexander's fingertips twitched slightly, as if still feeling the delicate touch of her waist...

He loosened his tie upon hearing someone fawn, "Mr. Vance, is Mrs. Vance not here? You've hidden the lady boss too well."

Someone nearby nervously tugged at the speaker.

Who didn't know Mr. Vance hated people prying into his private affairs?

Yet Alexander merely replied冷淡地, "Next time."

Everyone exchanged glances.

Mr. Vance was being so agreeable today?

...

Chloe Roland couldn't find Evelyn and felt disappointed.

She continued looking around.

Ryan Roland suddenly spoke. "I have good news."

"What is it?"

"Remember that Miss Xu who insulted you?"

Chloe's eyes darkened. "I remember."

That encounter still left her fearful.

Ryan's voice turned cold. "I cut off her supply sources."

"Brother..."

"Business requires toughness." Ryan's gaze was sharp. "She must pay the price."

Julian Evans approached unnoticed and interjected, "Mr. Roland did the right thing! That kind of person shouldn't be let off lightly!"

He looked at Chloe. "You're too kind. In my opinion, this punishment is too light."

Ryan nodded. "I should thank young Mr. Evans for the reminder."

Julian smiled. "It was nothing."

Chloe pursed her lips. "Where is my savior?"

Julian pointed toward the restroom. "She went to touch up her makeup."

Chloe immediately said to Mrs. Roland, "Mom, let's find our savior."

Mrs. Roland pushed the wheelchair toward the restroom.

Isabella Ashcroft happened to be coming out.

As they passed each other, Chloe suddenly turned her head.

Mrs. Roland asked, "What's wrong?"

Chloe clenched her fists.

She thought she had moved on, but seeing Isabella brought those vicious words rushing back.

"Love addicts should just die"—

This phrase had nearly shattered her last psychological防线.

Trembling, she took out her phone and texted Ryan: [Brother, that woman is here. I don't want to see her.]

Ryan received the message just as Isabella approached arm-in-arm with Julian.

She was about to greet him when Ryan asked coldly, "Is the other Miss Xu here too?"

Julian and Isabella both froze.

The three looked around but couldn't find the person they sought.

Isabella asked cautiously, "Is something wrong?"

Ryan looked displeased. "Is the Vance Group trying to slap the Roland family in the face?"

Julian immediately declared, "I'll handle this right away."

...

Evelyn remained alone in the corner.

Although she attracted many gazes, her aloof aura kept people from approaching easily.

A few who tried to chat her up were dismissed with a few words.

Jason Lawrence swaggered over, eyes bright with delight but pretending arrogance. "Why alone? Who brought you?"

His tone was full of disapproval toward her inviter.

Evelyn轻笑. "Quiet."

"True."

Jason wanted to say more but was interrupted by a commotion.

A group surrounded Julian, flattering him. "Young Mr. Evans, we hope for your favor in the future!"

Julian raised his glass with a faint smile. "I'm just a vice president."

"Don't be modest. We heard you're about to be promoted to project department manager!"

Everyone chimed in:

"Young Mr. Evans is so young and accomplished!"

"The collaboration with the Roland family is thanks to your efforts!"

Julian basked in the praise but remained humble. "It's all my uncle's doing."

"Mr. Vance is capable, but too unapproachable..."

"If we can secure ties with the Roland family, the group's development will be smoother!"

...

Jason snorted coldly. "Who knows what methods he used. He's been acting arrogantly in the company lately!"

He then said with frustration, "My cousin is too proud! He knows the Rolands but refuses to build good relations... If only I could make that connection!"

Evelyn glanced at Mr. Roland conversing with Ryan and stood up. "Come on, I'll introduce you to the Roland family."
###Chapter 75

Evelyn Ross came to this banquet hoping to ease tensions between Alexander Vance and the Roland family.

After observing for a while, she realized things weren't that simple.

Alexander appeared cold and stern, but was actually quite thoughtful.

From his care at the hospital to selecting her gown tonight, every detail showed his gentleness.

If he truly wanted reconciliation with the Rolands, it would have happened naturally by now.

What he resisted was clearly the woman who was his biological mother.

She didn't know why he brought her to the banquet, but since she was here, she might as well help Jason Lawrence.

At least make things easier for him at the company.

Whether Alexander cared or not was another matter.

Jason Lawrence was stunned. "You know the Rolands?"

"Yes, we've met once."

Evelyn was about to head toward the Roland family when music suddenly filled the banquet hall.

The dancing portion of the evening began.

The crowd automatically parted, clearing the central dance floor.

She stopped, deciding to wait until the dancing was over.

Several people came to ask her for a dance.

"Miss, may I have this dance?"

...

Not far away, Alexander Vance sat in the lounge area, speaking with several executives.

Someone试探ally asked, "Mr. Vance, won't you dance?"

Alexander shook his head lightly. "It wouldn't be appropriate."

"Mr. Vance is truly a model of integrity!"

Everyone laughed and teased him.

Alexander's gaze swept toward Evelyn's direction, and his expression darkened instantly.

She was surrounded by several young men, all vying for her attention.

Even more were waiting their turn.

These were all promising young talents from the Vance Group.

Alexander stood up abruptly. "Excuse me."

He strode toward that corner.

As he approached, he heard her refuse again. "Sorry, I don't dance."

Someone persisted. "I can teach you."

"...Not interested."

"Is it dancing you're not interested in, or the people? You have to choose one, or more will keep asking."

...

Evelyn was annoyed by the pestering and said bluntly, "I have a dance partner."

"Who?"

"Me."

A deep voice came from behind them. Everyone turned and saw Alexander standing there.

The men immediately grew nervous. "Mr. Vance."

Alexander ignored them and walked straight to Evelyn. "Sorry I'm late."

The surrounding men quickly dispersed tactfully.

The corner was finally quiet.

Cheers suddenly erupted in the hall.

Evelyn turned and saw Julian Evans leading Isabella Ashcroft onto the dance floor.

Julian was young and vibrant. Isabella wore a pink dress, her face flushed with shyness.

They didn't start dancing immediately. Julian took a step back and picked up a microphone.

"Isabella, with everyone here today, there's something I want to say to you."

Isabella smiled sweetly at him.

Julian spoke slowly. "In college, I was distracted by outside influences and overlooked your goodness. But spending time with you recently, I've discovered how kind and considerate you are. You worked hard to smooth things between me and Mr. Roland, which made this collaboration possible."

The crowd immediately began murmuring.

"So it was Young Mr. Evans' fiancée who mediated!"

"This is how business should be done—with an open heart."

"I heard Miss Ashcroft's mother is from New York. Maybe they can connect with the Sherwoods in the future!"

...

The compliments reached Isabella's ears, filling her with surprise and joy.

Julian was giving her face. The Ashcroft family had shallow roots. Marrying into the Vance family was already a step up for her. Now she finally had some standing.

Of course, this wasn't about love. It was about the shared glory and shame of a wealthy couple.

Isabella smiled gently. "Julian, it was the least I could do."

Julian took out a velvet box and opened it. Inside was a three-carat diamond ring.

He went down on one knee and held up the ring. "Isabella, marry me."

Isabella covered her mouth, her eyes reddening.

"Marry him! Marry him!"

"What a perfect match!"

Amid the cheers, Isabella extended her hand, and the ring was slipped onto her finger.

They embraced and began the first dance.

More people soon joined them on the dance floor, but the spotlight had already been stolen by the engaged couple.

Evelyn looked at Alexander. "Perhaps we shouldn't dance."

She didn't want to be accused of competing with the younger generation for attention.

Jason Lawrence said unhappily, "Julian really knows how to pick his moment. Now who will remember it was you, cousin, who negotiated the deal with the Rolands? All the credit goes to them!"

Alexander didn't seem to care. His phone vibrated. He said to Evelyn, "I need to take this call."

"Okay."

After he walked away, Jason frowned. "He's different with you."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "How so?"

Jason said, "He never tells anyone his whereabouts."

Evelyn was indifferent. Her phone vibrated lightly. It was a message from Chloe Roland: [My savior, I'm here too. I looked for you in the restroom but couldn't find you. Where are you? I'm coming to find you~]

Evelyn replied: [Northeast corner.]

Chloe Roland: [Be right there.]

###Chapter 76

Evelyn Ross set down her phone.

The first dance had ended.

Julian Evans and Isabella Ashcroft stepped out of the dance floor.

Tyler Evans swaggered over to her, a sneer on his face. "So what if you're Dr. Nan? A researcher can't compare to Miss Ashcroft's connections. Not just anyone can get close to the Roland family of New York!"

She wanted to go over and check on them.

"Evelyn, are you going to see Mr. Roland?" Isabella's voice was soft, but her eyes were nervous. "He might not want to see you right now."

"Miss Ashcroft is a lady of high status. Of course she has the right to speak!"

"Just leave! Don't ruin the important matters!"

Jason Lawrence strode forward.
