###Chapter 123

Identity

The roar of an engine approached from a distance.

Alexander Vance's expression was cold and stern as he sharply turned the steering wheel.

The car body cut a sharp arc.

Tires screeched against the pavement.

The black sedan came to a steady stop outside the grandmother's courtyard gate.

Alexander pushed the door open and stepped out.

He expected to see the girl being bullied.

But the scene before him made him freeze in place.

Evelyn Ross stood tall and straight.

Her eyes were sharp as daggers.

An intimidating aura radiated from her.

Security guards lay scattered at her feet.

Nannies hid in the distance, not daring to approach.

Julian Evans looked utterly shocked.

He clearly hadn't expected Evelyn to be so skilled.

He stared at her dumbfounded.

At this moment, she was like an unattainable flower on a high peak.

Sacred and inviolable.

The frustration of failing to win her over in college came flooding back.

It wasn't that she didn't like him.

He simply wasn't worthy of her.

The Evelyn he knew was quiet and introverted.

She always kept to herself.

She obediently completed all her schoolwork.

She never argued with anyone.

But now he understood.

That was just her disguise.

She was like a lurking leopard.

She couldn't be bothered to deal with the sheep.

Isabella Ashcroft was equally shocked.

When she was at the Ashcroft residence, Evelyn was easily manipulated.

When had she become so capable?

Seven or eight security guards couldn't even get near her!

She noticed the admiration in Julian's eyes.

Jealousy instantly surged within her.

"Evelyn Ross, how dare you start a fight at the Vance residence!" Isabella shrieked.

"Call the police! We must call the police!"

Margaret Evans snapped back to reality and hurriedly retreated.

She pulled out her phone to dial.

"Let's see if you dare to attack the police!"

Her phone was suddenly snatched away.

Evelyn played with the phone.

A cold smile curled her lips.

"You wouldn't let Auntie Fang make a call earlier."

"You think I'll let you call the police?"

Margaret stumbled back in fear.

She fell to the ground.

It was then that she heard footsteps.

Turning her head, she saw Alexander like a lifesaver.

"Alexander! Control this woman! She's gone mad!"

Evelyn glanced at Alexander.

Her eyes remained icy.

The fierce aura around her hadn't dissipated.

It seemed ready to erupt again at any moment.

Alexander strode forward and grasped her hand.

He frowned deeply.

"How could you resort to violence?"

Evelyn's eyes darkened.

Isabella seized the opportunity to exaggerate.

"Uncle, we were just talking to her properly."

"She suddenly started hitting people..."

"What if she disturbs the grandmother?"

Margaret quickly agreed.

"Exactly! Alexander, look at her."

"She doesn't act like a lady at all! So barbaric!"

Julian stared intently at their joined hands.

His face turned pale with anger.

Evelyn said coldly, "You think I shouldn't have fought back?"

"Of course not." Alexander's frown deepened.

Evelyn was about to pull her hand back.

But then she heard his urgent tone.

"Don't you know you're bleeding?"

"Don't you know how severe your anemia is?"

"How precious is your blood?"

Evelyn was stunned.

So he was worried about that.

Warmth spread through her heart.

The coldness around her gradually faded.

Alexander turned and ordered the nanny.

"Quick, call the family doctor!"

Soon, the nanny hurried over with the doctor.

The doctor asked nervously.

"Where is Miss Ross injured? Is it serious?"

Evelyn silently extended her hand.

The doctor looked at the small, already scabbed wound.

The corner of his mouth twitched.

"I arrived too late..."

"The wound has already healed."

Evelyn turned her face away.

Alexander cleared his throat lightly.

The doctor helplessly disinfected the area with iodine.

Under Alexander's watchful gaze, he applied a cartoon band-aid.

The scene left everyone dumbfounded.

Margaret was the first to recover.

"Alexander! How can you shield her like this?"

"Do you know how arrogant she was just now?"

Alexander shot her a cold glance.

"Miss Ross has always been reasonable."

"You must have pushed her too far."

Isabella stepped forward.

"Uncle, she was harassing Julian!"

"We only wanted her to leave because we were worried it would affect your uncle-nephew relationship."

Alexander looked at Julian.

"What a coincidence."

"There happen to be surveillance cameras near the courtyard."

"Shall we see who was harassing whom?"

Julian immediately became speechless.

"That won't be necessary..."

"I just wanted to make things clear with her..."

Margaret quickly changed the subject.

"Alexander, I was thinking of you!"

"Your wife is returning to the country soon."

"Seeing her would cause misunderstandings!"

Isabella added fuel to the fire.

"Uncle, you don't know."

"She just said she is the lady of this house."

"That she is your wife..."

Evelyn frowned.

She had never said that.

She was about to explain.

But she saw a flicker of amusement in Alexander's eyes.

The corner of his lips curled up slightly.

Just then, Eleanor Vance ran out angrily.

"You rascal! Tell them!"

"Is your granddaughter-in-law your wife or not?"
###Chapter 124
As Eleanor Vance’s voice faded, the courtyard fell into complete silence.

All eyes were fixed on Alexander Vance.

Evelyn Ross noticed his thin lips pressing together and quickly intervened. “Grandmother, I’m feeling a bit cold. Shall we go inside?”

Eleanor immediately became concerned. “My dear, are you cold? Let’s go in right away. You mustn’t catch a chill while trying to conceive…”

She took Evelyn’s hand and led her toward the house. “This is your home now,” she said firmly. “You’re not going anywhere. You’ll stay here from now on.”

Evelyn sighed and rubbed her temples.

She glanced back discreetly at Alexander.

Their situation was complicated, with their marriage entangled in too many interests.

Besides, they hadn’t openly addressed their relationship yet. They had to be cautious.

Margaret Evans couldn’t hold back. “Alexander, your grandmother is confused. This girl has tricked her! She has no morals and doesn’t belong in the Vance family. Don’t you make the same mistake!”

Alexander didn’t explain. His tone was cold. “Sister-in-law, you have no say in how Grandmother runs her household. And one more thing—”

He scanned the security team, his voice icy. “You dared to lay hands on my guest in Grandmother’s courtyard. Don’t bother coming back tomorrow.”

With that, he strode into Eleanor Vance’s residence.

The beaten security guards began to protest. “Mr. Vance, Mrs. Evans told us to do it! We’re innocent!”

Thomas Watson arrived promptly and stepped forward with a smile. “Innocent? You can’t even tell who the real master of this house is. Get out now, or I’ll make sure you never work in San Francisco again.”

The guards fell silent and slunk away.

Thomas surveyed the remaining onlookers, his expression warning. “The show’s over. Remember your employment contracts. Know what to say and what not to say.”

The servants quickly lowered their heads and dispersed.

In moments, only Margaret, Isabella, and Julian remained in the courtyard.

Margaret spat toward the doorway in frustration. She turned to Isabella, annoyed. “You spoke up without knowing the full story! I thought we could really drive that little tramp out!”

Isabella hung her head meekly. “Mother-in-law, I was wrong.”

Margaret took a deep breath. She noticed Julian’s distracted gaze and grew even angrier. She glared at Isabella. “I don’t care what tricks that woman used to enchant the old lady and those two men. Isabella, you must find a way to get rid of her! Otherwise, forget about having any authority in this household!”

She stormed off.

Isabella stood alone, thinking about how Evelyn was protected by both Eleanor and Alexander. She looked at Julian, who was still staring dreamily toward the house, and felt a tightness in her chest.

She lowered her head. “Julian, I forgot some of my personal things. I’m going home to get them.”

“Okay,” Julian replied absently.

Isabella returned to the Ashcroft residence in a fury.

If she couldn’t drive Evelyn away, someone else could—someone Evelyn would listen to.

She walked into the living room with a worried expression. William and Sophia Ashcroft were watching TV. William looked surprised. “Didn’t you move into the Vance residence? Why are you back?”

Sophia remained cold, still upset about Isabella’s insistence on marrying into the Vance family.

Isabella spoke softly to William. “I came back to get some clothes. I also wanted to mention that Evelyn… isn’t doing well at the Vances’.”

William didn’t respond.

But Sophia looked over. “What’s wrong with Evelyn?”

Isabella sighed. “She’s been acting very familiar with Mr. Vance. There’s been a lot of gossip in the household.”

Sophia frowned immediately. “Evelyn is staying at the Vances’ to care for Mrs. Vance. Why is there so much idle talk? I trust her character. Don’t mention this to me again!”

Isabella had expected this.

She seethed inwardly. Sophia had never trusted her this much!

Fortunately, she had come prepared…

Isabella lowered her head. “I thought so too, but today everyone at the Vances’ was talking about a photo. It’s an intimate picture of Evelyn and Mr. Vance…”

She took out her phone and showed the photo to Sophia.

Sophia frowned at first, thinking it was staged. But as she looked closer, her pupils contracted sharply.

William exploded beside her. “…Shameless! How could I have such a disgraceful daughter! She’s married and still fooling around with Mr. Vance?”

Isabella’s voice trembled. “Because of her, my mother-in-law now thinks I’m the same kind of person. She’s pressuring me to drive Evelyn out of the Vance household… But Evelyn won’t listen to me. Today, she openly defied Mrs. Evans at the Vances’, saying even if Mrs. Vance herself tried to kick her out, she wouldn’t leave… She also said… also said…”

William frowned. “Said what? Spit it out!”

“She said her mother lived with our family for years, and Mrs. Vance should learn from you, Mother, to be more tolerant. That it’s normal for men to have multiple women… Father, Mother, with her causing such a scene, how can I hold my head up in the Vance family?”

Sophia spoke sharply. “Impossible! She would never say those things!”

Isabella bit her lip.

William was furious. “How is it impossible? Sophia, you’re too trusting! It’s a fact that she’s living at the Vances’! It’s a fact that she’s being inappropriate with Mr. Vance! No wonder Mr. Vance helped arrange a hospital bed for her last time and has been looking out for her ever since. They’ve been involved all along! Only someone as kind-hearted as you would believe she hasn’t changed!”

Sophia stared at him in disbelief.

Whenever Evelyn was mentioned, William’s attitude felt foreign to her.

Why did he always assume the worst about his own daughter?

She suddenly remembered yesterday, when Evelyn said she wasn’t William’s daughter… She had fainted afterward, but she had heard those words clearly.

She had thought it was just anger talking at the time.

But seeing how William treated Evelyn…

Sophia spoke suddenly. “William, is Evelyn really your daughter?”

William froze, his expression shifting slightly.

Sophia didn’t miss the subtle change. She frowned. “Is it true? Is Evelyn not your daughter?!”
###Chapter 125

Sophia Ashcroft’s gaze was sharp.

Outwardly gentle and delicate, she was inwardly resilient and strong-willed.

William Ashcroft knew her temperament well. He quickly explained, "I never considered her my own daughter. Sophia, you know my heart belongs only to the daughters you bore me."

Sophia’s jaw tightened. "Is that true?"

Sophia’s expression softened slightly.

Isabella Ashcroft grew impatient with their conversation. She cut in abruptly, "Mom, Evelyn listens to you. Please talk some sense into her. She’s living at the Vance estate now, and even the housekeepers look down on her…"

Sophia lowered her eyes.

She had always been open-minded.

This applied to both Isabella and Evelyn Ross.

She never interfered in her children’s marriages.

Isabella’s scheming to marry into the Vance family had already disappointed her.

As for Evelyn…

When Sophia learned she was Dr. Nan, she felt immense pride. It was like watching a pearl she had polished finally shine.

Now one daughter had let her down, and the other seemed to be making reckless choices.

Evelyn had always been sensible. This was out of character. Perhaps there was more to the story?

Unable to set her worries aside, Sophia pondered for a moment. She took out her phone and sent Evelyn a message: "Evelyn, I haven’t met your husband since your wedding. Bring him home for dinner tonight."

She set her phone down, her eyes darkening.

She needed to see for herself what kind of man Evelyn had married.

If he was unreliable, she would find a way to annul the marriage. She would help Evelyn start over.

If he was dependable, she would encourage them to live together. A separated couple could not build a lasting future.

…

Evelyn received the message just after lunch with Eleanor Vance.

The morning’s events had left her drained. After the meal, Eleanor refused to rest. She fixed her gaze on Alexander Vance. "Why didn’t you tell everyone today that your wife is lawfully wedded?"

Evelyn looked up, about to speak, but Eleanor pressed on urgently. "Are you still planning to divorce her?"

Alexander was taken aback. "You worry too much, Grandmother."

Eleanor turned to Evelyn. "And you, my dear?"

Evelyn pressed her lips together. Feeling Alexander’s eyes on her, she replied softly, "No."

"Good!"

Eleanor took Alexander’s hand and Evelyn’s, placing them together. Her tone was earnest. "Finding someone you truly love is rare. Finding mutual love is even rarer. Cherish this bond. Never let go easily."

Evelyn instinctively glanced at Alexander.

He was already looking at her.

Their eyes met.

Evelyn caught a flicker of hesitation in his gaze.

He wasn’t fully committed yet.

The realization chilled her. She tried to pull her hand back, but his warm palm held it firmly.

Evelyn looked up at him. His jaw was tight.

She raised an eyebrow in silent question.

Alexander averted his eyes.

Eleanor interrupted their silent exchange. "Tell me honestly, do you love her or not?"

Evelyn’s heart skipped a beat.

Alexander hesitated, caught off guard by the directness.

Disappointment washed over Evelyn.

Had all their recent closeness been one-sided?

She had felt his affection…

She lowered her eyes, about to speak, when Alexander’s deep voice answered, "Yes."

Evelyn froze.

Her heart raced.

Eleanor turned to her. "And you, my dear—"

Evelyn cut in quickly. "Grandmother, it’s time for your nap."

Eleanor smiled knowingly. "Alright, alright. I’ll rest. My dear is shy. I won’t ask anymore."

She leaned on Fiona Vance and walked toward her bedroom, smiling.

Once inside, the elderly woman shed her confused demeanor. She peered through the peephole shrewdly. "Fiona, they’ve both confessed. It’s finally happening, right?"

Fiona remained silent.

Outside, the atmosphere was charged.

Alexander stood tall and impeccable in his suit. He was dazzling wherever he went. His striking looks and composed demeanor made it hard for Evelyn not to be drawn to him.

Avoiding his eyes, she cleared her throat softly. "Were you just saying that to comfort Grandmother?"

His jaw tightened. "I meant it."

Evelyn replied with a faint "Oh," her ears tinged with pink.

Her phone chimed.

Evelyn checked it. It was a message from Sophia. She raised an eyebrow. "Isabella must have complained. Sophia is worried I’ve actually become your mistress. She wants to talk."

Alexander chuckled. "Will you go?"

Evelyn typed a reply as she spoke. "I should check on her. I’m still concerned about her fainting spell yesterday."

"Good." His gaze softened. "I’ll go with you."

Evelyn looked up, surprised. "What?"

"I’ll accompany you," he said, looking down at her with amusement. "Didn’t she ask you to bring your husband to meet her? Or don’t you want to bring me?"

Evelyn was speechless.

It wasn’t that!

She took a half-step back, swallowing hard. She forced herself to sound calm. "Fine. I’ll reply."

Alexander added, "I’ll have gifts prepared. You see her as a mother. I can’t meet my mother-in-law empty-handed."

Mother-in-law…

Evelyn’s cheeks grew warmer. She turned aside to reply to Sophia: "Alright. See you tonight."
###Chapter 126

Gifts?

In the Ashcroft living room.

At 5:30 PM.

She couldn't even carry three or four at once.

Each item was exceedingly valuable.

The Vance family was indeed wealthy and powerful.

[We just set off.]

###Chapter 127

Evelyn put down her phone and looked at Alexander.

He was dressed casually today.

But she noticed he wore a crisp, new white shirt and a jacket she hadn't seen before.

His tall frame was accentuated by the well-tailored suit, making his long legs appear even straighter and more slender.

He looked handsome and distinguished.

Evelyn wore a simple beige sweater and light blue jeans, appearing fresh and natural.

Standing side by side, they made a perfect match.

The gifts Alexander prepared had already been loaded into the car by Thomas.

Thomas walked in and reported, "Mr. Vance, Miss Ross, the gifts are all loaded. We can leave now."

They nodded and headed toward the parking lot.

Eleanor waved happily behind them. "Fiona, look how perfect this young couple is! I can already imagine how adorable my great-grandchild will be!"

Evelyn, who had just stepped out the door, nearly tripped over the threshold.

They had just made progress, and the old lady was already thinking about great-grandchildren?

She gave Alexander a helpless look.

The tips of his ears turned slightly red.

They got into the Bentley, and the car slowly drove off.

Evelyn wasn't concerned about the gifts but reminded him, "Sophia treats everyone equally, regardless of background. If she speaks directly later, please don't mind."

Alexander chuckled softly. "Miss Ross, you seem to have some misconceptions about me."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

Alexander said seriously, "I always respect my elders. My upbringing would never allow me to be rude."

Evelyn suddenly smiled. "Is that so? But I remember someone who refused to believe we were married and had security throw me out several times?"

"..."

Alexander was speechless. He explained quietly, "I didn't know you then, I..."

"I'm just joking," Evelyn interrupted with a laugh.

Thomas curiously interjected, "Miss Ross, it really wasn't Mr. Vance's fault. Getting married without both parties present was just too strange... But I remember you had several part-time jobs back then?"

Evelyn nodded. "I had to support myself after moving out of the Ashcroft house in middle school."

Thomas looked surprised. "You weren't even an adult then?"

"Yeah, I could only do odd jobs and manual labor."

Evelyn gazed into the distance, as if recalling those days. "Actually, that time felt very free."

No constraints of being a love child, no nagging from Linda, no pressure from Isabella...

Though poor and lonely, those were her most carefree days.

Thomas sighed. "That must have been tough."

"It was alright," Evelyn said calmly.

She wasn't downplaying the hardship. She truly found it fulfilling.

Starting with various odd jobs, she slowly built her company. After becoming Dr. Nan, she handed those ventures over to James.

She still owned a repair shop, a private kitchen, a courier company... and even a construction crew.

She had literally moved bricks!

Evelyn turned to look out the window.

The weather had turned cooler, and a light rain began to fall.

San Francisco winters never saw snow, only cold, damp rain.

The car moved steadily forward.

Evelyn suddenly noticed a construction worker by a site.

He wore a yellow hardhat and dirty work clothes, his face speckled with mud.

He looked helplessly at the rain, using worn gloves to shield his head as if preparing to dash to the convenience store across the street.

Evelyn remembered her first job.

It was on a rainy day just like this.

She couldn't afford a taxi and ran home in the rain, soaked to the bone.

How she wished someone had offered her an umbrella then...

"Pull over, please," Evelyn said suddenly.

Though puzzled, the driver pulled to the curb.

Evelyn picked up the blue umbrella at her feet, got out, and ran to the worker.

She pressed the umbrella into his hand.

The worker was stunned, his grimy face breaking into a surprised smile.

His accent was a bit unusual. "Thank you! Thank you so much!"

Evelyn waved it off and ran back to the car.

"Let's go."

The car started moving again.

Evelyn smiled slightly.

That umbrella felt like a gift to her younger, helpless self.

She didn't notice that just after their car turned the corner, a luxury vehicle pulled over.

Two bodyguards in black got out under black umbrellas.

They approached the dirty worker. "Sorry, sir. Traffic delayed us. How was the site inspection?"

The man looked at the blue umbrella in his hand and smiled. "As the Chinese say, quite fruitful!"

He carefully put the umbrella away and got into the car, not minding the dirty seats.

"Find out where that Bentley ahead is going."

"Yes, sir."

...

At the Ashcroft house.

They prepared to welcome Evelyn and her husband.

Sophia frowned. "We should treat people based on character, not status."

As they spoke, a car stopped in front of the Ashcroft house.

Sophia stepped forward happily. "Evelyn, you're here! And your husband is...?"

The next moment, everyone froze.

DNA Test

Evelyn Ross never planned to get rich from stocks.

Borrowing money from Sophia Ashcroft was just an excuse.

But she never made a bad investment.
After getting the money, she really did buy stocks.

As Dr. Nan, her clinical drugs had saved many important people.

Those who couldn't wait for new drugs to hit the market often sought private deals with her.

Evelyn had built a vast network of connections because of this.

Recently, a powerful figure, after using her medication, shared a tip.

She didn't use it herself, but invested it for Sophia.

Now, on her phone screen, that stock was surging.

It was about to hit the limit up.

Everyone leaned in for a closer look, gasping in shock.

The principal of sixteen million had doubled in an instant.

Making money this fast was like printing it.

Evelyn:?

###Chapter 134

Dr. Nan had always kept her identity hidden. She clearly didn't want it exposed.

Everyone here was in the new energy sector. If they knew she was Dr. Nan, Evelyn would never have a moment's peace again.

Get Evelyn a job at the Vance Group?

James laid it bare. Everyone at the table looked at Sophia with disapproval.

Her earlier pretense of caring about a classmate was completely exposed as a direct attack on Evelyn.

Sophia's eyes reddened with feigned hurt. She looked at Evelyn, who remained completely detached, and sneered. "Exactly, James. Why are you making such a fuss? People might think you and Evelyn are an item!"

James was startled and immediately cried out, "How could I be worthy of her! Don't talk nonsense!"

This stung Sophia.

She liked James, but he said he wasn't worthy of Evelyn? That implied she was inferior to Evelyn!

Something about Dr. Nan marrying someone hastily for the IPO.

###Chapter 139
Husband!

Linda Lowell brainwashed her day and night.

Repeatedly telling her she owed the Ashcrofts and was indebted to Isabella.

"Mr. Klein?"

Mr. Klein was surprised.
"You know me?"

"Our company is collaborating with yours on the恒温能源系统 project."
"I'm an intern."

The young man explained awkwardly.

The young man immediately said, "An overseas Chinese returning for real estate!"
"His family's businesses are global."
"He's here to expand into the华夏 market."
"He's incredibly powerful!"

"A person like that is here for Mr. Vance?"

The Vance Group had shifted to real estate.
Alexander didn't recall any dealings with the Castillo family.

He was thinking when Mr. Klein hurried over to Anthony Castillo.
He pulled out a contract.
"Boss, the construction site we just inspected meets investment criteria."
"The contract is urgent, so I brought it for your signature."

He apologized to everyone at the table.

The room fell silent.

Alexander looked at Anthony.
His gaze turned cold.

Under the table, Evelyn had been holding his hand.
Even when discussing her background, she hadn't let go, as if drawing strength.

Now that attention had shifted, she relaxed and tried to pull her hand back.

But that large hand took control, gripping hers tightly!

Evelyn couldn't pull free.
She looked at Alexander in surprise.

The man's face showed possessiveness and sourness.

Jealous?

Evelyn followed his gaze to Anthony.
She found it both funny and exasperating.

Anthony's presence actually made Alexander feel threatened…

She stopped struggling.

All eyes were on Anthony.
He felt the intense stares, one particularly hostile.

Anthony finished signing and looked up.
He met Alexander's gaze.

He gave Alexander a gentle smile.
Then he turned to Evelyn.
"Miss Evelyn Ross, a formal introduction."
"Anthony Castillo."

He extended his hand to Evelyn.

Evelyn could only use her free hand for a brief, polite shake before quickly withdrawing.

Alexander's expression darkened further.

Anthony seemed not to notice.
He looked at Sophia.
"I am not a construction worker."
"I dressed like this after a site inspection."
"My apologies for the misunderstanding."

Sophia's mouth hung open, wide enough for an egg.

She looked sheepishly at Anthony, then at Evelyn.
She was speechless.

"I knew Dr. Nan wouldn't marry someone who lays bricks!"
"So he's a tycoon!"

"Dr. Nan and a real estate tycoon are a perfect match!"

"I just searched the Castillo family online."
"They're amazing!"

Isabella and Julian frowned at Evelyn.

How was this possible…

How could she marry someone like that?

Julian realized making her his mistress was impossible now…

He looked at Alexander.

Seeing his uncle's dark expression, he felt a secret satisfaction.

Someone like Evelyn… even his uncle couldn't have her.

Isabella suddenly spoke.
"Evelyn, is he really your husband?"

Evelyn smiled faintly.
"Do you want him to be or not?"

Isabella was stumped.

Evelyn stated directly, "He is not my husband."

Anthony sighed.
"I am not Miss Evelyn's husband."
"It rained yesterday."
"Miss Evelyn lent me her umbrella."
"I came to return it."
"Miss Isabella Ashcroft added me on WhatsApp and invited me."
"She said I could see Miss Evelyn here, so I came…"

He explained clearly.

Isabella breathed a sigh of relief.
"So it was my misunderstanding!"

Evelyn found it ridiculous.

When Anthony was a construction worker, they denied it.
Isabella didn't believe them and manipulated public opinion against her.

When Anthony turned out to be a tycoon, Isabella believed it immediately.

Evelyn looked at her.
"As long as you believe it, that's fine."
"Just stop trying to pin labels on me."

Her words exposed Isabella's intentions, making her uncomfortable.

Isabella forced a smile.
"My mistake for misunderstanding."
"But Evelyn, who is your husband really?"
"Won't you bring him to meet us?"
"Everyone wants to know!"
"Who is so fortunate to marry someone as outstanding as you, regardless of status?"

She emphasized "outstanding," full of sarcasm.

Professor Lowell looked at Evelyn.
"Evelyn, bring him next time for me to vet."

Evelyn smiled and nodded.

The man beside her spoke in a deep, commanding voice.
"No need for next time."
"I'm here now."
###Chapter 141

Alexander Vance's words plunged the private room into dead silence.

Everyone stared at him in shock.

Professor Lowell looked utterly bewildered. "He's here? Where?"

Alexander straightened his posture, only to watch Professor Lowell walk right past him.

The professor scrutinized every man in the room, starting with Anthony Castillo.

He finally turned back, still confused. "Evelyn, who is it?"

Alexander was speechless.

Evelyn Ross suppressed a laugh inside.

This was the first time she had seen such a rich array of expressions on Alexander's face.

She pressed her lips together and remained silent.

Isabella Ashcroft frowned. "Evelyn, did you marry one of our classmates? Who is it? Stop keeping us in suspense!"

Alexander's voice was low and firm. "Her husband is me."

The room fell completely silent.

All eyes instantly focused on the two of them.

Isabella opened her mouth, but after meeting Alexander's icy gaze, she swallowed her words.

Anthony was speechless.

The banquet atmosphere reached its peak.

After answering the call, his expression changed drastically. "I'll be right there."

He looked apologetically at Evelyn. "I have to go."

"Go ahead," Evelyn nodded.

Alexander scanned the room again. "The car is for you. Have the driver take you home."

He adjusted his suit and stood up.

As he passed Anthony, he suddenly spoke. "Mr. Castillo, everyone here is Evelyn's teachers and friends. Your presence might make them feel restrained. Perhaps you should leave with me?"

Anthony's mouth twitched, but he still rose gracefully. "Very well."

Before exiting, he turned back and smiled at Evelyn. "Ms. Ross, I'll be in touch often from now on."

Alexander's face darkened several shades.

After the two men left, the atmosphere in the private room finally relaxed completely.

"I'll have bragging rights forever! The people from the财经新闻 actually drank with me..."

"Alright, alright, we still have Dr. Nan with us!"

Everyone immediately looked at her.

James Klein frowned. "Isabella, you've been stirring up trouble all day. Sophia was just your pawn. What are you trying to do now?"

Isabella bit her lip, deciding to throw caution to the wind. "I just can't stand how far Evelyn is going! She actually convinced Mr. Vance to act out this charade with her!"

Evelyn frowned. "Haven't you caused enough trouble?"

Isabella sneered. "Dare to do it but not own up to it? Everyone in the Vance family knows Mr. Vance got married two years ago! You just graduated and got married. Tell me, how did you manage to get married across a two-year gap?"

Evelyn fell silent.

The complicated reasons behind it were something even she hadn't fully figured out.

She said calmly, "Mr. Vance and I are indeed married. Believe it or not, that's up to you."

Julian Evans had been wanting to speak but didn't dare earlier. He immediately scoffed. "That's right. Evelyn Ross, I pursued you for four years. How come I never knew you were my aunt?"

Isabella sighed. "She's just relying on her looks to trick Mr. Vance into playing along. Evelyn, your mother was a mistress. Now you're married and still having an affair, being someone else's mistress..."

Julian added, "I guess it runs in the family. Can't help but be the other woman?"

The words were vicious!

Even more insulting than Sophia's earlier remarks!

Even Professor Lowell couldn't listen anymore. "Enough! Isabella, Julian, shut your mouths!"

Isabella looked at him. "Professor Lowell, just because she's Dr. Nan, you're taking her side? Is someone with such flawed personal morals fit to be a特聘教授? Aren't you afraid of damaging the school's reputation?"

Evelyn's gaze was steady. "Mr. Vance and I are legally married. If the school is concerned, I can show my marriage certificate."

Evelyn glanced at it and couldn't help but laugh.

Wasn't that just her own number?!
###Chapter 142
The Aunt

Isabella finished speaking and immediately dialed the number.

A smug smile curled her lips, as if victory was already assured.

This number wasn't the one everyone knew for Evelyn Ross.

Evelyn had multiple numbers. One for daily use, known to all.

The others corresponded to different identities.

One exclusively for Dr. Nan, another for Researcher Nora, and several more whose purposes even she sometimes forgot. So she simply kept them all powered off.

Anything urgent could be sent via email. Her peace was not to be disturbed.

The moment Isabella's call connected, a cold automated voice responded: "The number you have dialed is currently switched off…"

Evelyn's eyebrow quirked slightly.

Isabella put her phone down but pressed on relentlessly. "Evelyn, what are you so smug about? I've done my homework. My aunt dislikes being disturbed, hence the switched-off phone, right? But since she's attending Great-grandmother's birthday gala, that means Uncle is preparing to publicly acknowledge her! Let's see how you handle things then!"

Evelyn shrugged. "No need for your concern."

Seeing her remain unfazed, Isabella suddenly laughed. "You don't actually believe Uncle is deeply devoted to you, do you? Evelyn, care to guess who that call was from, the one that just pulled Uncle away?!"

Evelyn paused slightly.

Isabella raised her voice. "From what I know, my aunt has poor health. She's been living in a sanatorium abroad and hasn't left it since returning. Uncle has visited her several times. Their bond is profound. He defied all opposition to marry her back then. Him being with you today is just an act! One call from my aunt, and he immediately abandons you!"

Evelyn's fingers tightened.

She suddenly remembered the night after her release from jail, in the hospital, when Alexander was called away and didn't return all night. Then last night, he couldn't accompany her to the Ashcroft residence after receiving a call…

And again, just now…

No one's career, no matter how demanding, should involve such frequent emergencies.

Unless…

The emergency was always the same matter. Or the same person!

Was he in some kind of trouble?

Evelyn's brow furrowed slightly, a thread of worry weaving through her.

But her expression was misinterpreted by those watching.

Isabella laughed triumphantly. "Speechless, are you?"

She turned to the others, her eyes reddening slightly. "I know everyone might think I'm being too aggressive today. But I just don't want my aunt to repeat my mother's mistake, to have her life invaded by a third party. Evelyn was the biggest crack between my parents. They spent a lifetime trying to mend it. Evelyn, I hope you stop this mistake. Live properly with your husband. Don't let your future child become a scar between Uncle and my aunt!"

This emotional appeal made it impossible for anyone at the table to criticize her further.

Yes, Isabella was a victim herself. Her targeting of Evelyn seemed understandable.

Even Jason Lawrence opened his mouth, unsure how to defend Dr. Nan, finally mumbling, "I believe Dr. Nan isn't that kind of person."

Julian Evans scoffed. "Actions speak louder than words! If you don't believe Isabella, I can have invitations sent to you all! See for yourselves who the aunt really is!"

Isabella and Julian's confident assertions forced everyone to believe.

Evelyn couldn't exactly produce her marriage certificate on the spot to prove her innocence. She simply lowered her gaze and said calmly, "Then we'll wait and see."

She had long understood that some truths, when spoken, aren't necessarily believed.

The birthday gathering ended on a sour note.

Classmates departed one after another. Professor Lowell looked at Evelyn with concern. "Evelyn, is it true? You and Mr. Vance?"

Evelyn sighed softly. "Professor, it's true."

Professor Lowell still looked worried. "Evelyn, you're young. Your future is bright. Don't be led astray by Mr. Vance's handsome face."

"…"

Evelyn's mouth twitched. If Alexander knew he was being compared to a seductive temptress, he'd probably be furious.

After seeing Professor Lowell off, Evelyn turned to find Jason standing not far away, hesitating, his face full of concern. "Dr. Nan…"

Evelyn spoke. "Just call me Evelyn. We're classmates. No need for formality."

Jason coughed awkwardly. "Well, Evelyn, if you need help, or if there are… extenuating circumstances, you can tell me. I'll help clarify things among the classmates…"

Evelyn rubbed her temple.

She understood Jason's meaning. He was always responsible among classmates, excelling which was why James Klein selected him for her company. He also didn't believe she and Alexander were married, worried she'd be maligned and wanting to help explain…

Evelyn sighed softly. "Julian gave you an invitation too, right? See you at the gala."

Jason still looked worried. "Are you sure?"

"Sure."

"…Alright."

Jason left, half-convinced, half-doubting.

Then, Evelyn's phone rang. Answering, she heard James Klein's voice. "Boss, about your classmate, Sophia Watson. I was about to act, but found someone beat me to it. She's been fired. She won't find work in San Francisco again."

A sharp glint flashed in Evelyn's eyes.

She was never one to turn the other cheek. Sophia had repeatedly humiliated her at the gathering today. Letting it slide wasn't an option.

This was just a minor lesson.

Evelyn asked, "Can you find out who did it?"

"Two parties. One is the Vance family, likely Alexander's doing. The other is a overseas Chinese named Anthony Castillo. A well-known real estate tycoon abroad, making quite a splash on his return to the country. But why would he help you?"

Evelyn: "…Who knows."

Hanging up, she noticed a new message on WhatsApp from Anthony Castillo: 【Miss Evelyn, I like you very much. Can you give me your life?】

Evelyn: ???

A murderous intent suddenly flashed in her eyes.

Who exactly was this Anthony Castillo? Helping her one moment, then sending threatening messages demanding her life the next?!

…

Meanwhile, in a luxury car, Anthony Castillo smiled delightedly at the umbrella in his hands, telling his assistant, "This umbrella marks the beginning of our connection. Asking her for it as a memento isn't too much, is it?"

Assistant: "…Not too much. But boss, are you sure you didn't make a mistake typing the message?"

"Of course," Anthony said with full confidence. "I might not know Pinyin, but I've studied Chinese characters. I used handwriting input. Absolutely no problem!"

Miss Evelyn and Alexander Vance didn't seem to be a real couple.

If Alexander showed up with his wife at the gala, he, Anthony, would be there to support Miss Evelyn!

Robert Vance favored his eldest son's family. He was cold to Alexander.

He was so exceptional, yet得不到父亲认可，被兄长算计。

###Chapter 144

The Vance estate had been bustling since dawn.

He was teasing her.

###Chapter 145

William Ashcroft raised his voice sharply. "I never acknowledged you as my daughter. But the Ashcroft family raised you. Is this how you speak to me?"

Evelyn knew this question was coming. She was about to explain when a cheerful voice interrupted. "My savior!"

Chloe Roland walked over slowly.

She had previously used a wheelchair due to weakness, but now she could walk normally. Dressed in a pink evening gown, she linked arms with Evelyn affectionately. "I've missed you so much!"

Her dependence was obvious.

Mr. and Mrs. Roland followed helplessly. Mr. Roland laughed. "This girl is so attached! She hasn't seen you in days, Miss Ross, and she's this affectionate."

Mrs. Roland pretended to be upset. "She's never this dependent on me. People might think you're her real sister!"

Chloe gave her parents a playful smile. She turned back to Evelyn and whispered, "Savior, I kept up with the news even in the hospital. I heard everyone is saying you and Mr. Vance are involved. It's not true, right?"

Evelyn saw the clarity in her eyes. Her trauma had healed, replaced by genuine concern. Evelyn smiled. "Yes, it's a rumor. Mr. Vance and I—"

Before she could finish, Chloe cut in. "Of course you're innocent! I absolutely believe you! Mr. Vance is so cold. He doesn't deserve you! You would never be interested in him! It's a shame you're married. Otherwise, I'd introduce you to my brother! He's a confirmed bachelor!"

Evelyn's mouth twitched. She was at a loss for words.

Why was it so hard to reveal her relationship with Alexander Vance?

Mrs. Roland was already scolding her. "How can you talk about your brother like that? If he were here, you wouldn't dare be so disrespectful!"

Chloe stuck out her tongue. She whispered to Evelyn, "My brother wanted to come to the birthday gala. The Roland and Vance families are cooperating now. But Dad insisted he wait until the photo is restored."

Evelyn asked with a smile, "Restoring an old photo?"

Chloe nodded. "Yes, a photo from twenty or thirty years ago… Dad, what photo did you ask Ryan to restore?"

Mr. Roland looked at Evelyn and Sophia. "That photo is related to Mrs. Ashcroft. I met Mrs. Nan a few times back in New York. We took a group photo."

Sophia's eyes lit up. "My mother? You must show me when it's restored!"

Mr. Roland seemed surprised. "You don't have any photos of Mrs. Nan?"

Sophia shook her head with a sigh. "Before we moved to San Francisco, there was a sudden fire at home. All the photos were destroyed…" Her eyes grew nostalgic. "All these years, with my poor health… It's been over twenty years. I've almost forgotten what my mother looked like…"

Mr. Roland sighed sympathetically. "I heard about it at the time and felt so sorry. How did Mrs. Nan pass away? She was only in her forties, right? Still in her prime…"

Evelyn finally understood. The reason Sophia had no contact with New York was that her mother had died young.

She listened intently as Sophia said weakly, "My mother died in an accident." She clearly didn't want to discuss it further.

Mr. Roland took the hint and didn't press. He said meaningfully, "Once the photo is restored, I will definitely show it to you and Miss Ross!"

Sophia paused slightly, then nodded.

Evelyn was puzzled. Why did Mr. Roland specifically include her?

But she didn't ask. She was also curious about what Mrs. Nan looked like. She must have been an impressive woman to raise someone like Sophia.

Mr. and Mrs. Roland noticed the crowd around Eleanor Vance was thinning. They said, "Chloe, let's go greet the matriarch first."

It was a matter of etiquette.

Though reluctant, Chloe followed her parents away.

After they left, some guests passed by Evelyn and Sophia. They were whispering.

"I heard Mr. Vance is introducing his mysterious wife today!"

"I'm so curious about who Mrs. Vance is…"

"Rumor has it Mr. Vance has a mistress. There are even photos. The staff at the Vance estate all know. Mrs. Vance must have returned because of this…"

After the guests moved away, Sophia looked at Evelyn with concern. "Evelyn, I'm not feeling well. Could you take me home?"

Evelyn was both amused and touched. She knew this was Sophia's excuse to help her leave the party early.

She took Sophia's arm. "Ma'am, let me show you something."

"What is it?"

"My marriage certificate."
###Chapter 146

Sophia Ashcroft paused her steps.

She looked at Evelyn Ross, her eyes questioning.

Evelyn nodded, signaling her to follow.

The two walked toward the lounge.

Evelyn wore a champagne-colored evening gown.

She only held her phone.

Her bag and Eleanor Vance's gift were next door.

She knew Sophia would worry.

After all they had been through, she understood words alone meant nothing.

The marriage certificate was the most direct proof.

They were about to enter the lounge.

A sudden commotion came from the entrance.

Evelyn and Sophia turned simultaneously.

Alexander Vance had arrived.

He wore a black tuxedo, his posture tall and straight.

The crowd automatically parted for him.

He walked directly toward Eleanor Vance.

The elderly lady smiled and extended her hand.

Alexander did not step forward.

He bowed deeply.

"Grandmother, I wish you endless happiness and longevity."

His blessing was brief but sincere.

Eleanor's eyes reddened slightly.

She beckoned. "Come here."

Alexander remained where he stood.

"Don't worry. I will live well."

Eleanor nodded. "Your marriage is my greatest concern."

Someone nearby interjected. "I heard we might meet Mrs. Vance tonight?"

Alexander's gaze swept across the room.

He did not find Evelyn.

He withdrew his eyes.

"Yes."

The crowd immediately buzzed with discussion.

"Mr. Vance has kept her hidden for two years. Finally, he's letting her be seen."

"Whoever she is, she must be extraordinary to earn such devotion."

Eleanor beamed with joy.

"My granddaughter-in-law is both brilliant and beautiful. I am very pleased."

Everyone chimed in agreement.

"A loving marriage is the greatest blessing."

"Mr. Vance is usually so stern. He must be very gentle with his wife."

Eleanor grew even happier.

The corner of Alexander's mouth lifted slightly.

He was about to continue searching for Evelyn.

His phone suddenly rang.

It was Thomas Watson.

"Boss, Miss Sophia is here."

Alexander frowned. "Who told you to bring her?"

Thomas sounded uneasy. "She insisted on coming. I couldn't stop her."

He paused. "Are you still announcing things today? Should we postpone?"

Alexander's tone was firm. "No need."

Thomas sighed. "Miss Evelyn will be criticized again."

Alexander's eyes darkened. "She is not your concern."

He rubbed his temples. "This will make Sophia Watson give up."

Thomas responded. "Understood."

Alexander asked again. "Where is she?"

Thomas replied. "Isabella took her to change clothes."

…

Evelyn led Sophia around the corridor.

Jason Lawrence leaned against the wall.

He saw Evelyn and immediately approached.

"Dr. Nan, a word."

He lowered his voice. "What exactly is your relationship with my cousin?"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "Why do you ask?"

Jason looked serious. "The company is buzzing about you two."

Evelyn nodded. "It's true."

Jason's expression was complex. "You shouldn't do this."

He wanted to say more but held back.

Evelyn saw his concern.

She was about to explain.

Jason suddenly spoke mysteriously.

"I know who Mrs. Vance is."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "Who?"

Jason whispered. "Sophia Watson."

He continued. "My cousin accompanied her for treatment abroad. They just returned recently."

He looked at Evelyn. "He's formally introducing her today."

Jason spoke earnestly. "Don't make a mistake."

Evelyn was silent.

Jason thought he had persuaded her.

"Where are you going?"

Evelyn said calmly. "To show Mrs. Ashcroft my marriage certificate."

Jason was startled. "Right, you're married too."

He followed. "I want to see who your husband is."
###Chapter 147

Another person wanted to see Evelyn's marriage certificate.

Evelyn supported Sophia as they walked.
Jason Lawrence followed behind them.
The three headed toward the lounge.

Jason spoke as they walked.
"Is your husband here yet?"
"He should make an appearance later to clear up those rumors…"

Sophia nodded.
"That's exactly what Evelyn plans to do."

Evelyn remained silent.
She reached out to push the door open.

The door did not open.
The lounge was locked.

This was Eleanor Vance's private lounge.
Eleanor was in the main hall.
Who was inside?

The lock turned.
Isabella emerged wearing a pink evening gown.

She saw Evelyn and Sophia.
She raised an eyebrow.
"Mother, Evelyn, what are you doing here?"

Sophia explained.
"Evelyn's bag is inside. We came to get it."

"You'll have to wait."
Isabella smiled brightly at Evelyn.
"Mrs. Vance is inside changing her gown!"

Evelyn was taken aback.
"Who?"

Isabella smiled even more triumphantly.
"Uncle's wife, Mrs. Vance!"
"Thomas Watson personally brought her here."
"He even asked me to help prepare a gown."
"I told you Uncle would introduce his wife tonight…"

Thomas Watson personally brought her…
Evelyn frowned.

Sophia frowned.
"Let Evelyn go in to get her bag."

Isabella pretended to be apologetic.
"Mother, this is Great-grandmother's lounge."
"Only the Vance family can enter."
"Auntie isn't feeling well."
"She shouldn't be exposed to drafts while changing."
"Better not to disturb her."

She emphasized the word "owner."
She looked at Evelyn with a smug smile.
"I'm afraid you'll all have to wait."

She linked arms with Sophia.
"Mother, Uncle is the head of the family."
"Auntie is the true lady of the house."
"Even I have to listen to her."
"Evelyn won't mind waiting, will she?"

Evelyn found it ridiculous.
"Are you sure it's really Mrs. Vance in there?"

"Of course."
Isabella stated confidently.
"Mr. Vance got married two years ago."
"The entire Vance family knows about Auntie's existence."
"Only some people refuse to believe it."
"They're still dreaming of climbing the social ladder!"

"Isabella!"
Sophia scolded.
"Watch your language!"

Isabella sneered.
"Mother, Evelyn is a Ross."
"I'm afraid she'll make a fool of herself and drag the Ashcroft name down."
"You taught us since we were young."
"Don't covet things that don't belong to you."

Sophia frowned.
"So you do remember."

But how had Isabella become like this?

Isabella didn't understand the implication.

There was movement from inside the room.
The door opened.

A girl in a blue evening gown stepped out.

Evelyn looked over.

The girl had delicate features.
She was the modest, pretty type.
Her skin was pale.
She was thin and frail.
She stood there panickedly, her eyes vacant.

Seeing so many people, she seemed to want to speak but didn't dare.
The stares made her uncomfortable.

She evoked a sense of pity.

Jason leaned close to Evelyn's ear and whispered.
"That's Sophia Watson!"

Isabella called out.
"Auntie, are you done changing?"

Sophia Watson nodded woodenly.
She tugged at her clothes unconfidently, trying to cover her exposed skin.

Her voice was small and timid.
"This dress… is it really okay?"

Everyone looked at her evening gown.

The light blue color made her sickly pale skin appear even whiter.
But her overly thin frame didn't fill out the dress.
It looked like a child playing dress-up in adult clothes.

Then there was the dress itself…

Sophia suddenly frowned and looked at Evelyn.

Not only were their dresses the same color, but the styles were also similar!

Isabella reassured her.
"It looks beautiful!"

Sophia Watson pointed timidly at Evelyn.
"Hers looks better… Should I change into something else?"

Isabella laughed.
She looked arrogantly at Evelyn.
"Evelyn, what a coincidence."
"You and Auntie have an outfit clash."
"Auntie is the lady of the house."
"Shouldn't you change your dress to show respect?"

This was basic banquet etiquette.

People usually avoided dressing similarly to the host.

Sophia wanted to say the same thing.
But she didn't know how to say it without hurting Evelyn's pride.

Evelyn didn't feel humiliated.
She sneered.
"You were there two days ago when Grandma chose this dress for me."
"You did this on purpose, didn't you?"

Isabella didn't bother hiding it.
"This dress is more suitable for the lady of the Vance family."

She stared directly at Evelyn.
"Evelyn, do you want to compete for attention with the hostess?"

Evelyn's face darkened.

Sophia took a deep breath.
"Evelyn, I'm not feeling well."
"Weren't you getting your bag?"
"Get your bag and take me home first."

It was the first time she used such a forceful tone with Evelyn.

Evelyn was taken aback.
"Madam…"

"Evelyn, if you still consider me your elder, come with me now."
Sophia grabbed her hand.
"I can't watch you humiliate yourself."

Evelyn clenched her jaw.
She sighed.
"Madam, let's look at the marriage certificate first before deciding, okay?"

Sophia didn't understand her insistence.
She could only say, "...Alright."
###Chapter 148
Evelyn ignored Isabella and walked straight toward Sophia Watson.

She was about to push the door open when Sophia spoke timidly. "May I ask who you are?"

Her voice was so soft it was almost inaudible.

Evelyn paused, frowning slightly.

Isabella immediately cut in. "Aunt Sophia, this is Evelyn Ross. Great-grandmother was confused and mistook her for you. Mr. Vance let her stay. Now everyone outside is saying she's the mistress Uncle Alexander keeps..."

"Isabella!" Sophia Ashcroft snapped sharply.

Isabella pouted but didn't continue.

The implication was crystal clear.

Evelyn ignored her, focusing only on Sophia Watson. "Miss Watson, are you truly Mrs. Vance?"

She couldn't figure this woman out.

The question was a test, and an opportunity for the other to clarify.

Isabella scoffed nearby. "Evelyn, you've outdone your mother. Daring to be this insolent in front of the lawful wife? Your mother wouldn't even dare breathe heavily in front of mine. If you want to be a mistress, at least learn your mother's ways!"

Sophia Ashcroft's face paled, her voice trembling. "Isabella!"

She didn't understand why her daughter had become so venomous.

Isabella seemed oblivious, raising her voice instead. "Am I wrong? What right does she have to question Aunt Sophia? Mom, you only tell me to shut up. Why don't you tell her to shut up?"

Sophia Ashcroft clutched her chest, shaking with anger.

Evelyn kept her eyes locked on Sophia Watson. "Answer me."

Sophia Watson looked terrified.

Her innocent deer-like eyes darted around nervously. "I... please don't be angry... My health is poor, I won't compete with you... As long as Alexander is willing to provide for me... Thank you for taking care of grandmother for me during this time..."

She lowered her head, twisting the hem of her dress. "Alexander has to take care of me, so he has no time for grandmother. I'm grateful you've been keeping her company. Alexander is grateful too..."

Evelyn clenched her fists, stepping forward. "So, are you Mrs. Vance or not?"

She had lost all patience.

This woman appeared fragile, but every word carried a sting.

She never explicitly confirmed her status, yet every statement implied she was Mrs. Vance.

Evelyn hated this ambiguity.

Sophia Watson flinched, hastily hiding behind Isabella. "I... I won't fight you... Please don't..."

Isabella immediately shielded her, accusing sharply. "What do you think you're doing? I know you have a quick temper, but this is Aunt Sophia! She's frail. If you lay a finger on her, Mr. Vance will never forgive you!"

Evelyn frowned. "I didn't touch her..."

Sophia Watson quickly agreed. "She didn't, really she didn't..."

Isabella sneered. "Only because you moved fast! Evelyn, you're getting more arrogant! Daring to threaten Mrs. Vance in front of everyone? Pressing her like this, do you want her to give up Mr. Vance for you? How shameless can you be? Bullying a sick person?!"

Rage burned in Evelyn's chest.

She took another step forward. "I just want a clear answer. You've been doing all the talking. Let her tell me herself. Is she Mrs. Vance or not?"

Isabella laughed coldly, turning back. "Aunt Sophia, don't be afraid! Show her the authority of the lawful wife! This is the Vance residence. We won't tolerate a mistress causing trouble!"

Sophia Watson nervously clutched her skirt, head bowed. "Miss Ross, I truly thank you..."

Isabella was exasperated. "Why thank her? She's stealing your husband! Stealing grandmother's affection! You're so weak, no wonder Uncle dares to fool around outside!"

Sophia Watson murmured meekly. "Don't say that... As long as Alexander doesn't abandon me..."

Isabella retorted. "If you keep this up, he'll abandon you sooner or later!"

"Slap!"

Sophia Ashcroft could take no more. She slapped Isabella across the face. "I told you to shut up! Stop stirring up trouble!"

Isabella covered her cheek, stunned. Then she roared. "Why should I shut up? She's the one in the wrong! Mom, in your eyes, I'm always wrong? I'm just helping Aunt Sophia defend her dignity! I want Evelyn to admit her mistake!"

She glared fiercely at Sophia Ashcroft. "You'd even stop this? You favor her even when she's a mistress? Have you ever considered me? How can I maintain my position in the Vance family if she acts like this here?!"

Isabella's self-righteousness made Sophia Ashcroft tremble. "You... Don't pretend to be noble! You're just targeting Evelyn!"

"Yes, I'm targeting her! So what? But if she did nothing wrong, could I target her? This time, I'm in the right! What did I do wrong?!"

Isabella pressed on aggressively. "Should we call everyone over to see this?"

Sophia Ashcroft clutched her chest, stumbling back.

"Madam!"

Evelyn moved to support her.

But Sophia Ashcroft pushed her away.

Evelyn looked up, startled, meeting Sophia Ashcroft's red-rimmed eyes.

Sophia Ashcroft seemed on the verge of collapse. "It's my fault... I failed to raise you both properly. That's why you've become like this!"

She looked at Evelyn with disappointment. "I always thought you were a good child... But today, Evelyn, you've disappointed me so much!"

Sophia Ashcroft's choked voice, her disappointed gaze...

It felt like an invisible hand squeezing Evelyn's heart.

She clenched her fists tightly, suddenly turning and rushing into the lounge.

She grabbed her bag and strode back out.

Feeling wronged, she pulled her marriage certificate from the bag and thrust it toward Sophia Ashcroft.
###Chapter 149

Evelyn Ross knew any words from her would be futile.

These people would never believe her.

Just like the last class reunion…

She didn't want to waste her breath anymore.

She only wanted to present the evidence for Sophia Ashcroft to see the truth.

She gazed at Sophia, her voice calm. "Madam, Mr. Vance and I are indeed—"

Before she could finish, a hand suddenly reached over and snatched the marriage certificate from Sophia's grasp.

Isabella Ashcroft's sharp voice cut her off. "You think a fake document can fool everyone? Evelyn Ross, do you take us all for fools?"

She didn't even glance at the certificate.

She tried to tear it with force, but her manicure made it difficult.

She simply threw it to the ground violently.

Her high heel stomped on it, grinding it repeatedly.

Evelyn's pupils constricted sharply.

She grabbed Isabella's arm and slapped her across the face.

Slap!

The crisp sound of the slap echoed through the hallway.

A clear palm print immediately appeared on Isabella's cheek.

Evelyn's eyes were icy. "Isabella Ashcroft, do you believe I will call the police right now? Damaging a government document is a crime that can land you in jail."

Isabella's fingers tightened.

She glanced at Sophia, then straightened her back. "Fine! Call them! Better yet, call the reporters too! Let's see who should be condemned! A mistress like you, or a righteous person like me!"

Evelyn's voice was severe. "I said I am not a mistress! Alexander Vance and I are—"

Pat.

Something lightly brushed against her cheek.

Evelyn's words stopped abruptly.

She froze in place, turning her head slowly in disbelief towards Sophia, who had struck her.

Sophia's eyes were red-rimmed, her body trembling slightly.

She stared at Evelyn. "Evelyn, if you still listen to me, leave this place with me now!"

Evelyn's eyes widened in shock.

It didn't hurt at all…

Sophia ultimately couldn't bring herself to use force.

Her cheek had only been lightly grazed.

She had been beaten by Linda Lowell growing up.

This was the lightest slap she had ever received.

But Evelyn had never felt such heartache before.

Her heart felt like it had been brutally crushed.

The pain made it almost impossible to breathe.

She clenched her fists tightly, staring blankly at Sophia.

It took her a long moment to find her voice. "...You don't believe me either?"

Sophia hadn't expected to hit her either.

She looked down at her trembling fingers and smiled bitterly.

She had always believed in Evelyn's character.

As a child, this girl would rather starve and rummage through trash bins.

She was nearly dying of hunger, yet she wouldn't steal the cake on the table…

Back then, Sophia had held a special admiration for Evelyn.

She gradually grew fond of her.

She never expected her to do something like this…

Sophia had made inquiries during this time.

Alexander Vance had gotten married two years ago.

Evelyn only received her certificate this year.

It never occurred to her that they could be husband and wife.

The photo Isabella showed her last time wasn't doctored. It was real…

Anyone could see the affection in Evelyn and Alexander's eyes in that photo…

How could she believe it?!

Sophia closed her eyes, dizzy with anger.

A sense of powerlessness washed over her.

In the end, she could only say to Evelyn, "You should go…"

Evelyn was stunned. "Madam, it's not what you think. Mr. Vance and I—"

Seeing this, Isabella felt extremely pleased.

Evelyn was too good at fighting, making Isabella always feel helpless.

But there was one person Evelyn would never fight back against: Sophia Ashcroft!

She said sarcastically, "Can't you hear what my mother said? She wants you to get out! She never wants to see a rotten person like you again! You and your mother are both shady mistresses!"

Evelyn still looked at Sophia. "Madam…"

Isabella stepped forward, placing herself between them. "Are you leaving or not? Staying here to anger my mother to death so your mother can take her place? What are you really plotting?!"

As soon as she finished speaking, Sophia swayed and collapsed softly to the ground!

"Sophia!"

William Ashcroft hurried over, supporting Sophia's faltering body.

He looked around in shock. "What happened?"

Isabella immediately said, "Mom thinks it's too shameful for Evelyn to be Mr. Vance's mistress here. She told her to leave, but she refused. Mom got angry!"

William glared furiously at Evelyn. "You wretched girl! Sophia has been so good to you since you were little! Has your conscience been eaten by dogs?!"

Evelyn looked at them and suddenly felt she had nothing to say.

She let out a low laugh, finding it all rather pointless.

No one had patiently listened to her explanation…

She lowered her gaze, her expression faint. "Madam, please take care of your health. Don't get angry because of me."

After saying this, she turned and left.

Sophia leaned against William, watching her retreating figure.

She wanted to reach out and call her back but didn't know what to say…

Isabella pointed to the lounge. "Dad, Mom, please go in and rest for a while…"

William helped Sophia inside.

After a long while, Sophia finally caught her breath.

William angrily scolded, "Sophia, why are you getting so angry over an ungrateful girl like that? She's not worth it! Her mother is shameless. She only had her by scheming against me back then. She has Linda Lowell's blood in her veins. She's selfish and has no morals…"

Sophia's gaze was vacant, her heart filled with disappointment.

"You always said Isabella wasn't as good as her. Now look, Evelyn's moral compass is far worse than Isabella's! No matter what, Isabella would never stoop so low as to be a mistress!"

William held Sophia's hand. "Sophia, promise me, stop caring about Evelyn Ross from now on. Draw a clear line. Don't anger yourself anymore, okay?"

Sophia closed her eyes in pain.

Had Evelyn really gone astray?

She clenched her fists tightly, still unable to believe it, but the facts were right before her eyes…

Seeing her silent, William said decisively, "Are you feeling better? If you are, let's go. Showing our faces today is enough to satisfy the Vances."

Sophia stood up shakily.

With William's support, she walked out of the lounge.

The outside had quieted down. Everyone had gone to the main hall.

As soon as they stepped out, they saw a marriage certificate lying alone on the ground.

Sophia paused, then stepped forward and picked it up.
###Chapter 150

The marriage certificate still bore a shoe print, stained and dirty.

Sophia Ashcroft took out a silk handkerchief and carefully wiped the cover.
Her eyelashes were lowered.
"Isabella can be as willful as she wants, but she shouldn't have damaged Evelyn's marriage certificate."

William Ashcroft snorted coldly.
"That doesn't justify hitting her! That Evelyn, with her rude and wild temper, why do you even bother with her?"

Sophia didn't respond.
After cleaning it, she was about to open it when William snatched the certificate from her hand.
"What's there to see? We've met that husband of hers, haven't we? A construction worker who lets his wife be someone else's mistress. Looking at such a useless man would only dirty your eyes!"

Sophia pressed her lips together tightly.
Finally, she said softly, "Before we leave, remember to return the marriage certificate to Evelyn…"

"…Fine. Sophia, you're just too soft-hearted."

They walked towards the banquet hall as they spoke.

…

Evelyn Ross did not return to the main hall.
She went alone to the back garden.

This winter was exceptionally bitter.
The cold wind cut through her thin evening gown, chilling her to the bone.
But this physical cold was nothing compared the ice in her heart.

She didn't blame Sophia for misunderstanding her.
She had no right to blame her.

Sophia was the only light in her dark childhood.

It was Sophia who taught her self-respect and self-love.
Who taught her she didn't have to live by others' opinions.

It was Sophia who, when Isabella enrolled in school, insisted on handling the procedures for her too, allowing her to receive an education.

Her first doll as a child was a gift from Sophia.
Though Isabella later secretly tore it to pieces.

Her first evening gown was also given by Sophia.
Who told her that beauty was for pleasing oneself.
Though Linda Lowell later forcibly took it off her and gave it to Isabella.

It was Sophia who helped her break free from Linda's control in middle school.
And who provided the identification documents for her college entrance exams, giving her the chance to attend university…

Without Sophia, she might have died in infancy.
Or succumbed to childhood beatings.
Or vanished into some remote mountain village after being trafficked…
Even if she had survived, she would now be just an empty shell.

She always held a deep, inseparable attachment to Sophia.

That's why being driven away moments ago had felt so utterly painful.

She stood quietly by the artificial lake, gazing at the mist-shrouded surface.
The winter fog of San Francisco blurred her vision.
It felt as if her future path was equally shrouded and uncertain.

In this darkest moment, a warm coat was suddenly draped over her shoulders.

Evelyn turned in surprise.
###Chapter 151

Alexander Vance stood behind her, having arrived at some unnoticed moment.
The night softened the sharpness he carried by day.
Only gentleness remained in his features.

He looked at her deeply, his voice low.
"Why are you out here alone? Aren't you cold?"

Evelyn's nose tingled with emotion.

Seeing him, all her pent-up grievances surged forth.
Her eyes grew hot.

She blinked hard, trying to force back the tears…

He suddenly pulled her into his embrace.

The warmth of his chest seeped through the fabric.
It slowly thawed her frozen body…

Somehow, every time she was sad or down, he always appeared at just the right moment.

Evelyn's emotions gradually settled.
She gently pushed him away.
"We should go back."

"Mm." The corner of Alexander's mouth lifted slightly.
"Grandmother is having people look for you everywhere. She can't wait to announce our relationship."

He took Evelyn's hand.
Suddenly, he looked at her seriously.
"Miss Ross, you know what announcing our relationship means, right?"

His intense gaze made her heartbeat quicken.
She asked, playing dumb.
"What does it mean?"

Alexander didn't spell it out.
He just said solemnly, "The marriage of the Vance Group CEO must be stable. Otherwise, it affects the company's stock price. So… once it's announced, we are husband and wife for life."

Evelyn was about to joke that it was a coincidence, her company was going public too…

But he seemed to see through her thoughts.
His voice suddenly dropped, low and magnetic.
He spoke first, word by word.
"Miss Ross, you know the situation with the Vance family. The road ahead is full of variables. I might be expelled from the Vance Group. I could end up with nothing. Knowing that, are you still willing to spend the rest of your life with me?"

Evelyn was stunned.

His words were realistic.
They carried a barely perceptible anxiety.
As if he was uncertain of her answer.

Evelyn tilted her head and smiled.
"If you really end up penniless… well, I'll support you then."

Alexander was taken aback.
Then he chuckled softly.

He had other arrangements beyond the Vance Group…
His words were just a warning about the difficult road ahead.
He hadn't expected this response.

This girl always surprised him.

From their first meeting.
To every subsequent encounter.
Somewhere along the way, he had fallen for her…
Maybe it was her quirky way of changing identities to get close to him.
Maybe it was her stubborn eyes when threatened by Julian Evans.
Maybe it was the confident curve of her lips.
Or maybe it was that inexplicable sense of familiarity the first time he saw her.

He remembered going to the Ashcroft residence to propose.
As soon as he entered, he glimpsed a girl quietly watching him from the shadows in a corner.

Her eyes were clear.
Holding only pure curiosity and assessment.

She thought she was hidden in the darkness.
But the shadows couldn't conceal the light she carried…

This was probably love at first sight.
That grew deeper with time.

Everything was just right.

Alexander looked down at her gently.
He said softly, "Alright. Then you'll have to work hard to earn money. My expenses aren't small."

"…No problem."

Only after replying did Evelyn realize.
At some point, Alexander had already interlaced his fingers with hers.

She paused slightly.

She was used to walking alone.
It wasn't that she hadn't thought about finding a partner.
Suitors had never been lacking.

But she never felt she needed to rely on anyone.

Now, however, she felt it was good to have him by her side.

A phrase suddenly surfaced in her mind.
To hold your hand, and grow old with you.

Hand in hand, they walked towards the banquet hall.

Their steps were firm.
They smiled at each other.
Side by side, they stepped through the back door.

At the same time, Sophia and William Ashcroft entered through the front hall.
They were looking for her everywhere.
Evelyn Ross followed Alexander Vance through the back entrance of the banquet hall.

She was about to head toward the main hall.

A maid suddenly rushed over from the side.

The maid's tray was filled with glasses of red wine.

She stumbled, about to crash into Evelyn.

Evelyn instinctively raised her arm to block.

Her waist was suddenly pulled tight.

Alexander shielded her behind him.

The sound of shattering glass echoed.

Red wine splashed across his white dress shirt.

A deep red stain quickly spread.

"Mr. Vance, Miss Ross, I'm so sorry..." The maid apologized in a panicked, trembling voice.

Evelyn looked up at the man in front of her.

She had always faced storms alone.

But now, someone was shielding her.

Alexander carefully checked if she was injured.

He sighed with relief after confirming she was unharmed.

He frowned at the wet, sticky feeling of his shirt.

He said sternly to the maid, "Be more careful. Don't bump into guests."

"Yes, yes..."

The maid hurriedly knelt to clean up the mess.

Alexander turned to Evelyn. "I'm going upstairs to change. Please stay with Grandma for now."

He couldn't reveal their relationship looking so disheveled.

"Alright."

Evelyn watched him go upstairs, then walked into the main hall alone.

The back door led to a lounge area.

Julian Evans's high school friends were gathered there chatting.

It was the same group as last time.

Isabella Ashcroft had been unable to find a job since being fired.

Her face was dark, her tone sharp. "I just saw Mrs. Vance. Evelyn was lying last time! She's clearly Mr. Vance's mistress! How shameless!"

James Klein tried to defend her. "There must be some misunderstanding..."

"What misunderstanding could there be?" Isabella sneered. "Mr. Vance himself said he was her man that day! Just wait until Mr. Vance and Mrs. Vance make their announcement. See how you explain it then!"

James was speechless.

Evelyn didn't approach them.

She looked toward where Mrs. Eleanor Vance usually sat.

The matriarch wasn't there. She might have gone to the restroom.

Evelyn stood waiting.

A commotion suddenly arose at the entrance.

Evelyn turned and saw Linda Lowell had slipped inside.

Linda was dressed plainly, standing out sharply among the glamorous crowd.

Guests whispered among themselves. "Who is that?"

Evelyn frowned, knowing Linda was there for her.

She moved to intercept her mother.

But Isabella was faster. "Aunt Linda, are you here to see Evelyn?"

Her voice was loud, drawing everyone's attention.

Linda put on a smile. "No, I'm here to see the lady of the Vance house..."

“Alexander, divorce her immediately!”

Alexander’s voice was icy.
“Reason?”

“Reason?” Robert sneered.
“Do you even know this woman’s background? She’s worse than Isabella! She’s a love child! Someone of that status is fit to be the lady of the Vance family?!”

Richard Evans chimed in.
“Alexander, Father is doing this for your own good. Didn’t you hear all the gossip just now?”

Evelyn lowered her eyes, her jaw tight.

But her hand was firmly held by the man beside her.
Alexander’s voice was low.
“I didn’t hear it.”

“…”

Richard was speechless for a moment, then gestured.
“Julian, tell your uncle what you heard.”

Julian stepped forward.
His eyes were fixed fiercely on Evelyn as he repeated the ugly remarks.

“They said this Ashcroft love child is incredibly cunning. Who knows what method she used to latch onto the Vance family!”

“They also said you’re bewitched, Uncle. Marrying her is worse than marrying a commoner. It’s utterly humiliating!”

“Some even said that with a love child as the lady of the Vance family, who would want to invite the Vances anywhere? Should they let a love child lord it over them?”

“There was even filthier talk I won’t repeat! In short, everyone is laughing at you, Uncle!”

Julian’s gaze was sinister.
When it swept over Evelyn, it held a destructive madness.

He said slowly, “Uncle, Evelyn is somewhat attractive. I pursued her for four years in college, but she always hid her status as a love child! I let go immediately after learning the truth! Families like ours could never marry a love child. It would make us the laughingstock of the city! Just like now, Uncle, you’re being pointed at and mocked…”

Alexander suddenly interrupted.
“What were you doing while they were pointing and mocking?”

Julian was taken aback.
“What?”

Alexander’s eyes were cold.
“The lady of the Vance family was being insulted, and you did nothing? Is this the Vance dignity you uphold?”

Julian’s Adam’s apple bobbed.

Richard hurriedly smoothed things over.
“Alexander, it was Grandma’s birthday gala today. Were we supposed to cause a scene at the party?”

“Is gossiping about the lady of the Vance family in private their idea of manners?”

Alexander stared directly at Julian.
“If you didn’t dare to confront them, give me the list. I’ll go find them myself.”

“…” Julian froze.
How could he hand it over? He’d offend the entire circle of San Francisco’s rich heirs!

Seeing his cowardice, Evelyn shot him a contemptuous glance.

That single glance ignited Julian’s rage.
He blurted out, “Uncle! Is this kind of woman worth it? You probably don’t know this, do you? You’ve been married for two years, but she was stringing me along in college! She almost accepted my confession! This promiscuous woman plays men like toys! If she weren’t so low-class, I would have—”

Thud!

Alexander kicked him flying.

Julian crashed heavily to the floor, curling up in pain.

Richard was furious.
“Alexander! What are you doing?! We can talk this out! How could you resort to violence?!”

“Big Brother, he was disrespectful to his elder. Since you didn’t teach him properly, I’ll do it for you!” Alexander’s expression was terrifying.
“If I hear you disrespect your aunt again, you’ll face family discipline!”

Julian trembled in fear.

Richard’s hand shook with anger.
“Alexander! You’ve gone too far! Dad…”

Smack!

Robert slammed the table.
“Alexander! You only have two choices today! Either divorce this love child! Or resign as CEO of the Vance Group!”

Alexander let out a cold laugh.

The butler cautiously entered.

“Sir, Mr. and Mrs. Roland have returned… They said they need to speak with you and Mrs… Evelyn.”
###Chapter 159

#Vance CEO's Wife Is A Love Child#

#Mistress Crashes Birthday Gala#

Various shocking headlines flooded the screen.

###Chapter 160

The night was deep.

Moonlight hung low, casting a silvery glow.

Evelyn Ross stepped out of the Vance mansion.

A cool breeze brushed her face, yet the knot in her chest had already loosened.

She spotted Mr. and Mrs. Roland standing not far away.

She quickened her pace toward them. "What happened to Chloe?"

"It's not about Chloe," Mrs. Roland said gently. "We were about to leave when we saw Linda Lowell making a scene at the Vance gate."

"We thought we should warn you."

Evelyn was slightly taken aback. "Thank you, Mrs. Roland."

She had thought they returned for something urgent.

It was just this.

Mrs. Roland spoke again. "We just called Ryan."

"We urged him to bring the restored photo as soon as possible."

"Are you free tomorrow night? We'd like to invite you to dinner."

She especially emphasized Evelyn seeing it.

Though unsure of the reason, she trusted Mrs. Roland meant no harm.

She nodded immediately. "I'm free."

Mrs. Roland gently patted her hand.

She took Mr. Roland's arm and turned to leave.
