"Without evidence, it only causes unnecessary worry."

"Once Ryan brings the photo tomorrow, everything will be clear."

"It's just one day."

Mr. Roland smiled. "Whatever you say."

Their whispers drifted on the wind.

Evelyn couldn't catch every word.

But watching their retreating figures, she felt an inexplicable warmth.

She thought of William Ashcroft's constant caution around his wife, Sophia.

His efforts to please her seemed overly deliberate and subservient.

It was far less natural and harmonious than the Rolands.

Since childhood, everyone said William adored Sophia.

But Sophia was aloof, not warm toward him.

People said it was because of Evelyn and Linda's existence.

That they made William unable to hold his head high before Sophia...

Evelyn shook her head, feeling she overthought it.

The Ashcroft family's dynamics were indeed strange.

Sophia, so proud, yet tolerated her husband's love child and mistress.

William disliked Evelyn, his illegitimate daughter.

He believed she ruined his marital relationship.

Yet Sophia treated Evelyn with exceptional gentleness...

Evelyn truly couldn't understand how a couple should interact.

"What are you thinking?" Alexander Vance's deep voice sounded by her ear.

Evelyn looked up. "What do you think we'll be like when we're old?"

The Rolands had reached their car.

Mr. Roland opened the door, carefully shielding the top to prevent Mrs. Roland from bumping her head.

Mrs. Roland turned and gave him a soft smile.

Alexander also saw this, a flicker of envy in his eyes. "Probably like the Rolands."

Evelyn was about to say he didn't have Mr. Roland's good temper.

But he continued, "With children, relying on each other for companionship."

"How many children do you want to have?"

Evelyn fell silent.

The topic shifted a bit too quickly.

She coughed lightly, quickly changing the subject. "Let's take Grandma home first."

Alexander nodded.

They returned to the banquet hall.

Nannies were cleaning up the aftermath.

The hall felt particularly spacious after the noise.

But Evelyn preferred this tranquility after the excitement.

She held Mrs. Eleanor Vance's arm. "Grandma, let's go back."

"Alright."

Mrs. Vance affectionately patted her hand.

She then glared fiercely at Robert Vance.

Robert's face was cold. "Mom, don't look at me."

"Your granddaughter-in-law has questionable origins. She's a love child."

"Even if she escaped today, if she ever does anything to shame the Vance family, I won't be lenient!"

He snorted coldly and went upstairs.

Mrs. Vance pointed at his retreating back, furious. "Ungrateful son! Biased!"

"You favor your eldest son, so I'll favor my youngest grandson!"

She spoke like a petulant child.

She tightly grasped Evelyn's hand. "Marrying my granddaughter-in-law is the Vance family's blessed fortune!"

"My granddaughter-in-law would never shame the Vances!"

"Right, boy?"

Alexander glanced at Evelyn with a smile. "Yes."

As the three walked toward the backyard, a car horn sounded from the front.

Fiona hurried over to report. "Miss Isabella has been sent back."

This was expected.

Isabella was pregnant.

The police couldn't hold her long.

At most, a verbal warning, a fine, and an apology.

Evelyn didn't pay it much mind.

It wasn't until she lay down at night and picked up her phone that she saw Isabella's string of abusive messages.

Pregnancy and tonight's humiliation had pushed Isabella over the edge.

She raged in the messages:

[Evelyn Ross, you love child, born trash! How dare you marry Alexander?!]

[You just want to one-up me, don't you?!]

[Well, forget it! You'll never surpass me in this lifetime! I still hold the household authority!]

[Think marrying Alexander makes you a phoenix? You can't change your origins!]

[You can't wash away the original sin in your blood!]

[Two years married and no pregnancy? That's your lowly blood's retribution!]

[I'll soon give birth to the Vance family's legitimate eldest grandson! You'll never compare!]

Evelyn stared at the glaring words "love child" on the screen.

Her jaw tightened.

She quickly replied: [Are you sure the child is a Vance's?]

The other side immediately fell silent.

No response for a long time.

Evelyn lowered her eyes.

Those three words had stirred her emotions...

The night passed without incident.

The next morning, Evelyn woke and checked her phone.

A message from Chloe Roland: [Savior, my brother fixed the photo!]

[He's on his way to San Francisco! He'll come find us once he wakes up!]

[Mom and Dad say they have a big surprise to show you!]
###Chapter 161

Photo?

Evelyn Ross paused, her fingers hovering over the screen.

She replied to Chloe Roland: "It's a six-hour drive from New York to San Francisco. What time will your brother arrive?"

Chloe responded instantly: "Around noon! My brother got the photo this morning and sent it to our parents immediately. They were extremely emotional and insisted on speaking with you in person."

Evelyn was taken aback.

Communication was so advanced now.

What couldn't be discussed over the phone?

They had to meet face to face…

That photo seemed related to Mrs. Nan.

Mrs. Nan was Sophia Ashcroft's mother…

This matter appeared to be of great significance.

She warmly took Isabella Ashcroft's hand. "Julian and Isabella are coming too. You can ride, right, Isabella?"

Isabella smiled and nodded. "Of course. My father took me to the club since I was a child. I even had my own pony… You remember that, Evelyn? I was too scared to ride back then. You had to lead the pony around for me."

Evelyn's eyes darkened.

Horseback riding was popular in elite circles.

When she was seven, Isabella got her own pony.

She wore exquisite riding gear, excitedly petting the small horse.

Evelyn could only hold the reins for her.

Isabella would yell while riding, "Faster! Did you not eat?"

…She really hadn't.

At seven years old, she had to attend to Isabella first thing in the morning. Linda Lowell never gave her time for breakfast.

She hated the weekly riding lessons the most.

Isabella was like a princess, while she…

Isabella's voice interrupted her thoughts. "Evelyn, come with us today. I might still need your help mounting. I haven't been back to the Ashcroft family for years, and I'm still not used to getting on a horse."

Evelyn's fists clenched abruptly.

That was her humiliating past.

She didn't kneel voluntarily.

Linda Lowell forced her head down, allowing Isabella to trample her dignity…

Her jaw tightened as she prepared to speak.

But Isabella lowered her head first. "We were young and didn't know any better back then, Evelyn. Please don't hold it against me…"

Evelyn let out a scornful laugh.

Young?

Isabella had always been rotten to the core.

She knew exactly how to hit Linda's pressure points, how to make Evelyn suffer more.

Just like now, apologizing with her words while her eyes gleamed with venom.

Evelyn looked at her coldly. "You said the wrong thing."

Isabella bowed her head. "Evelyn, I'm sorry. I shouldn't have brought up your childhood. I didn't mean to…"

Margaret immediately chimed in. "Evelyn, Isabella didn't do it on purpose. Stop being so aggressive! Besides, you were just a love child back then. Wasn't it natural for her to bully you?"

Eleanor was left speechless.

But Evelyn smiled. "Sister-in-law, I never said you couldn't use that term."

Margaret scoffed. "Then why did you say Isabella was wrong? Isn't it because she mentioned your childhood? How is that different from forbidding it?"

Isabella added, "Evelyn, I already apologized. You…"

Smack!

Evelyn raised her hand and delivered a stinging slap across Isabella's face.

Isabella covered her cheek, stunned.

Margaret shrieked, "Evelyn! How dare you? This is not your place to cause trouble!"

Isabella was rendered speechless.

Evelyn turned to Margaret. "Sister-in-law, it's fine to defend your daughter-in-law. But even a love child like me understands respect for elders. Don't you, a lady from a good family, know that? As an elder, I'm disciplining a junior, teaching her manners to avoid public embarrassment. Do you have a problem with that?"

Margaret was also left speechless.

Evelyn fixed her cold gaze on Isabella. "What were you trying to say just now? Say it again properly!"

Isabella seethed, her chest heaving, but she could only grit her teeth and say, "... Aunt Evelyn, at the club today, may I trouble you to assist me onto my horse?"

A deep voice came from upstairs. "Since you know she's your aunt, you should understand that an elder does not assist a junior onto a horse!"

Alexander Vance looked at Isabella with a cold, stern gaze, a flicker of murderous intent in his eyes.

Isabella recoiled in fear.

Margaret hurriedly interjected, "That won't do! Your身份 was just revealed last night. If you skip an important event today, people will think the Mrs. Vance is unfit for high society and that she's an embarrassment to you!"

A message from Chloe Roland: "So angry! My brother arrived in San Francisco but didn't come home. He went to the Equestrian Club with some公子哥 from New York! Said he'll see you tonight. This is too much!"

A smile touched Evelyn's lips. "I'll go."
###Chapter 162

A dark glint flashed in Isabella's eyes.

She lowered her gaze to conceal her excitement, exchanging a look with Margaret Evans.

They both smiled knowingly.

What was something the lower class never experienced, but the upper class considered commonplace?

Equestrianism, of course.

A single lesson cost enough to deter ordinary families.

A thoroughbred foal was worth millions.

Annual upkeep expenses were at least three hundred thousand.

Even the middle class struggled to afford it.

Only the truly powerful could indulge in equestrian sports.

Today, they had to make Evelyn Ross humiliate herself at the club.

They needed to give Robert Vance a reason to strip Alexander Vance of his position as family head.

This gathering was for young people.

Margaret Evans and Richard Evans couldn't attend.

Julian remained cold-faced the entire time.

His gaze was fixed on Evelyn ahead.

His expression was dark and unreadable.

A bodyguard in black stopped her.

Evelyn wore a white riding outfit.

Her posture was as straight and proud as a poplar tree.

Her stunning features glowed under the sunlight.

Alexander was in his usual black suit.

His sharp, handsome face was all severe angles.

Alexander narrowed his long, narrow eyes.

His dark pupils were bottomless.

His stare sent a chill down Isabella's spine.

She stared at Alexander in disbelief.

His stern face showed he wasn't joking.

She looked at Evelyn, who also seemed surprised.

Finally, she glanced at Julian.

Her husband merely frowned and stayed silent.

Isabella clenched her hands hard.

A wave of humiliation crashed over her.

How could this be...

Evelyn had always been her shadow since childhood.

Someone she could summon and dismiss at will.

Always meek and subservient.

Now, she was being stepped on by Evelyn!

How dare that bitch!

Isabella wanted to tear that face to shreds.

She wanted to devour her flesh.

But she didn't dare.

She could only grit her teeth and endure the shame.

She lowered her head and walked to the car, pulling the door open.

She forced the words through her teeth. "Aunt, please."

Evelyn glanced down and got into the car without a word.

Isabella slammed the door shut.

The window rolled up slowly.

Blocking out Isabella's twisted expression.

She knew.

Alexander had heard Isabella's provocation upstairs.

He was deliberately humiliating her in public.

She explained softly. "When she first learned to ride, she made me lead the horse on foot. After she could ride, she didn't need me anymore."

After all, people can't outrun horses.

She smiled. "I used to envy her when I watched her ride."

Before she could finish, Alexander took her hand.

His voice was low and tender. "You like riding? I'll teach you today."

Evelyn blinked, swallowing the rest of her sentence.

The corners of her lips lifted. "Okay."

Alexander gazed at her. "Everything you envied in the past, I will give it all to you from now on."

Evelyn nodded solemnly.

The motorcade arrived at the equestrian club.

The Vance family had a designated parking area.

After getting out, they headed to the changing rooms.

Evelyn was already in her riding clothes.

Alexander needed to change.

She waited outside the changing room.

Isabella and Julian were nowhere to be seen.

They were probably afraid of being ordered around in public again.

That was fine.

At least she could have some peace for a while.

"I heard some big shots from New York are here. One of them absolutely cannot be offended!"

"Which young master from New York isn't difficult to deal with?"

"This one is different... It's that Xu family!"

The other person gasped. "The New York Xu family?"

"Keep your voice down! Even the San Francisco Vance family has to bow their heads to that one. We must be extra careful today. Alexander Vance cannot be offended, but that one is even more untouchable!"

"Understood. Better to offend the Vances than to provoke that one..."

The two whispered as they walked away.

Evelyn watched their retreating figures.

Among the guests from New York, Ryan Roland should be there.

Just as she thought this, the changing room door opened.

Ryan Roland stepped out.

His gold-rimmed glasses paired with a black riding outfit.

It accentuated his tall, slender figure.

He looked surprised to see Evelyn.

He walked over. "Miss Ross, what a coincidence."

The previous awkwardness of mistaking her identity had faded.

Since Evelyn was on good terms with his parents, she asked directly. "Mrs. Roland asked me to come see some photos at your place tonight. What photos are they? Are they related to me?"

Ryan recalled the photo of a face strikingly similar to Evelyn's.

He smiled slightly. "They are."
###Chapter 163

Eighty Percent Resemblance!

Related?

Evelyn Ross's eyes flashed with confusion. "What relation?"

Ryan Roland thought of his parents' interest in her. "It's a long story," he said quietly. "It seems to involve the previous generation's grudges. My father knows the details. Chloe said you agreed to meet tonight? After the event, we can go see them together?"

Evelyn wasn't in a hurry.

Ryan studied her. "I heard you are Mrs. Vance?"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "Is there a problem?"

His gaze was deep.

Chloe had pestered him all last night. She said despite Alexander Vance publicly acknowledging Evelyn, the gala was still full of nasty whispers.

People mocked Evelyn's lowly background. A love child was utterly unworthy of the Vance family.

They said the high-society ladies' circle would never accept a mistress's daughter...

Chloe was worried. She insisted he help.

These New York socialites were Ryan's personal connections. Introducing them to Evelyn would at least improve her standing within the Vance household...

The most elite families and corporations in the country are in New York. Alexander Vance's stubbornness will eventually cost him his position with the board! As his wife, if you can help him secure connections in New York, the Vances will surely accept you."

They want to break into the New York market to use against Alexander Vance. These New York heirs suddenly showing up in San Francisco seem to be the elder branch's doing. This equestrian event is part of Mrs. Ashcroft's deal with the Vances! The condition for Julian Evans marrying Isabella was that Mrs. Ashcroft would introduce the elder branch to New York's social circles."

Evelyn fell silent.

She had been there when Mrs. Ashcroft, in poor health, visited the Vances. She didn't know what was discussed between Mrs. Ashcroft and Margaret Evans the next day. But Isabella and Julian registered their marriage and entered the Vance household.

So Mrs. Ashcroft arranged this equestrian club gathering to introduce Julian to the New York heirs?

She pressed her lips together, feeling a pang of worry for Alexander Vance.

A faint ache also bloomed in her heart...

Mrs. Ashcroft hadn't openly taken sides in the conflict between the elder branch and Alexander Vance. But this move undoubtedly aided the elder branch and helped Isabella.

Of course...

Isabella was her biological daughter. Besides, her relationship with Alexander Vance wasn't public then.

Mrs. Ashcroft choosing to help the elder branch was only natural.

He immediately saw Ryan and Evelyn together.

Evelyn wore white riding gear. Ryan was in blue. They stood side by side... strikingly well-matched.

Ryan wore a gentle smile.

A sharp sense of crisis surged in Alexander's heart. He strode over, took Evelyn's hand, and eyed Ryan warily, asserting his claim. "Mr. Roland, do you need something from my wife?"

Ryan was baffled. "Nothing urgent, just—"

"If it's not urgent, then don't keep us from riding."

Alexander's tone was icy. He turned to Evelyn. "Didn't you want to learn to ride? I'll teach you."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "...Alright."

Hand in hand, they walked away, leaving Ryan utterly bewildered!

This couple still had the mind to go riding?!

Was today about riding horses?!

It was like the emperor wasn't anxious, but the eunuch was!

Bah!

He was no eunuch.

Ryan frowned, muttering to himself, then called out, "You're leaving just like that?"

Alexander paused and looked back. "Or what?"

Ryan frowned. "The people from New York. I can introduce you."

Alexander's gaze turned cold. "Not needed."

"Why are you so stubborn!" Ryan was genuinely annoyed. "The Watsons are the Watsons. The others have no grudge against you. If this continues, how will you secure your position as chairman!"

Alexander's expression remained aloof, radiating powerful confidence. "Whether I secure my position or not won't affect the cooperation between the Vance and Roland families."

Ryan: "...That's not what I meant!"

"Oh." Alexander's attitude was distant. "Is there anything else, Mr. Roland?"

Seeing his cold demeanor, Ryan sighed inwardly.

He understood. Alexander deeply resented his maternal relatives.

Putting himself in those shoes, anyone cut out of their mother's womb at seven months, nearly dying, would hold no love for their birth mother.

Watching the couple head towards a secluded corner, he could only remind them, "Miss Ross, don't forget our appointment to see the photo tonight."

Alexander stopped walking, frowning. "What photo?"

Evelyn explained calmly, "Mrs. Roland said there's a photo related to me. I'm going to take a look."

Alexander looked displeased at Ryan. "Will he be there?"

Evelyn didn't understand. "Yes."

Alexander said directly, "Can't we see it now?"

"We can..."

Ryan smiled.

He leisurely took out his phone and opened the photographed image.

In the photo, a twenty-years-younger Mr. Roland stood beside a well-preserved woman in her forties.

The woman bore an eighty percent resemblance to Evelyn Ross!
###Chapter 164

Ryan Roland stared at his phone screen.

The restored photo from New York had initially made him think his father was overreacting.

But when the image appeared in its entirety, he was completely stunned.

The woman in the photo was absolutely related to Evelyn Ross by blood!

There were many beautiful women, but few in the world could combine ordinary features into such a dazzlingly radiant appearance.

Evelyn followed Alexander Vance back, moving closer to see the photo.

Ryan suddenly put away his phone. "It's possible, but my father wants to speak with Miss Ross in person. Mr. Vance, are you unwilling to let her go?"

Evelyn pressed her lips together.

Chloe Roland was innocent and kind-hearted. Evelyn cherished this friend deeply.

They had been spending a lot of time together recently. But Alexander's clear aversion to the Roland family made her hesitate...

Should she abandon friendship for love?

...She couldn't do it.

Evelyn was never the type to prioritize romance above all else. She wanted both her friends and her lover!

As she was thinking, Alexander frowned and looked at her. "Whether you go or not is your decision. I won't interfere."

Only then did Evelyn smile.

Alexander then turned to Ryan. "There's no need to try driving a wedge between us, Mr. Roland."

Ryan: !

That wasn't his intention at all! He simply felt Evelyn shouldn't be constrained. She deserved a wider world.

He wanted to explain, but Alexander had already taken Evelyn's hand and left, leaving Ryan alone and frustrated.

Alexander's final comment was definitely deliberate!

She stole glances at the man beside her.

A fitted black top accentuated his lean waist. Matching riding pants were tucked into leather boots, highlighting his long legs. His usual composed demeanor was replaced by a dashing, heroic aura, as if he were about to spur a horse into a gallop and conquer the world.

...So handsome.

Evelyn hurriedly looked away.

The club wasn't crowded. Most guests were chatting in the lounge area.

Staff brought over a majestic Akhal-Teke horse. Its pale golden coat shimmered under the sun, its eyes rebellious and untamed.

Alexander introduced it. "This is my horse, Zephyr."

Evelyn was slightly taken aback.

She knew this horse...

Years ago, an unruly Akhal-Teke had arrived in San Francisco. She had personally tamed it before selling it on.

Could Alexander be the buyer?

As they approached, the proud horse suddenly lowered its head. Its eyes clearly showed panic upon seeing Evelyn.

Alexander gently stroked its head, looking at Evelyn. "Zephyr has a temper. Dare to ride him?"

Zephyr snorted, his body trembling slightly, as if saying: The big boss is here! Who dares to cause trouble?!

Evelyn shot Zephyr a look, making him shudder again.

She smiled slyly. "With you here, I dare anything."

This pleased Alexander, dispelling his earlier displeasure about the Rolands.

Evelyn walked towards Zephyr, preparing to mount.

A large hand suddenly supported her waist. Alexander lifted her effortlessly. She smoothly parted her legs and swung onto the saddle.

The man's warm palm held her ankle through the fabric, guiding her foot into the stirrup.

The tips of her ears grew warm.

Alexander's voice was deep. "I'll lead him. Don't be afraid."

"..."

Evelyn curved her lips. "Okay."

Zephyr's eyes widened.

The big boss was afraid of him?

He recalled the fear of being dominated by the big boss...

The stern man remained oblivious, turning his head gently. "He's very docile today. I'll walk a bit faster. Don't be scared."

Evelyn played along. "Not too fast. I'm afraid I might fall."

"Don't worry. I'll catch you."

"Okay."

Zephyr: !

The big boss, who could sit steady even without a saddle back then, was afraid of falling now?

Ridiculous!

The most conspicuous among them was a man in white.

A flashy white riding outfit, a black cap, a handsome face carrying an air of unrestrained arrogance.

This was the guest of honor at today's banquet—Marcus Valentine.

Isabella Ashcroft and Julian Evans walked on the periphery of the group, speaking quietly.

Isabella whispered, "My mother said the Valentines are one of the five major elite families in New York. Vast business empire, deep connections... Julian, we must ensure he enjoys himself today."

Julian nodded. "I didn't expect your mother to actually get him to come. I heard Marcus loves horse racing and admires those with excellent equestrian skills."

Isabella frowned. "It's a pity I can't ride while pregnant."

Julian sighed. "This child really came at an inconvenient time."

They had finally managed to invite this important guest through Mrs. Ashcroft, but Isabella couldn't showcase her riding skills.

Isabella bit her lip, her gaze sweeping over the club. Suddenly, she smiled. "Julian, even if I can't shine, someone can still make a complete fool of themselves."

Julian followed her gaze and saw Evelyn.

They had just returned from their ride. Alexander was reaching up to help Evelyn dismount.

Sunlight bathed the embracing couple, stinging Julian's eyes.

Isabella immediately stepped forward with a smile. "That's my sister, Evelyn Ross. She truly never learned to ride."

Marcus scoffed disdainfully. "Why come to an equestrian club if you can't ride? This is a place for horsemanship, not for romantic dalliances!"

Evelyn and Alexander walked hand-in-hand towards them.

As the distance closed, Marcus got a clear view of Evelyn's face.

He suddenly froze.

They hadn't expected such an unexpected bonus.

Excitement gleamed in their eyes.

Alexander also frowned deeply, quickly mounting a horse to attempt a rescue.

Marcus's status was special. He couldn't be allowed to have an accident here.

But he was too far away. The spooked horse was too fast. He couldn't catch up.

Everyone was on edge, feeling disaster was imminent.

Zephyr's nimble figure suddenly leaped into the air.

Clearing the railing, it charged straight after Marcus!
###Chapter 170

Rescue!

The entire audience was stunned.

Zephyr leaped over the railing.

All four hooves were in the air.

His pale golden mane shimmered under the sunlight.

The astonishing height made everyone hold their breath.

An ordinary rider would have been thrown off instantly.

Even the professional coaches were sweating.

They could already picture Evelyn Ross being thrown to the ground.

Hooves trampling over her chest...

The image of blood splattering was terrifying.

Socialites screamed and covered their eyes.

The bolder ones stared intently at the arena.

The anticipated tragedy did not happen.

Evelyn was lying low on the horse's back.

She held the reins tightly.

She was as steady as a rock when Zephyr landed.

Their unity was like a war god descending.

"My God!"

The rich heirs couldn't help but exclaim.

"This love child is incredible!"

The coach relaxed for a moment before frowning again.

"This is just the beginning."

"The key is calming it down afterward."

Everyone's hearts were in their throats again.

A spooked horse is extremely difficult to tame.

It's impossible for anyone but a professional horse trainer.

...

Marcus Valentine felt despair.

He had tried every method but couldn't stop the horse.

The horse was becoming more frenzied.

Its speed kept increasing.

He was being jolted so hard his organs felt scrambled.

Falling at this speed would be fatal.

Marcus pressed his lips together.

He didn't regret provoking that love child.

Bullies deserved to be taught a lesson.

He only regretted that life was too short.

He hadn't found a wife for his third uncle.

He hadn't made his cousin marry Alexander Vance.

Most regrettably, he never met that legendary horse trainer.

This life was truly a loss.

The sound of hooves suddenly came from behind.

Marcus turned and saw Evelyn catching up.

Her expression was cold and stern, like a female knight.

Marcus immediately frowned.

"Why are you following me?"

Evelyn kept her eyes on the horse.

"Sit tight and hold on."

Marcus scoffed.

"Trying to save me? Don't overestimate yourself!"

Evelyn gave him a cold glance.

She continued observing the horse's condition.

Horses don't go wild without reason.

The Vance family's horses were all strictly trained.

Something must have spooked it.

Marcus grew impatient.

"Afraid my death will implicate you?"

"Stop bullying Mrs. Sophia Ashcroft and her daughter from now on!"

"I despise those who gloat when they gain power!"

Evelyn coldly snapped.

"Shut up!"

Marcus suddenly became serious.

"Can you do me a favor?"

Evelyn remained silent.

Marcus continued.

"My idol is a horse trainer."

"Tell her I admire her if you ever meet her."

Evelyn's eyes flickered.

This guy wasn't fundamentally bad.

He had protected her during the race.

Otherwise, she wouldn't risk saving him.

"Your horsemanship is quite good."

"Alexander Vance has decent taste."

"But stop chasing, you'll really die..."

Evelyn suddenly accelerated.

Zephyr caught up with the out-of-control horse.

Marcus was shocked.

"Are you trying to die together?"

"There's no need for a lovers' suicide..."

Before he finished.

Evelyn suddenly stood up.

She leaned sideways, hanging off Zephyr.

Using the momentum, she leaped into the air.

Marcus felt a weight behind him.

The girl had landed behind him.

Her arms reached around him to grab the reins.

Marcus was dumbfounded.

"Are you insane?"

"This won't work!"

"You'll be thrown off!"

He hurriedly turned his head.

He heard the woman's cool voice.

"Shut up!"

Marcus wanted to say more.

He heard another command.

"Sit tight and hold on!"

His waist was suddenly held.

Evelyn nimbly flipped over.

She instantly moved in front of him.

She took the reins and controlled the horse.

She leaned down to pat the horse's head.

She pulled hard on the reins.

She gently patted the horse's belly.

"It's useless, I tried!"

"Do you think you're my idol?"

Marcus gripped her shoulders.

He felt the horse's restlessness.

He had done the same thing.

It didn't work at all.

But a miracle happened.

The horse gradually calmed down.
###Chapter 171

Marcus Valentine was completely stunned.

This must be an illusion.

The horse was still galloping, but the scenery on both sides was clearly slowing down…

He stared in shock at the girl in front of him.

Evelyn leaned close to the horse's back, gently stroking its neck and body, whispering softly as if soothing it.

Zephyr ran ahead of them, gradually slowing down and guiding the horse to a stop.

Evelyn finally let out a sigh of relief.

Marcus gaped at her.

She actually did it?!

How was that possible…

Alexander Vance and the other safety officers rode up on their horses.

The group surrounded them, all stunned by the scene.

Alexander's gaze immediately fell on Evelyn, confirming she wasn't injured before he relaxed.

Then he looked coldly at Marcus's hand resting on Evelyn's shoulder.

If looks could kill, Marcus's hand would have been shredded a thousand times over.

He urged his horse forward and cleared his throat lightly. "Marcus, haven't you had enough riding? Aren't you afraid the horse might spook again?"

Marcus snapped back to reality and quickly dismounted.

Alexander looked at Evelyn. "Evelyn, you…"

"I'm fine." Evelyn didn't dismount, continuing to gently stroke the horse's head to calm it.

Someone couldn't help but speak up. "Mrs. Vance, was it you who calmed this horse?"

Another person chimed in. "In that state, just like that, it was calmed?"

The group gasped in unison.

Alexander's eyes deepened slightly.

Seeing the horse's emotions had stabilized, Evelyn straightened up and let out a breath.

Hearing the speculation, she glanced at Marcus, seeing his eyes wide with shock…

Evelyn chuckled lightly, neither denying nor confirming. "This horse was spooked because a wood splinter from the riding crop got stuck in its rear."

With a graceful turn of her hand, she revealed a wood splinter.

This was the splinter she had pulled out first when she flipped behind Marcus.

Seeing the thick splinter, everyone gasped, looking at Marcus. "Mr. Valentine, how could such a large splinter be there?!"

Marcus was momentarily speechless.

Evelyn said calmly, "Mr. Valentine used the riding crop to hit a tree, right?"

Marcus: ?

He suddenly remembered—it wasn't hitting a tree.

When he was bullying Evelyn, after using the riding crop to hit her horse's rear and pulling it back, it accidentally brushed against a wooden post…

This was truly reaping what he sowed!

Marcus looked embarrassed.

The atmosphere froze for a moment.

Just then, Julian Evans and the others arrived by car and on horseback. The second wave of people had finally arrived.

Not having seen what just happened, they immediately surrounded Marcus. "Mr. Valentine, are you alright?"

Julian directly attacked. "Evelyn, why did you have to compete with Mr. Valentine? If you hadn't insisted on racing, Mr. Valentine wouldn't have had an accident! Uncle, you must give Mr. Valentine an explanation today!"

Marcus felt even more awkward.

Alexander sneered. "Marcus, what kind of explanation do you want?"

Marcus rubbed his nose. "Well…"

Julian was instantly shocked.

He was too far away to see the details clearly, but Evelyn saved Marcus? How was that possible!

Since when was her horsemanship this good?!

Julian wanted to say something but knew it wasn't the right time. He could only follow Marcus back with the group.

This was troublesome!

Owing such a big favor to this love child, how could he help Third Uncle protect Mrs. Ashcroft and teach her a lesson?

This is really too much!

This man called her 'wife' again!

###Chapter 172

The Photo!

Evelyn answered Ryan Roland's question.

Her gaze swept into the distance.

Marcus Valentine's motorcade was approaching slowly.

Inside the distant car.

The Roland family held considerable status in New York.

If she had married an ordinary person, no one would constantly emphasize her status. But she had to marry Alexander Vance…

She didn't invite him to the Roland family dinner. Firstly, he was busy. Secondly, he had issues with the Rolands.

Chloe was in San Francisco for rehabilitation, so Ryan had simply bought a villa. Coincidentally, it was in the same gated community as the Ashcroft family.

She would not set foot there again.

###Chapter 173

Evelyn took the photo.

The photo was yellowed and worn at the edges.

The pixels were blurry, as if it had been restored.

###Chapter 174

In Evelyn Ross's childhood memories, the Ashcroft family's semi-basement was her entire world.

She always looked up at the floors above.

The laughter of Sophia Ashcroft and Isabella Ashcroft would drift down.

That warmth had nothing to do with her.

Every time she returned from school and reached the front gate, she would instinctively lower her head.

Her status felt like a heavy shackle.

The weight of their upbringing pressed down on her, making it hard to breathe.

After leaving the Ashcroft home, she occasionally sneaked back.

Standing outside the door, she would still habitually bow her head.

But today was different.

They had visited Sophia Ashcroft before.

The nanny immediately composed herself and hurried back inside.

Evelyn and the three Rolands waited outside.

That place had once represented a warmth she could never reach.

The living room was empty now.

At this hour, Sophia Ashcroft was probably having her meal.

Marcus Valentine was a good option.

Marcus frowned at Evelyn.

Remembering how she had saved him at the equestrian club, he ultimately remained silent.

The face of Mrs. Nan from the photograph flashed in her mind.

Isabella bore a fifty percent resemblance to William Ashcroft, but shared no features with Sophia Ashcroft.

This discovery made Evelyn even more certain of her suspicions.

She remained silent.

Evelyn still said nothing.

Nothing would stop her from seeing Sophia Ashcroft today.

Ryan answered for her. "Miss Ross has important matters to discuss with Mrs. Ashcroft."

Isabella looked at Marcus with a pleading expression.

Marcus indeed stepped forward. "Ryan Roland, must you protect this love child?"

###Chapter 175

###Chapter 176

Recognition!

Sophia Ashcroft suffered from chronic insomnia.

The Ashcroft villa had been specially soundproofed during its renovation.

Soundproofing material was embedded within the walls.

Neither the living room nor the bedrooms could hear any noise from outside.

Sophia was having a meal.

She heard none of the commotion outside.

It wasn't until the group rushed in.

That she looked up in surprise.

Evelyn Ross quickly stepped forward.

She handed the photo to Sophia.

Her voice was full of anticipation. "Madam, do you recognize the person in this photo?"

The woman in the photo had delicate features.

She looked almost identical to Evelyn.

She looked at the photo.

Mr. Roland was completely stunned.

He looked at the photo.

Then he looked at Sophia.

The two women did not resemble each other.

Mrs. Roland pulled his arm. "Lao Song, did you remember wrong?"

Mr. Roland was bewildered.

Mrs. Roland looked at Evelyn. "Evelyn, this might be your maternal grandmother…"

Mr. Roland pulled her back. "Don't talk nonsense!"

Mrs. Roland then realized her mistake.

She glanced at William Ashcroft.

She lowered her voice. "Evelyn doesn't look like Mr. Ashcroft either…"

Neither of them dared to say more.

Evelyn pressed her lips together tightly.

Sophia examined the photo again.

The woman in the photo felt familiar to her.

But she couldn't remember where she had seen her.

The events in New York were over twenty years ago.

She handed the photo back to Evelyn.

She was about to speak.

Her gaze suddenly fell on Marcus Valentine.

She paused slightly. "You are?"

Marcus stepped forward. "Mrs. Ashcroft, I am Marcus Valentine. My Third Uncle sends his regards."

Third Uncle…

Sophia swayed.

Nancy quickly supported her.

Sophia's face turned pale.

Her eyes looked dazed.

It was as if she was seeing that person through him.

William Ashcroft called softly. "A-Shu."

Sophia regained her composure.

She took a deep breath. "What brings you here?"

Marcus smiled. "You invited Young Master Li. Third Uncle asked me to come to San Francisco for a visit. Are you and my Third Uncle close?"

He studied Sophia carefully.

His Third Uncle had never married.

Yet he showed particular concern for this lady.

Sophia's face grew even paler.

She forced herself to remain calm. "We are merely acquaintances."

Her fingers tightened within her sleeves.

William looked at Isabella. "Young Master Xu, let Isabella show you around San Francisco."

Sophia's body stiffened.

William whispered in her ear. "A-Shu, let Isabella bond with her cousin."

Sophia remembered that night over twenty years ago.

She had discovered she was pregnant.

She fled New York for San Francisco.

She gave birth to Isabella.

Her face was deathly pale.

She could barely stand.

William said urgently. "Young Master Xu, Mr. Roland, A-Shu is unwell. We cannot entertain you today."

Marcus nodded in confusion.

Isabella and Julian Evans stayed.

The others gradually left.

Evelyn turned away, heartbroken.

She felt like a joke.

Her extravagant hope had been dashed once again.

She lowered her head and walked toward the door.

Mr. Roland was full of guilt.

He didn't know how to apologize.

Sophia watched them leave.

Marcus unintentionally walked next to Evelyn.

They walked side by side.

Their similar peach blossom eyes jolted Sophia awake.

She remembered.

The woman in the photo was Mr.Valentine's mother!

Marcus's grandmother!

Sophia's pupils constricted sharply.

She looked at Evelyn in disbelief.

Nancy had just shown her the DNA report this morning.

Evelyn was not William's daughter.

She had been puzzled.

Now she finally understood.

Evelyn resembled Mr.Valentine's mother.

Because Mr.Valentine was her father!

Evelyn was her daughter with Mr.Valentine!
###Chapter 177
Sophia Ashcroft's pupils constricted sharply.

She stared fixedly at Evelyn Ross's retreating back, her lips trembling. "...Evelyn!"

Evelyn, about to leave, paused and turned back in confusion.

Sophia trembled all over, her eyes reddening.

Countless memory fragments surged violently.

That small figure hiding in the corner, stealing glances at her.

Every time, she had seen it…

But for Isabella's sake, and because of Evelyn's special status, she had never approached.

She had witnessed Linda Lowell's abuse too many times, hardening her heart each time, only intervening when life was at risk.

She had always felt an inexplicable fondness for Evelyn, thinking it was because William was good to Isabella, so she should be good to William's daughter…

Yet she always restrained this kindness, afraid Isabella would be jealous, afraid Isabella would be hurt.

So, Evelyn was her real daughter!

At this moment, every image of Linda abusing Evelyn flashed through her mind…

Newborn Evelyn nearly starved to death in her room. It was Sophia who found her in time and fed her a few mouthfuls of milk…

The tiny infant grasped her finger, its softness melting her heart.

From then on, she could no longer resent this child.

Linda had pretended to be meek and submissive, saying she had no milk due to poor nutrition and the child was allergic to formula.

Sophia believed her and even bought goat milk formula.

Later, Evelyn was always thin and small, rummaging for food in trash bins. William told her not to bother with this love child. Unable to bear it, she gave Evelyn the leftover cake from Isabella.

Then she started regularly leaving snacks on the stone table in the park.

Evelyn grew up on those snacks!

Isabella attended an international bilingual kindergarten at age three. Every time Sophia saw her off to the car, she could feel a pair of longing eyes watching them from behind the half-window in the basement.

She paid no attention…

It wasn't until Isabella started elementary school that she casually enrolled Evelyn.

From then on, Linda began daily lectures, ordering Evelyn to be Isabella's follower, saying she must repay the Ashcroft family's kindness.

Sophia never wanted repayment. She helped Evelyn only because she felt sorry for the child.

She also always believed she had done more than enough for Evelyn.

In between educating Isabella, she would say a few extra words to Evelyn, discovering the child was exceptionally smart.

She remembered in first grade, Evelyn scored first in the grade but dared not speak up, holding her report card.

Isabella saw it and cried with jealousy, asking, "Mom, am I worse than a love child?"

She only told Isabella to work harder herself.

Later, seeing Linda whip Evelyn, shouting she must never score first again, Sophia clearly wanted to stop it. But thinking of Isabella's tear-streaked face, she held back.

Then later…

When Evelyn wanted to move out for middle school, she actually agreed.

She didn't want Evelyn to stay here anymore, making her feel both heartache and helplessness. Their natural opposing statuses made her unsure how to face Evelyn.

And then?

After Evelyn left, she had more worries.

She didn't understand why the child's departure affected her so much, only hoping she would come back to visit someday.

Even in dreams those years, Evelyn called out "Mom" to her…

All these years, she had no news of Evelyn.

She wanted to send someone to inquire, but feared Isabella's jealousy and tantrums, so she restrained herself…

Every time she asked Linda, the answer was, "That ungrateful wretch, why bother with her?"

Sophia could never understand why Linda treated Evelyn so poorly.

When Evelyn was four, she was taken by traffickers. Linda said, "Just a girl. If she's lost, she's lost. Not worth much."

She was cold-blooded and selfish!

Only now did Sophia understand. How could there truly be a mother in this world who hates her own child?!

Linda was cruel to her because Linda wasn't her mother!

She herself was!

Thinking of this, Sophia's eyes burned red!!

Evelyn noticed her agitation and stepped forward puzzled. "Madam, what's wrong? Do you have something to say?"

Something to say?

Sophia felt she had a thousand things to say. She wanted to say sorry for turning a blind eye to her past suffering. She wanted to say it was her negligence that made Evelyn endure so much hardship…

All words finally choked in her throat.

Just then, there was movement at the door.

Linda entered, still meek and submissive, but showing disgust upon seeing Evelyn. "You little wretch! Who let you into the Ashcroft house? Here to pester young master Julian again? Let me tell you, Miss Isabella is the Ashcroft family's young lady! You, a love child, are nothing! Don't think marrying into the Vance family gives you free rein! You'll never repay the debt of raising you in this lifetime!"

Linda's words made Sophia shake with rage.

Her ears filled with the scoldings Linda had hurled at Evelyn over the years, which she had occasionally overheard:

"You're just a love child! Know your place! Miss Isabella is your benefactor! You must always bow and scrape before her!"

"You little wretch! How dare you glare at Miss Isabella? You and her are worlds apart!"

"Miss Isabella was born a lofty fairy! You? You only deserve to live in the basement! In the gutter!"

"…"

Sophia felt fury burning through her. She looked at Linda as if she wanted to devour her!

Linda had switched their daughters.

Yet she had placed Isabella high on a pedestal, pampering and spoiling her since childhood.

But what about her Evelyn?!

Her Evelyn was in hell! A hell she and Linda had built together! A hell Evelyn could never escape!

Sophia closed her eyes deeply.

She tried to calm her breathing…

Evelyn, watching her strange behavior, asked softly, "Madam, what is really wrong with you?"

William roared, "Linda Lowell! Who let you in? If you've upset Ashbook, I'll make you pay! Get out now, take your disgraceful daughter with you! Ashbook, how are you feeling?"

Linda lowered her head and went to pull at Evelyn. "What are you doing here again? Making your father angry, getting me thrown out too… You ungrateful wretch! Can't ever help, just cause me trouble!"

Evelyn's face tightened.

She gave a bitter smile.

Yes, what right did she have to be here?

She turned silently, ready to leave with Linda. But as she turned, someone grasped her arm.

Evelyn started slightly and looked back to find it was Sophia.

Sophia had opened her eyes now. Tears fell before she could speak…

Two unstoppable streams rolled down her cheeks. Trembling, she clutched Evelyn's hand tightly. "Evelyn, don't go!…"
###Chapter 178
Evelyn noticed Sophia's trembling.

She immediately grasped Sophia's hand.

William steadied Sophia's shoulders.

Julian anxiously looked toward the door.

Linda covered her cheek, not daring to speak.

Isabella moved to intervene.

Evelyn covered her ears.

Alexander rushed out.

He wrapped his arm around Evelyn's shoulders.

Evelyn followed numbly.

The girl's silence broke his heart.

Outside the hospital ER.

After what felt like an eternity.

The doctor emerged.

The Ashcroft family had always been a source of pain for Evelyn. She normally would never go there.

They were originally going to the Roland family to look at photos today, but she ended up taking the photos to the Ashcroft residence… There must be a reason.

Evelyn nodded slightly.

They found a restaurant near the hospital.

Alexander ordered a bowl of porridge.

Alexander remembered the first time he met her. The girl had blinked her sly peach blossom eyes and called him husband. Later, she appeared in various identities, always playful.

She was like a little sun, bursting into his life.

Evelyn kept her head down, saying nothing.

Evelyn slowly looked up.

The man spoke at a gentle pace, his voice deep and magnetic, carrying a lightheartedness that gradually calmed her.

Reason slowly returned.

Marcus was a local of New York. Maybe he really would know…

Alexander understood her meaning and immediately said, "Alright, I'll arrange it."

Evelyn stopped eating, stood up, and walked out.

Alexander quickly followed.

Marcus frowned as he watched their retreating figures, then looked down at the photo in his hand…
###Chapter 180

Dominant!

As if Alexander was better than him.

At graduation, his Third Uncle even said if they couldn't become close friends, they should find a way to arrange a marriage alliance.

Unfortunately, the main family didn't have any suitable girls.

He and his Third Uncle picked two outstanding girls from the collateral branches.

They were adopted into his eldest uncle's line.

They wanted Alexander to choose one.

Who knew Alexander would directly refuse!

This man simply didn't put the Valentine family of New York in his eyes.

A message from Third Uncle: [How did it go meeting Mrs. Ashcroft tonight?]

The only one who could oppose Alexander Vance.

How could she compete with Evelyn?

Isabella's eyes darted.

She suddenly rushed toward the room. "Mom! How is my mom?"

Black-clad bodyguards easily stopped her.

Isabella struggled violently.

The bodyguard's arm was like a steel vise.

She couldn't break free.

This was the effect Isabella wanted.

She turned abruptly.

Dropped to her knees with a thud before Alexander.

Everyone was stunned.

Isabella cried out. "Uncle! Please let me in! That's my mother... What's wrong with my mom? Why hasn't she woken up? Uncle, I know I've treated Evelyn poorly. I was wrong. I'll kowtow to her, okay? Please!"

Tears streaked her face.

Julian Evans felt a pang of pity.

He couldn't help speaking up. "Uncle! The Vance family answers to you, but you can't be this domineering! Mrs. Ashcroft is Isabella's mother! Isabella has the right to go in!"

Marcus also frowned. "Alexander Vance, let her in."

No matter the relationship between Mrs. Ashcroft and Evelyn.

Isabella was her daughter.

Mrs. Ashcroft would surely want to see Isabella while critically ill!

Alexander glanced into the room.

Evelyn had already heard the argument.

Isabella's crying was irritating.

She turned and glared at Isabella. "You can come in. But if I hear one more sob, I'll personally throw you out the window."

Isabella immediately fell silent.

The bodyguards outside hesitated for a moment.

Remembering Alexander's concessions today.

They immediately stepped aside.

Isabella finally entered the room.

Marcus looked at Alexander. "If Evelyn hadn't spoken, you really wouldn't let Mrs. Ashcroft's own daughter in? Alexander Vance, you're too tyrannical! Let me tell you, Mrs. Ashcroft is an old acquaintance of my third uncle. With me here, I won't allow you to bully her!"

Julian looked into the room.

Isabella swayed unsteadily.

She had knelt too hard.

Her knees must be in agony.

She bent over to rub them after just a few steps.

They were definitely bruised.

Julian said to Alexander, "Uncle, even if you won't consider my feelings, you should treat her better for the sake of the Vance blood in her belly!"

Alexander's eyes turned cold.

Isabella was carrying Liam Lowell's child.

He and Evelyn knew this perfectly well.

Evelyn hadn't exposed it to avoid trouble.

But Alexander said coldly, "Are you sure it's Vance blood?"

Julian immediately frowned. "Uncle, what do you mean? I know what I did. The child Isabella is carrying is mine!"

Alexander sneered. "I suggest you get checked. Don't be oblivious while wearing horns."

If it's not mine, whose could it be? Uncle, you've been married to Evelyn for two years with no news. Could it be you're... incapable?"

Alexander looked at him coldly.

Julian cowered, muttering, "My mother said I must protect Isabella's child at all costs. You don't have children of your own, so you look down on mine... She guessed right!"

Julian froze.

His tone uncertain but stubborn. "Of course! I'm sure!"

"Suit yourself."

Alexander tossed out these two words.

He walked into the room and stood by Evelyn's side.

Julian tried to follow.

He was stopped again by the black-clad bodyguards.

Inside the room.

The doctor finished the examination.

The attending physician frowned. "The patient's condition is very strange. She should have woken up by now, but remains unconscious. All her vitals are normal. This prolonged unconsciousness without cause is very rare..."

Evelyn grew anxious immediately.

Alexander took her hand. "I've already had Thomas summon all the neurologists in San Francisco. Mrs. Ashcroft will be fine."

Evelyn nodded.

Outside, Marcus was bewildered.

Wasn't Mrs. Ashcroft supposed to be fine?

Why couldn't she wake up?

He began to panic.

His phone suddenly rang.

Marcus looked down.

It was a call from his third uncle.

He was startled and quickly answered. "Third Uncle..."

"Which room?"

A deep, mature voice came through.

Marcus reported the room number.

He was confused. "You know Mrs. Ashcroft is sick? Wait... how did you know?"

He definitely hadn't told his third uncle.

Then he remembered some of the bodyguards with him were his third uncle's men.

Marcus understood.

He asked further, "Are you coming to San Francisco? You should bring some specialists from New York... Mrs. Ashcroft's condition isn't good."

The voice on the other end immediately became urgent. "How bad?"

"Still unconscious." Marcus heard a car horn through the phone. Hesitantly, he asked, "You're already on the way? Where are you?"

"At the hospital entrance."

The cold voice carried a hint of unease.
###Chapter 183

Alexander Vance, I can't handle you, but Third Uncle can.

Alexander's words had planted doubt in Julian's heart like a thorn.

Thomas Watson entered with five senior specialists.

Doctors and nurses moved aside to make room.

William Ashcroft was forced to step back half a pace, but remained close to the bed.

His eyes were bloodshot, lips cracked and dry.

He hadn't eaten or drunk anything since the incident, keeping vigil by his wife's side.

His kindness to her had always been because she was Mrs. Ashcroft's daughter.

If he knew the truth...

Anyone could see that if Mrs. Ashcroft didn't wake up, he would follow her.

Evelyn Ross clenched her fists.

However flawed William might be, his love for Mrs. Ashcroft was genuine.

Without them, Mrs. Ashcroft would have been truly happy.

Marcus Valentine went out to meet him and brought him upstairs.

Marcus scratched his head. "Third Uncle, what's your relationship with Mrs. Ashcroft? To make you come here personally?"

Mr.Valentine's expression was stern, his coat billowing slightly.

Nearly fifty, he still stood tall and straight.

He shot Marcus a cold glance.

Marcus immediately fell silent, miming zipping his lips.

Mr.Valentine looked away and strode forward.

He didn't even wait for the elevator, taking the stairs directly to the third floor.

Marcus followed. "Third Uncle, don't worry. The doctors said Mrs. Ashcroft is fine, just sleeping."

Mr.Valentine's expression eased slightly, but his pace didn't slow.

Mr.Valentine said coldly, "Most of them. Your grandmother took me to many social events."

Marcus rubbed his nose.

Mr.Valentine didn't respond, quickening his steps.

Marcus pushed the door open and entered.
###Chapter 184

Marcus Valentine pushed open the hospital room door.

His gaze swept across the room.

The atmosphere was relatively calm.

He breathed a small sigh of relief.

His expression changed instantly.

His fingers tightened.

A slight tremble ran through his body.

He was afraid of that man's appearance.

Isabella had a similar appearance, but the spirit was different.

Mr.Valentine said calmly, "I'm waiting for Miss Nan to wake up."

Mr.Valentine suddenly spoke. "If you need anything, you can come to me."

He took Evelyn Ross's hand.

Her birth and position gave her the right to condemn Evelyn…

"And me, Evelyn. I should have had the happiest family. Without you, even if Father made a mistake, there would have been time for Mother to forgive him! Time heals all wounds. Mother would have forgiven Father… But your existence constantly reminds them of the past! I carefully coaxed my parents every day. As a child, I woke up crying so many times, afraid they would divorce…"

Isabella's voice was icy, each word piercing Evelyn's heart.

"All of this is because of you!!"

Evelyn swayed and fell into a chair.

She was shocked and stunned.

She looked at Isabella in disbelief, her eyes confused and fearful, her body trembling slightly.

Yes…

She had always been a mistake.

Sophia's excessive worry was because of her!

Evelyn clenched her fists, once again despising her origins. She even felt the blood from Linda flowing in her veins was dirty…

Isabella looked at her coldly.

This was her ultimate weapon against Evelyn.

Evelyn had a strong moral compass and feelings for Sophia. She would always blame herself for her existence. Combined with a lifetime of brainwashing, even as an adult with changed perspectives, she still felt her birth was a sin.

Isabella suppressed a smile and leaned close to Evelyn's ear.

"So, you owe me! You owe the Ashcrofts! Evelyn, you can never repay it!"

Evelyn bit her lip hard and looked at Isabella, then at Linda.

Linda's face was even more detestable than Isabella's.

"Yes, you wretched love child! How dare you compete with Isabella? How shameless! Admit your fault now! Bear all the guilt! Repay your debt!"

She viciously pinched Evelyn's ear and pointed at Isabella.

"Love child! Kneel before Isabella!"

Love child…

It was like a curse!

Evelyn uncontrollably stood up and looked at Isabella.

Her eyes were full of despair.

It was as if she had returned to childhood, to that cramped basement…

She was the one who made Sophia unhappy. She was the one who made Sophia cry in secret… Her existence hurt Sophia.

A rotten person like her only deserved to stay in the gutter forever, right?

Evelyn smiled bitterly.

Isabella pressed again.

"So, Evelyn, will you grant my request? Go tell Julian you forged the report… This is what you owe me!"

She pushed Evelyn toward the door.

"Go tell him now…"

But Evelyn suddenly stopped and spoke softly with her head down.

"Isabella…"

Isabella was stunned.

Meanwhile, at the detention center.

Grace received the WhatsApp voice message and immediately brought Liam in for questioning.

She played the recording as soon as she entered.

Isabella's vicious and cold-hearted words cut into Liam's heart like knives!

He clutched his chest in pain.

"No, it can't be! How could she do this to me?!"

Grace looked at him coldly, like an iron-faced judge.

"Liam, you heard the recording. Are you still sure you want to keep secrets for someone like this?!"

Liam clenched his fists.

His chest heaved violently. He was on the verge of collapse, tears streaming down.

Suddenly, a vicious expression appeared on his face.

"Fine, Isabella! You sacrificed me and our child for wealth and status! Don't blame me for being ruthless!"

He looked at Grace and spoke slowly.

"I'll tell you a secret. Actually, Evelyn is Mrs. Ashcroft's real daughter!! Isabella is fake! Linda switched the babies back then! Hahahahaha!"
###Chapter 194

Liam Lowell laughed hysterically.

Grace Lorenz stared at him, eyes wide with shock. "What did you say? Impossible!"

Several thoughts raced through her mind. She suddenly looked at Liam. "You and Isabella... you two..."

Liam stopped laughing. "That's right. Her mother is my aunt..."

He lowered his head, covering his face. "Do you want to call me a monster? But I truly didn't know!"

He only knew his father was a gambler.

During those dark, despised days, Isabella was like a beam of light in his life.

She tutored him and took him out for good food.

She seemed like an unattainable goddess, and he fell for her uncontrollably.

He just had a crush on the girl next door... she was only two years older. After he got into college, they couldn't resist their feelings.

He genuinely knew nothing.

Until Isabella told him his father was threatening her. He demanded two million... where could she get two million? Isabella cried bitterly, planting the idea of killing his father in his mind!

His father could hurt anyone, but not Isabella.

Liam devised a perfect plan...

He took the money to the abandoned hospital to meet Leo Lowell. He took out his phone and asked Leo to record a few sentences. He lied, saying it was to threaten Evelyn Ross if she caused trouble.

Leo wasn't a good father. He gambled everything away.

But Leo was also a good father.

Liam's tuition came from all his father's scams and schemes. At least he never let Liam go hungry.

In this world, Leo's only concern was his son.

After recording, Leo told him, "I have something on Isabella. Use this to keep her in line. If anything happens to me, use it to threaten her."

Liam nodded. He wasn't interested in his unattainable love's secret... he just wanted to help her get rid of this man!

He shoved Leo hard toward the steel rebar behind him!

As the rebar pierced Leo's chest, he couldn't believe it. He murmured, "Why? Why?"

Liam sneered. "It's one thing for you to make me lose face, but you should never have threatened Isabella! Dad, she's the woman I love most..."

Leo stared in shock. "How... how could you love her? No, you can't..."

"Because she's the only one who was ever good to me."

Leo was stunned. Understanding dawned on him before death. With his last strength, he cried out his final words. "But she's your aunt's daughter! Your aunt and I switched the babies years ago!"

After saying this, he could speak no more.

Liam was completely dumbfounded.

But he had no time to think. Evelyn was coming through the door...

He jumped out the window and escaped. Then he followed the plan and entered the room. His mind buzzed as he played his part, framing Evelyn as the suspect.

Fortunately, he had just lost his father. The police could understand his complex emotions.

His confused, shocked reaction fit the situation.

After giving his statement and returning to his dorm, he lay on his bed, staring blankly at the ceiling.

Some things, he didn't dare to think about.

But he suddenly understood. Why did Isabella appear when everyone avoided him? When his family couldn't afford a shower and he was dirty?

She was clean and pure. Her smiling face was beautiful.

But her closeness was only because Leo threatened her...

She never truly liked him. She was just afraid of angering him, afraid Leo would be dissatisfied. So she played along with his crush? Didn't dare reject his advances?

No, that couldn't be...

Liam covered his face with the blanket. Tears rolled from the corners of his eyes.

In that moment, he finally understood. The person who loved him most in this world had always been his father... But he still refused to believe it.

Later, Evelyn fought him with public opinion.

He was almost driven mad by the insults at school. His mental state suffered. Whenever he reached out to Isabella, she was impatient.

She seemed eager to cut ties with him.

The more she acted this way, the more he understood the truth. The more guilty and miserable he felt...

Finally, he couldn't take it anymore. He used the secret to threaten her! Forced her to see him!

He thought he could be like his father. Just mentioning the secret would make her comply. But he was no match for his father...

Isabella knew him too well. Knew his indecisiveness. Knew he wouldn't mutual destruction. She threatened him back.

A strange balance formed between them.

But he knew he was losing.

Later, he realized he had lost from the very beginning.

Evelyn and Officer Grace Lorenz saw through his disguise. They caught him.

Since the murder was exposed, how could he let Isabella, who manipulated him into killing his own father, walk free?

Liam went crazy then.

He would reveal Isabella's secret!

But he never expected, as he was being arrested, as the police took his phone as evidence, he saw a document Isabella sent.

It showed she was pregnant.

Five weeks pregnant.

Five weeks... that child was his!

Liam didn't know whether to laugh or cry.

He wanted to cry because his life was over. He would spend the rest of his days in prison.

He wanted to laugh because he had a child. His child would be raised as a Vance. Like a little princess or prince. They would never have to live the life he did.

So he kept his mouth shut again.

He knew Isabella had him completely under her thumb...

During this time, Liam lived in anticipation.

He silently calculated the baby's growth every day. Counted down the days until birth...

This child became his only hope.

But now, even that hope was shattered.

Isabella was too cruel.

Liam lowered his head. He suddenly covered his face. After revealing Isabella's secret, he foresaw her fate. But for some reason, he felt no satisfaction from revenge. Only emptiness.

He broke down sobbing.

His whole life was indeed a joke.

How could he kill the father who loved him most, all for a woman?!

He finally began to feel regret. It was too late for remorse.
###Chapter 195

This was her mother.

The reporters looked grave.

In the parking lot.

This sister's scheming was far deeper than she imagined.

Isabella's move was truly brilliant!

She had bet correctly!

As long as the eldest branch insisted the report was fake, Evelyn could do nothing!

Unexpectedly, with conclusive evidence, the other side could still distort the truth.

Grace's voice suddenly rang out. "Who says there's no evidence?!"
###Chapter 197

###Chapter 199

Evelyn Ross listened to the reporters and Linda Lowell's slander, simply lowering her gaze in silence.

Sophia, due to her chronic illness, had her medical information stored in the healthcare database.

The police retrieved and expedited the comparison.

Results came back in half an hour.

###Chapter 200

Alexander Vance did not stop Mr.Valentine this time.

All these years, she had coddled Isabella, coaxed Isabella, even carefully currying favor in private.

Isabella always looked down on her, her eyes often filled with hatred. Linda always comforted herself that Isabella didn't know.

But if she had known all along, aware that she was her biological mother, why did she treat her like this?

Especially in private, how many times had she tried to get close to her daughter, only to be met with malice, treated entirely like a servant!

Linda suddenly felt utterly stupid.

She had given everything for her daughter, but what had Isabella done?

Never showing an ounce of respect or closeness was one thing, but just now, Grace said she seduced Liam and instigated him to kill Leo?!

That was her own uncle!

Linda could no longer restrain herself. She sprang up and rushed to Isabella. "Isabella, tell me, is what she said true?"

Isabella clenched her jaw tightly.

She was panicked, on the verge of collapse.

How did it come to this...

Why had things developed this way?

It should have been her turning the tables, so how did everything flip in an instant?

Linda grabbed her arm, which disgusted her immensely.

She impatiently pushed Linda away. "So what if it's true? Are you going to settle accounts with me?"

Linda was stunned. "I'm not settling accounts with you, but what about your uncle? How could you..."

Isabella insisted she knew nothing. "Don't talk nonsense. I've never killed anyone. Leo's death has nothing to do with me!"

Grace immediately said, "Isabella, Liam has already confessed. Are you still denying it?"

Isabella sneered. "That's his word against mine. Does he have any evidence to prove it?"

When Isabella seduced Liam, she never spoke directly, always hinting and complaining.

She was extremely cautious and had indeed left no traces.

Grace frowned. She had intended to trick her, but Isabella didn't fall for it.

This Isabella was cunning.

Seeing Grace fall silent, Isabella felt a hint of triumph. She sneered, "Grace, if you have evidence, come arrest me. If not, stop slandering me here! Otherwise, I will definitely file a complaint against you!"

Grace's jaw tightened.

Just as Isabella was feeling smug, the next moment—

"You monster! You wretched monster!"

Linda couldn't believe her own daughter could be so cold-blooded!

Leo was her only brother, the only male heir of the Lowell family!

Liam was Leo's only son, and Linda had always doted on him.

But now, the two men of the Lowell family—one killed by Isabella, the other sent to prison by her!

Linda couldn't accept it. She rushed to Isabella and slapped her hard across the face!

Slap!

Isabella covered her face, staring at Linda in disbelief. "You dare hit me? What right do you have to hit me?"

"The right of being your mother!"

Isabella sneered. "Mother? You, a mistress, are worthy of being my mother?"

Her emotions flared. "Do you have any idea how terrified and desperate I was when I found out I was a love child, after believing I was the Ashcroft family's young lady all along? Do you know how carefully I've had to tread all these years, afraid my parents would find out, how hard my life has been? It's all because of you! I'd rather have been born into an ordinary family than suffer this落差!"

As she shouted, she clutched her abdomen, her face twisted and terrifying.

She then looked at the surrounding reporters. "Everything I've done today was forced by them! I've done nothing wrong!"

Seeing her still refusing to admit fault, Mr.Valentine, standing in the corridor, sneered. He directly chambered a round and prepared to push through the crowd.

He didn't care about the numerous reporters.

Seeing his movement, Alexander realized Mr.Valentine had only brandished the gun earlier as a deterrent, not actually intending to shoot.

He shook his head helplessly and followed.

If Mr.Valentine really killed Isabella today, he would handle the aftermath.

But before the two could approach, they suddenly heard Linda scream, "Blood... Isabella, what's wrong?"

They halted. Isabella's pants were soaked in blood...

Reminded, Isabella looked down and realized she was hemorrhaging heavily!!

She panicked, her eyes wide with fear.

The more nervous she became, the more she felt the warm blood gushing out, completely unstoppable...

No matter how much Linda hated her, Isabella was still her biological daughter, whom she had doted on since childhood. Linda urgently cried out, "Doctor, doctor!! Help!!"

To avoid leaving evidence, Isabella hadn't taken abortion pills. To induce a miscarriage, she had really slammed into the table and fallen heavily to the ground.

After such a violent impact, she should have rested properly, but she insisted on getting up to frame Evelyn.

Physically and emotionally exhausted, combined with the intense stress, caused her to suddenly hemorrhage heavily...

Fortunately, they were in a hospital. She was promptly wheeled away by nurses for emergency treatment. If this had happened outside, she would have bled to death within two minutes.

After Isabella was taken away, security cleared out the reporters, and the corridor returned to silence.

For a moment, only Evelyn, Alexander, and Mr.Valentine remained.
###Chapter 201

Evelyn Ross watched Isabella Ashcroft's disappearing figure expressionlessly.

After a moment of thought, she still followed.

She was not a soft-hearted person.

She felt no concern for Isabella.

She simply wanted to see her final outcome.

After a few steps, he glanced back at Mr. Valentine.

He needed to give an explanation.

Slap!

Isabella's life hung in the balance.

He was her legal guardian.

She went to the operating room door.

He had even thought he was almost worthy of her.

He should have cherished her…

He had done so many wrong things!

The pain made him bend over.

He tugged at the corner of his mouth, trying to smile.

But tears streamed down his face instead…

Alexander and Marcus walked past him.

Neither looked at Julian.

"Hey, is your love… your wife's father William Ashcroft?"

"Why does she look nothing like him?"
###Chapter 202

Marcus Valentine always felt the atmosphere between his third uncle and Mrs. Sophia Ashcroft was peculiar.

His third uncle had always valued Alexander Vance highly, even privately advising Marcus not to make an enemy of him, saying the family's future relied on his support.

There was something strange about those words… but that wasn't the main point.

The main point was that Alexander, who was held in such high regard, was nearly the cause of a falling-out between his third uncle and Mrs. Ashcroft.

He once suspected his third uncle had feelings for Mrs. Ashcroft, even wondering if her daughter could be his uncle's biological child.

Alexander had no interest in continuing this conversation.

This was Evelyn's private matter. Speaking of it would only stain Mrs. Ashcroft's reputation with accusations of infidelity. He chose to remain silent.

Knowing Alexander's temperament, Marcus didn't press further. Instead, he studied Evelyn's face, trying to find any resemblance to his third uncle…

Linda Lowell rushed forward. "I'm her mother! I'll sign!"

The doctor scrutinized her. "You're the biological mother?"

Linda was speechless.

Whether by household registration or birth certificate, she currently had no legal standing!

Linda broke down completely. "Mr. Evans! After the hysterectomy, Isabella can never have children! She's only twenty-two! Her life is ruined! How could you…"

Julian took two steps back, cast one last look at the ER doors, then turned and walked away resolutely.

When she switched the children all those years ago, it was to give Isabella a life of luxury. How did it come to this?

In her childhood, Linda was her nightmare.

Even as an adult, she often dreamt of that helplessness and pain from being whipped.

How dare Linda think she would help?

She had taken it from Mrs. Ashcroft's hospital room. Now, with nothing left to lose, she aimed it straight at Evelyn's heart!

Remembering his third uncle's orders, this woman not only bullied Evelyn but also had the audacity to attack?

He kicked hard at Linda's knee.

A sickening crack echoed. Her knee dislocated. Linda screamed, dropping to one knee.

Marcus exclaimed, "Still holding the knife? That's too dangerous!"

He bent down and efficiently dislocated Linda's left arm!

Linda lay completely incapacitated on the floor.

Grace Lorenz: "…"

She already had a warrant. Now, with attempted murder with a deadly weapon, and even assaulting an officer…

The combined charges were enough to put her away for life.

Not to mention Marcus had shattered her leg bone. She would never walk again.

Sweating profusely from pain, Linda rolled on the ground.

The others watched coldly, unmoved.

Evelyn ignored her.

Evelyn's expression remained calm.

But Alexander and Marcus clenched their fists.

Marcus rubbed the back of his hand, sucking in a sharp breath. "Why do my hands itch again?"

Linda trembled in fear. Knowing she was doomed, she decided to go all out!

She glared venomously at Evelyn, spewing insults:

This vicious woman felt no remorse, only that fate was unfair!

###Chapter 204

She still lay quietly, eyes closed, breathing steadily. Her complexion was rosy, as if she were merely asleep.

Evelyn longed for her to wake up. To ask who her biological father was. To unravel the secrets of the past…

Her lips moved. She wanted to call her “Mom,” but no sound came out.

Evelyn paid it no mind. Ambulances at a hospital were normal.

They checked Sophia Ashcroft’s condition. Everything was normal.

Evelyn initially didn’t pay attention.

Because Sophia had something to say before fainting.

To go back with Alexander to see Grandma!

Down in the parking lot.

###Chapter 210

"Mom, would you like some water?"

"Mom..."

A series of "Moms" made Marcus think of his five-year-old nephew.

His cousin's wife got a headache from the noise every day.

But Mrs. Ashcroft always smiled gently.

Without the slightest annoyance.

He rushed into Mr. Valentine's room to complain about Evelyn's ingratitude.

Marcus's heart tightened.

Mr. Valentine pondered for a moment. "Can't go to the Valentine residence. The Vance residence should be fine. I heard the old lady is ill. I'll pay a visit tomorrow."

Marcus's eyes widened.

Backing!

The old lady had Alzheimer's.
She was sometimes lucid, sometimes confused.

Cecilia was much more cunning than Julian.

Evelyn would never trust Eleanor to such a scheming woman.

###Chapter 216

Evelyn stood outside Eleanor's hospital room.

Evelyn raised an eyebrow, saying nothing.

Cecilia was stunned.

Cecilia's smile stiffened. "Since it's not a major concern, let's forget it."

Evelyn let out a soft, derisive sound.

Mr. Valentine gave a slight nod. "I heard Mrs. Ashcroft's daughter married into the Vance family. It's only right I visit Mrs. Vance and see how Evelyn is doing."

Robert and Richard's faces froze.
They exchanged a glance, sweat beading on their temples.

Mr. Valentine walked toward the manor casually. "Mrs. Ashcroft and I are old classmates. I'm here today to formally acknowledge Evelyn as my goddaughter."

The urgent matter was investigating if Dr. Lawrence's theft was connected to Dr. Frank Melville.
