###Chapter 218

Mr.Valentine stood frozen.

For a moment, he saw his mother in her youth.

The girl before him bore a striking resemblance to his late mother, making it hard to look away.

He stared blankly at Evelyn Ross, his feet moving forward on their own.

Evelyn noticed the handsome older man approaching and paused.

She glanced around, confirming no one else was nearby. Was he coming for her?

As she wondered, Mr.Valentine sat across from her. Their eyes met.

Evelyn asked, "Can I help you?"

Mr.Valentine replied, "Weren't you the one staring first?"

Evelyn was confused.

Had she just been targeted for looking too long?

She smiled lightly. "I just thought you looked familiar, sir."

"What a coincidence." His voice was deep and cool, yet nostalgic. "So did I."

He hadn't seen his mother in over twenty years. This girl's face...

He asked suddenly, "May I ask your father's surname?"

Evelyn was puzzled.

The man seemed odd, yet strangely familiar. She didn't want to lie.

William Ashcroft wasn't her biological father. Mr.Valentine might be, or might not. Last time, Sophia Ashcroft hadn't answered, her eyes filled with sorrow. Evelyn hadn't pressed further.

After a moment, she said, "I don't know."

Mr.Valentine was surprised. "How can you not know?"

"I've never met him."

Evelyn shrugged. "Is that strange?"

Seeing her casual demeanor, he chuckled and shook his head. "Not at all."

A happy smile spread across Evelyn's lips. "Exactly. Who he is doesn't matter. Having my mom is everything."

Mr.Valentine asked, "You're close with your mother?"

"Yes."

Since learning the truth about her origins, these past days had been her happiest.

The suppressed joy in her heart longed to be shared.

She was usually quiet, rarely opening up to anyone. Alexander Vance had been worried about his grandmother lately. She didn't want to add to his burdens.

But this unfamiliar uncle made her want to talk.

She looked at Mr.Valentine and spoke slowly. "You know what? My mom is the best in the world. When I failed exams, she never scolded me. Instead, she bought cake to cheer me up. She never demanded I achieve great things. She just wanted me to be happy."

That was how Sophia had raised Isabella Ashcroft.

Evelyn had dreamed countless times of being Isabella, held gently by Mrs. Ashcroft, comforted softly.

Those dreams used to make her guilty, as if she had stolen something from Isabella.

She shouldn't feel even a hint of envy.

But now she knew they weren't just dreams.

Evelyn continued, "I got married. Mom never said I married beneath me. When she heard my husband was a troublemaker, she just smiled gently. She asked me to bring him home to meet her."

She chattered on and on.

Only when she saw the handsome uncle's stern face soften did she snap back to reality. "Sorry, I talked too much."

Mr.Valentine didn't mind. Gazing at her face, he thought of his own mother. Nostalgia filled his eyes. "Your mother sounds wonderful. But my mother was better."

Evelyn was taken aback.

She didn't like that.

She rolled up her sleeves, smiling insincerely. "Impossible! No one is better than my mom!"

Mr.Valentine frowned. "I've never seen a more open-minded mother than mine."

"You haven't met enough people." Evelyn scoffed. "My mom is not only gentle, but also beautiful!"

Mr.Valentine said coldly, "My mother was more beautiful! She was known as the top beauty of her time!"

Evelyn dismissed it. "If you met my mom, you'd understand. No one can resist her charm!"

Mr.Valentine was intrigued. "Then take me to meet her?"

"Fine! Let's go now!" Evelyn stood up angrily, eager to show off her mom to the world.

The tension between them was palpable. Other diners glanced over. A waiter nervously approached to intervene, but then...

Evelyn and Mr.Valentine glared at each other, then suddenly burst into laughter together.

Evelyn felt silly and inexplicable!

A smile appeared on Mr.Valentine's stern face. He felt absurd for arguing with a young girl at his age.

Mr.Valentine said slowly, "You have plans tonight?"

Evelyn nodded. "Yes, my husband will be here soon."

Mr.Valentine suggested, "I heard this private kitchen is excellent. I'd like to try more dishes, but ordering alone is wasteful. Shall we share a table?"

Evelyn agreed immediately. "No problem! Are you from out of town? This meal is on me!"

Mr.Valentine, always direct, nodded. "Then I'll accept your generosity."

The handsome uncle's personality appealed to Evelyn. She felt more favorable toward him. She waved to the waiter. "Bring all your signature dishes!"

It was her restaurant anyway. They could eat as much as they wanted.

Mr.Valentine didn't object. He said slowly, "Shall we exchange WhatsApp?"

"Sure!"

Evelyn took out her phone to scan his code.

Mr.Valentine hinted, "I don't usually add just anyone."

Evelyn smiled faintly. "Same here."

After adding each other, Evelyn smiled again. "Uncle, from now on, you're my friend. If you need anything, just ask."

Mr.Valentine said immediately, "Actually, I have a problem. I need your advice."

Evelyn asked, "What is it?"

"I accidentally offended a younger relative. How can I make it up to her?"

Evelyn thought for a moment. "Boy or girl?"

"Girl."

Mr.Valentine nodded, taking notes. "I'll try that."

"Okay. If it doesn't work, message me. I'll give you more ideas."

Evelyn shook her phone. She saw a message from Alexander Vance: [I'm at the entrance. Are you in the main hall or a private room?]

She immediately looked toward the door and saw Alexander getting out of his car.

Her eyes lit up as she waved to him.
###Chapter 219

Sophia Watson's sobbing grated on his nerves.

He was nine when he was trafficked to a mountain village.

The traffickers gradually allowed her to do chores outside.

She and Alexander became friends.

He asked for her name.

She said her mother hadn't given her one yet.

He was shocked a four-year-old had no name.

He decided to save himself.

He told the girl to drug the traffickers.

She successfully drugged them and unlocked the cell.

When he sent people to find Little Dumpling, they only found Sophia Watson.

It was true she had no name before age four.

At eighteen, she was covered in wounds and had severe depression.

Alexander brought her abroad for treatment.

She constantly pressured him to honor the marriage pact.

He didn't know if his childhood promise still held weight.

He owed her too much.

Only four square meters.

Leaving only Evelyn here.

Pulled out a cardboard box.

When she left the Ashcroft home back then.

Saying she was afraid Sophia would divide her love with Evelyn.

Afraid of causing more harm.

Extremely valuable.

When they were separated after being trafficked as children.

The big brother gave her this jade ring.

It was her only hope when she was near death.

With real force.

Using childhood memories to emotionally blackmail him.

Her only reason to live was him.

###Chapter 221

Fiona whispered her complaints, treating Evelyn as the lady of the house. "...All of these were meant for you by the Madam. Now she's taken everything!"

Eleanor Vance was still asleep. Evelyn checked her complexion and the monitor readings. Everything was stable, which put her mind at ease.

The moonlight was bright and beautiful indeed.

But he only glanced at it before his gaze fell on the girl's profile.

Her features were sharp, her skin fair and almost translucent. Long eyelashes... a straight nose that gave her a spirited look, paired with full lips...

Alexander's Adam's apple bobbed.

Evelyn was about to walk forward when she noticed the man had stopped.

She turned curiously and instantly fell into the man's deep eyes.

Those eyes were like a vast starry sky, bottomless.

They gazed at each other. Alexander suddenly stepped forward, closing the distance.

The winter night was chilly and quiet. No insects chirped among the trees.

It was so quiet they could hear their own heartbeats.

Evelyn pressed her lips together, her breathing growing heavier.

The atmosphere suddenly became charged.

Alexander lowered his head...

His face drew closer and closer... his breath almost brushing her cheek.

The scent of vanilla from the man enveloped her. Evelyn instinctively closed her eyes.

"Thump, thump, thump..."

Her heart raced.

Was he going to kiss her?

Evelyn was never afraid of anything, but now she felt so nervous her chest tightened...

Evelyn: ?

"What else?"

"Nothing."

Evelyn stiffened completely!

After a gentle exploration, the man began to claim her!

The winter wind was cold.

Evelyn struggled to breathe and tried to pull back, but the man's large hand held the back of her head.

She unconsciously wrapped her arms around the man's waist...

Liam faced Alexander. "Three percent shares for two more years of the matriarch's life. Do you agree? If not, I will not provide any more medication! I'll pay for the three missing doses myself. Consider it a gift to the matriarch!"

Cecilia quickly added. "But Cousin said stopping the treatment will accelerate her decline!"

Liam nodded. "The matriarch originally had one or two months. Stopping now… she might last two weeks at most."

Robert Vance's expression darkened.

Elder Howard Vance's eyes reddened. "Do it! We must do it! If you won't give the shares, I will!"

His children urgently tried to stop him. "Father!"

Richard looked at Alexander. "Cecilia has already done her filial duty. Our branch can't afford three percent. Whether you save Grandma is up to you now."

All eyes turned to Alexander.

Evelyn felt nothing but scorn.

Matriarch Eleanor Vance suddenly spoke. "I won't take the medication anymore."

Everyone was stunned.

The matriarch was calm. "I've lived long enough. Trading three percent shares for two more years isn't worth it."

Elder Howard was agitated. "Sister-in-law! You must fight for those two years!"

Robert glared at Alexander. "Your grandmother dotes on you most. Can't you even spare three percent shares? Will you just watch her die?"

Alexander's eyes were dark.

Giving three percent would cost him his position as chairman.

But he didn't care.

He was about to speak—

"No."

Evelyn cut him off decisively.

She stepped in front of Alexander. "Want the shares? Not a chance."

Robert was furious. "Ungrateful son!"

Elder Howard was heartbroken. "Alexander's wife, you disappoint me deeply! If you won't give them, I will!"

A flash of triumph crossed Richard's eyes.

Evelyn said calmly, "You don't need to do that."

Elder Howard trembled with anger. "They are my shares! Can't I decide for myself?!"

He looked at Alexander. "Does your wife speak for you?"

Alexander gazed at Evelyn.

Though he didn't know her plan, he nodded firmly. "Yes."

Elder Howard staggered back. "Alexander… You and your grandmother relied on each other all these years. Would you abandon her for money? I am so disappointed!"

Robert added fuel to the fire. "Uncle, I told you this beast is heartless!"

Richard was about to speak—

The butler rushed in. "This is terrible!"

Everyone turned.

The butler was breathless. "There's a man at the gate… He says he's Frank Melville… Director of the New York Research Institute!"
###Chapter 227

Dean Frank Melville?

His voice was firm.

"I didn't bring the third dose! There will be no more medication!"

Richard Evans said coolly.

Frank Melville walked straight to Dr. Lawrence.
"Liam Lawrence, I trusted you so much! I thought as a Truman family relative, no one could pressure you. I never expected you to steal from within!"

He had heard what Evelyn said yesterday and thought it was a misunderstanding. He rushed back to New York overnight.

Upon opening the safe, he found one of the fifty medication packages missing.

He was terrified and immediately returned to give Dr. Nan an explanation.

Dr. Lawrence hurried to explain.

She looked at Alexander.

Alexander cooperated.
"Approximately…"

The punishment was based on the medication's price. If it was really worth billions, he could get life imprisonment!

"I didn't ask for the shares! She was the one who wanted them! I didn't take a single cent! I just wanted to do a favor for the Truman family! Check my bank account if you don't believe me! There are no large deposits!"

This was the truth.

Being a Truman family relative was just that. He wanted to use this to strengthen his ties with the Trumans. The future benefits were immeasurable.

Cecilia Evans turned deathly pale.

Evelyn smiled coldly with satisfaction.
"Then calculate it at a hundred times the price after the medication hits the market."

Clinical medication before marketing was more expensive than after.

Frank Melville understood.
He quoted a number to the police.
The amount wasn't high. Dr. Lawrence would get at most two or three years.

But his reputation in the field was ruined. After prison, he could never work as a researcher again.

That was the heaviest punishment.

The police took Dr. Lawrence away.

Cecilia Evans was ashen.

###Chapter 228

###Chapter 229

What if that truth…

Both parties need to be present to get a marriage license.

Neither of them was disabled.

There was no proxy.

What exactly happened back then?

Was it a setup?

Was her Nora identity exposed?

Did someone want to use the Vance family against her?

Her deep thought made Alexander's heart sink.

The unspoken truth between them had never been addressed.

Even though he had publicly acknowledged her.

He had never said he loved her.

She hadn't thought about divorce for a long time.

He used Grandma to keep her for a month.

Without genuine feelings.

Not appropriate.

She really couldn't rely on him.

Handsome Uncle: [Okay, I'll try.]

She put down her phone.

On the other side.

She didn't allow her to take anything.

Cecilia nodded. "I understand. Dad, don't worry. I have a plan."

Richard breathed a sigh of relief.

He doted on this love child.

Not because of her mother.

In powerful families, sons usually inherit.

Following her advice always brought benefits.

Suppressing Alexander when they were young.

Making him hide abroad and only return this year.

Were all his daughter's ideas.

She wasn't in San Francisco.

###Chapter 230

Evelyn Ross hesitated for a moment before speaking softly. "Grandma, was it you who arranged for my marriage certificate with Alexander Vance?"

Eleanor Vance took her hand and smiled affectionately. "How could that be? Do you really think the wealthy can control everything? Both parties must be present to get married!"

Evelyn grew even more confused but didn't press further, listening quietly as the elderly woman continued.

Eleanor hadn't been this clear-minded in years. She spoke slowly. "It was about two years ago, I can't remember the exact date. Alexander had just returned from studying abroad and had been home for a week."

"I woke up from my nap feeling disoriented and wanted to talk to him. Somehow I wandered out of the house and kept walking until I fell on the road. My legs were weak and my arm was injured. I tried to get up but couldn't. I struggled on the ground for a long time, asking several passersby for help... Ah, no one dared to help me up. I understand - people are afraid of being scammed by the elderly these days. That's when you appeared, dear! Do you remember?"

Evelyn suddenly recalled the incident.

She had always had an excellent memory. When she helped the elderly woman that day, many people had tried to dissuade her.

But Evelyn paid them no mind.

She asked someone to record a video as evidence before approaching to help the woman up.

Discovering the woman's leg injury, she carried her to a nearby hospital for treatment.

Then came the dramatic turn.

After the nurse finished bandaging the injury, Evelyn prepared to leave, but the elderly woman grabbed her hand, refusing to let go.

Evelyn thought she had encountered one of those scam artists and frowned.

But the old woman's eyes shone brightly as she asked, "Good child, are you married?"

Evelyn shook her head.

The woman smiled. "I have a hopeless grandson. Would you consider making do with him?"

Evelyn was stunned.

"Good child, we're fated! You were born to be my granddaughter-in-law. Let me introduce you to my grandson? He's very handsome!"

Eleanor took out her phone to show a photo.

Evelyn glanced at it and felt both amused and exasperated. "Grandma, I'm not thinking about marriage yet!"

Eleanor looked genuinely disappointed. "Is that so... Well, if you ever want to marry but can't find the right person, come find me! My grandson says he'll never marry in this lifetime. It's so worrying."

Eleanor handed her a business card.

Evelyn didn't say much. Over the years, she had met many capable elderly people and learned numerous skills from them.

She knew well that older people often developed eccentric personalities.

Leaving the hospital, she placed the business card on a nearby trash can and walked away.

It was just a minor incident in an ordinary day, gradually fading from memory.

Only when Eleanor mentioned it today did she recall that day, superimposing the woman before her with the memory.

Evelyn exclaimed in surprise, "So that was you!"

"Yes, that's why I say we're fated, dear!" Eleanor chuckled and continued.

"I thought you really didn't want to get married and felt regretful for several days. Then on the fourth day, you suddenly called saying you'd changed your mind. I tricked that rascal into coming, and you two went to City Hall to get married!~"

Eleanor gave her a reproachful look. "After your marriage that day, you brought us home. You said you'd go back to pack your things and move in, leaving the marriage certificate with me for safekeeping. But you never returned... Dear, why didn't you come back to us? Did you change your mind?"

Evelyn's eyes widened in astonishment.

She had clearly thrown away the business card. How could she have gone back to find the old woman?

She asked seriously, "Are you sure the person who came to you later was really me?"

"It was you!"

Eleanor nodded emphatically. "Your marriage registration was completely proper. The photo was taken on the spot. Isn't that you in the marriage certificate photo?"

Evelyn's jaw tightened.

She suddenly looked at Alexander Vance, noticing his slight frown, as if this wasn't surprising to him.

Evelyn asked directly, "Mr. Vance, according to what Grandma says, you personally registered the marriage. How could you not remember later?"

Alexander gave a bitter smile, massaging his temples helplessly.

Before he could speak, Eleanor interjected. "Dear, that rascal has one weakness - he can't handle alcohol. One glass makes him drunk. But he's different when drunk. Outwardly he seems normal, but he becomes extremely obedient. He'll do whatever I tell him to!"

Eleanor said triumphantly, "So back then I tricked him into taking a sip. While he was muddled, I made him go with you to get registered!"

Evelyn: "..."

Since meeting Alexander, she had indeed never seen him drink alcohol.

She looked at Alexander again. The man sighed softly. "When Grandma first showed me the marriage certificate, I had my suspicions. It turns out I was right."

Alexander looked at his grandmother. "Grandma, you were too reckless. Fortunately it was Evelyn. If it had been someone else..."

The consequences would have been unthinkable!

Evelyn's cheeks flushed slightly. What did he mean by "fortunately it was Evelyn"?

Eleanor snorted. "I was dressed in rags that day. So many people passed by without helping. Only you came to my aid, showing your good heart. Did you think I would choose just anyone to be your wife?"

Eleanor teased, "You rascal, just admit it - are you satisfied or not?"

Alexander didn't speak, but his gaze softened as he looked at the girl.

He noticed Evelyn's serious expression, as if facing a great crisis.

Remembering their earlier conversation, he frowned slightly and asked quietly, "What's wrong? Is there a problem?"

"Of course there's a problem."

Evelyn looked grave as she met his eyes. "Because after helping Grandma that day, I never went to find her again! The person who registered the marriage with you wasn't me at all!"

Alexander's pupils contracted.

Eleanor also froze, then stated firmly, "Impossible! Dear, how could I mistake someone else for you?"

As if to prove her point, she hurriedly took out her phone. "Right, right! After you agreed to marry, while we were waiting for that rascal, you told me many things. You said your mother was Sophia Ashcroft, and even showed me a photo of you together..."

She produced that photoshopped group photo again.

When Eleanor had previously shown this photo, insisting Sophia was her mother, Evelyn had thought someone was playing a cruel trick on the old woman. Now that she and Sophia had been reunited...

This meant that the imposter had known all along that she was Sophia's biological daughter?!

Evelyn felt a chill run down her spine.

This was all too surreal, like a dream!

Eleanor suddenly understood something. "Now I know what happened!"
###Chapter 231

Evelyn and Alexander turned to Eleanor simultaneously.

Evelyn asked, "You know what's going on?"

Alexander pressed, "Grandmother, what exactly happened?"

Eleanor glanced around mysteriously and lowered her voice. "Does my granddaughter-in-law have multiple personalities? The primary personality usually suppresses the secondary one, so she doesn't remember anything afterward!"

Evelyn fell silent.

Alexander remained speechless.

Eleanor continued on her own. "That's how it always is in TV dramas! There's even this novel where the male lead has seven or eight personalities. The female lead essentially married several husbands. What a steal!"

Evelyn stayed quiet.

Alexander's lips twitched. "Grandmother, mind your dignity."

Eleanor hastily wiped her mouth and glared at him before turning to Evelyn. "Granddaughter-in-law, do you really have schizophrenia?"

Evelyn massaged her temples. "Really, I don't."

She underwent comprehensive physical exams every year, with particular attention to mental health.

Eleanor looked puzzled. "Then what else could it be?"

Evelyn shook her head.

Alexander took her hand. "Whatever the situation, you are my wife. We'll face whatever comes together."

Noticing Eleanor's tired expression, Evelyn changed the subject. "Grandmother, you should rest now."

Eleanor nodded and grasped Evelyn's hand. "You are legally married. You must live well together."

Evelyn agreed.

As they were about to leave, Eleanor suddenly pulled Evelyn back. She waved Alexander away. "You go out first. I have some private words for my granddaughter-in-law."

Alexander had no choice but to exit.

As the door closed, he faintly heard Eleanor ask, "Is that rascal decent in bed? If he's not, don't hide it from me…"

Alexander stood frozen outside.

Inside the room, Evelyn's cheeks flushed slightly.

Eleanor regained her composure and sighed softly. "Actually, I wanted to talk to you about Sophia Watson."

Evelyn's eyes flickered.

Eleanor spoke gravely. "Alexander appears cold but is warm-hearted. He has an overwhelming sense of responsibility. It's both a strength and a weakness."

She tightened her grip on Evelyn's hand. "When I arranged your marriage, I didn't know about Sophia Watson's existence. Now that you are husband and wife, Alexander would never let you down. As for Sophia Watson… you need to be careful."

Evelyn felt a warmth in her heart. "Thank you, Grandmother."

…

Evelyn and Alexander moved back to the main house.

Cecilia Evans tried to stay at the Vance residence but was firmly refused by Alexander.

Robert Vance and Richard Evans attempted to oppose, but Alexander remained resolute.

His previous compromise was due to the special medicine. Now that Evelyn had provided the medication, Alexander had no more reservations.

Richard Evans used the Truman family as leverage. "Cecilia is now engaged to the Trumans. She must be married from the Vance home!"

Alexander sneered. "If you can't bear to part with your daughter, we can divide the family. The Evans branch should have moved out long ago. Then you can welcome whoever you want home."

Richard Evans was instantly silenced.

Night fell, moonlight spilling down.

Evelyn walked slowly, still pondering the day's events.

She felt an invisible hand was manipulating her marriage to Alexander.

Her left hand was suddenly enveloped in a warm palm. Alexander asked softly, "What are you thinking about?"

Evelyn replied, "I'm thinking about who is scheming against us."

She had checked her schedule from the day they registered their marriage. She was working in the lab all day.

Who was that person who appeared at the Vance home and knew her secret?

Alexander also frowned.

Unable to figure it out, they decided to set it aside for now.

As they entered the courtyard, Alexander's phone rang.

A soft, feminine voice came through. "Alexander, can you come have dinner with me?"

Alexander's tone was cold. "I'm busy."

The voice turned tearful. "I'm sick again… I can't eat without you here… The doctor says my stomach will fail if I don't eat…"

Alexander was silent for a moment. "Wait for me."

Evelyn's heart sank.

She turned to leave, but Alexander grasped her wrist.

His gaze was intense. "Come with me?"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

He wanted to make a clean break.

She nodded simply. "Alright."

The atmosphere in the car grew heavy.

Seeing Alexander's solemn expression, Evelyn spoke up. "What exactly is between you and Sophia Watson?"
###Chapter 232

Alexander Vance's breath hitched.

He looked out the car window, loosening his tie with a finger.

"She saved my life."

"I promised to marry her when I grew up."

He bore responsibility for Sophia Watson's current obsession.

Sophia wasn't wrong about one thing.

The little girl nicknamed "Dim Sum" was different from other trafficked children.

Dim Sum was adorable and well-behaved.

Even the traffickers showed her extra kindness.

They promised to find her a good family.

Back then, Dim Sum even ate at the same table with them.

Evelyn Ross froze at his words.

Long-buried memories surfaced.

A young boy had once made her the same promise.

He said he would come back for her.

He said he would marry her.

But he never appeared all these years.

No wonder Eleanor Vance always said Alexander valued duty above his own life.

The car entered the psychiatric hospital.

The grounds were quiet and shaded by trees.

Sophia was the only patient in the entire facility.

It showed how thoroughly Alexander cared for her.

The moment the car stopped, Evelyn followed Alexander toward the patient rooms.

They ran into Sophia's attending physician.

The doctor looked relieved to see Alexander.

"Mr. Vance, Ms. Watson has refused to eat since yesterday."

"Everything she tries to eat, she vomits back up."

"If this continues, her digestive system will fail even with IV nutrients."

Evelyn thought to herself.

Could Sophia not survive without Alexander?

Before they reached the room, a weak crying voice carried out.

"Is Alexander here?"

"I won't eat anything unless he comes."

"Vomiting hurts so much. I need Alexander..."

Alexander paused.

He turned and took Evelyn's hand.

He led her into the room together.

Evelyn looked up.

Sophia wore an oversized hospital gown.

She was thinner than at the Vance family birthday banquet.

The clothes hung loosely on her frame.

Her face was pale as paper.

Her cheeks were sunken, her body gaunt.

Seeing Alexander, her eyes lit up.

"Alexander..."

Alexander's jaw tightened.

"I heard you're not eating properly."

"I brought your sister-in-law to see you."

He emphasized "sister-in-law."

Sophia's gaze finally left him and landed on Evelyn.

Panic and hostility flashed in her eyes.

Her voice trembled.

"Alexander, what do you mean?"

Alexander spoke bluntly.

"Ms. Watson, I am married."

"Please learn to be independent."

Sophia stared, tears instantly falling.

"Alexander, are you abandoning me?"

Alexander looked down.

"I will continue covering your medical expenses."

Sophia looked incredulous.

"You... won't come to see me anymore?"

Alexander remained silent.

Sophia cried harder.

"I'll die if I can't see you!"

"Alexander, you can't do this to me..."

She struggled to get out of bed.

Weak from hunger, she swayed unsteadily.

She still reached out for Alexander, trying to grasp him.

"Alexander, I saved your life!"

"You promised to marry me... Have you forgotten?!"

Alexander's voice was calm.

"A nine-year-old's childish words shouldn't be taken seriously."

"But I took them seriously!"

Sophia screamed hoarsely.

"I took them seriously!"

"I waited for you since childhood, hoping you would marry me!"

"You are my salvation, Alexander!"

"When you first found me, you said you would take care of me forever..."

"Now you've married someone else. Are you throwing me away?"

After shouting, she collapsed back onto the bed, exhausted.

Alexander frowned.

"I promised to take care of you, but I never said I would marry you."

"But you're married!"

"You're starting a new life. You'll have your own children..."

"You'll slowly forget me! You are abandoning me!"

She cried pitifully, like an abandoned young animal.

"If even you don't want me, who else in this world will?"

"What's the point of me living?"

"Alexander, you know my condition..."

"If you won't take care of me, just kill me now."

"It's better than me torturing myself later!"

Alexander's jaw clenched.

Evelyn stepped forward with a cold laugh.

She shielded Alexander and looked directly at Sophia.

"Calling him 'brother' over and over. Are you a hen trying to lay an egg?"

Sophia's crying stopped abruptly.

Evelyn's voice was icy.

"Take responsibility for your own life."

"Why should someone else carry it for you?"

Sophia pointed a trembling finger at Alexander.

"He promised to marry me!"

"Childhood jokes. Who takes them seriously?" Evelyn scoffed.

"I had a brother who said he would marry me when we were kids."

"He's still nowhere to be seen."

"Should I go force him to marry me?"

Sophia was speechless.

But Alexander suddenly looked at Evelyn.

"What brother?"

Evelyn glanced at him.

"Who didn't play house as a child? There's no need to take it seriously."

Alexander fell silent.

Sophia regained her composure and cried.

"It's not the same!"

"I saved Alexander with my life!"

"If not for me, he would have been beaten to death!"

"Those people were kidnapping for ransom. I was just trafficked alongside..."

"I saved Alexander!"

"We agreed then. He had to take responsibility for me. He had to marry me..."

"Alexander, you can't go back on your word!"

Evelyn suddenly froze.

Trafficked?

Why did this experience sound so familiar?
###Chapter 233

Evelyn looked at Alexander.

He had only mentioned that Sophia once saved his life.

She never expected it was this kind of life-saving grace.

No wonder he always showed her such leniency.

Alexander tightened his grip on her hand. "I'm sorry. I shouldn't have brought this up."

He looked at Sophia, his voice low. "A childhood promise is still a promise. I originally planned to never marry. To be there for you whenever you needed me. But then I met Evelyn."

He paused. "I have a marriage pact with her. I fell in love with her. I've done a lot of thinking lately."

His tone was firm. "Between you and her, I must choose. I choose her."

"Miss Watson, I won't be visiting again. I will continue covering your medical expenses. You can direct any requests to the hospital director. I will try to fulfill any need, except for my companionship."

Sophia's face instantly turned pale.

Alexander didn't linger. He took Evelyn's hand and turned to leave.

Sophia's heart-wrenching sobs echoed behind them.

Alexander didn't look back.

Evelyn held his hand tightly.

His coldness toward another was his depth of feeling for her.

As long as he didn't let go, she wouldn't either.

They got in the car and drove away.

Alexander's phone kept ringing.

He glanced at it and blocked Sophia's number directly.

Soon, the hospital director called. "Mr. Vance, Miss Watson is threatening suicide again!"

Alexander's voice was calm. "She is your patient. I am not a doctor."

The director was stunned.

Alexander looked straight ahead. "Do not contact me about this again."

"O-okay."

The director clearly hadn't expected such finality.

Evelyn rode home with him in silence.

They went to check on Eleanor first.

Seeing she was sleeping peacefully, they felt reassured and returned to their room.

Evelyn was heading for the guest bedroom when Alexander stopped her.

He looked at her deeply. "Evelyn, stay with me tonight."

Her heart raced.

She understood his meaning.

Though shy, she nodded. "Okay."

Blushing, she entered his room.

The moment the door closed, he embraced her from behind.

Evelyn stiffened slightly.

He just held her tightly, burying his face in her neck.

He made no further move.

She relaxed.

She heard his soft laugh. "Evelyn, I'm very happy today."

Evelyn smiled too.

She hadn't expected him to be so decisive.

He had ended things before she even had to ask.

This was good.

She returned his embrace. "I'm happy too."

That night, they slept in each other's arms.

She could feel his restraint.

She wasn't truly ready yet.

Taking it slow was perfect.

...

The next morning.

Elder Howard came to have breakfast with Eleanor.

Hearing that Alexander and Evelyn were still asleep, Eleanor beamed.

Fiona had told her early that they entered the same room last night.

She was overjoyed.

Elder Howard frowned. "Elder sister, isn't it disrespectful for the younger generation to sleep this late? Do they usually show you proper respect?"

He was concerned about Evelyn neglecting Eleanor.

Eleanor immediately waved her hand. "What's wrong with young people sleeping in? My granddaughter-in-law is wonderful! She's filial, kind, and protects Alexander. I couldn't be more pleased!"

Elder Howard felt relieved. "Filial piety is good. It's just her background is somewhat lacking."

Eleanor was displeased. "What's lacking about it?"

"I heard she's just from an ordinary family in San Francisco?" Elder Howard sighed. "The illegitimate daughter of the main family is about to marry into the Truman family. If the Trumans support the main family, Alexander will be under great pressure. We can't hope for one of the Five Great Families of New York, but even a first-tier family would be better…"

Eleanor knew he meant well, but she still scolded him. "Don't you dare say that in front of my granddaughter-in-law! Nobody is perfect. She has everything else, so what if her background is lacking?"

Elder Howard shook his head and sighed.

A marriage alliance is also a form of support.

Evelyn's background was indeed a pity…

As they spoke, Evelyn and Alexander emerged from the bedroom.

Fiona came in from outside and handed Evelyn a box. "Madam, Mr. Valentine sent this over first thing this morning."

Evelyn looked at the box.

It bore the logo of a luxury jewelry brand.

Just yesterday, someone said jewelry could be used to appease the younger generation.

Today, Mr. Valentine sent some.

Could it really be a coincidence?
###Chapter 234

Big Brother

Could the handsome older man be Mr.Valentine?

Evelyn immediately opened the large box.

Inside was a delicate small box tied with a pink bow, clearly a gift for a girl.

She opened the small box and saw a luxury bag from the same brand.

Evelyn instantly breathed a sigh of relief.

What a coincidence that would be!

Her phone vibrated with a message from Marcus.

"Did you receive the gift from Third Uncle? Can we meet now?"

Evelyn thought for a moment.

Flowers yesterday, a bag today. If she ignored him, he would probably send something else tomorrow.

She replied, "Please tell Uncle Xu I'm not angry. But a meeting isn't necessary."

Marcus relayed the message immediately.

Mr.Valentine fell silent for a moment before sighing softly. "Then forget it. We return to New York the day after tomorrow."

"Alright."

Marcus asked, "Third Uncle, didn't your friend say he was sending jewelry? Why a bag?"

Mr.Valentine shook his head. "Jewelry is too expensive. Miss Nan wouldn't let her accept it."

Jewelry from that brand cost millions.

Mr.Valentine thought it would be presumptuous, so he chose a bag worth a few thousand dollars.

Girls liked them all the same anyway!

Mr.Valentine also felt he was being stubborn.

He couldn't understand this persistent urge to see her.

His phone vibrated with a message from his young friend.

Young Friend: "Handsome Uncle, did you manage to appease your junior?"

Mr.Valentine chuckled and replied. "It's settled. Returning to New York the day after tomorrow. Don't forget our dinner plans."

Young Friend: "Don't worry! I'm bringing my mom~ I'll show you the best mom in the world!"

Mr.Valentine smiled. "Alright. Let's see whose mom is better."

…

Evelyn didn't return the bag.

A few thousand dollars wasn't worth the hassle.

She set it aside and had breakfast with Alexander.

They were having Cantonese dim sum today.

Alexander placed a shrimp dumpling on her plate.

Evelyn took a bite, her eyes lighting up. "So juicy and tender! Delicious!"

"Really?"

Alexander leaned over and ate the remaining half from her chopsticks.

He looked at her lips and smiled. "Indeed very sweet."

Evelyn felt teased!

Since last night, this man had become much bolder.

Evelyn stuffed another shrimp dumpling into her mouth, chewing fiercely.

She didn't realize her supposedly fierce peach blossom eyes now looked captivating and alluring.

Grandma Eleanor burped, feeling full from all the dog food.

After the meal, Alexander looked at Evelyn. "I'm heading to the office first. I'll be back for lunch and will work from home this afternoon."

He was proactively reporting his schedule.

Evelyn nodded with a smile.

Alexander turned to leave.

His phone rang abruptly!

The ringtone made Evelyn's heart clench with a bad premonition.

Alexander took out his phone. It was a call from Thomas.

Whatever was said on the other end made him freeze instantly!

Evelyn walked over, frowning. "What's wrong?"

Alexander's jaw tightened, his face dark.

The invisible pressure he had shed last night returned.

He gripped Evelyn's hand tightly and said slowly, "Sophia Watson jumped off a building."

…

In the hospital ER.

Sophia Watson breathed weakly, covered in blood.

Doctors and nurses rushed in and out, bringing numerous blood bags.

Evelyn stood with Alexander outside the operating room.

The woman on the bed was pale and thin, her arms and legs exposed with multiple abrasions.

Her internal organs were damaged, bleeding continuously.

Rough estimates suggested the blood in her body had been replaced four times.

She jumped from the tenth floor of the psychiatric hospital and had no pulse upon arrival.

Critical condition notices had been issued four times…

Evelyn once thought Sophia was being dramatic.

Starving herself was just a threat tactic.

She never imagined Sophia truly didn't want to live without Alexander.

She looked at Alexander again.

The man's face was icy, his eyes fixed blankly on the person on the bed.

The emotions in his eyes were complex and profound, impossible for Evelyn to decipher.

When Alexander made his decision yesterday, he had anticipated this outcome.

He knew Sophia had severe psychological issues. They were real.

But knowing and witnessing were two different things.

The frail Sophia on the bed reminded him of his childhood.

Few knew the traffickers who captured him weren't there to sell him.

They were paid by the main family to kill him.

So the food they gave him was poisoned.

He didn't dare eat it.

The other children, starving, obediently ate. He did not.

The other children thought he was arrogant, not knowing he couldn't eat…

He was only nine, starving hungry at night, too hungry to sleep…

He once thought he would starve to death.

He even saw a black cat jump into the room at night, staring at him with eerie eyes.

He seemed to have read in a fairy tale that black cats came for the dying, to eat their souls.

He looked into those pupils, terrified from the bottom of his heart.

He didn't want to die. He still had his grandmother…

Just then, the door creaked open, and a small figure slipped in.

She shooed the black cat away and pulled half a steamed bun from her pocket, handing it to him.

She said, "Big brother, stop starving yourself. Eat. When you're dead, there's nothing left…"

He survived the traffickers not only because of her final help but also because of her secret feedings over those three months.

What he owed her was more than just one life!

Alexander clenched his fists tightly.

The psychiatric hospital director approached, bowing apologetically. "Mr. Vance, I'm sorry. Miss Watson said she understood she couldn't rely on a man, she had to rely on herself… Then she went to sleep quietly. We didn't dare relax our vigilance, but the night nurse accidentally dozed off. When she opened her eyes, the patient was gone. The window was open, the curtains blowing in the wind…"

The director paused, noticing Alexander's livid expression, and didn't dare continue.

Alexander's jaw was tight. He muttered to himself, "Was I wrong?"

Evelyn knew he was consumed by deep guilt.

She didn't know how to comfort him…

She instinctively said, "You weren't wrong. I once saved a big brother too…"

Alexander looked at her. "What big brother?"
###Chapter 235

Alexander Vance understood Evelyn Ross was comforting him.

He followed her lead and changed the subject.

Evelyn spoke softly. "I was trafficked as a child too."

She paused. "I was locked up with an older brother back then…"

Trafficked?

Alexander was taken aback. "How old were you?"

Sophia Watson on the hospital bed suddenly started coughing violently.

Her body twisted and struggled.

Doctors rushed over immediately. "Not good! Blood is entering the trachea!"

"Arrange for a thoracotomy immediately!"

Sophia Watson received her fifth critical condition notice.

Alexander's attention was instantly captured.

He stared intently toward the hospital bed.

Evelyn also set aside her casual chatting mood.

Three hours.

Five hours.

Sophia Watson was finally saved once more.

When the doctors announced she was temporarily out of danger.

Evelyn let out a long sigh of relief.

She turned to look at Alexander.

The man's tightly clenched fist finally relaxed slowly.

Evelyn gently patted the back of his hand.

Alexander gave a bitter smile.

Sophia Watson had multiple fractures throughout her body.

Multiple internal organ ruptures.

After being wheeled out of the operating room.

She seemed to gain some consciousness.

Even while unconscious.

She still waved her arms and called out. "Alexander…"

Her thrashing made it impossible for medical staff to administer IV.

The doctor held down her hand.

She continued to struggle and call out. "Big brother…"

Trying hard to grab onto something.

"Otherwise, the life we just saved won't be preserved!"

Alexander remained silent for a long time.

Finally, he stepped forward. "I'm here."

The restless Sophia Watson immediately quieted down.

She seemed to sense the voice.

Subconsciously grabbing Alexander's hand.

Alexander tried to pull back.

Sophia Watson immediately frowned. "Big brother…"

This address made him recall the past.

Alexander's movement halted.

The doctor looked at him with pleading eyes again.

Alexander looked at Evelyn.

His gaze hesitant and conflicted.

Evelyn clenched her fists.

She didn't speak.

Watching Alexander accompany Sophia Watson into the ICU.

Looking inside through a glass door.

They were only a few meters apart.

But that glass seemed to separate two different worlds.

Doctors and nurses shuttled back and forth.

Checking vital signs every half hour.

Alexander couldn't hear outside sounds inside the ICU.

But Evelyn heard the experts' discussion.

"It's a miracle she was saved!"

"Mainly because Mr. Vance is willing to spend money."

"Her entire body essentially got four blood transfusions."

"One bleeding point was guessed blindly, and we actually got it right."

"But she'll be disabled for life."

"Why would she do this to herself…"

"She came from a mental institution."

"Has severe depression, simply can't get out of it."

"Her only hope for living is Mr. Vance."

"Mr. Vance got married and stopped visiting her, so she attempted suicide."

Others asked curiously. "What's their relationship?"

"Rumored to be lovers. Mr. Vance has been treating her illness for five or six years."

"Always abroad."

"Now he transferred his affection to someone else?"

"Anyone would struggle with a mentally ill girlfriend."

"It's normal to transfer affection."

"But it still seems somewhat heartless."

"He's the patient's only hope."

"The patient couldn't handle the breakup."

"Mr. Vance publicly announced his marriage long ago."

"Mrs. Vance wasn't happy about it."

"Mr. Vance said he wouldn't see her anymore, so she jumped."

"When she first arrived, it was simply unbearable to look at…"

The group chatted as they walked away.

Evelyn tightened her jaw.

Wanting to say something but not knowing how to begin.

She understood Alexander.

His decisive choice of her moved her deeply.

But seeing Sophia Watson lying on the hospital bed with her own eyes.

Even she felt her heart race.

It was a human life.

Yesterday, they were arguing vividly.

Today, her life hung by a thread.

But were childhood promises really that important?

Should Alexander be dragged down by promises for life?

Evelyn thought of that older brother.

She had saved him.

He promised he would come marry her.

Take her to his home.

Although he never came all these years.

That sentence became her hope for survival.

Growing up, she learned relying on others isn't as good as relying on oneself.

She was doing well now.

Completely moved on.

If she weren't strong enough.

She might not have made it through.

She lowered her head in quiet contemplation.

"What are you thinking about?"

Alexander's voice suddenly sounded.

Evelyn looked up.

The man stood before her, having arrived at some point.

He looked tired, powerless in his eyes.

Evelyn didn't want to pressure him.

Didn't mention "you said yesterday you wouldn't see her again."

She was never a tragic female lead.

Evelyn blinked and suddenly spoke. "Thinking about the little brother I saved when I was young."

Alexander sat down beside her. "What about him?"

Evelyn smiled. "He said he would marry me, and even gave a betrothal gift."

"But now I don't need it anymore. Should return the betrothal gift to him."

Alexander paused slightly. "What betrothal gift?"
###Chapter 236

Evelyn blinked.

"When I was little, I didn't understand. Now this ring seems very valuable."

She pulled the jade thumb ring from her pocket.

"It might be someone's family heirloom."

As a child, she hadn't known the ring's worth.

Afraid Linda would discard or sell it, she had kept it hidden.

Only upon returning home did she notice its exceptional quality.

A sharp alarm blared from the heart monitor in the hospital room.

Alexander Vance bolted up and rushed inside.

Doctors and nurses swiftly responded, performing emergency measures on Sophia Watson.

Alexander stood by anxiously, watching.

Evelyn held the ring, observing him quietly for a long time.

She rose and left the hospital.

Staying felt too awkward.

She wandered the streets aimlessly.

Eventually, she found herself outside a courier station.

Evelyn paused.

She looked down at the jade ring in her hand.

She entered the station.

The clerk recognized her, surprised. "Boss, what brings you here?"

Evelyn owned several courier companies but rarely visited.

"I need to mail a package."

She asked the clerk for a box and packing materials.

She wrapped the ring meticulously, layer upon layer.

"Do you have paper and a pen?"

The clerk provided them.

Evelyn wrote a brief note:

"Dear Big Brother,

I hope this isn't an intrusion.

I don't know if you remember me. Or if you've looked for me over the years.

I'm writing to say you don't need to search for me anymore.

Please don't feel obligated by childhood promises.

I'm married now, very happily. My life is stable and secure. No need to worry.

This jade ring seems precious, likely important to you. I'm returning it."

She wrote because of Alexander.

Putting herself in his shoes, she wondered if her childhood friend might also be conflicted if he had found someone.

She wanted to clarify her situation and close this chapter.

Yet, finishing the letter, she felt a pang of reluctance.

During her darkest childhood moments, beaten by Linda and feeling hopeless, the thought of her big brother coming for her had kept her going...

Evelyn thought for a moment, then added:

"By the way, I had no name as a child. Now I do.

I'm Evelyn Ross. My number is 183xxxxxxxx.

If you ever visit San Francisco, I'd love to have dinner."

She placed the note and the ring in the box, cushioning them with foam.

She handed it to the clerk. "Please mail this."

The clerk took it. "What's the destination address?"

Evelyn smiled. "76 Sunset Road, Sunset Village, Cloud Town."

Saying the address aloud made her reconsider.

How significant could a number 76 in a village be?

The clerk asked, "Recipient name?"

Evelyn frowned, thinking.

Her big brother never gave his name, only said he was from that family, the fifth son.

What was the surname? He might have mentioned it, but she couldn't recall.

She suddenly remembered his words: "My second grandfather says I'm stubborn. Calls me Stubborn Fifth."

"Write 'Stubborn Fifth.'"

The clerk's expression remained neutral.

Evelyn was curious. "The name doesn't seem odd to you?"

The clerk shrugged. "It's pretty normal. You haven't been in delivery lately. Recipient names get wild. Once had a 'My Daddy.' Had to ask, 'Are you My Daddy?' on calls. Delivering that package made me feel like I lost a generation!"

Evelyn chuckled.

After a moment, she added, "Please deliver it personally. The ring is fragile. Handle it carefully."

Sunset Village felt far in childhood memories, but it was only a five or six-hour drive.

Uncertain of the ring's value, sending a trusted person felt safer.

The clerk nodded. "Sure thing. I'll go now. Guarantee it reaches the recipient directly."

"Thank you."

Watching the clerk drive away, Evelyn didn't return to the hospital.

She texted Alexander and went back to the Vance residence.

Entering, she saw Elder Howard Vance saying goodbye to Eleanor Vance.

Elder Howard seemed reluctant. "Take care, sister-in-law. I'll see you at the ancestral ceremony for the New Year."

Eleanor smiled warmly. "Of course. I'll be there."

Noticing Evelyn, Eleanor asked, "Where's that rascal? He promised to see his second grandfather off today."

Evelyn smiled gently. "He might be held up with something."

Eleanor huffed. "What could be more important than family?"

Elder Howard intervened. "Don't scold Alexander, sister-in-law. Running the Vance Group keeps him busy. He's a good boy, wouldn't miss this intentionally. We're family. We can see each other anytime."

He turned and got into the car.

Evelyn steadied Eleanor.

Eleanor waved. "Drive safely. No rush."

"Understood."

Elder Howard looked at Robert Vance and Richard Evans, his expression stern. "Behave yourselves."

Robert pressed his lips together, silent.

Richard smiled. "We will, Second Grandfather. Have a safe trip."

He had hoped to speak with Alexander again, having watched him grow up.

He came worried about Eleanor's health and initially misjudged Alexander and Evelyn due to Richard.

He wondered if the boy was upset and avoiding him.

Elder Howard muttered under his breath, "That stubborn fifth child."
###Chapter 237

Evelyn Ross looked up sharply.

She thought she heard something.

Elder Howard Vance waved them off.

The Vance Residence.

…

Even someone as unflappable as Eleanor was stunned by this news.

Eleanor could easily verify this, so Evelyn told the truth. "She almost died. She's still in critical condition."

Eleanor's brow was deeply furrowed. She was silent for a long while. "How could this happen? What could possibly be so unbearable?"

Both Evelyn and Eleanor were strong-willed women.

Neither noticed Richard Evans stepping out from around a corner.

He stared at their retreating backs, his eyes gleaming.

Richard expected his daughter to be shocked by the major news.

Cecilia said, "I had people watching Sophia Watson since yesterday. Uncle Alexander is at the hospital with her right now. 
###Chapter 238

I bribed a nurse. I got the doctors' chat logs."

Richard's eyes lit up. "This is our big chance, isn't it?"

Cecilia nodded. "I'll send the evidence to you. You know what to do next, right?"

"Absolutely!"

Richard was overjoyed.

He would make this a huge public scandal.

Even if Alexander tried to cover it up, the court of public opinion would crush him.

Alexander had been dominating him since returning to the country.

His every counterattack had been thwarted by Evelyn.

That woman came from nothing, but she was capable.

If he could make them divorce, and force Alexander to marry that mentally ill woman…

He would lose a powerful ally and gain a burden.

Then, Richard would finally be able to compete with Alexander.

Alexander might even end up without an heir.

It had been maliciously edited, completely twisting the original meaning.

Richard smirked and posted it directly online.

Defending her grandson felt like a betrayal of her granddaughter-in-law.

She escorted the elderly woman back to her room.

As she left, she received a message from James Klein.

[Boss, check out this post. What's going on?]

Evelyn opened it.

It was an edited audio recording of doctors, completely altering the meaning.

It made it seem like Alexander had callously abandoned Sophia because of her illness and gotten together with Evelyn.

The comments were full of vitriol.

Evelyn skimmed a few.

They mostly called Alexander a scumbag and her a homewrecker.

She didn't read further and replied to James.

[Suppress the influence influence (rèdù - heat/hotness) - Assuming this means 'heat' as in 'public attention' or 'trending'. Suppress the trending status.]

James replied quickly.

[It's been suppressed. Thomas handled it. Probably on Alexander's orders. Boss, what's going on with Alexander? Is he still at the hospital with that woman? Is he trying to have his cake and eat it too?]

Evelyn frowned.

It included her, James, Thomas Watson, and Alexander Vance.

Evelyn was puzzled.

Evelyn answered reluctantly.

Alexander did not join.

Thomas connected.

Before Evelyn could speak, James started ranting.

"Thomas, your boss needs to give my boss an explanation today! Don't think you can smooth this over just because my boss is reasonable! What is he thinking? Why is he at the hospital with that woman? What is she to him?"

Thomas sighed. "Ms. Ross, please don't misunderstand the boss. He feels responsible for Sophia Watson. His true feelings are for you."

James sneered. "True feelings? Then how could he stand by and let my boss be called a mistress? How could he still be at the hospital right now if his feelings are true?"

Thomas said, "Ms. Watson saved the boss's life once. He feels he has no choice."

"How did she save his life? Let's hear it. I want to know just how 'no choice' he really is!"
James Klein's voice was cold and hard.
"If we don't get an explanation today, this isn't over. Does the Vance Group think it can just step on people?"

Thomas Watson sighed.
"That was from when they were kids. The boss didn't go into detail. I pieced it together from things Miss Watson said."

James swore more fiercely.
"Childhood stuff is even more ridiculous! Are you certain that Miss Watson is really his savior? Don't tell me he misidentified her again!"

Thomas shook his head.
"Certain. The token was sold by Miss Watson's mother. But we administered a lie detector test. She was telling the truth."

"Is that Miss Watson really mentally ill?"

"Yes. Experts from here and abroad consulted. She has severe claustrophobia and autism. The boss is her only reason to keep living..."

Thomas looked at them, trying to explain.
"Miss Ross, I know you're angry with the boss right now. But he never intended to marry Miss Watson. It was all her one-sided desire! All these years, he's only been helping her get treatment. But every doctor says her illness is too severe..."

Thomas sighed again.
"I've been watching her all these years. Hoping she'd make a mistake. Just one misstep, and I could convince the boss to let her go. But she never did. The only time she overstepped was impersonating Mrs. Vance at the birthday gala. She later explained she was afraid of causing trouble for the boss. After that, she stayed quietly in the psychiatric hospital..."

James felt frustrated.
"So innocent? Then what? Is Alexander supposed to watch over her every time she tries to kill herself? What's the point of my boss being married then?"

Thomas gave a bitter smile.
"I think that's why the boss chose Miss Ross and gave up on Miss Watson. But she did save his life. Can we really stand by and watch her die?"

James fell silent.

As a man, he understood the weight of responsibility.

If Alexander was cruel to Sophia Watson today, would he do the same to Evelyn Ross tomorrow?

Both men were quiet.

Evelyn lowered her eyes and spoke directly.
"The public opinion surge seems odd. Look into it."

Thomas responded immediately.
"We already did. It was the eldest branch's doing."

"Suppress it. Don't let it escalate."

Thomas nodded.
"Understood."

James scoffed.
"The heat is suppressed, but now someone is live streaming from the hospital!"

After hanging up, Evelyn opened the live stream link James sent.

A reporter had started the stream.
Though blocked by Vance family bodyguards, the hospital room was faintly visible.

Sophia Watson clutched Alexander's hand desperately, refusing to let him leave.

Only after her body could handle a heavy sedative did she finally calm down.

Alexander was finally able to break free.

The reporter couldn't get close for an interview.
He spoke into the camera instead.
"Her only salvation is him. Yet Mr. Vance abandons her cruelly. How is that different from pushing her to her death? How can Mr. Vance be so heartless?"

"This is Gossip Daily. We will continue to follow this story."

Alexander's背影 at Sophia's bedside filled her vision.

That背影 had once been as reliable as a mountain.
Now it seemed on the verge of collapse.

Night deepened.

Sophia's condition stabilized. She was no longer in mortal danger.

The doctor's words echoed in his ears.
"Miss Watson's will to live is very weak. If you truly won't accompany her going forward, there's no need for further resuscitation. She will undoubtedly attempt suicide a second time... Even if we monitor her constantly, she is determined to die. She'll refuse food and water. What can we do? She has a hundred ways to end her life. If we are even slightly negligent... Mr. Vance, I'm sorry. I cannot guarantee the patient's safety."

He got out of the car.

The老夫人's courtyard was very quiet.

"Let's divorce."

Evelyn's voice was very soft.

...

He grew more apprehensive the further he drove.

Because No. 76 was no ordinary villager's home.
An imposing large-tiled house occupied an entire street!

It was clearly the ancestral home of a wealthy, prominent family.

The driver nervously pulled up to the gate and knocked.

Someone came to answer the door.

The driver asked, "Excuse me, is there someone here called Stubborn Fifth?"

The servant frowned.
"Stubborn Fifth? Never heard of him! Go away, go away!"

This was the老板's mail. He had promised to deliver it.
The driver refused to leave. He shouted inside, "Stubborn Fifth! Stubborn Fifth! You have a delivery!"

Elder Howard Vance, who had just entered the courtyard, heard the commotion.
Puzzled, he walked over and asked with a frown, "Whose delivery?"

The servant said, "This delivery driver says it's for Stubborn Fifth. We don't have anyone by that name! I told him it's a mistake, but he won't leave!"

As soon as the servant finished, Elder Howard's eyes widened in disbelief.
"Who did you say?"

"Stubborn Fifth!"

Elder Howard immediately became excited.
He stepped forward to the delivery driver. "Yes, yes! We have that person here!"

Thatguy Alexander, after being rescued and returning home, used to run to the gate every day waiting for letters.

Later, when he went abroad, he repeatedly told him.
If any letters arrived, he must be told.

Even after going abroad, he would call every so often asking if any mail had come...

It's just been these last five years he stopped calling.

He took the mail and said to the driver, "Make another trip. Deliver this mail to Alexander personally!"

Remembering Alexander's repeated words from years ago, he added, "You must place it directly into his hands!"

The delivery driver beside him, thinking of his promise to the老板 to deliver the letter directly to the recipient, immediately said, "I'll go with you!"
###Chapter 239

She Was Leaving Him

Vance Mansion.

Evelyn's words did not surprise Alexander.

She had always been free-spirited.

But his heart clenched painfully.

He opened his mouth.

He wanted to refuse.

He wanted to ask for more time.

He wanted to say he would find a solution.

The words never came.

Evelyn stood up.

She looked at him with determination. "I'm not pressuring you. This is the only way."

Alexander's jaw tightened.

His fists clenched.

"Evelyn, I—"

"You chose me. I know." Her voice was calm. "But you can't just let her die."

Alexander frowned. "I can."

"For now." Evelyn met his gaze. "What about later?"

Alexander froze.

"You'll feel guilty. That guilt will haunt you forever. Alexander, I don't want that for you."

The room was dim.

Evelyn couldn't see his expression clearly.

Her shoulders slumped slightly.

She looked exhausted.

"Alexander, I know how that feels."

For twenty-two years.

She had lived with guilt.

Believing her existence ruined William and Sophia's marriage.

She served the Ashcroft family for over a decade.

Even after leaving.

The weight never lifted.

Every time she faced Isabella.

She told herself she wasn't wrong.

But she still felt ashamed.

It was helpless remorse.

Some things are predetermined.

Like life and death.

Death is final.

No amount of regret can change it.

Alexander appeared cold.

But he valued loyalty deeply.

Sophia Watson saved his life.

If he abandoned her.

He would live with guilt forever.

Evelyn refused to let that happen.

Alexander stepped forward.

His voice trembled. "But losing you scares me more."

Evelyn looked down.

She smiled faintly. "Alexander, be rational. This is best for both of us."

"I—"

Evelyn suddenly hugged his waist.

Alexander's words stuck in his throat.

Evelyn chuckled softly. "Alexander, don't look so defeated. I prefer your confident CEO self. Besides…"

Her voice muffled against his shirt. "This marriage felt forced from the start. Someone might be pulling strings behind the scenes. If we divorce, maybe they'll show themselves."

She spoke lightly.

Then she straightened up.

She patted his shoulder.

"Alright. City Hall tomorrow. Don't be late."

She turned to leave.

But his hand caught her wrist tightly.

Evelyn didn't look back.

She wasn't one for drama.

But seeing Alexander with Sophia Watson.

Hearing her call him brother.

It stirred jealousy.

Love is selfish by nature.

They stood僵持着.

Time seemed to stretch.

Finally, Alexander released her.

Evelyn walked away quickly.

She entered the room next door.

As the door closed.

Tears fell.

One shouldn't be too greedy.

Someone like her, who had suffered so much.

Now had Alexander.

And Sophia as a mother.

It figured.

Fate gives.

And fate takes away.

Evelyn collapsed onto the bed.

She buried her face in the quilt.

If she had known this would happen.

She should have guarded her heart.

She didn't regret proposing the divorce.

Because she knew.

If she didn't say it.

He eventually would.

This was better.

She wasn't being abandoned.

She was leaving him.

...

Next door.

Alexander stood frozen by the door.

Her resolute背影 left an emptiness in his chest.

He tugged at his tie irritably.

He walked to the balcony.

The temperature had dropped.

San Francisco was below freezing in winter.

He took off his jacket.

Standing there in just a shirt.

He looked towards Evelyn's room.

Her lights were off.

Maybe she was asleep.

Or maybe she was lying awake like him.

Alexander lit another cigarette.

The cold made his hands tremble slightly.

After lighting it.

He took a deep drag.

The strong smoke made him cough.

His eyes stung.

Something wet landed on his hand.

Alexander glanced down.

He wiped it away quietly.

...

Morning came.

The sky lightened.

Evelyn opened her eyes.

Unsure if she had slept at all.

She felt groggy all night.

She thought she heard coughing next door.

The soundproofing in Eleanor's wing was excellent.

She knew it was her imagination.

She got up and opened the door.

Alexander was also leaving his room.

Their eyes met.

He looked tired.

After a moment.

Evelyn smiled. "Morning."

Alexander's lips moved.

He spoke with difficulty. "Morning."

Neither had an appetite for breakfast.

Evelyn said directly, "Let's go."

Alexander opened his mouth.

But said nothing.

He turned and walked ahead.

After they left.

Fiona emerged from behind.

She watched them with concern.

She rushed into Eleanor's room. "Madam, something's wrong with Mr. Vance and Mrs. Vance. Mrs. Vance took their marriage certificate. Are they going to get a divorce?"

Eleanor clutched her chest.

This time, she didn't play coy.

She just sighed. "Fiona, I can't force Evelyn. My grandson is the one who wronged her..."

Fiona's eyes reddened. "Such a wonderful wife... Mr. Vance will regret this for life!"

Eleanor felt even worse.

"Enough. Let the young people handle their own affairs."

She turned over and lay down.

Tears streamed from the old woman's eyes.

She couldn't help muttering, "That foolish boy!"
