Evelyn followed Alexander to the parking lot.

They got into the car.

The driver headed for the main gate.

Outside.

A delivery person from Evelyn's team arrived at the same time as a courier from the Vance family.
###Chapter 240

Don't Get a Divorce!

As Alexander Vance's car drove out of the estate gates, the Vance family's private car and a delivery van arrived one after the other.

Alexander cast an indifferent glance at the family vehicle, paying it no mind.

Elder Howard Vance often sent people to pay respects to Eleanor Vance or deliver items, always heading directly to her residence.

As for the delivery van…

With over a hundred people living on the estate, it was likely a package for some staff member or butler.

His car passed the two vehicles…

After he had driven away, the door of the family car opened.

The driver jumped out, looking in the direction Alexander had left, and urgently asked the guard, "Was that Mr. Vance who just left?"

The guard nodded. "Yes."

The driver immediately grew anxious. "I'm here to deliver an important document to him. Where is Mr. Vance going?"

The guard shrugged. "How would I know the master's business!"

Realizing he had asked the wrong person, the driver quickly got back in the car, drove into the estate, and headed straight for Eleanor Vance's residence.

The delivery van tried to follow but was stopped by the guard. "Whose delivery is this? You can't enter empty-handed."

The delivery person: "…"

He was ruthlessly stopped at the gate!

After a moment's hesitation, he sent a message to his boss: [Boss, I delivered your package to Sunset Village first, but that Mr. Stubborn Fifth is in San Francisco. I followed the driver here to San Francisco. This is a wealthy family's estate; they won't let me in.]

Evelyn Ross quickly replied: [Just come back. No need to wait.]

Since she had left her contact information, her eldest brother should contact her once he saw it… right?

Evelyn was momentarily lost in thought.

But she quickly dismissed the idea.

She glanced at Alexander.

The man's expression was cold and stern, remaining silent the entire way.

The car soon arrived at City Hall.

Evelyn got out first. "It's open. Shall we go in?"

Alexander didn't move.

Evelyn sighed softly, holding the car door open as she quietly watched him.

They were at a stalemate for nearly a minute before Alexander slowly got out of the car.

Evelyn walked towards City Hall.

After taking two steps, she sensed something was wrong.

Turning back, she saw Alexander looking up at the City Hall building with an unfamiliar gaze.

Of course.

He had been unconscious when they got their marriage certificate and had no memory of this place.

She walked over to Alexander.

Then she heard the man say, "We can't get a divorce today."

Evelyn was stunned. "Why?"

Was Alexander willing to let Sophia Watson die rather than separate from her?

As she was thinking this, the man said in a low voice, "I forgot to bring the marriage certificate."

Evelyn: !

She glanced at Alexander and coughed lightly. "Then let's go home and get it."

Alexander: "I have a meeting this morning."

Evelyn: "Then this afternoon."

"Afternoon, I have a business engagement."

Evelyn: ?

She looked at him with an amused smile. "Mr. Vance, do you know what you look like right now?"

Alexander felt somewhat uncomfortable under her gaze, averting his eyes guiltily. "What?"

"Like a child throwing a tantrum for candy!"

Alexander stared at her. "If I really threw a tantrum, would you not get a divorce?"

"No."

Evelyn sighed helplessly. "Marriage isn't a game, Alexander. If we don't get divorced, when do you plan to resolve the issue with Sophia Watson? Can I wait a month? Three months? But can you make me wait three or five years?"

Alexander's jaw tightened.

If her illness could be cured, it would have been cured in the past five years…

And if he cut ties with her before she recovered, Sophia Watson would commit suicide…

Alexander was caught in a dilemma.

In the short term, he either had to harden his heart and watch her die, or there was no solution.

But he couldn't just watch his savior die because of him.

He looked at Evelyn again.

A carefree woman like her, if not bound by this absurd marriage, would have long since left the Vance family.

If they divorced, she might truly never return…

This thought made his heart flutter with panic.

He said in a low voice, "Evelyn, I really don't have time today. Let's come back tomorrow, alright?"

Evelyn looked at him quietly and finally nodded. "Alright."

Alexander offered her the car. "You go home first. I'm going to the company…"

But Evelyn shook her head. "In my current status, it's not appropriate for me to return to the Vance family."

Alexander was stunned, his heart growing even more panicked.

He clenched his fists, wanting to say something but not knowing how to phrase it.

In the end, he just responded and got into the car.

His retreating figure carried a hint of embarrassment.

Evelyn watched the car drive away, lowered her eyes, and sighed softly.

She wanted to say there was no need to be so conflicted.

If they were truly meant to be, this deadlock would eventually be broken.

Perhaps in a year or so, Sophia Watson's illness would be cured?

Or maybe…

She didn't want to think about the other possibilities.

Evelyn took a taxi back to the Ashcroft residence.

As soon as she entered the living room, she heard William Ashcroft's voice. "If you're worried, why not call and ask?"

Sophia Ashcroft immediately refused. "That would just put pressure on Evelyn."

Hearing this, Evelyn understood they had seen the news.

These days, reporters were everywhere.

Keeping something confidential was truly difficult.

She pushed the door open and entered.

Sophia said in surprise, "Evelyn, you…"

"Mom, I'm planning to get a divorce." Evelyn didn't hide it.

William immediately objected. "Nonsense! How can you get a divorce? Husband and wife should face difficulties together!"

Evelyn remained silent.

William looked somewhat embarrassed and turned to Sophia. "Sophia, talk some sense into her."

But Sophia said, "Alright. This home will always welcome you back!"

William was stunned. "Sophia, you…"

Sophia looked at him. "Some things can be faced together, but when a third party is involved, someone always has to step back. I don't want Evelyn to get hurt in this love triangle. I respect her choice. Evelyn, the upstairs room isn't renovated yet. Sleep with me tonight."

William was momentarily dazed upon hearing this.

Then he realized that Mrs. Ashcroft had always been so decisive.

She had always been like this…

As for why she could tolerate Linda Lowell's existence.

Of course, it was because… she didn't love him.

A trace of self-mockery flashed in William's eyes.

Neither Evelyn nor Sophia noticed his unusual expression.

Sophia took Evelyn's hand and went upstairs. "We mother and daughter have never slept together before. It's a good chance to talk tonight."

"Alright."

Evelyn suddenly said, "Mom, I want to eat the tomato and egg noodles you make for lunch."

Sophia was taken aback and laughed. "Silly child, the noodles I make aren't tasty…"

When she was younger, she had also cooked, wanting to be a good wife and mother.

But the noodles she made were disliked by Isabella.

Yet Evelyn had always looked on with envy.

Such a simple thing, Linda had never done for her.

She really wanted to taste Sophia's cooking.

She insisted stubbornly, "But I want to eat it."

Sophia paused. "…Alright."

Mother and daughter spent the day at the Ashcroft residence.

At noon, they ate the longed-for noodles.

At night, they shared a bed.

Between Evelyn and Sophia, it was as if those twenty-two years apart had never happened.

They were as intimate as any ordinary mother and daughter.

Evelyn understood that Sophia was being considerate of her feelings.

But the intimacy between them couldn't be faked.

Late at night, she took out her phone.

Suddenly, she noticed an unread text message.

Nowadays was the digital age.

Most people contacted each other through WhatsApp or other means, rarely sending text messages.

Evelyn's inbox was mostly filled with ads and spam.

Thus, this text message stood out.

A strange number sent a short sentence: [Do not divorce Alexander Vance.]

So mysterious…

It must be the person who had arranged her marriage to Alexander!

Evelyn got up and walked to the balcony, dialing the number back.
###Chapter 241

Your Package Has Arrived!

The call connected and was immediately hung up.

A message popped up on the screen: [Don't call. The child just fell asleep.]

Evelyn froze.

A child?

She hadn't anticipated this at all.

She had assumed the schemer was a mastermind, but they had a child with them?

An image of a flustered mother instantly formed in her mind...

She replied: [Who are you?]

The response was immediate: [Evelyn, I'm doing this for your own good. You must not get a divorce! Only Alexander Vance can protect you!]

Evelyn frowned.

Protect?

Over the years in San Francisco, despite Linda Lowell's attempts to suppress her, she was far from needing protection.

She looked down and typed: [I don't need protection.]

The reply came urgently: [Yes, you do. You just don't know it yet. Evelyn, trust me. I would never harm you! My intentions are only good!]

Good intentions...

Evelyn pondered for a moment.

Marrying Alexander was with Eleanor Vance's consent and hadn't caused any losses.

For an ordinary person, marrying into a top-tier family like the Vances would indeed be considered incredibly fortunate.

She pressed again: [Tell me who you are.]

The response: [I can't.]

Evelyn applied more pressure: [Then how did you know I'm Sophia Ashcroft's daughter? If you knew, why didn't you tell me sooner?]

Years ago, this person had photoshopped images to inform Eleanor, ensuring she remembered those words.

But how did they know something even she herself wasn't aware of?

The reply came: [The later you know, the safer you are.]

Evelyn: ?

She was utterly confused, finding it absurd.

She replied directly: [Without a reasonable explanation, I will get divorced tomorrow.]

The response: [What will it take for you to believe me?]

Evelyn: "..." They were the one who orchestrated her marriage without consent, yet now they sounded more wronged and innocent than her!

She felt like the villain here!

Evelyn continued her questioning: [How did you obtain the marriage certificate for me that day?]

On the date of the marriage certificate, she was in the lab and couldn't possibly have gone to City Hall.

Moreover, she later had that extensive conversation with Eleanor...

The person who obtained the certificate couldn't possibly have been her!

But what about the photo?

Could modern makeup really be that convincing?

The more Evelyn thought, the more perplexed she became.

Another message arrived: [It's too complicated to explain via text. But please, you must believe me. I would never harm you!]

That eerie feeling again.

The words "please" indicated the other person's composure had shattered.

Evelyn seized the opportunity to press further: [I didn't have the marriage certificate today, but Alexander and I have an appointment at City Hall for divorce tomorrow.]

The response: [Why won't you believe me? Someone like me would never harm anyone... Evelyn, you must not get divorced. You absolutely must not!]

Evelyn stared at her phone. [I won't believe you without a clear explanation.]

The other side fell silent, seemingly struggling.

Five minutes later, a reply finally came: [I will come to see you tomorrow morning. Once you see me, you will understand!]

Evelyn frowned.

Tomorrow morning...

They were anxious but unwilling to come overnight, which meant they weren't in San Francisco?

After a moment's thought, she replied: [What time? I have plans at noon.]

She had that farewell lunch with the handsome older man.

The reply was immediate: [I'll be there by 11. I'll contact you then. Please don't go for the divorce in the morning! You must wait for me!]

Evelyn replied: [Okay.]

She put down her phone but felt wide awake.

Standing on the balcony, gazing into the distance, she felt a truth was approaching... Tomorrow might unravel the mystery of the marriage certificate, but what lay behind it all?

A faint sound came from behind.

Turning, she saw Sophia getting up, holding a shawl, and walking over to gently drape it over her shoulders.

Warmth instantly enveloped her, even her heart feeling slightly warmer.

Evelyn's expression softened. "Mom, why are you still up?"

"First time sharing a bed with my daughter, feeling a bit excited." Sophia looked at her. "Evelyn, do you really want to go through with this divorce?"

Evelyn was silent for a moment. "No, I don't."

Sophia hadn't expected such a direct answer. She stepped forward, wrapped an arm around Evelyn's shoulders, and gently rested Evelyn's head against her own shoulder.

She patted Evelyn's back softly, her voice gentle. "Evelyn, love has its sweetness and its bitterness. It's okay to cry if you want to. Mom won't laugh at you."

Evelyn's nose tingled with emotion.

Feelings she had suppressed for so long surged from her chest, impossible to hold back any longer.

She was slightly startled, wanting to say something, but a sob escaped instead.

Large teardrops rolled down.

She didn't know if they were for the love that had ended before it began or for the grievances of the past twenty-two years...

She didn't notice the black Bentley parked on the street outside the courtyard.

Alexander sat silently in the back seat.

The driver didn't dare to breathe too loudly.

Mr. Vance's mood was clearly off today. He had handled a week's worth of work at the company.

He hadn't gone home in the afternoon to deal with international affairs but had stayed at Vance Group.

After work, he hadn't returned to the Vance estate but had come here.

Yet he just sat there silently, staring at the Ashcroft residence...

Just as the driver was about to suffocate, Alexander finally spoke. "Let's go home."

The driver breathed a sigh of relief.

Starting the car, he stole a glance at Alexander through the rearview mirror and saw his boss with his eyes closed, resting.

For some reason, the usually formidable boss now evoked a faint sense of pity...

The car drove back to the Vance estate.

After getting out, Alexander walked silently towards Eleanor's courtyard.

As he approached, he saw the Vance family driver squatting by the door, chin in hand, looking bored.

Seeing Alexander, he jumped up abruptly. "Mr. Vance, you're finally back! There's a package for you, delivered to the main house!"

###Chapter 242

Alexander Vance spent a sleepless night.

He sat in his study with a document spread open before him.

He couldn't focus on a single word.

The sky outside gradually lightened.

He rubbed his temples and stood up, walking to the window.

Now was not the time.

He and Evelyn Ross had agreed to finalize their divorce today.

Questions flew at her one after another.

Sophia's mother suddenly dropped to her knees, kowtowing heavily.

The reporters' cameras immediately focused on the scene.

Evelyn watched it all with a cold expression.

She knew exactly who was behind this—the main branch of the Vance family.

At the Vance family estate.

"What did you say?"
###Chapter 243

Stubborn Fifth!

Alexander Vance strode quickly toward the car door.

He stared down at his phone screen.

A live stream was playing, showing the scene outside the Ross family home.

Sophia Watson's mother had backed Evelyn Ross into a corner!

Alexander was frantic with worry.

He didn't even hear the driver from the Vance family estate, starting the car immediately.

Reporters were swarming Evelyn.

"Mrs. Vance, her mother is begging you like this. Do you really have no reaction at all?"

"A life is at stake. How can you be so cold?"

"Are you and Mr. Vance really going to stand by and let Miss Watson die?"

"Since he promised to marry her, why did Mr. Vance go back on his word? Was it because you interfered? Mrs. Vance, don't you feel any guilt? Aren't you afraid in the dead of night?"

The reporters' questions grew increasingly harsh.

Live stream comments scrolled wildly:

—Oh my god! A mistress taking the wife's place?

—A promise is a promise. Abandoning her when she's sick is just evil!

—This is pure betrayal!

—If Miss Watson knew how the person she saved would treat her, would she have even bothered?

—A real-life version of the farmer and the snake!

—The worst part is this Mrs. Vance. Not saying a word. Playing dead?

…

Alexander slammed his foot on the accelerator.

He knew Evelyn was strong, but these comments still enraged him.

He ran red lights, speeding down the road.

He would personally clear Evelyn's name!

As he turned a corner, Evelyn's cool voice came through the live stream.

"Since everyone is so concerned with our private affairs, I'll make things clear today."

Alexander frowned deeply.

Evelyn never liked to explain herself. She must have been pushed to her limit.

"Did any of you play house when you were children?"

The reporters were taken aback. One answered, "Yes."

"Did you tell a girl you would marry her when you grew up? Did you actually follow through?"

The reporters fell silent.

A reporter arranged by the main branch quickly retorted, "It's not the same! Miss Watson saved Mr. Vance's life!"

Evelyn looked at the person. "So she developed depression because she saved Mr. Vance?"

The reporter was speechless.

Evelyn lowered her gaze calmly. "Five years ago, when Mr. Vance found Sophia Watson, she was being beaten by her parents. They were about to sell her to an old man as a wife. Mrs. Watson, you didn't love your daughter so much back then."

Mrs. Watson's face changed. She cried out, "I just wanted her to get married! I didn't want her dead!"

"Do you know how her illness started?"

Evelyn scanned the crowd. "You only care about whether Mr. Vance abandoned her. But have you considered the cause? Her parents' abuse drove her to despair! Mr. Vance was merely her medicine! Since when do we blame the medicine for a death, instead of the actual perpetrator?"

The reporters fell silent again.

Evelyn took a step forward. "Sophia Watson developed severe depression five years ago. If she had been married off, she would either be living in a daze or would have taken her own life long ago! She wouldn't be as she is now, rationally stating that she'll die without Mr. Vance!"

Both the reporters and the live chat went quiet.

Evelyn continued. "Over the years, Sophia received treatment both domestically and abroad at great expense. Mr. Vance built a psychiatric hospital for her here. He has been at her beck and call for five years… Can any of you claim you would do the same?"

"Now her condition has worsened, and she's threatening to jump. And you blame the medicine for not being administered in time? Why not ask the actual perpetrators why they hurt her?"

Evelyn turned sharply to Mrs. Watson. "You claim you want what's best for your daughter. Are you even fit to be a mother?"

Mrs. Watson swallowed hard. "I don't care! My daughter is dying because of Mr. Vance! You say I abused her? Where's your proof?"

Evelyn gave a cold laugh. "Everyone on the street knows you abused your child. Ask any elderly neighbor. The police can gather the evidence."

"But you've reminded me. I'm calling the police right now to report you for abusing Sophia Watson to the point of mental illness!"

Evelyn immediately took out her phone and called the police.

Grace Lorenz arrived quickly, filed a report, and took Mrs. Watson away.

Thinking of the money promised by the main branch, Mrs. Watson struggled and shouted, "Mrs. Vance! Are you really going to abandon my daughter? You have to see a rescue through to the end! She saved his life! How can he shift his affections to someone else! You homewrecker!"

The reporters pressed again. "Mrs. Vance, everything you said makes sense. But the medicine can't be stopped now. What are you and Mr. Vance going to do?"

Evelyn looked directly into the camera, her gaze resolute.

Driving, Alexander suddenly felt a sense of foreboding. He pulled the car over sharply.

After the screeching halt, he heard Evelyn's cool voice.

"Since everyone is here, I'll make a clarification. I am not a mistress. Mr. Vance and I fell in love freely. He never had a romantic relationship with Sophia Watson. He only wanted to help her get treatment. As for the medicine issue…"

Evelyn stared straight into the lens, her peach-blossom eyes filled with determination.

"Don't worry. The medicine won't stop, because Mr. Vance has chosen to divorce me."

Alexander's heart clenched violently. He could barely breathe.

She said it…

She said it in front of all the reporters.

Alexander punched the steering wheel.

She had preserved his reputation. Made everyone think he was a good man…

But he never wanted to be the good man!

Alexander watched as the reporters left, satisfied. The comments began praising them…

The live stream ended. He received her text: [City Hall, 2 PM. Bring all your documents this time.]

Alexander stared at the message, his eyes red. Finally, he replied: [Okay.]

He didn't remember driving home.

As he parked, the driver from the Vance estate was waiting. "Mr. Vance, why didn't you let me finish?"

The driver wiped his sweat and sighed. "That package… The old master said I must tell you. It's the package you've been waiting for most. The one you've been anticipating. It was sent to Stubborn Fifth!"

Stubborn Fifth…

Alexander's head snapped up. "You said… who?"
###Chapter 244

Stubborn Fifth.

The name struck Alexander Vance's mind like a lightning bolt.

Besides Elder Howard, only one person had ever called him that.

Little Dumpling.

The little girl who had been trafficked.

He had never told her his real name.

Only that he was the fifth child in his family, and that Elder Howard always called him Stubborn Fifth.

But Little Dumpling was Sophia Watson.

Why would Sophia Watson send him a package?

Alexander's sharp gaze turned to the driver. "When did this package arrive? Did Elder Howard forget to give it to me?"

The driver quickly shook his head. "No, it arrived the night before last. The Elder specifically instructed me to rush back overnight and deliver it to you personally. He said you had mentioned this package was extremely important."

The night before last…

Could it have been sent before Sophia jumped?

Alexander's heart sank heavily.

He lowered his eyes, his voice deep. "Understood."

Returning to the study, his eyes fell on the package box.

He walked over and picked it up.

Inside was a box carefully wrapped in foam.

A letter was tucked beside it.

What would Sophia write in the letter?

A suicide note?

A mocking curve touched Alexander's lips as he tossed the letter back onto the desk.

He leaned back in his chair, his gaze sweeping over the marriage certificate and household register on the desk.

During the live stream, Evelyn Ross's words had made her resolve clear.

There was no point in him clinging on.

He picked up the marriage certificate and opened it.

The girl in the photo smiled shyly at the camera, the teardrop mole at the corner of her eye identical to Evelyn's…

Alexander sat up abruptly.

No.

The girl in the photo wasn't her!

Evelyn's eyes were always resolute, never this shy and timid. This woman could mimic Evelyn's appearance, but she couldn't replicate her spirit.

Alexander's jaw tightened.

Even if this woman was an imposter, legally, this marriage certificate was real…

A knock interrupted his thoughts.

He looked up to see Eleanor Vance trembling as she entered, supported by Fiona.

Alexander immediately stood. "Grandmother…"

Eleanor sighed. "You foolish boy. Is my granddaughter-in-law divorcing you?"

Alexander remained silent.

Eleanor continued, "You foolish boy, some things you must know when to hold and when to let go. If you can't resolve the Sophia issue, don't drag my granddaughter-in-law down. What is she to do now? How awkward is her situation?"

Alexander's jaw tightened further.

He hadn't expected even his grandmother, who usually feigned illness to stop the divorce, to withdraw her support…

He opened his mouth, but no words came out.

The internal struggle and pain tore at him like a wild beast, savaging his heart and lungs…

He clenched his fists, his voice hoarse. "Grandmother, in this life, besides you, I have no family. She is the only person I want to hold onto. I don't want to let go…"

Eleanor walked over and gently patted his shoulder. "You foolish boy, I support my granddaughter-in-law's decision. You place too much weight on gratitude. If your lifesaver just dies like that, you'll never move on. This could become an insurmountable obstacle between you and my granddaughter-in-law in the future. It's better to divorce first, set her free, and give yourself time to handle the Sophia matter."

She smiled faintly. "Don't be too pessimistic. If you and my granddaughter-in-law are truly destined, I believe you will find your way back to each other…"

Alexander's eyes turned icy. "But Grandmother, what if she gives me no second chance? What if after the divorce, she falls in love with someone else?"

Eleanor was speechless for a moment, finally sighing helplessly. "Then it means it wasn't meant to be. You foolish boy, think it over carefully."

With that, she turned and left.

The moment the study door closed, Alexander slammed his fist into the wall beside him.

"Bang!"

A bloody mark was left on the wall.

Alexander turned, his gaze falling on the marriage certificate on the desk…

Finally, he strode over, stuffing the marriage certificate, ID card, and household register into his pocket.

There were still three hours until two in the afternoon.

In three hours, Evelyn would no longer be his Mrs. Vance…

Alexander let out a low laugh.

The sound was bitter and despairing, filled with the helplessness of being trapped by circumstances.

He leaned back in his chair and violently swept everything off the desk!!

Then he clutched his head tightly.

His fists were trembling.

Coincidentally, the box fell open upon impact, and the jade thumb ring inside jumped out, rolling across the floor with a "clatter."

Alexander froze.

He followed the sound. The jade thumb ring rolled a few times on the floor before coming to a stop…

When Alexander clearly saw the jade thumb ring, his pupils constricted violently.

This jade thumb ring… Didn't Sophia say her mother had sold it?

Why was it here?!

This was Eleanor's family heirloom. After Sophia said it had been sold, he had Thomas search for it all these years.

He had intended to buy it back and return it to his grandmother.

After all, his grandmother had wanted to give this jade thumb ring to her granddaughter-in-law…

So how did it suddenly appear here?

Did Thomas find it?

…No!

The jade thumb ring was delivered to the old mansion along with the letter!

Could it be…

A shock went through Alexander's pupils. He hurriedly pushed his chair back, knelt on the floor, pushed aside the documents, and finally found the letter he had tossed aside.

His hands trembling, he slowly opened the letter…

The handwriting wasn't Sophia's, but it held a strangely familiar quality.

This familiarity sparked an incredible guess in Alexander's heart…

He hurriedly read on…

The moment he saw the signature, he was stunned, rooted to the spot!!

The letter read:

"By the way, I didn't have a name back then, but I do now. My name is Evelyn Ross. My number is 183xxxxxxxx. If you come to San Francisco, I'll treat you to a meal."
###Chapter 245

Alexander Vance's gaze froze on the last sentence.

That name and phone number made his breath catch.

"My name is Evelyn Ross…"

He suddenly remembered what Evelyn had said at the hospital.

She had also saved a big brother when she was little.

Why hadn't he thought of it sooner?

Perhaps that big brother was him.

Alexander suddenly felt like laughing.

But his eyes uncontrollably reddened.

He finally saw a way out of this desperate situation.

No divorce.

He could finally be with Evelyn.

He took out his phone to make a call.

But he paused while dialing.

He wanted to give Evelyn a surprise.

Though their relationship was understood.

He had never formally proposed.

The photo on their marriage certificate wasn't even her.

He had to go to City Hall this afternoon.

But not for divorce.

To replace their marriage certificate.

He wanted to properly register with Evelyn.

To become lifelong partners.

He exited the dialing screen.

Contacted Thomas Watson directly.

"Help me prepare some things."

After listing what he needed.

Thomas hesitated before speaking.

"Boss, this sounds like a proposal setup."

"Are you trying to leave a lasting impression on Miss Ross even during divorce?"

Alexander only replied with one word.

"Get lost."

"Right away."

"Come back."

Thomas immediately stopped hanging up.

"What else do you need, boss?"

Alexander had calmed down now.

He trusted Evelyn.

He knew she wouldn't joke about such things.

But he also needed to explain the misunderstanding.

How had Sophia Watson deceived them?

Alexander frowned.

His expression turned serious.

"Find out how Sophia fooled us."

When Sophia lost the token initially.

Alexander had indeed doubted its authenticity.

But Sophia detailed her experiences with Little Dumpling.

She even passed a lie detector test.

That lie detector was world-class equipment.

It rarely made mistakes.

Unless someone had special agent training.

Sophia was a mental patient.

How could she have received training?

What exactly happened here?

Thomas was stunned.

"Boss, what do you mean fooled us?"

He suddenly understood.

"You mean…"

"Yes."

Alexander's voice trembled.

"She wasn't the one who saved me back then."

"It was Evelyn."

He had been searching for a solution day and night these past few days.

Never expecting it would be her.

Fortunately, it was her.

Half an hour later.

Thomas reported the investigation results.

"I spoke with the chief physician."

"They performed an EEG on Sophia."

"Found she never actually deceived us."

Alexander laughed coldly.

"Are you saying Sophia was really that person?"

"That Evelyn is lying?"

The chilling coldness made Thomas speak immediately.

"That's not what I meant."

"Several psychiatrists reviewed the entire case."

"They reached a conclusion."

"What?"

"Sophia didn't deceive us."

"She deceived herself."

Alexander was taken aback.

"What do you mean?"

Thomas sighed.

"The doctors said after years of monitoring Sophia's mental state."

"They found she's been abnormal since childhood."

"She passed the lie detector tests because."

"She genuinely believed she wasn't lying."

"She was frequently abused by her parents as a child."

"Was already mentally disturbed when trafficked."

"Sophia was indeed trafficked with you."

"She watched silently among the group of children."

"Saw your interactions with Little Dumpling."

"Witnessed your mutual salvation."

"Saw how Evelyn helped you all."

"Watched you make promises to Evelyn."

"After being rescued and returning home."

"When abused by her parents again."

"She always believed you would come save her."

"Over time, she inserted herself into Evelyn's role."

"Truly believed she was the one who saved you back then."

"Really considered you her only salvation."

"That's why treatment never worked all these years."

Hearing the investigation results.

Alexander was slightly stunned.

His original anger suddenly had nowhere to go.

He thought everything was deception.

Thought he'd been foolishly tricked for five years.

Never expected this situation.

But this explained everything.

Alexander's expression turned cold.

He remembered now.

Among the trafficked children back then.

There was indeed a dirty child.

Her small face hidden beneath hair.

Always secretly watching him and Little Dumpling.

Little Dumpling was different from her.

No matter how bad the environment.

Little Dumpling washed her face clean every day.

They had completely different personalities.

That's why something felt off when they found Sophia.

Hence the lie detector test.

Originally thought her mental abnormality was due to illness.

Now he just wanted to slap himself.

How could he have mistaken the person?

How could he have gotten it wrong?

If he hadn't mistaken her.

If he had found Evelyn sooner.

She wouldn't have suffered so much later.

What was Evelyn doing five years ago?

She left the Ashcroft family to work while studying.

Thinking of those work-study documents.

Alexander's heart ached as he pressed his chest.

Thomas cautiously asked.

"Boss, what should we do about Miss Watson now?"

Facing a mental patient.

Simply abandoning her seemed inhumane.

Alexander remained silent for a long time.

Finally laughed coldly.

"Tell her the truth when she wakes up."

"You'll handle her matters from now on."

"Don't cut off her treatment funds."

"But I won't see her again."

"Understood."

Thomas didn't say more.

This was already the best outcome.

...

Elsewhere.

After easily dealing with the reporters.

Evelyn received another text message.

The person from last night sent a message.

[Evelyn, meet me at The Orchid Tea Room at 11:30.]

Evelyn immediately headed over.

Just sat down at a table.

When someone suddenly appeared before her.

Evelyn looked at her.

Her pupils slightly contracted.

"It's you?"
###Chapter 246
Believe Her?

The black baseball cap was pulled down low.

Black sunglasses obscured most of her face.

A black mask covered the rest.

A loose, oversized black jacket.

Her hands were shoved in her pockets.

She was completely bundled up.

One could barely tell she was a woman.

Evelyn narrowed her eyes. "It's you?"

The other woman nodded. "It's me."

Evelyn leaned forward. "The one who impersonated me to get the marriage certificate?"

The woman nodded again. "It's me."

Evelyn leaned back. "Who are you, really?"

The woman was silent for a moment. "Evelyn, I can't say. But please believe me, I mean no harm. Alexander must stay with you. Only he can protect you."

Evelyn scoffed. "I don't need protection."

"You do!" The woman suddenly became agitated, placing her hands on the table. "You have no idea how dangerous things are around you!"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "Dangerous? I've lived over twenty years. The biggest danger was Linda's beatings."

The woman shook her head. "That was just the tip of the iceberg."

Evelyn stood up. "You, hiding your face, seem far more dangerous."

She made to leave.

The woman urgently grabbed her arm. "Evelyn!"

Evelyn turned back.

The woman seemed to reach a decision. She swiftly removed her sunglasses and mask.

Evelyn's pupils constricted.

She was looking at a face identical to her own.

Only it lacked the tear mole at the corner of the eye.

Evelyn's voice tightened. "You?"

The woman put her mask back on. "Now you should believe me."

Evelyn stared intently. "Who are you?"

The woman bit her lip. "I'm your twin sister."

Evelyn froze. "What?"

The woman pulled her into a private room, carefully closing the door.

She removed all her disguise again.

Long, dark hair cascaded down.

This face appeared softer than Evelyn's, her eyes filled with anxiety.

Evelyn frowned. "Mom only had me."

Tears slid down the woman's face. "No, we are twins. I wasn't breathing when I was born. William Ashcroft threw me away immediately. He hid it from Mom, afraid she'd be heartbroken."

Evelyn pressed. "Mom doesn't know?"

"She never knew." The woman choked up. "She also never knew you and Isabella were switched."

Evelyn found it odd. "No prenatal checkups during the pregnancy?"

The woman nodded. "Mom never had any prenatal checkups."

Evelyn's gaze turned sharp. "How do you know all this?"

The woman shook her head in panic. "I can't say. If I do, my child will be in danger."

Child...

Evelyn remembered last night's message.

The woman gripped her hand tightly. "Evelyn, I'm your biological sister. I would never harm you. Please believe me!"

Evelyn looked directly at her. "Swear on your child."

The woman didn't hesitate. "I swear on my child, I will never harm you! If I'm lying, may my child..."

She suddenly stopped, her eyes full of struggle.

Evelyn's heart softened. "Enough."

She asked softly, "Is your child a boy or a girl?"

A gentle smile appeared on the woman's face as she unlocked her phone. "This is my daughter's birthday."

The photo album showed a little girl, about two or three years old.

Her peach blossom eyes sparkled.

Evelyn's heart inexplicably trembled.

The bond of blood made her instantly soften.

She looked at the woman.

Her intuition told her this was real.

Evelyn had long suspected Sophia Ashcroft's secrets.

It was entirely possible she had no prenatal checkups back then.

Her sister being discarded, herself being switched... it was likely all William Ashcroft's doing.

Evelyn fell into thought.

The woman put her phone away, her eyes pleading. "Evelyn, do you believe me now? You must not get divorced..."

Evelyn gave a bitter smile.

It wasn't that she wanted a divorce.

But Alexander had already made his choice.

Her phone vibrated.

A message from Alexander Vance: 【City Hall at 2 PM.】

Her heart ached.

Her phone vibrated again.

A message from Sophia Ashcroft: 【Meeting the handsome older gentleman at Le Jardin Secret at noon. I'll go first. Come when you're free.】

Evelyn replied: 【Private room 8. Go in if you arrive first.】

A message from the handsome older gentleman followed immediately: 【Little friend, I'm in private room 8. When will you arrive?】

Evelyn quickly replied: 【My mother will be there first to entertain you.】

After handling all the messages, she turned her attention back to the sister before her.
###Chapter 247

Evelyn Ross stared at the girl before her.

"What's your name?"

The girl hesitated for a moment.

She smiled bitterly. "We're connected by fate, in a way. My name is Grace Nan."

Evelyn was slightly taken aback.

"Nan?"

The same surname as her mother, Sophia Ashcroft.

Was it a coincidence?

The girl nodded. "My surname is Nan. My given name is Gege. It was fine when I was little, but as I grew up, everyone just called me Evelyn."

Evelyn… Evelyn…

No wonder she said they were connected.

The girl's eyes were clear and gentle.

Evelyn found it hard not to feel a connection to her.

She had always been strong and resilient.

But she had a soft spot for gentle, delicate girls like this.

Evelyn was about to speak.

Grace Nan spoke first. "Evelyn, I've told you everything I can. Do you believe me now?"

Her eyes were timid yet urgent.

Evelyn pressed her lips together and nodded.

Grace Nan immediately looked relieved.

"Then don't go through with the divorce!"

Evelyn gave a bitter smile.

"At this point, the divorce is out of my hands."

Grace Nan froze.

"Is there no room for negotiation?"

"No."

Evelyn lowered her gaze.

A shadow passed through her eyes.

Just yesterday, Alexander Vance was still being difficult.

He clearly had the documents but claimed he didn't.

Today, he was the one who initiated contact.

Evelyn suppressed her bitter smile.

She looked up to see Grace Nan's anxious, worried expression.

It was as if some great danger was approaching.

She couldn't help but ask, "What exactly are you so afraid of?"

Grace Nan nervously clenched her fingers.

She released them, then clenched them again.

After repeating this several times, she seemed on the verge of tears.

"I… I can't say…"

Evelyn frowned.

She checked the time.

It was almost time for her lunch with the handsome older man.

She looked at Grace Nan. "Will you come with me to see Mom?"

Grace Nan immediately shook her head.

She hurriedly put on her mask and hat.

Her eyes filled with regret.

"I can't. Reuniting with Mom would put her in danger. Evelyn, you must remember, there are dangers in this world beyond your imagination. Please, beg Alexander Vance again. If you can avoid divorce, do it. If he refuses, go to Eleanor Vance. She likes you…"

Grace Nan's voice was choked with tears.

She sounded utterly desperate.

Evelyn's warm hand closed around Grace's wrist.

Grace Nan stopped speaking and looked at her.

Evelyn spoke calmly. "Don't be afraid. We'll face whatever comes. I'll remember your warning."

Grace Nan's panic gradually subsided under Evelyn's steady gaze.

Evelyn continued, "I don't know what difficulties you're facing. But if you need help, you can tell me directly."

Grace Nan opened her mouth.

Finally, she just patted Evelyn's shoulder.

"Evelyn, I'm your older sister. I should be the one protecting you."

Evelyn felt a warmth in her heart.

This fragile girl was saying she would protect her…

She patted Grace Nan's shoulder.

She looked down with a light laugh. "Okay. Then… thank you, sister?"

The word "sister" made Grace Nan blush.

She shyly lowered her head.

"You're… you're welcome."

Seeing Evelyn check the time again, Grace Nan said, "You must be busy. You should go. I need to head home too."

She glanced at her watch.

"I have to pick up my daughter from school at five."

Evelyn nodded. "Alright. You go first."

Grace Nan opened the door to leave.

It was lunchtime.

A strange, fragrant scent wafted in from outside.

Evelyn's body stiffened slightly.

…

Elsewhere.

In front of City Hall.

Thomas Watson had brought everything Alexander Vance requested.

He had just parked the car.

He saw his boss standing in the parking lot waiting for him.

Alexander walked over as soon as he saw the car.

"Did you bring everything?"

Thomas was speechless. "Boss, if I recall correctly, your appointment with Evelyn Ross is at two PM, not noon, right?"

It was only eleven-thirty.

Why the hurry?

Alexander gave him a cool glance.

He went straight to his own car and opened the trunk.

"Stop wasting time. Bring the stuff over."

Thomas immediately carried two boxes over.

"Boss, I specifically asked when I bought them. They can decorate it for you. Just drive the car over. You're…"

"Doing it myself makes it more meaningful."

Alexander only said that.

He opened the boxes and looked at the white and pink balloons inside.

He took out a pump and started inflating them one by one.

Then he used double-sided tape to attach the balloons together.

As he worked, Alexander thought about the surprise scenes he'd seen online.

A smile touched his lips.

Would Evelyn be surprised when she saw all this romance?

She was so brave.

She probably wouldn't be surprised.

Just pleased?

Thinking this, Alexander pumped the balloons with more vigor.

When he had enough, he stood up.

He took a small box from his pocket.

He opened it.

Inside was a jade thumb ring.

He tied the ring to a red string.

Then he attached it to a balloon.

It was a helium balloon that could float in the air.

He placed the balloon in the trunk.

He secured it under the trunk lid.

This way, when the trunk opened…

It would be filled with pink and white balloons.

And beautiful roses.

Add some lights…

Alexander coughed at the thought.

He cleared his throat and said softly, "Little bun, big brother is here to take you home."

Then he chuckled softly.

It felt a bit embarrassing.

Alexander sighed quietly.

Suddenly, he realized something.

He slowly turned his head.

He saw Thomas and the driver standing not far away.

They were both staring at him as if they'd seen a ghost.

When he looked over, Thomas and the driver immediately averted their eyes.

They looked elsewhere, clearly pretending they hadn't heard anything.

Thomas said, "Hey, Old Li, I suddenly had a ringing in my ears just now. Didn't hear a thing."

Driver Old Li looked particularly earnest. "Mr. Watson, you only had ringing? I suddenly went deaf! I need to go to the hospital after work!"

Thomas: "?? You win!"

Alexander: "…"

…

Le Jardin Secret.

Mr. Valentine sat in private room number eight.

Marcus Valentine was with him.

They both stared intently at the door.

Mr. Valentine checked the time.

It was already noon.

It seemed the young lady was going to be late?

Outside.

Sophia Ashcroft stepped out of her family car.

She looked at the name "Le Jardin Secret."

She smiled slightly and entered.

She headed for private room number eight.

She kept her head down.

Debating in her heart whether she should wait for Evelyn…

She checked the time.

Evelyn was already late.

As her mother, she should entertain the guests on Evelyn's behalf.

She couldn't let the guests wait too long in the room.

That would be impolite.

Sophia pushed the door open…

Inside.

Mr. Valentine paused at the sound of the door opening.

He looked up.

When he saw the figure in the doorway—one that had appeared in his dreams countless times—he froze completely!!

He pulled back his chair and stood up instantly.

Sophia's pupils contracted when she saw him!

Over twenty years had passed.

Mr. Valentine bore the marks of time.

But he still looked exactly as she remembered.
###Chapter 248

Mr. Valentine's heavy gaze fell upon Sophia Ashcroft.

More than twenty years had passed.

They were now the most familiar strangers to each other.

The air grew still.

Marcus Valentine cleared his throat, breaking the silence. "Mrs. Ashcroft, what brings you here?"

Sophia's fingertips trembled slightly.

She suddenly realized.

This Mr. Valentine was the handsome older man Evelyn had mentioned.

Had he seen Evelyn's face?

If he knew Evelyn was her daughter…

A phone ringtone cut through the air abruptly.

Sophia saw Evelyn's name on the screen and answered immediately.

A cold, electronic voice spoke from the other end. "I have your daughter."

Sophia's pupils constricted sharply. "What?"

"I've sent you the location."

The call ended abruptly.

Sophia looked down at her phone.

The GPS location showed it was out at sea!

Her face instantly turned pale.

Mr. Valentine noticed something was wrong and stepped forward immediately. "What's happened?"

Sophia trembled as she called the police. "I need to report a kidnapping. My daughter has been taken…"

She turned and rushed out of the private room.

Mr. Valentine followed without hesitation. "What's going on?"

Sophia clenched her phone tightly. "Valentine, please help me…"

…

Evelyn Ross jolted awake.

Her heart pounded like a drum.

She found herself tied up inside a ship's cabin.

A strange fragrance lingered in the air.

The effects of a potent sedative made her head feel heavy and muddled.

Grace Nan lay unconscious across from her.

Handcuffs locked her wrists to a metal pole.

Water was seeping into the bottom of the boat.

The vessel was slowly sinking.

The water level was about to submerge the entire ship.

The air in the cabin grew increasingly thin.

If they didn't escape within a minute, they would certainly die.

Whoever did this wanted them dead!

Evelyn's eyes turned sharp.

She didn't hesitate to dislocate her finger joints.

The intense pain instantly cleared her mind.

She freed herself from the handcuffs and rushed to Grace's side. "Wake up!"

Grace's eyes fluttered open groggily.

Seeing their situation, her pupils dilated in shock. "It's them… They found us! They're going to kill us!"

Evelyn checked her handcuffs. "Who are they?"

Grace's finger joints were too large.

She couldn't break free like Evelyn had.

Evelyn picked up a rod and smashed it against the handcuffs.

"Clang!"

The handcuffs didn't budge.

Tears streamed down Grace's face. "They want you dead… It's my fault they found you!"

Evelyn continued to strike the handcuffs.

Breathing became increasingly difficult.

Grace looked out the window.

The boat had completely submerged underwater.

"Evelyn, leave me! Get out of here!"

Evelyn didn't stop her efforts.

Grace smiled bitterly in despair. "Maybe it's better if someone like me dies…"

"Shut up!" Evelyn said sharply. "Think about Chloe! Would you let that mistress abuse her?"

Grace's eyes suddenly filled with determination. "You're right… I can't die!"

She looked at her hand. "Smash my hand! Break it if you have to!"

Evelyn tightened her grip on the rod.

Water rushed into the cabin wildly.

Time was running out.

"...Open your palm."

Grace closed her eyes, trembling.

Evelyn struck down ruthlessly!

"Thud!"

Grace let out a muffled groan.

Her little finger was a bloody mess.

Evelyn quickly dislocated the joint.

She pulled Grace's hand free from the handcuff.

"Go!"

Evelyn smashed the window.

The glass shattered upon impact.

The current swept furniture toward them.

Both women struggled to swim out of the cabin.

They fought their way upward toward the surface.

Sunlight was just within reach.

But Evelyn suddenly felt all her strength leave her.

Her vision began to darken.

Her iron deficiency anemia was acting up.

Her body sank uncontrollably.

Grace looked back at her.

Struggle flashed in her eyes.

She gritted her teeth.

And continued swimming upward.

Evelyn slowly closed her eyes.

Maybe it was better to end it like this…

A hand suddenly grabbed her wrist!
###Chapter 249

Evelyn Ross's eyes snapped open.

Grace Nan's pale face was inches from hers.

In the rippling water, her features looked fragile, as if they might shatter at a touch.

They couldn't speak.

But the determination in Grace's eyes was unmistakable.

Evelyn suddenly understood.

Grace had surfaced earlier to catch her breath.

Now she was trying to drag them both upward.

But Grace was too frail.

Swimming with another person was impossible.

Evelyn gave her a faint smile.

It was enough that her sister had come back for her.

She gestured weakly in the water. Leave me. Save yourself.

Grace shook her head.

Her bloodied finger pointed upward. Together!

Evelyn's eyes burned.

She wanted to gesture again, but her body had no strength left.

Escaping the sinking ship had drained her completely.

She could only watch as Grace stubbornly pulled her, inching them toward the surface.

Splash.

They broke through the water.

Evelyn gasped for air, but her body kept sinking.

Her consciousness faded.

Nothing but endless ocean surrounded them. No shore in sight.

Tossed by the waves, she knew they were far from San Francisco.

Weakness and anemia blurred her vision.

A ringing filled her ears, drowned out by the sound of water.

"Evelyn, hold on! Don't fall asleep!"

Grace's voice reached her in fragments.

"It's my fault... I shouldn't have come to see you... I led them here..."

Sobs mixed with ragged breaths.

"You can't die! I won't let you die!"

Evelyn felt Grace's strength fading.

When she was pushed above the waves again, she heard Grace cry out. "Look! A plank! We're saved!"

Evelyn couldn't see anything.

Everything was dark.

A sliver of reason kept her aware.

She felt Grace trying repeatedly to push her onto the wooden plank.

She wanted to tell Grace to save herself, but no sound came out.

She tried to push her away, but couldn't move a finger.

Grace didn't give up.

Attempt after attempt. The plank kept sliding away.

After what felt like an eternity, Evelyn was finally heaved onto the plank.

She floated on the surface.

She turned her head and saw the relief on Grace's face.

She wanted to pull Grace up, but couldn't move.

Grace sighed, exhausted, with no strength left to climb aboard.

A flash of sorrow crossed her eyes.

"Take care of Chloe for me..."

Those were her only words before she sank beneath the waves.

Sister!!

Evelyn screamed inside, powerless to struggle.

Darkness swallowed her.

A single tear traced her cheek.

…

So cold.

Evelyn felt herself sinking endlessly.

Suffocation enveloped her.

In her haze, she thought she heard voices.

Indistinct.

She didn't know how much time had passed when her eyes flew open.

One moment she was floating at sea. Now she lay in a soft bed.

As if no time had passed at all.

Evelyn frowned, studying the room.

Rustic style. Floral duvet. Bright and tidy.

She turned her head and saw a little girl lying beside her.

The girl stirred, opening her eyes.

She saw Evelyn and burst into tears. "Mommy's awake! Mommy didn't die! Didn't leave Chloe!"

Chloe…

Grace's daughter?

As Evelyn froze, the little girl scrambled barefoot off the bed.

She soon returned with a cup of water. "Mommy drink. Then won't leave Chloe."

Her baby voice made Evelyn's heart sink.

She flexed her fingers. Strength was returning.

She sat up slowly, looking around.

Was this Grace's home?

They thought she was Grace?

"Chloe, how long have I been sleeping?"

"Mommy slept two days... Almost died in pool. Nice uncle took to hospital. Daddy said not dying, come home..."

Pool?

Nice uncle…

Evelyn looked at the back of her hand. An IV mark.

Someone had replenished her iron in time?

She remembered a boat approaching after she was pushed onto the plank…

She was saved. But what about Grace?

She looked for a phone to contact San Francisco…

The door suddenly swung open.

A man in his mid-twenties walked in.

He had delicate features, with a mocking, disdainful look.

Chloe hid behind Evelyn, whispering, "Daddy."

Grace's husband?

Evelyn was about to explain when the man sneered. "Good, you're awake. Get ready. We're leaving immediately."

Evelyn frowned.

Chloe said softly, "Mommy sick. Uncle said need rest…"

The mansneered. "But she's not dead, is she?"

He called outside. "Mom, come help her pack!"

A middle-aged woman grumbled as she entered. "Nuisance! No hands of her own?"

Grace's mother-in-law.

She said to the man, "Zack, you spoil her too much!"

Zack Davis smiled. "Mom, we're in a hurry. Don't waste time."

"Where to, so urgent?"

"Just heard Alexander Vance's wife from San Francisco passed away. Attending the funeral. Chance to meet some big shots."

Evelyn was about to speak when her pupils contracted.

She stared at Zack in disbelief. "Who did you say?!"

The Mrs. Vance… wasn't that her?

Unless…

Had her sister died?!
###Chapter 250
No, it can't be.

How could my sister be dead?

Evelyn stared at Zack, her voice trembling. "Mrs. Vance is dead?"

Zack glanced at her and scoffed. "Why are you so worked up? It's not like you knew her..."

Mrs. Davis immediately asked, "The Vance family? The top elite in San Francisco? Isn't the head of the family quite young? How did she die?"

Zack lowered his voice. "I heard she was kidnapped and thrown into the sea to drown. Her body was so bloated when they fished it out. It had been in the water for twenty hours!"

"What a shame. She finally married into wealth, only to die young. Zack, can we attend the funeral? We're not close to the Vances..."

Zack said smugly, "Mom, don't be silly. Funerals don't turn guests away. I heard Mrs. Vance came from an ordinary background. We'll just say Grace was her classmate coming to pay respects. We can slip in that way."

Mrs. Davis grew excited. "So that's why you insisted on bringing this jinx. But why say Grace was her classmate? Wouldn't it be easier to say you were classmates to get closer?"

"Grace and Mrs. Vance are the same age. I'm three years older!"

Zack grabbed Evelyn's wrist. "Why aren't you moving? Hurry up!"

Evelyn frowned and pulled away. "Don't touch me!"

Zack sneered. "Grace, you're my wife. If I don't touch you, who should I touch?"

Evelyn looked straight at him. "I'm not Grace."

Zack studied her, then laughed scornfully. "Grace, what game are you playing now? If you're not Grace, who are you? Get up!"

He grabbed her hair and yanked hard.

Evelyn instinctively wanted to fight back. She tried to flip him over her shoulder, but her strength hadn't returned. Her hands felt weak.

He dragged her off the bed, and she fell to the floor.

"Mommy, Mommy!" Chloe rushed over. Her small body shielded Evelyn as she spread her arms and shouted at Zack. "Daddy bad! Don't hit Mommy!"

Zack snorted dismissively and ignored her. He turned to the middle-aged woman. "Mom, help her get ready. I'll get the car."

Mrs. Davis grumbled as she opened the wardrobe. She tossed out a few clothes. "Other families enjoy blessings when they get a daughter-in-law. Our Zack married into your family. What terrible luck! Now I have to wait on you, this jinx, and this worthless girl!"

Married into...

Evelyn caught those words sensitively.

Her fingers were enveloped by a soft, small hand. She looked up into Chloe's eyes. "Mommy, does it hurt? Blowing on it makes it better..."

The little girl puffed out her cheeks and blew on Evelyn's forehead.

The gesture made Evelyn's heart ache.

She quickly stood up from the floor.

Mrs. Davis walked over to open another wardrobe. Seeing Chloe in the way, she kicked the child's bottom. "Move! Worthless girl. No sense at all. Can't you see I need to get clothes?"

Chloe stumbled and fell face-first.

Evelyn helped the child up and saw her arm was bruised. The little one's eyes welled with tears, but she dared not let them fall.

Evelyn glared at Mrs. Davis.

Mrs. Davis sneered. "What are you staring at? Everyone falls when they're little!" She glared at Chloe. "Cry, cry, cry! That's all you do! I'll hit you if you cry!"

Chloe hiccupped in fear. She sobbed, "Chloe no cry, no cry..."

Her small body trembled. She widened her eyes and looked down.

Evelyn followed her gaze. The two-year-old couldn't fully control her bladder yet. Her pants slowly darkened as a puddle spread on the floor.

Evelyn had never raised a child. She was about to comfort her when Mrs. Davis roared, "Who said you could pee?! Worthless girl! Shameless!"

Chloe's face crumpled. Large tears rolled down.

Mrs. Davis immediately raised her hand to strike. "Hold those tears back! Who said you could cry? Feeling sorry for yourself? What do you have to be sorry about? This jinx has been lying around for two days. I'm the one taking care of you! Even a dog knows not to pee indoors. You're worse than a dog!"

Smack!

Evelyn caught her wrist, furious.

Chloe was only two years old. She didn't understand anything!

Grace's mother-in-law was too vicious!

Her eyes turned dark and dangerous as she stared at Mrs. Davis. "Don't hit the child!"

Mrs. Davis easily shook off her weak grip. "Jinx! You dare tell me what to do? I'll hit you too if you're not careful!"

Evelyn's voice turned icy. "You wouldn't dare."

Mrs. Davis laughed scornfully. "Why wouldn't I? You're my daughter-in-law. What's wrong with hitting you?"

Her casual arrogance showed this wasn't the first time.

Evelyn trembled with rage. Her sister had been bullied like this by her husband and mother-in-law!

She took a deep breath. "I told you, I'm not Grace! Touch me, and you'll regret it!"

Mrs. Davis scoffed. "Grace, have you lost your mind? If you're not Grace, then who are you?!"

The door opened again. Zack appeared, impatient. "What's going on? All this crying and shouting. Are you ready? Get in the car. The body is being cremated today! We'll miss it if we're late!"

Mrs. Davis grumbled, "It's this jinx! She says she's not Grace. And this worthless girl peed her pants again!"

Zack looked at Evelyn and laughed coldly. "Don't want to help? Grace, you think I can't handle you?"

He picked up Chloe and strode out. "Follow me if you want your daughter back!"

Evelyn's eyes narrowed. She glared at Zack's retreating back.

After a moment's thought, she followed.

Since they didn't believe her, she would go to the Vance family. Let Alexander deal with this mother and son!

Walking out of the bedroom, she spotted the landline phone on the living room table.

Evelyn immediately picked it up and dialed Alexander's number.

She needed to tell Alexander she was still alive.
###Chapter 251

The call went out.

The dial tone sounded.

Evelyn held her breath.

Her sister must be safe...

"Beep, beep—"

Her heartbeat quickened.

No one answered.

An engine sounded outside.

Chloe cried for her mother in fear.

Evelyn frowned.

Forget it.

She was going to the Vance estate anyway.

She ended the call.

Stepped outside.

It was a small villa.

A business car waited at the door.

Zack Davis threw Chloe into the back seat.

He took the driver's seat.

Mrs. Davis followed with the luggage.

She sat in the passenger seat.

Zack urged impatiently.

Evelyn got in silently.

Held the trembling Chloe.

She found clean pants from the luggage.

Changed Chloe's diaper.

Secured her in the car seat.

Chloe whimpered softly.

Too scared to cry aloud.

Evelyn pulled the phone from her pocket.

The screen lit up.

It showed a photo of Grace Nan and Chloe.

This was her sister's phone...

The car entered the highway.

Scenery flew past the window.

Evelyn calmly sorted through events.

The drugged incense in the restaurant private room.

Escaping the sinking ship.

Grace Nan pushing her onto the wooden plank.

Rescue by the large ship.

Waking up as Grace Nan.

Her sister's phone in her pocket.

The doctor uncle Chloe mentioned.

Iron infusion.

Fainting by the pool...

None of it made sense.

Grace Nan's words echoed in her ears.

They found us.

They want to kill us.

They want to kill you, Evelyn.

I exposed you...

Evelyn's brow furrowed tightly.

So "they" wanted to kill her?

But got the wrong person?

No.

Why take Grace Nan onto the ship after drugging them?

Their most obvious difference was the teardrop mole.

Who was behind this?

Who wanted them dead?

Who saved them?

How did she end up at Grace Nan's home?

It was a tangled mess.

Evelyn took a deep breath.

Looked at the phone.

Remembered the password Grace Nan mentioned.

She asked for Chloe's birthday.

Chloe answered in a baby voice, June twenty-sixth.

She entered 0626.

The screen unlocked.

The messages were empty.

Not even spam messages.

They had definitely texted each other.

Grace Nan must have deleted each message after sending.

WhatsApp only had everyday chats.

No clues.

Evelyn put away the phone.

Decided to wait and see.

First, the Vance estate.

Zack focused on driving.

Mrs. Davis watched her from the rearview mirror.

She sneered.

The company is yours.

Zack is making money for whom.

Is it so hard to ask you to do a simple thing.

Zack scoffed.

Grace doesn't like socializing.

Thinks it's beneath her.

Beneath her?

Does she really think she's some wealthy heiress.

All that talk about recruiting a live-in son-in-law was exaggerated.

Turns out the pathetic little company only makes two hundred thousand a year?

Mrs. Davis looked at her son.

You got the short end of the stick marrying into this family.

She must have tricked you.

Evelyn refused to let her sister be slandered.

She retorted directly.

Zack.

Did Grace Nan really deceive you when you got married?

Changed the subject.

Why bring up the past.

Evelyn knew she had guessed correctly.

Grace Nan was gentle and honest.

She must have explained the situation during the marriage talks.

The suburban villa in New York was worth two million.

The company's two hundred thousand annual income covered expenses.

No need to exaggerate.

Mrs. Davis wouldn't let it go.

Behave well at the Vance estate later.

Don't be so lifeless.

Otherwise, why did we bring you.

Remember that.

Evelyn lowered her eyes.

Look at that dead face again.

Mrs. Davis tattled.

Useless at everything.

Only knows how to upset me at home.

Zack scoffed.

Don't worry, Mom.

She's from a modest background and hasn't seen the world.

Once she sees the Vances' luxury.

She'll naturally know how to please people.

Mrs. Davis sneered.

True.

She stays cooped up at home all day.

What kind of experience could she have.

Evelyn couldn't be bothered to respond.

The car arrived at the Vance estate.

She looked out the window.

And froze.
###Chapter 252

White cloth draped the Vance estate.

A long line of cars waited to pay their respects.

Zack Davis's car was stuck in traffic.

He honked the horn impatiently.

Mrs. Davis peered out the window.

Her eyes gleamed. "This entire estate belongs to the Vances? It's so grand!"

Zack licked his lips. "This is a rare chance to go inside and see for ourselves."

Evelyn Ross sat in silence.

She only cared if the corpse was her sister.

Zack saw her expressionless face and scowled. "Grace, don't embarrass us later. Be smart about this!"

Little Chloe had just woken up. She hugged Evelyn. "Daddy, don't yell at Mommy!"

Evelyn's eyes turned cold.

She remembered Zack pulling her sister's hair.

Did this man abuse her?

Zack sneered. "I won't hit her if she behaves."

Chloe looked at Evelyn timidly. "Mommy, behave..."

The little girl's eyes resembled Evelyn's.

But they held fear.

Evelyn gently patted her head. "Don't be afraid, Chloe. No one will hit Mommy anymore."

She couldn't bear to tell the child the truth.

The line of cars slowly moved forward.

Evelyn realized the child hadn't eaten anything during the trip.

She frowned. "Is there any food?"

Evelyn found some spicy snacks.

Mrs. Davis snatched them away and shared them with Zack.

Evelyn kept searching and found a can of formula.

"How do I prepare the formula?"

Evelyn understood immediately.

The little girl was pale, sweating, and secretly chewing her finger.

She had low blood sugar.

Evelyn made a decision. "I'll ask the Vances for hot water."

Zack was impatient. "Just let her eat some spicy strips to tide her over!"

Mrs. Davis rolled her eyes. "She's just a burden anyway. It's better if she's gone."

Evelyn was furious.

Mrs. Davis screamed in fear.

Zack instinctively unlocked the doors.

Evelyn immediately grabbed Chloe and the bottle, rushing out of the car.

He wore a black suit with a white flower on his chest. He frowned. "What's going on here?"

Evelyn turned around sharply.

Her eyes lit up when she saw Thomas.
###Chapter 253

Cars streamed by in both directions.

Evelyn turned her head to look.

Zack Davis had already grabbed her arm, shooting her a wary glance.

Evelyn said nothing, simply gazing calmly at Thomas Watson.

Seeing her resolute attitude, Zack immediately fawned at Thomas. "Mr. Watson, we came from New York for the funeral. The baby needs milk, but we have no hot water... The mother just wanted to ask if the Vance residence has hot water..."

Evelyn offered no further explanation, just watched Thomas quietly.

To her surprise, Thomas merely glanced at her and said, "The Vances have plenty of hot water. Should I have someone bring some over?"

Evelyn frowned slightly.

What did Thomas mean by that?

Did he not recognize her?

Confusion swirled in Evelyn's mind.

She wanted to speak but held her tongue.

In that moment, countless doubts flooded her thoughts, making her extra cautious.

Thinking carefully, the truth about her marriage had suddenly been revealed.

She had inexplicably gained a twin sister.

Grace Nan had just met her, and they both immediately faced mortal danger.

This meant the "they" Grace mentioned who wanted them dead had been watching her all along?

Had Thomas noticed something? Was that why he didn't dare acknowledge her?

As her mind raced, Zack was already waving his hands in fear, his posture humble. "No, no, I'll just go with you to get some!"

He snatched the baby bottle from Evelyn's hand.

He was about to tell her to return to the car when horns blared from behind their vehicle in the procession.

Zack turned and saw the cars ahead had already moved.

Their car remained stationary.

The vehicles backed up behind them were all expensive luxury cars.

Zack didn't dare delay.

Thomas also frowned and asked, "Whose car is that?"

Thomas said impatiently, "Let your wife come with me to get water then!"

Mrs. Davis immediately got out from the passenger seat.

She smiled obsequiously at Thomas and grabbed Evelyn's arm. "Mr. Watson, let's go, let's go..."

Mrs. Davis wanted to ingratiate herself with Thomas, but his grim expression made her too afraid to speak.

She asked in a low voice, "What's going on?"

She said warily, "What are you doing? Don't speak out of turn!"

Then she pulled Evelyn's hand and said to Thomas, "Mr. Watson, thank you for your trouble. We'll return to the car now!"

But he simply nodded, his gaze vacant and fixed ahead, his expression full of grief.

She kept looking back at Thomas.

She was utterly confused, unable to understand why Thomas didn't recognize her.

Mrs. Davis curled her lip in disdain and returned to the car with Evelyn.

Hearing this, Evelyn was slightly stunned.

Wash her face?

She subconsciously pulled out her phone.

Using the black screen as a mirror, she took a quick glance and froze instantly.

No wonder Thomas didn't recognize her.

Her face was entirely purple, clearly coated with thick iodine.

Her whole face was almost unrecognizable...

She then remembered escaping from the boat underwater.

Floating debris had likely hit her body, leaving some scratches on her face.

So they had applied iodine for disinfection?

Evelyn: "..."

She had just spent all that time thinking it was some conspiracy, pondering so many possibilities.

And it was just this?

"What are you still standing there for? Get in the car!"

Mrs. Davis had already gotten into the car and shouted impatiently.

As Evelyn approached, Mrs. Davis threw her a wet wipe. "Wipe that face of yours clean! Don't embarrass yourself here!"

Evelyn didn't refuse.

After getting in the car, she first used the water she'd fetched to prepare formula for little Chloe and gave it to her.

Then, using her phone as a mirror, she wiped the iodine off her face.

The iodine must have been applied by a nurse at the hospital two days ago.

She had been unconscious for the past two days, so no one had cleaned it for her.

After wiping for a while, some traces remained, but her original features were somewhat visible.

While she was doing this, Zack had already driven into the Vance estate.

Security guards directed them to park in the parking lot.

Evelyn followed Zack towards the main house where the funeral was being held...

The funeral was set up in the main house.

There were accounting offices on both sides handling the funeral gifts.

People lined up, first adding their monetary gifts, then proceeding to the funeral hall to pay their respects...

Little Chloe fell asleep after her milk.

Zack had Mrs. Davis stay with her in the car.

He and Evelyn walked to the accounting area.

The Vance family's butler was in charge of keeping accounts.

The butler's eyes were slightly red as he buried his head in the record books.

Evelyn and Zack quickly queued up and reached him.

Zack was a bit nervous.

He coughed and said to Evelyn, "Grace, you've never seen a scene like this, right? Don't be nervous. Later, just say you were Mrs. Vance's classmate..."

He whispered instructions.

Finally, it was their turn.

The butler asked, "May I ask who you are?"

Zack immediately nudged Evelyn.

Evelyn glanced at him and said softly, "Grace Nan."

Hearing this name, the butler paused slightly.

He subconsciously looked up.

When he saw Evelyn, the butler was completely stunned.

He stared at her in disbelief. "You, you... Madam?"
###Chapter 254

The iodine on Evelyn Ross's face hadn't been wiped clean yet, making her complexion appear sallow and somewhat plain.

But those facial features… the butler felt he couldn't possibly be mistaken.

As he stood shocked, Zack Davis handed over a stack of funeral money, saying guiltily, "My wife, Grace Nan, was Mrs. Vance's high school classmate. She insisted on coming to pay her respects after hearing about the tragedy."

After saying this, he pulled Evelyn toward the memorial hall.

The butler looked down at the funeral money. It bore the words "High School Classmate Grace Nan."

Just a classmate?

The butler rubbed his eyes. When he looked up again, the figure had disappeared into the crowd.

It must have been a trick of the light.

…

The memorial hall was set up in the center of the main house. It was spacious, allowing everyone to view the remains.

Someone nearby whispered an explanation, "After Mrs. Vance fell into the sea, her entire body was bloated from the water. Her face was… unrecognizable. It's not suitable for viewing. That's why the casket is closed…"
