Bloated from the water?

She clenched her fists, her eyes scanning the memorial hall.

Richard Evans and Margaret Evans were receiving guests. They pretended to be grief-stricken, but their eyes shone with something else.

Julian Evans stood to the side, his gaze vacant, as if genuinely heartbroken.

Evelyn didn't see Sophia Ashcroft.

Given Sophia's health condition, she must have been overwhelmed by the shock and fainted again.

Where was Alexander Vance?

Just as she thought this, she heard Margaret explaining to mourners, "Alexander is too grief-stricken. He refuses to accept that it's his wife. He's hiding in his room, unwilling to come out. He won't even see her off on her final journey…"

Whispers immediately rose around them.

"Mr. Vance is truly devoted. His love for his wife runs so deep…"

"But this isn't right, is it? Mrs. Vance's body is in that state… She should be laid to rest peacefully. He's being too emotional…"

"I heard Mr. Vance hasn't been to the company for two days. Thomas Watson is handling everything…"

Evelyn lowered her eyes.

Zack was busy networking with others, not paying attention to her.

Evelyn quietly turned and left the memorial hall.

Her face was sallow, and she wore Grace Nan's gentle, elegant-style clothes. No one recognized her as she walked.

She quickly turned into Eleanor Vance's courtyard.

Most of the servants in the courtyard had gone to the main house to help.

Fiona was talking to another maid.

Fiona looked miserable. "Is the master still refusing to eat?"

The other maid nodded. "Ever since Mrs. Vance's body was recovered, the master has been completely broken…"

Fiona wiped her tears. "The master values感情 deeply. The madam is also heartbroken and has taken to her bed. Ah!"

The two walked away, still talking.

Evelyn thought for a moment. She walked to the small house's backyard, went directly to the second floor, and jumped onto the balcony of Alexander's bedroom.

Through the glass door, she saw the man curled up on the sofa.

The room was filled with smoke. The floor was littered with cigarette butts.

Alexander clenched his fists, his eyes empty.

He was remembering two days ago, waiting foolishly at City Hall.

Waiting until two PM, three PM…

He was actually happy that Evelyn was late.

She didn't come. Did that mean she also couldn't bear to divorce?

But what he received was news of her death!

When he rushed to the seaside, Sophia Ashcroft was standing strong, accompanied by Mason Xu and William Ashcroft.

Divers searched back and forth in the water.

In that moment, his legs went weak!

Every time a diver surfaced, he both hoped for news and dreaded it.

The shipwreck had happened over ten hours ago… No one could survive that long at sea!

Evelyn must have been swept away by the currents, carried to some other shore… She knew how to swim!

But James Klein's words shattered his last hope.

James was frantic, muttering to himself, "It's over… Boss was supposed to get her IV drip today… It was originally scheduled for last night, but she said she had the divorce in the afternoon, so it was moved to tonight…"

The divorce…

Alexander staggered.

If he hadn't been stubborn on the first day, if the divorce had gone smoothly, Evelyn would have gotten her IV drip on time.

Then, even if she fell into the sea, there might still have been a chance of survival…

Just as he thought this, there was news from the divers.

A female corpse, bloated beyond recognition, was recovered…

Remembering this, Alexander felt as if ten thousand arrows had pierced his heart. The pain was intense and overwhelming.

These past two days, he had grown accustomed to this pain.

He clutched his chest and lit another cigarette.

A knock came from the door. Fiona's voice called out, "Sir, Mrs. Vance's body is being taken to the funeral home. Won't you come out to see her off?"

The usually elegant and gentlemanly man looked up sharply.

Alexander's eyes were bloodshot. He grabbed an ashtray from the coffee table and hurled it violently at the door.

"Bang!"

The ashtray shattered into pieces.

Alexander roared, "Get out! That's not Evelyn! Evelyn isn't dead!"

Fiona sighed outside the door. "Sir, the Madam is here."

Alexander didn't move.

Eleanor Vance's trembling voice came from outside the door. "You foolish boy, my granddaughter-in-law cared about face and propriety when she was alive. Now, bloated and lying in the house, do you know how awful she looks? Do you want my granddaughter-in-law to die in such an undignified state?"

These words stabbed into Alexander's heart.

The steady, reserved man buried his head in his knees, holding it tightly with both hands. His voice was muffled. "Grandmother, that's not Evelyn. My intuition tells me it's not her!"

Eleanor sighed. "I know the body is unrecognizable, but the police have already done a DNA test. Foolish boy, you must face reality!"

Evelyn's heart clenched on the balcony.

Hearing everyone say the body was unrecognizable, she had still held onto a sliver of hope. Now, reality crushed her!

Her sister looked so much like her. They must have been identical twins. Their DNA would be nearly identical!

Was that body really her sister?

Evelyn saw Alexander in the room, clenching his fist and smashing it hard against the marble floor…

"Bang!"

Blood seeped out, staining the ground.

Alexander said in agony, "Grandmother, stop… It's not her!"

Silence fell outside the door for a moment. Then Eleanor spoke again. "Foolish boy, in life, one must learn to part, learn to face reality. Avoiding it like this is disrespectful to my granddaughter-in-law. Open the door. Come out. See her off properly on her final journey!"

Alexander's fists trembled.

He lowered his head and slowly knelt on the ground.

Evelyn watched him, her heart aching terribly.

She stepped out from the shadows and gently knocked on the balcony's glass door.

Alexander Vance, don't be sad. I'm not dead.
###Chapter 255

The Murderer!

The room was deathly silent.

Evelyn's knocking sounded especially abrupt.

Alexander Vance paused.

He thought he heard Evelyn's voice...

He knew it was a foolish hope, perhaps a hallucination. This had happened several times over the past few days.

But he still looked up with anticipation, gazing toward the balcony!

Through the glass door, the balcony was empty.

Alexander frowned, slowly straightening up as he walked toward the balcony.

On the balcony.

Evelyn was being held in the corner, a hand clamped over her mouth. She struggled fiercely but had no strength!

Just then, she saw Alexander walking toward her. Evelyn's eyes lit up, and she stretched out her foot to kick a nearby flowerpot.

If she could make a noise, Alexander would definitely find her!

But the moment her foot touched the pot, the person behind her spoke in a low voice. "I mean no harm. I just want to remind you that Evelyn Ross died because of you. If you want to get Alexander Vance killed too, then go ahead and reveal yourself!"

Evelyn froze!

Her outstretched foot hung in mid-air.

What did he mean Evelyn died because of her... Did this person mistake her for Grace Nan?

She remembered what Grace said when she woke up on the boat, crying. "...Evelyn, it was me coming to find you that led them here... I exposed you!"

Exposed...

So the danger wasn't brought by Grace. It had always been there!

Grace once explained that pretending to be her and marrying Alexander was to protect her... She said someone wanted to kill them...

Evelyn didn't believe it at the time.

This was a society governed by law. How could that be possible!

But now, Grace was really dead!

Evelyn had almost died too!

Some unknown danger had been lurking in the shadows all along, but she had been completely unaware...

Evelyn could sense the person behind her meant no harm. Otherwise, given her current physical state, he could have easily killed her with a knife. There was no need to say all this.

She stopped struggling. The person behind her slowly released his grip.

Both remained silent. They could feel Alexander staring at the balcony from inside the room for a long time before he finally turned and walked away.

The person behind her breathed a sigh of relief. The moment Evelyn turned her head, he jumped off the balcony.

Evelyn only saw a man in a black baseball cap and mask, blending into the crowd moving through the main hall...

Evelyn did not give chase.

She looked again at the balcony door.

Despite what that man said, she still decided to confess to Alexander. She couldn't let him continue to suffer in the pain of her death...

But before she could act, another knock came from Alexander's room. Thomas Watson spoke from outside. "Boss, Miss Sophia is calling again. She says she wants to see you, or she won't eat..."

"Then let her die!"

Alexander's words were measured, his voice cold and resolute!

Silence fell outside the door. No one dared to speak further.

Evelyn was also rooted to the spot.

Everything with Grace happened too suddenly. She inexplicably gained a sister, and that sister was inexplicably murdered!

These changes were too rapid, with too many twists. After experiencing a life-and-death situation, she had completely forgotten her original circumstances. Moments ago, she only thought about reuniting with Alexander as soon as possible...

She had even forgotten about Sophia Watson's existence.

She also forgot that, before the kidnapping, she and Alexander were on their way to get a divorce...

Evelyn smiled bitterly. It was like a bucket of cold water had been poured over her, jolting her awake.

Even if they revealed their identities to each other, what then?

It would only force Alexander into an even more difficult choice.

She was in danger. Alexander would not abandon her. But what about Sophia Watson?

Why cause trouble for others?

Thinking this, Evelyn lowered her eyes. She quietly climbed down from the balcony and headed toward the funeral hall.

She was very familiar with the path from the老夫人's courtyard to the main hall.

Looking at the familiar scenery around her, Evelyn's gaze grew profound.

She made a decision.

She would live as Grace Nan for now. Because only Grace Nan knew so many secrets. She would stay in San Francisco to investigate who kidnapped her and killed her sister!

She was never one to run away. She would never just wait for death.

She would slowly uncover this unknown danger and eliminate it completely!

Evelyn's gaze grew increasingly determined, a sharp glint appearing in her eyes!

She did not go to the funeral hall. Instead, she went to the parking lot.

Mrs. Davis was watching short videos, laughing heartily, completely ignoring Chloe.

Chloe had finished her milk from the bottle by herself. She was now sitting in her car seat, bored and playing with her fingers. She looked well-behaved and sensible.

Evelyn directly picked up two-year-old Chloe and walked toward the funeral hall.

Only then did Mrs. Davis notice her. Phone in hand, she followed behind. "Grace, what are you doing with the child? The funeral hall is a solemn place. If this child cries and ruins Zack's affairs, I won't let you off!"

Evelyn walked quickly, completely ignoring her threats.

She and Chloe arrived at the entrance of the funeral hall, looking from a distance at the ice coffin.

Evelyn lowered her eyes, put Chloe down, and said softly, "Good child, kowtow to the ice coffin."

Little Chloe didn't understand anything, but she was always obedient.

Without asking anything, the little girl directly knelt on the ground and kowtowed twice with a thud.

The little girl's eyes inexplicably reddened, tears welling up. Chloe stood up. "Mommy, I suddenly feel like crying..."

Evelyn stared straight ahead, her own eyes suddenly reddening. She clenched her fists tightly.

Sister, I will definitely avenge you!

I will definitely take good care of Chloe, and help you... take back everything that belongs to you!!

She bent down and picked up Chloe.

Mrs. Davis arrived at that moment, about to curse at her.

Evelyn stopped her with a calm sentence. "Are you sure you want to make a scene here?"

Mrs. Davis immediately looked around nervously, covering her mouth.

Then she glared at her viciously and directly snatched Chloe from her arms. "I'm taking the child back to the car. I'll settle this with you after we leave the Vance family!"

After Mrs. Davis left, Evelyn took a mask from her pocket and slowly put it on to avoid being recognized by others.

With the mask on, she turned to leave.

Mrs. Ashcroft hadn't come to her funeral. She must be unwell. She needed to go see her mother!

But as she turned, she saw Mr. Valentine standing behind her, his eyes wide with surprise.

Evelyn instinctively called out, "Handsome Uncle?!"

Mr. Valentine immediately came to her. "Little friend, it really is you!"
###Chapter 256

Recognition!

Mr. Valentine's gaze swept past her.

It finally settled on the ice coffin.

He studied Evelyn Ross with hesitation.

His brow furrowed slightly.

Evelyn still didn't know his identity. "Handsome Uncle, what's wrong?"

Mr. Valentine's expression was grave.

They had agreed to have dinner together.

Then Sophia Ashcroft suddenly appeared.

She said her daughter had been kidnapped and was drifting at sea.

He immediately mobilized all his personnel in San Francisco.

He even called for reinforcements from New York.

They searched the ocean day and night.

Finally, they retrieved a swollen corpse.

The body was unrecognizable.

Sophia denied it was her daughter after just one glance.

The police conducted a DNA test.

It confirmed the deceased's identity.

Sophia fainted on the spot.

She still hasn't woken up.

William Ashcroft has been by her side ever since.

He came today on Sophia's behalf to pay his respects.

He couldn't let the child leave this world alone.

He never expected to meet his young friend here.

He looked at Mrs. Vance. "You know Mrs. Vance?"

Evelyn lowered her eyes. "I was her high school classmate."

Mr. Valentine nodded. "I'm sorry for standing you up that day."

Evelyn's throat tightened. "I had something come up that day too."

A matter of life and death.

Mr. Valentine saw her pained expression. "Since you know Mrs. Vance, do you know Mrs. Ashcroft?"

Evelyn was taken aback. "Why?"

Mr. Valentine sighed. "She's not doing well. You should go see her."

Evelyn grew anxious. "What's wrong with Mrs. Ashcroft?"

Mr. Valentine nodded.

Evelyn immediately said, "I'll go right now!"

She turned to leave.

Zack Davis grabbed her arm. "Grace, don't run off!"

Evelyn frowned. "Mrs. Ashcroft isn't well. I'm going to visit her."

Zack's eyes lit up. "Finally coming to your senses? Making Mrs. Ashcroft happy will benefit the company!"

He hesitated. "But I'm not done here yet…"

All the industry big shots were here.

He couldn't bear to leave.

Evelyn said quickly, "You stay. I'll meet you at the hotel tonight."

"Alright."

Evelyn turned and left.

Zack was about to mingle.

A voice spoke up. "Is that your wife?"

Zack looked at Mr. Valentine.

Seeing his imposing presence, Zack smiled. "Yes, my wife Grace Nan. She was Mrs. Vance's high school classmate. They were very close. She came as soon as she heard the news."

Mr. Valentine caught it. "The surname Nan?"

Zack was startled. "Yes!"

Mr. Valentine frowned. "That's a rare surname."

"It certainly is…"

Mr. Valentine asked again, "Her father's surname is Nan?"

Zack became wary. "Who are you? Asking about private matters?"

Mr. Valentine extended his hand. "Valentine the Third."

Zack was stunned.

Then, ecstatic. "You're Mr. Valentine?! I didn't recognize you!"

…

Evelyn put on a mask as she left.

The driveway outside was congested.

There were too many mourners.

Everyone wanted to network.

Progress was slow.

The exit route was clear.

The entrance was completely blocked.

Ride-shares couldn't get in either.

Evelyn was anxious.

A car nearby could turn around and get out.

The car was very conspicuous.

James Klein's yellow sports car!

Evelyn walked over.

James was on the phone.

His eyes were red and swollen.

He had been crying hard.

"The boss is dead. I'm not in the mood for a meeting! The documents are important, but today is the funeral! Postpone all meetings!"

James rubbed his eyes.

He got out of the car, grumbling.

"These damn people, they're all just here for show! How many are genuinely mourning? Blocking the road so I can't get through!"

He yelled at Thomas Watson. "Why did you let everyone in? How many of these people are actually sincere?!"

Thomas sighed. "I know you're upset. I won't argue with you."

Evelyn got into the driver's seat.

She stepped on the gas and turned around.

The car changed lanes and exited the Vance estate.

James rushed over.

He got into the passenger seat.

He roared, "Who are you? Stealing my car?!"

Evelyn ignored him.

She drove toward the hospital.

James cried and cursed. "Unlucky! My boss dies and I lose my car? Car thief, pull over! I'm not in the mood to deal with this today…"

"Wahhh, my poor boss! Couldn't you pick someone else to steal from, car thief? I have no conscience? I'm the only one here sincerely mourning!"

Evelyn: "...Shut up!"

The familiar voice made James shut up.

The next moment, he cried even harder. "Your voice sounds just like the boss! If the boss were alive, she wouldn't let you make her angry…"

Evelyn took off her mask.

She looked at him.
###Chapter 257

James Klein immediately shouted, "You dare take off your mask, car thief? I'm taking a picture of your face for the police!"

His eyes were swollen and red from crying, barely able to open.

But the moment he clearly saw Evelyn Ross's face, his crying abruptly stopped.

"Huh..."

James's jaw dropped in shock, his trembling finger pointing at her. "You... you..."

Evelyn gave him a cool glance and put her mask back on.

James stared intently at her. After a long moment, he cautiously asked, "Boss, what's on your face? It's so yellow and scary. Ugly as hell."

Evelyn looked up at the sky speechlessly.

James suddenly burst into even louder sobs. "Wah... Boss, you're not dead! I've cried almost all my tears these past two days! I cried even harder than Alexander Vance!"

He was both crying and laughing, tears streaming uncontrollably.

Evelyn silently handed him a tissue. "Wipe it off. It's unsightly."

That familiar tone of disdain finally convinced James—his boss was truly alive!

As James's emotions settled slightly, Evelyn was about to speak when the roar of an engine approached from afar.

Alexander Vance's black Bentley shot past her side like an arrow!

Though it was just a flash, both clearly saw Alexander himself in the driver's seat.

James instantly bristled. "Damn! Where is Alexander going? Today is your funeral. He's not even coming to see you one last time?"

Evelyn froze momentarily.

She recalled Thomas Watson's words earlier—Sophia Watson was threatening suicide by starvation...

He had softened after all, hadn't he?

She understood. In Alexander's heart, she was already dead. The living always matter more than the dead.

Yet a fine, sharp pain still pierced her heart...

Seeing her dim expression, James flared up in anger. "He's going to see that lunatic again? Boss, how could he do this to you!"

James's eyes reddened with rage, his fists clenched tightly.

Evelyn held the steering wheel steadily, lowering her gaze. "I'm already dead. I can't let him lose another."

James was stunned. "Boss, what exactly happened? If you're not dead, who is in the coffin? The body was too disfigured by the water, but I specifically ran a DNA test..."

Evelyn's fingers tightened. "And the result?"

James scratched his head. "It said it was you!"

Evelyn's last shred of hope shattered.

Eleanor Vance might make a mistake, but James never would.

She pressed her lips together. "That wasn't me. It was my twin sister."

James was baffled. "What? Didn't Mrs. Ashcroft only give birth to one daughter back then? How is it twins?"

Evelyn's tone was calm. "I want to know that too."

She decided to temporarily assume her sister's identity to lure out the mastermind.

But she wouldn't fight alone. She chose to trust James, asking for his help in the investigation.

Though the first person she thought of was Alexander Vance...

James, having followed her for years, was used to her ways. He didn't ask further and stated directly, "What do you need me to do?"

Evelyn's eyes turned cold. "Find out who drugged us at the western restaurant that day and threw us on the boat!"

The perpetrator acted in San Francisco. There must be traces left behind.

She had operated in the city for years. Her courier company was the perfect intelligence network!

Find the culprit first, then follow the clues... uncover Grace Nan's secrets!

James responded immediately. "I already sent people to investigate! The restaurant's surveillance was deleted. We're checking nearby cameras now..."

He paused, then added, "The Vance family is also looking into this. But the culprit was very sly. Left almost no clues."

Evelyn was silent for a moment before speaking. "Change the direction of the investigation."

"To what?"

"That strange scented narcotic is very rare. There should be transaction records on the black market. See who purchased it recently."

James had a sudden realization. "Understood!"

"Also help me investigate Grace Nan's family background and social circle in New York."

Since she was going to impersonate, she needed to do her homework thoroughly.

They said no more. Evelyn asked for Mrs. Ashcroft's hospital address and drove there.

Getting out of the car, she put on a black baseball cap and mask.

Entering the hospital doors, her reflection in the glass showed her all-black outfit. Evelyn paused slightly.

Grace Nan had dressed exactly like this when she came to San Francisco to find her.

Suppressing her complex emotions, she quickly walked toward the inpatient wing.

She happened to overhear two nurses discussing Mrs. Ashcroft's condition.

"So tragic. Just found her daughter, only to lose her. The patient is heartbroken, almost doesn't want to live..."

"Being unconscious now, she can at least rest properly, right?"

"It's forced rest, I suppose..."

The nurses' conversation made Evelyn's heart clench.

She hurried to Mrs. Ashcroft's room. Seeing William Ashcroft leave to fetch water, she slipped inside immediately.

...She didn't trust William Ashcroft.

She had never felt any familial affection for this father.

She gently closed the door and turned toward the hospital bed—

Only to unexpectedly find an uninvited guest in the room.

Alexander Vance was standing by the bed, reviewing the medical chart.

At that moment, he turned and quietly watched Evelyn, clearly having witnessed her furtive movements.

Evelyn had a question mark over her head: ?

...Wasn't this man going to see Sophia Watson?

What was he doing here!

Just as she was silently complained, Alexander's gaze suddenly turned sharp.
###Chapter 258

Alexander Vance stared at the figure at the door.

She wore an oversized sweater, her silhouette indistinct.

But her gait made his heart skip a beat.

It was too familiar.

So familiar it stole his breath.

"Evelyn?" he asked, his voice hoarse.

The woman's eyelashes fluttered. Her voice was soft. "Do you know me?"

Alexander froze.

The woman looked down, timid. "I'm Grace. Evelyn Ross's high school classmate. I heard about what happened. I came to see Mrs. Ashcroft."

She deliberately softened her tone, imitating her sister's remembered cadence.

Alexander's eyes darkened.

He gave a self-mocking twist of his lips.

What was he thinking?

The DNA tests had been done again and again.

The results didn't lie.

His Evelyn would never be this timid and shrinking.

He clenched his fist, his voice low. "I was her husband."

Those two words were filled with blood and pain.

Evelyn felt a sharp sting in her chest.

She turned her face away, unable to look at his haggard profile.

"My condolences."

She walked quickly to the hospital bed.

The chart read 'stress-induced shock,' but no life-threatening danger.

Evelyn breathed a sigh of relief.

She leaned close to Mrs. Ashcroft's ear. Her voice was a whisper. "Mom, I'm alive. You need to be well. Wait for me to come back."

Mrs. Ashcroft's fingers twitched slightly.

Evelyn stood up and left.

She didn't look back.

She was afraid one more glance would make her lose control.

Not long after Evelyn left, William Ashcroft returned.

He looked calm, not as distraught as before.

Alexander spoke evenly. "Take care of yourself."

William nodded. "I will."

Alexander left the hospital room.

The moment the door closed, Mrs. Ashcroft sat up abruptly.

"Evelyn! Where is my Evelyn?"

William quickly held her. "Sophia, you just woke up. Don't get agitated."

Mrs. Ashcroft gripped his arm. "William, Evelyn isn't dead! She was just here. I heard her speak!"

William's expression was complicated. "Sophia, that was a dream..."

"It wasn't a dream!" Mrs. Ashcroft was emotional. "She was really here! She said she's still alive!"

William sighed, gently patting her back. "Alright, she's not dead. Our Evelyn isn't dead."

Mrs. Ashcroft gradually calmed down.

She insisted on checking the surveillance footage.

William had no choice. He called a nurse.

Half an hour later, the footage was sent to his phone.

The video fast-forwarded.

Alexander keeping vigil in the room.

William leaving to fetch water.

Then—

A girl in a baseball cap and mask slipped into the room.

Mrs. Ashcroft's tears fell instantly.

"It's Evelyn! She's really alive!"
###Chapter 259

Sophia Ashcroft's fingers trembled.

She pointed at her phone screen, sobbing uncontrollably.

William Ashcroft set his phone down.

He patted her back gently. "The person in the video is covered up. It might not be Evelyn. Don't panic. I'll investigate immediately."

"Go now…"

Sophia pushed William out of the room.

William left the ward with a frown.

Sophia thought for a moment.

She doubted William could identify the girl. The person wore a baseball cap and a mask. Her face was completely hidden.

Her daughter had returned but refused to acknowledge her. Evelyn must know the killer was nearby!

Sophia clenched her fists.

She retrieved her phone from the bedside table.

She opened WhatsApp.

She found the contact she had added just two days ago: Marcus Valentine.

She had lived a secluded life in San Francisco for years. She lacked the means to protect herself. Even if she never wanted to see Marcus again, she had no choice now.

She typed quickly: [Can you do me a favor?]

Marcus replied instantly: [Anything you ask. Just name it.]

Seeing those eight words, Sophia's fingers paused.

She pressed her lips together, emotions swirling inside her.

Those past events were buried deep. She had intended to never have any contact with him again. But for her daughter…

Sophia took a deep breath.

She continued typing: [I have news about our daughter. I need your help…]

As she was typing, the ward door suddenly opened.

Sophia looked up.

William had returned.

She asked casually, "Did you find anything?"

William walked toward her, his brow furrowed. "I've sent people to investigate. Sophia, don't worry…"

His gaze fell on her phone. "Who are you messaging?"

Sophia had nothing to hide from Marcus. She turned the screen toward William. "I thought our resources might be insufficient. I planned to ask Mr. Valentine for help. This concerns Evelyn's life."

She let William glance at the chat interface.

She was about to continue typing. "With his involvement, we should find Evelyn faster…"

Before she could finish, her phone was snatched away.

Sophia looked up in surprise.

William had taken her phone. Worry clouded his features. "Sophia, you don't trust me?"

Sophia was taken aback by his unusual mood.

She was silent for a moment. "William, that's not what I meant. Someone is trying to kill Evelyn. I want to protect her. That's why I asked Marcus for help…"

"And then what?" William sat by the bed, his eyes sorrowful. "Marcus helps you find Evelyn. He sees her face, so similar to her mother's. He will realize she is his child. Given his nature, he will take you both by force. I am not strong enough, Sophia. I can't compete with him."

Sophia smiled bitterly. "I won't go with him."

"But he is domineering! Once he knows Evelyn's parentage, he will never let go!"

William's voice trembled.

Sophia sensed his anxiety. She held his hand. "I am old and faded now. The follies of youth should be over. William, you are overthinking this…"

"I am not!"

William stood up abruptly. "Sophia, you are not old at all! To me, you are always as unattainable as a fairy! The way Marcus looked at you was the same! He never married all these years. He clearly still has feelings for you!"

Sophia frowned and clenched her fists.

William's eyes reddened seeing her reaction. "Sophia, I want to ask you a question."

Sophia spoke gently. "Go ahead."

"We have been together for twenty-two years, Sophia. Even though you never let me touch you, in my heart, you have always been my wife. I love you more than myself. But you? Sophia, have you ever loved me?"

Sophia was stunned.

William stared at her intently. "When we got together, you said you only wanted a surname for the child. I said I didn't expect your love. I only wanted to stay by your side. But human hearts are greedy, Sophia. Tell me, after all these years, have you ever loved me, even for a moment?"

Sophia sighed. "William, at our age, why talk about love?"

"But I love you! All these years, my love has never diminished! I thought this life was fine. Even if our daughter was locked up, I didn't care. As long as you stayed with me…"

William ran his hands through his hair agitatedly.

He paced around the ward. "But once Marcus appeared, everything changed! Sophia, you are different! Since you woke up last time, you often stare out the window. You look at your phone, wanting to call Marcus! Even… the way you look at him has changed! That unexpected meeting, there was no hatred in your eyes. Only deep longing, as if you would leave with him any second!"

William rushed to the bedside.

His eyes were red. His whole body shook. "Now when you have a problem, the first person you think of is still him! Do you also want to tell him that she is his daughter? Sophia, I have protected you for twenty-two years, four months, and three days! All these days and nights, couldn't they warm your heart even a little?! Have you… have you ever loved me even a tiny bit?!"

He looked as sorrowful as an abandoned child.

Sophia was stunned. Her jaw tightened. She was not moved by his words.

Her eyes gradually cooled. "William, that time I met Marcus with Evelyn, I didn't know it was him. How did you know how I looked at him?"

William was speechless.

Sophia suddenly understood. "Were you watching me?"

She remembered meeting Marcus. They were about to introduce themselves when a kidnapping call interrupted them…

Sophia's pupils constricted.

She looked at him in disbelief. "Was it you… mmm!"

William lunged forward and covered her mouth. "Sophia, don't say it!"

Sophia's eyes widened in shock.

She struggled fiercely but couldn't push the man away. She bit his finger hard.

William cried out in pain and released her.

William gasped, looking at the bite marks and blood on his hand.

He was about to speak when Sophia said decisively:

"It was you. You tried to kill Evelyn!"
###Chapter 260

Help!

William Ashcroft froze.

He lowered his bleeding hand.

His gaze fell on Sophia Ashcroft's face.

Her eyes were clear and calm.

Any excuse would sound hollow.

He clenched his fists.

A bitter smile twisted his lips. "Sophia, I did all of this for you."

Sophia was on the verge of a breakdown. "For me? By trying to kill my daughter? William, you're insane!"

William suddenly lunged forward.

He pinned her hands down forcefully.

Weak from two days in a coma, Sophia was helpless.

She panicked. "What are you doing?"

"We've been married for twenty-two years, and you never let me touch you," William said, his voice low. "Today, I'm just exercising my rights as a husband."

He buried his face in her neck.

His body trembled with suppressed emotion.

Years of pent-up desire finally erupted.

He took a deep breath, a satisfied smile on his face.

Sophia struggled desperately. "Let me go! You're crazy!"

"I'm not crazy!" William's voice was pained. "Twenty-two years. Even a stone would have warmed by now. But your heart remains cold."

Sophia closed her eyes. "I made it clear from the beginning. I would never develop feelings for you."

William chuckled darkly. "At first, just being able to look at you was enough. But human hearts are greedy… Especially watching you carry Alexander's child… How I wished it was ours?"

A sudden realization dawned on Sophia. "You knew all along that Linda switched the babies?"

Every newborn has an identification band.

How could Linda have managed that alone back then?

William saw no reason to hide it anymore. "I hinted that I would only acknowledge your child. She came up with the idea herself. I just helped clean up the mess."

"Madman! You're a madman!" Tears streamed down Sophia's face.

She never imagined the man who had been by her side for twenty-two years could be so monstrous.

"You drove me to this," William said, starting to tear at her clothes.

A cold dread washed over Sophia.

She fought back with all her might, but it was useless.

William tormented her slowly.

He never went all the way.

Sophia just felt utterly cold.

William stared at her. "Over twenty years without intimacy. Do you even want it?"

Her gaze was clear. "I only feel disgust."

The word "disgust" stung William.

He jerked upright.

Sophia felt a moment of relief.

"Smack!"

A sharp slap landed on her face.

Her vision blurred, her ears rang.

William's voice sounded distant. "If gentleness doesn't work, we'll try another method. Remember Evelyn before she was fifteen? How obedient she was in the Ashcroft household…"

Images flashed in Sophia's mind.

Linda would frequently hit and scold Evelyn.

She often withheld food.

The servants said the abuse was worse in private.

There was even a whip specifically for her…

The little child curling up and begging for mercy…

Sophia clenched her fists. "William, you're a pervert!"

She struggled toward the door.

William yanked her back.

The hospital door was so close, yet out of reach.

Sophia screamed at the top of her lungs. "Help! Nurse, help!"

William laughed softly. "I told them you're mentally unstable from the shock. No one will come."

Sophia collapsed to the floor.

William looked down at her.

Finally, he had dragged her down from her pedestal.

A perverse sense of satisfaction surged within him.

A phone suddenly rang.

Sophia looked toward the bedside.

William grabbed the phone first.

A WhatsApp message from Alexander popped up:

[What kind of help?]

[Talk to me.]

[Are you okay?]

[I'm coming to the hospital!]

Alexander called directly.

William's pupils constricted. "You still have his contact!"

He ended the call harshly.

He pulled out his belt and swung it through the air.

"Crack!"

William's smile was manic. "Want to try the taste of the whip?"

Outside the hospital.

A screech of tires echoed.

Alexander burst out of the car and sprinted upstairs.
###Chapter 261

Something was wrong.

Mr.Valentine's face was tense.

He rushed up the stairs, heading straight for the hospital room.

Sophia Ashcroft never asked for help.

The last time she begged him was when her daughter went missing.

Now she had sent another plea for assistance...

His heart raced. No replies to his messages. No answer to his calls. Had that killer targeted her again?

Mr.Valentine's eyes reddened.

He charged to the room and shoved the door open.

William Ashcroft blocked the entrance.

Mr.Valentine halted.

William spoke slowly. "Third Master, what brings you here?"

Mr.Valentine's voice was urgent. "Where is Sophia?"

William coughed. "She's freshening up."

Mr.Valentine froze.

William wore an apologetic look. "Sophia just woke up. She felt uncomfortable and is showering. It's not a good time for visitors."

Mr.Valentine glanced toward the bathroom.

Behind the frosted glass door, water rushed loudly.

He couldn't see the chaos inside.

Sophia's hospital gown was torn. A cloth gagged her mouth. Her hands and feet were bound.

Hot water drenched her entire body.

She struggled to make noise, but the sound was swallowed by the water.

Outside, Mr.Valentine looked away, still uneasy. "She just messaged me saying she needed help."

William nodded. "We sent it together."

He pointed to the hallway.

Mr.Valentine stepped back.

William exited the room and closed the door.

He faced Mr.Valentine directly. "You and Sophia agreed to never meet again. You already broke that promise last time. Sophia just lost her daughter. She's emotionally fragile. She asked me to tell you to return to New York immediately. Do not set foot in San Francisco again."

Mr.Valentine was stunned.

He instinctively looked toward the room.

William blocked his view. "Third Master, this is her only request. Please don't stay and see her in such a state."

Mr.Valentine's jaw tightened. His fists clenched.

William said calmly, "You just promised her you would grant any wish. Third Master, are you going back on your word now?"

Mr.Valentine glared at him. "You read our messages?"

William smiled. "Twenty-two years of marriage. We have no secrets between us. Third Master, please do not disturb our lives."

Do not disturb her.

That was indeed Sophia's consistent attitude.

Twenty-two years.

He knew she was in San Francisco, but never visited, afraid to make her uncomfortable...

Even wanting to know how she was doing, he had to ask Alexander Vance to check for him.

Mr.Valentine thought Sophia was truly heartless...

But she had always been this way.

In the past. And now.

Mr.Valentine stepped back with a bitter smile. His voice was hoarse. "Fine. I'll leave. If Sophia ever needs anything in the future, just ask."

William nodded. "Thank you, Third Master. Evelyn is gone. Sophia is exhausted. As long as you do not disturb us, we can live in peace..."

Husband and wife...

The words stabbed Mr.Valentine's heart.

He nodded reluctantly and turned to leave.

William watched his retreating back, a meaningful smile appearing.

He turned, entered the room, and locked the door.

He walked to the bathroom and pushed the door open.

He turned off the water, took a towel, and dried Sophia's hair.

"Sophia, you need to dry off. Don't catch a cold..."

His voice was still gentle, but Sophia trembled violently!

William carried her out of the bathroom and placed her on the hospital bed.

He removed her soaked pajamas and threw them in the trash.

He dried her body with the towel, revealing the vicious whip marks on her back.

William took out ointment and applied it gently.

He whispered meticulously, "Sophia, does it hurt? Blowing on it will make it better..."

Sophia closed her eyes. The gag was removed from her mouth.

She begged softly, "William Ashcroft, just kill me."

"I love you so much. How could I bear to kill you? Sophia, I did all this just to keep you..."

William finished applying the medicine and took out her everyday clothes.

"Sophia, we're going home. The hospital has too many people. It's inconvenient..."

A light sparked in Sophia's eyes.

If she could leave this room...

Just as hope rose, a sharp pain stabbed her arm!

Sophia turned her head and saw a needle piercing her skin.

Her entire body went limp instantly, collapsing into William's arms.

"You... drugged me..."

Her tongue felt heavy. Her words were slurred.

William smiled. "Sophia, this is a muscle relaxant. You will be very obedient now..."

Sophia's pupils contracted.

She watched helplessly as she was dressed like a doll.

William picked her up and strode out the door.

A nurse saw them and asked, "Mr. Ashcroft, what's happening?"

William sighed. "Sophia just woke up and insisted on going home. I'm taking her back..."

The nurse nodded. "Mr. Ashcroft, you are so devoted. Mrs. Ashcroft, you must stay strong. What would happen to Mr. Ashcroft if something happened to you? Last time you fainted, he stayed by your side without eating or drinking. We were all so moved!"

She looked at William. "Mr. Ashcroft, you must take care of yourself too."

William replied gently, "Don't worry. I will take care of myself. I still need to grow old with Sophia..."

Hearing this, Sophia suddenly felt clear-headed.

William's affection might be real. That's why his disguise was perfect.

He had fooled everyone. Even she hadn't detected the darkness inside him!

Who would have thought he was the one who killed Evelyn?!

She watched helplessly as William carried her downstairs and into a car.

They didn't notice Mr.Valentine frowning in a corner of the parking lot, watching the car drive away.

After a long moment, Mr.Valentine opened WhatsApp and found that chat interface.

[Kid, I think something is wrong with Mrs. Ashcroft! Can you go check on her for me?]
###Chapter 262

Marcus Valentine observed his uncle’s expression and spoke cautiously. “Uncle, since Mrs. Sophia doesn’t wish to see you, perhaps you should return for now. She’s emotionally unstable. Your presence might only upset her further.”

The Third Master shot him a cold glance.

Marcus immediately realized his mistake and cleared his throat. “You mentioned something felt off earlier. What exactly seems wrong?”

The Third Master lowered his gaze. “Ms. Sophia has always been strong-willed. Her daughter was murdered, and the killer remains at large. Why would she ask me to leave? When she sent that message, I found it strange. Given her character, she should have asked for my help in tracking down the culprit.”

Sophia Ashcroft was never a weak woman.

Even with her poor health and frequent fainting spells, her inner resilience remained unshaken.

Marcus looked puzzled. “Then why not just go in and ask her directly?”

The Third Master gave him another sharp look.

Marcus’s eyes widened in realization. “No way? Uncle, you fear nothing, yet you’re actually afraid of making Mrs. Sophia angry?”

The Third Master fell silent.

He seriously considered throwing his nephew out at that moment.

He was indeed concerned that this might be Sophia’s genuine wish. Barging in could risk alienating her further.

The Third Master sighed and glanced at his phone.

There had been no reply to his message.

It reminded him of the last time he had stood someone up. He had been preoccupied accompanying Sophia to the coast to search for someone. Amid the tense wait during the salvage operation, he had suddenly remembered his prior engagement and sent a hurried apology.

That young friend had never responded either.

He had assumed she was upset with him. But when they met later at the Vance family’s memorial service, she hadn’t seemed to hold any grudge.

The Third Master couldn’t quite figure it out. However, an idea soon came to him. He directly messaged “Zack Davis”: 【Where are you staying?】

Zack replied immediately: 【The Four Seasons Hotel, Third Master. Is there something you need?】

The Third Master: 【I’ll come find you. I’d like to treat your entire family to dinner tonight.】

It would serve as an apology for last time’s cancellation.

…

After visiting Mrs. Sophia, Evelyn Ross gave James Klein several directives, emphasizing the urgency of finding the killer.

As they parted, James asked, “Boss, when do you plan to resume your true identity?”

Evelyn frowned. “After we find the killer.”

Only by identifying the murderer and their motive, ensuring all was safe, could she return to living as herself.

Evelyn headed to the Four Seasons Hotel.

She had just located the room where Zack and the others were staying when she found Zack and his mother excitedly picking out clothes. Mrs. Davis was even holding up a little dress for Chloe, meticulously planning her outfit.

Seeing Evelyn, Zack lifted his chin. “Good, you’re back. Change into something more formal. Don’t embarrass me tonight.”

Evelyn paused slightly. “Dinner?”

Zack’s face lit up with excitement. “Yes! The Third Master and I hit it off. He’s treating us to dinner tonight! Do you even know who the Third Master is? He’s the head of the Valentine family, one of the five great elite families of New York!”

Mrs. Davis chimed in with flattery. “Son, you’re truly remarkable. You said coming to San Francisco might lead to meeting important people, and you actually connected with someone! The Valentine family of New York! We usually only see figures like that in financial magazines. He must see great potential in you!”

Hearing the name ‘Third Master,’ Evelyn was momentarily taken aback.

The Third Master had tried to meet her several times before, but she had always put him off. Ironically, now that she had assumed Grace Nan’s identity, the opportunity had arisen.

But how could someone of Zack’s caliber possibly have impressed the Third Master?

She glanced at Zack. With his refined and scholarly appearance, he certainly had a pleasing exterior—no wonder he had managed to deceive Grace Nan.

Inside, however, he was utterly useless!

Evelyn’s look of disdain made Zack snort. “I told you I have a talent for business. Today, I discussed economic trends with the Third Master, and he listened patiently. Now he’s invited us to dinner. He must appreciate my insights!... Hurry up, don’t waste time. We can’t keep the Third Master waiting.”

Evelyn said nothing more.

Since she had decided to use Grace Nan’s identity to investigate her sister’s death, she would play along for now.

She lowered her head and went inside to change into a appropriate wool coat.

She then entered the bathroom to carefully wash the iodine from her face…

Although the stain wouldn’t fade completely immediately, it at least looked less yellow and unsightly than during the day.

After changing, the group headed downstairs.

Zack started the car, setting the navigation for “Le Jardin Secret.”

Mrs. Davis asked, “What kind of place is Le Jardin Secret?”

Zack said proudly, “It’s a famous private dining club in San Francisco. Rumor has it the owner is Mrs. Vance. It’s incredibly hard to get a reservation! The city’s elite all dine there. The fact that the Third Master is hosting us there shows how highly he thinks of me!”

Mrs. Davis shot a glare at Evelyn. “Son, I always knew you’d go far. You were the most outstanding in our village. It’s a shame you became a live-in son-in-law for Grace! That title doesn’t sound good. Grace, from now on, tell everyone you married into our family! And that little burden—change her surname to Davis as soon as we return to New York, or I won’t acknowledge her as my granddaughter!”

Zack added, “Changing a girl’s surname isn’t that important. Grace, when we get back to New York, transfer the company shares and legal representative status to me. Also, add my name to the property deed…”

Evelyn sneered inwardly but remained silent.

Since she was using her sister’s identity, she would reclaim everything that rightfully belonged to her sister.

But for now, she couldn’t be bothered to argue.

Mrs. Davis, seeing her lack of response, grew angry. “What’s your attitude? Right now, you’re eating off Zack, living off Zack, even getting to dine at Le Jardin Secret is thanks to him! You will make those changes when we get back, understand?”

Evelyn’s eyes turned cold. Zack cut in, “Alright, Mom, that’s enough. We’re here.”

Mrs. Davis glared fiercely at her. “Hold that little burden properly. Behave well later. Don’t get dazzled by the wealth and embarrass my son, or you’ll answer to me when we get home!”

After parking the car, they had just gotten out when someone approached them. “Mr. Davis and family? The Third Master has been waiting in the private room for some time.”

Zack put on an ingratiating smile. “Yes, yes, that’s us. Please, lead the way…”

The Third Master’s assistant held a significant position within the Valentine family. Even minor elite families wouldn’t dare slight him.

The assistant smiled and guided them inside.

Zack and Mrs. Davis tread carefully as they entered the main hall of Le Jardin Secret.

The decor was classically elegant, with small bridges and flowing water, exuding a refined and lofty atmosphere.

Mrs. Davis warned in a low voice, “Grace, hold that burden properly. Everything here is expensive. If you break something, selling you wouldn’t cover the cost!”

Evelyn let out a scornful laugh.

They arrived at the door of the private room. Zack nervously adjusted his clothes, cast a proud glance at Evelyn, and pushed the door open.

Evelyn glanced inside casually and saw a mature man sitting within.

He was brewing tea. Hearing the door open, he looked up.

His every movement exuded an air of nobility that commanded respect.

Zack’s face was full of sycophantic讨好 (fawning) admiration. Mrs. Davis didn’t dare breathe too loudly.

Only Evelyn was slightly stunned. She instinctively called out, “Handsome Uncle?”
###Chapter 263

Zack Davis was about to leave.

A cool voice called out from behind him. "Handsome Uncle."

Everyone said Mr. Valentine's WhatsApp was extremely difficult to get.

Her mother absolutely did not want to see him.

They added each other on WhatsApp again.

She didn't seem very good at all...

She truly couldn't answer that question.

The surname Nan was indeed rare.

She only knew that Grace Nan had adoptive parents, but she wasn't clear on the specifics.

Before she could respond, Zack hurriedly interjected. "It's from her adoptive father!"

Mr. Valentine was stunned. "Adoptive father?"

Zack forced a smile. "Yes, she was an abandoned infant. Thrown away right after birth. Later adopted by her foster parents. She's lived in New York all her life. After her adoptive parents passed, they left her a villa and a small company..."

Both adoptive parents were dead?

Evelyn was also surprised.

No wonder Grace Nan had no one to rely on and was easily manipulated.

Mr. Valentine, being who he was, had already sensed the微妙 relationship between Evelyn and Zack.

He simply sipped his tea, passing the teacups to everyone in turn.

After the tea, the dishes from Le Jardin Secret were served one after another.

Mr. Valentine invited everyone to move to the dining table.

Seeing the table full of delicacies, Zack quickly flattered. "Sir, the food here is absolutely amazing! We've never had such authentic San Francisco cuisine in New York! This must be the work of Le Jardin Secret's head chef, right?"

The waiter sighed softly. "Our head chef... was Mrs. Vance. They say she interned under a state banquet chef during her training and learned the real skills. That's why her cooking was exceptional. But Mrs. Vance only cooked one table per month. It was impossible to get a reservation before... What a pity..."

The waiter lowered his head.

The restaurant owner had always been very mysterious. They had never seen them.

It wasn't until the news of Mrs. Vance's death came that the manager broke down in tears, and they learned the truth.

He looked at Evelyn. "You went to see Mrs. Ashcroft today. How is she?"

Evelyn was startled. "What favor?"

Mr. Valentine shook his head. "I'm not sure. I went to the hospital but didn't see her. Later, she was discharged."

Evelyn became agitated. "Mrs. Ashcroft is so weak. How could she be discharged?"

Mr. Valentine noticed her unusual reaction and looked at her curiously.

Evelyn realized she had lost her composure and coughed lightly. "I just think it's illogical."

Evelyn immediately stood up. "I have time now."

She looked at Zack. Before she could speak, Zack hurriedly waved his hand. "Attending to Sir's matters is more important. You should go!"

Evelyn nodded and turned to leave quickly.

She could hide the truth from everyone.

But she could never hide it from her mother.
###Chapter 264

Evelyn Ross hailed a taxi after leaving Le Jardin Secret.

The night was deep and dark.

She watched the fleeting street scenes outside the window, her fingers tightening slightly.

Too much had happened today.

In the past, she only wanted a stable, peaceful life.

Now, she had to find the real culprit and reclaim the life that belonged to her.

Alexander Vance stood in the darkness.

He looked haggard, with deep shadows under his eyes. A cold aura surrounded him.

He had been investigating near the restaurant all day.

All surveillance footage had been erased. There were no leads.

His wife had vanished into the sea.

After their reunion, she had become a complete mama's girl.

Even if she was gone, he would take care of her mother for her.

It was this Grace Nan again.

Her concern for Mrs. Ashcroft was excessive.

She didn't dare stop this distinguished guest.

Her baseball cap hid her eyes. Her mask covered her face.

But her height was similar to Evelyn's.

Evelyn quickly followed.

Evelyn pointed at Alexander. "I'm with him."

His intuition told him this woman meant no harm.

He had been too rough today. The marks couldn't be shown.

###Chapter 265

Remove Your Mask!

###Chapter 266

Evelyn feigned hurt. "Zack, Mom, I didn't…"

"Still lying? We saw everything! You were practically throwing yourself into his arms! Shameless!"

Mrs. Davis shoved Evelyn. She then snatched Chloe from Zack's arms and sneered. "Useless girl. Look how cheap your mother is. Don't you grow up to be like her!"

Chloe reached out her small hands, calling out in a milky voice. "Mommy, up…"

Evelyn reclaimed the child, explaining softly. "He said I looked suspicious wearing a mask… He wanted to check…"

Zack was half-convinced, half-doubting. "Really?"

Evelyn looked at Alexander, her tone pleading. "Mr. Vance, could you please explain? My husband has misunderstood…"

Alexander watched the scene unfold.

The masked woman's expression was unreadable. But Zack, his mother, even the child—none of them seemed to be lying.

Could she truly not be Evelyn?

The DNA report for that body was still at home…

Had he been refusing to accept reality, deceiving himself all this time?

Zack assessed Alexander. Though he seemed distraught, his bearing was undeniably refined. Zack's tone softened slightly. "My wife's face is injured. She applied iodine and can't show it, hence the mask. Please don't take offense."

Alexander looked at Evelyn.

The skin visible around the edge of the mask did appear yellowish.

Was that why she wore the mask?

A profound sense of disappointment washed over Alexander.

He clenched his fists, his voice hoarse. "My apologies."

With that, he turned and left.

His retreating figure looked both wretched and heartbroken.

Zack curled his lip. "Weirdo."

He turned and saw Evelyn staring after the departing man. Annoyed, he snapped, "What are you looking at? He's a scion of a wealthy family. You think he'd be interested in someone like you?"

According to Grace's usual character, she should be meekly begging for forgiveness.

But Evelyn merely gave him a cool glance, then turned and carried the child back to the car.

That look sent a chill down Zack's spine.

He snorted coldly but said nothing more.

The group arrived at the hotel.

Evelyn was pondering how to get rid of Zack that night when he proactively stated, "You sleep with Chloe. I'll stay with Mom."

Evelyn was taken aback.

They had specifically booked two queen rooms!

He was going to share a room with his mother?

Mrs. Davis lifted her chin defiantly. "What are you staring at? Zack always sleeps with me at home. Why should a hotel change that?"

Evelyn was speechless.

Zack gave a thin, unpleasant smile. "Grace, my dad died young. Mom has slept beside me my whole life. Don't make a scene. It's embarrassing."

Evelyn found this mother-son pair utterly absurd.

She picked up Chloe and entered the other room.

Never having cared for a child before, she searched online for guidance. But she found Chloe remarkably well-behaved.

She didn't cry or fuss. She bathed herself, climbed into bed with her bottle, and patted her own bottom to lull herself to sleep.

Evelyn's heart ached watching her.

Such懂事ness spoke volumes about past hardships.

It wouldn't be like this anymore.

As long as she was here, Chloe would never suffer again.

She lay down but tossed and turned, unable to sleep.

Thinking of Sophia Ashcroft lying comatose, something felt off.

Her mother's coma this time was just too strange.

Evelyn didn't sleep a wink all night.

Dawn gradually broke.

Today was the day of Mrs. Vance's cremation and burial.

The Ashcroft Residence.

William Ashcroft sent Nancy Ashcroft away, then turned to look at Sophia.

She had regained some strength and was glaring at him fiercely.

William approached with a syringe, his tone gentle. "Be good. After this injection, I'll take you to see Evelyn off."

Sophia shook her head, her eyes full of entreaty.

William stroked her cheek. "Just bear with it a little longer… After today, we'll leave. No more medication, alright?"

His gentle tone made Sophia tremble.

The needle pierced her skin.

Icy liquid flowed into her veins.

Sophia lost all strength.

Her eyes reddened as she continued to glare fixedly at William.

William lightly kissed her lips. "Sophia, you finally belong completely to me…"

His hand slowly trailed downward, closing around her ankle.

His breathing grew ragged.

A tear slid from the corner of Sophia's eye.

William wiped it away. "Still unwilling to give yourself to me? Sophia, when will you finally accept me…"

He suddenly pinched her chin, forcing her head up, and bit down hard on her shoulder!

Sophia cried out in pain.

Nancy's voice came from outside the door. "Sir, Madam, everything is prepared."

Sophia looked pleadingly at William.

William chuckled softly, patting her cheek. "Let's go see Evelyn off first. Tonight… I will have you."

Sophia trembled all over.

She clenched her fist, as if overcome with humiliation.

William didn't notice the small piece of paper clutched tightly in her palm.

He picked her up and carried her out.
###Chapter 267

Inside the car.

No one suspected William.

Everyone in San Francisco knew him as the famously devoted husband.

Twenty-two years and four months of maintaining that image had made it impervious.

William wheeled her into the funeral hall.

Grandmother Eleanor still needed him.

Facing Evelyn's funeral for the first time.

Memories flooded back.

Their first meeting at the Ashcroft residence, her raising an eyebrow and calling him "husband."

Seeing her again at the company, standing defiantly in her delivery uniform.

Their reunion at the restaurant, wearing an apron and inviting him with a smile.

And when she was angry, enunciating his full name deliberately.

Her voice seemed to echo in his ears.

But that person now lay in a cold coffin, unrecognizable.

Guests approached to offer condolences, but he remained silent.

Evelyn still wore her mask and baseball cap.

Realizing the man from last night was Alexander Vance.

Alexander Vance, the richest man in San Francisco?

Harassing his wife?

Sophia recognized her daughter instantly.

Evelyn!

This was definitely her Evelyn!

A stroke?

###Chapter 268

William Ashcroft.

He even confessed his obsessive possessiveness toward Sophia.

"Only the useless Grace Nan was allowed to live..."

William's breathing grew weaker.

In his final moments, he murmured to himself.

"Sophia... Did you ever love me..."

His hand went limp.

His eyes stared forever at the ceiling.

Evelyn stood up.

She called the police to handle the aftermath.

William Ashcroft sent a message.

The helicopter had vanished over the sea.

There was no trace to follow.

Evelyn gripped her phone tightly.

Sophia's surname was Nan.

Grace Nan's surname was also Nan.

This was no coincidence.

She had to keep pretending to be Grace Nan.

It was the only way to lure out the mastermind.

The night was deep.

Evelyn's gaze was resolute.

She would save her mother.

Her phone suddenly vibrated.

A WhatsApp message from Zack Davis urged her on.

Evelyn replied succinctly.

She walked toward the elevator.

Her thoughts were on her daughter.

Unaware that little Chloe was no longer in the room.

Alexander Vance stood in the center of the living room.

An oppressive aura radiated from him.

Zack Davis smiled obsequiously.

"Mr. Vance, she will be here any moment."

The lock clicked softly.

Evelyn pushed the door open.

Alexander's head snapped up.

His eyes, sharp as eagles, locked onto her.
###Chapter 273

It's really you!

Evelyn Ross walked to the door.

Mrs. Davis stood there.

She stepped forward quickly. "Is Chloe asleep?"

"Asleep."

Mrs. Davis avoided her gaze. She took out a bottle. "Chloe refused to drink tonight. Taste this. Is it spoiled?"

Evelyn narrowed her eyes.

She removed her mask. She took a sip without letting the milk touch her lips.

"It's fine."

Mrs. Davis waved her hand. "She must be full then. We'll change brands tomorrow... Don't waste the rest."

Evelyn hadn't eaten since morning.

She asked softly, "Has Mrs. Vance been buried?"

Mrs. Davis sounded impatient. "Buried! Do you really think you're her classmate? Drink it!"

Evelyn's eyes flickered. She finished the milk in a few gulps.

She was about to wash the bottle. Mrs. Davis snatched it away.

"I'll wash it. You've worked all day. Rest early."

Mrs. Davis took the bottle back to her room.

Something was definitely wrong.

Evelyn had a resistance to drugs.

She unlocked the door. She had only taken two steps inside.

Zack Davis walked toward her.

His usual coldness was gone. He smiled warmly. "You're back?"

Evelyn frowned.

Zack laughed. "Go rest... I won't disturb you two!"

He slipped past her. As he left, he dropped a line. "Chloe is with me. Serve the man in that room well!"

Evelyn grabbed his arm sharply.

She wanted to see what trick Zack was playing!

The moment she turned around, she froze.

A familiar figure sat on the sofa.

Alexander Vance!

Why was he here?

In her daze, Zack broke free and left.

Click.

The door locked.

Evelyn pulled hard on the door.

The sound of a chain wrapping around came from outside.

Zack had locked the door dead.

He clapped his hands. "Grace Nan, serve Mr. Vance well. The company can be saved! Don't you dare resist!"

Zack was immensely pleased with himself.

After meeting Mr. Valentine at the funeral, the man had ignored him.

When he noticed Grace's resemblance to Mrs. Vance, he hatched this plan.

Offer Grace to Mr. Vance. Then he could connect with the Vance family!

Mrs. Davis appeared behind him. "Son, will this work?"

Zack sneered. "It's just that between men and women. Grace is in poor health. She can't bear a son. We'll rely on Kelsey to continue the family line. That face of Grace's is finally useful. I'm not even afraid of being cuckolded. Why are you worried?"

Kelsey was his mistress. She was pregnant with a son.

Mrs. Davis was waiting to hold her grandson.

"That's not what I meant... What if Grace refuses?"

"Relax..." Zack said smugly. "They've both been drugged. That stuff is strong..."

Mrs. Davis asked another question. "Why do Grace and Mrs. Vance look so alike? Their resemblance shocked me when I saw the funeral photo... Are they twins?"

Zack rubbed his chin. "Grace is an orphan. She was adopted. They're probably twins. This funeral was worth attending!"

Mrs. Davis frowned. "If they're twins, couldn't we connect with Mr. Vance through that relationship? Why send her to his bed?"

Zack waved his hand. "His wife is dead. Why would he keep ties with her family? Grace's face is pretty. Offer her to Mr. Vance. We'll have future connections. Relying on Grace's relationship would mean looking to her for favors in the future. Now we hold evidence of her affair... Heh. I'll be in charge of this house from now on!"

Mrs. Davis's eyes lit up. "Son, you're so clever!"

They discussed in low voices as they returned to their room.

Inside the room.

Evelyn didn't hear their words. But she guessed Zack's plan.

She couldn't help but feel contempt.

Grace Nan, what kind of husband did you find!

"Evelyn... It's really you!"

A deep, hoarse voice came from behind.

Evelyn stiffened.

Her mask was long gone. There was no time to put it back on.

Alexander Vance had seen her face!

Evelyn clenched her fists. She turned slowly.

She looked at Alexander. Her eyes were full of longing.

Her mother had been taken.

Her sister was dead.

Her family had fallen apart.

Now, it seemed Alexander was all she had left.

This thought shattered her.

The burning heat inside her didn't want to be suppressed.

She had been holding back too much. She needed release!

Forget his unattainable love. Forget his savior.

Evelyn walked toward Alexander.

She had just taken a step. Her legs went weak.

She fell into Alexander's arms.

Alexander had been drugged too.

Seeing her face. Smelling her familiar scent. His reason collapsed.

He didn't restrain his longing. He lowered his head and kissed her lips fiercely.
###Chapter 274

Alexander Vance carried Evelyn Ross to the bed.

His tall frame covered hers.

Their cheeks pressed together, noses touching, breaths mingling, hot and intense.

He stared at her, afraid to blink, terrified she might vanish again.

His lips moved to her ear, his breath warming her sensitive skin.

His voice was hoarse with boundless joy. "Evelyn, you're finally back... You're really alive..."

His words made her heart ache.

She wrapped her arms tightly around his waist and kissed his chin.

The touch made him tremble.

He could no longer resist the drug's effects. He cupped her chin and kissed her deeply.

His burning hands gently but firmly undressed her...

She was fit and skilled, once imagining their wedding night as an equal match.

Half her body hung off the bed's edge...

He held her close, feeling her real presence, terrified she might leave again...

He was still asleep, exhausted, his fingers still interlaced with hers.

On the road back to New York.

How was the connection made?

Mrs. Davis nodded. "Right... But when I entered the room this morning, it looked pretty intense... Grace is pretty and has a good figure..."

Zack sneered. "No matter how good, she's not Mrs. Vance. Who knows if Mr. Vance would break his routine and come to New York for her?"

Zack said disdainfully, "Mom, you don't know. Grace is dull and rigid. In bed, she's like a mummy. No excitement at all. I doubt she could hold Mr. Vance's attention. What a waste of that face."

They didn't notice a black Bentley speeding toward New York!!

###Chapter 275

###Chapter 276

Marcus Investigates DNA!

This explained why Grace never divorced him despite knowing about his affair.

Mina complained. "We've been friends for over a decade, but it couldn't compare to your three years with Zack. You were completely bewitched these past three years. You lost yourself in that marriage. Grace, I told you when you got married—he was after your money. Do you believe me now?"

"Yes."

Evelyn narrowed her eyes.

Zack could wait. The key was... Mina had known Grace since childhood?

Such a long-time friend... Mina must know about Grace's adoptive parents!

She looked at Mina and asked directly. "Mina, do you remember our secret?"

Mina immediately frowned.

She nervously glanced around and lowered her voice. "Of course I remember! What's wrong? Are they bothering you again?"

They...

Evelyn's pupils contracted. "Mina, did I ever tell you who they are?"

Mina frowned. "Why are you acting so strange today? Don't you remember?"

"Of course I do."

Evelyn remained expressionless. "I just wanted to see if you still remembered my business after six months without contact."

"How could I forget!" Mina said anxiously. "I always keep your matters close to my heart!"

"Then tell me, who are they? What do they usually come to me for?"
###Chapter 279

Mina’s brows instantly furrowed.

She stared at Grace.

Grace pressed her lips together, trying to appear gentle.

Mina looked her up and down, then suddenly stood and walked over.

She reached out to feel Grace’s forehead.

Grace was speechless.

“No fever. Why are you talking nonsense? Back then, you refused to say who they were. You said it would endanger me. Why are you asking me now?”

Grace experienced Evelynge’s meticulousness for the first time.

At least in this matter, she hadn’t made a mistake.

Grace let out a faint “oh,” her expression calm. “I was afraid you might have found out something. It seems you really don’t know. That’s for the best.”

Mina was successfully misled. “Grace, you’re too cautious. Didn’t you say you didn’t even complete the first task? They wouldn’t come looking for you again.”

Grace was startled. “The first task?”

Someone very important…

She couldn’t help complaining. “I considered you my best friend. We’ve been at the same school since kindergarten. All these years of friendship, and I come after Chloe in your heart. Fine. But I’m even less important than your mother-in-law and Zack. For them, you abandoned me… Forget it. I don’t want to talk about it. It still hurts.”

She was genuinely hurt.

Mina pouted.

Grace didn’t know how to comfort people. She remained silent.

The sound of the door opening came from the entrance.

Mrs. Davis hummed a square dance tune as she walked in.

She didn’t look at the living room and headed straight for the kitchen. “Grace, what’s for dinner? I’m starving after dancing!”

Entering the kitchen, she found it empty.

Mrs. Davis was taken aback. She walked out grumbling. “Grace, what were you doing all afternoon? It’s so late, and you haven’t made dinner?! You…”

She noticed Mina and sneered. “Well, if it isn’t Mina Sherwood? Haven’t seen you in half a year. What? Finally came to your senses? Here to deliver the cooperation agreement to Zack?”

Mina gave a sarcastic smile. “Mrs. Davis, half a year apart, and your bitterness has only grown.”

Mrs. Davis’s face darkened. “How dare you speak to me like that!”

Mina shrugged. “Just calling it as I see it.”

“You!”

Mrs. Davis clutched her chest in anger. She pointed at Grace and yelled, “Grace, are you just going to let her insult me like this? Let me tell you, her treating me this way means she doesn’t respect you! Otherwise, why would she make things difficult for you?”

“Grace, kick her out right now! And don’t you dare see her again, or I’ll tell Zack you let your friend come here and bully me!”

Mina’s jaw tightened.

She looked at Grace.

This was exactly what happened six months ago.

Zack and his mother’s words made Grace block her.

Later, she ran into Zack. He shamelessly said that if her family’s company cooperated with him, he would make Grace add her back.

Mina was furious.

She felt Zack was treating friendship like a commodity to be bargained with.

She was worried for Grace. She was frustrated that Grace was so blind to have fallen for someone like that.

But Mina was also afraid…

Afraid that Grace would be influenced by Mrs. Davis’s words again…

Mina didn’t dare speak.

Mrs. Davis became more arrogant. “What’s wrong? Cat got your tongue? You had plenty to say earlier.”

Mina took a deep breath.

Even though Grace mentioned divorce, she hadn’t fully committed yet. As anxious as she was, Mina knew this was Grace’s life. She couldn’t make decisions for her.

Not wanting to put Grace in a difficult position, Mina stood up. “Grace, I’ll head out. Contact me if you need anything.”

She started walking toward the door.

Grace followed. “Okay, I’ll see you out.”

Mina paused, her heart feeling empty.

Grace clearly hadn’t made up her mind yet.

Her jaw tightened. She turned back at the door. “Grace, you can always work hard again for anything else. But freedom is your own. Don’t forget what you said earlier.”

Grace nodded calmly. “No rush. I have a plan.”

How could Mina not be anxious?

She wanted to say more, but after their previous fallout, she was afraid of pushing too hard and getting blocked again. She held back her words and left with a conflicted expression.

After Mina left, Grace turned around.

Mrs. Davis sneered. “What are you looking at? Hurry up and make dinner! You’re so dull and clumsy. How can you compare to Kelsey? No wonder Zack never comes home!”

Grace’s eyes darkened.

The corners of her lips curled into a faint smile. “Okay, I’ll get right to it.”

Taking back everything that rightfully belonged to Evelynge was effortless for her. She already had a plan.

Chloe needed to eat too. Since that was the case…

Grace walked into the kitchen.

She quickly prepared three dishes and a soup. After setting the table, she went upstairs to bring Chloe down.

The three of them sat at the table to eat.

Grace served food for Chloe.

Mrs. Davis took a reluctant bite. Her eyes lit up. “Grace, the food is quite good today.”

Grace said lightly, “Yeah, I followed an online tutorial.”

She lowered her gaze.

Using her skills as a disciple of a master chef to spoil Mrs. Davis’s palate was the first step of her revenge!

She had many straightforward ways to force Zack to leave with nothing.

But she didn’t want to expose her identity. She needed to avoid using her past identity as much as possible to avoid arousing “their” suspicion again. Besides, Evelynge had suffered for so many years. She had to make them pay.

Not only would she get a divorce, but after the divorce, she would make sure the scumbag, the other woman, and the wicked mother-in-law all got what they deserved!!

…

That night, Mina tossed and turned in bed, unable to sleep.

After leaving earlier, she had thought about turning back to give Grace a few more words of advice. But through the glass window, she saw Grace obediently going into the kitchen to cook for Mrs. Davis. The sight made her heart sink.

Would Grace Nan really get a divorce?

She loved Zack so much that a single threat from him made her block her best friend.

Zack would never initiate a divorce for the sake of the company’s money and the house. If Zack said a few sweet words, wouldn’t Grace just soften again?

Mina took a deep breath and turned over again.

After a while, she suddenly sat up. “You’ve got to be kidding me. Is Grace sick? After all that, she’s still cooking for that wicked mother-in-law?! She was definitely lying about getting a divorce! Liar! I’m blocking her. That way, she can’t abandon me again or block me first!”

Thinking this, her eyes reddened.

She immediately picked up her phone, fuming as she was about to block Grace Nan, whom she had just added back. Suddenly, a message popped up in the chat interface…

###Chapter 281

###Chapter 282

###Chapter 283

Exactly his type.

Even when family urged him to marry, her face was the first that came to mind.

They were protective!

###Chapter 284

Mina Sherwood pushed open the front door.

Their branch was increasingly marginalized.

Second Uncle snorted.

Her grandmother had been the second wife.

After Grandfather died, the uncles had joined forces to evict Grandma from the ancestral home.

Her father was filial.
He always wanted Grandma to rest with Grandfather eventually.

He left immediately after sitting down."

After the divorce, she would use this to re-sign with Sherwood Group.

###Chapter 287

Marcus Valentine stood right beside her, his brow furrowed tightly. He had clearly overheard the entire conversation.

Marcus had seen Mina passing by and noticed her anxious, distressed expression.

He had blocked the closing door, then felt he was overstepping, so he sent his assistant in to help.

He hadn't expected Mina to directly leverage his influence.

If it was just about signing a contract, he didn't mind being used once. Business was like war, and Mina was quite beautiful...

But she had used his influence to target her own cousin, Grace!

Marcus's favorable impression of her instantly plummeted to freezing point.

He had originally thought Mina was just foolish, fooling around with Grace. Now it seemed this woman was both stupid and vicious! To do this to her own best friend!

Marcus had always valued loyalty and hated backstabbers the most.

Mina crashed against his solid chest, her head spinning from the impact.

The drug's effects were gradually taking hold. She tried to shake her head to stay clear. "You..."

Marcus leaned down, his mouth near her ear. "For what just happened, how will you repay me?"

His warm breath brushed her ear.

Mina shuddered, her body's reaction intensifying.

She went completely soft against Marcus, her gaze dazed. Her hands suddenly wrapped around his neck...

Marcus's body stiffened.

He had only meant to scare her, but this woman had directly latched onto him?

He was about to push her away when he heard Mina murmur, "Marcus, I like you so much..."

Marcus sneered. "How many people have you said that to?"

Mina giggled and held up one finger.

Marcus's breath hitched.

He was no gentleman and had long been interested in that face and figure. Now she was offering herself up...

Besides, Mina wasn't a good woman anyway. What harm was there in having some fun with her!

Marcus bent down, swept Mina into his arms, and carried her into the adjacent private room.

He placed her on the sofa, then leaned over her. Touching her burning skin, Marcus finally realized something was wrong...
###Chapter 288

Deliberately setting contractual traps just to crush Grace.

###Chapter 289

She is Mr.Valentine's Daughter!

It was his first time...

By that calculation, he was the one at a loss?!

He irritably loosened his tie, frustration burning inside him.

His phone suddenly rang.

Back then, Mina was at least the granddaughter of the head of the Sherwood family. Everyone gave her some face.

But after her grandfather passed away, the main branch took over the Sherwoods. The second branch gradually declined, and Mina became more and more invisible in their circles...

His voice was cold. "What is it?"

Mina's voice came through. "Didn't you say earlier that as long as my request wasn't excessive, you would agree?"

A mocking smile instantly appeared on Marcus's face. "So that was indeed playing hard to get? Miss Sherwood, there's no need for such games with me!"

Mina was speechless.

He thought Mina would force him to take responsibility, or ask for benefits for the Sherwood second branch, at least help stabilize her position in Sherwood Group. Instead, he heard Mina say:

Marcus immediately frowned.

"Won't do it."

Marcus coldly spat out three words and hung up directly.
