"Even if she weren't my uncle's daughter, is this how you treat an ordinary friend?!"

Marcus snapped angrily, then let out a cold laugh. "If she really can't stand someone like Grace, then she should stay away. It was Mina herself who got close, just to embarrass Grace?! Was their over-a-decade-long friendship all fake?! If Uncle finds out about Grace's identity and Mina's actions, Mina can forget about staying in San Francisco!"

The assistant was silent for a moment, then couldn't resist asking, "Young Master Xu, are you still not going to tell Third Master the DNA test results?"

Marcus frowned deeply.

The DNA test results had come out long ago.

He had initially believed his uncle's confident claims, really thinking Grace was his father's illegitimate child. Those two days at home, he hadn't given his father a single good look.

He even got beaten up by his father later.

But the results showed that Grace was his uncle's daughter!

He was utterly shocked at the time.

He had planned to tell his uncle immediately, but after hearing his assistant's report on Grace's life, Marcus hesitated.

His uncle had always been proud.

All these years, not a single woman had caught his eye. Marcus once asked, "Why not find a partner?"

His uncle replied, "You can't compromise on blood relations. Better none than settle."

If his uncle found out his daughter lived such a wretched life, being played for a fool by some social-climbing man from a small town, he would probably be furious.

His illustrious, brilliant life would be ruined by this daughter, Grace!

So Marcus had been delaying meeting his uncle, not reporting this matter.

His uncle was out searching for Mrs. Xu, Sophia. The two hadn't seen each other since that day.

Marcus sighed. "Let's not say anything for now. I'm afraid Uncle will get sick from anger."

The assistant asked again, "Then this matter... are we getting involved or not?"

Marcus's jaw tightened.

He thought maybe it was better not to get involved. Let Zack abandon Grace; it might cure his cousin's lovesick foolishness.

But he also felt this was his cousin's own choice.

The Xu family upbringing taught them from childhood to act according to their own wishes. The elders never forcibly interfered with the excuse of "it's for your own good."

Instead, they asked, "What do you want?" and tried their best to fulfill it.

So, if Grace deeply loved Zack, Marcus's choice would be to help her keep Zack in check, make him never dare to divorce her for life. After all, the Xu family could protect her for a lifetime!

Just as this thought crossed his mind, his WhatsApp rang again. It was still Mina.

Mina hurriedly explained, "No, I want her to get a divorce."

Mina was angered by him. "How do you know she doesn't want to?!"

"Grace wants the divorce herself?"

The logic didn't add up.

["Zack hid it from me because he cares about me! He just made a mistake any man could make! If you hadn't told me, he would have come home eventually! I didn't need your reminder!"]

Was she determined to take revenge on Grace?

His voice was icy.

Her uncle had let her go today because he mistakenly thought Marcus was interested in her.
If Marcus personally clarified the situation...

Mina hadn't yet realized the consequences of angering him.

[Gracie, I did something big today! I'll tell you later!]

Her parents were kneeling in the center of the hall.

He spent over a hundred thousand on Kelsey. Entertainment costs took another chunk. Now, he only had a little over ten thousand in savings.

Three years, and all he had to show was ten thousand.

Zack panicked.

If they didn't divorce, he'd be jointly responsible for millions in debt as her husband.

Third Master Valentine had made it clear he wouldn't help.

Grace couldn't appear too eager. She continued to weep pitifully.

In reality, she was struggling hard not to laugh.

###Chapter 292

Would she really make Grace and Chloe homeless?

Mina was stunned. “What?”

Evelyn held her hand. “Tomorrow, I’ll go with you to Sherwood Group.”

Mina clenched her jaw. “You want to beg for more time? Sherwood Group has strict rules. Withdrawing a lawsuit after filing requires a valid reason. It’s not easy…”

“Not to beg.” Evelyn’s gaze was firm. “To discuss cooperation!”

…

Marcus returned home.

He saw the light on in his third uncle’s study.

He paused.

Then he walked over.

Grace had come to her senses.

She was planning a divorce.

That meant there was still hope.

In that case.

He should tell his third uncle.

It was time for father and daughter to reunite.
###Chapter 296

Marcus Valentine pushed open the study door.

He called out for his third uncle.

His assistant immediately gestured for him to be quiet.

Marcus followed his gaze.

Mr.Valentine was fast asleep on the sofa.

A month had passed since their last meeting.

Mr.Valentine had lost a significant amount of weight.

His cheeks were deeply sunken.

His closed eyes revealed profound exhaustion.

A dark stubble covered his chin.

His complexion showed signs of weariness from travel.

Marcus lowered his voice. "How did he get so exhausted?"

The assistant signaled for them to step outside.

They gently closed the study door behind them.

The assistant sighed. "The Third Master hasn't slept for three days."

"Someone claimed to have seen Madam Nan in Licheng."

"He rushed there overnight."

"He turned Licheng upside down."

"But still couldn't find her."

"He collapsed and fell asleep as soon as he returned to the study."

William Ashcroft was dead.

Sophia Ashcroft could no longer exist as Mrs. Xu.

Mr.Valentine now referred to her as Miss Nan.

But others found it inappropriate.

They now called her Madam Nan.

Marcus frowned. "Uncle He, Third Uncle is neglecting his health."

"Is a woman really worth this?"

He Wei had been by Mr.Valentine's side for over twenty years.

He sighed deeply. "You don't understand."

"The lengths the Third Master would go for Madam Nan."

"His refusal to marry all these years was entirely because of her."

Marcus's jaw tightened. "He likes her that much?"

"Correct."

"After Madam Nan left with William Ashcroft all those years ago."

"The Third Master shut himself away for five full days."

"San Francisco became his forbidden city."

"He desperately wanted to see her."

"But Madam Nan made a pact with him to never meet again in this lifetime."

"The Third Master endured it for twenty-two long years."

"Tell me, how deep must that love be?"

He Wei spoke and sighed again.

"I'm actually glad they never ended up together."

Marcus was puzzled. "Why?"

"Madam Nan is clearly backed by a much more powerful force."

"We've been searching for so long without a single lead."

"The Xu family simply can't compare to the other side."

"If the Third Master had truly been with Madam Nan back then."

"He would have fought to the death when she was in danger…"

Marcus fell silent.

He finally spoke after a long pause. "Can love really make someone this crazy?"

He shook his head and left the study.

The matter regarding Grace Nan could wait until tomorrow.

But the next morning.

Marcus arrived at the study only to find it empty again.

The butler said a call came in the early hours.

Someone had spotted Madam Nan in Jincheng.

Mr.Valentine immediately rushed over.

Marcus looked up at the sky in frustration.

Who on earth had taken Madam Nan?

They left absolutely no trace!

He took out his phone hesitantly.

Should he call and tell Third Uncle about Grace Nan?

After a moment's thought, he put the phone away.

He would wait until Grace's divorce was finalized.

No need to distract Third Uncle now.

He was already worried about Madam Nan and concerned about Grace Nan.

Just then, his assistant hurried over.

"Young Master Xu, Lady Grace Nan and Mina Sherwood have gone to the Sherwood Group."

Marcus immediately frowned. "Going to beg for help?"

He turned and strode out.

He stopped halfway.

Realizing he was being too eager.

He coughed to cover it up.

"Going to watch their spectacle."

"Let that woman see how insignificant she is without me."

The assistant silently lowered his head.

The Young Master was clearly worried they would be bullied.

So stubborn. No wonder Miss Sherwood never gave him a nice look.

Sherwood Group.

Evelyn Ross arrived at the company early with Mina Sherwood.

Mina led her into the New Energy Project Department.

They met the department head.

Mina was about to introduce her.

The door was flung open.

William Sherwood strode in.

The department head quickly stood up. "Mr. Sherwood."

William shot a cold glance at Mina.

He didn't know the full details of last night's events.

He called Mr. Locsitant this morning only to find his phone switched off.

Inquiring at the hotel revealed Mina was never with Mr. Locsitant.

This niece of his dared to defy him openly!

He had to teach her a lesson today!

William sneered. "I'm here to see how my dear niece abuses her power for personal gain!"

He glared at Mina. "You privately signed an investment agreement with NanTech Ltd."

"You failed to deliver the results on time."

"I suspect you colluded with outsiders to embezzle company funds!"

"Mina Sherwood, do you admit it!"

Mina immediately refuted. "I did not!"

"NanTech Ltd. has the capability."

"We conducted due diligence before investing. The procedure was fully compliant!"

William scoffed. "Compliant? Then why couldn't they deliver?"

"I heard their research staff resigned right after getting the investment."

"How do you explain that?"

Mina urgently explained. "That was a coincidence!"

"It's precisely why the delivery was delayed."

"They have a solution now."

"We're here today to discuss continuing the cooperation!"

William sneered. "A solution now? It's too late!"

"The contract deadline has passed!"

"The Sherwood Group isn't your personal company."

"Favoritism and misconduct will not be tolerated!"

He looked at the department head.

"This matter must be strictly enforced according to the contract!"

The department head wiped his sweat. "Understood."

William turned his glare back to Mina. "You must take responsibility for this."

"You're fired!"

"If the penalty fee isn't recovered, I will pursue legal action against you!"

Mina's chest heaved with anger.

Evelyn Ross spoke calmly. "Are you sure you want to strictly enforce the contract?"

"The contract states that if we breach."

"Sherwood Group will refuse any future cooperation, correct?"

William scoffed. "Of course!"

"Sherwood doesn't cooperate with small companies lacking credibility!"

"You should focus on repaying the twenty million first."

"I heard your husband divorced you? Heh."

"My personal affairs are none of your concern."

Evelyn looked at the department head.

"I came today intending to discuss another cooperation."

"Since you insist on strictly enforcing the contract."

"I cannot partner with Sherwood on the Hydrogen Fuel Project I currently hold."

"Such a pity."

The department head laughed derisively. "Hydrogen Fuel?"

"The most advanced technology currently is developed by Dr. Nan."

"You can't even handle simple R&D, and you're talking about Hydrogen Fuel?"

"Who would want to cooperate with you!"

Evelyn handed over the project proposal. "Are you sure no one is interested?"

"Absolutely!"

The department head glanced at it disdainfully.

Then he froze abruptly.

What had he just seen?!
###Chapter 297

Don't Say I Didn't Warn You.

The head of the R&D department froze.

He stared at the document in Evelyn Ross's hand, his pupils contracting sharply.

This was the latest development plan for Hydrogen Fuel!

He snatched the project document, his fingers trembling slightly.

The front page was boldly printed with the words "Hydrogen Fuel Technology Breakthrough."

He quickly skimmed the contents, his breathing becoming急促. "You've already made substantial progress?"

Evelyn nodded faintly.

Director Flores excitedly grabbed Thomas Watson's arm. "Mr. Watson, we must collaborate on this project! This is the most cutting-edge technology in the industry right now. So many major companies are fighting for it..."

Thomas cut him off coldly. "Lao Xiang, have you been bribed too?"

Director Flores hurriedly defended himself. "Mr. Watson, I'm thinking of the company!"

"A breach of contract is a breach of contract!" Thomas's tone was firm. "Sherwood Group never shows favoritism. The twenty million penalty must be paid!"

Director Flores was so anxious sweat beaded on his forehead. "Mr. Watson, the value of this plan far exceeds twenty million! If our competitors find out..."

Thomas scoffed disdainfully. "What kind of good plan could a small company possibly have? If they really had this capability, we'd just poach their entire team!"

He was determined to teach Mina Sherwood a lesson today.

His niece was too beautiful. If she would just obediently help him with social engagements in the future, what deal couldn't he close?

Director Flores tried to plead further. "Mr. Watson, this plan is likely Dr. Nan's work. She's the only one who can solve this problem now..."

Thomas refused to believe it. "If NanTech can develop this, why can't Sherwood Group? Lao Xiang, this matter is not up for discussion today!"

He turned to Mina with a smug smile. "Mina, this is just the beginning. If you don't behave, your parents will be next!"

Mina clenched her fists, her nails digging into her palms.

Thomas barked at Director Flores. "Why are you still standing there? Call security and have them thrown out!"

Director Flores looked at the plan in his hand, full of reluctance.

Thomas threatened him. "If you don't do it now, you're fired too!"

Director Flores's face turned pale. "Shouldn't we consult Chairman Sherwood..."

Thomas directly pulled out his phone and called HR. "Process the termination for Mina and Lao Xiang! Legal department, expedite the lawsuit!"

Director Flores's face instantly turned ashen.

Mina stepped forward. "Uncle, you—"

Thomas pinched her chin, his grip so tight it reddened her skin. "In this company, strength talks! If you have no ability, just be a pretty花瓶!"

Evelyn shoved him away and shielded Mina. "Let her go!"

She checked Mina's chin, her eyes turning cold.

She had originally thought Mina was just a simple-minded socialite. She hadn't realized her situation was so difficult.

Even a cooperation handed to them on a silver platter was being obstructed.

Evelyn let out a cold laugh. Supporting Mina, she looked at Thomas. "You don't want the plan? Fine. I'll find another company to合作. Don't say I didn't warn you!"

Thomas laughed arrogantly. "You? Make me regret passing on a project worth hundreds of millions? Dream on!"

Evelyn's gaze was sharp. "Fine. Let's go!"

Any major group would eagerly offer hundreds of millions for this plan.

The subsequent value brought by a breakthrough in Hydrogen Fuel technology simply couldn't be measured in money.

Those in the know understood exactly what this meant.

Director Flores had pleaded repeatedly precisely because he understood its value.

It was a pity Thomas was obstinate and had even fired him.

Director Flores followed the two women out of the office without hesitation.

Meanwhile.

A black sedan pulled up outside the Sherwood Group building.

Chairman Kevin Sherwood personally greeted Marcus Valentine as he stepped out of the car.

This fifty-year-old middle-aged man had a refined demeanor. As the head of one of the Five Great Families, he took the Valentine family's visit very seriously.

The two men walked into the building side by side. The elevator went directly to the top-floor conference room.
###Chapter 298

I should be thanking you!

Kevin Sherwood looked at Marcus Valentine. "What brings you to the company today, nephew?"

Marcus smiled. "Just looking around. Uncle Kevin, can we visit the new energy R&D department?"

Kevin immediately thought of something. "Are you here to see Mina?"

He had a deep impression of that niece from the second branch.

Not only was she exceptionally beautiful, but her abilities were also top-notch. She was the only junior from the second branch to enter the Sherwood Group.

The Sherwood family had once wanted to arrange a marriage with the Valentines. Several girls were introduced to Marcus, including Mina.

Later, the second branch said it didn't work out.

Kevin didn't pay much attention. Marriage alliances depended on fate. Even his own daughter hadn't been chosen.

Marcus cleared his throat lightly. "I need to discuss something with her."

"I'll accompany you over."

"Thank you, Uncle Kevin."

They walked toward the elevator.

Marcus and Kevin entered the executive elevator. It went directly to the R&D department.

But Evelyn Ross and Mina Sherwood were nowhere to be seen.

Kevin directly asked a staff member. "Where's Mina? Marcus is here to see her."

"She was just fired by President William! He said she was lining her own pockets by investing project funds in her friend's company. Now she can't deliver results…"

Kevin was stunned. "Fired? Where's Director Flores? Call him over!"

The staff member looked uneasy. "Director Flores was also fired. President William said they were in cahoots."

Kevin fell silent.

He looked awkwardly at Marcus, momentarily at a loss.

Marcus frowned.

He was too late.

See? Things fell apart without him.

Kevin took out his phone and called Mina. "Mina, where are you? Come over and explain this… The underground parking lot? Then you…"

Before he could finish, Marcus had already turned and headed downstairs.

Kevin quickly changed his tone. "Don't leave! We're coming to find you!"

Mina hung up the phone. She looked nervously at Evelyn. "Grace, my uncle is coming to find me!"

Evelyn nodded. "Oh."

Seeing Mina's anxious expression, she asked, "Are you afraid of him?"

"Of course! Everyone is afraid of him!"

Mina swallowed hard. "He's the head of the Sherwood family. Even when Grandpa was alive, my eldest uncle from the main branch was very authoritative. All us juniors were afraid of him. After the family split, he became even more imposing. We hardly dare to speak in front of him…"

As she was speaking, a voice called out. "Mina! Miss Nan!"

They turned to see Director Flores running over.

Mina was full of guilt. "Director Flores, I'm so sorry for getting you involved…"

Director Flores, however, looked excited. "What are you apologizing for? I should be thanking you!"

Mina was stunned.

The position of R&D Director was very high. Director Flores had worked for twenty years to get there. Had being fired driven him mad?

Director Flores then looked at Evelyn. "President Nan, would it be possible… for me to join your company?"

Evelyn didn't hesitate. "Yes."

Director Flores had always spoken up for them. Her company was short on management staff. His timing was perfect.

Director Flores became excited. "Really?"

"Yes."

Mina hesitated. "Director Flores, are you sure? Grace's company is very small… It can't offer a high salary… And it currently owes the Sherwood Group a huge debt…"

Evelyn had mentioned a new plan last night, but Mina didn't understand the research. She didn't grasp the project's importance.

Director Flores immediately said, "Salary isn't important! With President Nan's project, the company is destined to become a leader in the new energy industry! That debt… President Nan, don't worry. I just called the Valentine family's R&D department. Their investment manager is on his way. He can cover the two million at any time!"

Mina was speechless.

She thought Director Flores was exaggerating.

As the three were talking, William Sherwood also came downstairs.

He walked over and sneered. "What? Can't bear to leave? Old Xiang, just admit your mistake, and I'll let you come back to work!"

Director Flores's mouth twitched. "President William, that won't be necessary. I've found another position."

William frowned. "So quickly? You must have been planning to leave for a while!"

He then looked at Mina. "Have you realized your mistake? Kneel down and kowtow to admit your fault. If I'm in a good mood, I might give your friend's company a few more days!"

Mina took a deep breath. "Uncle, that won't be necessary!"

She didn't dare to speak harshly. Her parents and grandmother still depended on him.

Her parents and grandmother were used to a life of luxury. They refused to move out and insisted on staying under his thumb.

Mina worked hard to enter the Sherwood Group to make life easier for her family.

Since she joined the company, her uncle's attitude toward their family had improved significantly.

Now she had offended him again. Life would be even harder from now on.

Seeing her stubborn refusal, William turned livid with anger. "Mina, don't push your luck! Do you believe I'll strike your grandmother and father from the family registry right now? I'll make you leave with nothing!"

Mina's pupils contracted.

Her grandmother's final wish was to be buried with her grandfather.

If they were struck from the registry… her grandmother wouldn't be able to handle the blow! Her parents wouldn't be able to accept it either!

She clenched her fists, her eyes reddening. "You…"

Just then, "Ding!"

The elevator chimed.

Marcus Valentine and Kevin Sherwood strode out of the elevator…
###Chapter 299

The elevator doors slid open.

Evelyn Ross and her two companions stood nearby.

Marcus Valentine and Kevin Sherwood stepped out, their eyes immediately finding the group.

William Sherwood quickly masked his aggression with a strained smile. "Brother, Mr. Valentine, what a coincidence! Heading out?"

Kevin remained silent.

His sharp gaze swept over them, instantly noting William's lingering hostility and Mina's visible distress.

His brow furrowed. "What's going on here?"

William spoke first. "It's nothing serious. Mina was being irresponsible, favoring a friend with a project. The contract expired without delivery, so the company followed procedure and filed a lawsuit. Now she's causing a scene at the office, even trying to drag Director Flores into some scheme!"

Director Flores hurried to explain. "Chairman Sherwood, that's not—"

"Enough of your lies!" William cut him off sharply, turning to Kevin. "Brother, don't listen to him. They just claimed NanTech secured a project worth hundreds of millions. Isn't that absurd?"

As chairman, he didn't handle technical details, but a project worth that much sounded excessive.

He shook his head dismissively. "Is this your plan? Invent a new project, claim it's worth a fortune... How desperate!"

William immediately agreed. "Exactly! What a ridiculous lie! Even more absurd, Director Flores supports them, insisting it's true... Brother, I failed to manage Mina properly, allowing this farce. But I've fired both Director Flores and Mina. NanTech must be held accountable. The lawsuit stands."

Marcus added, "That severe? No wonder these young women resorted to such a wild story."

He glanced at Kevin, tone mocking. "Mr. Sherwood, isn't your company too strict? Harsh on partners? A few days delay on a proposal, and I hear it ruins lives?"

Evelyn spoke calmly. "The project is complete."

Kevin pressed, "Any impact on us?"

Mina shook her head. "None at all."

Kevin decided instantly. "Then drop it. Withdraw the lawsuit."

William tried to argue, but Kevin shot him a sharp look. "Business is business, but we're one of New York's Five Great Families. We should show magnanimity. A three million project ruining lives is excessive."

William fell silent, chastised.

Kevin turned to Mina. "You can return to work."

Kevin's tone was firm. "Mina has the most drive among the younger generation. She's a Sherwood. She deserves some privilege here."

The matter was settled.

Mina felt a surge of relief.

Staying at Sherwood Group meant her parents would be proud. Though she'd offended her uncle, her job was secure.

Kevin looked at Director Flores. "Old Xiang, you—"

William interrupted again. "Brother, Mina is young and impulsive, but Old Xiang is worse! He didn't correct her, he helped them deceive. Talking about a project worth hundreds of millions... Who knows what NanTech offered him to spout such nonsense!"

Director Flores, confident with Evelyn's project, stood his ground. "Chairman Sherwood, I won't be returning. I've accepted a position with Ms. Nan. But Sherwood Group is my old employer. Let me offer advice: Are you certain you don't want to partner with NanTech on this new project?"

Kevin frowned. "This new project... is it truly that impressive?"

William scoffed. "Impossible! Brother, don't be fooled! See? His true colors show. Just fired, and he joins NanTech. What's your price, Old Xiang? Betraying the company like this?"

Director Flores's expression tightened.

William said sternly, "Old Xiang, this behavior is grounds for a lawsuit. We could sue for fraud!"

Director Flores felt frustrated.

Reasoning with these non-technical managers was impossible!

Just then, a sports car screeched to a halt outside the building.

A figure rushed in urgently. "Old Xiang! Old Xiang! Is it true? That project proposal—we want it at Valentine Group! Let me see it. I brought a contract!"
###Chapter 300

His address for her changed from 'Mina' to the more affectionate 'Mina'.

William's face turned ashen.

With Mina as Director, he could no longer suppress the second branch.

###Chapter 301

Grace Valentine and Evelyn Ross!

Marcus Valentine's words made Evelyn's eyes flicker.

Was Mr.Valentine really her father?

But her mother Sophia Ashcroft's words, "We shall not meet again in life or death," had kept her from confirming it.

She had agreed to divorce Zack Davis today.

She needed to get the divorce certificate quickly to avoid complications.

He was studying her. "Grace, you really are my cousin."

After all, no one could refuse such a family background.

Evelyn immediately adopted Grace's characteristic gentle and timid demeanor.

"He said I was Mrs. Vance's high school classmate."

Hearing that name, Evelyn's heart clenched.

It felt like an invisible hand was squeezing it.

For the past month, she had worked hard to imitate Grace.

She took care of Chloe.

At night, she read Grace's diary.

She searched for traces of "the Nan family" to save her mother.

She tried to forget that name.

But when Marcus mentioned it, she realized Alexander Vance was like a thorn buried deep in her bones.

It hurt at the slightest touch.

She lowered her eyes. "Yes, I've seen him."

"Has Alexander Vance seen you?" Marcus asked curiously.

He had already investigated Grace's life.

He was sure the person before him was Grace, not Evelyn.

Evelyn countered, "Mr. Valentine, what do you mean?"

Marcus sensed her displeasure. He explained, "Alexander has been in pain since Mrs. Vance passed away."

"He hasn't moved on."

Evelyn was slightly stunned.

She tightened her jaw.

He hadn't moved on only because there hadn't been enough time!

Time could heal all wounds.

Besides, he still had his Unattainable Love and his savior to accompany...

Marcus sighed. "Forget it. There's no point talking about this."

"You don't know them."

"Where are you going now?"

Evelyn looked down without speaking.

Marcus realized something. "To divorce Zack?"

Evelyn paused, then nodded.

Marcus misunderstood her expression. "Still can't bear to part with that scumbag?"

"Grace, I'm speechless. Can't you be stronger?"

Evelyn remained silent.

Marcus said disdainfully, "Lift your head up!"

"What's there to be ashamed of?"

"Looking like a doormat every day..."

"I'm not used to seeing Evelyn's face with that expression!"

Evelyn was puzzled.

Marcus continued, "Can't you be more assertive?"

"Speaking of which, you also have the surname Valentine. You should be Grace Valentine..."

He stopped speaking.

Grace Valentine... Evelyn Ross?

He paused. "Never mind. The name isn't important."

"Be more forceful! Don't let people push you around!"

"Even if you can't be like Evelyn, at least have a third of her presence!"

Evelyn held back her irritation. "Mr. Valentine, did you eat too much?"

Marcus replied, "I haven't eaten..."

"Hey, are you saying I'm meddling in your business?"

Evelyn had already hailed a taxi and gotten in.

The car drove away.

Marcus was furious. "I was trying to help her! And she says that to me!"

His assistant said, "...Mr. Valentine, the car is here."

Marcus got into the car angrily. "Follow them."

The assistant was confused.

Marcus couldn't help adding, "She's my only blood-related cousin."

"I can't just watch her get bullied alone."

The taxi arrived at City Hall.

Evelyn got out and walked inside.

She saw Zack Davis and Kelsey Lopez waiting impatiently.

Zack stepped forward. "Grace, why are you so late?"

"Don't want to get divorced?"

Kelsey immediately said, "Hurry up! Zack doesn't love you anymore."
###Chapter 302

Evelyn clutched the divorce certificate tightly.

The paper's edge dug painfully into her fingertips.

A month of endurance had finally paid off.

The property was secure.

Chloe's custody was also hers.

If her sister could see this from heaven, she would be comforted, right?

Evelyn's nose tingled, and her eyes gradually reddened.

Zack Davis stared at her slightly reddened eyes, suddenly feeling dazed.

The Grace from university was always timid, her eyes moist and deer-like when she looked at people.

Now, that face had shed its youthful innocence, but it was even more captivating.

He spoke without thinking, "Don't be sad... I'll come to see you and the child often..."

Kelsey Lopez pinched his arm sharply and screeched, "Grace! Stop pretending to be pitiful and seducing people! You're divorced now, get lost, hear me?"

Zack frowned, wanting to argue, but Kelsey immediately glared at him. "What? Can't bear it? Then go accompany her in paying off the debts!"

Zack shut his mouth instantly.

Kelsey raised her chin triumphantly and pointed at Evelyn. "Get lost! Don't delay me and Zack from getting our marriage license!"

Evelyn lowered her eyes, hiding the cold glint within.

She couldn't resort to violence.

Grace's persona was weak and easily bullied.

But she had other methods.

She took half a step back and suddenly raised her voice. "Zack, are you planning to finalize the divorce and remarriage all in one day?"

The hall instantly fell silent.

All eyes focused on the three of them.

The red divorce certificates were clutched in Evelyn and Zack's hands.

Kelsey stood close to Zack, her makeup impeccable, her clothes brand new.

Anyone could see the situation clearly.

Immediately, people began to murmur. "Remarrying right after the divorce? That woman must be the mistress!"

"The ex-wife's eyes are red from crying, how pitiful..."

"How dare the mistress be so arrogant? Where does she get the nerve?"

An older woman rushed over and spat fiercely at Kelsey. "Disgusting! Are you proud of stealing someone else's husband?"

The woman pulled Evelyn over. "Don't be afraid, dear! I'll help you curse this wretched pair!"

Kelsey, her collar soiled with spit, shrieked in disgust. "You crazy hag! What are you doing?"

The woman was thoroughly enraged. She grabbed Kelsey's hair and forced her down. "I'm cursing you! Shameless mistress!"

Kelsey struggled and yelled, "Let go! I'll call the police!"

The woman's relative immediately held up a phone to record. "Go ahead! Let the whole internet see your true face! Forcing the divorce and daring to remarry the same day? Have you no shame?"

Kelsey panicked instantly.

The woman dragged her and pushed her towards Evelyn. "Apologize to her!"

Kelsey gritted her teeth and glared at Zack.

But Zack pointed at Evelyn and scolded, "Grace! If you didn't want the divorce, you should have said so! Hiring people to cause trouble, what kind of trick is that?"

Before he finished speaking, the woman slapped him. "Cheating and you still have the nerve? Asking for a beating!"

Blood immediately streaked across Zack's face. Terrified, he covered his head and fled, rushing out of the City Hall without looking back.

Kelsey stamped her foot and shouted, "Zack! We haven't gotten the license yet!"

Zack shouted from afar, "Another day! I can't stand this embarrassment!"

Kelsey trembled with rage.

She straightened her disheveled hair and glared viciously at Evelyn. "Zack wanted the divorce, what does it have to do with me? She was the one who couldn't keep him! Even her mother-in-law disliked her! Zack's mother adores me!"

The woman looked at Evelyn sympathetically. "Dear, your mother-in-law is truly awful..."

Evelyn shook her head gently. "Thank you, ma'am."

She turned to Kelsey, a faint smile curling her lips. "Since your future mother-in-law likes you so much, I wish you a long and happy union. You must endure it."

Over the past month, she had indulged Mrs. Davis into becoming excessively picky and difficult.

With her, the submissive ex-daughter-in-law, as a comparison, Kelsey's future days would likely be chaotic.

Evelyn turned and walked away, her back straight.

No longer mimicking Grace's habitual slouch.

She strode out of the City Hall decisively, as if stepping into a new life.

The scenery sped past the taxi window.

Evelyn gazed absently at the streets.

What was Alexander doing right now?

Was he still at the hospital with his savior?

She smiled bitterly.

Since childhood, she had always walked alone.

Finally, she had found a mother and Alexander...

But Sophia was imprisoned.

Alexander... was no longer hers.

From now on, she would have to walk the path alone again.

The taxi stopped outside the Nan family's small yard.

Evelyn pushed the door open to get out, only to see the nanny trimming plants in the yard.

Chloe's clear laughter came from inside the house.

Evelyn paused slightly. "We have a guest?"

The nanny nodded. "She said she's your friend."

Besides Mina, who else did Grace know?

Puzzled, Evelyn pushed open the door.

Backlit, a tall, lean figure was lifting Chloe high into the air.

The familiar silhouette made her heart stop.

The man turned slowly at the sound.

Sunlight spilled from behind him, outlining his gaunt profile.

A month apart, Alexander had lost weight.

But his eyes burned intensely, locked onto her face.

His voice was hoarse as he spoke, word by word.

"Evelyn, I recognized you."
###Chapter 303

Evelyn gazed at his face.

This past month, she often felt lonely.

Longing washed over her like a tide.

But she knew she couldn't disturb him and Sophia Watson.

Letting him believe she was dead was perhaps the best outcome.

She understood his meaning, yet still asked, "Mr. Vance, why are you here?"

Alexander placed the child in his arms down and handed her to his assistant.

Thomas wasn't with him.

Thomas remained in San Francisco handling Vance Group affairs.

Only Thomas could keep Richard Evans in check.

The assistant took the child and led Chloe to play nearby.

Alexander walked toward her step by step.

He had lost weight, his face haggard.

Each step he took made her heartbeat quicken.

Evelyn silently retreated.

She didn't know how many steps she took before her back hit the living room sofa.

There was no more room to retreat.

She watched helplessly as Alexander approached.

The man stood a head taller than her.

Up close, she could see his sunken cheeks.

He carried the familiar scent of vanilla.

It was his usual body wash...

Evelyn clenched her jaw, remaining silent.

Alexander slowly leaned in, whispering, "You wanted to divorce as Grace before, so I didn't disturb you. The divorce is final today, Evelyn. Can you come home now?"

Evelyn's pupils constricted sharply.

He had recognized her all along?

Was it that night?

Recalling carefully, he had chased after her the next day, but she hadn't acknowledged him.

After that, Alexander didn't press further and simply left.

Had he sensed she had another plan?

Evelyn pondered for a moment...

No...

She had never slipped up.

Grace's existence was real.

Alexander couldn't have recognized her.

Evelyn adopted Grace's fragile demeanor again.

A month of pretending had made her adept at playing weak.

She said softly, "Mr. Vance, I don't understand what you mean..."

After speaking, she received no response for a long time.

Evelyn slowly looked up to gauge his reaction.

She found the man watching her with a gentle smile.

His expression was tender and indulgent.

As if humoring a petulant child.

Evelyn felt a complex mix of emotions.

She even wanted to throw herself into his arms and tell him she was Evelyn.

To let him help rescue her mother and uncover the truth.

To hand all her burdens over to him...

But the thought was fleeting.

She insisted on playing Grace, refusing to acknowledge him.

After a long silence, the man sighed. "Evelyn, I spent this month investigating you and Grace. Grace appears weak but is strong inside, unlike your personality. She always wanted a divorce too. Your actions this month were flawless. But do you know the biggest difference between you?"

Evelyn desperately wanted to know.

But she couldn't say.

She lowered her head, remaining silent.

Alexander didn't need her answer. He whispered, "The two of you have one major physical difference..."

He lifted her chin.

His rough hand touched the corner of her eye.

Alexander looked at her intently. "Evelyn, I won't say it's about the eyes or a feeling. Because I didn't know Grace well. But you have a tear mole here. Grace doesn't. That's the biggest difference."

His finger rubbed the corner of her eye.

Trying to wipe away the foundation, to reveal the mole...

But his finger moved, then paused in surprise.

Evelyn's eye corner was smooth. There was no tear mole at all!

Alexander's expression instantly shifted to panic.

He lost his earlier composure, frantically rubbing her eye corner...

Still nothing!

His pupils contracted, staring at her in disbelief. "How... how is it not there?"

Evelyn stood quietly.

She had always known this difference from Grace.

To avoid detection by the "Nan family" who took her mother, she used a special solution to conceal the mole.

It was impossible to discover.

She bit her lip, speaking with feigned vulnerability and委屈. "Mr. Vance, you're hurting me..."

Alexander's fingers stilled. Suddenly, he wrapped his arms around her, holding her tightly. "Evelyn, I know it's you. Please don't scare me, alright? This entire month, I've been waiting for you. I can't lose you again..."

His words made her eyes redden.

She sighed inwardly, clenching her fists.

So Alexander hadn't been recuperating this month. He had been lying in wait.

Perhaps he wasn't completely sure she was Evelyn, but he used this belief to keep going.

If she denied it now, it would be like stabbing him a second time.

Should she admit it?

Her heart was torn, engaged in a fierce internal struggle.

Before she could decide, her body responded honestly.

She raised her arms and wrapped them around his waist.
###Chapter 304

Evelyn Ross never imagined a simple embrace could feel so comforting.

His arms were strong and secure, offering an instant anchor for her drifting heart.

Even the most independent woman needs someone to lean on sometimes.

She rested her cheek gently against his shoulder.

They stood together in silence.

Alexander Vance's low voice reached her ears. "Evelyn, is that you?"

He sounded uncertain.

Just as she was about to answer, commotion erupted outside.

The nanny protested urgently. "This is a private residence! You can't just barge in!"

Mrs. Davis shrieked. "Private? Where's Grace? Hiding us out, is she cheating in there?"

Zack Davis roared. "Out of my way! I'm her husband, here to settle things!"

The nanny was no match for two troublemakers.

Footsteps drew closer.

Evelyn quickly pushed Alexander away, pointing toward the first-floor nursery.

Alexander's gaze darkened, but he remained still.

Evelyn clasped her hands together, eyes pleading.

All traces of vulnerability vanished, replaced by her usual sharp intensity.

This was the real Evelyn.

Alexander seemed momentarily taken aback.

Evelyn didn't wait for his hesitation. She shoved him directly into the room.

She barely closed the door when Zack and his mother stormed in.

Mrs. Davis glared at her immediately. "Grace, where'd you get money for a nanny?"

Evelyn responded coolly. "None of your business."

Evelyn scoffed. "Everything was settled before the divorce. Having regrets now? Fine. Since we just divorced today, I'll让步. Zack can have all the assets. I'll take Chloe and leave with nothing?"

The deals with Sherwood Group and Ashcroft Corp weren't public yet.

Her eyes narrowed with sudden realization. "You sold your wedding jewelry, didn't you? Grace, the court's deciding your fate tomorrow! That money should've bought me supplements! Why waste it on a nanny?"

For the past month, Evelyn had indulged her every whim.

Mina Sherwood gave a few thousand for living expenses. Evelyn spent it all on Mrs. Davis—nutrition supplements, new clothes—treating her like royalty.

Evelyn met her with a cold stare. "We're divorced now. You're not my mother-in-law anymore. How I live is none of your concern."

"You!" Mrs. Davis's finger trembled with rage. "Ungrateful wretch!"

Evelyn cut her off. "Why are you really here today?"

Zack snarled. "That scene at City Hall—was it deliberate? Kelsey came home with stomach pains! You will apologize to her right now!"

Evelyn looked at him as if he were a fool.

Grace Nan must have been blind to marry a man like this.

She lowered her eyes. "Zack, we're divorced. Why should I listen to you?"

Zack froze.

Evelyn's voice was icy. "After the divorce, we have no connection."

Zack grabbed her wrist violently. "Grace, what do you mean? Cutting ties the second we're divorced? Is there another man?"

Evelyn laughed scornfully. "Zack, you're the one who cheated. I stayed home, cared for my husband, looked after my mother-in-law. I never stepped out of line!"

Zack was speechless.

Evelyn turned to Mrs. Davis. "I treated you with respect and care. I believed I was a good daughter-in-law. Why did you insist Zack divorce me?"

Mrs. Davis sneered. "Looking after your mother-in-law is your duty. Anyone could do it. You think you deserve praise? Not bearing a son is your original sin!"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "Any daughter-in-law could care for you that well?"

Mrs. Davis lifted her chin. "Of course! Kelsey Lopez will be even more attentive! She'll take care of me better than you ever did!"

"Really?"

Evelyn didn't argue further.

Zack dragged her toward the door. "Enough talk! Apologize to Kelsey now! And you'll care for her until the baby's stable! Or else!"

Evelyn shook him off. "Let go!"

Crying came from the nursery. "Mommy…"

Then soft soothing sounds followed.

Zack's face twisted with fury. "So it's true, Grace! You are hiding someone! No wonder you agreed to divorce so easily!"

Mrs. Davis rushed over. "Shameless slut! You had a lover during the marriage, didn't you? Zack, open that door! Let's see what kind of man wants a divorced woman with baggage!"

She grabbed Evelyn's arm. "Zack, catch them! Make that man pay! We're not leaving until he does!"

Hearing this, Zack charged at the door, ready to kick it down.

The door opened from inside.

A tall, imposing figure stepped out.

Zack and his mother stared, stunned. "M-Mr. Vance? What are you doing here?!"
###Chapter 305

Revenge!

Alexander Vance's gaze fell upon the two men.

The humiliation Evelyn Ross had endured this past month surged through his mind.

Killing intent nearly shattered his reason.

But this was New York.

He forcefully suppressed his rage.

He meticulously smoothed his sleeves.

"That debt from a month ago is due."

Several black-clad bodyguards materialized silently at the doorway.

A chilling aura instantly filled the space.

Evelyn watched him quietly.

A profound sadness for Grace Nan welled up in her heart.

This was the man to whom she had once entrusted her entire life.

They didn't even stop to right the overturned coffee table.

They practically crawled out of the villa.

Only after escaping the gated community did they dare stop to catch their breath.

"She must have landed a powerful patron! No wonder she's so bold now!"

Remembering Alexander's imposing presence moments ago.

The words made him feel slightly better.

Complex emotions churned within Zack during the ride.

He had thought he was the one who discarded Grace.

That she would eventually come begging, burdened with a child.

Now, someone else cherished her.

He recalled Grace's stunningly beautiful face.

So many rich heirs had pursued her in college, yet she refused them all.

She had chosen a life of hardship with him.

His mother saw his expression.

"She couldn't even bear a son! Kelsey is carrying my grandson!"

They comforted each other all the way home.

Leftover takeout from a烧烤摊 (Note: Translator's note: "烧烤摊" is a Chinese street food stall specializing in grilled skewers. The specific food is not critical to the plot, so it is described generally as "takeout") lay scattered on the coffee table.

It clashed starkly with the apartment's refined decor.

Zack's mother frowned immediately.

"Kelsey! Clean up this table!"

Kelsey was in the bedroom playing on her phone.

"Mom, my stomach hurts. Could you please take care of it?"

Zack's mother flared up.

"Stop pretending to be delicate! Grace did everything when she was pregnant!"

She pointed at the dirty dishes in the kitchen.

"You've just been lying around the two hours we were out?"

Kelsey looked at Zack pleadingly.

"Honey, I really don't feel well..."

In the past, Zack would have sided with his mother.

Now, he waved a hand irritably.

"Mom, just clean it up. Stop arguing."

His mother was astounded.

"Since when do I do chores? I have my dance class!"

Zack pushed her out of the bedroom and shut the door firmly.

His mother stood frozen outside.

After a month of being waited on like royalty.

She could no longer stomach this treatment.

She pounded violently on the door.

"Kelsey Lopez, you jinx!"

"I didn't raise my son to serve you!"

"Get out here right now!"

Their furious shouts pierced through the door.

All of this unfolded exactly as Evelyn had calculated.

A month of meticulous planning.

Had finally ushered in the moment of revenge.

Back at the Nan Family Villa.

Alexander Vance was gazing at her intently.
###Chapter 306

Daniel and Mrs. Davis slunk away in defeat.

Evelyn took a deep breath.

She looked at Alexander, her heart tangled in knots.

That embrace had said everything.

Denying it now would be pointless. He wouldn't believe her.

But what good would admitting it do?

Before the words faded, Mina burst into the room.

She spotted Alexander and paused. "And who is this?"

She studied his face for a long moment, a flicker of confusion in her eyes. "Have we met before?"

Evelyn's heart clenched.

Mina was a Sherwood.

Alexander's biological mother was also a Sherwood, making her Mina's aunt by marriage.

His resemblance to his mother was undeniable…

Evelyn quickly interjected. "Mr. Vance, please excuse us. Mina and I need to talk."

Alexander's eyes darkened at the sight of a Sherwood.

His lips pressed into a thin line. After a moment of silence, he finally spoke. "Evelyn, I don't know what you're planning, but I will be back."

Of course it was strange.

He'd called her Evelyn, not Grace.

Mina took her hand. "You got divorced! I'm taking you somewhere special tonight to celebrate!"

A warmth spread through Evelyn's chest.

She knew Mina was worried about Grace being sad…

Little Chloe walked over and tugged Evelyn's hand. "It's okay, Mommy. You go have fun with Aunt Mina. Nanny Lopez can stay with me."

It had facilities for both children and adults.

Mina grinned. "We used to come here all the time as kids. We always said we'd bring our own children here one day. Grace, I wanted to bring Chloe after she was born, but first she was too little, and then…"

Then they had lost contact. The opportunity never came.

She believed if her sister were here, she would say the same.

The ball pit arena was massive. A seemingly bottomless pool of colorful balls stretched out, with a winding slide descending from above. Mina took Chloe down the slide once.

They expected her to be scared, but the little girl loved it. She wanted to go by herself.

Mina and Evelyn shared a smile, letting Chloe slide down on her own.

She let out an exaggerated yell as she slid into the balls, burying herself. "Chloe, come find Auntie!"

Chloe giggled as she slid down, digging through the balls near Mina.

Evelyn watched, amused.

As Chloe was digging, a chubby boy around five years old suddenly picked up a ball and threw it at her.

The balls were made of soft plastic. It didn't hurt, and everyone in the pit was throwing them around. Chloe thought the boy was playing.

Good-naturedly, she picked up a ball and gently tossed it back. It lightly bumped his arm.

The boy flew into a rage. "Why did you throw that at me!"

Chloe was stunned by his yell. "S-sorry… I thought you were playing…"

Before she could finish, the boy burst into tears. "Mommy! She hit me with a ball!"

Evelyn sensed trouble and immediately moved to go down. Mina also looked up from the balls.

But they were both too late.

A woman dressed in expensive clothes rushed over. She slapped Chloe across the face, then shoved her hard!

The push was vicious. Chloe's small body was thrown backward, landing heavily in the ball pit!

Terrified, Chloe flailed her little hands in the balls, bursting into loud sobs.

Evelyn and Mina both rushed to her side, pulling her from the balls. Evelyn held the tiny girl tightly.

Chloe wasn't her biological daughter, but after a month of朝夕相处, coupled with their strange blood connection, she had become Evelyn's only family in this world.

Evelyn cherished her deeply.

She soothed her quickly. "Shhh, it's okay, don't cry… Mommy's here…"

Chloe had often been abused by Mrs. Davis at home. She had only known stability for this past month. Thoroughly frightened, she immediately closed her mouth at the words "don't cry," letting out a small hiccup.

Her pitiful expression broke Evelyn's heart.

Mina turned her fury on the parent. "What do you think you're doing!"

The woman was comforting the boy. She turned with a cold laugh. "I should be asking you that! Someone, call the manager! I want to know what will be done about this little brat hitting my son!"

She was utterly arrogant, pointing at Chloe and shouting. "Where did this little bastard come from? How dare she play here! No manners at all! Who said you could hit my son? Do you have any idea who we are?!"

Whispers rose from the surrounding crowd.

"That's Miss Valentine! The Valentine Group's cousin!"

"Oh god, it's her. No wonder she's so arrogant… The Valentine family branches wanted a daughter but never had one, so they adopted this distant cousin into the main family. Spoiled rotten since childhood… Her brothers are not to be trifled with! Especially young master Marcus Valentine! He's a notorious troublemaker!"

The woman sneered. "I did. What are you going to do about it?"

"You!"

Mina was too angry to speak. Suddenly, she felt a tap on her shoulder.

She turned to find the usually timid Grace looking frighteningly calm.

Evelyn handed Chloe to her, her voice low. "Hold her for me."

Mina was taken aback. Before she could process it, she instinctively took the child.

Then, her eyes widened in shock!!
###Chapter 307

Evelyn's eyes turned sharp.

She grabbed the woman's arm and slapped her hard.

A crisp sound echoed through the air.

She covered her face and shrieked. "How dare you hit me?!"

What the woman did to Chloe, she would return in kind.

This was completely in line with Grace's character.

A mother's bottom line was always her child.

Even bad novels had more variety.

She had been too stunned by Grace's actions to react earlier.

This woman was one of the distant cousins adopted by the Valentines, named Fiona Valentine. The other was named Penelope Valentine.

Their names alone showed how much the Valentine family longed for a biological daughter.

Unfortunately, the first and second branches of the family tried desperately, but only produced seven sons.

The third branch, Mr. Valentine III, remained unmarried to this day.

The Valentines could only dote on their two adopted daughters as precious treasures.

Their treatment was no different from that of a real heiress.

Outsiders always showed them respect.

Marcus Valentine had once tried to arrange a marriage between Fiona and Alexander Vance, but Alexander refused.

Fiona later married someone in New York.

Fiona knew perfectly well.

Her son was a little tyrant on the playground, but she never thought it was his fault.

Even if my son beat your daughter to death, we'd just pay a million in compensation. But if you dare lay a finger on my son, I'll ruin you. You could never afford the damages!"

She took out her phone to call for backup. "You hit me? I won't let you leave this amusement park today!"

Mina frowned and stepped forward. "Fiona, should we let Marcus settle this?"

Fiona's finger paused.

She was adopted by the main branch. Marcus was like her own older brother.

But Marcus was the heir groomed by the third uncle, raised under his guidance since childhood.

His position in the Valentine family was special, even more influential than her own father's.

Fiona and Penelope from the second branch were both spoiled at home.

Their other brothers also doted on them as younger sisters.

But both were most afraid of Marcus, the family's troublemaker.

Fiona sneered. "I know you went on a date with my brother recently. But he rejected you. You think he'd fancy you? Who are you trying to scare?"

Fiona pointed at Evelyn. "She attacked me. Teach her a harsh lesson!"

Mina tensely protected Evelyn.

What should they do?
###Chapter 308

Ye Min immediately shoved Chloe into Grace's arms.

She coldly scanned the surrounding bodyguards. "Who dares to move?"

The bodyguards halted.

These professional enforcers sensed imminent danger.

Fiona shrieked. "What are you waiting for? Get them!"

Evelyn raised her phone. "Fiona, do you want to be tomorrow's headline?"

The screen showed an active recording.

Fiona's arrogant sneer was captured perfectly.

Fiona's face paled. "You dare record me secretly?"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "Information spreads fast online these days."

Fiona trembled with rage.

She glared viciously at Evelyn. "What's your name?"

Ye Min started to speak.

Evelyn cut in first. "Grace Nan."

Fiona gritted her teeth. "Fine, Grace Nan. I'll remember you!"

She shot the manager a furious look.

Then she stormed off with her bodyguards.

Ye Min looked worried. "Fiona holds grudges. You've made a powerful enemy."

Evelyn patted her shoulder. "Don't worry. Marcus will handle it."

Ye Min smiled bitterly. "Marcus always protects his own. He'll only side with Fiona."

The manager approached nervously.

"Ladies, my apologies..."

He wiped sweat from his brow. "We can no longer serve you."

Ye Min's expression darkened.

The manager bowed repeatedly. "Please understand. Our small business can't afford to offend the Valentines."

His voice trembled. "Your entry is complimentary. Please leave quickly."

Ye Min and Evelyn exchanged a look.

They carried Chloe out of the amusement park.

The manager's orders followed them.

"Remember their faces. They are permanently banned!"

"Prepare generous gifts. We must apologize to the Valentines tonight..."

Ye Min clenched her fists in anger.

"She was the one in the wrong..."

Her voice dropped. "Is family status really that important?"

Evelyn remained silent.

Ye Min suddenly grabbed her hand. "Grace, you must leave New York immediately!"

Evelyn shook her head. "That's unnecessary."

She met Ye Min's concerned gaze and spoke softly. "Actually, I am the daughter of the third Valentine lord."
###Chapter 309

Mina froze at those words.

She remained silent for a long time.

So long that Evelyn thought she wouldn't speak again.

Mina finally forced a weak smile. "Grace, that's not a funny joke at all."

Evelyn was slightly taken aback. "I'm not joking…"

Before she could finish, Mina linked arms with her. "Don't you remember? You already told me about your background."

Evelyn stared blankly. "What background?"

Mina's eyes darted away. "About your biological parents… I asked if you wanted to find them. You said…"

She paused, lowering her voice. "You said you weren't abandoned. You had parents. They just didn't like you. They thought you were too slow. So they had your adoptive parents bring you to New York. Told you never to come back."

Evelyn's heart clenched.

Why would Grace make up such a lie?

William's final words echoed in her ears—her sister was taken by the Nan family.

The identities of those adoptive parents were also highly suspicious.

Everything seemed shrouded in mist.

"Grace, are you upset again?" Mina looked at her with concern.

Evelyn shook her head. "Maybe too much has happened lately. Some things are just blurry."

Mina immediately looked understanding. "It's okay. Don't you keep a diary? You write all the important things down."

Evelyn nodded.

She had indeed read Grace's diary.

But Mina continued. "But you have one locked diary. You never let me see it."

A locked diary?

Evelyn had never seen it.

She suppressed her questions and didn't press further.

The topic ended there.

Mina walked her and Chloe home as evening fell.

At the doorstep, Mina suddenly clutched her stomach. Her face turned pale.

Evelyn asked urgently, "What's wrong?"

Mina frowned. "Stomach cramps. My period might be starting. Can I use your bathroom?"

Evelyn nodded.

Mina hurried inside.

The nanny had dinner ready and was feeding Chloe.

Mina emerged from the bathroom looking even paler.

Evelyn steadied her. "Are you okay?"

Mina gave a pained smile. "My period is three days late. It just won't come. But the cramps are terrible."

Evelyn looked puzzled. "You're always so regular."

Mina nodded. "Yes. Always every 28 days. I don't know what's happening this time…"

Halfway through, she suddenly remembered something. Her face changed dramatically.

Evelyn understood immediately. "I'll get a pregnancy test."

Mina grabbed her hand nervously and nodded.

Half an hour later.

Two clear pink lines appeared.

They stared at each other in silence.

Evelyn looked at Mina. "You didn't tell me you were seeing someone."

Mina's face was ashen. "I'm not seeing anyone… This was an accident."

Evelyn was stunned. "What?"

Mina looked on the verge of tears. "Grace, what should I do?"

Evelyn was silent for a moment. "Who is the father?"

Mina bit her lip. Her cheeks flushed.

She didn't look scared. She looked shy.

Evelyn guessed instantly. "Marcus Valentine?"

Mina looked up, shocked. "How did you know?"

Evelyn sighed helplessly.

Mina's expression whenever Marcus was mentioned had long betrayed her feelings.

"What are you going to do? Tell Marcus? He's not married…"

"No." Mina's attitude was firm.

Marcus had a poor impression of her. She didn't want to trap him with a child.

She lowered her eyes. "I can't keep this baby."

Evelyn asked softly, "Are you sure?"

Mina nodded. "I'll make an appointment for the procedure tomorrow… Grace, please come with me. I'm scared. And I don't want my parents to know."

"…Okay."

Evelyn respected her choice.

That night, Mina stayed at the Nan house because she was afraid.

Evelyn put Chloe to sleep. She asked the nanny to stay with the child and returned to the master bedroom.

She remembered Mina's words from the day and began searching for Grace's possible hidden locked diary.

Suddenly, a faint sound came from the window.

Evelyn's eyes sharpened. She silently hid behind the curtains.

A dark figure nimbly climbed through the window.

The moment the person landed, Evelyn swiftly struck out and grabbed their wrist!

The intruder instinctively tried to counterattack but suddenly stopped.

Evelyn's internal alarms blared.

Was this someone sent to kill Grace? Or was it related to Grace's secrets?

She used a necktie to bind the person's hands, restraining them from behind. Then she moved to face them.

Moonlight illuminated the intruder's face.

Evelyn's pupils constricted violently.

Alexander Vance?!
###Chapter 310

Evelyn froze for a moment, then quickly moved to untie the tie from his wrist.

Alexander watched her, amusement glinting in his eyes. "Grace was skilled at hiding her talents, but she never learned martial arts. Evelyn, you've given yourself away."

She freed his wrist, her peach-blossom eyes lifting slightly. "Mr. Vance, I don't know what you mean."

"Is that so?"

He flexed his wrist and walked to the sofa, sitting down. His gaze remained fixed on her.

Uncomfortable under his stare, she looked down. "Mr. Vance, it's quite late for you to be here. It's inappropriate."

He lowered his eyes, a slight smile touching his lips. "Miss Nan, you bear a striking resemblance to my late wife. I miss her terribly. I hope you don't mind me visiting."

"...Suit yourself."

Ignoring him, she began searching the master bedroom for the diary Mina had mentioned.

She had lived in this room for a month but had never found any trace of a diary.

Evelyn stood by the desk, deep in thought.

Where would Grace hide a diary?

The closet had been organized long ago. Nothing.

The desk drawers had been thoroughly searched. Nothing.

Over the past month, she had not only cared for Mrs. Davis but also cleaned the entire house, searching for any clues about Grace.

Every item had been sorted through. She had even read Grace's ordinary diaries. Nothing seemed unusual.

If Mina hadn't mentioned a private, locked diary, she would never have known it existed.

She felt around the bed and checked under it. Still nothing.

Alexander's low voice broke the silence. "When I was a child, if I wanted to hide something, I usually buried it in the ground."

Buried in the ground...

Evelyn turned sharply, her eyes landing on the villa's small garden.

The villa had a small yard. Grace loved gardening. Flowers of all colors grew in the corner, and there was a tree...

Evelyn immediately stood and hurried downstairs.

Chloe and the nanny were already asleep. Mina was resting in the guest room.

Evelyn grabbed gardening tools and walked to the tree. She tested the ground with her foot and noticed one spot was unusually hard.

She began digging with a small shovel.

After a few strokes, she hit a small metal box.

The box was just large enough to hold a book. It wasn't locked.

Evelyn dropped the shovel, pulled out the box, and brushed off the dirt. Inside lay a notebook with a floral cover.

A small lock secured the notebook, but the key wasn't in the box.

After a moment of silence, she reburied the box and went back upstairs.

As she was about to close the door, she noticed Alexander had followed her.

She had almost forgotten about him.

She shot him an annoyed look and entered the room.

"Finding the key might be difficult," he said.

She glanced at him. "Who said I need to find a key?"

He paused, surprised.

Evelyn picked up a pair of scissors. With two sharp cuts, she snipped off the locked portion of the notebook.

Alexander was speechless.

She checked to ensure the contents were intact and sighed in relief.

But she didn't open it immediately. Instead, she looked at Alexander. "Mr. Vance, aren't you leaving? Trespassing is illegal."

"Is it?"

He took a step forward, closing the distance between them.

Suddenly, he bent down and pulled her into a deep embrace.

Evelyn tried to struggle, but he whispered, "If you push me away, you prove you're Evelyn, not Grace. Grace would never have the courage."

She froze, stiffening in his arms.

He was right. Grace, feeling inferior, would only try to please him. She would be too afraid to resist...

As she thought this, he buried his face in her neck.

His warm breath against her skin made her shiver. Her heart raced.

She pushed at his chest, mimicking Grace's timid tone. "Mr. Vance, please don't..."

He chuckled softly. "Don't what? This isn't how you refused me that time at the San Francisco hotel..."

Evelyn went rigid.

His large hand cupped her face. "Evelyn, last time, I undid your clothes like this..."

His fingers hooked the belt of her robe. It fell open, pooling at her feet.

Her eyes widened. Instinctively, she moved to cover herself, but he caught her wrists. "You were very cooperative last time, too..."

He leaned in, pressing her down onto the bed. He loomed over her. "You were so passionate..."

Evelyn said nothing.

He lowered his head, his hot breath making her heart pound even faster.

But he went no further. He simply rested against her neck. "Evelyn, how can you be so cruel? Why won't you acknowledge me?"

His voice was full of hurt and confusion.

It tugged at her heart.

She knew she couldn't hide it any longer.

Her eyes slowly lowered.

After a long moment, she gave a soft laugh. She pushed him away and retied her robe. "What difference would it make if I did?"

Hearing this, seeing her finally admit it, Alexander stood frozen.

A low laugh escaped him, but his eyes reddened.

He had been sure it was her, but until Evelyn admitted it herself, he couldn't be certain.

For this past month, he had watched her from afar countless times.

He saw her play the part of Grace flawlessly. Sometimes, he even doubted his own judgment.

Until now.

His anxious heart finally settled.

It was her...

It really was her...

He was about to speak when she turned her back to him. Her voice was complex with emotion. "You couldn't stand by and watch your unattainable love die. I can't tolerate you playing both sides. Since that's the case, why does it matter who I am? We're getting a divorce anyway."

Hearing this, Alexander reached into his pocket and pulled out the ring...
###Chapter 311

Divorce?

Alexander Vance’s smile instantly froze.

He quickly realized the problem.

Evelyn refused to acknowledge him because of this misunderstanding?

His eyes stung, but the corners of his mouth lifted.

He stepped forward and took Evelyn’s hand, guiding it to his left hand.

Evelyn frowned and tried to pull away.

How could this man be so shameless?

They were about to divorce, yet he still bothered her?

Just as she was about to withdraw her hand, her fingertips touched something cold.

Looking closely, it was that jade ring.

Wasn’t this the token she had sent to her childhood savior?

The emerald ring was valuable. How did it end up with him?

She looked up in a daze, hearing Alexander’s low voice.

“When I was kidnapped by traffickers as a child, a four-year-old girl saved me by drugging them.”

“She saved me but had to return home to suffer. She cried and asked me to marry her…”

Evelyn’s pupils constricted sharply.

“But Little Dumpling had no name. When I found Sophia Watson, she recounted everything clearly.”

“She said the token was lost. She had been abused and developed mental illness, waiting for me to save her…”

He gazed at Evelyn. “It wasn’t until I received this ring that I realized I had mistaken the person.”

“My Little Dumpling has been suffering all these years, and I failed to find her…”

Evelyn’s eyes reddened, her throat tight.

She looked at Alexander, unsure whether to laugh or cry.

“The day you had the accident.”

Evelyn lowered her eyes. “Then why did you still want to divorce me? You said ‘see you at two, no exceptions’.”

His voice trembled. “I never expected you to have an accident…”

Evelyn felt his body shaking and suddenly understood everything.

She reached out and hugged him back.

His response was a passionate, trembling kiss.

Her freshly put-on clothes slipped off again.

Two people不善言辞 expressed the joy of reunion through actions.

Again and again.

Alexander wished he could meld her into his bones and blood, never to be apart again.

Night deepened.

The white gauze curtains swayed gently in the wind, unable to conceal the room’s spring scenery.

Even the moon shyly hid behind the clouds, peeking out occasionally.

A night of intimacy.

A night of madness.

Alexander released all his suppressed longing.

As a result, Evelyn didn’t wake until ten the next morning.

She sat up in alarm, glanced at the sleeping man beside her, and rushed out the door.

Mina Sherwood was sitting in the living room playing on her phone.

The nanny was mopping the floor.

Evelyn urgently asked, “Where’s Chloe?”

Mina winked. “Knowing you wouldn’t wake up early, we sent her to preschool!”

Evelyn was stunned.

Mina leaned in with a sly smile. “Grace, not bad! No wonder you suddenly agreed to divorce Zack Davis. So you have a new lover!”

“Last night was pretty intense, huh…”

Evelyn’s cheeks burned.

Remembering last night’s madness, her ears turned red.

Who knew Alexander, usually so proper, had so many tricks? When he pressed her against the door, she couldn’t help but cry out…

She quickly stopped her wild thoughts and cleared her throat. “Well, I…”

Mina waved her hand. “No need to explain. I get it.”

“Zack cheated first. It’s only fair you find a young hunk.”

She added, “But pace yourselves next time. There’s plenty of time!”

“Don’t wear down that iron rod into a needle!”

“Pfft—”

Evelyn spat out her water.

Mina dodged disdainfully as her phone vibrated violently.

Evelyn picked up a sandwich and took a bite, hearing Mina exclaim in amazement. “Grace, New York is about to change!”

“How?”

“The Vance family from San Francisco announced their entry into New York!”

“They started planning a month ago. They’re the richest family in San Francisco, as powerful as the five great families.”

“Their arrival will probably reshuffle New York’s landscape!”

Alexander had mentioned entering New York last night.

Evelyn wasn’t surprised.

Mina continued, “I heard the Vance family head, Alexander Vance, is not even thirty yet.”

“He’s about to stand equal with the five great families. Impressive… but it’s none of my business.”

The master bedroom door suddenly opened.

Alexander had OCD and wouldn’t wear yesterday’s clothes. Evelyn had to find him unworn loungewear that belonged to Zack.

The man wore a white short-sleeve shirt. The cotton pants were too short, ending above his ankles.

The usually powerful business magnate now resembled a pure college boy.

Mina’s eyes lit up. She poked Evelyn. “Grace, good taste! Which school is he from?”

“This boy toy is much better than Zack!”

Evelyn：“……”

She and Mina admired Alexander’s new look together.

His usual suits had suppressed his beauty. Now, with messy hair and no makeup, his stunning features stood out.

Mina approached Alexander. “Young man, take good care of my friend, understand?”

“Serve her well, and you might really marry into a rich family. Save twenty years of hard work~”

Alexander’s expression stiffened.

Mina asked again, “By the way, what’s your name?”
###Chapter 312

Alexander Vance turned his gaze to Evelyn Ross.

His eyes held a silent question.

After their intimacy last night, Evelyn had confided everything about Grace Nan's death.

Including the truth about her mother.

Alexander had immediately revealed he'd been searching for Sophia Ashcroft's whereabouts.

Unfortunately, no leads had surfaced yet.

Even the combined efforts of the Vance and Ashcroft families found no trace.

Their only option was to use Grace's identity as bait to lure the snake from its hole.

Terrified she might vanish if he blinked.

Alexander changed into a white T-shirt and casual pants.

Evelyn found it refreshing.

"Of course not."

Mina: "…"

She said it on purpose.

Sure enough, at the hospital entrance.

Mina stared at the sign.

Her feet felt like lead.

In the OB-GYN waiting area.

Evelyn helped Mina get registered.

They sat on a bench, waiting for their number.

Mina was so nervous her palms were sweating.

Outside the hospital.

Marcus Valentine had just exited the main doors.

Then thinking of Mina currently inside getting an abortion.

The child was clearly his.

And he had the nerve to stand here making jokes.

###Chapter 313

Obstetrics and Gynecology.

Mina Sherwood's fingertips were ice cold.

Scheduled the procedure time.

But a small life was growing there.

An unplanned pregnancy was a major scandal in New York's social circles.

Mina walked out with red-rimmed eyes.

When she discovered she was pregnant.

A sense of duty would make him marry her.

###Chapter 314

Mina Sherwood saw Evelyn Ross.
Her taut nerves finally relaxed.

She took a deep breath.

Without asking, she would never know the truth.
