"Never."

Evelyn gently patted her back. Even after arriving home, Chloe still wouldn't release her grip.

That evening, Evelyn found herself with a tiny appendage.

During dinner, Chloe insisted on sitting on her lap.

While working, Chloe demanded to be held…

By the time Evelyn finished her tasks, it was late. She had just stood up when Alexander approached, extending his arms. "I'll put Chloe to bed."

Evelyn handed the child over.

But the moment she left Evelyn's embrace, Chloe began to squirm restlessly in her sleep, reaching out and murmuring, "Mommy, Mommy…"

Evelyn had no choice but to take her back.

She carried Chloe to the children's room and placed her on the bed, but the little one still clutched her sleeve tightly.

Evelyn simply lay down beside her.

After some time, she heard movement at the door. Turning her head, she saw Alexander standing there.

The bedroom was dark, the hallway light outlining his silhouette.

He had just showered. His hair was damp, the collar of his robe slightly open. His dark eyes watched her quietly.

Though he said nothing, the invitation was clear.

Evelyn blinked, pointing to the sleeve still held fast. She didn't move.

Alexander seemed to understand. He turned and left.

Evelyn thought he had accepted it. She adjusted her position, preparing to sleep beside Chloe.

Just as she was drifting off, she heard a faint sound.

Evelyn slowly turned her head. The man's tall figure stood beside the bed, bending over as if doing something.

Then, a soft "snip." The sleeve gripped by Chloe suddenly went slack.

Evelyn: ??

She looked down. Alexander had used scissors to cut away the part of her pajamas held by Chloe!

Evelyn was speechless.

She was about to speak when he placed a finger to his lips. His strong arms slid under her shoulders and knees, lifting her effortlessly.

His steps were silent, not disturbing Chloe. He quickly carried her out of the children's room and back to the master bedroom.

Evelyn: "...What are you doing?"

"Testing the new bed."

Evelyn smiled at him. "Just the bed?"

They were both adults. Evelyn knew exactly how much Alexander, after 28 years of celibacy, craved intimacy once he'd experienced it.

Alexander's face was stern, but the tips of his ears turned pink.

This man was so easy to tease…

As Evelyn thought this, he suddenly shrugged off his robe. "Testing the person again too."

"..."

The lights in the master bedroom went out. Soft rustling sounds filled the air.

Evelyn considered herself physically strong, but she was no match for him…

She didn't know when she fell asleep. The last thing she remembered was utter exhaustion, drifting into unconsciousness when he whispered in her ear, "Is the bed comfortable?"

She replied, "Yes."

She was so tired!

He asked again, "And the person? Is he comfortable?"

Evelyn: "..."

She slept soundly through the night. Mrs. Lawrence, however, spent the entire evening anxious.

Her husband had been working late, striving to secure the Vance Group project for their company.

He returned home late to find Mrs. Lawrence looking worried. He quickly asked what was wrong. She explained the incident at the kindergarten gate, apologizing, "I'm sorry. I might have ruined your big opportunity."

Mr. Lawrence was reasonable. "She saved your life. We couldn't stand by and do nothing. You did the right thing."

Mrs. Lawrence felt warmed by his words, but also more guilty.

She leaned into her husband's embrace. "What if we don't get the project?"

Mr. Lawrence comforted her. "Then we remain a second-tier wealthy family. Isn't the money our company makes enough to live on?"

Mrs. Lawrence smiled. Then she added, "But Grace and that man with her said you should take the contract to the Vance Group tomorrow to sign…"

Mr. Lawrence laughed. "How is that possible? I haven't received any notification… Besides, it was unlikely we would win the bid this time. Another company is stronger and more suitable."

Mr. Lawrence sighed. That was why he had been working so late.

Mrs. Lawrence nodded.

They went to bed. After just three hours, Mrs. Lawrence sat up. "Maybe you should go to the Vance Group and try? My savior sounded so certain. She didn't seem like someone who would lie…"

Mr. Lawrence smiled wryly. "I haven't received a call. What would I be trying?"

Mrs. Lawrence pushed him. "Just take the contract and go. It can't hurt to try!"

Half an hour later.

Mr. Lawrence stood outside the Vance Group's temporary office in New York, laughing at himself.

He must be out of his mind, listening to his wife and coming here.

Mr. Lawrence looked down at the contract, about to turn and leave, when Thomas Watson emerged from the Vance Group building. Spotting him, Thomas immediately called out, "Mr. Lawrence, isn't it?"
###Chapter 321

Mr. Lawrence spun around sharply.

Thomas Watson stood before him, beaming.

The man who had ignored them yesterday now extended his hand warmly.
"Mr. Lawrence, I've heard so much about you!"

Mr. Lawrence was bewildered.
"Mr. Watson?"

Thomas got straight to the point.
"Do you have the contract?"

Mr. Lawrence nodded instinctively.
"Yes."

"Good. Let's sign it now."

Dazed, Mr. Lawrence followed Thomas into the office.

Even after walking out with the signed contract, it felt like a dream.

He returned home in a trance.

Seeing his dazed expression, Mrs. Lawrence assumed the worst.
"What happened? Did the receptionist stop you?"

Silently, Mr. Lawrence handed her the contract.

Mrs. Lawrence glanced at it and froze.

Mr. Lawrence murmured,
"Just who is that savior of yours?"

Mrs. Lawrence thought for a moment, then her eyes lit up.
"Maybe my good deeds are finally paying off?"

Mr. Lawrence nearly rolled his eyes.
"Do you believe that?"

"No."

He sighed.
"Whatever their background, we aren't associating with them for gain."

Mrs. Lawrence nodded firmly.
"Right!"

...

The next day, Evelyn didn't take Chloe to preschool.

She had only enrolled her to avoid Zack's mother's harassment and to set a trap.

Now that the divorce was final and the house was peaceful, she withdrew Chloe immediately.

A two-year-old needs security and is best kept close.

Besides, Fiona Valentine was cunning.
Evelyn didn't want Chloe suffering any more mistreatment.

Hearing she didn't have to go to school, Chloe's face broke into a smile.

Evelyn was playing with her when Mina Sherwood video called.

Mina propped her phone on the coffee table.
"Goddaughter, give godmom a kiss!"

Chloe shyly buried her face in Evelyn's chest.

Mina complained,
"Look how shy she is! Grace, it's your fault for blocking me for six months..."

Evelyn wanted to explain for her sister, but didn't know where to start.

Mina continued,
"But it's fine. I've done some soul-searching these past months. I wasn't caring enough. People who lack love fall for scum like Zack."

She smiled at the camera.
"From now on, I'm going to love you more, mwah!"

Evelyn couldn't help but laugh.

It seemed her sister's friendship with Mina was stronger than she thought.

Before she could speak, a loud crash came through the video.

Mina's parents burst into the room.

Mina didn't have time to end the call before her mother slapped her.

"Smack!"

Mrs. Sherwood had struck her.

Mina held her cheek, stunned.
"Mom, what are you doing?"

Mrs. Sherwood's face was dark with anger.
"What am I doing? You should ask yourself what you've done!"

She threw a pregnancy test report in Mina's face.

Mina saw it and her pupils contracted.
"I..."

"Who is the bastard's father?!" Mr. Sherwood roared.

He stepped forward, glaring at his daughter.
"What did we teach you? Have some self-respect! Who will want you now?!"

Mrs. Sherwood began to cry.
"Is this why you refused to meet Mr. Vance? Are you involved with some man? How could you be so foolish!"

Mina clenched her fists.
"Dad, Mom, is getting pregnant in a relationship foolish, but marrying that divorced old man Alexander Vance isn't?"

Evelyn, who had been worried about Mina: ??

She glanced at the man nearby.

The "divorced old man" twitched his mouth.

An undeserved insult.

Evelyn coughed and focused on the video.

Mina was usually the strong one at home, protecting her parents.
###Chapter 322
Evelyn hadn't been too concerned.

But then Mr. Sherwood stepped forward and slapped Mina again.
"You disgrace! Mr. Vance is no ordinary man! If you have the guts, why didn't you sleep with Marcus Valentine? Make him take responsibility!"

Mina was speechless.

Mr. Sherwood continued yelling,
"Who will want you now? If you don't marry into a wealthy family, how can your mother and I stay here? Your grandmother will be thrown out! She won't be buried with your grandfather! Mina, don't you understand?!"

Mina suddenly laughed.
"So I'm just a commodity to you? Something to trade for your luxury?"

Mrs. Sherwood paused.
"How can you say that?"

Mr. Sherwood's face was grim.
"No matter what you say, that child must be terminated. Don't tell anyone."

Mrs. Sherwood pulled out an abortion pill.
"I bought the medication. Take it now. It'll be over in two hours. You'll still be a pure girl..."

Mina's eyes widened.

She looked at her parents, feeling chilled to the bone.

She never imagined they would do this to her...

Mina instinctively protected her abdomen.
"Mom, I won't take it... I want this baby!"

Mrs. Sherwood frowned.
"Why won't you listen?"

Mr. Sherwood grabbed Mina's arm.
The usually timid man was surprisingly strong.
"Mina, don't blame us. We have no choice... You must take the pill... You have to marry into a wealthy family..."

Terrified, Mina struggled, but couldn't break free from her father's grip.

He pried her mouth open.
Mrs. Sherwood prepared to force the pill in...

Evelyn shouted urgently,
"Uncle! Aunt! You can't terminate the baby!"

Mina struggled and cried, but couldn't escape her parents' hold...

Mrs. Sherwood said sternly,
"Grace, I know you're good friends, but you don't understand. You'll regret youthful impulsiveness! This bastard can't stay!"

Evelyn watched helplessly as the pill neared Mina's mouth, wishing she could reach through the phone.

She could only yell,
"The baby is Marcus Valentine's!!"
Evelyn’s words hung in the air.

The phone fell silent.

Mrs. Sherwood froze, staring at her phone in disbelief. "What did you say?"

Mina was pinned to the chair, her jaw clamped shut. Silent tears streamed down her face.

Evelyn’s voice was calm. "The baby is Marcus Valentine’s. If you harm this child, Marcus will destroy the Sherwood family."

It was the only way to protect Mina now.

Mrs. Sherwood’s hand trembled. The pill dropped to the floor.

Mr. Sherwood released Mina, staring at her stomach in shock. "Is it true?"

Mina slumped onto the bed, exhausted.

Mrs. Sherwood rushed to her side. "Mina, are you okay? Did your father hurt you? Does your stomach hurt?"

Mr. Sherwood nodded eagerly. "Is the baby alright?"

Mina covered her stomach and moved away from their touch. Their fake concern felt like a cruel joke.

Mr. Sherwood frowned. "Mina, don’t blame us. We only want what’s best for you. A girl like you should marry into wealth! Why didn’t you tell us sooner? If we knew it was Mr. Valentine’s child, I would never have treated you this way."

Mrs. Sherwood chimed in. "Exactly! When did this happen? Does Mr. Valentine know? What did he say?"

Mr. Sherwood rubbed his hands together excitedly. "Does he refuse to take responsibility? Don’t worry! I’ll speak to your uncle. We’ll go to the Valentine family and demand answers! We’ll make him marry you!"

Wealth and status seemed within their grasp.

Mrs. Sherwood beamed. "The dowry must be substantial too! Marcus Valentine is far superior to Alexander Vance! Mina, you have excellent taste!"

Mina looked up sharply. "Don’t you dare go to him!"

Her parents stared at her, stunned.

Mina trembled with shame. She could already imagine Marcus’s mocking smile and contemptuous gaze.

Her parents filled her with despair.

Her eyes red, she declared, "If you go to Marcus Valentine, I will terminate this pregnancy immediately."

Mr. Sherwood scowled. "What nonsense are you saying?"

Mrs. Sherwood panicked. "You can’t terminate the pregnancy! How will you marry into the Valentine family then? Mina, have you lost your mind?"

Mina felt utterly drained.

She sank back onto the bed, her voice resolute. "If anyone goes to Marcus Valentine, I will get an abortion."

Mrs. Sherwood and Mr. Sherwood exchanged suspicious glances. "Is the child really not Mr. Valentine’s?"

Mina saw her mother glance at the abortion pill again. She clenched her fists.

How pathetic.

She only wanted to preserve her last shred of dignity in front of Marcus. Yet her parents kept dragging her deeper into disgrace.

She remained silent, staring at them defiantly.

Evelyn spoke up quickly. "It is Marcus Valentine’s. The pregnancy is still early. He refuses to acknowledge it. We need to wait three months for an amniocentesis."

Mr. and Mrs. Sherwood frowned. "Mr. Valentine denies it? What did you do to anger him?"

Why would he deny it?

Why did Marcus think she was promiscuous? It was all for her parents’ benefit. She had attended countless business dinners to secure projects for them.

And now they had the nerve to ask what she did wrong.

Tears finally streamed down Mina’s face. She closed her eyes in despair. "Get out! Both of you, get out!"

Mr. Sherwood was startled. "Don’t get upset! Stress is bad for the baby! We’re leaving, we’re going now…"

Mrs. Sherwood added hurriedly, "Mina, don’t worry. If the child is Mr. Valentine’s, he won’t get away. Just stay calm… We’ll go."

Mr. and Mrs. Sherwood left the room, closing the door gently behind them.

Mina lowered her head into her hands.

Evelyn called out to her through the video. "Mina, are you okay?"

Mina buried her face in her knees, her voice muffled. "Grace, I want to leave this place… I want to go to your home."

"Okay, I’ll come get you right away."

Evelyn stood up and handed Chloe to the nanny.

The well-behaved little girl didn’t cling to her.

Evelyn put on her jacket and headed downstairs.

Alexander followed her. "I’ll drive you."

He worried about Evelyn driving, especially since he’d never seen her behind the wheel.

Evelyn paused. "Can you… go there?"

Alexander’s jaw tightened.

It was the Sherwood residence, his mother’s family home. Although Mina had moved out, she lived close to the main estate. The Sherwoods were always around…

Evelyn feared Alexander would be upset seeing them. She opened the driver’s door. "I’ll go alone."

Before she could get in, Alexander stopped her.

He placed a hand on her shoulder and guided her to the passenger seat. Then he slid into the driver’s seat.

Evelyn started to protest, but Alexander spoke in a low voice. "I don’t trust your driving."

Evelyn understood. He was worried those people might confront her again. He had become her constant shadow, even neglecting his work.

She didn’t argue further.

The car started and soon arrived at the Sherwood residence.

A graceful figure stood at the entrance.

Mr. and Mrs. Sherwood saw the visitor and rose quickly, bowing respectfully. "Miss Yvonne, what brings you here?"

Yvonne Sherwood was the eldest daughter of the main branch, same generation as Mr. Sherwood. She had married into the Vance family in San Francisco for an alliance. Upon discovering Robert Vance had a love child, she underwent a C-section at seven months, left the baby at the Vances, and returned alone to the Sherwoods in New York.

She was Alexander’s mother.

Her status in the main family was exceptional. Even Kevin Sherwood respected his elder sister.

Yvonne spoke calmly. "I heard Mina wasn’t feeling well. I came to see her."

Mr. and Mrs. Sherwood nodded, watching her ascend the stairs.

Before she reached the top step, a car pulled up outside. Evelyn and Alexander got out.

Yvonne’s gaze swept over them, then froze on Alexander.

Evelyn had intended to leave Alexander in the car. But after witnessing the Sherwoods’ behavior, Alexander insisted on accompanying her to ensure Mina’s safe departure.

As they entered, they encountered Yvonne.

Her cool elegance was impossible to ignore.

Alexander’s eyes met hers unexpectedly.
###Chapter 323

Alexander Vance’s steps halted abruptly.

His entire body stiffened like stone.

His gaze locked onto the woman before him.

Yvonne Sherwood had left him since birth.

This was the first true meeting between mother and son.

Alexander had secretly looked at her photos as a child.

Though he no longer needed a mother as an adult, he never forgot her face.

He recognized her instantly.

The world spun.

Violence and gloom surged within him instantly.

He stared at her with resentment in his eyes.

But the next second—

Yvonne indifferently looked away.

She turned and went upstairs without expression.

As if he were just an insignificant stranger.

Alexander’s heart clenched painfully.

It felt worse than before.

So his years of lingering resentment meant nothing to her.

Of course, she never cared about this son.

She didn’t care about his frail body from being born seven months premature.

She heartlessly abandoned him at the Vance family, leaving him to fend for himself.

The hostility around Alexander gradually faded.

Only a dead, hollow emptiness remained.

In this world, he truly had no one who cared for him.

Evelyn Ross keenly sensed his emotional shift.

Just as she was wondering, Mrs. Sherwood quickly stepped forward, blocking Alexander’s view.

Mrs. Sherwood spoke meaningfully, "Grace, is this your friend? Don’t stare like that. The eldest lady is of noble status. She’s not someone you can look at directly."

Eldest lady?

Evelyn understood instantly.

Seeing the loneliness surrounding the man, her heart swelled with both pain and anger.

Angry that Yvonne didn’t recognize her own son.

Heartbroken for Alexander’s current sorrow.

She didn’t bother maintaining Grace’s persona and went straight to Alexander.

She tightly held his hand.

Alexander was slightly startled and turned to look at her.

Evelyn nodded firmly.

Alexander lowered his gaze, his voice hoarse. "I’m fine."

This suppressed demeanor was nothing like "fine."

She gently patted the back of his hand and turned to Mrs. Sherwood. "I’m here to see Mina."

Mrs. Sherwood quickly nodded. "Good, Grace. You’re Mina’s best friend. Persuade her not to do anything foolish. Marrying into the Valentine family with this child means a life of endless luxury!"

Evelyn listened with a tense face, then looked at Alexander. "Wait for me here."

But Alexander followed closely behind her. "I’m fine."

Evelyn had no choice but to take him upstairs.

Just outside Mina’s room, they heard Yvonne’s cool voice. "Mina, if you don’t want to marry Marcus Valentine, don’t keep this child. You can’t let a child bind your entire life."

A low, mocking laugh came from Alexander.

Evelyn’s heart ached again.

She knocked on the door, ignoring Yvonne, and spoke directly to Mina. "Mina, I’m here to take you home."

"Okay."

Mina had already packed a few clothes. She immediately came out upon hearing this.

Evelyn took the suitcase and was about to carry it downstairs when Alexander silently took it and went down.

Yvonne suddenly spoke. "Wait."

Alexander paused.

Yvonne walked over, her eyes sweeping over him again before landing on Evelyn. "Please take care of Mina."

"It’s no trouble. We’re good friends." Evelyn’s voice held a hint of hostility. "I’m not heartless enough to abandon Mina."

Her words carried a deeper meaning.

Yvonne seemed not to notice, merely nodding faintly.

Evelyn helped Mina downstairs.

The three of them, each lost in their own thoughts, left in silence.

With Yvonne present, Mr. and Mrs. Sherwood didn’t dare to stop them too much. They could only watch Mina leave.

They returned to the Nan Family Villa.

The three-story house had five bedrooms.

They prepared a guest room for Mina.

Evelyn looked at Mina, then at the dejected Alexander, unsure who to comfort first.

While hesitating, Alexander spoke again. "I’m fine."

Three "I’m fine"s in a row were clearly a cover-up.

She said to Mina, "Mina, rest first."

Then she pulled Alexander back to the master bedroom. After closing the door, she immediately hugged him.

She tried to use her warmth to dispel the cold emptiness around him.

Alexander hugged her back. In a angle she couldn’t see, the corner of his lips curled up slightly.

Seeing Yvonne did upset him.

But Alexander was used to restraining his emotions.

Since childhood, he had grown accustomed to this kind of pain.

After the initial shock, he had already calmed down.

There were too many people in this family who received her care and concern.

First Chloe, and now Mina.

If he didn’t act a bit pitiful, when would she have time for him?

Completely unaware she was being manipulated, Evelyn: "..."

...

Mina’s sadness came quickly and left just as fast.

By the next morning, she had returned to normal, even busy with work. "President Nan, the cooperation agreement between the Sherwood Group, Valentine family, and NanTech Ltd. has been signed. There’s a celebration banquet tonight. We need to attend."

Evelyn nodded. "No problem."

She instinctively looked at Alexander.

The man said slowly, "I’ll go too... is that okay?"

Mina couldn’t help poking Evelyn. "Your little pup is quite clingy, isn’t he?"

Evelyn was both amused and exasperated.

That evening, they left Chloe with the nanny. The three of them dressed up and attended the celebration banquet.

Alexander didn’t bring a suit and still wore his sporty casual clothes.

The car stopped at the banquet entrance. Evelyn and Mina got out first. Alexander went to park the car.

He was treated completely like a driver.

Yet he showed no sign of being offended.

Evelyn and Mina waited briefly at the entrance. After Alexander parked the car and joined them, the three entered together.

The banquet guests were all from the Valentine family, Sherwood Group, and NanTech Ltd. There was no formal dress code. Both women wore business professional attire.

Entry required signing in.

Evelyn bent down and wrote: Grace Nan.

Just then, a luxury car pulled up ostentatiously at the entrance.

Two uninvited guests got out: Fiona Valentine and Penelope Valentine.

They wore gorgeous evening gowns and became the center of attention the moment they stepped out.

Valentine Group employees immediately surrounded them. "Second Young Miss, long time no see!"

Penelope smiled in response.

Evelyn: "..."

She finished signing and moved aside.

Mina was still watching the spectacle and said to Alexander, "You sign next."

"Divorced man" Alexander Vance shot her a cold glance and signed his name with a flourish.

It was Mina’s turn to sign.

She finally looked away from the commotion and turned to the sign-in sheet.

She still didn’t know the name of Grace’s little pup...

###Chapter 324

What is written on this?

Why does the first character look so much like "second"?

Second… marriage… man?

What kind of name is that!

Who would choose a name like that!

Since Evelyn's last encounter with danger, and with Sophia Ashcroft's inexplicable arrest, he hadn't left her side.

This was probably a code name.

There was an email address and a phone number listed after it.

Fiona input the number and sent a message: [Hello, I'm from the Ashcroft Group. Tonight is the signing banquet for NanTech Ltd., the Ashcroft Group, and the Sherwood Group. Are you here?]

On a nearby sofa, Evelyn's phone vibrated.

She picked it up and saw the message.

She didn't know who it was, but she replied: [Here]

There wasn't even a punctuation mark. It was extremely brief.

Evelyn replied: [Regarding?]

The research expert was indeed aloof.

Fiona sent another message: [Any thoughts about changing jobs? Someone of your talent is wasted at NanTech Ltd. The Ashcroft Group welcomes you. Compensation is negotiable.]

Evelyn: ?

She looked at the text, momentarily stunned, feeling this couldn't be Marcus Valentine's intention.

She raised an eyebrow and replied: [I'm not interested.]

Your research results are worth hundreds of millions. How much salary can NanTech give you? How many shares? Evelyn: !!!

She curved her lips into a smile: [I'm willing.]

Fiona: ???

She stared at the text, completely baffled.

She glanced at Evelyn in the distance. The woman was leaning lazily against the wall, looking at her phone. Her bare face was particularly striking under the lights.

Fiona hated that look.

She was just middle-class, yet she dared to act arrogantly in front of her, and even hit her...

But she had to admit, that face was indeed easy on the eyes.

Fiona sent another message: [She's divorced and is keeping a boy toy. She doesn't care about you at all! Are you sure you want to throw yourself at her?]

Evelyn: "..."

She twitched the corner of her mouth, admiring the other person's imagination, and directly replied: [I'm willing.]

Fiona: "..."

This lovesick fool was hopeless!

Was the research expert's brain broken?

Fiona decided to talk face-to-face. She sent a message: [Where are you? Let's meet and talk on the second balcony.]

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. She wanted to know who had the audacity to poach from right under her nose. This was something to take up with the Ashcroft Group for breaking industry rules.

Evelyn and Mina turned around and saw Fiona Valentine standing there, acting bossy.

Evelyn frowned.

Mina sneered. "It's a public area. What do you mean you're using it? We were here first!"

Fiona coldly laughed. "This hotel belongs to the Ashcroft Group. I said I'm using it, so I'm using it! I've arranged to meet someone here. You two, get lost!"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "You arranged to meet someone?"
Evelyn Ross felt no guilt about poaching talent.
She lifted her chin defiantly.
“That’s right, I have a meeting. Want to know with whom?”

She crossed her arms, a smug smile curling her lips.
“I’m meeting your top researcher, N.
We just texted.
He’s very interested in joining the Valentine Group.
We’re meeting here to discuss it.”

Fiona Valentine’s plan was cunning.
Whether N showed up or not, her words would plant seeds of doubt in Grace Nan’s mind.
Even if N changed his mind later, Grace would never trust him again.
He’d have no choice but to join the Valentines.
She thought her strategy was brilliant.

“Grace, you’ve been so neglectful of N.
Don’t you think that’s cold?
He’s devoted to you.
You finally got divorced, and instead of acknowledging him, you’re dating some college kid.
How is that fair to him?”

“You should cherish talent.
You won’t even reveal his name.
You take all the credit for his work.
Today’s such a major event, and you didn’t give him any spotlight.

Evelyn Ross exchanged an amused glance with Mina Sherwood.
###Chapter 325
They both laughed.

Mina fired back immediately.
“Fiona Valentine, Grace’s company is none of your business.”

Fiona pretended to be concerned.
“I just can’t stand seeing someone so talented being suppressed.
If you’re afraid of losing him, treat him better.
The Valentine Group is different.
We offer a much larger stage for talent.”

Evelyn Ross cleared her throat lightly and nodded.
“Yes, the Valentine Group is certainly unique when it comes to poaching.”

Fiona pointed at her angrily.
“What do you mean ‘poaching’?
This is mutual interest!
Don’t make it sound so ugly.
Unless… you’re not going to let him go?”

Evelyn Ross raised an eyebrow.

Fiona immediately frowned.
“You’re really not going to release him?
He can file a labor dispute.
The Valentine Group will cover any penalty fees!
Grace, you’re outrageous.
Working him to the bone without giving him any recognition?
Your company is downright shameless!”

As they argued, Penelope Valentine approached with her entourage.
She overheard the last part and sighed.
“Ms. Nan, if what my sister says is true, I advise you to part on good terms.
A researcher like that has a bright future.
He shouldn’t be trapped in a small company.
Rather than a messy fallout later, it’s better to let him go now…”

Her followers, believing Evelyn was suppressing N, grew indignant.

“That’s right, Ms. Nan! You can’t treat a researcher this way!”

“N should be the star today, but no one even knows who he is!”

“Suppressing an employee like this is grounds for a lawsuit!”

Evelyn scanned the crowd and scoffed.
“Who said I’m suppressing any employee?”

Fiona feigned innocence.
“If you’re not suppressing him, why isn’t he credited on the project?
Only a code name!
It can’t be that he doesn’t want recognition, right?”

Most of the Valentine employees present were researchers.
Credit was extremely important to them.
They’d want the world to know if they worked on a major project.
Using a code name was unthinkable.

The crowd grew more outraged.
“Yes, you can’t suppress people! How could you treat a researcher like this?”

“If there’s no suppression, just have N come out and explain!”

“Is he here today? Did you even let him attend the banquet?”

“Oh my god, that’s too cruel!”

“I can’t stand this injustice! Ms. Nan, please let N join the banquet!”

Mina rolled her eyes.
“You’re all ranting without knowing the truth.
You’re ridiculous!”

The crowd fell silent.

Fiona frowned.
“How can you say that?
I’ll call N right now and have him come explain!”

She took out her phone and pretended to dial N’s number.
The moment the call connected, a phone rang—from Evelyn Ross’s pocket.
###Chapter 326

The room fell silent.

All eyes turned toward the source of the ringing phone.

Their gazes landed on Evelyn Ross.

Fiona Valentine froze.

She watched as Evelyn slowly pulled out her phone.

Fiona scoffed. "How convenient. One might think you're actually N."

Evelyn answered the call and put it on speaker. "Hello."

The same voice came from Fiona's phone. "Hello."

Fiona's eyes widened in disbelief. She stared at Evelyn, then looked down at her own phone. "You... you..."

The same two words echoed from both devices.

The crowd understood immediately.

Penelope Valentine was the first to react. "You're N?"

Evelyn ended the call and gave a slight nod.

Mina Sherwood sneered from the side. "N stands for Nan. Our Grace has always been low-key."

She continued, "Who would have thought you'd accuse her of suppressing her own employees?"

Mina turned to Fiona. "Explain how NanTech could possibly suppress its own founder?"

Fiona's face turned pale. Her entire body trembled.

Evelyn spoke calmly. "Malicious poaching constitutes unfair competition."

She stated firmly, "The Ashcroft Group owes me an explanation."

Fiona looked at Penelope in panic.

Penelope remained composed. She stepped forward. "Director Nan, this is all a misunderstanding."

She explained, "My sister isn't an Ashcroft employee. This isn't a case of poaching."

Penelope added, "She simply saw N's name missing from the project document and acted impulsively..."

Her words completely absolved Fiona.

Fiona quickly nodded. "Yes, I was just defending what I thought was right. It was a misunderstanding."

Penelope sighed. "Sister, you must learn to control your impulsiveness."

They turned to leave.

"Stop."

Mina's voice cut through the air.

They froze.

Mina sneered. "Aren't you going to apologize for your mistake?"

Fiona frowned.

Penelope gave her a look.

Fiona gritted her teeth. She muttered, "Director Nan, I apologize."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "What was that? I couldn't hear you."

Fiona's face flushed red with anger. She shouted, "I'm sorry!"

Evelyn finally seemed satisfied. "Be more careful next time."

Fiona struggled to contain her rage.

The crowd immediately swarmed around Evelyn. Their attitudes did a complete one-eighty.

"Director Nan, you developed the project independently? You're amazing!"

"Yes, you're truly incredible! And so modest!"

Someone, noticing Evelyn's youth and beauty, joked, "Director Nan, are you married?"

Those in the know looked uncomfortable.

Fiona seized the opportunity. "You asked the right person!"

She announced, "Director Nan isn't just married. She's recently divorced!"

Fiona continued with a meaningful smile, "It's because of this project. Her ex-husband was afraid of the penalty fees. He ran away."

She added pointedly, "Director Nan, a woman should focus on her family. Spending all day in the lab... no wonder your husband left."

The single men's eyes, however, lit up.

Divorced? That meant there was a chance.

Mina immediately counterattacked. "Good riddance to that scum! He saved Grace the trouble of splitting assets!"

She declared, "Our Grace has plenty of charm. She already has a new boyfriend!"

Fiona glanced toward Alexander Vance in the corner. "Is Director Nan's new boyfriend that boy toy over there?"

Mina lifted her chin. "A boy toy is a standard accessory for a successful woman. It proves our Grace has appeal!"

Fiona scoffed. "Yes, so much appeal... enough to trick a college student into thinking she's a sugar mama."

She said smugly, "A power couple is better. Like our Penelope. She's engaged to the Vance family."

Fiona turned to Evelyn. "Director Nan, which family is your boyfriend from? You didn't really pick a boy toy with no background, did you?"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow, deep in thought.

How should she answer this?

Just then, there was a commotion at the entrance.

Marcus Valentine and Kevin Sherwood entered together, chatting happily.

Kevin was asking, "I heard the Ashcrofts and Vances have a marriage arrangement. An alliance?"

Marcus paused.

Grace Nan and Evelyn Ross were twins. Evelyn was also his third uncle's daughter.

So, technically...

Marcus frowned. "The two families are already allied..."

Even if Evelyn was deceased, she was still his cousin!

Thinking of Evelyn, Marcus felt a pang of regret.

Compared to Grace's timidity, he much preferred Evelyn's bold and capable nature. If only she were still alive.

Marcus looked up and scanned the banquet hall.

His eyes immediately found Alexander Vance, even though he was sitting inconspicuously in the corner.

His longtime rival!

Marcus froze, thinking he was seeing things.

What was he doing here?

Hearing that the Ashcrofts and Vances were already allied, everyone looked at Penelope.

Fiona exclaimed in surprise, "Penelope, it was already settled? Why didn't you tell me..."

Penelope also looked bewildered.

She hadn't heard anything about it.

She looked at Marcus, but saw him excuse himself from Kevin and walk directly toward the "boy toy" that Grace Nan had brought...
###Chapter 327

Marcus Valentine's gaze landed on Alexander Vance.

The man wore simple casual attire.

He looked much younger than his actual age.

He could easily pass for a college student.

His handsome features remained striking.

Just like during their study abroad days, he still drew the attention of every woman nearby.

Marcus knew this guy had been in New York recently.

He had tried to arrange several meetings.

Alexander had declined every time.

Marcus assumed he was still drowning in grief over losing Evelyn.

He never expected to find him here.

Marcus was about to approach.

Fiona Valentine and Penelope Valentine walked over.

They spoke in unison. "Big Brother."

The three Valentine branches had not separated.

The younger generation was ranked by age.

Marcus was the eldest son of his generation.

He nodded at his two cousins.

They weren't blood-related.

But they had grown up together.

Their relationship was relatively close.

He looked back toward Alexander.

He was just about to speak.

Fiona cut in first. "Big Brother, do you know him?"

She pointed at Alexander on the sofa.

Marcus was about to nod.

Then he saw Alexander shake his head slightly.

He didn't want his identity revealed?

Dressed like that and showing up here.

Not wanting to be recognized.

What was he trying to do?

Marcus was utterly confused.

Fiona continued. "He's Grace Nan's little boyfriend."

"I heard she found him right after her divorce."

"He seems to be a college student."

"Brother, do you know him?"

Marcus was stunned.

He looked at Alexander in disbelief.

Alexander had always been aloof and cold at school.

Now he was pretending to be someone else.

Posing as a college student.

Being Grace Nan's boy toy?

Marcus followed Fiona's pointing finger.

He noticed Mina Sherwood first.

He quickly averted his eyes.

Then he looked at Evelyn.

Finally, his gaze returned to Alexander.

His lips twitched uncontrollably.

No way?

So proper on the surface.

But playing so wild in private?

Engaging in some stand-in romance?

No wonder when Evelyn first passed away.

He had looked utterly devastated.

But today his eyes held light and his expression was soft.

He had found a replacement?

Marcus's mind instantly conjured up a dramatic soap opera.

His expression became complicated when he looked at Alexander again.

He felt a sense of betrayal.

He also felt sorrow and bitterness.

And anger.

He felt betrayed on Evelyn's behalf.

She had only been gone for over a month.

And he had already found someone else?

He felt sorrow and bitterness for Alexander.

His wife had only passed away for over a month.

He could only find solace in a lookalike.

He must be struggling internally?

He felt anger for Grace Nan.

This love-struck fool.

She divorced so decisively because she already had a backup.

But did she know?

The young hunk she deeply loved.

Only saw her as a replacement?

Marcus's thoughts were a tangled mess.

He found it difficult to look directly at Alexander.

What kind of heart-wrenching plot was this?

Unbelievable!

He cleared his throat lightly.

Fiona pressed again. "Big Brother, do you know him or not?"

Marcus covered for him. "No, I don't."

Fiona breathed a sigh of relief.

Of course.

How could a boy toy possibly know her brother.

She glanced at Penelope.

She asked the question on her mind. "Big Brother, you mentioned the Vance-Valentine alliance marriage earlier."

"Has Mr. Vance arrived in New York?"

"When can we arrange to meet him?"

Marcus instinctively looked at Alexander.

"Alexander Vance?"

"Why meet him?"

Fiona smiled. "Of course, it's for Penelope... for us to meet him."

"We'll be connected through marriage eventually."

Penelope lowered her head with a shy expression.

Marcus frowned. "Is that necessary?"

Fiona and Penelope were taken aback.

They wouldn't even meet before the wedding?

A truly blind marriage?

But then again.

Even if they met and found it unsuitable.

What could they do?

They still had to go through with the alliance...

Fiona gave Penelope a reassuring look.

"Fine, we don't have to meet."

"But do you have a photo of Mr. Vance?"

"Can you show us?"

Marcus remained silent.

He did have one.

But he couldn't show it.

He cleared his throat. "No."

"Why would a man keep another man's photo?"

Penelope was disappointed again.

Fiona asked directly. "Then what does Mr. Vance look like?"

"Is he very old? Probably not."

"Wealthy aristocrats know how to maintain their appearance."

"I heard Mr. Vance is dashing and elegant..."

"Big Brother, what does Mr. Vance actually look like?"

Marcus was annoyed by her pestering.

He simply pointed at Alexander.

"He looks like him!"
###Chapter 328

Fiona and Penelope Valentine were both stunned.

They simultaneously looked at Alexander Vance.

Despite being openly discussed, the college student remained perfectly composed.

His gaze was fixed intently on the teacup before him. He didn't even lift an eyelid.

…

There had been no prior symptoms at all.

Evelyn quickly supported her as they hurried into the restroom.

His imposing aura forced Miles to take a step back.

He looked directly at Kevin, his gaze icy.

"Don't know you."

Miles was momentarily speechless.

###Chapter 329

Miles, apologize to this gentleman at once."

Daring to mention Alexander's mother again?

Evelyn's temper flared once more. She rolled up her sleeves, ready to step forward and argue, but Alexander stopped her.

Alexander looked down, his voice detached. "No apology needed."

A cold glint flashed in his eyes. "I have no mother."

"Nothing." Kevin shot his son a stern look. "Watch your mouth from now on."

Miles shut his mouth, aggrieved.

Even though they were already some distance away, Evelyn was still fuming, her cheeks flushed bright red.

Alexander: ?

Help her curse at himself?

This woman's train of thought was always so uniquely bizarre.

But… he was good at insulting people…

It reminded him of when they weren't close yet, and Evelyn had posted on social media. He'd commented underneath, helping her curse at himself. Those memories felt like just yesterday…

Seeing him lost in thought, Evelyn successfully diverted his attention and finally relaxed a little.

She then looked back toward the restroom with concern.

How was Mina doing now?

At that moment, Mina was bent over, vomiting violently.

She had thrown up everything in her stomach. Now, even acid was coming up.

Why was pregnancy so miserable?

She felt utterly weak. Her eyes and nose were red and sore from the strain. Fine beads of cold sweat dotted her forehead.

Finally catching her breath, she leaned against the sink, fumbling around trying to find a tissue to wipe her mouth.

After searching for a while without success, a hand offered her a tissue.

Mina quickly took it and wiped her mouth, turning her head. "Thank—"

The last word caught in her throat.

Standing before her was Marcus Valentine.
Mina Sherwood looked up and saw Marcus Valentine standing nearby.

She tossed the tissue into the trash can.

She straightened her posture and smoothed her slightly disheveled hair.

"Mr. Valentine, this is the ladies' room."

"I know."

Marcus leaned against the wall, a mocking smile playing on his lips.
"I came to see how badly you were throwing up. If the pregnancy is this miserable, why not just terminate it?"

Mina's fingertips trembled slightly.

She clenched her fists.
"My affairs are none of your concern, Mr. Valentine."

Marcus let out a derisive laugh.
"An out-of-wedlock pregnancy? That won't sound good if it gets out, will it?"

Mina's jaw tightened.
She remained silent and walked past him.

"Who is the father, really?"

Mina stared directly at him.
"I told you. It's you."

Marcus smirked coldly.

"In three months, we can do an amniocentesis for a DNA test."

Mina Sherwood was pregnant?

Mina furrowed her brow.

Fiona brought the glass close to Mina's nose.

The smell of alcohol hit her, and Mina's stomach churned violently.
She covered her mouth, fighting the urge to vomit.

Fiona grabbed her arm.
"Director Sherwood, pretending to be unwell is just pathetic."

"If you don't want to drink, just say so. Why put on an act?"

The executive's face turned stormy.
"Fine, if you don't want to drink, forget it. My mistake for asking!"

Other executives also showed expressions of disdain.

Mina gritted her teeth.

On one side were her professional relationships.
On the other was her secret pregnancy.

She took a deep breath, fighting back the nausea.
"I'm sorry. It's not that I won't drink. I can't drink—I'm pregnant!"
###Chapter 330

The executive halted his steps.

Every gaze in the banquet hall focused on Mina Sherwood.

Miles Sherwood suddenly pushed through the crowd.

He had heard the discussion. His face was dark with anger.

The Sherwood family still had several unmarried daughters. They could not be tainted by this scandal.

He blocked Mina's path. "Stop! You must clarify this today. Whose child is it?"

Mina instinctively glanced toward a corner of the banquet hall.

Marcus Valentine leaned against a wine table, leisurely swirling his champagne glass.

He had clearly heard the commotion, but didn't even lift an eyelid.

As if that glass of wine was more important than her life or death.

A bitter taste rose in Mina's heart.

In his eyes, this child belonged to someone else.

Why would he ever stand up for her?

Miles's eyes were icy. "This slap is for your lack of self-respect! Speak! Who is that man!"

In the corner, Marcus Valentine finally set down his glass.

His knuckles were slightly white.
###Chapter 331
Marcus Valentine paused mid-step, his gaze settling on Mina Sherwood.

Old-money families prized their public image above all.

A young woman's reputation was particularly fragile.

Fiona Valentine and Penelope Valentine had skillfully guided the conversation, and the crowd's perception of Sherwood women had already soured.

Without a clear resolution, Sherwood daughters would indeed struggle to find suitable matches.

The whip, soaked in saltwater and chili solution, inflicted agony beyond description.

Miles had experienced a single lash as a child. The memory still haunted him.

He couldn't bear the thought of his sister enduring that pain.

Mina clenched her fists, biting her lip in silence.

She couldn't say it.

Marcus Valentine would never acknowledge it.

Why humiliate herself further?

Still, Mina said nothing.

Miles scanned the crowd. "Is he here right now? Look, Mina! He hasn't stepped forward to protect you! Is a man like that really worth this?"

Mina kept her eyes downcast, silent.

Kevin arrived swiftly.

He took in Mina's condition, the surrounding crowd, and the reporters.

He knew a public resolution was essential today.

Otherwise, Sherwood family scandals would dominate tomorrow's headlines.

He looked at Mina. "Mina, this is your last chance. Will you speak or not?"

Mina shook her head.

Kevin took a deep breath and addressed the crowd. "Will no one come forward? We support love matches. Step forward now, and the Sherwood family will not pursue this. We will provide a generous dowry for Mina!"

Silence filled the room.

Evelyn glared at Marcus Valentine.

Seeing his complete indifference, she felt a surge of anger and panic.

She turned to Alexander.

He spoke calmly. "I will not help with this."

Evelyn was speechless.

Asking Alexander to claim the child was indeed unreasonable.

But she couldn't just stand by and watch Mina suffer!

His voice was quiet, yet carried to Marcus. "Mina has shamed her family. Consequences are inevitable. After five lashes, she cannot use medication due to her pregnancy. The wounds will heal poorly..."

In the center of the room.

Kevin saw that no one came forward, and Mina remained silent.

He sighed. "Bring me the whip."
###Chapter 332

Kevin Sherwood's words caused an uproar in the room.

Everyone exchanged glances, unable to believe what they had just heard.

Fiona Valentine and Penelope Valentine shared a look.

Both saw surprise in each other's eyes.

Prestigious families like the Sherwoods did indeed uphold traditions of family discipline.

But such punishments were always carried out behind closed doors.

There had never been a precedent for public execution.

Miles Sherwood was also stunned.

He hesitated before speaking. "Father, perhaps we should handle this back at home—"

Kevin cut him off decisively. "Since we've already been disgraced, let everyone see the Sherwood family's rules!"

Miles wanted to say more but held back.

He felt his father was acting unusually today.

Kevin had always been quite lenient with the collateral branches.

Moreover, Mina had already moved out and started her own household.

Such severity seemed unnecessary.

Mina stood frozen in place.

She had only intended to protect the family's reputation.

She never expected Kevin to take it this seriously.

A public punishment...

Her hand instinctively moved to cover her abdomen.

Her face paled slightly.

Evelyn Ross stepped forward with a frown. "Mr. Sherwood, administering private punishment in public is illegal."

Kevin looked at her. "I am disciplining my own junior. Who would dare interfere?"

He then turned to Mina. "Will you call the police?"

Mina pressed her lips together and remained silent.

She was indeed in the wrong today.

Miles still wanted to persuade his father.

Kevin barked sharply, "Go and retrieve the family discipline tool now!"

Miles had no choice but to turn and leave.

The hotel wasn't far from the Sherwood residence.

The round trip would take at least half an hour.

Kevin found a seat and sat down.

Mina remained standing, her fingers unconsciously twisting the hem of her clothes.

She never once looked in Marcus Valentine's direction.

Evelyn wanted to rush over and take her away.

Alexander Vance held her wrist.

"Don't rush," he said quietly. "Kevin must have his reasons for doing this."

Evelyn frowned. "What reasons?"

Alexander's gaze was deep. "Think about it carefully."

Evelyn calmed down.

Kevin wasn't an impulsive man.

He always valued the family's reputation.

Why would he do something so damaging publicly?

Unless...

Evelyn's eyes widened suddenly. "He knows the child is Marcus's?"

Alexander gave a slight nod. "If Yvonne Sherwood knows, how could Kevin not know?"

Evelyn understood immediately.

When they picked up Mina that day, Yvonne was urging her to have an abortion.

The news must have come from Mina's parents.

Kevin was forcing Marcus to take a stance!

Evelyn looked at Marcus.

He was frowning as he stared at Mina.

Clear impatience was written on his face.

Evelyn was so angry she wanted to hit him.

Alexander held her back. "It's no use. Marcus only trusts his own judgment."

Evelyn gritted her teeth. "Are we just going to watch Mina get beaten?"

Alexander's gaze remained calm. "Let's wait and see."

Evelyn could only suppress her anxiety.

Time passed minute by minute.

Fiona and Penelope moved closer to Marcus.

"I heard the Sherwood whip is really vicious..."

"One lash would definitely cause a miscarriage..."

"Good thing if it's lost. A bastard child shouldn't be kept anyway."

"But Mina seems to care a lot about this child..."

They discussed the situation with gleeful schadenfreude.

Marcus's expression grew darker.

Miles finally returned with the whip.

It was a long, black whip that gleamed.

Small barbs covered its surface.

Kevin took the whip and gave it a casual flick.

The whip struck a leather sofa.

It instantly scraped off a layer of leather.

Exposing the cotton stuffing inside.

The crowd gasped.

If that whip struck a person...

Mina trembled in fear.

She looked at the mark on the sofa, her face deathly pale.

"Kneel!" Kevin ordered sharply.

Mina bit her lower lip and pleaded softly, "Uncle, can we wait until after the baby is born—"

"Sherwood family discipline allows no negotiation!" Kevin interrupted. "Either name the father, or take five lashes!"

Mina closed her eyes in despair.

Marcus didn't believe her.

How could he possibly step forward and admit it?

She slowly bent her knees and knelt on the floor.

Kevin raised the whip.

The shadow of the whip cut through the air.

With a sharp whistling sound.

It was about to land on Mina.

"Stop!"
###Chapter 333

Evelyn's heart raced.

The whip was about to strike Mina.

She couldn't hold back any longer and was ready to rush forward to stop it.

A voice rang out first.

"Stop!"

It was Marcus.

Evelyn halted and looked at him.

Kevin's hand trembled violently.

If Marcus hadn't arrived, this act would have been impossible to conclude.

He lowered the whip and turned to Marcus.

He continued his performance.

"Marcus, the Sherwood family is carrying out its discipline. Do you intend to interfere?"

Marcus understood his implication and coughed lightly.

"The child is mine."

Gasps echoed around them.

Fiona and Penelope were stunned.

"Brother, what nonsense are you saying?"

"How could Mina's child possibly be yours?"

Marcus ignored them, his gaze fixed on Mina.

Kneeling, Mina looked up, her face a mix of shock and delight.

Was he finally acknowledging it?

Her eyes reddened, a wave of bitterness washing over her.

Kevin feigned surprise.

"It's really yours? When did you two get together?"

Mina bit her lip and remained silent.

Marcus spoke.

"It was love at first sight during our last blind date. I haven't had the chance to inform you yet, Uncle."

"Hahaha!"

Kevin immediately threw down the whip and helped Mina up.

He looked at Marcus.

"Why keep such good news from me? When do you plan to get engaged?"

Mina stiffened and glanced at Marcus.

His expressionless face made her heart sink.

What did he mean?

Marcus said calmly, "As soon as possible. I will discuss it with my parents."

Kevin laughed.

"Good! I'll await your good news!"

He turned to Miles.

"What are you standing there for? Put away the disciplinary tools! Don't let people laugh at us!"

Miles, scolded for no reason, looked aggrieved.

Kevin gave Mina a deep look and smiled.

"You young people enjoy yourselves. I'll take my leave now."

He left with Miles.

The crowd immediately swarmed around to offer congratulations.

"Congratulations, Mr. Valentine!"

"Double happiness for Minister Sherwood!"

"Wishing you a lifetime of happiness!"

Evelyn was pushed aside, a slight smile on her lips.

She asked the man beside her.

"Do you think Marcus believes it? If not, why did he step in? Unless..."

Her eyes lit up as she looked at Alexander.

"Is it because he likes Mina?"

Before she could finish, she stopped.

Alexander was staring toward the entrance.

Kevin and Miles had disappeared there.

His eyes were dark, clearly thinking of his birth mother once more.

His own uncle was right before him, yet they didn't know each other.

He hadn't even seen a photo.

The Sherwood family clearly didn't care about this nephew.

Evelyn's heart ached.

Her attention immediately shifted from Mina.

She held Alexander's hand.

"It's alright. You still have me."

Alexander snapped out of his thoughts and looked down with a soft sigh.

He tightened his grip on her hand.

"Evelyn, don't leave me."

"Okay."

"Everyone else has someone who cares for them. I only have you and Grandma. Grandma is unwell... recuperating in San Francisco. Right now, I only have you."

"I know."

Evelyn hugged him, feeling something was off.

Alexander's large hand tightened around her waist.

"Evelyn."

Evelyn stopped overthinking.

"I'm here."

...

The banquet ended with the news of Mina and Marcus's engagement.

The crowd dispersed.

Mina finally had a chance to speak with Marcus alone.

She got into his car and asked him to drive her home.

In the car.

Mina still felt it was unreal.

Was she finally going to be with the person she had loved since her youth?

Lost in her joy, Marcus's cold voice suddenly broke the silence.

"Find a time to get an abortion."

Mina froze.

She looked at him in disbelief.

"What did you say?"

Marcus irritably loosened his tie.

"Mina, stop pretending. You and your uncle put on this act just to force me, didn't you? Fine, I'll play along. But I won't be tricked into raising another man's child!"

What Alexander had seen, Marcus had also discerned.

Kevin was clearly performing.

Mina felt her entire body turn cold.

"You still don't believe the child is yours?"

Marcus tightened his tie.

"I told you I have oligospermia!"

He had seen countless doctors, tried both Eastern and Western medicine.

The doctors all said it was up to fate and to be prepared for the possibility of no children.

How could he suddenly have a child?

Was Mina creating a medical miracle for him?

Mina was shocked.

"If you don't believe it, why did you step forward?"

Why?

Marcus was taken aback.

He didn't know either.

At that moment, he had only one thought: he didn't want to see Mina get hurt.

...

Elsewhere.

Alexander drove Evelyn back to the Nan family home.

On the road, Evelyn carefully observed his expression.

The man was still frowning, his expression gloomy.

Evelyn wanted to comfort him when her phone suddenly rang.

She glanced at it. The caller ID showed an unknown number.

Frowning, she answered.

An electronic voice came from the other end.

"Grace, Mina and Marcus are engaged. You've completed your first task."

Evelyn's heart skipped a beat, her pupils constricting.

She wanted to ask who it was, but she calmed down.

Grace wouldn't ask that.

She lowered her eyes and spoke softly.

"I want to go home. Can I?"

"Heh, want to come back now? Because your mother, Sophia, has been captured?"

Evelyn clenched her fingers.

"How is she? I want to see her."

"You can."

The other party laughed.

"I'll give you the route home."
###Chapter 334

Evelyn Ross showed no hint of joy on her face.

She asked directly, "What are the terms?"

"Complete the tasks I give you."

Evelyn scoffed.

She knew they wouldn't make it easy.

"What if I refuse?"

The voice on the other end laughed. "Grace, your mother is a traitor to the Nan family. What do you think happens to traitors who are caught?"

Evelyn's fingers trembled. "What happens?"

"Solitary confinement is the lightest punishment. For your mother's case, it would be the water dungeon. Only a little food each day. Some are paraded through the streets and whipped. The most severe punishment is the water cage."

Evelyn found it utterly absurd.

She even glanced around her surroundings.

Was this really the twenty-first century?

How could such private punishments still exist?

But she wasn't naive.

The world was vast. There were always corners the sunlight couldn't reach.

The more they said this, the more it proved Sophia Ashcroft was currently safe.

Evelyn lowered her gaze. "Haven't I been obedient? I handled the situation with Mina Sherwood and Marcus Valentine."

"Hahaha, indeed. Grace, I underestimated you before. You hid your true self so well. You've surprised me. Now show me your full capability! Let me see if you deserve to come home!"

The voice was filled with excitement. "The first task is nearly complete. The second task will be sent to your phone in a couple of days."

The call ended.

Evelyn stared at her phone, her brow furrowed.

Alexander Vance had muted himself earlier and now spoke. "What's wrong?"

Evelyn relayed the conversation.

Alexander's eyes darkened. "That place might not be within the country. I'll have people search globally."

The world was too big.

Her mother could be hidden in any corner.

Even on some unknown, isolated island.

No wonder the Ashcroft and Vance families couldn't find her.

Evelyn pressed her lips together tightly.

She only had one path left.

Complete the tasks.

Inside Marcus Valentine's car.

Marcus irritably loosened his tie.

He looked at Mina. "If you want to keep this child, forget about marrying me. Your choice."

Mina gave a bitter smile.

She turned to look out the window. "Don't worry. I won't cling to you."

Her attitude annoyed Marcus even more.

The rest of the ride passed in silence.

The car stopped outside the Nan family villa.

Marcus finally realized. "You're not going back to the Sherwood residence?"

"Staying with Grace for now."

Mina pushed the car door open. "Thanks. Goodbye."

She walked through the gate.

Leaning against the back of the door, she covered her heart.

Her jaw was tight, and her eyes slowly reddened.

Marcus's words cut like a knife.

She did like him, but she wasn't a masochist.

She would go abroad tomorrow.

Take the child and leave forever.

Her phone vibrated.

A WhatsApp message from Kevin Sherwood: [Mina, the Valentine family is coming to propose tomorrow. Remember to come back.]

Mina gripped her phone.

She replied: [Uncle, he and I aren't suited.]

She walked towards the living room.

But she overheard a conversation inside.

Grace's voice was low. "Why was their first task to make Mina marry Marcus Valentine?"

Mina froze instantly.

She hadn't misunderstood.

She suddenly remembered Grace mentioning a task before.

Back then, Grace said: "They want me to scheme against the most important person."

She thought the most important person was Zack Davis.

It was her all along?

And later…

Grace was soft-hearted, yet she cut ties because of something Zack said.

Was that to protect her?

Mina trusted Grace.

She knew Grace wouldn't harm her.

Her situation with Marcus was just a series of misunderstandings.

The voice of the boy toy came through: "Not sure. But you accidentally completed the first task."

Grace said, "If Marcus treats Mina poorly, I won't let them get married. I need to get the full story when Mina gets back."

Mina's eyes welled up.

She knew it…

The boy toy asked, "What about your mom? How will you save her if you don't complete the tasks?"

Mina's pupils constricted.

Save her mom?

No wonder Grace had been so preoccupied lately.

Her mother was captured?

So Grace was being forced to complete tasks?

Grace's voice was firm. "No matter what, we can't sacrifice Mina's happiness."

Mina's eyes reddened.

Grace always thought of her first…

She quietly retreated.

She stood outside the villa.

Her phone vibrated again.

Another message from Kevin Sherwood: [Good girl, had a fight with Marcus?]

Messages from her parents also popped up:

[Mina, stop making trouble. Marrying young master Valentine is your good fortune! Your reputation is ruined. Who else can you marry besides him?]

Mina looked at the messages and suddenly laughed.

Her refusal and plans to leave were just prideful delusions.

It turned out she never had a choice.

She replied: [Come pick me up.]
###Chapter 335

Mina Sherwood moved back home.

Evelyn Ross was surprised when she received the video call. "So suddenly? What about your parents..."

Mina beamed on the screen. "I'm Marcus Valentine's fiancée now. They practically worship me. They wouldn't dare lay a finger on me. Don't worry! I'm the Sherwood family's treasure now!"

Mina nodded. "He believes it. It's a shotgun wedding."

She sighed softly. "But with his playboy nature, he wouldn't marry me if not for this child."

"Are you stupid?"

Mina winked at her. "Of course I have to seize this great opportunity. I don't want him. I want the status of being a Valentine! Once I marry him, let's see if Fiona and Penelope Valentine dare act arrogant in front of us again!"

Evelyn wanted to persuade her further, but Mina suddenly turned serious. "Grace, I'm not like you. I was born into a powerful family. Marriage alliances are an unavoidable duty. My reputation is already ruined. If I don't marry him, my uncle will just sell me off to another family. After thinking it over, this is the best outcome."

Evelyn still looked at her with concern. "Are you really sure?"

Mina smiled. "His parents are here to discuss the wedding. I have to go now~"

The camera panned to show several luxury cars entering the courtyard. Evelyn could only nod.

Alexander said calmly, "He was willing to wear the green hat for Mina. What else wouldn't he accept?"

Evelyn tilted her head. "Is that really it?"

Of course not!

Marcus Valentine was currently staring at Mina, fury burning in his eyes.

She clearly said yesterday she wouldn't marry him.

His parents came today to propose. He specifically came along to see if she had changed her mind. He never expected her to actually agree?

Mrs. Valentine held Mina's hand and praised her. "Good girl! We spoiled Marcus. If he ever bullies you, come find me! I'll stand up for you!"

"Okay."

Mrs. Valentine grinned from ear to ear. "You're so beautiful. My precious grandchild will be gorgeous too!"

Mr. Valentine stared directly at Mina's belly. It was rude, but he couldn't look away thinking about his precious grandchild being there.

All these years, the company was managed by his third brother. It would be passed to his son later, bypassing him, the mediocre eldest son.

He had been idle at home with nothing to do.

If this child was born, wouldn't he get to dote on his grandchild?!

His whole life, he relied on his father when young.

Relied on his brother as an adult.

Now nearly fifty-five, he would rely on his son.

At eighty, he would rely on his grandson!

Perfect!!

Both of them were extremely satisfied with this future daughter-in-law!!

Marcus watched them, then his gaze fell on Mina. His expression shifted several times, extremely unpleasant.

The two families began discussing the wedding date.

Marcus spoke coldly. "No rush. She's pregnant, after all."

Mrs. Valentine immediately cut him off. "Nonsense! We need to hurry because she's pregnant! It won't look good when her belly gets big! Right, Mina?"

Mina lowered her head. "You're right, ma'am."

"I think within three months is best!"

Mrs. Valentine made the decision directly. "How about picking an auspicious date next month?"

It was only the beginning of the month now...

Remembering the task Grace had given her, the sooner the wedding the better, Mina spoke softly. "Ma'am, that... might not be appropriate?"

Marcus sneered inwardly. At least this woman had some shame.

Just as he thought that, he heard Mina say, "I want to have it this month."

Marcus: ???

Mrs. Valentine and Mr. Valentine were both stunned. Even Mina's parents and Kevin Sherwood coughed repeatedly, giving her meaningful looks.

No girl should be so desperate to marry!

Mina ignored them, only looking at Mrs. Valentine. "Is that possible?"

Mrs. Valentine nodded. "It's possible, but it would be so rushed. The wedding dress, venue... you might feel wronged..."

Mina said urgently, "We can just get the marriage certificate. The ceremony isn't necessary."

"How can that be?" Mrs. Valentine gripped her hand tightly. "You are our recognized daughter-in-law. We can't wrong you! This month it is! With the Valentine and Sherwood families' resources, I refuse to believe we can't arrange a wedding in a few days!"

Mina nodded. "Okay."

Marcus: ????

The parents picked a date. Mina chose the earliest one, just one week later.

The two families began discussing wedding details. They asked Mina to take Marcus to a room to rest.

Mina agreed obediently and led Marcus upstairs. The moment they entered the room, Marcus grabbed Mina. He pinched her chin, forcing her to look up.

Marcus said viciously, "What? Planning to abort the child? The wedding is in a week. There's still time for the abortion pill, right?"

Marcus stared at her, then suddenly laughed scornfully. "So that's why you're in such a hurry to marry me. You know the test can be done after three months. You insist on getting married before three months?!"

Mina's face paled.

###Chapter 336

###Chapter 337

Alexander hadn't lost weight?

It was just that Evelyn had seen his sunken cheeks.

She had found ways to help him regain it in the past week.

Evelyn was the best at cooking.

Her meals were nutritious, balanced, and delicious.

Alexander was nurtured back to vitality.

A calm, confident smile graced his face.

He was completely different from the dazed man at the funeral.

So Chloe could tell at a glance.

He wasn't sad at all.

This was someone else's private matter.

He couldn't just rush over because of Chloe's words and ask, "Mr. Vance, why haven't you lost weight?"

Chloe was so agitated tears streamed down.

Chloe's chest heaved with anger.

Her small face was pale.

"This is his freedom."

"Brother, I won't allow him to stray!"

"He can be busy with work."

"But he cannot stray, brother!"

"Chloe, everyone walks their own path in life."

"You should learn from him."

"Try to move on soon..."

"No!" Chloe shook her head.

"I don't want to move on!"

"I want to remember our benefactor!"

"Our benefactor was so good."

"She deserves to be remembered by everyone in this world!"

With that, Chloe suddenly stood up.

"So, he cannot stray!"

"He doesn't deserve to stray!"

Ryan quickly stood up and followed behind her.

They headed towards Alexander and Evelyn's table.

Unfortunately, they had only taken two steps.

The people at the table had already finished eating.

Alexander had found Evelyn again after losing her.

His eyes and heart held only her.

Now, he held her by the waist.

The two of them, with their backs to Chloe, went out towards the parking lot.

Chloe chased closely behind.

Unfortunately, both Alexander and Evelyn were impatient people.

They walked very fast.

Chloe couldn't catch up no matter how hard she tried.

She arrived in the underground parking lot, breathless.

But there was no sign of the two.

She lowered her eyes in disappointment.

Bent over, she supported herself on her legs, gasping for air.

Ryan came up behind her and held her.

"You haven't eaten properly for so many days."

"Low blood sugar, right?"

Chloe didn't speak.

Her eyes still searched everywhere.

Just then, a car's headlights turned on ahead.

It started slowly and drove past them.

Chloe immediately saw Alexander in the driver's seat!

She chased after it for two steps.

Then she saw the face of the woman in the passenger seat...

Chloe was instantly stunned.
###Chapter 338

Chloe Roland's pupils constricted violently.

That face flashed before her eyes for just an instant.

Her heart stopped beating.

Her breath froze in her chest.

She stared dead ahead.

Her fingers unconsciously clenched the hem of her clothes.

Her fingertips trembled slightly.

Was it an illusion?

Or had her longing made her sick?

The car turned the corner and disappeared.

Chloe snapped back to reality.

She grabbed Ryan Roland's arm. "Brother! Did you see that? It was our savior! It must be her!"

Her voice was choked with tears.

Her eyes were already red.

"Quick, follow them!"

Chloe's voice was urgent.

Ryan nodded. "Alright."

They quickly got into the car.

The engine roared as they sped after the other vehicle.

She leaned against the passenger seat, her brow furrowed.

"Mina is definitely hiding something from me."

The smiling face in the video was too perfect.

It was so perfect it didn't seem real.

Evelyn sighed. "I want her to be happy."

Her phone suddenly vibrated.

Evelyn looked down.

Her body instantly went rigid.

"The mission is here."

A text from an international number.

The content was brief and cold.

[First mission nearing completion. Second mission: Secure cooperation with the remaining three families in New York.]

Evelyn read it aloud.

Her expression grew serious.

"That's simply impossible."

The five great families kept each other in check.

No company had ever collaborated with three at once.

Alexander raised an eyebrow. "You know the New York situation well?"

Evelyn gave a light laugh. "I haven't been idle this past month."

The Roland family might still be a possibility.

But the Evans and Cortez families would be extremely difficult.

She rubbed her temples.

It seemed she would have to call in some favors.

The car entered the Nan family garage.

Evelyn went straight into the study.

She opened her laptop and began researching plans.

Alexander followed and sat opposite her.

But his gaze kept drifting toward Evelyn.

He suddenly remembered something.

His fingers paused on the keyboard.

She was late this month.

There had been no signs last night either.

He secretly opened a web browser and searched.

"Reasons for a late period"

The first result showed it was a normal phenomenon.

As he scrolled down, his pupils constricted.

"Possible pregnancy"

The four words slammed into his vision.

Alexander shot to his feet.

His laptop nearly crashed to the floor.

Evelyn looked up. "What's wrong?"

"Nothing."

Alexander's voice was hoarse.

He walked over to Evelyn.

His eyes fell on her flat stomach.

He stared for a long time without looking away.

The nanny passed by the door and shook her head with a sigh.

The master was far too clingy.

Like a large cat keeping watch.

…

Outside the Nan residence.

Their savior actually lived here.

With Alexander Vance.

It didn't match their status.

###Chapter 339

No one was outside.

Chloe nodded vigorously, tears suddenly welling up.

DNA test results couldn't be falsified.

###Chapter 340

This reason was flawless.

He had to protect his boss's identity.

A message from James Klein: [Boss, should we postpone this month's iron infusion?]

That was when she needed the iron infusion.

The result was just a delayed period.

She couldn't get pregnant now.

At the Roland residence.

###Chapter 344
