Chloe glanced at her several times, feeling it was too much.

Once her own makeup was done, Chloe lowered her lashes. "Go do her makeup."

The makeup artist nodded and walked over.

The others immediately looked at Chloe.

Chloe kept a straight face. "I just don't want to make things difficult for my cousin."

It wouldn't look good if one of the six bridesmaids was left without makeup.

The others exchanged glances and chuckled softly. "Even with makeup, it's useless. During the groom's procession, the groomsmen will escort the bridesmaids. With her status, which groomsman would invite her?"

In the groom's procession, the bride is taken away by the groom, and the bridesmaids are escorted by the groomsmen.
If no groomsman invited Evelyn, the Sherwoods would arrange a car, but it would be embarrassing.

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.
Alexander was certainly qualified to be Marcus's groomsman, but he wasn't coming today—he didn't want to come to the Sherwood house, didn't want to reveal his identity, and only wanted to be her personal bodyguard.

If no groomsman came for her, she'd just drive herself!
No big deal!

...

Time passed.

The six bridesmaids finished their makeup and waited another half hour before Mina's bridal makeup was ready.

The bridesmaids returned to Mina's bridal suite.

As soon as Evelyn entered, she met Mina's worried gaze.
She smiled slightly to indicate everything was fine, and Mina sighed in relief.

The subsequent events went much more smoothly.

Evelyn noticed these society girls knew how to act proper and gracious in public.

Except for Chloe shooting her a few dirty looks, no one else caused trouble.

Chloe also knew it wasn't the place to make a scene, though her expression remained unfriendly.

Soon, it was time for the groom to arrive.

But there was no movement outside.

Tina muttered under her breath, "Why aren't they here yet?"

Mina looked at the clock on the wall, her thoughts drifting back to yesterday when she and Marcus registered their marriage...

Getting married the day before the wedding was an agreement between the two families.

She arrived at City Hall early and waited a long time.
It was almost closing time when Marcus finally showed up.

He offered no explanation, directly taking out his household register and ID to complete the registration.

Holding the freshly issued marriage certificate, Mina felt dazed.

She had never dared to hope she could marry the man she had secretly loved for years...

Lost in thought, Marcus suddenly looked at her, his voice cold and sharp. "Mina, we're married now. Shouldn't you finally tell me who the father of this child is? At least let me know whose green hat I'm wearing!"

Mina was stunned.
After a long moment, she replied seriously, "It's yours."

Marcus scoffed. "Still won't admit it even now?"

Mina lowered her eyes. "Whether you believe me or not, this is the last time I'll explain: that night was my first time, and you were the only man. I am certain the child is yours!"

Marcus was utterly stunned. "What did you say? That night was your first time? But you said then... it wasn't your first?"

It was because of those words that Marcus had labeled her a loose woman.

Mina clenched her fists, smiling bitterly. "You said to me, 'Not your first time, right?' What was I supposed to say?"

Marcus was completely dumbfounded.
Marcus Valentine froze.

He then let out a scornful laugh.

"Fine. You're really something."

He flung a test report at her. The paper grazed Mina Sherwood's cheek.

"See for yourself! I actually believed your nonsense, thought there was that one in a million chance, and got tested again! And the result? I humiliated myself for nothing!"

Marcus's eyes were red-rimmed, his fists clenched.

That night at the Sherwood residence, he thought he had the upper hand, tormenting Mina to vent his anger.

Afterward, he felt no satisfaction.

He despised himself for bullying a woman, yet he couldn't stop thinking about her words.

Compelled, he went to the hospital.

The doctor who had been treating him for years looked at the report and shook his head with a sigh. "Mr. Valentine, azoospermia is indeed incurable. You need to accept it."

Azoospermia...

Oligospermia offered a sliver of hope. Azoospermia meant none. Zero.

His eyes bloodshot, Marcus gripped her shoulders. "You forced me to acknowledge the child, and I did. You wanted to get married, so we are. You wanted to keep the baby, I agreed. Now I'm just asking one thing. Who is he? Who is worth you protecting like this?"

Mina stared at the report, her expression dazed.

Her silence infuriated him.

He released her with a cold laugh. "Won't say? Fine. I won't be at the wedding tomorrow. Let's see how you handle that!"

He stormed out, slamming the door.

Mina looked at the wall clock.

The auspicious time for the groom's procession had arrived. Outside, it remained silent.

Her parents kept glancing anxiously toward the door. Her mother hurried in, urging, "Mina, why isn't Mr. Valentine here yet? We'll miss the timing if we don't leave soon! Call him, quickly!"

Mina lowered her gaze and obediently dialed his number.

It rang twice before being abruptly disconnected.

A bitter taste rose in her heart.

He really wasn't coming.

A wedding without a groom.

After today, she, Mina Sherwood, would become the laughingstock of all New York.

Her mother, seeing she'd made the call, rushed out again.

Chloe Roland and Tina Sherwood watched the drama unfold from the side. Evelyn Ross stayed close, asking softly, "What's wrong?"

Mina forced a smile. "It's nothing."

Evelyn immediately took out her phone and called Marcus.

Her call was also hung up.

She stood up. "I'll go find him."

Mina grabbed her wrist. "Don't."

She looked up, her eyes unsettlingly calm. "The auspicious time has come. If he's not here, we'll go to the venue ourselves."

Her words caused an uproar in the room.

It wasn't just the bridesmaids present; many relatives and friends who had come to see the bride off were there. Instantly, whispers filled the air.

"Did Mr. Valentine call off the wedding?"

"Did he change his mind about Mina? But she's quite pretty…"

"After the family split, the second branch treats her like a tool. I heard she often accompanies clients for drinks!"

"Really? That's so degrading!"

"Once they branched out, they're not the main family anymore. Life must be hard…"

"That child in her belly… maybe it's not Mr. Valentine's?"

"Mr. Valentine isn't stupid. Would he acknowledge it if it wasn't his? But I heard he only acknowledged it last time because Kevin Sherwood set him up…"

"A shotgun wedding, then."

"Mina sure has skills, managing to land Mr. Valentine as her backup plan after all these years…"

The comments grew increasingly harsh.

Mina's nails dug into her palms, but she felt no pain.

A wave of humiliation washed over her, making her eyes burn.

She thought she was prepared.

She knew he wouldn't come. She knew she would face this. She told herself not to care.

But the words wormed their way into her ears, the damage already done.

It turned out ignoring other people's judgment was this difficult.

Among the bridesmaids, Tina Sherwood leaned toward Penelope Valentine. "Miss Valentine, is your elder brother really not coming?"

Penelope frowned. "I'm not sure either… I saw him going out drinking last night. Maybe he overslept?"

Someone nearby immediately asked, "Drinking the night before his wedding? Why?"

Penelope seemed to realize she had said too much and quickly waved her hands. "I don't know… Big brother should come. The marriage certificate is already signed. He can't just get a divorce, right?"

The word "divorce" ignited the gossip.

"Mr. Valentine regrets it? He really wants a divorce?"

"Getting dumped the day after the wedding… the bride is so pitiful…"

Everyone sighed as if Mina had already been discarded.

Evelyn looked at her with concern. "Mina, you…"

Mina gripped her hand tightly. "Evelyn, I'm fine."

Her voice carried a slight choke.

She stood up abruptly, gathering her gown. "We're not waiting. Let's go to the wedding venue!"

Her back was straight, her chin slightly raised, maintaining the last shred of her dignity.

She walked out, step by step, and descended the stairs.

Her father, seeing this, rushed over and scolded harshly, "Did you offend Mr. Valentine? Why isn't he here!"

Mina remained silent.

"Such a disgrace on your wedding day! How did I end up with a daughter like you!"

Enraged, her father raised his hand, intent on reclaiming the lost face from her.

Just then! A commotion erupted at the entrance.
###Chapter 347

Everyone turned their heads.

A line of wedding cars entered the Sherwood family gates.

The procession came to a neat stop.

The lead car door opened.

Marcus Valentine stepped out in a black suit.

His face was dark. His eyes were tired.

Mina Sherwood stood frozen.

She stared at him in disbelief.

He had clearly said he wasn't coming...

Marcus ignored the surrounding gazes.

He strode toward Mina.

Mr. Sherwood hurried to greet him.

"Mr. Valentine, was the traffic bad?"

Mr. Sherwood tried to offer him an excuse.

Marcus scoffed.

"I overslept."

His voice was hoarse and impatient.

Anyone could see his displeasure.

Mr. Sherwood forced an awkward smile.

"It's not late at all. We're just glad you're here."

He reached out to usher Marcus inside.

"No need."

Marcus cut him off.

"We'll miss the auspicious time if we delay further."

He looked at Mina.

"Are you coming or not?"

Mina's eyes grew warm.

She lowered her head and whispered, "Yes."

Marcus turned and walked toward the wedding car.

He did not carry her.

Mina gathered her wedding dress and followed.

They got into the car.

Mina suddenly remembered Evelyn Ross.

She turned to look for her.

Outside, the crowd shifted.

It was impossible to distinguish anyone.

The groomsmen began arranging the bridesmaids into cars.

The Sherwood young ladies, along with Penelope Valentine and Chloe Roland, had dedicated escorts.

Only Evelyn Ross was left standing alone.

Her appearance was too striking.

She stood out prominently among the bridesmaids.

A groomsman moved to approach her.

Penelope Valentine stopped him.

"Miss Ross offended Chloe."

"Don't cause trouble."

Chloe Roland was the Roland family treasure.

No one wanted trouble.

The groomsmen all avoided Evelyn.

She stood there alone.

The center of attention.

Yet she showed no trace of embarrassment.

Evelyn remained completely composed.

She even surveyed the car line.

Looking for an empty car to enter herself.

Chloe Roland walked over to her.

"Miss Ross, feeling awkward?"

Evelyn looked at her calmly.

Chloe had changed.

She had become strange and sharp.

No trace of her former self remained.

"I can help you."

Chloe lowered her voice.

"I can have my brother come escort you to a car."

"The Roland family can also partner with NanTech Ltd."

She stared at Evelyn.

"Just give Alexander Vance to me."

Evelyn refused directly.

"Impossible."

Chloe's eyes turned cold.

"Alexander Vance doesn't have that much influence in New York."

"One word from me, and you won't be able to move an inch."

Evelyn scoffed and remained silent.

Chloe was thoroughly angered.

"Fine. Don't blame me for what happens next!"

She looked at the surrounding groomsmen.

"No one is to take her!"

"Don't let her in any of the cars!"

The procession leader looked troubled.

"Miss Roland, this isn't really appropriate..."

Penelope immediately supported her.

"What's inappropriate about it?"

The leader hesitated. "The wedding will be short a bridesmaid..."

Tina Sherwood smiled and interjected.

"Miss Ross can just drive herself."

The leader didn't dare say more.

Charles Valentine walked over.

"What's going on?"

The leader briefly explained the situation.

Charles frowned at Penelope.

"What's with this childish temper?"

"It's our eldest brother's wedding day. Don't make a scene."

Penelope pretended to be wronged.

"Second Brother, it's not me causing trouble."

"She hit our eldest sister before..."

"How can a daughter of the Valentine family tolerate such disrespect?"

Charles immediately became angry.

He was extremely protective of his two sisters.

Hearing that Evelyn had hit someone, his expression changed instantly.

"Someone like her doesn't deserve to be in a wedding car!"

"She shouldn't even be at the wedding!"

Charles waved his hand.

"Everyone, get in the cars!"

Tina Sherwood fawned over Penelope.

"Miss Valentine, your brother really dotes on you."

Penelope smiled smugly.

Chloe gave Evelyn one last look.

That face, so similar to her savior's, softened her heart for a moment.

But thinking of Alexander Vance hardened her resolve again.

Anyone could forget the savior.

But Alexander Vance could not.

People gradually entered the cars.

The procession slowly started.

No one paid any attention to Evelyn standing by the roadside.

She stood in her bridesmaid dress.

Watching calmly as the cars drove away.

Taxis were unavailable in the villa area.

Evelyn sighed softly.

She was pondering how to get to the wedding venue.

A black car suddenly stopped beside her.
###Chapter 348

Evelyn Ross paused in surprise.

The car door opened.
Ryan Roland sat in the back seat, looking at her.
"Miss Nan, need a ride?"

Ryan was Mina Sherwood's cousin.
Since Chloe Roland was a bridesmaid, he was naturally present.

The wedding procession headed toward the hotel.
As part of the bride's family, he was attending the banquet.

The Sherwood family cars followed behind.
Ryan's gaze was probing.

Evelyn looked around.
The procession was long.
If Mina couldn't find her at the hotel, she would worry.
She stopped hesitating and got in the car.
"Thank you."

The car started.
Evelyn took out her phone.
Mina had sent a message: [Are you in a car?]

Seeing no reply, she sent five more messages in a row.
She also called via voice chat.

Evelyn replied immediately: [I'm in a car. Don't worry.]

She put away her phone.
Sensing Ryan's scrutiny, she turned to him directly.
"Is there something you need, Mr. Roland?"

Behind his gold-rimmed glasses, Ryan's eyes were sharp.
He seemed to be searching for a flaw, but found nothing.

He asked directly, "Miss Nan's records indicate a gentle personality?"

Evelyn smiled lightly.
"I used to be."
"I thought being a good wife and mother was enough."
"But my ex-husband taught me to reinvent myself."

She gave a self-mocking smile.
Fixed impressions were misleading.
Her sister seemed like such a delicate woman.
Yet deep down, she had incredible strength.

In the ocean, facing life and death, her sister had given her the chance to live.
How could someone like that be weak and incompetent?

A trace of sorrow flickered in Evelyn's eyes.

This expression confused Ryan even more.
He studied Evelyn carefully.

Could a major marital change really transform a person so completely?
It was possible...

Ryan felt he was overthinking it.
Evelyn Ross's body had been found in San Francisco.
The woman before him had no tear mole at the corner of her eye...

After a moment of silence, he spoke again.
"Alexander Vance is with you because you look exactly like his late wife."
"Did you know that, Miss Nan?"

Evelyn paused, lowering her gaze.
"My ex-husband heard about Mrs. Vance's funeral."
"He took me there."
"After he realized I resembled Mrs. Vance, he..."

She seemed to struggle with the words.
"My ex-husband gave me to Mr. Vance..."
"I didn't understand at the time."
"I only realized later."

Ryan was taken aback.
"That's it?"

Evelyn tilted her head.
"What else?"

Ryan continued, "I see Alexander Vance treats you unusually well."

Evelyn smiled bitterly.
"Just a substitute."
"He can't move past the grief of losing his wife."
"He seeks comfort in me, that's all."

Ryan suddenly understood.
At Evelyn's funeral, Alexander had refused to acknowledge the body was hers.
But multiple DNA tests had confirmed it...

Everyone said Mr. Vance had gone mad then.
Ryan had visited him.
In just a few days, he had become haggard...

Thinking about it now, he was stubbornly treating Grace Nan as Evelyn Ross.
Fooling himself.

Ryan figured out the logic and fell silent.

Evelyn didn't let the opportunity slip.
She turned to him with a smile.
"Mr. Roland, about the cooperation between our companies..."
"Will you really not reconsider?"

Ryan frowned.
"It's just some raw materials."
"I don't want to upset my sister."

Before he finished speaking, a phone appeared before him.
Evelyn opened a cooperation document and handed it to him.
"What if we collaborate on developing a new project?"

Ryan was stunned.
He looked at the content on the phone.
His eyes immediately lit up.

Evelyn said slowly, "As far as I know, the Roland family is one of the Five Great Families."
"But you've already fallen behind the other four."
"If you don't catch up, the Roland family will truly decline."
"Aren't you worried, Mr. Roland?"

Ryan's jaw tightened.
Because the Roland family had fallen behind, they had gone to San Francisco to cooperate with the Vance family.
They hoped to improve their standing.

After the cooperation, their status had recovered somewhat.
But it still wasn't enough.

The project Evelyn mentioned was too attractive.
The company's core technology needed updating.
But they lacked technical personnel.
Dr. Nan's latest technology was with James Klein's company.
Which now belonged to Grace Nan...

Seeing his silence, Evelyn took back her phone.
"Mr. Roland, you should think it over carefully."

Ryan fell into deep thought.
His sister was important, of course.
But could a great family abandon significant benefits for personal feelings?

If it were just a small matter, he would definitely indulge his sister.
But now...

Ryan rubbed the armrest, lost in thought.

It was a thirty-minute drive from the Sherwood residence to the hotel.
The procession stopped.

Evelyn looked at Ryan.
"Mr. Roland, if you change your mind, you can contact me anytime."

She opened the door and got out.
She quickly walked toward the bridal car.

Mina had already gotten out and was waiting for her.
Seeing Evelyn, she breathed a sigh of relief.

Everyone entered the hotel's backstage rest area to wait for the wedding.

Among the bridesmaids, Chloe Roland saw Evelyn and was stunned.
She asked unhappily, "Whose car did you come in?"

Penelope Valentine also spoke up.
"What's going on?"

Someone nearby said quietly, "I just saw her get out of Mr. Roland's car."

Chloe was confused.
"Which Mr. Roland?"

The other person pointed at Ryan, who had just gotten out of the car.
"Your brother!"

Chloe's face immediately darkened.
She tossed out a sentence, "You go ahead first. I'll catch up later."

She stormed over to Ryan.
Her eyes were red with anger as she glared at him.
"Brother, how could you bring Grace Nan?!"
"Don't you know I hate her?!"

Ryan was helpless.
Seeing his sister's state, he knew he had to give an explanation.

After a long silence, he finally said, "I suspect Grace Nan is Evelyn Ross."
###Chapter 349

Chloe Roland was stunned.

She couldn't believe her ears.

She grabbed Ryan Roland's hand. "And then?"

Ryan rubbed his temples. "I tested her a few times in the car."

"She felt familiar, yet also different."

"It's hard to explain."

Chloe released his hand. "So is it her or not?"

"I'm not sure."

Chloe's eyes reddened. "Brother, are you going soft on her because she looks like our savior?"

"You must not help her anymore!"

"She is not our savior!"

Ryan sighed helplessly. "I know. I know."

"You don't understand at all!"

Chloe shook off his hand, her eyes blazing red. "You're all drawn to her just because she resembles our savior!"

"This is blasphemy against our savior!"

"She is not her!"

"Brother, I forbid you to look at her again!"

Chloe was agitated, breathing rapidly.

Ryan immediately stepped forward and gently patted her back.

He sighed deeply.

He recalled the doctor's diagnosis yesterday when he took Chloe for her appointment.

"Miss Roland's depression is severe."

"Her emotions are extremely unstable."

"She requires careful nurturing and must avoid any stimulation."

Thinking of this, Ryan softened his voice. "Alright."

"I won't look at her."

"I won't help her."

"Is that okay?"

Chloe slowly calmed down.

They entered the wedding venue together.

Ryan escorted her to the backstage lounge.

Chloe watched Ryan closely.

She was satisfied only when she saw he truly wasn't looking at Evelyn Ross.

She entered the room and sat on the sofa.

Tina Sherwood and Penelope Valentine were chatting.

They immediately surrounded her upon seeing her.

"Chloe, did you find out?"

"Why did your brother help her?"

"Is he interested in her?"

Chloe immediately refuted them. "My brother just didn't want any trouble at the wedding."

"Stop guessing wildly."

"That's good then."

Someone else chimed in. "This Grace Nan is as pretty as Mina Sherwood."

"No wonder they stick together."

"Can they do whatever they want just by relying on their looks?"

"Hmph, Mina is acceptable."

"But that Grace Nan has the face of a seductress."

"Back at the Sherwood residence, several men were looking at her with improper intentions."

Chloe's expression changed drastically. "She is not a seductress!"

"Don't you dare say that about her!"

"Her face is perfect!"

"Do not insult it!"

Their savior had the same face.

Insulting Grace Nan was insulting their savior!

The surrounding women froze, their expressions stiff.

Chloe glared at them with disgust. "No personal attacks against Grace Nan."

"Especially about her face!"

The others exchanged glances.

They walked away grudgingly.

They whispered among themselves once they were further away.

"Is Chloe Roland insane?"

"I was helping her curse someone, and she cursed me instead?"

"Don't mind her."

"She's mentally unstable."

Chloe ignored them.

She just stared blankly at Grace Nan.

Grace Nan was accompanying Mina Sherwood.

The two were whispering and laughing softly.

They didn't notice this side at all.

Grace Nan's side profile was clear.

Her peach blossom eyes had long eyelashes.

It was exactly the same as their savior.

She didn't know what was said.

But Grace Nan smiled slightly, the corners of her lips lifting.

Chloe couldn't help but curve her lips upwards too.

But the next second, Chloe snapped back to reality.

Slap!

She slapped herself hard across the face!

She wished she could slap herself a few more times.

She had actually seen a shadow of their savior in Grace Nan just now!

No!

Absolutely not!

No one in this world could replace their savior!

Chloe stood up, her eyes red.

This action stunned everyone around.

Tina Sherwood asked in confusion, "Miss Roland, what's wrong?"

Chloe said with a dark expression, "It's nothing."

She had almost done the same as Alexander Vance.

Finding a substitute!

She immediately turned her head away.

She refused to look at Evelyn Ross again.

Just then, the emcee came in to synchronize the流程.

He held the rings. "Ladies, the wedding ceremony later requires a bridesmaid and a groomsman to come on stage to deliver the rings."

"May I ask who was chosen?"

"I need to go through the流程 with the bridesmaid…"

The room fell silent instantly.

Everyone looked at Mina Sherwood simultaneously.

This was an opportunity.

All these bridesmaids were suitable due to their status.

Chloe was the cousin.

Penelope was the groom's sister.

The Sherwood girls were Mina's cousins…

Everyone thought Mina would choose from among them.

But Mina said, "It was decided long ago."

"Grace Nan will do it."

It was her wedding.

Even if it wasn't blessed, she wanted her best friend by her side.

Moreover, Mina didn't know the specifics of Grace Nan's task.

Having Grace Nan deliver the rings might help her complete her task better.

As soon as Mina finished speaking, the bridesmaids' expressions changed.

Penelope and the Sherwood women curled their lips in disdain.

Only Chloe objected vehemently. "On what basis?!"

Mina was puzzled. "What do you mean, on what basis?"

Chloe sneered. "On what basis should she go on stage to deliver the rings?"

"I disagree!"

She would never allow this person to show off using their savior's face.

Wouldn't she become even more unscrupulous in the future?

Mina frowned. "Chloe Roland, this is my wedding."

"I have the final say."

Chloe was being unreasonable. "I just disagree."

"Marcus Valentine won't agree either."

"Call him here if you don't believe me!"

Back in San Francisco, Marcus Valentine and Evelyn Ross eventually reconciled.

Marcus had repeatedly praised Evelyn's superb horseback riding skills.

He genuinely admired her…

So Chloe was certain Marcus wouldn't agree.

Mina scoffed. "Chloe Roland, it's my wedding."

"My bridesmaid."

"I don't need anyone else's agreement!"

The two were at a stalemate.

The emcee wisely retreated.

He quickly went to fetch Marcus Valentine.

Marcus entered the room impatiently. "What's all the noise about?"

Chloe immediately pointed at Evelyn Ross. "Marcus Valentine, look at Mina's best friend!"

"Mina actually wants her to go on stage to deliver the rings!"

Chloe didn't know how to explain.

She just said hatefully, "She is not worthy!"

"Marcus Valentine, she is not worthy!"

"Why is she not worthy?!" Mina pressed.

Why?

Of course because she wasn't worthy of having the same face as their savior!

But Chloe couldn't say that.

She struggled for a long time.

She suddenly looked at Penelope Valentine and Tina Sherwood beside her.

Remembering what they had said…

Although she felt it was wrong, she blurted out without thinking, "Because her status is not worthy!"

"The marriage alliance of the Five Great Families is a major event."

"The bridesmaid going on stage must come from one of the Five Great Families."

"How can you let some commoner go on stage?!"

She knew these words were wrong.

Their savior often said everyone was equal.

She never judged people by their status.

But now, Chloe was speaking recklessly.

In her extreme state, she even nodded after saying this.

"Marcus Valentine, am I right?!"
###Chapter 350

Evelyn stared at Chloe.

She barely recognized the girl before her.

The once gentle and shy Roland heiress was now covered in thorns.

She was sharp, like a completely different person.

Mina frowned deeply. "Chloe Roland, what makes you so superior? I insist on having Grace as my maid of honor!"

Chloe ignored her completely.

Her eyes were fixed on Marcus. "Marcus Valentine, answer me!"

Marcus let out a cold laugh. "Chloe, you ask why I chose her? Because among everyone here, her status is the most suitable!"

Grace was his biological cousin.

The Valentine family ranked second among the Five Great Families.

Which other elite family present could compare to the Valentines?

But his uncle hadn't returned yet. He couldn't publicly acknowledge her.

Marcus pushed the thought aside and looked at Chloe. "You're unwell. Go home and rest."

He signaled to his subordinate. "Please summon Mr. Roland."

Soon, Ryan arrived in a hurry.

He took one look at Chloe's state and sighed. "Chloe, let me take you home first."

Chloe's eyes were red-rimmed, swaying unsteadily.

Ryan scooped her into his arms and apologized to the guests. "Please excuse us."

He arranged for their father to accompany Chloe home.

He himself stayed to manage the social obligations.

Before getting into the car, Chloe grabbed her brother's sleeve, tears welling in her eyes. "Brother, everyone has betrayed our savior... You must not be kind to her. Promise me!"

Ryan patted her hand gently. "Alright."

...

The wedding proceeded as scheduled.

Evelyn personally presented the ring to Mina.

Watching her best friend's happy expression, she remembered her sister's diary.

Her sister had always wished for Mina to find happiness.

She would have been comforted to see this scene.

Evelyn lowered her gaze, suppressing the moisture in her eyes.

The ceremony concluded smoothly.

Marcus remained expressionless throughout.

When the officiant asked if he would take Mina as his wife, he remained silent for a long time before uttering two words. "I do."

The ceremony was completed without further incident, and the banquet began.

Evelyn took some food and walked alone to the balcony.

From the 48th floor, she overlooked the bustling city of New York.

Footsteps sounded behind her.

Ryan walked to her side. "Miss Nan."

Evelyn smiled. "Have you reached a decision, Mr. Roland?"

Ryan was silent for a moment. "We can cooperate. But don't let Chloe know."

Evelyn raised her champagne glass. "No problem."

As their glasses touched, Ryan suddenly froze.

Evelyn followed his gaze and turned around.

Chloe had returned and was standing not far away.

She had forgotten her bag and come back, only to witness this scene.

Her brother was actually cooperating with Grace!

And he planned to hide it from her!

Chloe felt a chill through her entire body, her eyes turning red. "Brother... you lied to me!"

Ryan hurried forward. "Chloe, let me explain..."

"I won't listen!" Chloe covered her ears and screamed, then turned and ran away.

Ryan immediately gave chase.

Evelyn remained where she was.

She knew her presence would only further upset Chloe.

Five minutes later, the banquet hall suddenly erupted into commotion.

"Chloe Roland is going to jump!"

"She's on the rooftop terrace!"

Evelyn's pupils constricted sharply.

She put down her plate and rushed towards the stairwell!

The rooftop security door was wide open.

The crowd murmured in low voices.

"The Roland family won't let anyone near. They're afraid of agitating her..."

"What happened? Why does she suddenly want to jump?"

"I heard it's because Mr. Roland is interested in Grace Nan? Chloe doesn't get along with her..."

"No! I heard Chloe thinks Grace resembles her savior. She believes her brother has transferred his affection and can't bear it..."

Evelyn froze abruptly.

All the clues instantly connected.

Chloe had never betrayed her.

Competing for Alexander was because she thought he was seeking a substitute.

Targeting her was because she wouldn't allow anyone to replace the savior.

This foolish girl...

Evelyn's eyes reddened instantly.

She pushed through the crowd and rushed towards the terrace.

She had to tell Chloe the truth.
###Chapter 351

Evelyn rushed to the rooftop entrance.

A crowd blocked her path.

Ryan Roland's assistant stood guard at the door.

"Miss Nan, Miss Roland jumped because of you. Must you provoke her further?"

Whispers spread through the crowd.

"What grudge does she hold against Miss Roland?"

"Driving a Roland heiress to jump? Impressive."

"Rushing forward now? Is she impatient for Miss Roland to jump faster?"

Marcus Valentine and Penelope Valentine arrived.

Marcus snapped, "Enough with the nonsense!"

Penelope spoke softly, "Brother, they're not wrong."

"Miss Roland was already upset this morning about the bridesmaid situation."

She looked at Grace and sighed. "Grace, Miss Roland is delicate. Couldn't you have been more accommodating?"

All eyes turned to Grace.

Marcus frowned. "What conflict did they have?"

Penelope hesitated. "I'm not entirely sure..."

"But when Miss Roland asked her to leave, she threw a chair."

"And threatened us."

She lowered her voice. "I think I saw her speaking with Mr. Roland..."

"It seemed like business discussions. That must have angered Miss Roland."

Marcus looked at Grace. "Are you that desperate for money?"

"Isn't the Valentine and Sherwood collaboration enough?"

"Why provoke her?"

Evelyn's cold gaze swept over the crowd.

"Let me through. I can save her."

Penelope gave a light laugh. "How rude, Miss Nan."

"Calling Miss Roland by her full name. Are you that familiar?"

Condemnation erupted around them.

"How dare you address Miss Roland so informally?"

"Think you're aristocracy just because your friend married into the Valentines?"

"Know your place..."

Evelyn took a deep breath.

"Step aside!"

Penelope looked at Marcus. "You see, brother?"

"She's had this attitude since morning. No wonder Miss Roland was upset."

Marcus frowned but remained silent.

Evelyn tried to move past him.

Marcus grabbed her arm. "Grace..."

"Chloe Roland jumped because of me."

"Only I can save her."

"Any delay could cost her life!"

Evelyn's eyes burned with urgency.

That look reminded Marcus of Evelyn from San Francisco.

Same face, entirely different presence.

Yet in this moment, they overlapped perfectly.

Marcus released her arm. "I'm coming with you."

"Fine."

Penelope followed closely. "Brother, I'm coming too."

"Miss Roland and I are acquainted."

On the sixty-eighth floor rooftop.

A fierce wind whipped against them, making it hard to stand steady.

Penelope's steps faltered slightly.

She adjusted and continued forward.

Evelyn searched desperately for Chloe Roland.

Rooftop structures obstructed her view.

Sobbing guided her direction.

Chloe's voice trembled with tears.

"Brother, even you lied to me!"

"You want to work with her because she resembles your savior, don't you?"

"Are you transferring your feelings for your savior onto her?"

Ryan's voice was frantic. "Chloe, I was wrong."

"No more collaborations. Just come down!"

"No!"

Chloe screamed. "You liar!"

"You brought her to the hotel during the groom's procession."

"You're projecting your savior's qualities onto her."

"You're not my brother! I'll never trust you again!"

"Chloe! It's not like that, let me explain—"

"I won't listen! I don't want to see her!"

"If even you and our parents forget our savior..."

"If Alexander Vance forgets our savior..."

"I'd rather die to make you remember her!"

Chloe's voice broke.

"It's only been a month..."

"Just one month..."

"Alexander Vance finds a replacement..."

"You and Marcus Valentine both focus on Grace..."

"But she's not our savior!"

"How could you transfer your affections?"

"Are you betraying our savior's memory?"

Chloe choked back sobs.

"Brother, search your conscience..."

"When you see her, don't you think of our savior?"

Ryan fell silent.

Chloe gave a bitter laugh. "I knew it."

"I saw it too..."

"How can I see our savior's shadow in someone else?"

"If our savior were still here..."

"To discover in just one month..."

"That woman became her husband's lover..."

"Became the boss of her subordinate..."

"That woman completely replaced her..."

"How heartbroken would our savior be?"

Ryan's eyes reddened.

Marcus halted his steps.

Memories of Evelyn surfaced, bringing sorrow to his eyes.

That vibrant spirit could never be replaced.

She was his own cousin.

How could Grace's appearance dilute his grief?

Evelyn broke through the crowd.

She sprinted toward the rooftop edge.

The wind whipped her long hair wildly.

She saw Chloe's fragile figure.

Standing outside the railing, teetering dangerously.

"Chloe!"

Evelyn called out.

Chloe whipped her head around.

Her tear-blurred eyes flew wide open.

Evelyn continued forward.

Her voice cut through the wind.

"I'm here."

"You wanted to see me, didn't you?"
###Chapter 352

Marcus Valentine's hesitation did not escape Penelope Valentine's notice.

Penelope couldn't resist asking, "Big brother, who is this benefactor Miss Roland mentioned?"

"Do you know them too?"

If he didn't, Chloe wouldn't have brought up Marcus just now...

Marcus fell completely silent at her question.

Evelyn Ross paid no attention to their emotional state.

She moved to step around them and continue forward, but Marcus blocked her path once more.

Marcus looked at her intently. "Grace, Chloe's mental state is unstable right now."

"I really don't think you should go over there..."

Evelyn felt a surge of frustration at his words.

She was about to push past his obstruction when he spoke again.

"But there is someone who would be suitable."

Evelyn paused. "Who?"

"Evelyn Ross."

Evelyn was taken aback.

Marcus kept his eyes fixed on her. "I know you must have heard of Evelyn Ross."

"You know she is your twin sister."

"The two of you look very much alike."

"Grace's presence upsets her because of Evelyn."

"So, could you pretend to be Evelyn Ross right now?"

"Go and persuade her to come down?"

Evelyn was speechless.

She was supposed to impersonate herself?

Evelyn had no intention of pretending.

She just wanted to hurry over and reveal her identity.

She couldn't risk Chloe actually jumping.

Moreover, this gentle girl had gone to such extremes for her.

How could she not be deeply moved?

Seeing Marcus's suggestion, she couldn't be bothered to explain.

She simply nodded. "Fine."

"Can I go over now?"

Marcus immediately agreed.

He then looked at the corner of her eye.

He turned to Penelope. "Lend me your mascara or an eyebrow pencil."

Evelyn knew what he intended to do.

After a moment's thought, she didn't stop him.

Penelope looked at them both, confused.

But she obediently opened her purse.

She handed Marcus the cosmetics she used for touch-ups.

Marcus quickly opened an eyeliner pencil.

He drew a teardrop mole at the corner of Evelyn's eye.

It was identical to her real one.

Evelyn was silent.

Once he finished, Marcus examined her carefully.

"Now it's even more similar."

"Go ahead."

"Oh, and Evelyn calls her 'Chloe.'"

"You should know what to do."

Evelyn nodded.

She then strode forward.

Just as she rounded a concrete wall ahead, the scene before her made her freeze.

Now she understood why Ryan Roland didn't dare force a rescue.

Chloe was standing right on the edge of the rooftop.

A strong wind whipped her bridesmaid dress around her.

The long skirt billowed wildly in the wind.

She looked utterly precarious.

It seemed the next gust could easily carry Chloe away.

The sight was heart-stopping.

Evelyn's eyes instantly reddened.

She immediately stepped forward and called out, "Chloe!"

Both Chloe and Ryan turned at the sound.

They were stunned to see Evelyn.

Ryan tried to rush forward while Chloe was distracted.

But Chloe noticed and shouted, "Brother, don't come any closer!"

"If you take another step, I'll jump right now!"

Ryan had no choice but to stop.

Chloe then turned her longing gaze back to Evelyn.

Her eyes slowly traveled over Evelyn's figure.

She seemed to be trying to determine if this was Evelyn Ross or Grace Valentine.

The girl's expression was calm and composed.

Her cool eyes lacked Grace's gentleness and timidity.

They held Evelyn's distinctive gaze.

Chloe's focus finally returned to the corner of Evelyn's eye.

There was a teardrop mole...

A look of joy spread across Chloe's face.

She gazed at her uncertainly and whispered, "...Benefactor?"

"Chloe, it's me!"

Evelyn slowly approached.

She stopped beside Ryan and reached out her hand.

"Come down quickly."

"Come to me."

Chloe's eyes seemed glazed.

It was clear her mental state was indeed fragile...

Tears welled in her eyes as she asked again, uncertainly, "Benefactor?"

Evelyn nodded immediately.

Chloe cried tears of happiness.

Large drops rolled down her cheeks.

"You... you're not dead?"

"How are you here?"

Suddenly, she seemed to realize something.

Her expression instantly turned wary.

"Those clothes you're wearing..."

"Isn't that what Grace was wearing?"

"Aren't you Grace?"

"How can you be my benefactor?"

Evelyn hadn't reached her yet.

Seeing this, she urgently called out, "Chloe, I am Evelyn Ross."

"I am not Grace."

"You remember my voice."

"You remember my teardrop mole, don't you?"

Chloe's eyes returned to the mole.

She then nodded. "Yes, I recognize your mole..."

"So come back..."

Evelyn reached out her hand to her.

"Come back, alright?"

"I'm not dead."

"You can't die either..."

"Okay, okay..."

Chloe began crying again.

She reached out her hand to Evelyn like a child.

Ryan and Marcus, who had followed, breathed sighs of relief.

But Penelope, observing the scene, frowned.

This Grace... was truly detestable!

If she really saved Chloe, she would have latched onto the Roland family...

Yet another competitor would emerge in New York high society.

Moreover, judging from what Chloe had said earlier...

This Grace looked very similar to Mrs. Vance?

Wouldn't Alexander Vance easily be seduced by her?

Her own marriage prospects would be ruined!

A malicious glint flashed in Penelope's eyes.

She stepped forward and said, "That's right, Miss Roland."

"Why are you so desperate?"

"Nothing is more important than your own life..."

As she spoke, she pulled out some cosmetics from her purse.

"Your makeup is all cried off."

"Let me help you touch it up."

"We can go out looking beautiful later..."

She deliberately held up an eyebrow pencil.

She waved it in front of Chloe.

Then she reached out and touched her own nose wing.

Chloe watched her movements.

She suddenly remembered what the makeup artist said earlier that day.

It was currently fashionable to draw a mole on the nose.

It looked sexy.

Drawing a mole...

Chloe suddenly understood.

She sharply turned her gaze back to Evelyn.

"You're not my benefactor!"

"You're Grace!"

"The teardrop mole at your eye was drawn on!"

Marcus's face instantly paled.

Even Ryan was momentarily stunned.

Both men sensed something was wrong.

They instinctively lunged forward!

But Chloe felt deceived.

She took a step backward.

"Stay away from me!"

"Don't come any closer!!"

That single step back placed her foot on empty air.

Her body suddenly tilted backward!!

Below her was a sheer drop into the abyss!
###Chapter 353

A flicker of delight crossed Penelope Valentine's eyes.

If Chloe Roland fell, she would certainly die.

The elite social circle of New York would lose one competitor.

Evelyn Ross's failed rescue would only deepen the conflict.

The Roland family would despise her to the bone.

Two birds with one stone.

She looked over excitedly.

A figure lunged toward the edge of the rooftop.

Evelyn Ross gripped Chloe Roland's wrist tightly.

Chloe dangled precariously in midair.

Evelyn leaned most of her body over the ledge.

Gravity pulled her downward.

At the critical moment, Ryan Roland and Marcus Valentine arrived.

They desperately grabbed Evelyn's legs.

The four of them barely stabilized the situation.

Chloe looked up at the person holding her.

Faintly, she remembered an afternoon three years ago in the hospital.

When her husband and mother-in-law had tormented her nearly to death.

That light who helped her make a phone call.

The two figures slowly overlapped.

She shook her head violently.

Tears streamed down her face.

"You're not my savior... How could I be mistaken again..."

Evelyn's arm muscles strained to their limit.

She shouted sharply, "Stop being foolish! I am Evelyn Ross!"

Chloe froze.

Then she began struggling wildly.

"Your tear mole is drawn on! I don't believe you!"

She even dug her nails into Evelyn's skin.

Evelyn felt no pain.

She roared behind her, "Pull me up!"

Ryan and Marcus exerted force simultaneously.

Evelyn's body slowly moved backward.

She never let go.

Only when Ryan grabbed Chloe's arm.

Did Evelyn collapse to the ground.

Her arms were weak and sore.

Her chest burned with pain.

Her white evening gown was scraped and bloodstained.

She ignored her own condition.

She quickly looked at Chloe.

Chloe lay on the ground staring at the sky.

Her eyes were empty and lifeless.

Her white skirt fluttered in the wind.

Like a butterfly with broken wings.

Ryan's eyes reddened.

"Do you only have Evelyn Ross in your eyes?"

"Are your parents and brother no longer important?"

Chloe's eyelashes fluttered.

"Three years ago, when I insisted on marrying to San Francisco."

"You should have considered me dead."

Ryan's throat tightened.

He suddenly understood.

Without Evelyn Ross.

His sister would have died in that hospital.

The Chloe Roland alive now.

Existed only for her savior.

Depression made her stubborn.

Saving her once couldn't save her every time.

Ryan clenched his fists.

Helplessness washed over him.

Evelyn crawled to Chloe's side.

She stared into the other's eyes.

"I only made a phone call."

"Your family gave you life and raised you!"

"How could you treat your life so lightly?"

Chloe wept bitterly.

"Grace, stop pretending."

"You saving me makes it impossible to face my savior."

Evelyn pulled her up abruptly.

"Come with me!"

She dragged Chloe through the safety door.

They entered the restroom.

Evelyn looked directly at her.

"Do you have makeup remover wipes?"

Chloe froze.

Evelyn's tone was firm.

"If the tear mole remains after removing makeup."

"Can that prove I am Evelyn Ross?"

Chloe sneered.

"Fine, let's see you perform!"

She pulled out a makeup remover wipe and handed it over.

Evelyn vigorously wiped the corner of her eye.

Traces of black eyebrow pencil appeared.

Chloe was about to mock her.

But she froze the moment she looked up.
###Chapter 354

Chloe Roland's pupils contracted sharply.

Evelyn Ross wiped away the drawn tear mole with a makeup remover wipe.

Her original, familiar red mole was revealed.

Chloe's breath hitched.

The position and shape of that mole were all too familiar to her.

It was her savior.

She stared blankly at Evelyn, completely stunned.

She rubbed her eyes hard, her eye rims instantly turning red.

"Wah—"

Chloe suddenly burst into loud sobs.

She cried heartbreakingly, without any regard for her image, like a child who had suffered immense grievance.

Evelyn had originally intended to scold her.

She wanted to ask why she didn't value her own life.

But seeing her crying so bitterly, those words got stuck in her throat.

Chloe was extreme.

But all her extremism was for Evelyn's sake.

Anyone in the world could criticize her, except Evelyn herself.

Evelyn sighed softly and stepped forward to embrace her.

Chloe was half a head shorter. Her body stiffened for a moment before she tightly wrapped her arms around Evelyn's waist.

She buried her face in Evelyn's chest, crying so hard she could barely catch her breath. "Savior, you're not dead…"

Evelyn patted her back gently. "I'm not dead."

"Thank goodness!" Chloe held her even tighter, afraid she might disappear.

Evelyn nodded again. "I'm not dead."

"Wah…"

Chloe cried muffledly, finally releasing all the grievances she had suppressed for a month.

Outside the door.

Ryan Roland and Marcus Valentine exchanged bewildered looks.

They pressed their ears against the door, only hearing Chloe's cries from inside.

Ryan was frantic with worry. He reached for the door handle.

The door was locked from the inside.

"Chloe! Miss Ross! Open the door!" Ryan pounded on the door forcefully.

The only response was even louder crying.

Ryan paced anxiously in circles. He shouted at Marcus, "Quick, find the key! This hotel belongs to your family!"

Marcus immediately sent someone to fetch the key.

He leaned close to the door and called out, "Grace, calm down. Don't do anything excessive…"

Penelope Valentine looked on with concern. "Big brother, Mr. Roland, could Grace be harming Miss Roland inside because of what happened this morning?"

Ryan's face paled instantly. He took a step back. "Move aside!"

Marcus sidestepped to make way.

Ryan lifted his leg, ready to kick the door down!

At that moment, the door opened.

Ryan's kick met empty air, nearly causing him to do the splits.

He steadied himself with a hand on the floor and urgently looked inside.

Evelyn stood there.

"Where's Chloe?" Ryan asked anxiously.

"She…"

Before Evelyn could finish, Penelope rushed forward accusingly. "Grace, how could you? Miss Roland is an honored guest. What were you trying to do by locking her in the bathroom?"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

Penelope continued, "I know Miss Roland offended you this morning, but you can't seek revenge on her! She was clearly so heartbroken. Couldn't you just endure it?"

Evelyn crossed her arms and smirked coldly. "Finished?"

Penelope was speechless.

She turned to Ryan. "Mr. Roland, I was just worried about Miss Roland…"

Chloe walked out of the bathroom, her expression cold. "We don't need your fake concern!"

She had just washed her face. Her makeup was completely ruined, but her spirit seemed significantly improved.

Though her eyes were red, the madness was gone.

Ryan breathed a sigh of relief.

Penelope was stunned, unable to comprehend what had happened.

She hesitated, then looked at Evelyn. "Miss Ross, you do know she's Grace, right? Her tear mole was drawn…"

Before she could finish—

Slap!

Evelyn slapped her across the face.

Penelope covered her cheek, eyes wide with shock. "What are you doing?"

"Retaliation." Evelyn's gaze was sharp. "On the rooftop, did you intentionally use the makeup to remind her my tear mole was drawn?"

Penelope's eyes darted evasively.

She looked at Marcus and Ryan with a pitiful expression. "How could you think that of me? Am I that kind of person?"

She forced out tears. "I just saw Miss Roland's makeup was ruined and wanted her to touch it up…"

"Is that so?" Evelyn asked mildly.

Penelope nodded vigorously.

The next second—

Slap!

Another slap landed on her other cheek.

Penelope whipped her head around, glaring at her furiously. "Grace, how dare you!"

Evelyn dusted off her hands. "I already did. What's there to dare?"

"You…" Penelope turned teary eyes toward Marcus. "Big brother, are you just going to let her hit me?"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "Whether you did it intentionally or not, you nearly got Miss Roland and me killed today. Two slaps are getting off lightly. Mr. Valentine, any objections?"

Penelope hurriedly said, "Big brother, you have to stand up for me! I'm a daughter of the Valentine family. How can I let someone bully me like this? Where would the Valentine family's reputation be if this got out?"

She cried pitifully.

Marcus, however, let out a derisive laugh. "A family dispute. Outsiders shouldn't interfere."

Penelope was stunned. "A family dispute? Big brother, you're mistaken. How could she and I have a family dispute?"
###Chapter 355

Ryan Roland turned his gaze to Marcus Valentine. "A domestic dispute?"

Marcus did not answer.

He checked his watch. "The wedding is still going. Let's not waste time."

His eyes swept over Chloe Roland, confirming she was fine. Then he turned and went downstairs.

Penelope Valentine stared at Evelyn Ross, utterly confused.

But the looks from Ryan and Chloe made her uneasy. She hurried after Marcus.

After they left, Ryan immediately looked at Chloe. "Chloe, are you…"

Chloe softly interrupted. "Brother, I'm fine now."

Ryan was stunned. "Fine?"

"Yes." Chloe lowered her eyes. "Grace saved me. I've figured things out. I can't keep living like this. My savior wouldn't want to see me like this… I will pull myself together."

Ryan was speechless.

He had told her this countless times. Chloe had never listened.

Now, after being saved once, she had an epiphany?

He looked at Evelyn. "How did you do it?"

Evelyn coughed lightly and glanced at Chloe. "I scolded her a bit."

Ryan fell silent.

His eyes moved between their faces, finally resting on the corner of Evelyn's eye.

The tear mole there was gone.

Of course, he didn't know Evelyn had covered it with foundation right after they recognized each other…

By the time the three went downstairs, the wedding had ended.

Evelyn's duties as a bridesmaid were over. She watched Mina Sherwood and Marcus get into their car and left the hotel.

She was about to find a ride home when she spotted Alexander Vance outside the door.

He wore a baseball cap and a mask, dressed in loose athletic wear. The moment she stepped out, the corners of his eyes crinkled. He walked toward her.

Evelyn asked, "What are you doing here?"

"Taking you home."

His voice was deep, carrying a reassuring strength.

Evelyn felt a warmth in her heart and nodded.

Then she saw Alexander's smile freeze at the corners of his eyes.

Following his gaze, she saw Chloe walking out the door, staring at her eagerly.

Chloe shuffled closer awkwardly. She looked down and said, "Miss Nan, can I come to your house to play?"

"No."

"Yes."

Alexander and Evelyn spoke simultaneously.

Alexander frowned at Evelyn, his eyes signaling: How can you let her come to the Nan house?

Evelyn raised an eyebrow: Why not?

Alexander continued the silent conversation: She has improper intentions toward me! This is like inviting a wolf into your home!

Evelyn: "…"

She coughed lightly, thinking this man was too conceited.

In the end, Chloe still squeezed into Alexander's car.

On the way, Chloe kept staring at Evelyn, quiet and well-behaved.

But her phone was blowing up with messages to Ryan: [Brother, Alexander is so scary. Will he not let me come see Grace?]

Ryan replied: [Possibly. Who told you to act like that?]

Chloe said urgently: [I know I was wrong. How do I get him to agree?]

Ryan: [Speak nicely. Alexander is stern on the outside but soft inside. If you're gentle, he might be nicer to you.]

Chloe: [Got it!]

She blinked, her gaze shifting from Evelyn to Alexander.

They soon arrived at the Nan residence.

Alexander parked the car and pulled Evelyn aside. He lowered his voice. "She just declared she wants to marry me. How could you bring her home?"

Evelyn patted his shoulder. "You're overthinking it. She's here for me…"

She turned back to Chloe. "I'm going to make dinner."

Chloe answered obediently. "Okay~"

Alexander watched Chloe with a frown.

Here for Evelyn?

Did she recognize Evelyn?

Impossible.

Evelyn's identity was confidential. She wouldn't reveal it easily.

Chloe must be tricking her…

As he thought this, he saw that Chloe, this little tail, didn't follow to the kitchen. Instead, she looked at him hesitantly.

Her eyes were full of unspoken words. She clearly had something to say!

Alexander's face turned cold.

He knew it. This person still had ulterior motives!

He walked into the living room. The nanny was playing with Chloe Davis.

He was about to talk to Chloe Davis when Chloe Roland shuffled closer.

She looked at Alexander, but when he glanced over, she quickly turned to Chloe Davis. "You're Grace's daughter? So cute~ I'll play with you!"

Alexander: "…"

He turned and sat on the sofa, his expression cold.

He had just sat down when Chloe Roland dawdled over again. She stopped beside him.

Alexander put down his phone and looked at her. "Miss Roland, do you have something to say?"

Chloe swallowed. "Um…"

She was still struggling with how to begin. Alexander said directly, "No."

Chloe was stunned. "I haven't said anything yet. How do you know it's no?"

Alexander scoffed. "I already know what you're thinking. Miss Roland, my heart belongs only to Grace. I'm not interested in you!"

Chloe was bewildered. "No, I…"

Alexander stood up. "I said no means no!"

He turned and went upstairs.

Chloe Roland: "…"

……

The Valentine Residence.

The Valentine family lived together, not separated into branches.

Marcus was newly married. Penelope Valentine stared in the direction of the newlyweds' suite, clenching her fists.

She had been bullied today, and the family head didn't even defend her!

She couldn't swallow this anger!

Her eldest brother had never been close to her since childhood. It was normal for him not to help her…

Just then, she saw Marcus come out of the newlyweds' suite.

"Third Uncle? You finally got in touch!"

"I got married today, and you didn't even attend the wedding!"

"You're coming back tomorrow? Good. I have something important to tell you…"

Penelope hid to the side, her eyes lighting up.

Third Uncle was back. She could complain to him.

Third Uncle was the most protective of his own!
###Chapter 356

Penelope Valentine left, thoroughly satisfied with her thoughts.

Her figure vanished at the end of the hallway.

Marcus Valentine's voice sounded again.

"It's about Grace Nan…"

Third Uncle's weary, teasing voice came through the phone. "What? Did you find out she's your father's or your second uncle's love child?"

Marcus's mouth twitched.

"I'll report it to you in person when you return."

He was afraid Third Uncle would get too excited and fly back overnight.

Third Uncle snorted.

"Learning to be secretive now?"

He sounded like he was in a decent mood.

Marcus ventured a question. "How are things on your end? Any leads on Mrs. Sophia?"

Sudden silence answered him.

Silence was an answer in itself.

Marcus softened his voice. "Come back and rest for a few days first. She's on Earth. It's not like she escaped to outer space."

Third Uncle responded flatly. "Understood."

Marcus hung up, his brow furrowed.

Third Uncle had been searching for so long with no news.

Could Mrs. Sophia have already met with misfortune?

The thought weighed heavily on him.

He glanced back at the bridal suite and strode inside.

Mina Sherwood had already removed her makeup and changed out of her wedding dress.

She wore a conservative nightgown.

Seeing him enter, she lowered her eyes. "You take the bed. I'll sleep on the sofa."

Her cold demeanor ignited a fire in Marcus's chest.

He let out a cold laugh. "Mina, I didn't marry you just for decoration!"

Mina stiffened.

Marcus walked to the bed. "Tonight is our wedding night. Don't ruin my mood."

Mina's face tightened. "What do you want?"

Marcus looked down. "If you don't want a divorce, then make me happy. This is a joyous occasion. Don't wear a funeral face."

Mina clenched her fists.

How dare he ask why?

Who could possibly smile after such humiliation?

She took a deep breath. "Marcus, since you don't believe me, we'll divorce after the baby is born."

Marcus frowned. "What?"

She went through all that trouble to marry into the family, and now she mentions divorce?

Mina walked to the table and took two agreements from her bag.

The agreements stated she would leave with nothing after the child's birth, taking only the baby.

She had already signed them.

It didn't seem fake.

Marcus was stunned. "You're serious about divorcing?"

Mina nodded. "This kind of marriage is torture for both of us. You don't want to be cheated on? Then we'll divorce after the baby is born."

Nine months was enough time for Grace Nan to complete her task.

Then she would be free.

Marcus slammed the agreements on the table. "Mina, what game are you playing? You finally managed to marry in. You'd give that up? Is this a strategic retreat?"

Mina smiled bitterly. "The agreements are signed. What game could I possibly be playing?"

Marcus scoffed. "Who knows? You schemed so hard to marry me, and now you bring up divorce on our wedding night… Isn't that strange? This contract might have traps. I'll have my lawyer look at it first!"

He grabbed the contracts and headed for the door.

At the doorway, he let out a cold laugh. "Mina, you truly don't deserve the position of Mrs. Valentine. At least you have the self-awareness not to dirty the Valentine bloodline with a bastard!"

He strode away.

Mina had a vague feeling his retreating figure looked somewhat flustered, almost like he was fleeing.

How could that be?

He didn't even want to marry her. Why wouldn't he want a divorce?

Mina shook her head with a bitter smile.

She looked around.

The room was decorated with celebratory 'double happiness' characters, full of joy everywhere.

But on her wedding night, she was alone.

This bride was truly pitiful.

…

Marcus drove out, heading straight for a private room at a bar.

He summoned his lawyer and tossed him the contracts. "Check these for me. See if there are any traps."

The lawyer, woken in the middle of the night, took the contracts in a daze.

He finished the two pages quickly. "No traps. They're very favorable to you. Miss Sherwood leaves with nothing, only taking the child."

Marcus was stunned. "She's serious?"

The lawyer was confused. "Mr. Valentine, what do you mean?"

Marcus lowered his gaze.

Why did Mina want a divorce?

Once the baby was born, a DNA test could be done. If it wasn't his, she would be exposed.

That would be her perfect chance to escape.

The more Marcus thought about it, the angrier he became. He slammed his glass down heavily.

What exactly was she thinking!

Marcus ran his hands through his hair, feeling like he was going crazy.

At that moment, he remembered Mina's resolute expression.

Her confident demeanor every time she talked about the child.

He suddenly felt a twinge of self-doubt.

Could the child really be his?

But what about the hospital report?

Marcus tapped his fingers lightly on the sofa.

He remembered the oligospermia diagnosis from his physical at eighteen.

He had been receiving treatment all these years, even cutting back on his beloved horseback riding.

But there was no improvement.

It was why he had remained unmarried.

Could it have been a misdiagnosis?

He had always gone to the family hospital for check-ups and treatment, keeping it secret to protect his image.

Marcus frowned deeply.

The lawyer spoke cautiously. "Mr. Valentine, if there's nothing else, I should head back?"

Marcus suddenly looked up. "Do you know a good urology clinic?"

The lawyer was taken aback. "Yes… Why?"

Marcus looked down. "Contact them for me now. I want to get checked tonight."
###Chapter 357

Attorney Miller looked puzzled but immediately agreed.
"Alright."

Most testing facilities were closed late at night.

Attorney Miller used his connections to find a small private institution.

Marcus Valentine put on a mask and hat, wrapping himself up tightly.

He followed Attorney Miller out of the hotel quickly.

The sampling process was fast.

The institution informed him that the earliest results would be available tomorrow morning.

Marcus left Attorney Miller to wait and drove away alone.

His car wandered aimlessly through the streets.

He didn't want to go home.

His parents' expectant gazes and Mina Sherwood's cold expression flashed through his mind alternately.

He turned the steering wheel and found himself outside Grace Nan's house without realizing it.

The villa was brightly lit.

Marcus parked his car and called Alexander Vance.

Soon, Alexander came out.

He was wearing loungewear and looked relaxed.
"What are you doing here on your wedding night?"

Marcus smiled bitterly.
"Have a drink with me?"

Alexander frowned.
"I don't drink."

"Then you have tea. I'll drink."
Marcus's tone was pleading.
"For old times' sake."

Alexander hesitated.

Thinking of Evelyn Ross chatting with Chloe Roland inside, he felt inexplicably irritated.

"Let's go."
He opened the car door and got into the passenger seat.

They went to a private room in a bar.

Marcus ordered a lot of alcohol and started drinking immediately.

"What do you think Mina is really thinking?" He put down his glass.
"She went through so much trouble to marry me. She didn't even care when I left?"

Alexander took a sip of tea and glanced at his phone.

Evelyn hadn't messaged him to ask where he was.

He snorted coldly.
"Women are hard to understand. Who can tell what's real and what's not?"

Marcus slammed the table heavily.
"Exactly! We're in the same boat!"

Alexander pulled his hand back.
"Don't group me with you."

His tone was firm.
"Evelyn has me in her heart."

Marcus shook his head drunkenly.
"I don't believe it! Let's see who's right!"

He slapped his phone on the table.
"Let's see whose woman comes looking first!"

Alexander scoffed but still placed his phone on the corner of the table.

He wasn't doing this to compete. It was just convenient.

Yes, that was it.

Alexander picked up his teacup and took a sip to hide his expression.

"Beep—"

Their phones chimed simultaneously.

Both looked at the table.

Marcus grabbed his phone first, then threw it back on the table in disappointment.
"Another ad!"

Alexander secretly breathed a sigh of relief.

He felt crazy for playing this game with a drunk.

What would winning even prove?

He and Evelyn didn't need this kind of test.

Alexander thought proudly but secretly opened WhatsApp.

He sent a period to Thomas Watson.

Thomas replied instantly.
"Boss?"

Alexander frowned.

His phone was fine. WhatsApp wasn't frozen.

Why hadn't she contacted him yet?

He put his phone back on the table but couldn't help glancing at it again.

Marcus was already asleep with his head on the table.

Empty bottles were scattered everywhere.

Alexander's eyes swept over Marcus's phone screen.

Did it just light up? Probably not Mina.

Two hours later.

Alexander's expression grew darker.

He stood up abruptly and walked out.

Marcus looked up drowsily.
###Chapter 358
"Where are you going?"

Alexander didn't look back.
"Home!"

Marcus mumbled something and fell asleep again.

He slept until daylight.

Marcus was awakened by his vibrating phone.

Attorney Miller had sent several messages.

He immediately called back.

Attorney Miller's voice came through.
"Mr. Valentine, the results are out."
Marcus Valentine woke with a throbbing headache.

He fumbled for the water glass on the coffee table.

Bringing it to his lips, he caught the sharp scent of alcohol.

He slammed the glass back down.

His lawyer’s voice came through the phone.

Marcus stilled, his fingers trembling slightly.

He stared down at the carpet pattern, his voice hoarse. "The results?"

He didn't even notice the hope in his own tone.

The lawyer was silent for a few seconds. "It's... azoospermia."

The voice on the line was fearful, as if privy to some terrible secret.

Marcus said nothing for a long time.

His nails dug into his palms. "Understood."

The dial tone echoed in the room.

He slumped back on the sofa, a bitter twist to his lips.

How ridiculous.

Because of Mina Sherwood's words, he'd doubted the family doctor's diagnosis again and again.

He had humiliated himself repeatedly.

What must the lawyer think now?

The infallible Marcus Valentine, lacking the most fundamental ability of a man.

Azoospermia wasn't impotence.

But to outsiders, there was no difference.

That riding accident years ago had damaged him critically.

The prognosis had never been good.

Only he and the family doctor knew this secret.

Now the lawyer knew too.

Marcus let out a low laugh and hurled his glass against the wall.

Glass shattered, fragments flying everywhere.

His headache intensified.

He loosened his tie and stood up.

Familiar laughter drifted from the next room.

"Did you go to the Valentine-Sherwood engagement banquet yesterday?"

"Of course! But I don't get it. Why would Marcus Valentine marry that?"

"You mean Mina Sherwood? I heard she's slept with half the elite. Is it true?"

"She's a high-class call girl! Whoever does business with her uncle gets a night with her. Everyone knows!"

"The Sherwoods would allow that?"

"Her uncle branched off from the main family. Lost their status completely..."

"Between us, I've slept with Mina Sherwood..."

"Aren't you disgusted?"

"She's a beautiful woman. Don't tell me you've never been tempted."

"Damn it! I have been, just never had the chance..."

"I did it! Her skin was so soft. A real society lady, that texture..."

Before he could finish, the door was kicked open.

The two rich heirs, deep in conversation, jumped and turned.

"Who the hell—"

A fist smashed into Justin Evans's face.

Marcus recognized him now. Justin Evans, fifth son of the Evans family.

The Evans family was on par with the Valentines.

But Justin was just a playboy who usually smiled obsequiously around Marcus.

Being hit in public was a massive blow to his pride.

"Marcus Valentine, are you insane?!"

Marcus's eyes were icy. "Slandering my wife. What do you think?"

He flexed his wrist slowly, knuckles cracking.

Justin's friend tried to mediate. "Mr. Valentine, Justin was just mouthing off. Now that Miss Sherwood is with you, how could she—"

Justin shoved his friend away, spitting blood. "It's not slander! I did sleep with her! What are you so arrogant about, Marcus Valentine? You're just an heir who hasn't even taken over yet!"

His friend paled. "Justin, apologize!"

Justin sneered. "Apologize for what? Marcus Valentine, didn't you check her reputation before marrying her? It was before the marriage! Haven't you slept with her too? Otherwise, where did the child come from?"

Marcus's expression changed drastically.

Justin caught it immediately and laughed louder. "No way? You haven't slept with her? So you're raising someone else's kid? You'd wear that green hat? If she's pregnant and you haven't touched her, doesn't that prove I'm right?"

Marcus grabbed him by the collar. "Shut up! The child is mine!"

"Yours?"

Justin scoffed. "So sure? The baby's over a month old, right? Funny, I slept with her around then too. Maybe it's an Evans! You better get a DNA test, Mr. Valentine. Don't let some bastard corrupt the Valentine bloodline!"

Fists rained down.

Marcus pinned him to the floor, punching ruthlessly.

"He's killing me! Call the police! Call the police!"

Justin screamed in agony.

His friend fumbled for his phone but froze under Marcus's glare.

Marcus landed punch after punch on Justin's face. "Tell the truth!"

Bloodied and broken, Justin finally begged. "Stop! I'll talk! I'll tell the truth!"
###Chapter 359

Marcus’s knuckles were bleeding.

He stopped.

Justin’s face was bruised and swollen.

Blood trickled from his nose and mouth.

Marcus glared at him. “Tell me the truth. You never touched Mina! How could she ever be interested in someone like you?”

Justin clenched his fists.

It was true. She hadn’t been interested.

Six months ago, Mina’s uncle wanted to collaborate with their family.

He seized the opportunity and asked for Mina.

Mina was truly beautiful.

Even with her bad reputation, many still wanted to sleep with her.

Every now and then, someone would claim to have slept with her.

This led everyone to believe she was promiscuous.

Even knowing she was easy, Justin still wanted her.

Mina’s uncle immediately arranged it when he heard the request.

But at the hotel, after meeting Mina, he was beaten up by her.

She cursed at him a few times and ran away.

He didn’t succeed, but he had already boasted about it.

When his friends asked if he had slept with Mina and how it was, Justin had to brazenly say it was great!

This left him no room to backtrack within their circle.

These useless rich kids, accustomed to flattery, were used to it.

How could he admit failure in front of his friends?

So, gritting his teeth, he spoke again. “Marcus, I don’t want to lie to you. I really did sleep with her! But I’m definitely not the father of her child… I slept with her six months ago. Just over a month ago, I saw her coming out of a hotel with a man…”

Seeing Justin beaten to a pulp but still sticking to his story, along with the test report, Marcus now completely believed him.

After all, he had azoospermia.

That night with Mina, she had personally said it wasn’t her first time… and she was pregnant.

Marcus slowly retracted his fist and let out a low, bitter laugh.

How ridiculous!

She had deceived him and married him, yet he was still defending her!

Marcus sneered coldly, stood up, and staggered out.

He rushed into the bathroom, splashed cold water on his face, then leaned against the wall and took a deep breath.

At this moment, he suddenly felt a strong urge to vent his frustrations.

He took out his phone, scrolled through his contacts, and saw the message he had sent Alexander last night.

After a moment’s thought, he immediately called.

The call was answered quickly. Alexander’s voice was cold. “What do you want?”

Marcus scoffed. “Are you angry?”

“…”

There was no response from the other end.

Marcus suddenly realized something. “No way, you went home last night, right? What? Don’t tell me you didn’t go home, and Evelyn didn’t call you?”

Alexander’s voice turned even colder. “I went home. Why would she call?”

Marcus took a deep breath. “Right, you went home. Then why are you so angry?”

Of course it was because!

Even though Alexander had returned home and appeared before Evelyn, he found her, Chloe, and Chloe Roland already asleep in the secondary bedroom.

She might not even have realized he had been missing for two hours!

It was utterly infuriating!

Alexander decided… he must teach Evelyn a lesson!

As for what kind of lesson?

Hmph, if he spoke to her before breakfast, he’d be a dog!

Alexander felt wronged but put on a tough front. “You can’t even sort out your own life. Don’t worry about mine!”

With that, Alexander hung up.

Then he headed to the dining room.

Usually, Evelyn would get up to prepare breakfast for him and Chloe.

Today, they hadn’t woken up yet—they must have stayed up late last night.

So, he would make breakfast for them.

Alexander busied himself in the kitchen.

After finishing breakfast, he heard the sound of a door opening upstairs and footsteps coming down.

Alexander’s face was still dark.

When Evelyn appeared, she looked at the three breakfasts on the table in surprise. “You got up so early to make breakfast?”

The man who was angry with her snorted. “Hurry up and try it before it gets cold.”

Breakfast time had arrived. He wasn’t angry anymore!

“Okay~”

Evelyn sat down and asked curiously, “Have you eaten already?”

Chloe Roland, who had come downstairs with Chloe, walked over and placed Chloe in front of a breakfast clearly meant for a child.

Then she went to another breakfast.

As soon as Chloe Roland sat down, the breakfast was coldly pulled away from her. “Sorry, this one is mine.”

Chloe Roland: ??

She stared blankly at Alexander, sensing the cold aura he emitted, and looked at Evelyn with grievance.

Seeing this, Evelyn found it both amusing and exasperating.

She raised an eyebrow but didn’t intervene, merely glancing toward the kitchen. “If you want to eat, make it yourself. The bread slices are ready.”

Since Chloe Roland had openly declared her liking for Alexander before… she would have to face the consequences.

Hearing this, Chloe Roland obediently stood up. “Okay.”

Then she walked into the kitchen.

Evelyn pretended not to notice.

She casually glanced and indeed saw the trace of smugness at the corner of the man’s lips, despite his dark expression this morning.

Evelyn: …

She suddenly remembered a saying: Men remain boys until they die.

This Alexander was really being childish today!!

She shook her head helplessly but picked up the fried egg from her bowl and gave it to him. “You have more.”

Alexander nodded happily.

Evelyn also silently breathed a sigh of relief.

She liked runny eggs.

Today’s fried egg was clearly overcooked and probably wouldn’t taste good… Well, she was definitely just showing concern for Alexander.

…

At the Ashcroft residence.

After being hung up on, Marcus had nowhere to go.

The company, knowing he had gotten married, considerately gave him a week off, so he had no work to attend to either.

In the end, he could only helplessly return home.

When his car stopped at the Ashcroft gate, Marcus was still listless.

But when he saw a familiar car, his eyes immediately lit up!

Wasn’t this his third uncle’s car?

He quickly strode inside and saw that although Third Uncle Ashcroft looked weathered, aside from fatigue, there was no other expression on his face, and he showed no signs of injury.

He immediately breathed a sigh of relief.

Third Uncle Ashcroft also heard him return.

He immediately put down his phone and looked over.

Although puzzled why Marcus was returning from outside on his wedding night, he first asked the question he cared about most. “You said on the phone yesterday, whose daughter is Evelyn? Your father’s or your second uncle’s?”

Marcus: “…”
###Chapter 360
Marcus Valentine paused, his gaze sweeping over him.

Noticing his silence, Third Uncle immediately sensed something amiss.

He frowned, cleared his throat softly, and spoke slowly.

"Is it your father's? Marcus, the Valentine family has always been devoted to one love."

"Your father couldn't have made such a mistake. There must be more to this story."

"As the eldest son, you should be more forgiving. Forgive him."

"After all, this is Valentine blood. You should know, the third branch still has no biological daughter…"

Third Uncle was unusually talkative today.

Every word was urging Marcus to accept this sister.

Marcus's mouth twitched.

He looked up at him. "Uncle, is it possible she's your daughter?"

"Absolutely impossible!"

Third Uncle stated firmly. "I know exactly what I've done in my life."

Marcus's mouth twitched again. "But she really is your daughter."

Third Uncle waved his hand dismissively. "I said it's impossible."

"Besides Ms. Nan, I've never touched another woman."

Marcus watched him quietly, saying nothing.

Third Uncle suddenly realized his slip of the tongue.

He coughed to cover it up. "And with Ms. Nan, we never crossed the line… It's not what you think!"

He and Sophia had known each other since they were young.

Their affection remained within the bounds of propriety.

They never overstepped.

Other women couldn't get close to him.

He was absolutely certain of this.

Back in San Francisco, when he learned Evelyn wasn't William's biological daughter, he had ruled himself out first.

He had told Marcus with conviction that it was impossible.

Seeing his certainty, Marcus shrugged helplessly.

"The DNA test results show that Grace is your daughter."

He frowned and pressed, "What?"

"It's true." Marcus knew the next part would be even harder to accept.

"Grace and Evelyn are twin sisters."

Third Uncle's entire body stiffened!!

The results were conclusive.

"I was only five years old at the time. I don't know the details."
