Third Uncle clenched his jaw, staring at the phone.

Images of his time with Sophia flashed through his mind…

Back then, Third Uncle was a rebellious young man.

More troublesome than Marcus is now, he was a tyrant in New York.

Yet he fell in love with Sophia, a unique woman.

Her every word and action carried the gentle grace of a scholarly family.

She spoke unhurriedly.

Just being near her calmed his restless heart.

So, they had been together.

When he was twenty-two, they went to the beach together and shared a room…

His heart had been pounding like a drum.

He could see Sophia's flushed face, her lowered head, and her flustered hands and feet.

The two of them, deeply in love, couldn't help but draw closer, kissing…

That time, they almost went all the way.

But at the last moment, he stopped in time.

He looked at Sophia and promised to marry her.

But Sophia wrapped her arms around his neck, her hand reaching in…

She was like a gentle older sister, guiding him…

If Evelyn and Grace were twins, both Sophia's and his daughters, then the only possibility was that time… Sophia had taken his sperm!!

Third Uncle clenched his jaw, his eyes suddenly deepening.

Later, that incident happened, and he and Sophia broke up…

But when they were together, Sophia had indeed mentioned wanting to have a child with him…

So, after breaking up, she used his sperm to get pregnant?

Why would she have stored his sperm…

What exactly happened back then!

Third Uncle's desire to find Sophia grew even stronger.

But he couldn't find her…

He had practically turned the earth upside down, but there was no trace of her.

After being taken away from the Valentine family by helicopter that time, she seemed to have vanished from the face of the earth…

And Evelyn…

That Evelyn he had helped Isabella suppress, that Evelyn he had never met… was actually his daughter?!

Third Uncle's calm eyes suddenly turned red.

He looked sharply at Marcus. "Where does Grace live?"

Back then, he and Grace were just friends with a significant age gap.

He thought her husband was unreliable, but it was beyond a friend's place to remind her.

But if Grace was his daughter…

That bastard husband of hers… could not be let off lightly!

"See if he often bullies her! My daughter cannot be toyed with by anyone!"

Marcus coughed. "...Well, that shouldn't be necessary."

"Unnecessary?!" Third Uncle said anxiously, grabbing his coat and putting it on.

"Her husband is too opportunistic and has always had ill intentions toward her."

"Before, I thought it inappropriate to remind her, lest it damage their marital relationship."

"Now that I know the truth, they must divorce! That kind of man is no good!"

He fastened his buttons, frowning at Marcus.

"You say it's unnecessary because you think she's love-struck and should stay with her husband?"

"Marcus, she is my daughter! As a father, I should tell her what's right and wrong!!"

"I can't just stand by and watch her be bullied!"

Marcus's mouth twitched again. "No, Uncle, what I mean is… she's already divorced."

"Grace came to her senses herself."

"She used some tricks to make her husband leave with nothing."

Third Uncle: "…"

Third Uncle lowered his head, continued fastening his buttons, and then walked out.

Marcus was stunned. "Where are you going?"

"To claim my daughter!"

"Of course, to bring the Valentine princess home!"
###Chapter 361

Evelyn Ross felt like a spinning top.

Chloe and Chloe were just being acting cute.

As a friend.

###Chapter 362

Because of the testimony last time.

###Chapter 368

Because both the Vance and Ashcroft families couldn't uncover the Nan family's background.

Grace could orchestrate her marriage to Alexander, which showed she was very capable.

In the car behind.

Grace grew up in New York.

She sighed at Mrs. Lawrence. "Mrs. Lawrence, please don't make a scene. You understand. You've offended someone. The other party has influence. Reason won't work here."

Mrs. Lawrence反而 smiled.

Mrs. Lawrence raised an eyebrow. "Has the group issued the appointment?"

"Not yet, but it's definitely her. You've offended someone. Please leave now. Otherwise, I'll call security. The preschool has strict security."

The teacher said smugly, "You'd better go beg Miss Valentine. If you keep causing trouble, we'll say you're trespassing and endangering the children! Be sensible and get out of here now!"

The principal looked helpless and gave Mrs. Lawrence and Evelyn an apologetic smile. "You should just leave. Find another school for your child..."

Before she could finish, an Ashcroft family car suddenly pulled up to the entrance.

Mr. Xu's secretary got out and strode over.
###Chapter 369

Principal Brown had always held little real authority at the school.

The wealthy, influential parents dictated the rules, rendering her position largely symbolic.

She had felt it was wrong to expel Chloe Davis previously and had even pleaded on her behalf.

Now, she pulled Mrs. Lawrence and Evelyn Ross aside, lowering her voice. "Don't fight this. The Valentine family is the new School Director, and they're targeting you specifically. Fiona Valentine is determined to win this. Arguing further is pointless. You should find a new school for your children immediately."

She sighed. "Miss Valentine is serious this time. She didn't just expel your children. She's made it known throughout our circles. No school in New York will accept them now. Your best option might be to consider studying abroad, to find another path."

This well-meant advice was overheard by Miss Lee, the teacher who had expelled Benny Lawrence. She immediately sneered. "Principal Brown, don't you think you're talking too much? Showing such favoritism. Should I inform Miss Valentine and suggest she transfer you to a school that would actually take them?"

Principal Brown frowned. "I was merely offering advice. Miss Lee, you should know when to stop. The children are innocent."

"Innocent? Their parents offended Miss Valentine. That makes them far from innocent!"

Principal Brown had heard enough. "Besides, procedurally, Fiona Valentine isn't the School Director yet. She has no authority to expel students."

Miss Lee crossed her arms, embodying the classic villain, her posture dripping with arrogance. "See for yourself. The Valentine family's appointment letter has arrived. Miss Valentine will be School Director momentarily."

She jerked her chin toward a specific direction.

Everyone followed her gaze and saw the secretary of Mr. Valentine the Third. They were stunned into silence.

Principal Brown gave a bitter smile to Mrs. Lawrence and Evelyn.

She suddenly took a deep breath and turned to Miss Lee. "I've seen enough. First, expelling Chloe. Today, expelling Benny. What's next? Will Fiona Valentine's son become the little tyrant of the entire school? This is detrimental to the children's development! I must speak out against this!"

Miss Lee stepped forward and shoved her. "You old hag! What do you want to say? Go ahead and say it! I'll call Miss Valentine right now so you can say your piece! Perfect timing. Once you're fired, I can get promoted to Principal!"

Principal Brown's chest heaved with anger. "You... you are a disgrace to the teaching profession! So obsessed with climbing up, you've abandoned all basic decency!"

"Decency? What is decency in the face of power? Old woman, you've worked in early childhood education for so many years. You were good to the children, but what was the result?"

Miss Lee took another step closer. "A few years ago, you had a chance for a promotion and transfer. But because you didn't know how to suck up to Miss Valentine, you got stuck in this dump, clinging to your Principal title, preventing the rest of us from advancing! You'd better go explain yourself to Miss Valentine and leave quickly. Let me try being Principal!"

Mrs. Lawrence frowned. "Miss Lee, one should have a conscience! Karma will get you for this!"

Miss Lee kept her arms crossed. "Karma? Do you think this is a TV drama? You're from a wealthy family yourselves. Don't you understand this world doesn't operate on some simplistic 'good is rewarded, evil is punished' system?"

She lifted her chin, looking at Mrs. Lawrence and Evelyn. "You two, go beg Miss Valentine now. Maybe if you appease her, she'll leave a path open for your children."

Evelyn let out a derisive laugh. "The master hasn't even spoken, and the dog is already barking?"

Miss Lee was instantly enraged. "Who are you calling a dog?"

"Whoever recognizes themselves, I suppose~"

"You... you..."

Miss Lee was so angry her fingers trembled. She pointed at Benny Lawrence. "Benny, remember this! The reason you can't go to school anymore is because she offended me!"

Benny looked confusedly at Evelyn, then at Mrs. Lawrence, before finally hiding in Mrs. Lawrence's embrace.

Mrs. Lawrence was furious. "Why are you targeting a child again? A teacher like you is nothing but a disgrace in scholar's clothing! One bad apple spoils the whole barrel!"

Miss Lee raised her chin even higher. "Even if I'm that bad apple, I can still stay here! What about you? You'll have to beg everywhere just to get your child into a school!"

She snorted coldly and glared at Principal Brown. "And you! I'll remember this! Speaking up for them? I will definitely tell Miss Valentine!"

Principal Brown took a deep breath. "Go ahead and tell her! I've long wanted to quit! This job? I resign!"

Her eyes reddened.

She had entered this field because she genuinely loved children.

All these years, she had worked hard to be a good teacher and educate every student well.

But ever since Fiona Valentine's son enrolled three years ago, her nightmare began.

Fiona's son was overweight, affecting his health. She tried to control his diet. The chubby boy went home crying, and Fiona directly filled the classroom with snacks.

The children ate too many snacks and refused their meals...

She tried to manage it, but after being scolded by Fiona a few times for meddling, she gave up.

Later, her son became the school bully, leading other children in bullying the weak. Countless children had been victimized.

But the parents, knowing it was the Valentine family, could only tell their own children to placate the little tyrant, making him increasingly unruly.

Yes.

No one could stand against capital.

The Valentine family was now the School Director. Fiona Valentine would be even more unrestrained.

Good children like Chloe and Benny were expelled on a whim.

Hearing Principal Brown wanted to resign, Miss Lee laughed. "You resign? Miss Valentine won't give you the chance! You'll definitely be fired!"

Evelyn chuckled. "I can give you the chance to resign. Do you want it?"

Miss Lee was stunned, then burst into laughter. "What nonsense are you talking? You'll give me a chance? Miss Valentine's appointment letter is here! What chance can you give me? Anyone hearing you would think you're the School Director!"

As soon as the words left her mouth, Mr. Valentine the Third's secretary, having watched this farce long enough, finally spoke calmly. "Ah, but she is."
###Chapter 370

The secretary’s words left the principal and the teacher stunned.

They both stared at Mr. Valentine’s secretary, their eyes blank.

The teacher asked in disbelief, “What did you say?”

“She is the new School Director!”

Mr. Valentine’s secretary handed the appointment letter to the principal. “From now on, Miss Nan will be in charge of the preschool. Is that clear?”

The principal was completely dumbfounded.

She opened the appointment letter. The name “Evelyn” was clearly written in the appointment section.

She looked at Evelyn in shock.

The teacher beside her exclaimed, “Impossible! Why would the Valentine family choose an outsider? It should be Miss Fiona Valentine!”

The principal snapped back to reality and looked at Evelyn and Mrs. Lawrence.

She murmured, “So you’re not here to beg for mercy…”

Mrs. Lawrence smiled. “We’re here to enroll him.”

Evelyn stepped forward. “To be precise, we’re here to revoke the expulsion notice.”

The principal’s eyes lit up. She immediately nodded. “No problem! I’ll withdraw the notice and issue a clarification!”

Evelyn looked at the teacher.

The teacher took a step back, forcing an ingratiating smile. “Miss Nan, I…”

“You’re fired.”

Evelyn was direct and gave her no chance.

The teacher said urgently, “You can’t fire me. There’s no reason…”

“Abusing a child is reason enough.”

Evelyn stepped closer. The teacher retreated in fear.

The teacher stammered, “I didn’t abuse anyone. You have no proof!”

“I have proof!”

The principal suddenly spoke up. She took out her phone and played a video.

In the video, the teacher withheld Benny’s lunch and pinched his arm while making him stand in punishment.

When Benny cried, the teacher pricked his arm with a needle. “Cry all you want. See if I care.”

Mrs. Lawrence had already been furious hearing her son’s account.

Seeing the video, her eyes turned red. She rushed forward and grabbed the teacher’s hair.

She slapped her hard across the face, repeatedly.

Slap! Slap! Slap!

“How dare you bully my son! How dare you!”

Mrs. Lawrence cried as she hit her.

Evelyn did not stop her. The principal’s eyes were also red.

She had recorded these videos intending to expose the teacher but feared retaliation from the Valentine family.

She was a coward, tormented by her conscience every day.

Now she could finally bring them out…

The principal felt like she had been reborn.

Mrs. Lawrence had hit enough and released the teacher.

Miss Lee’s cheeks were swollen like a pig’s head. She wept bitterly.

She wiped her tears. “I’m calling the police. I’m calling the police…”

Mrs. Lawrence’s palms were numb. She looked dazed.

“I’m calling the police too.”

Evelyn spoke calmly. “I’m charging you with child abuse. You will pay for this!”

Hearing this, the teacher’s legs gave way. She collapsed to the floor.

Mr. Valentine’s secretary stepped forward and handed the appointment letter to Evelyn. “Miss Nan, this…”

Evelyn glanced at it and looked back at the car not far away.

Through the window, she saw Mr. Valentine watching them.

Evelyn was silent for a moment before accepting the appointment letter.

She said to the secretary, “Handle the police report.”

Then she looked at the principal. “Let’s go inside.”

Since she was taking over the preschool, she would clean up its toxic culture.

The principal understood. Her eyes reddened, and she immediately led the way. “Yes, yes… Director Nan, this way please!”

She walked ahead.

Evelyn and Mrs. Lawrence followed with Benny.

Entering the campus, they happened to see Fiona Valentine’s son, Joey, taking snacks in a classroom.

Evelyn frowned and walked over.

Joey grabbed some snacks and turned to leave.

The classroom teacher was used to it and did not scold him.

Joey ate his snacks while teasing other children. He also tore up another child’s drawing.

When Evelyn entered the classroom, she saw a child crying while the teacher ignored it.

She frowned deeply and looked at the principal.

The principal immediately stepped forward, snatched Joey’s snacks, and picked up the snack box. “No more snacks in the classroom! Joey, stop bullying other children!”

Joey looked at his empty hands and burst into loud tears.

His cries were deafening, as if they could lift the roof.

The principal and Evelyn covered their ears.

The principal tried to soothe him. “Don’t cry. The teacher is doing this for your own good.”

Joey was spoiled and wouldn’t listen. He cried even louder.

It was irritating.

Evelyn lowered her gaze and lazily walked over to him.

Her cold aura made Joey’s crying pause.

Evelyn glanced at him, grabbed him by the collar, and carried him to the yard. “Cry here if you want. Don’t disturb the other children.”

This wasn’t retaliation.

Misbehaving children required special methods.

Joey was startled and hiccupped.

Evelyn took the principal to inspect other classrooms…

Outside the preschool.

Mr. Valentine’s secretary waited for the police to handle Miss Lee before getting into the car.

He had just sat in the passenger seat when Mr. Valentine said slowly, “Are there any auspicious dates coming up?”

The secretary immediately replied, “There’s one in a week.”

“Good. Let’s hold the banquet on that day.”

The secretary was puzzled. “What banquet?”

Mr. Valentine smiled and looked out the window. “The Family Reunion Gala.”

The secretary was stunned. “You’re going to publicly acknowledge Miss Nan?”

“Yes.”

Mr. Valentine’s face seemed to have grown younger.

The secretary couldn’t help but twitch his mouth. “If I may remind you, Miss Nan doesn’t seem to want to acknowledge you. What if she doesn’t come?”

Mr. Valentine lowered his gaze and spoke lightly. “She will come.”

The secretary was confused. “Why?”

“Because she just acknowledged me.”

The secretary was even more confused.

Mr. Valentine said no more. He only smiled meaningfully.

Given Evelyn’s personality, accepting the appointment letter was her way of acknowledging him as her father.

She wouldn’t let him be humiliated at the Family Reunion Gala. She would definitely come…
###Chapter 371

Not bad.

Marcus Valentine was almost certain.

The woman before him, Grace Nan, was Evelyn Ross.

He had investigated Grace Nan.

For over twenty years, Grace had been timid and weak.

A person's character doesn't change overnight.

Moreover, her driver was Alexander Vance.

Alexander wouldn't just fall for someone else.

That man was stubbornly loyal.

He couldn't have moved on from Evelyn so quickly.

So, it had to be Evelyn.

Marcus's smile slowly faded.

He felt both joy and sorrow.

Joy that Evelyn was still alive.

Sorrow that the one who died was the real Grace Nan.

Both were his biological daughters.

Marcus lowered his gaze.

"Let's go home," he said.

He still needed to find Sophia Ashcroft…

The car returned to the Valentine estate.

Upstairs, Penelope and Fiona Valentine were trembling in their room.

Fiona was frantic. "Penelope, is Grace really Uncle's daughter? If she comes back, where does that leave us?"

Penelope was irritated. "Keep your voice down! We're adopted. We'll still have a place, but our status will drop."

The Valentine family had no biological daughters.

They were the prized jewels.

If Grace returned, they would no longer be special.

Right now, they were treated like princesses.

Even more cherished than Chloe Roland or the Sherwood heiresses.

If Grace came back…

Penelope clenched her fist.

Suddenly, she spoke. "The Valentine and Vance alliance marriage is still pending…"

Fiona grasped at this. "Yes! The Valentine-Vance marriage. You're the only unmarried one here! Grace is divorced with a child. The Vances would never accept her! Penelope, our future depends on you!"

Penelope withdrew her hand.

She looked at the fool with disdain.

The sound of a car arrived from downstairs.

She immediately raised a hand to Fiona. "Shh…"

They opened the door.

They crept into the hallway and peered down.

Marcus Valentine approached. "Uncle, how did the recognition go? Will you announce Grace as your biological daughter? My true cousin?"

Marcus shook his head. "Not yet…"

The banquet was in a week.

The two upstairs sighed in relief.

Fiona whispered, "It must be because Grace's scandals are too shameful. Uncle is afraid acknowledging her will bring disgrace!"

Penelope thought the same.

A divorced woman with a burden…

The Valentine family having such a daughter would be a laughingstock.

No one would marry her.

No matter how much Uncle doted on her, he wouldn't acknowledge her and make the Valentines a joke.

At most, he would give some compensation.

As they were thinking this, Marcus spoke. "Has the Vance boy arrived in New York?"

Marcus Valentine replied, "He's been here for a while. Uncle, are you thinking of restarting the Valentine-Vance marriage? Is the alliance still on?"

Marcus smiled. "Yes, it's still on!"

Evelyn and Alexander were meant to be!

What was there to continue? They were already together!

Marcus Valentine misunderstood, thinking his uncle approved of Grace and Alexander.

He nodded. "Alright, I'll continue pushing for it. The alliance between our families should be announced publicly."

A major corporate marriage had commercial value.

Announcing it would benefit the Valentines.

It would also help the Vances, who were new to the New York market.

Rumors had already spread.

Neither family had responded.

A response now would build momentum.

As they spoke, they walked toward the study.

Marcus suddenly asked, "Last night was your wedding night. You weren't home. Where did you go?"

Marcus Valentine paused.

Marcus asked again, "Where is your wife?"

Marcus Valentine paused once more.

He quickly changed the subject. "Uncle, you've been away from New York this month. There are company matters that need reporting…"

"No need to report. You handle it. I'm getting old. The company will be yours sooner or later. Now go bring your wife home."

Marcus Valentine fell silent.

Marcus lowered his gaze and patted his shoulder. "Marcus, you're still young. You don't understand yet. Cherish what you have. Sharing a path together is fate."

Marcus Valentine's steps faltered.

Marcus entered the study without looking back.

Marcus Valentine pressed his lips together, sighed silently, and turned to leave.

On the second day of his marriage, Mina Sherwood had disappeared from home. It really didn't look good.

Valentine stocks couldn't fluctuate because of him.

After Marcus Valentine left, Fiona and Penelope straightened up.

Fiona smiled obsequiously. "Penelope, did you hear? The Valentine-Vance marriage is still on… You can rest easy now! Once you're Mrs. Vance, you won't have to worry about being an adopted daughter of the Valentines!"

Penelope curled her lips into a smile.

Fiona breathed a sigh of relief.

With Penelope's support, her future wouldn't be too bleak.

Just as she thought this, her phone rang.

She answered. A kindergarten teacher's voice came through. "Hello, is this Joey Valentine's parent?"

Fiona immediately said, "Yes, it is."

"Please come to the kindergarten right away!"

Fiona clenched her fingers. "Who told you to call me?"

The phone was snatched away.

Evelyn's cool voice came through. "It's me. Get over here now!"

"Grace?! What did you do to my son?!"

Fiona shouted. "Are you getting back at me? Don't go too far! I'm not someone you can bully! I'm coming right now!"

After shouting, Fiona's eyes reddened with urgency.

She looked at Penelope. "Penelope, I have to go to the kindergarten!"

Penelope nodded.

Fiona, worried about her son, drove straight to the kindergarten.

Entering, she saw Joey Valentine standing in the yard as punishment.

The little fatty was crying, his whole body trembling.

Evelyn said directly, "You're just in time. Take him for a check-up… He's sick."

While scolding Joey earlier, she noticed something was wrong.

The child had a problem.

Despite her conflicts with Fiona, she wouldn't take it out on the child.

She immediately called Fiona to warn her.
###Chapter 372

Fiona Valentine shrieked, cutting her off. "Grace Nan, what are you doing! The child is innocent!"

Evelyn Ross narrowed her eyes.

Fiona rushed to Joey Valentine's side.

Joey wrapped his arms around her waist. "They won't feed me, they won't let me tear the paintings... Mommy, hit her!"

Fiona glared viciously at Evelyn. "I'm reporting you for child abuse!"

Evelyn paused, then let out a derisive laugh. "Where was this righteousness when Chloe and Benny were being slandered? It's true, you never feel the pain until the stick hits your own back!"

"You..."

Fiona's eyes reddened. "Vicious snake!"

Evelyn shrugged. "Say whatever you want. But I'm not like you. I have principles. I asked you here to take your son to the hospital for a check-up."

Fiona's eyes widened. "What did you do to my son?"

She grabbed Joey, inspecting his body. "Son, where does it hurt? Did the teacher hit you?"

Joey squirmed. "No..."

"Really? They're not letting you talk?" Fiona turned to the principal. "Did you do something to Joey? Don't think I don't know about your methods! Abusing children and brainwashing them!"

She was frantic.

The principal sneered. "Don't worry. I'm not as cruel as you!"

Fiona froze.

Evelyn spoke calmly. "There's something wrong with his body. He might have a brain issue. You should take him to the hospital."

Fiona laughed in anger. "I think you're the one with the brain issue! Grace Nan, if you want revenge, just say it! I admit defeat. I'll send my son abroad if I have to!"

"If you want to expel him, at least find a good reason! This one is pathetic and ridiculous! It's just a stupid preschool! We're leaving!"

Fiona grabbed Joey's hand. "Son, let's go!"

Joey followed her.

Evelyn took a few steps after them, her voice soft. "Fiona Valentine, I'm not a saint. But your son is only five or six. I couldn't bear to see him have an episode, so I'm meddling. You should take him to the hospital. I'm almost certain he has a brain tumor. It's causing his uncontrollable appetite and violent temper..."

"You're the one with a violent temper! My son is big because he has a good digestion! Look how strong he is! Not like your Chloe, skinny as a twig! I heard she was abused by her grandmother? Can't even take care of your own child, but you have time to criticize mine!"

Fiona, mistaking Evelyn's concern for mockery, spat angrily. "You became a school director, so impressive. I won't let my son suffer here!"

Evelyn narrowed her eyes, thinking her ungrateful.

But looking at Joey, a life was at stake. She added, "You don't have to believe me. But if he starts vomiting, you must take it seriously..."

Fiona scoffed. "Vomiting? My son never gets enough to eat! How could he vomit! I won't let this expulsion go easily!"

She stepped closer to Evelyn. "You think you're so great because you're the third uncle's daughter? Let me tell you, the third uncle has no intention of acknowledging a disgraced daughter like you! This school director position is probably just compensation! Enjoy being a preschool director. I don't want the spot. You can have it!"

She stepped back. "As for me, I'm still the cherished adopted daughter of the Valentine family. A hundred times better than you!"

With that, she took her son's hand and left.

Evelyn watched their retreating figures, saying nothing more.

The principal frowned. "Director Nan, are we really not going to do anything?"

Evelyn laughed coldly. "I said it. I'm not a saint. After how she treated Chloe, my warning is more than enough. Whether she believes it is up to her."

The principal sighed. "Alright."

...

Fiona drove Joey home, growing angrier by the minute.

She pulled into the driveway and saw her mother-in-law and husband approaching.

Her mother-in-law looked surprised. "Why are you back so early?"

Fiona was about to speak when Joey burst into tears. "Waaah! The bad teacher at school bullied me!"

Her mother-in-law panicked. "What happened? Fiona, aren't you the school director? How could he still be bullied?"

Fiona's eyes flickered. She sneered. "That Grace Nan seduced the third uncle. He made her the school director. Now she's getting back at me by expelling Joey!"

Her mother-in-law's face darkened. "How could he do that? And he's your elder! No, I'm going to see him! We can't take this lying down! What reason did they give for expelling my grandson?"

Fiona found it ridiculous. "She actually said Joey has a brain tumor. Told me to take him to the hospital. Mom, isn't that laughable?"

Her mother-in-law snorted. "This is an insult! If she has a problem, she should take it up with the adults! Why involve a child? Fiona, let's go to your house now. Talk to your third uncle! Even if Grace Nan is his biological daughter, he can't be this unreasonable!"

Fiona's husband, Roy Valentine, nodded. "Right. Our Valentine family might not be one of the five great families, but we won't be bullied like this! Let's go. Take our son and confront them!"

The group, along with bodyguards, was about to leave in a fury when Joey suddenly stopped crying.

His face looked uncomfortable.

Fiona immediately asked, "Son, what's wrong?"

Before she finished speaking, Joey suddenly projectile vomited.
###Chapter 373

The Valentine home descended into chaos.

Everyone rushed toward Joey.

Fiona was sprayed with vomit.

She fought back nausea and grabbed tissues to wipe herself.

Roy supported his son's back.
"What's wrong? Where does it hurt?"

Joey was crying and vomiting uncontrollably.

He only began to recover after his stomach was completely empty.

Fiona cleaned herself off.

But she still felt like the smell lingered.

She looked at the family doctor.
"Doctor, what's wrong with him?"

The doctor reviewed the medical report.
"Did the young master cry at preschool today?"

Fiona frowned and looked at her son.

Joey lay listlessly, complaining he was hungry.

"Have Nanny Wu prepare dinner."
"I want chips! The teacher wouldn't give me any and made me stand in timeout! I don't want to go to school!"

Fiona's expression changed instantly.
"They made you stand in timeout?"

The doctor nodded.
"It's windy outside. Inhaling cold air while crying can cause vomiting. Let's observe him for now."

Roy and the elderly lady didn't seem concerned.

Joey had always been prone to spitting up since infancy.

But Fiona remembered Grace's words.
"...you must take vomiting seriously."

She immediately shook her head to dismiss the thought.

How could she believe that woman?

She coaxed Joey through his dinner.

She only left the room after he fell asleep.

The housekeeper approached quickly.
"Madam, someone from the Ashcroft family is here."

Ethan Valentine stood in the living room.

He was tall with sharp, intense features.

He currently managed the project department at Ashcroft Group.

Fiona's eyes instantly welled with tears.

Ethan had come to deliver an invitation.

The Ashcroft family was hosting a grand banquet in one week.

The reason was unclear, but formalities had to be observed.

Noticing her expression, Ethan frowned.
"What happened?"

Roy spoke first.
"Joey was bullied at school! The new School Director made him cry in the cold courtyard! He vomited as soon as he got home!"

Ethan was stunned.
"Isn't the preschool director from the Ashcroft family? Isn't it Fiona's responsibility?"

Fiona looked down.
"Elder Brother replaced them."
"With whom?"
"Grace Nan."

Ethan recalled her.

The bridesmaid at his elder brother's wedding.

He immediately misunderstood.
"Did Elder Brother give the position to Grace Nan as a favor to his wife?"

Fiona's eyes reddened further.
"His wife dislikes me, I understand. But now that she's the lady of the house, both my child and I are being mistreated..."

Ethan's face turned cold.
"A daughter of the Ashcroft family will not be bullied! An adopted daughter is still a daughter! Leave this to me!"

He stood to leave.
"Don't worry. An Ashcroft daughter should never suffer injustice."

Fiona breathed a sigh of relief.

It seemed Third Uncle wouldn't acknowledge that daughter.

With Third Brother handling it, it was enough.

Ethan drove home.

His assistant cautiously reminded him.
"Miss Fiona is spoiled and has deep conflicts with the Madam. Young Master Marcus dotes on his wife. He may not support you."

Ethansneered.
"She is my sister."

He looked toward the lit study on the second floor.
"If I can't convince Elder Brother, I'll go to Third Uncle!"
###Chapter 374

The only person in the Valentine family who could control the unruly Marcus was Third Uncle.

Ethan Valentine had thought it through clearly.

He had only taken two steps before halting abruptly.

He frowned deeply and suddenly looked at his assistant.
"Big Brother went out drinking on his wedding night last night.
Has his wife come home tonight?"

The assistant immediately shook his head.
"Not yet."

Ethan hesitated.

Something seemed off between Big Brother and his wife.
They clearly cared for each other, yet they were caught in some inexplicable quarrel.
Would complaining to Third Uncle now only worsen their conflict?

He pondered for a moment before finally making a decision.
"Forget it.
I'll go to the preschool tomorrow!"

…

Mina Sherwood indeed had not gone home.

She and Chloe Roland were both staying at the Nan residence.
The already cramped house felt even more crowded.
Both guest rooms were occupied.

Alexander Vance was displeased.
As they prepared for bed that night, he couldn't help but complain.
"Don't they have homes of their own?"

Evelyn Ross was focused on her computer screen, fingers flying across the keyboard as she worked on something.
She didn't even look up at his words.

Alexander slowly walked behind Evelyn and glanced at the document title: Preschool Restructuring Plan.

Below it was a detailed list of the preschool's current issues, along with a series of new rules!

The first rule prohibited storing snacks in any classroom.

No preschool would ever do such a thing!

Alexander looked at her profile, absorbed in her work, and asked softly, "Taking it this seriously?"

Evelyn nodded.
"Since I've taken on this responsibility, I'll see it through to the end.
Besides, Chloe is almost three and will need to attend school soon.
How can I feel at ease without creating a proper environment for her?"

Alexander nodded and said nothing more.

This was just who Evelyn was.
Whatever she did, she gave it her all.

Otherwise, during her part-time jobs back in the day—whether as a repair technician, chef, or courier—she had excelled in every role.

Alexander watched her, sensing there were still secrets about her he had yet to uncover.

Evelyn finally finished the document and established the latest set of rules before stretching lazily.

Alexander immediately looked over with burning intensity.

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.
"Tonight, I promised to do face masks with Mina and Chloe."

Alexander: ???

Before he could respond, Evelyn had already risen and left the room.

Alexander could only lie down boredly and pull out his phone.
He happened to see a message from Marcus: [Is Mina at your place?]

Alexander gritted his teeth: [Can you please take your wife home?]

Evelyn did not return all night.
After applying face masks with Mina and Chloe, the three of them ended up squeezed into one room and fell asleep.

Evelyn woke up feeling refreshed.
The next morning, she opened the door and was startled.
Alexander stood there with heavy dark circles under his eyes, gazing at her resentfully.

His voice was low and husky.
"Enjoying having a woman on each side, were you?"

Evelyn: "…"

…

After breakfast, Evelyn asked Mina and Chloe to stay home and look after the children.
Alexander acted as chauffeur, driving her directly to the preschool.

It was Wednesday.
The preschool held a weekly all-staff meeting mid-week.

Evelyn intended to use this opportunity to announce the newly established rules to all the teachers.

10 a.m.

One teacher was left on duty in each classroom.
All other teachers, especially those who had previously slacked off and followed Fiona Valentine's lead, were summoned by the principal to the conference room.

Evelyn sat at the head of the table, scanning the room.
Without preamble, she distributed the rules she had drafted the previous day to everyone.

She stated decisively, "From now on, the preschool will operate entirely according to the new regulations.
Does anyone have any objections?"

The veteran employees were about to raise their hands in protest when Evelyn coldly added, "Objections will be noted but not entertained.
If you don't want to comply, you can leave.
I don't care how things were done under Fiona Valentine's management.
Under my watch, you will follow my rules.
At the very least, children in the classrooms will no longer be ranked or treated differently!"

Someone couldn't resist speaking up.
"But the children here all come from special backgrounds.
If we don't differentiate, can we really treat them all equally?"

"Absolutely."

Evelyn looked at them.
"Children come to school to receive an education, not to seek a sense of superiority.
Furthermore, expelling students arbitrarily will no longer be permitted!"

The teachers lowered their heads, not daring to say another word.

Just then, the office door was suddenly pushed open.

Ethan Valentine strode in, followed by Fiona Valentine and Joey Valentine.

Ethan found a seat and sat down arrogantly.
"Ms.
Ross, is it? I'm here today to demand an explanation!"

Evelyn glanced at Fiona, then at the man who bore a fifty percent resemblance to Marcus, guessing he was likely a Valentine cousin.

But her expression remained cold as she replied indifferently, "What explanation?"

Ethan pointed at the preschool rules projected on the screen.
"I see your first rule prohibits mistreating children.
So, yesterday, you made Joey cry outside and stand there as punishment.
Does that count as corporal punishment?"

The principal hurriedly explained, "That wasn't punishment!
Joey was crying uncontrollably.
Staying in the classroom would have disturbed the other children, so we took him outside to calm him down…"

In preschool, the children are all very young.

If one starts crying, it's easy for others to join in.

So taking a crying child outside separately is actually standard practice.

Moreover, Joey had been particularly unruly yesterday, and Evelyn had wanted to teach him a lesson…

Ethan sneered.
"So in the future, any time a child misbehaves, they can be taken outside to 'calm down'? How is that different from punishment!"

He stared directly at Evelyn, his gaze fierce.
"Furthermore, your preschool made my child stand outside as punishment, crying and exposed to the cold wind, which caused him to vomit incessantly after returning home last night.
This matter requires a proper resolution!
Otherwise…"

Ethan tilted his head and gave Evelyn a sinister smile.

He left the threat unspoken, but it sent a chill down everyone's spines.

The principal turned pale with fear, looking nervously at Evelyn.

Evelyn remained seated upright, calmly lowering her gaze.
"Joey only cried for a few moments in the yard yesterday and stood there for about ten minutes in total.
His vomiting wasn't due to crying; it's because he has a brain tumor.
Have you taken him to the hospital for an examination?"

"How dare you spout such nonsense here!"

Fiona glared venomously at Evelyn.
"Are you a doctor?"

"No."

"Then what gives you the right to say my son has a brain tumor!"

Of course, it was because, in her current drug research, there was a treatment for this very condition!

Evelyn had seen far too many pediatric brain tumor patients.
Every one of Joey's symptoms matched those of a brain tumor patient exactly!

Evelyn was absolutely certain he was ill.

But just as this thought crossed her mind, Ethan suddenly shot to his feet.
With both hands, he violently flipped the table in front of him.

The crashing sound of shattering and clattering echoed through the room, startling all the teachers into taking several steps back.

Evelyn's first instinct was to look at Joey.

Brain tumor patients are exceptionally sensitive to sound.
Sure enough, Joey covered his ears in terror, his face deathly pale, swaying unsteadily on his feet…
###Chapter 375

Joey's face was pale.

His throat moved slightly.

He looked like he was about to vomit again...

While Evelyn was observing him, Ethan was already furious.

"Grace, right?"

"You're here cursing the children at this preschool, saying they're sick."

"I can see you have malicious intentions!"

"I won't let this matter rest today!"

Fiona stood nearby with her arms crossed.

She looked at Evelyn smugly.

"Grace, I know you're good at fighting."

"You took down all our bodyguards yesterday."

"But my third brother is a mixed martial arts champion!"

She stared arrogantly at Evelyn.

Her only thought was revenge.

She didn't notice her son's discomfort at all.

Evelyn found them ridiculous.

The principal immediately stepped forward.

She feared Evelyn would get hurt.

"There's been a misunderstanding, Mr. Valentine."

"Let's all calm down and discuss how to handle this properly."

"Violence won't solve anything!"

Ethan glared at her.

"Fine."

"Then I want the teacher who punished Joey yesterday to Kowtow and apologize to him."

"And I want the expulsion notice revoked."

"He will continue his studies here."

He then turned to Evelyn.

"Miss Nan, who was it that dragged him out of the classroom yesterday?"

The principal started to speak.

But Evelyn answered directly.

"It was me."

"Then you will apologize to my child!"

Ethan cracked his neck and flexed his wrists.

"I have a short temper."

"Don't make me do this the hard way."

Evelyn let out a scornful laugh.

A mixed martial arts champion?

It had been a while since she'd had a good fight.

She stood up.

She was about to suggest they find a more spacious place.

She didn't want to wreck the conference room.

But a teacher rushed in from outside.

She went straight to the principal.

She showed her phone and whispered something.

The principal looked shocked.

She immediately went to Evelyn's side.

She spoke in a hushed tone.

"Director Nan, someone is live streaming this."

"They're broadcasting what's happening in the conference room online."

Evelyn frowned at this.

She looked at the principal's phone.

Sure enough, there was a live stream.

The camera wasn't pointed at anyone's face.

It was aimed at their feet.

So it didn't violate anyone's portrait rights.

Following the angle, she quickly spotted the teacher holding the phone.

The principal marched over.

"Who told you to do this?!"

The teacher, caught red-handed, remained unruffled.

She glanced at Fiona.

"I simply can't stand you treating a child this way."

"I want to expose the dark side of this preschool."

"I know I won't have a job here after today."

"But that's fine."

"The world needs people like me who speak the truth."

What did it matter if she lost her job?

Fiona had already paid her enough to live on comfortably.

This live stream was all part of Fiona's scheme.

Ethan causing a scene.

Fiona starting a live stream.

It would immediately damage the preschool's reputation.

The school's board would hold Grace responsible.

The Valentines, despite their power, cared about their image.

Grace would surely be forced to step down.

As the victim, and the only Valentine with a child enrolled...

Her position on the board would naturally be restored.

Fiona had it all figured out.

A smug smile played on her lips.

Evelyn looked at the live stream.

Many people were watching.

The viewer count was climbing rapidly.

The influence was clearly artificial.

Someone had bought a trending topic.

The comments were all criticizing her.

"What kind of leader treats a child like this?"

"That child is only five or six years old!"

"Kicked out of the classroom to stand in the cold?"

"The teacher is way out of line!"

"Does anyone else think the kid's dad is kinda hot?"

"Yeah, he flipped a table, but I get it."

"If someone messed with my kid, I'd want to burn the place down too."

...

The clueless spectators were enjoying the drama.

The principal was furious.

"Shut down the live stream immediately!"

It was the only way she could think to control the damage.

The teacher shook her head.

"No."

"My viewers wouldn't allow it."

"Everyone wants the preschool to give an explanation."

Comments immediately flooded in.

"Why shut it down?"

"Got something to hide?"

"Child abuse at a preschool is a serious issue everyone should care about."

"Already called the police. You're welcome."

"This keeps happening at preschools."

"Why do people without any ethics become teachers?"

"Child abusers deserve to die."

"I hope your own kids go through the same thing!"

...

Ethan frowned.

He hadn't expected a live stream.

The Valentines were on the board.

A public scandal would hurt the family's name.

He moved to grab the phone and stop the stream.

Fiona held his arm.

She whispered to him.

"Third Brother, it's already happening."

"Stopping it now might make things worse."

"The best solution is to have them apologize on camera."

"Let's downplay the whole thing."

Ethan looked at her.

"You're willing to let this go?"

Just an apology for someone who bullied his nephew?

Fiona sighed.

"For the family's reputation, yes."

Ethan thought for a moment.

"Fine."

"She's getting off easy."

"But don't worry, I'll make it up to you elsewhere."

He turned to Evelyn.

"Alright."

"We're reasonable people."

"Come here, apologize properly to the child."

"Revoke the expulsion notice."

"That's the end of it."

"We won't hold the preschool responsible."

At that moment, the teacher with the phone turned.

Joey accidentally came into view.

He looked worse.

He leaned against the wall.

He seemed on the verge of collapsing.

A comment suddenly appeared in the stream.

"I'm a neurologist."

"That child really doesn't look well."
###Chapter 376

Ethan Valentine felt he had already compromised.

Grace should know her place.

But Evelyn Ross kept staring at him. "I disciplined him because he tore his classmate's painting."

"You sent your child to school for the teacher to educate him, didn't you?"

He knew Joey was mischievous.

But Fiona was a daughter of the Valentine family.

A child should only be disciplined by his own family.

No one else could lay a hand on him.

Moreover...

"This is corporal punishment!"

Evelyn scoffed. "He vomited because he's sick."

"Brain tumor."

"It has nothing to do with me."

"Can't you understand plain English?"

"You...!"

Ethan's hands itched to act.

But he couldn't throw a punch during a live stream.

He would lose the moral high ground.

Ethan stayed silent.

Fiona spoke up. "Grace, my son is perfectly healthy."

"He gets a check-up every six months."

"We have a family doctor."

"Whether he's sick or not, I know better than you."

"Stop making excuses!"

Fiona began to cry. "We have some personal history."

"How could you take it out on my child?"

"My child is innocent!"

Her tears made the live stream explode.

—So there's a personal grudge!

—Child abuser! The parents know their child's health better than you!

—This person is unfit to be a teacher. And she's the director? Report her!

While the comments raged, police sirens wailed.

Two officers entered. "We received a report of child abuse here. Is it true?"

The live stream celebrated.

—Who called the police? A hero for the people!

—Officer, it's her. She bullied the child. Take her away!

—Justice is here!

—Child abusers should die!

...

Evelyn looked at the police but said nothing.

Ethan stepped in front of her.

The man was tall and burly, like a small mountain.

He spoke. "Grace, I know you're close to my sister-in-law."

"You don't want to make things difficult for her."

"Apologize now."

"Let's both take a step back."

"I won't pursue this further."

Evelyn studied this simple-minded giant.

She felt he lacked intelligence.

"...Did God close your intelligence window?"

Ethan was stunned. "What?"

Evelyn coughed. "Tsk. Simple mind."

"Strong body."

Ethan:!!

He understood!

His face flushed dark red. "You don't know what's good for you!"

"Don't blame me for being ruthless!"

He turned to the police.

He hesitated before speaking.

He looked back at Evelyn.

He wanted to give her one more chance.

He didn't hesitate to hit people.

But calling the police required caution.

He saw no fear in Evelyn's eyes.

Ethan grew angrier.

While he hesitated, Fiona spoke. "Yes, officer. It's Grace."

"She abused my son yesterday!"

The officer took out a pen and notepad. "How did she abuse him?"

Fiona cried and blamed everything on Evelyn.

"She bullied my son."

"She made him vomit."

"She even issued an expulsion notice."

"She's expelling us!"

"Can a kindergarten expel a child arbitrarily?"

The officer took notes.

He looked at Evelyn with a stern face. "Miss Ross. Is what she said true?"

Evelyn felt helpless. She hadn't expected things to escalate so much.

She spoke. "Officer, the child vomited due to a brain tumor."

"The expulsion notice was issued so he can focus on his treatment."

"This illness requires at least a year or two of rest..."

She left one thing unsaid.

This illness might not be curable.

Joey might not live to see next year...

Fiona screamed. "Officer, did you hear that? She cursed my son!!"

"She's insane. I can't accept this."

"Take her away. I will press charges!"

The two officers looked at Evelyn. "Miss Ross. Watch your words! No insults allowed!"

Evelyn: "...I wasn't cursing. I was telling the truth!"

The officers looked Joey up and down.

He was chubby and seemed healthy.

His face was a little pale...

Fiona spoke. "Officer, my son turned pale after she bullied him into vomiting."

The officers looked at Evelyn again. "Miss Ross. Have you seen his medical records?"

"What right do you have to issue an expulsion notice?"

"Can you randomly diagnose an illness?"

"Miss Ross. The parents say there's no illness."

"There's no evidence of any illness."

"We suspect you of child abuse."

As soon as he finished speaking, Joey covered his mouth.

He felt like vomiting again...
###Chapter 377

Evelyn was left speechless by this.
She simply waved a hand.
"He is genuinely ill…"

The police officer also frowned.
"Ms. Ross, please come with us."

The live stream chat went wild with celebratory messages.

She looked at the officer.
"Can we take the child to the hospital for an examination first? You can take me in after the results come out."

The officer looked at Fiona.

Fiona sneered.
"A CT scan has radiation. Why should I listen to you?"

"Evelyn, stop making a scene. Go with the police for questioning."

He turned to the officer.
"We do not accept mediation! She abused a child and is now slandering us. We will pursue this to the fullest extent!"

The officer nodded.
"Understood."

Evelyn's heart sank.
She realized she had been too impulsive.

If Fiona absolutely refused the examination, and Joey's brain tumor couldn't be confirmed, the charge of child abuse would be solid.

She had only wanted to warn them for early detection and treatment.
But she had backed herself into a corner.

She couldn't actually wait for Joey to get sick, could she?
What if it happened a month later?
Was she supposed to sit in jail for a month?

Evelyn's gaze swept over Joey.
She saw his pale face and rapid breathing.
She suddenly turned and scooped the child up.

"Joey!"

"What are you doing?!"

"Let him go!"

Evelyn calmly pulled out a pen and held it to Joey's neck.
"Don't come any closer!"

The officer spoke sternly.
"Ms. Ross, what do you think you're doing?"

Ethan reached out.
"Let the child go. We can talk this through!"

Fiona's legs went weak, barely able to support her.
She shrieked.
"Take it out on me! Don't touch my son!"

Evelyn looked directly at them.
"I'll let him go on one condition. Take him for a CT scan. Now."

Fiona was stunned.

The officer and Ethan wore expressions of utter disbelief.
"Are you insane? You're still hung up on that now?"

Evelyn's wrist moved slightly.
The pen tip pressed into the skin.

Joey burst into terrified sobs.

Fiona hurriedly cried out.
"We'll do it! We'll do the CT scan!"

Evelyn looked at the police.
"Prepare a car. I won't hurt him."

The live stream exploded.

--Is this teacher crazy?!

--A final, desperate struggle? Taking someone down with her?

--This is incomprehensible!

--Officer, shoot her!

...

The police car sped towards the nearest hospital.

Fiona and Ethan followed closely behind.
The teacher running the live stream also followed.

The incident rapidly gained traction, hitting the trending topics.

The hospital activated a green channel.
Evelyn, still holding Joey, walked into the CT room.

Fiona's legs were so weak she could hardly walk.
Penelope arrived just in time to support her.
"Sis, calm down! Uncle and big brother are on their way. Joey will be fine!"

Fiona finally calmed down slightly.

The crowd surged into the CT room.
The police officer said to Evelyn, "We're here. Please release the child."

Evelyn scoffed.
"I'm going in with him."

If she didn't personally ensure the CT was completed, Fiona would undoubtedly take the child and leave immediately.

The officer was speechless.
In all his years on the force, he'd never encountered a demand like this!

A doctor brought protective gear.
Evelyn put on the heavy suit and walked into the CT room with Joey.

The machine started.
The head scan was completed.

Evelyn let go.

Two officers instantly rushed in and subdued her!

Fiona lunged forward like a madwoman.
"You vicious witch! Making my son suffer radiation for nothing! I will not let this go! Officer, arrest her! She must be severely punished!"

The live stream chat was furious.

--Scum of society! Doesn't deserve to be a teacher!

--So what if she's a school director? Fire her!

...

Evelyn allowed the police to cuff her hands.
She said calmly, "The CT is done. Can we see the results now?"

The officer was taken aback.

Fiona screamed.
"See what results? My son is definitely fine! You can rot in jail for all I care!"

As the words left her mouth, a doctor rushed out of the CT room…
###Chapter 378

The hospital corridor was thick with tension.

Police were handling the emergency of a child's abduction.

A doctor suddenly rushed into the restricted area.

An officer immediately stopped him. "Doctor, step back, please!"

Evelyn Ross wore handcuffs and couldn't resist.

But her swift action in securing the child had put the police on high alert.

The doctor held up a CT scan film. "The results are in!"

Fiona Valentine glared viciously at Evelyn. "Grace, you exposed my son to radiation! I won't let this slide!"

The live-streaming teacher's phone camera was pointed directly at them.

The comments section exploded with hateful messages.

They all condemned Evelyn, saying she was unfit to be a teacher.

Little Joey suddenly went limp and collapsed to the floor.

Fiona held him but lost her grip.

The child fell hard.

"Joey! What's wrong?!"

Fiona frantically knelt, shaking her son.

She looked up, furious. "This is your doing!"

Evelyn was held firmly by the police, her arms twisted painfully behind her back.

She was beyond frustrated.

If she had known it would come to this, she would never have gotten involved.

But now she had to set the record straight. "The police were watching my every move. What could I possibly have done?"

Fiona shrieked, "You scared him!"

Ethan Valentine also raged, "Grace, the Valentine family will not forgive you!"

Evelyn said coldly, "Let's hear what the doctor has to say first."

Everyone turned to the doctor.

The doctor wiped his sweat. "Who are the parents?"

Fiona hurriedly responded, "I am! What's wrong with my son?"

The doctor handed her the CT scan. "There's a tumor in his brain."

Fiona's eyes widened in disbelief. "Impossible!"

The doctor pointed to a shadow on the film. "The tumor is pressing on a nerve. It's growing rapidly. Any later would have been too late!"

He immediately called for the nurses. "Get him to emergency, now!"

Nurses rushed in with a gurney and took Joey away.

Fiona was stunned, numbly following the gurney out.

The CT room fell silent.

The live-streaming teacher was speechless.

But the online comments went wild.

"???"

"A plot twist?!"

"The school director spotted a brain tumor just by looking?"

"This kindergarten is incredibly responsible!"

"Sunflower International Kindergarten? Just looked up the tuition. Never mind."

"You get what you pay for. It's worth it!"

...

The public opinion had completely reversed.

Everyone in the CT room was still processing what happened.

Evelyn moved her shoulders. "Officer, can you release me now?"

The police quickly let go. "Our apologies. We misunderstood the situation."

"It's not your fault."

Evelyn rubbed her sore arms.

She couldn't blame the police.

Without medical proof, her attempt to remove the child from school was against protocol.

She had assumed any parent would be concerned about their child's health.

A simple mention, and Fiona would have taken her son for a check-up...

She never expected it to escalate like this.

The police offered a formal apology.

They clarified the situation on the live stream before leaving.

Evelyn turned to the live-streaming teacher. "Unauthorized live streaming violates school policy. You're fired."
