She noticed Ethan Valentine standing to the side, frowning at her.

Ethan cleared his throat. "Thanks."

Evelyn scoffed. "Don't mention it."

She walked out coldly.

She had no patience for a brother who enabled someone like Fiona.

Ethan snorted.

If she didn't want his gratitude, fine.

Who needed it anyway?

If it weren't for the bad blood between Grace and his sister, he might suspect she did it for revenge.

Ethan had always wanted a sister.

Surrounded by annoying brothers, he doted on Penelope and Fiona after they were adopted.

But it always felt like there was a distance.

After all, they weren't blood-related...

Ethan sighed in disappointment and walked out.

He arrived home and ran into Marcus Valentine heading out.

Remembering the incident that had trended online, Marcus must have heard about it.

Ethan coughed awkwardly. "Big brother, I accidentally offended your wife's friend. I apologize..."

Marcus just smiled. "No need to apologize to me. You didn't offend your sister-in-law's friend. You'd better go see Uncle and accept your punishment."
###Chapter 379

Ethan Valentine was utterly confused.

He didn't understand the meaning of that statement at all.

Why would he need to seek punishment from the Third Uncle for offending Grace Nan?

He looked at Marcus Valentine with a furrowed brow, perplexed. "Does Grace Nan and the Third Uncle... have some special relationship?"

"Yes."

"What kind of relationship?"

"Go ask the Third Uncle."

Marcus dropped that line and turned to leave. "I have matters to attend to. I'm leaving."

"Big brother, what matters could you possibly have?"

Ethan pressed.

Marcus didn't stop walking.

What matters could he have?

Of course, he was going to pick Mina Sherwood up and bring her home!

He hadn't looked for her last night, and she actually stayed out the entire night. How could the future matriarch of the Valentine family not return home overnight?

Marcus soon arrived at the Nan residence.

After a servant opened the door, he saw Mina patiently feeding Chloe Davis her supplemental food.

She held a small spoon, carefully scraping apple puree, and gently feeding it into Chloe's mouth.

A gentle smile was on her face, her eyes full of affection.

...She really liked children.

This realization instantly irritated Marcus.

He couldn't give her a child...

Marcus's face darkened, and he strode over to her. "Mina, you seem quite comfortable here?"

Mina was startled by the sudden voice.

She turned and saw Marcus, immediately standing up. "Why are you here?"

Marcus sneered. "To see how my wife is enjoying herself outside!"

Mina lowered her eyes and remained silent.

This attitude again...

Marcus felt a surge of inexplicable anger. He grabbed her chin, forcing her to look up. "Mina, do you find it ridiculous that I can't give you a child?"

Mina endured the pain. "I've never thought that."

"Then why are you using this bastard to humiliate me?"

Marcus's eyes reddened. "I've already told you the truth, yet you insist the child is mine... I accepted it, so who are you putting on this pitiful act for?"

He was utterly frustrated!

He knew it was the Sherwood family's scheme, yet he still stepped forward and claimed the child.

And the result?

Mina showed no gratitude whatsoever. Instead, she became more distant and cold, even proposing divorce on their wedding night!

Was he really that easy to bully?

Mina looked at him. "Marcus, did you come here just to say these things?"

Marcus paused, stubbornly retorting, "Of course not! If the Third Uncle hadn't told me to pick you up, do you think I would have come?"

He released her and pushed her away.

He took out a handkerchief and meticulously wiped his hands, as if he had touched something dirty.

Every movement pierced Mina's heart...

He despised her.

He believed she was a woman who had been with countless men, hence this disgust...

Mina's face turned pale. She smiled bitterly. "I stayed outside because I thought you didn't want to see me..."

"I indeed don't want to see you," Marcus stared at her, using the most vicious words to attack her, alleviating his own heartache. "But newlyweds living apart will affect the Valentine Group's stock price. As the future head of the Valentine family, my every word and action represents the family. If you're photographed staying here by reporters, what will they write?"

Mina was taken aback.

Marcus continued, "Will you obediently return to the Valentine residence yourself, or get an immediate divorce?"

An immediate divorce was impossible for Mina!

Grace Nan still needed her to be married to find her mother...

Mina clenched her fingers, enduring the humiliation. She closed her eyes and took a deep breath. "Fine, I'll return tonight."

Marcus stared at her for a long time, snorted coldly, and turned to leave.

As he reached the door, he bumped into Evelyn Ross and Alexander Vance returning.

Evelyn looked at him coldly. Alexander stood quietly behind her, appearing obedient...

Marcus felt somewhat awkward.

He didn't speak, preparing to walk past Evelyn.

Evelyn suddenly spoke. "You know about what happened at the preschool today, right?"

"Yes." Marcus thought she was referring to Fiona Valentine. "Don't worry, the Third Uncle knows. He will punish Fiona and Ethan, giving you an explanation."

He paused. "Ethan will be punished more severely. Fiona... will be cast out. The Valentine family has a real cherished daughter now..."

Evelyn showed no reaction, still looking at him. "That's not what I meant. I'm saying some people clearly have an illness but claim they don't. And some people..."

She looked Marcus up and down, then lowered her eyes. "Some people clearly have no illness but insist they do!"

Marcus: ??

He frowned, his expression unpleasant.

Evelyn said slowly, "I've studied patients with oligospermia and azoospermia. Their performance in bed is usually unsatisfactory. Some even need medication. But from what I've heard from Mina, you seem... adequate?"

Marcus: ?!

His face instantly flushed red. "What nonsense are you spouting! Alexander, aren't you going to control her?"

Alexander: "So you're not adequate?"

Marcus: ???

Furious, he flicked his sleeve and left!

Marcus got into the car fuming. After settling in, he gradually calmed down, the shame and anger fading from his face.

Oligospermia and azoospermia... would cause inadequacy?

But those two times with Mina, he was more than adequate!

So...

Marcus frowned and said to the driver, "To the hospital."

The driver asked, "Which hospital, Young Master Valentine?"

After a moment's thought, Marcus avoided their family hospital and chose an international hospital.

The driver immediately set off.

Marcus secretly entered the hospital under an assumed name. After providing a sample, he personally handed it to the testing doctor.

This time, he would monitor the entire process!

Ensuring the results would absolutely not be wrong!
###Chapter 380

Marcus Valentine was a man of significant status and immense wealth.

He oversaw the entire testing process personally.

From the sample collection to its delivery to the lab, he never left its side.

Even though he didn't understand the medical procedures, he ensured no one could switch the samples.

He watched every step, preventing any possibility of tampering.

The final results were uploaded to a computer.

During the test, a doctor had offhandedly remarked on the excellent viscosity of his sample.

That comment had rekindled a spark of hope in Marcus.

Maybe there was nothing wrong with him after all?

He sat in the room, waiting for the final verdict.

As the machine data streamed onto the computer screen, he immediately leaned in.

"Doctor, what's the result?"

The doctor stared at the screen, his face suddenly paling.

Marcus's fingers tightened.

The doctor double-checked the results, then looked at him.

"The test indicates... azoospermia."

Marcus's brow furrowed deeply.

A profound sense of disappointment washed over him.

If not for the doctor's earlier teasing, if not for Evelyn Ross's repeated insistence that he was fine…

He wouldn't have allowed himself to hope.

Now, even though the result was expected, it was still bitterly hard to accept.

His fists clenched, making the doctor turn pale with fear.

"M-Mr. Valentine..."

Marcus fixed him with a stare. "You know what can be said, and what must remain unsaid. Understood?"

The doctor swallowed hard and nodded repeatedly.

Marcus turned and left.

The doctor wiped cold sweat from his forehead, his face ashen.

An assistant muttered under his breath, "The result can't be right, can it? Mr. Valentine seems so... virile..."

"Silence!"

The doctor snapped sharply.

The assistant froze.

The doctor lowered his voice to a harsh whisper. "What happened today stays buried. You tell no one, no matter who asks!"

The assistant hastily agreed.

The doctor stared at the computer screen, taking a deep, shaky breath.

Marcus Valentine's information was already registered in the system of a mysterious medical organization.

No matter where he went for testing, the result would only ever be azoospermia.

What was that organization trying to achieve?

The doctor didn't dare dwell on it, merely muttering to himself, "I know nothing..."

…

The testing took several hours.

By the time Marcus left the hospital, dusk had fallen.

Sitting in his car, he let out a bitter laugh.

Another exercise in self-humiliation.

He scoffed derisively and looked out the window.

The car drove back to the Valentine estate.

Marcus went straight to his study, opened a bottle of liquor, and took a long swig.

The sound of a car came from outside the window.

Mina Sherwood was home.

She walked slowly upstairs, pausing for a moment outside the bedroom door.

She pushed it open. Not seeing Marcus, she relaxed slightly.

But before she could even catch her breath, a pair of hands grabbed her tightly from behind.

The strong smell of alcohol assaulted her.

Mina's entire body went rigid.

Marcus roughly threw her over his shoulder, dumped her on the bed, and began tearing at her clothes.

"Marcus! What are you doing? Let me go!"

Marcus buried his face in her neck, kissing her roughly, his large hands ripping the fabric.

"Marcus!"

Mina panicked. "No! You'll hurt the baby!"

The baby...

Marcus paused, a deeper scorn flashing in his eyes.

"Someone else's bastard. Better off gone!"

He continued relentlessly.

In her慌乱, Mina slapped him across the face.

Smack!

The sting brought Marcus a moment of clarity, but he still glared at her fiercely. "Mina, don't push me too far!"

Tears streamed from the corners of Mina's eyes.

"Please, don't hurt him... I can... I can do something else..."

Marcus's fist clenched and smashed into the mattress beside her ear.

Mina turned her head away.

Marcus abruptly stood up. "Mina, remember this. You owe me! For these ten months of marriage, you will always owe me!"

…

Light spilled from the master bedroom window.

The silhouettes of two entangled figures were cast upon the sheer curtains.

Ethan Valentine, kneeling in the family shrine, caught a glimpse of the scene and curled his lip.

Big brother and his wife sure had stamina.

He shifted his sore knees and let out a disgruntled snort.

The next morning.

The door to the shrine creaked open.

Ethan, who had knelt all night, turned his head and saw Third Uncle enter.

Ethan had idolized Third Uncle since childhood, but now he deliberately looked away.

"Do you understand your mistake now?"

"No."

Ethan stubbornly held his neck straight. "The family motto says we don't let our own be bullied. You're being unfair, Uncle! Picking on me for some outsider, even throwing Fiona out! I won't accept it! Fiona might be adopted, but I've always treated her like a real sister!"

Third Uncle glared. "What outsider? Do you even know who Grace Nan is?!"
###Chapter 381

Ethan Valentine scoffed. "Who else could it be?"

He suddenly remembered Marcus's words last night.
Marcus had said he didn't need to apologize to his sister-in-law.
He just needed to beg forgiveness from Third Uncle.

Yesterday, he went to see Third Uncle.
He wasn't even allowed inside before being punished to kneel at the Family Shrine.

Could Grace really have a connection to Third Uncle?
Third Uncle had even expelled his adopted daughter for her.
Now he was saying she wasn't an outsider...

Ethan's eyes suddenly lit up.
He looked cautiously at Third Uncle. "Is she related to you?"

Third Uncle nodded.

Ethan instantly understood. "So that's it! Congratulations, Third Uncle!"

He was finally getting a Third Aunt!
Though Grace was young enough to be his daughter.
And she was divorced with a child.
But Third Uncle had been single his whole life.
It was good he had someone to keep him company in his later years.

Third Uncle thought he understood.
He personally helped Ethan up. "Be good to her from now on."

Ethan nodded repeatedly. "Don't worry, Third Uncle. I'll be very courteous!"

Third Uncle: "No need for too much courtesy. Just be closer. After I'm gone, you brothers must take care of her."

Ethan felt a pang of sadness. "Third Uncle, you will live a long life! I will be filial to you and her!"

Third Uncle: ?

Being filial to him was enough.
What did filial piety to a cousin mean?

But he didn't say more.
Ethan had always been simple-minded.

He patted Ethan's shoulder. "Go on."

Ethan nodded and left.

Walking away, he glanced back.
He saw Third Uncle standing alone in the Family Shrine, murmuring.
"In six days, we'll hold a banquet to announce her identity."
"Mom, Dad, I'm bringing her back for you to see..."

Ethan's eyes reddened.
Grandmother had worried most about Third Uncle's lifelong bachelorhood on her deathbed.

So the banquet in six days was Third Uncle's wedding!
Third Uncle was being too low-key.

He needed to prepare a meeting gift for Third Aunt!
What should he give?

Ethan thought it over. "I'll ask my master!"

He was a disciple of the Cortez family, one of the Five Great Families.
The Cortez family didn't engage in business.
They specialized in martial arts.

The Cortez School encompassed the top martial artists of the Chinese martial world.
Many descendants of the Five Great Families studied martial arts here.

Ethan was a martial arts prodigy.
He had been favored by the Cortez direct line since childhood.
He was an outstanding disciple within the school.

His master was the younger brother of the Cortez School's leader, Second Uncle-Master.
He was a direct-line closed-door disciple of the Cortez School.
His status was revered.

...

Outside the Cortez School.

Evelyn Ross stood quietly.
The Cortez School accepted disciples from a young age.
Martial arts training required building a foundation.

She hadn't come to seek a master.
She had her own martial arts instructor.

But the mysterious "Nan Family" member demanded cooperation with the Five Great Families before revealing the next step of the plan.

Only the Cortez and Zhao families remained now.
She decided to approach the Cortez family first.

The Cortez School had many disciples.
They must have sources of income.
If all else failed, hiring people for protection could count as cooperation, right?

Evelyn stepped forward and knocked on the iron gate.

The Cortez family was located in a remote part of New York.
The ancient iron gate exuded a deep sense of history.

She knocked on a small side door.

A man in training clothes opened the door.
He was startled to see her. "Young lady, who are you looking for?"

Evelyn thought for a moment. "I'm looking for business affairs."

The man looked her over. "What business?"

Evelyn said sincerely, "I'd like to hire a few security personnel..."

The man asked, "Which family are you from?"

Evelyn paused. "The Nan family."

"I've never heard of that family! How many people? How big is your residence?"

Evelyn: "...A three-story small villa."

"Then why are you here?"

The man had spoken more because Evelyn looked乖巧 and pretty.
Hearing "small villa," he became annoyed.
"The Cortez School is not a security company!"
"To hire bodyguards from us, you need to be at the level of the British royal family or the Five Great Families!"
"Are you joking with me?"

Evelyn immediately said, "The price is negotiable..."

"We don't take odd jobs!"

The man snorted and pushed her.
"Get lost! Little girl, don't come here to waste our time..."
"We're busy training and meditating. No time for nonsense!"

He started to close the door.

The roar of a sports car came from afar.

The man craned his neck to look.
A blue Maserati sped towards them.

The man's eyes lit up. "Second Senior Brother is here! Move aside!"

He pushed Evelyn away.
The car hadn't even arrived yet, but he was already opening the large iron gate, excitedly waving.

The car didn't stop, driving straight in!

The man still grinned and waved. "Hello, Second Senior Brother!"

Evelyn: "..."

Too obsequious?

The man saw her expression and said proudly, "What do you know?"
"This is Second Senior Brother!"
"The most powerful of the younger generation!"
"Second Uncle-Master's closed-door disciple!"
"A young master of the Valentine family!"

As soon as he finished speaking, the sports car reversed and stopped at the gate.

Ethan Valentine jumped out of the car.
He walked respectfully up to Evelyn. "Third Aunt?!"
###Chapter 382

Evelyn Ross took a step back.

She looked at Ethan Valentine. "What did you just call me?"

Ethan paused.

He remembered his uncle's instructions.

He cleared his throat. "I know about your relationship with my uncle. If you don't want me calling you that in public, I understand."

Evelyn was confused.

Before she could process it, Ethan turned to the gatekeeper. "This is my elder... I mean, my friend. Treat her with respect from now on."

The gatekeeper immediately clasped his fists. "Understood, Second Senior Brother!"

He bowed to Evelyn. "My apologies, miss. If you had mentioned knowing the Second Senior Brother earlier, I would have let you in. But the Cortez Sect truly doesn't take on freelance work."

Ethan looked at Evelyn. "Freelance work?"

Evelyn coughed lightly. "I wanted to collaborate with the Cortez Sect."

"Collaborate?"

Ethan was stunned.

The gatekeeper laughed. "Miss, at best, that would be hiring. Out of respect for the Second Senior Brother, we could assign a few men to protect you. That isn't collaboration."

Evelyn pressed further. "What counts as collaboration then?"

Ethan explained. "The Cortez Sect doesn't collaborate externally. Unless you join the Cortez Sect and become an Inner Disciple. Only then are you considered one of us."

Evelyn was speechless.

Is this how martial arts families collaborate?

She thought it was about business partnerships.

She thought for a moment and asked, "How does one join the Cortez Sect? To become an Inner Disciple?"

Ethan was exasperated.

He lifted his chin proudly. "Becoming an Inner Disciple is very difficult. It primarily depends on innate talent. Children are brought here around three or four years old for bone assessment. Only those with exceptional bone structure are accepted."

Ethan patted his chest. "Out of the seven Valentine brothers, only I was chosen!"

Evelyn thought... Who's 'us' with you?

But...

Does the Cortez Sect only accept children?

She couldn't exactly turn back into a three-year-old.

She thought of Chloe.

But no.

Chloe's bones are too soft. She's better suited for dancing than martial arts.

Evelyn frowned. "Has anyone older ever been accepted?"

"Of course!"

Ethan replied. "The First Senior Sister was recognized by the Sect Leader when she was fifteen!"

The gatekeeper added excitedly, "Yes! The Sect Leader said the First Senior Sister had extraordinary bone structure. Especially resilient. She was trained hard since childhood. Built like a fortress. Unfortunately, the Sect Leader trained her in secret. None of us have ever seen her."

Ethan looked wistful. "The Sect Leader said the First Senior Sister would return to the Cortez Sect to guide us when she turns twenty-three. It should be happening soon..."

The gatekeeper said eagerly, "Second Senior Brother, you're undefeated in the Cortez Sect. When the First Senior Sister arrives, you should spar with her!"

Ethan smacked his head. "Nonsense! How could I compare to the First Senior Sister? The Sect Leader said she completed her training at eighteen. She narrowly beat the Sect Leader by half a move at twenty. I can't even defeat the Sect Leader yet. Don't set me up for failure."

The gatekeeper chuckled.

Evelyn interrupted them. "My bones are quite good too. I've been resilient since childhood. Can you take me to see the Sect Leader?"

The gatekeeper and Ethan fell silent simultaneously.

They both looked at Evelyn with strange expressions.

The gatekeeper glanced at Ethan. "Not to discourage you, but you're over twenty, right? Your bones have hardened. Without a foundation, it's impossible. The First Senior Sister could train because she grew up under harsh conditions. Her body didn't develop normally..."

Evelyn explained, "I started martial arts training at fifteen. Maybe it's possible?"

She remembered those years.

She left the Ashcroft family at fourteen. Sold balloons in the park while studying.

A middle-aged man had watched her for a long time.

He eventually approached and said, "Young lady, you have exceptional bone structure. Become my disciple?"

Evelyn almost called the police.

Only after he agreed to pay her five hundred dollars daily for one hour of training did she consent.

She trained for four or five years. Her master said he was going traveling. Told her to practice on her own. Would find her when the time was right.

Two years had passed. She didn't even know his name.

Evelyn looked at Ethan. "Take me to see him. Whether it's possible or not, we'll know after the assessment."

Ethan wanted to refuse.

But he remembered his uncle's instructions. To be filial to his aunt.

He twitched his mouth. "Fine. Follow me."

Ethan tossed his car keys to the gatekeeper. Led Evelyn inside.

The Cortez Sect was vast. Everywhere, people practiced martial arts. Grunts and shouts filled the air. The atmosphere was intensely masculine.

After walking for half an hour, they reached a simple house.

Ethan said, "The Sect Leader is meditating inside. I'll call him."

Inside the room.

Cortez Sect Leader Joseph Cortez was weeping bitterly. He looked at a photo on his phone with his disciple Evelyn Ross. Tears streamed down his face.

He had only ever taken this one disciple in his life. How could she have drowned so young?!

Ethan's voice came from outside. "Sect Leader, I've brought someone to see you. Is it convenient?"
###Chapter 383

Evelyn Ross was about to turn twenty-three.

Joseph Cortez had already sent people to San Francisco to bring her back to New York.
He wanted everyone to meet the legendary First Senior Sister.

He was particularly fond of this disciple.

Joseph Cortez had always been meticulous.
Since becoming the sect leader, he had been extremely cautious about accepting disciples.
It concerned the succession of the next Cortez Sect leader.

He personally examined the bone structures of all the children in his family, his own son, and every child in New York who came seeking martial arts training.
Yet, not a single one met his standards.

Years passed like this.

He adhered to the principle of quality over quantity.
He refused to accept a disciple easily.
As a result, Ethan Valentine, the eldest disciple of his second junior brother, became the popular candidate.

Everyone speculated that Ethan Valentine might marry into the Cortez family.
He would inherit the Cortez Sect and become the new leader.

Joseph Cortez had also considered this idea.

But it was a last resort.
If he had a choice, he would find the best candidate in the world.

He began traveling across the country to find the most suitable successor.
Seven years ago, he arrived in San Francisco.

That day in the park, he was observing the children running and playing.
But his attention was drawn to a little girl selling balloons.

The girl seemed to have just finished school.
She arrived late and had to sell balloons from a corner.

Other vendors bullied her.
They forced her to sit down, complaining that she was blocking the light.

The girl didn't have a stool.
She simply assumed a horse stance and stood there.

Joseph Cortez found it amusing.

Any martial artist knew this stance was extremely taxing.
He wanted to see how long the girl could hold it.
An hour passed... then two hours...

The girl maintained that half-squat for three and a half hours!

Even more astonishing, she showed no signs of strain.

Joseph Cortez immediately examined her bone structure.
The moment he did, he was captivated by Evelyn Ross.
It was like a starving wolf seeing meat.

That led to him pestering her later.
He insisted on teaching her martial arts and forcing her to call him master.

It was only later that he learned this girl had a formidable mother.
She had been beaten and scolded since childhood.
Her resilience and strong physique were forged through hardship.

Even though the child was fifteen, long-term malnutrition made her physical age only about ten.
This coincidentally meant she hadn't missed the best period for martial arts training.

An ordinary child couldn't have grown up in such extreme conditions.
Her neglectful mother had, unintentionally, done one good thing.

Joseph Cortez, delighted by her talent, wanted her to drop out of school and follow him.
But Evelyn Ross insisted on continuing her education.
Joseph Cortez had no choice but to stay in San Francisco for her.

At first, she practiced for one hour a day.
Then it became two hours.
In the end, Evelyn Ross actually defeated him!

Joseph Cortez had nothing left to teach.
He decided to leave.
He told her he would bring her back to New York after she graduated from university, when she turned twenty-three.

How could the Cortez Sect's First Senior Sister remain outside?

After returning to the Cortez Sect, he mentioned this to his second junior brother and the disciples.
From then on, everyone eagerly awaited the First Senior Sister's return at twenty-three!

Finally, this day arrived.
Joseph Cortez was tied up with sect affairs and couldn't go personally.
He sent people to fetch her.
But the people he sent returned and told him that First Senior Sister Evelyn Ross, also Mrs. Vance, had drowned!!

Joseph Cortez couldn't believe it.
He went to San Francisco himself.
He indeed saw her tombstone and the photo on it.

It was his beloved disciple!

Joseph Cortez was devastated.
He returned to New York deeply distressed.
He shut himself in his room, depressed.

Now, the entire Cortez Sect still didn't know about the First Senior Sister's accident...

He didn't dare imagine the grief that would sweep through the Cortez Sect if this news got out!

He looked at the photo of him and Evelyn Ross on his phone.
The girl in the picture was aloof and stunning.
Her voice and smile felt so vivid, making his eyes redden.

His good disciple probably never knew, even in death, that she was the First Senior Sister of Cortez Sect, the largest martial arts sect in the country.
She never knew how important she was to the Cortez Sect!

Thinking of this, Joseph Cortez clutched his chest again.
###Chapter 384

Tears welled up in his red, crying eyes.

So when he heard Ethan Valentine's words, he panicked. "Not convenient!"

He was always the authoritative sect leader to the outside world.
How could he see anyone crying like this?!

Ethan Valentine paused.

He seemed to notice the unusual tone. "Sect Leader, are you unwell?"

Unwell? He was heartbroken!
Who could have imagined the future of the Cortez Sect, his beloved disciple, would drown!

Didn't she know how to swim?

Joseph Cortez's eyes welled with tears. "I'm fine."

Ethan Valentine: ??

Outside the door.

Ethan Valentine and Evelyn Ross exchanged glances.
Both were a bit confused.

But...

Why did the voice inside sound somewhat familiar?

But her master always loved to laugh.
The voice inside sounded tearful, making it hard for her to tell.

Ethan Valentine thought since Grace Nan could diagnose Joey Valentine's brain tumor, her medical skills must be exceptional.

What's wrong with his throat today? I heard there's a virus going around that specifically affects the throat. Sect Leader, did you catch it? You can't delay; you need treatment promptly!"

Joseph Cortez: "..." This annoying brat is so bothersome!

He quickly grabbed a tissue to dry his tears.
He tried to sound authoritative. "I'm fine. Leave! It's not convenient to see guests today!"

Ethan Valentine wasn't willing to leave just yet.

What if Grace Nan complained to Third Uncle that he handled things poorly?

He coughed lightly. "Sect Leader, what's not convenient? Your throat is like this; you must be unwell! I'm coming in now~"

With that, he pushed the door open directly.

He then winked at Evelyn Ross and led her inside boldly!

"Sect Leader, my friend has excellent medical skills. Let her take a look at you? And, could you also do my friend a small favor…"

Joseph Cortez: ??
Evelyn followed Ethan Valentine, thinking his actions were too reckless.

The Grandmaster had clearly stated he couldn't see visitors. She could have waited patiently.

But since the door was open, she didn't want to miss this chance.

Her master once said she had great potential for martial arts.

She followed Ethan into the room, her gaze immediately going to the Grandmaster.

Then she heard a faint sound.

When she looked, the Grandmaster had already turned his back, clearly unwilling to face the newcomers.

Ethan scanned the room and suddenly sensed something was off.

Many tissues were scattered on the floor.

Coupled with the Grandmaster's hoarse voice earlier...

"Grandmaster, were you crying?"

The blunt Ethan asked without any filter.

Joseph Cortez: "..."

This wretched disciple!

He must tell his second brother to discipline him properly!

Joseph Cortez took a deep breath. "Get out!"

Ethan finally felt the Grandmaster's anger. Not daring to delay, he quickly backed away with Evelyn. "Yes, Grandmaster. We're leaving. We won't disturb you. Please continue... Do you need me to bring more tissues?"

His answer was a shoe flying straight at his face!

Ethan instinctively wanted to dodge, but Evelyn was standing behind him.

He turned his body hard, and the shoe hit his rear with a solid thud!

Another shoe followed right after, hitting the door and slamming it shut.

Ethan rubbed his sore backside, looking awkwardly at Evelyn. "Miss Nan, the Grandmaster is in a bad mood today. Perhaps we should come another time?"

But Evelyn was looking elsewhere. She suddenly suggested, "Could you introduce me to someone else then? Or maybe I can just apprentice under you? Would that count as being a core disciple?"

"No, absolutely not!"

Ethan waved his hands repeatedly. "That would mess up the seniority!"

This was his future aunt. How could she become his disciple?

Evelyn thought about it. It was true.

They were cousins. A master-disciple relationship would indeed confuse their family ranks.

Besides...

Ethan cleared his throat slightly, lifting his chin a little. "I don't just take any disciple. The Cortez Sect's core disciples have very strict requirements..."

Even with the First Senior Sister now, if she didn't marry into the Cortez family, the future Grandmaster position would still be his.

As the Cortez family heir, taking a disciple concerned the next Grandmaster candidate. So the Cortez Sect had its own strict standards for accepting disciples.

Evelyn asked, "How strict?"

Ethan explained, "First, all prospective disciples must go to the Bone Assessment Hall for an evaluation. Then they train for a month. Only those who persevere get a chance to be noticed by the core disciples. Then the core members choose their disciples. That's how I was selected back then!"

Evelyn pressed, "Do older applicants also get the bone assessment?"

Ethan nodded. "Of course. No exceptions. The bone assessment is quite mystical. It can determine if someone is suited for martial arts, if they have talent, and even their current level of training..."

Evelyn decided immediately. "Take me to the Bone Assessment Hall."

Ethan was stunned. "Wait, it's full of little kids right now. Are you sure you want to go?"

Evelyn's tone was firm. "Yes."

Her master said her foundation was absolutely solid.

Perhaps the Cortez Sect would make an exception once they saw her talent?

Seeing her determination, Ethan could only rub his nose. "Well... alright."

Half an hour later.

A peculiar scene unfolded at the Bone Assessment Hall.

Evelyn stood in a line of four- and five-year-old children, looking extremely out of place.

The children in front and behind her giggled as they looked at her.

People around cast curious glances.

Someone teased,

"Miss, isn't it awkward standing among children?"

Another laughed and said to Ethan,

"Second Senior Brother, who is this? She's so old, her bone sutures are fully closed. No future there! Quickly take her away. Don't make a spectacle here!"

Ethan smiled awkwardly, sighing inwardly.

If this were his own sister, he would have dragged her away long ago.

But this was the person his uncle favored. His future aunt!

He had to show some respect to his aunt.

He cleared his throat and responded, "What's the big deal? The First Senior Sister was only discovered by the Grandmaster when she was fifteen. Maybe my friend here has good bone structure?"

"The First Senior Sister is a one-in-a-million genius. How many like her can there be in the world?"

"Exactly. The Grandmaster traveled for nearly ten years before finding the First Senior Sister. And the Grandmaster said her exceptional achievements were due to her unique life circumstances. Did your friend have a very hard childhood too?"

Ethan: "..."

After learning Evelyn might become his aunt, he had specifically inquired about her.

This Evelyn came from a comfortable, happy family. She just married the wrong person later and suffered a bit in love.

He looked at Evelyn. "Maybe we should just leave? It's really... quite embarrassing."

Evelyn said calmly, "You can go. I'll stay by myself."

"How could I do that? If Uncle finds out, he'll beat me up!"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow slightly. "Thank you, then."

"It's nothing. But after the bone assessment, you'll give up, right?"

Evelyn nodded. "Yes. If I still can't become an inner disciple after the assessment, I'll give up."

Ethan comforted her. "Don't be too disheartened. If you really want to join the Cortez Sect, I'll try to think of another way."

Evelyn knew this was just consolation.

If there were another way, Ethan would have suggested it already. Why let her embarrass herself here?

Although she found this experience novel, something she had never gone through before.

But looking at Ethan's expression, like he wanted to hide his face...

The queuing process was long and boring.

There was only one assessment master, but a constant stream of people came daily to seek apprenticeship.

Two hours passed before the child in front of her finally entered the room.

Evelyn breathed a slight sigh of relief.

It was almost her turn!

Five minutes later, the child ahead walked out dejectedly. Seeing their waiting parents, they burst into loud sobs.

Ethan felt his scalp prickle.

If his future aunt wasn't selected, or even got insulted by the assessment master, would she cry too?
###Chapter 385

"Next!"

A commanding voice came from within the room.

Evelyn Ross took a deep breath and pushed the door open.

The Cortez Sect retained an ancient architectural style.

The Bone Assessment Hall was a single-story building with gray bricks and tiles, its interior simple yet solemn.

An elder with white hair and beard sat on a meditation cushion.

He wore traditional training attire, his demeanor ethereal and detached.

Elder Xavier had presided over the Bone Assessment Hall for decades.

Countless gifted disciples had been discovered by him and recommended to the sect leader and core elders.

Since the sect leader took a disciple and announced the First Senior Sister surpassed even himself, Elder Xavier's burden had lightened.

Unless he encountered a true prodigy, disciples of ordinary aptitude could only remain as outer disciples.

He sipped his tea leisurely and glanced toward the door.

Seeing Evelyn, he paused. "Parents are not allowed inside."

He looked behind her. "Where is the child?"

Evelyn felt slightly awkward. "I am here for the bone assessment."

"Nonsense!"

Elder Xavier laughed in exasperation. "Your bones are already set. What is there to assess? I have never assessed an adult!"

Evelyn's expression was serious. "I wish to become a disciple."

Elder Xavier studied her carefully, then suddenly recognized her. "You are Grace Nan?"

Evelyn was startled. "You know me?"

"Of course!" Elder Xavier was both amused and annoyed. "An outer disciple of the Cortez Sect, personally approved by me. How could I not know?"

Evelyn frowned.

So her sister had already tried to join the Cortez Sect. The Nan family had indeed made arrangements long ago.

Elder Xavier recalled, "Your parents brought you here when you were four or five. I assessed your bones then. Your aptitude was average, so I refused. Yet you came back every year after that!"

He shook his head helplessly. "Your parents begged and pleaded, insisting you join the Cortez Sect. There are so many martial arts schools outside. Why were they so fixated on this one?"

The Cortez Sect was not an ordinary martial arts school.

It was one of the few orthodox ancient martial arts lineages left in the country.

In the age of firearms, ancient martial arts had long declined.

The Cortez Sect survived due to its profound heritage and top-tier martial status.

Joining the Cortez Sect meant dedicating one's life to martial arts. It was not a trivial matter.

Yet Grace Nan's parents were unusually persistent.

They offered gifts, blocked doors, even knelt for long periods, demanding their daughter pass the assessment.

Elder Xavier, forced into a corner, finally granted her outer disciple status.

After all, outer disciples were mostly merchants who handled the sect's operations.

Inner disciples could never be accepted without true merit.

Only then did Grace Nan's parents relent.

Elder Xavier looked at her. "After you became an outer disciple, your parents finally calmed down. But I heard you performed poorly among the outer disciples. You couldn't endure hardship. Last time, there were even rumors you wanted to quit. Why are you here again?"

Evelyn's gaze sharpened.

Her sister had a resilient personality. How could she not endure hardship?

There must be more to this.

Now was not the time to investigate. She met Elder Xavier's eyes directly. "I wish to become an inner disciple."

Elder Xavier sighed. "I know you are fixated on this, but bone structure is determined by heaven. Grace, go back. Just train with the outer disciples to strengthen your body. Don't waste your time."

Evelyn stepped forward. "I have trained diligently these past few years. I feel I have made significant progress. My skills have greatly improved. Please assess me once more!"

Elder Xavier's face darkened. "Aptitude cannot be changed through effort! You come every year. Do you think I don't know your limits? Leave quickly. There are children waiting behind you!"

Evelyn stood her ground. "The bone assessment has no age restrictions. I queued according to the rules. This is within sect regulations. Why won't you assess me? Right now, you are the one wasting time, not me."

Elder Xavier was furious. "Grace, you are being deliberately unreasonable! If you don't leave now, I will have you expelled. Your outer disciple status will be revoked too!"

Ethan Valentine heard the commotion and hurried in.

He tried to smooth things over. "Elder Xavier, please don't be angry. This is my friend…"

Elder Xavier's expression softened slightly, but he still snorted coldly. "Then take your friend and leave. Don't delay important matters!"

Seeing Evelyn showed no intention of backing down, Ethan could only smile apologetically. "Elder Xavier, since she's already here, could you please assess her? As a favor to me."

As the Second Senior Brother, Ethan might succeed the sect leader in the future.

This was a face Elder Xavier could not refuse.

He snorted reluctantly. "Fine. Make it quick."

Evelyn immediately stepped forward.

Elder Xavier placed his hand on her wrist.

His expression changed abruptly.
###Chapter 386

Elder Xavier's expression froze.
His brow furrowed slightly.

He had assessed countless bone structures, but Evelyn Ross's remained unforgettable.

Her joints had been weak and floppy.
Her bones were brittle and porous.
She simply wasn't built for martial arts.
Even as she grew older, there was no improvement.

Her parents had begged him many times, but he never relented.

With that bone structure, no matter how hard she trained, the martial path was closed to her.
Perhaps studying dance was a better option.

An adult's bone structure shouldn't change significantly.

But this time, as he touched her, he was utterly stunned.

Mina was taken aback. "Yes."
