She understood his intentions, but for her parents' sake, she reluctantly went.

As soon as she finished speaking, Ethan slammed his teacown onto the coffee table!

Crack!

Tea splattered everywhere.

Ethan glared at Mina angrily. "Sister-in-law, how can you be so shameless? How do you justify this to big brother?"

Mina looked utterly bewildered.

Penelope handed her a phone. "This is circulating in social circles today. It's full of detailed stories about you and Justin Evans. The details are quite explicit. Sister-in-law, you owe the Ashcroft family an explanation!"
###Chapter 392
Mina trembled as she took the phone Penelope handed her.

The screen was lit.

The document title was today’s date.

She opened the file.

Justin had been changed to Justin Evans.

Mina became Mina Sherwood.

Their relationship was described in the crudest language.

It was filled with words like "breasts" and "buttocks."

Mina’s vision darkened.

In the story, she was a high-end escort.

She only served the five major families of New York.

She seduced Justin Evans relentlessly.

She even followed him to his hotel room and begged.

All to secure a business deal between their families.

The details were too graphic to read.

Mina’s fingers shook.

She felt as if she had been stripped naked.

It was all fabricated.

Yet it felt terrifyingly real.

Even the hotel suite number for the meeting was accurate.

There were anonymous comments below the document.

Someone confirmed the Ye and Evans families had met there that day.

Mina and Justin were indeed alone in the suite.

Another claimed to have seen them leaving together in a car.

Mina’s breathing quickened.

She wanted to defend herself.

But no words came out.

She instinctively looked at Marcus.

Others followed her gaze.

Ethan spoke first.

"Marcus, your wife has done this. And it's public knowledge now. The Valentines must give an explanation to the world."

Charles immediately agreed.

"This can't be ignored. Mina, did you or did you not have an affair with Justin?"

Mina shook her head desperately.

"No."

Penelope asked gently.

"Then why would a hotel employee testify otherwise? Mina, if you calculate the timing... could this child be Justin’s?"

Ethan remembered the oligospermia report.

He glanced at Marcus.

His brother was gripping his teacup so tightly his knuckles were white.

Ethan stayed silent.

Charles turned to Marcus.

"Marcus, if this child really is Justin’s, it cannot be kept! How would the Valentines save face?"

Ethan nodded in agreement.

"Just arrange an accident. Make her miscarry. That preserves our reputation. People will forget in time. If the child is born, the truth will always be in question."

Penelope looked at her third brother in surprise.

She didn’t understand why he was being so extreme.

She looked at Marcus.

Her eldest brother’s face was cold.

He seemed like a different person.

Her heart sank.

Could the child really not be his?

She spoke with feigned concern.

"Stop pressuring Marcus and Mina. The child is innocent. It’s almost three months. We can do a DNA test after the birth."

Ethan immediately objected.

"No DNA test!"

That would ruin everything.

Everyone looked at him.

Marcus lifted his eyes.

Ethan cleared his throat.

"I mean, a test would only give people more to talk about. The priority is to terminate the pregnancy. The child’s origins are unclear."

Mina listened to them.

She felt cold all over.

She looked at each Valentine family member.

Finally, her gaze settled on Marcus’s face.

"I will not have an abortion."

Marcus’s expression was dark.

Even now.

She still protected the bastard.

Who was the man?

Was he worth this?

Marcus’s eyes reddened.

He let out a cold laugh.

"It’s not me asking you to abort. The Valentines need to give the world an explanation!"

Mina was stunned.

She clenched her jaw.

"The child is yours. We can do an amniocentesis at three months to prove it."

Marcus stood up abruptly.

"Do you want the Valentines to become the laughingstock of New York?"

He rushed to Mina.

He lowered his voice.

"You know who the father is. Why make me say it? Mina, terminate the pregnancy. You can remain a Valentine."

Tears welled in Mina’s eyes.

Her reputation wasn’t great.

But she never expected this humiliation.

She lowered her gaze.

"If the Valentines are ashamed, we can divorce. I won’t drag you down."

She turned to leave.

Marcus grabbed her wrist.

"You think you can just walk away? Let the Valentines be mocked by everyone?"

Mina struggled fiercely.

"What do you want me to do?"

Marcus stared at her.

His eyes fell on her abdomen.

This child was like a thorn.

Piercing his heart.

Every time Mina defended it.

He felt the father must mean everything to her.

Marcus smiled bitterly.

After today.

She would probably hate him.

But he couldn’t take it anymore.

Hatred was fine.

They would still spend their lives together.

He looked at a servant.

"Prepare the abortion pill."

The servant trembled in fear.

She hurried away.

Mina’s eyes widened in disbelief.

She clenched her fists.

"Marcus, don’t..."

"Mina, it will be over soon. Once it’s done, everything will be clean. I’ll treat you well from now on."

Marcus held her tightly.

He refused to let go.
###Chapter 393

Tears fell from Mina's eyes in large drops.
"No, Marcus, I beg you..."

"I won't soften my heart this time."

Marcus's voice was cold and hard.

He gripped Mina's wrist with one hand.
With his other hand, he gently stroked her hair.
"The child is gone, and the rumors will naturally die down."
"We will live a good life from now on..."

"Marcus! No!!"
Mina struggled desperately, her voice hoarse.
"I beg you! Don't do this!"

Marcus said nothing more.
He directly dragged her towards the guest bedroom.

In the living room, Ethan, Charles, and Penelope stood frozen.
All three looked in the direction they had left, their faces full of shock.

Penelope glanced at Ethan and coughed lightly.
"Third Brother, is the child really not Big Brother's?"

Ethan looked troubled, hesitating to speak.
He had never been good at hiding his thoughts.

Penelope understood.
Her guess was indeed correct.

Charles couldn't help but speak.
"Has Big Brother become suspicious?"
"Was he tricked by Mina?"

Ethan snorted coldly.
"She was improper before marriage."
"I really don't understand why Big Brother married such a woman."
"And Third Uncle, he..."

"What about Third Uncle?"

Third Master's voice suddenly sounded.
Ethan immediately stiffened.

He slowly turned around, forcing a flattering smile.
"Third Uncle, you are wise and mighty, elegant and graceful!"

Third Master frowned.
"Stop the flattery."

He looked upstairs.
"Where are your Big Brother and sister-in-law?"

Ethan was stunned, his eyes darting towards the guest room.

Third Master frowned.
"What's going on?"

Ethan coughed lightly.
"Big Brother and sister-in-law are in the guest room."

"What are they doing in the guest room?"

Third Master spoke as he walked towards the guest room.

Penelope hurriedly stopped him.
"Third Uncle, the newlyweds are having some private talk."
"We better not disturb them."

Third Master nodded and sat down on the sofa.

Soon after, Marcus's parents arrived.

Third Master greeted them.
"Big Brother, sister-in-law."

Marcus's father was very polite to his third brother.
He smiled and nodded, then looked around.
"Where is Chimo? Why hasn't he come yet?"
"This child is so unreasonable, actually making Third Uncle wait for him?"

Marcus's mother quickly explained to Third Master.
"He must have been delayed by something."

Third Master waved his hand, signaling for them to sit down.

Penelope's gaze remained fixed on the guest room direction.
Mina married into the family by relying on the child.
If the child was gone, her uncle and aunt would never tolerate her!
They had dared to challenge her before the marriage.
Now let Mina taste the feeling of being isolated and helpless!

A nanny brought over a bowl of medicine.
A strong bitter smell filled the living room.

Third Master, Marcus's father, and mother all saw the nanny carrying the medicine towards the guest room.

Marcus's mother stood up nervously.
"What medicine is this?"
"Is Minmin unwell?"
"She is pregnant and can't take medicine randomly!"

She stood up to leave but was pulled back by Penelope.
"Aunt, there is something I didn't want to say, but now I have to."

She took out her phone and handed a document to Marcus's mother.

Marcus's mother glanced at it and immediately pushed it away.
"What filthy thing!"
"Penelope, where did you get this at such a young age?"

Penelope looked embarrassed, then straightened her expression.
"This is circulating in our circle."
"It's about sister-in-law!"

Marcus's mother was stunned.
"What?"

Penelope glanced at Ethan.
"Everyone says this was written by Justin."
"He admitted personally that he had a one-night stand with sister-in-law over two months ago."
"Big Brother heard this and dragged sister-in-law to the guest room."
"He had the nanny bring abortion medicine..."

Penelope pretended to be worried.
"Aunt, even if sister-in-law really had something with Justin, it was a mistake before marriage."
"Isn't Big Brother being too harsh?"

Ethan couldn't help but speak.
"How is Big Brother being too harsh?"
"She married in pregnant with another man's child, making Big Brother a cuckold."
"Just getting rid of the child is already being polite!"

Penelope pretended to be surprised.
"Third Brother, what do you mean by that?"
"Is the child really not Big Brother's?"

Marcus's mother, who was about to rush over, stopped in her tracks.
She looked at Ethan.

Ethan choked, unable to speak for a long time.
Finally, he said, "Yes, it's not."
"Big Brother is doing the right thing. He should do this!"
"To avoid damaging the family's reputation!"

Penelope pressed further.
"How do you know it's not?"

Ethan kept his mouth shut this time.
"Anyway, I know. Don't ask how I know!"

Marcus's father and mother exchanged a look, their eyes full of worry.
If the child really wasn't Marcus's, should they still stop it?

...

In the guest room.

Marcus trapped Mina on the sofa, staring at her intently.
His eyes were bloodshot.

Mina was still begging.
"Marcus, no, this child is really yours!"

Marcus found it utterly ironic.
He had believed Mina's lies, which was why he kept going to the hospital for checks.
But every result came back as oligospermia...
It couldn't be that three hospitals were all lying to him!

The one lying to him could only be Mina.

He watched Mina begging bitterly.
Suddenly, he grabbed her neck.
"Mina, shut up! I won't believe you again!"

Mina's voice cut off abruptly.
Her eyes were red, tears welling as she looked at Marcus.

Marcus leaned in closer.
"Minmin, get rid of the child."
"Behave properly from now on, and I will treat you well..."

Mina closed her eyes in pain.
Two clear tears streamed down.
She lowered her head in resignation.

At that moment, the guest room door was kicked open with a bang!!
###Chapter 394

A flicker of hope ignited in Mina's eyes.

She looked toward the doorway as if grasping at a lifeline.

Marcus's body tensed instantly.

He whirled around.

Third Master Valentine strode purposefully into the living room.

He positioned himself directly between the two.

Marcus clenched his fists.

The usually defiant man now had red-rimmed eyes.

"Uncle," he rasped, his voice hoarse.

Third Master Valentine frowned deeply.

Marcus's parents rushed in close behind.

His mother spoke urgently.

"Marcus, what on earth is going on?"

"Mina, is the child really Marcus's?"

Mina gave a bitter, faint smile.

She looked directly at Marcus.

Her hands were clenched into white-knuckled fists.

"It's his."

Marcus roared in fury.

"Shut up!"

Even now.

Mina persisted in claiming the child was his.

Was she so desperate to keep this baby?

Had she abandoned all dignity?

Did this child mean that much to her?

Mrs. Valentine frowned deeply.

"Marcus, is the child yours or not?"

Marcus stared fixedly at Mina, his eyes bloodshot.

Mina lay collapsed on the sofa, looking up at him.

Her eyes were filled with a stubbornness and sorrow he couldn't comprehend.

Marcus pressed his lips tightly together and remained silent.

Mrs. Valentine sighed.

"Marcus, I don't know why you doubt the child is yours."

"Perhaps it's because of that file."

"But the baby is almost three months along."

"An amniocentesis after three months will reveal everything."

"Don't be impulsive today, alright?"

She walked over to Marcus.

"I know this is embarrassing for you."

"But a man's honor isn't earned through a woman."

"Marcus, calm down."

"If you force her to take this medicine today."

"The baby will truly be gone."

"If it turns out to be yours later."

"You'll regret it for the rest of your life."

"This will also cause irreparable damage between you."

Marcus's knuckles were white from clenching.

Mrs. Valentine turned to Mina.

"Mina, let me say one more thing."

"We don't want to dwell on what happened before the marriage."

"It's the twenty-first century now."

"People are very open-minded."

"But if the child isn't Marcus's."

"Please drink this medicine."

"We will take this secret to our graves."

"If the child is Marcus's."

"Then we'll do the amniocentesis in three months."

"Can you accept that?"

Mina looked at her with gratitude.

"I choose the amniocentesis."

"Just one more week until three months!"

Mrs. Valentine nodded.

"Good."

She looked at Marcus.

"It's settled then."

"Stop making a scene."

"Everyone, come out now."

"It's time for dinner."

She turned to leave.

But Marcus remained rooted in place.

Mrs. Valentine looked at him.

Marcus seemed frozen.

He stared intently at Mina.

"Do you have to push it this far?"

Mina met his gaze, her own eyes red-rimmed.

Marcus suddenly lowered his head.

"Whether the child is mine or not."

"We both know the truth."

"Why pretend to be wronged?"

"There are no choices in this matter today."

His voice was gravelly.

His eyes held an unprecedented ruthlessness.

If he wasn't harsh with Mina now.

She would leave after giving birth.

She had already prepared the divorce papers!

This thought sent a wave of agitation through Marcus.

He loosened his tie and looked at Mina again.

"You must drink this medicine today."

"If you won't drink it, I'll make you."

He snatched the bowl from the nanny.

He walked over to Mina.

He looked down at her from his height.

"Mina, will you drink it yourself, or shall I help you?"

Mrs. Valentine was alarmed by his demeanor.

"Marcus, can't you wait just one more week?"

Ethan Valentine quietly stepped forward.

"Aunt, don't ask."

"Big Brother knows what he's doing."

"He knows what he did."

"Please don't interfere in their matters."

Mrs. Valentine was taken aback.

Had Marcus never been intimate with Mina?

If that were true.

Then the child truly couldn't be Marcus's…

As both a mother and mother-in-law.

She really shouldn't interfere too much in the young couple's life.

She frowned and ultimately chose silence.

Mr. Valentine also said nothing.

Only Third Master Valentine looked at Marcus.

"Are you sure you won't regret today's actions?"

Marcus gave a low laugh.

"No."

He knew forcing Mina to drink the medicine would send their relationship to a freezing point.

But it was better than losing her.

Let her hate him.

At least they could remain entangled for the rest of their lives…

Third Master Valentine's face was stern.

Seeing Marcus's determination.

He could only sigh.

"This is ultimately the couple's private matter…"

Mina clenched her fists, staring at Marcus.

Her gaze fell on the bowl of medicine in his hand.

She wanted to say something.

But she was tired of repeating that the child was his.

No one believed her.

Because her reputation was too tarnished.

So tarnished that everyone believed the post the moment they saw it.

Mina's eyes gradually reddened.

Tears welled up.

She looked at Marcus again.

The boy who had stood up for her when she was bullied.

His halo gradually faded.

Finally morphing into Marcus's current ferocious expression.

She clenched her fingers tightly.

Her lips trembled.

"Marcus, I can drink the medicine."

"But after I drink it, I want you to do a paternity test with the… remains."

"Do you dare?"

She would make him regret this for the rest of his life!!

Marcus was stunned by her stubbornness.

Penelope suddenly spoke.

"Mina, what do you mean by this?"

"Big Brother's attitude is already very clear."

"The child isn't his."

"If there was even a sliver of possibility it was his."

"Why would he force you to abort?"

"So what's the point of doing a test?"

Ethan chimed in.

"Sister-in-law, after you drink the medicine, we won't look down on you because of today."

"Stop being stubborn."

Penelope nodded.

"You've tarnished the Valentine family's reputation."

"The Valentines have been lenient enough with you."

"What more is there to hesitate about?"

But Mina stared fixedly at Marcus, enunciating each word slowly.

"Do. You. Dare?"

Marcus frowned.

A sudden sense of powerlessness and panic surged within him.

It was as if he had just lost something immensely important…

He wanted to grasp it firmly.

He spoke directly.

"I dare!"

"Fine, then I'll drink it."

Mina took the bowl from his hand!
###Chapter 395

Mina stared at the bowl of medicine.

The dark liquid emitted a bitter odor.

But no bitterness could compare to the coldness in her heart.

Her gaze lowered to her abdomen.

She was less than three months pregnant, with no visible bump yet.

But she had already grown accustomed to the baby's presence these past months.

She would instinctively protect her belly while walking.

She often dreamed of the child's face at night.

She imagined his eyes resembling Marcus', his lips like hers...

Now she couldn't keep him after all.

Tears blurred her vision.

Despair washed over her like a tidal wave.

She looked up at Marcus Valentine.

She didn't know how much to blame him.

After all, she had lived recklessly these past few years.

Her uncle had taken her to business dinners and social events.

In others' eyes, she was already a disreputable escort...

Mina lowered her head with a bitter smile.

She suddenly spoke softly. "Marcus, don't do the DNA test."

Marcus froze.

The girl kept her eyes downcast, her expression calm.

She wasn't insisting on the test anymore, as if admitting the child wasn't his.

Yet he felt inexplicably uneasy inside.

Large teardrops fell into the medicine bowl.

Mina's voice was very quiet. "Not doing it sets you free."

If the baby was gone, doing the test would only hurt Marcus more.

In her anger earlier, she just wanted him to know the truth.

Now she decided to let him go.

She lifted the bowl to drink.

Her wrist was suddenly grabbed.

Mina looked up to see Marcus' complicated expression.

Even he didn't understand why he stopped her.

He immediately released her and stepped back upon realizing.

Mina remained silent and raised the bowl again.

Suddenly, a voice shouted from the doorway. "Mina!"

Mina turned toward the sound.

Evelyn Ross and Alexander Vance rushed in.

Evelyn was dragging Justin Evans, who was bruised and battered.

The Valentine family butler followed behind, apologizing frantically. "Mr. Valentine, Young Master Marcus, I'm sorry I couldn't stop them..."

Ethan Valentine's eyes widened. "Evelyn, how dare you! You even brought this boy toy..."

Before he could finish, Mr. Valentine interrupted. "From now on, don't stop Miss Evelyn when she visits."

The butler looked confused but responded. "Yes."

Mr. Valentine approached Evelyn. "What brings you here?"

But Evelyn was staring at the bowl in Mina's hand. She rushed over and snatched it. "What is this?"

No one answered.

Evelyn glared at Marcus. "Speak! What is this?"

Marcus' face darkened. "Abortion pill."

Evelyn trembled with anger.

She looked at Mina trapped on the couch.

Then she scanned everyone present. "He's being unreasonable, and none of you stopped him?"

Mr. and Mrs. Valentine sighed helplessly.

Ethan looked at Mr. Valentine.

Expecting his uncle to react.

But Mr. Valentine seemed hesitant to speak.

Even Marcus remained silent.

Ethan couldn't hold back. "Evelyn, this is our family matter. It's none of your business..."

Evelyn ignored him completely and questioned Marcus. "You doubt her because of rumors? Did you even ask Justin?"

Marcus' cold gaze fell upon Justin.

Justin stiffened on the floor.

Marcus didn't explain that he had already tortured Justin for answers, but Justin still insisted he slept with Mina.

Evelyn's chest heaved with rage.

She turned and kicked the tied-up Justin. "Speak! What really happened between you and Mina?"

Justin, bruised and trembling, looked terrified.

He glanced secretly at Alexander Vance and shuddered.

The memory of what happened at the bar still haunted him.

When Evelyn attacked him, he refused to admit anything.

Until that man stepped in.

Wet paper covered his face, bringing suffocation and near-death.

He knew he would really die if he didn't tell the truth.

Marcus might still consider the Evans family's reputation.

But this man showed no restraint at all.

Marcus spoke slowly. "I've already asked him. He won't clarify... Evelyn, don't waste your effort."

Before he finished, Justin wailed. "I'll clarify! Who says I won't? You're lying!"
###Chapter 396

Marcus Valentine stood frozen.

The surrounding crowd exchanged bewildered glances.

Justin Evans wailed, "I did have my eye on Mina! Everyone in New York knows she's the famous unattainable ice queen!"

"Countless men wanted to be the one to bring her down a peg!"

"When she was still the Sherwood heiress, none of us dared make a move. But after her family fell from grace... let's just say the talk among men got pretty lively."

He looked at Mina, his voice trembling. "Men like to boast. Save face."

"That last time she had dinner with me and her uncle... her uncle made it perfectly clear she was there to entertain me."

"But the moment he left, she tried to run?"

"You think I'd let a piece of meat right in front of my mouth just fly away? I tried to force her..."

Marcus's eyes turned icy cold, sharply focusing on him.

Justin shrank back in fear. "Wait, don't be mad! I did try to force her, but she fought like hell! She got away in the end!"

"But I'd already bragged to my friends! When they asked later, I couldn't just admit I failed, could I? I had to double down and say I slept with her!"

The entire hall fell into dead silence.

After a long moment, Penelope Valentine spoke softly. "That article described everything in such detail. It even mentioned the mole on sister-in-law's lower back."

"Big brother, is that true?"

Marcus remained silent.

He had read that article over and over.

Every single detail was burned into his mind.

Especially the descriptions of her most private areas. They had made him see red, nearly driving him insane.

That was the real reason he had been so insistent on the abortion today.

Not just the mole on her lower back. Even more intimate places were described with perfect clarity.

How could anyone know such details unless they had seen them with their own eyes?

His silence was answer enough.

Penelope looked at Evelyn. "Did you use some method to force him to change his story?"

Evelyn Ross let out a cold laugh, but before she could speak, Justin shouted again.

"Penelope Valentine, do you have some kind of grudge against me? I said I didn't sleep with her, so I didn't!"

"Those details about her body have been circulating in our circle for ages!"

"Just because I didn't succeed doesn't mean others didn't!"

He looked at Mina, his tone turning vicious. "She's been the well-known escort girl in our circle for a while now."

"No wonder I was so pissed when she rejected me that day."

"Thinking about it now, she must have already latched onto a bigger fish. Trying to cut ties with all her previous clients, huh?"

Evelyn directly kicked him in the abdomen, sending him flying.

Justin screamed as he skidded across the floor, curling into a ball from the pain. "I won't say it anymore! I'll clarify! Mina Sherwood and I are completely innocent!"

"I swear! If I ever slept with her, may I be struck by lightning!"

His words convinced most of the people present.

Mr. and Mrs. Valentine hurried forward, pushing Marcus aside.

Mrs. Valentine helped Mina up. "Mina, you've been wronged today."

Since Mina had married into the Valentine family, Mrs. Valentine had always treated her well.

Mina nodded gratefully, but her eyes remained on Marcus.

Mrs. Valentine pushed her son. "Marcus, how could you doubt Mina because of some outsider's words?"

"Apologize to her! You haven't even been married that long. How could you do this?"

Marcus remained silent.

Mrs. Valentine was about to say more when Ethan Valentine cut in. "Aunt, big brother has his reasons."

"Justin says he didn't sleep with her, but who knows about others..."

"Shut up!"

"Shut up!"

Evelyn and Third Uncle shouted in unison. They glanced at each other.

Third Uncle looked at her with a deep, unreadable gaze.

Evelyn coughed lightly and looked away.

Ethan glanced between them, pursing his lips.

This Evelyn Ross and Third Uncle were really in sync.

Evelyn looked at Marcus. "The baby will be three months along in a few days. Can't you wait until then for an amniocentesis and DNA test?"

"Is even a few days too long to wait?"

Mrs. Valentine quickly agreed. "Marcus, I don't know what's happened between you two."

"But since the wedding, Mina has either been at home or with Miss Ross."

"I've seen her character for myself. You can't judge a person solely on rumors."

"Perhaps she had her reasons for those business dinners back then. There must be some misunderstanding between you."

Marcus still said nothing.

Mrs. Valentine walked to Mina's side. "Mina, as long as there's even a one percent chance this child is Marcus's, I'm willing to wait for you to prove your innocence!"

Mina's eyes instantly reddened.

She felt a mother's love from Mrs. Valentine she had never experienced before.

Mr. Valentine also said, "Yes. From now on, you and Marcus will live separately."

"Don't worry. We won't let Marcus hurt you again before the DNA test!"

"No need."

Evelyn spoke coldly. "Mina is coming with me."

"On what grounds?"

Ethan jumped in again. "Evelyn Ross, you're overstepping. This is Valentine family business!"

"Big brother and sister-in-law just have a small misunderstanding. What right do you have to take her away?"

Evelyn ignored him, looking directly at Marcus. "I'm taking her."

"When she's three months along, I'll take her to my facility for the test."

She took Mina's hand. "Let's go."

The fact that all these people had been standing around, yet no one had truly stopped Marcus, infuriated Evelyn.

She would not leave Mina here.

Mina also wanted to protect her child and silently followed.

Alexander Vance followed closely behind.

The three of them walked straight out.

The butler looked at Third Uncle. A single gesture from him would have people intercept them.

But Third Uncle merely pressed his lips together and did not stop them.

The butler stood frozen.

The Valentine family could only watch as Evelyn Ross barged in and then took Mina away.

Only after the sound of a car driving away faded did Ethan look at Third Uncle. "Third Uncle, she's too arro..."

"Shut up!"

Third Uncle snapped at him again.

Ethan was truly angry now. "Third Uncle, how can you do this?"

"Letting big brother suffer such humiliation for a woman? Have you lost your mind?"

"And that woman is clearly no good! She even brought a little..."
###Chapter 397

Ethan Valentine was seething.

He thought his entire family had gone insane.

His eldest brother Marcus, who was diagnosed with oligospermia, had married a heavily pregnant woman.

And his Third Uncle was completely blinded by love. How could he tolerate Evelyn bringing her boy toy right to their doorstep?

Third Uncle Valentine frowned at Marcus.

Ethan’s words were strange, as if he was certain the child wasn’t Marcus’s? Was there some hidden truth?

Marcus was furious. He suddenly raised his hand.

Smack!

A sharp slap landed on Ethan’s face. "Shut up!"

Ethan was completely stunned. He had always been mischievous and reckless, but the family atmosphere was usually harmonious. At worst, Third Uncle would spank them. The seven brothers had always been close, never keeping secrets from each other.

Now his eldest brother had hit him over two women?

He trembled with rage. "I was standing up for you, and this is how you treat me? Eldest Brother, Third Uncle, you’ve really let me down!"

Ethan turned and stormed out of the living room.

Charles Valentine hurried after him. "Third Brother, wait! Don’t be impulsive!"

Marcus stared blankly at his own hand.

He had confirmed the child wasn’t his. Yet he had instinctively defended Mina’s reputation. His heart was full of conflict.

He lowered his gaze, looking dazed.

Third Uncle Valentine said sternly, "Come with me!"

This family dinner was destined to end badly.

Everyone gradually left. Marcus’s parents glanced at him with concern, but ultimately said nothing.

Marcus had been raised and groomed by Third Uncle Valentine as the designated heir. His parents had always taken a hands-off approach. Now was not the time to interfere.

In the study, Third Uncle Valentine got straight to the point. "What is really going on?"

Marcus was silent for a long time before speaking quietly. "I have oligospermia."

Third Uncle Valentine looked slightly surprised, but quickly regained his composure. "Confirmed?"

He wasn’t obsessed with having heirs. If Evelyn and Grace weren’t his daughters, he never planned to have children anyway.

So he was quite accepting of the oligospermia diagnosis.

Marcus smiled bitterly. "Three different hospitals confirmed it."

Third Uncle Valentine frowned. "Then the child?"

"Mina insists it’s mine." Marcus gritted his teeth. "No matter how I pressure her, she won’t budge. Who is that man? Does she love him so much that she must have his child?"

Third Uncle Valentine coughed lightly. "Evelyn’s attitude didn’t seem fake."

Marcus took a deep breath. "Then Evelyn was fooled by her too! You saw her just now, acting so wronged. But Third Uncle, I am suffering more!"

Marcus dropped all pretenses in front of his Third Uncle. He collapsed onto the sofa. "I think I’ve really fallen for Mina. I told her to get an abortion. I made her choose between the child and me. She chose to have the child and then divorce me…"

"Third Uncle, what am I to her? Just a tool to get her child a legal status?"

Third Uncle Valentine offered a cruel reminder. "Single parents can register their children now."

Marcus was speechless.

He hung his head in defeat. "I just want to keep her. Without this child, there would be no barrier between us…"

Third Uncle Valentine sighed softly. "Marcus, the tighter you grasp, the more you lose. Sometimes, letting go is how you gain the whole world."

Marcus shuddered, stunned into silence.

…

Ethan rushed into the parking lot, ready to drive away to clear his head.

Charles caught up and climbed into the passenger seat. "Third Brother, what’s wrong with you? Eldest Brother and Third Uncle didn’t say anything. Why are you so upset on their behalf?"

Ethan glared resentfully at the brightly lit mansion. "Forget about Eldest Brother. He even tried to force Sister-in-law to have an abortion today. The most outrageous one is Third Uncle!"

"There’s a Family Reunion Gala in four days. Do you know what it’s for?"

"I don’t know."

Charles was still in university and didn’t know much about family affairs.

Ethan said through gritted teeth, "We’re getting a new Third Aunt!"

"What?!"

Charles was excited. "That’s great news! Why are you angry?"

"The new Third Aunt is Evelyn! Did you notice the man she brought with her today?"

Charles nodded blankly.

Ethan sneered. "That’s her boy toy!"

Charles was shocked. "Wh-what?!"

Ethan scoffed. "Hard to believe, right? Third Uncle is so shrewd, yet he’s been bewitched by a little temptress in his old age. And she’s two-timing him! She had the audacity to bring her lover right to our door…"

Charles stammered, "Could there be some misunderstanding?"

"What misunderstanding could there be?"

Charles thought for a moment. "Should we go ask Third Uncle for clarification?"

"Hah!" Ethan laughed mockingly. "I barely said a word today, and Eldest Brother hit me!"

Charles’s eyes lit up. "Then let’s go ask Evelyn!"

Ethan immediately perked up. "Right! We’ll go find Evelyn. We’ll make her choose between her boy toy and Third Uncle!"

"Okay~"

The two impulsive young men were in agreement. They drove straight to Evelyn’s residence.

At the entrance of the Nan residence.

Evelyn had just helped Mina upstairs to rest.

Mina was physically and emotionally exhausted. She wanted to be alone and insisted she was fine, telling Evelyn to leave.

Evelyn understood and exited the room. She sighed and asked Alexander Vance, "Do you think that idiot Marcus ever got a proper medical check-up?"

Alexander was about to answer when there was a knock at the door.

They went downstairs and saw Ethan and Charles standing outside.

Ethan’s face was full of anger, a clear slap mark still visible. He sneered and demanded, "Evelyn, between this boy toy and Third Uncle, who do you choose?!"

Evelyn was baffled. "Why should I choose?"

Ethan retorted, "How can you face Third Uncle if you don’t choose? If you want to be with my Third Uncle, you should be devoted to him! Cut off all these rotten ties!"

Evelyn was utterly confused. Then she remembered Ethan calling her ‘Third Aunt’ at the Cortez Sect…

She couldn’t help but laugh and explain, "You’ve misunderstood. Your Third Uncle and I don’t have that kind of relationship!"

"Then what other relationship could you have?!"
###Chapter 398

"We..."

Evelyn Ross had just started to speak when Ethan Valentine cut her off sharply.

"Stop making excuses! I'm asking you, are you breaking up with this boy toy or not?"

Evelyn said nothing.

She crossed her arms, too lazy to explain.

She watched Ethan jump around like a clown, frantic with anger.

She glanced back at Alexander Vance.

The man stood there with a raised brow, a smile playing on his lips. "Boy toy?"

Evelyn said lazily, "You eat my food and live in my house. What else would you be?"

Alexander chuckled, his gaze deep. "I do live in your house."

Evelyn fell silent.

This guy was always making innuendos since they got together.

She cleared her throat and looked away.

Ethan roared, "Grace! You two are making eyes at each other! Still trying to deny it?"

Evelyn said, "I'm not denying it."

"You...!"

Ethan was furious. "Don't think just because Uncle dotes on you, I'll tolerate this endlessly! A core disciple of Cortez Sect? Don't even think about it!"

Evelyn spread her hands. "Is that so? Want to try?"

Ethan was infuriated by her attitude. He turned and stormed off.

Charles Valentine followed him, shooting her a glare. "You'll regret this!"

The two brothers slammed the door on their way out.

Evelyn pouted and closed the door.

As soon as she turned around, a scorching hand wrapped around her waist.

She pushed him. "What are you doing?"

"I don't just eat your food and live in your house..." Alexander's voice was low. "I'm also going to sleep with you..."

Evelyn's cheeks instantly flushed red. "What nonsense are you talking about?"

Alexander chuckled. "Sleep in your bed. What were you thinking?"

Evelyn said nothing.

That night, Alexander slept in her bed, and he also slept with her.

The next morning.

When Evelyn got out of bed, the man looked refreshed and thoroughly satisfied.

She couldn't help but sigh.

"What's wrong?" Alexander asked.

Evelyn replied, "I'm thinking about sleeping in separate rooms."

Alexander stiffened. "That's not necessary, is it?"

"Then let's discuss restraint."

"Seven times a week?"

Evelyn was speechless.

"Six times?"

"..."

"Five times. That's the absolute minimum!"

Evelyn gritted her teeth. "Medical advice suggests two or three times a week!"

"You don't understand," Alexander said slowly. "Women hit their peak at thirty, but men start to decline. I need to make up for what I owe you before I turn thirty."

"..."

Evelyn felt this conversation was pointless.

Where was the aloof, domineering CEO she was promised?!

She fled the room as if escaping.

She ran into Mina Sherwood at the door.

Mina's eyes were red and swollen, clearly from crying.

Evelyn was about to comfort her when Mina suddenly spoke. "Grace, I gave Marcus Valentine the divorce papers. Can you help me get him to sign them?"

The three-month DNA test had to be done.

But she would wait until after the baby was born to divorce.

Evelyn was confused. "You want to divorce immediately?"

Mina paused. "I want my child to be born legitimately."

Having a child within a marriage was better than having a bastard.

Evelyn understood. "I'll help you. Until you give birth, you'll live with me."

Mina nodded and smiled.

...

Evelyn didn't stay home long.

The Cortez Sect's official martial arts tournament was today.

It was difficult for outer disciples to enter the inner sect.

The tournament used a draw system, eliminating one out of every two.

With bad luck, the third strongest could face the first and be immediately eliminated.

Every year, some advanced through luck alone...

Evelyn couldn't rely on luck.

After drawing her lot, John Evans came over frowning. "Grace, your luck is terrible!"

Evelyn was slightly taken aback. "Why?"

John pointed at the lot. "Liam Lowell! The best fighter under Jason Evans's command! You're in big trouble against him!"

He sighed. "You hit Jason yesterday. Someone saw him going to the Discipline Hall. If Second Senior Brother hadn't intervened, the Cortez Sect would never let assaulting a superior slide."

The Cortez Sect had strict rules against subordinates challenging superiors.

Evelyn was a little worried Jason would cause trouble...

As for Ethan Valentine...

She rubbed her chin.

Should she clarify her relationship with Third Uncle?

She felt... somewhat reluctant?

Then she wouldn't clarify.

The truth would come out at the family reunion gala in three days anyway.

It would take the Cortez Sect's Discipline Hall a few days to process things...

As she was thinking, Jason Evans walked over with a dark expression, followed by his men.

He stopped in front of her. "Grace! You hit my brother Justin Evans yesterday. I've reported it! Wait for the Cortez Sect's punishment!"

Evelyn glanced at him and calmly rotated her wrist. "Looking for another beating?"

"You..."

Jason gritted his teeth in anger and looked at Liam. "When you face Grace later, don't hold back! Teach her a proper lesson!"

Liam nodded, glaring darkly at Evelyn.

Evelyn raised a brow and asked John, "Is Liam very strong?"

"Extremely strong!" John sighed. "Top three among outer disciples! He had bad luck the past two years, always drawing the number one, so he stayed."

Evelyn didn't know much about martial arts and wasn't sure how powerful her master was.

She was a bit worried. "Can I beat him?"

John shook his head. "Definitely not! Grace, don't you know your own strength?"

Evelyn fell silent.

Damn!

She suddenly felt nervous.

If she couldn't win on stage and couldn't escape...

She coughed and took out her phone to text Alexander. [If I lose later, remember to come bail me out.]
###Chapter 399

Outside the Cortez Gate.

Inside the car.

Alexander Vance stared at his phone screen, a slight smile on his lips.

Thomas Watson sat in the back seat, speaking cautiously. "Sir, aren't you worried about Miss Evelyn?"

Alexander tossed his phone aside. "She seems to have some misunderstanding about her own abilities."

Thomas was stunned. "But Miss Evelyn doesn't seem skilled in fighting... She was once pinned down by bodyguards in San Francisco."

Alexander gave him a cool glance. "That was because of Grandma."

Thomas suddenly understood. "Right, it was because of Mrs. Eleanor! She's already in New York, waiting for you to bring Miss Evelyn home."

Alexander looked toward the Cortez Gate, his gaze darkening. "Tell Grandma we won't be back for now."

Until Nange's identity was clarified, he couldn't formally introduce Evelyn to his grandmother.

…

Inside the Cortez Gate.

Evelyn sent the message but received no reply from Alexander. She frowned slightly.

Was there a problem with the phone signal?

Jason Evans approached, his eyes cold. "Calling anyone now is useless! Even if Ethan Valentine were here, he'd have to follow the draw! Nange, kneel and beg for mercy. I'll tell Liam to go easy on you."

Evelyn pressed her lips together.

Kneeling and begging for mercy was absolutely out of the question.

Her only choice was to defeat Liam with all her strength!

She took a deep breath. "Let's go."

She walked to the base of the擂台.

The martial arts tournament had a擂台. Participants fought in the order of the draw.

Evelyn had drawn number fifteen.

The people before her fought until their faces were bruised and swollen. The losers were covered in injuries, carried off the stage.

Evelyn watched with a racing heart.

Her iron-deficiency anemia was a hidden danger. What if she got injured and bled...

Why wasn't Alexander replying?

James was still in San Francisco handling the company's IPO. Distant water couldn't put out a nearby fire.

What should she do if she lost too much blood?

She looked at John Evans. "If I pass out, remember to send me to the hospital for a blood transfusion. And iron supplements."

John was stunned. "Nange, are you really going to fight?"

"I'll try."

Evelyn's tone was uncertain. "Winning gives me hope of entering the inner sect."

John nodded. "There is hope... But how can you defeat Liam? Remember? The two of us together couldn't beat him back then... Maybe just admit defeat now!"

"The tournament rule is, the fight continues until one side admits defeat."

Jason Evans walked over with a smile. "Nange, have you thought it through? Do you really want to fight Liam?"

"Don't think just because you lasted three moves against the Second Senior Brother you're something special! Everyone knows he goes easy on you! Last year, you couldn't even handle the Third Senior Brother's second move!"

"It's not too late to admit your mistake now... It's almost your turn."

Evelyn picked at her ear. "Are you a frog? So noisy."

Jason's face turned ashen. "You're asking for it! Don't blame Liam for being ruthless!"

Evelyn curled her lip.

In the spectator stands.

Ethan Valentine looked through the match list. His brow furrowed when he saw Nange was paired against Liam.

During the last screening, he went easy on others. But with Nange, she somehow dodged his three moves.

He still found it unbelievable.

But no matter what, she couldn't possibly defeat Liam.

Liam was one of the best among the outer disciples...

Elder Xavier quietly slipped into the stands. "Has Nange finished her match?"

"Not yet."

Several elders replied.

Elder Xavier nodded and sat down.

The number fourteen match ended. The loser was carried to the hospital, covered in blood.

Evelyn grew more worried about her own life.

The presiding elder called out number fifteen.

Evelyn reminded John again. "Remember, send me to the hospital for iron supplements."

John was speechless.

The crowd fell silent.

Elder Xavier in the stands also frowned.

During the bone assessment that day, Nange's aptitude felt exceptional. Why was she so lacking in confidence against an outer disciple?

Outer disciples had ordinary talent. Inner disciples could easily crush them...

Elder Xavier suddenly doubted his judgment that day.

Had he made a mistake?

Had he reported to the sect leader for nothing!

He thought his luck had turned, that he had found a good seedling like the sect leader...

Disappointment welled up in Elder Xavier's heart. He slumped into his chair.

On the stage, Evelyn and Liam faced each other.

Evelyn was 1.67 meters tall with a slender build.

Liam was 1.9 meters tall with bulging muscles.

The disparity in strength was vast. The outcome seemed certain.

Evelyn's pretty face was tense. Her eyes were alert, as if facing a great enemy.

The presiding elder shouted, "Begin!"

Liam roared and charged forward!
###Chapter 400

Evelyn was startled.

Her expression tightened.
She dared not be careless.
She mobilized all her strength to meet Liam's heavy punch.

John saw this and shouted anxiously, "Dodge it! Liam's punches are notoriously fierce. You'll lose in a direct clash!"

Evelyn's heart clenched.

Don't confront it head-on?

But her master said offense is the best defense.

The punch was already upon her.
It was still possible to withdraw and evade now.
But she felt it unnecessary...

Evelyn steeled her heart.
She gathered all her strength into her palm.
She pushed out violently!

"Bang!"

The punch landed solidly on her palm.

John shut his eyes tightly.
He could almost imagine the gruesome scene of Grace's arm dislocating and her whole body being sent flying.

But the expected scream did not come.

John cautiously opened his eyes.
He saw Evelyn also looked stunned.
She stared blankly at Liam's fist.
Her eyes were full of confusion.

Then she gave a gentle push.

Liam staggered back two steps.
Evelyn remained completely motionless!

Evelyn: ?

The crowd below was dumbfounded.
Jason was hopping mad.
"Liam, what are you doing? Getting soft now? Teach that bitch a lesson!"

Evelyn suddenly understood.

So Liam wasn't a bad person.

He disapproved of Jason's actions.
He was deliberately holding back?

Evelyn's gaze softened as she looked at Liam.
Since the other party intended to yield, she didn't need to go all out either...

Liam charged forward fiercely again.
His face was ferocious.
His acting was quite convincing.

Evelyn easily caught his fist.
She didn't feel much force at all!
He was definitely putting on a show for Jason!

She gave Liam a grateful smile.
She pushed him away smoothly.
She said clearly, "I appreciate your kindness. But I must win this match. I have reasons I must win. Please, no more holding back!"

Liam: ??

Ten thousand beasts stampeded through his heart!

Holding back? Who's holding back for you?!

Liam hurriedly looked toward the stage.
Jason's eyes were already icy cold.
He explained urgently, "I didn't! I really used my full strength!"

Jason glared at him viciously.
"Fine, Liam! How dare you defy me openly! What? Also enchanted by this woman's face? Beat her to death!"

Liam: !!

He really had used his full strength!

To prove his innocence, he threw another punch at Evelyn.
But the woman just used a light palm again.

She seemed to use only twenty percent of her strength to block the attack.
She gave him a gentle smile.
"Are you sure you're not using your full strength? Thank you."

Liam: "..."

Then he was sent off the stage by the opponent's soft punch.

Landing meant losing.

Liam: "..."

He lost?

He actually lost!

The slender figure on the stage still smiled gently at him.
"I concede."

Liam: "..."

He turned back dazedly.
Jason had already rushed to him.
He threw a hard punch right at his face!

"Bang!"

Jason's fist hit his face.
"Bastard! You dare disobey me? When did you take a liking to this cheap girl? Protecting her like this! Is this a martial arts match? Even playing house is more serious than you two!"

Evelyn jumped off the stage.
She frowned.
"Jason, don't go too far! Liam disapproves of your tyranny! There's nothing between us!"

Liam: !!

He hurriedly spoke.
"I didn't, I..."

"Slap!"

Another hard slap landed on his face.
It cut off his words.
Jason pointed at his nose.
"Fine, fine, fine! I'll remember this!"

Jason left angrily with his men.

Liam: "..."

He was about to collapse!

He glared at Evelyn.
He gritted his teeth.
"Why... did you lie!"

He had just taken two punches.
His front teeth were knocked out.
His speech was slurred.

Evelyn: "..."

She looked at Liam apologetically.
"Sorry, my acting isn't good. I got you into trouble."

Liam: "..."

He looked around at the crowd watching the excitement.
He knew the conversation just now would soon reach Jason's ears.
He felt like he had fallen into an ice cellar.

Evelyn, however, was completely unaware.
She walked to John's side.

John looked her up and down.
"It's good you're okay! Did Liam really go easy on you?"

Evelyn looked at Liam.
She saw him throwing a pleading look.
She immediately understood and nodded.
She gave an OK gesture.
She said clearly, "How could that be? I've gotten stronger!"

John understood.
He quickly agreed.
"Right, right. Going from useless to an expert in half a year! Amazing! Liam is really... quite something!"

Liam: "..."

Their terrible acting was seen by everyone.
Could they act any faker?!

Jason would believe him even less now!!

Liam wailed inwardly.

Evelyn walked out in a happy mood.

Liam yielded the match.
She was one step closer to entering the inner sect!

Completing the cooperation with the Cortez family left only the Evans family...

Uh, the Evans family...

Evelyn rubbed her chin thoughtfully.
A figure suddenly flashed before her eyes.
It was Elder Xavier.

Elder Xavier stared at her intently.
He circled around her several times.

After a long while, he suddenly grabbed her wrist.
"A prodigy! A true prodigy!"

The outer disciples watching couldn't tell if Liam had gone easy.
But it couldn't escape his eyes!

This Grace, average in talent as a child, even mediocre.
Unexpectedly, she became a prodigy when she grew up?!

He had misjudged her back then!
Fortunately, he discovered it in time!

Though he had never seen such a prodigy, Elder Xavier would never allow any genius to be buried in the Cortez Sect.
He excitedly pulled Evelyn.
"Come! I've spoken with the Sect Leader. I'll take you to see him. Let him assess your bones!"

Hearing this, Evelyn's eyes immediately lit up.
###Chapter 401

Evelyn Ross was about to leave with Elder Xavier.

A mocking voice interrupted them.

"A prodigy? Elder Xavier, your eyes must be failing you."

Ethan Valentine strode over, his gaze sweeping over Evelyn with contempt.

Elder Xavier quickly explained, "I've been observing her for some time. Besides, she did hold her own against you for three moves."

Ethan sneered, "That was because I underestimated her. If we spar now, she wouldn't last a single move."

Evelyn frowned. "Then let's have a match. If I can withstand five of your moves, you will allow Elder Xavier to take me to see the Sect Leader."

She still wasn't entirely sure of her own capabilities.

Ethan scoffed. "I won't spar with you. But you can't go either. Elder Xavier, inner disciples must pass rigorous evaluations. Character is paramount. This Grace Nan has a despicable character!"

Elder Xavier was taken aback. "How so?"

"In every way possible!"

Ethan glared daggers at Evelyn. "She's two-timing and shows no remorse. Grace Nan, someone like you is unworthy of joining the inner sect. Elder Xavier, she and I are sworn enemies. The choice is yours."

With that, he turned and walked away.

Elder Xavier looked troubled. He turned to Evelyn anxiously. "Grace Nan, provoking the Evans family is one thing, but why did you have to anger this little tyrant? His eldest brother runs wild in New York. He's a notorious troublemaker within the Cortez Sect. He's also the Second Senior Brother under the First Senior Sister! If you've offended him, the inner sect will never accept you!"

The Cortez Sect valued harmony above all. They never accepted sworn enemies of existing inner disciples.

This prevented internal strife.

Elder Xavier's brow furrowed deeply. "Since your skills have improved and you wish to join the inner sect, you must go apologize to the Second Senior Brother immediately! Mend this relationship first. How did you offend him anyway?"

Evelyn found the situation both frustrating and amusing.

It was all because of Ethan's self-righteous misunderstanding!

She couldn't be bothered to explain. She simply said to Elder Xavier, "In three days, the Valentine family is holding a Family Reunion Gala. After the event, this misunderstanding between us will be resolved."

Elder Xavier nodded. "Very well. Once your misunderstanding is cleared up, I will take you to see the Sect Leader."

"Thank you."

Evelyn's attitude was humble and respectful.

Elder Xavier clasped his hands behind his back, speaking earnestly. "Young people should prioritize harmony…"

…

Evelyn had won her match that day.

Her next match was scheduled for three days later, after the Family Reunion Gala.

She walked straight out of the Cortez Sect.

Alexander Vance's car was already waiting outside.

She got in, and they drove home together.

After the car had driven some distance away, Ethan Valentine stormed out to the Cortez Sect's entrance.

He stared furiously in the direction the car had gone, thinking this woman was utterly arrogant!

Was she so certain his uncle would indulge her unconditionally?!

Ethan pulled out his phone and initiated a group voice call in the family chat.

The group only had the seven brothers.

Marcus Valentine was busy with something and didn't answer.

This didn't affect the other six from connecting.

Their eldest brother, destined to inherit the family business, was always busier than the rest of them.

The call connected quickly.

Charles Valentine's voice came through. "Third Brother, are you alright?"

Ethan sneered. "That Grace Nan is causing trouble even within the Cortez Sect! During her match today, her opponent blatantly went easy on her!"

Charles was silent for a moment. "...How could Uncle fall for someone so fickle?"

Other voices chimed in one after another.

"What's going on? I received an invitation to the Family Reunion Gala even though I'm abroad. I thought we were recognizing some long-lost relative. Turns out it's for a new aunt?"

"Uncle's woman is called Grace Nan? What kind of name is that? What's wrong with her?"

Ethan laid out his version of the truth over the call.

After he finished, the group fell silent.

After a long pause, someone finally spoke.

"I never thought Uncle would be such a lovesick fool."

"Is love in later years always so impulsive and irrational?"

"Why must Uncle demean himself like this? This makes me angry!"

…

Everyone shared their thoughts, growing more and more indignant.

Seeing their anger, Ethan himself calmed down.

Shared anger was better than anger alone.

Now, he felt psychologically balanced.

He proposed, "Brothers, shouldn't we unanimously oppose her at the Family Reunion Gala?"

"Agreed! I'll definitely rush back. If Uncle introduces her to me, I will never acknowledge her as my aunt!"

"...When I saw the invitation, I thought our long-lost cousin had finally returned! Turns out it's for an aunt!"

"Wishful thinking! Our family doesn't have the genes for daughters! Both the eldest and second branches only have sons! Seven brothers like the Calabash Brothers! Thankfully, we adopted two girls, or the family's balance would be severely off!"

"Adopted ones can't compare to a real cousin. Do you think our mom could still have a baby at forty-five?"

"Seventh Brother, you are truly a filial son."

…

The conversation drifted further and further off topic.

Time slipped away unnoticed.

In the blink of an eye, three days had passed!

The Valentine Family Reunion Gala was a grand affair.

New York's elite gathered together.

Everyone was curious, unsure exactly what kind of relative the Valentines were recognizing.

Alexander Vance drove Evelyn and Mina Sherwood to the event.

Mina didn't want to see Marcus Valentine, but this was her best friend's recognition ceremony. She had to witness it personally.

The two women got out of the car and walked towards the banquet hall.

As they approached, they saw seven men of different temperaments but equally striking presence standing ahead.

They were the seven Valentine brothers.

Marcus Valentine saw Mina, his eyes turning cold as he looked away.

Mina immediately lowered her gaze.

The fetus in her womb was almost three months old, her belly slightly rounded…

She also pretended not to see Marcus, walking past the seven men with Evelyn.

Penelope Valentine, the only girl in the Valentine family, was also there greeting guests.

Seeing Evelyn, she nervously watched her brothers' reactions.

She was afraid they would favor their blood cousin and neglect her.

To her surprise, except for Marcus, the other six brothers glared at Evelyn with clear hostility. Their faces plainly said "keep away"!

Penelope was stunned.

Weren't these brothers the ones who most desired a sister?

Why were they so hostile towards Grace Nan?

Excellent!

She had been most afraid that her blood cousin's return would steal her brothers' affection.

Now, it was clear her brothers preferred her!

Grace Nan, even if you are the blood cousin, so what?

The one who grew up with them is me!

And you, in their eyes, are nothing!

As Penelope secretly rejoiced, commotion came from inside the hall indicating Mr. Valentine's arrival.

Someone asked loudly, "Mr. Valentine, exactly what relative is the Valentine family recognizing at today's Family Reunion Gala?"
###Chapter 402

Alexander Vance was always aloof and rarely close to anyone.

The person speaking with him now was a childhood friend, so he took the question very seriously.
He answered directly, "No rush. I'll introduce you to her when she arrives."

Everyone burst into laughter.

"Of course I know!" Penelope looked innocent.

Penelope: ?

Why would it be wrong toward Third Uncle?

Did it mean she should have sought Third Uncle's permission before dating someone?

"What should we do now?"

"This family reunion gala would have a proper purpose then!"

"But would she agree to be your adopted sister?"

He then ran over to Evelyn.

Evelyn was holding a plate, lingering at the buffet area.
Banquets always had this downside—she never felt full.

The shelves were full of fancy but impractical things...

Evelyn picked up a cookie and turned to find Mina.
Instead, she found Charles standing before her, hands in pockets, sneering, "I ask you, will you be my adopted sister or not?"

Evelyn: ??

She raised an eyebrow and followed Charles's gaze to where the five brothers, led by Ethan, stood.

Then she chuckled softly and said calmly, "Alright."

Charles's eyes brightened, "I knew you'd make the right choice! Later, you tell Third Uncle this news. Don't let him announce you as his wife, understand?"

Charles didn't look much older than her.
His eyes held a naive innocence.

Evelyn realized the Valentine boys were quite simple-minded...

The Valentine family upbringing was good.

She lowered her eyes, "Fine."

"Yes."

"Good, it's settled then!"

Charles ran back excitedly.
Evelyn waved her plate at the six brothers and continued lingering near the food.

"How did it go?"

"How was it!"

As soon as Charles returned, everyone crowded around to ask.

Charles told them about Evelyn's reaction, and everyone was thrilled, "That's great!"

Charles added, "Third Brother, I think Grace is quite nice. When we talked, her smile was very cute!"

Ethan sneered, "Silly boy, don't be fooled by her appearance!"

The group laughed and joked...

After eating a few cookies, Evelyn wanted more but was stopped by Mina, "There's still the formal dinner later. If you eat so many cookies now, won't you have room for the meal?"

Evelyn paused, "There's still a dinner?"

Mina nodded and pointed to a private room, "This is just the buffet area. There's a formal banquet..."

Then she pointed to the head table, "You're Third Uncle's daughter and today's main guest. You'll sit at the head table later."

Evelyn: "........"

She thought these cookies were dinner.
So high-society banquets were this elaborate!

Evelyn waited a while longer as others gradually moved toward the banquet tables.

Evelyn went with Mina first to see her to the secondary table.
As Marcus's wife, Mina had a seat there.

Evelyn then looked at the name cards to find her own seat.

As she turned, she saw the Valentine group approaching.
Penelope was among them with Ethan.
Seeing Evelyn, Penelope immediately looked at Ethan, "Third Brother, is Grace's seat at the head table tonight?"

Everyone immediately looked at Evelyn.

Even Penelope clenched her fingers.

She feared that once Evelyn returned home, she would surpass her as the Valentine family's most esteemed daughter.
So she was setting a trap.

She wanted Ethan to announce Evelyn's seating publicly.

Ethan looked at Evelyn, sneered, and said directly, "Of course not. The head table is for elders. As for Grace..."

Ethan looked around the venue... He was in charge of guest reception and seating arrangements today.

He immediately pointed to the third table, "You sit there! That seat suits your status!"

Mina frowned, "Third Brother, how can you arrange it like this?"
###Chapter 403

Ethan Valentine sneered.
"Then what do you suggest? Have her sit at the head table? Surrounded by elders? Is she even worthy?"

Mina Sherwood frowned.
"Evelyn is the guest of honor today. Why shouldn't she sit there? You—"

Ethan cut her off.
"Guest of honor or not, if your status isn't high enough, don't overstep."

If she were a legitimate daughter of the Valentine family, sitting at the head table would be fine.

But what was Evelyn? Just his uncle's illegitimate love child!

"You…"

"Sister-in-law, the seating was arranged in advance. Please don't make a scene. Everyone is waiting for the banquet to begin."

"By the way, which branch are you from?"

Someone addressed Evelyn directly.

Evelyn ignored them, picking up her chopsticks to take some cold appetizer.

These people were deliberately talking about her within earshot, thinking she was an easy target.

Why should she give them the time of day?

Her attitude made the young women even more disdainful.

"So rude. She doesn't even answer when spoken to."

"I know her. Evelyn Ross. She just got divorced recently. Came from a humble background. Has no class."

They immediately began gossiping again.

They also cast ingratiating looks toward Penelope.

Penelope had always boasted about how much the Valentines valued her, but outsiders were半信半疑. Otherwise, Fiona Valentine wouldn't have married into the Jiang family, a less prestigious match.

Now, seeing the illegitimate daughter seated below Penelope, someone immediately flattered.

"In my opinion, Miss Penelope grew up with the brothers. Their bond is deep. No love child can compare."

"Exactly! The seating was arranged by Ethan Valentine. His attitude is clear."

"But is she Ethan's full sister? Or Charles Valentine's full sister?"

Someone suddenly interjected.
"Couldn't she be from the third branch?"

Everyone fell silent.

The third branch only had Third Master Valentine, who was unmarried. If she were his daughter, she'd be the legitimate heiress. Her status would be higher than all seven brothers. Why would she be sitting here?

The speaker laughed at herself.
"Just talking nonsense. How could that be!"

Evelyn continued eating,懒得理会 these无聊的议论.

If Third Master Valentine had specifically called her back just to humiliate her, then perhaps this recognition wasn't worth it.

As she thought this, the light beside her dimmed.

Penelope stood next to her, leaning down to whisper in her ear.
"Evelyn, so what if you're the biological one? The brothers and the guests only recognize me as the Miss Valentine! You're just a divorced, used woman. You're nothing!"

He walked directly to Evelyn.
"Why are you sitting here?"

###Chapter 404

Mr. Valentine’s words sent shockwaves through the room.

The dining hall fell silent.

All eyes turned to Evelyn Ross.

The young socialites who had mocked her were stunned.

They couldn't believe it.

Ethan Valentine’s complaint died in his throat.

He stood frozen.

The surrounding guests gave him exasperated looks.

Someone nearby couldn't help but ask, "Mr. Valentine, is she your biological daughter or god-daughter?"

Mr. Valentine smiled warmly. "She is my flesh and blood."

His gaze was tender. "I loved her mother once. We separated for various reasons. I never knew she existed all these years. Thankfully, we found each other now."

Evelyn’s eyes swept toward Ethan.

Ethan stood rigidly in place.

Meeting her amused gaze, he swallowed hard.

He was finished.

She wasn't his uncle's mistress.

She was his cousin!

The sister they had longed for all these years!

Ethan remembered her explanation at the Nan residence.

He wanted to slap himself.

If his uncle found out he had given her trouble…

His legs would be broken!

Evelyn spoke suddenly. "I was sitting in the wrong place."

Ethan was startled.

Was she helping him?

Mr. Valentine smiled knowingly. "The head table is over here."

He led Evelyn toward the seats of honor.

As she passed Ethan, Evelyn paused.

She smiled slightly. "Third Brother, may I sit at the head table?"

Ethan’s scalp prickled.

Evelyn took her seat with grace and poise.

Guests began murmuring amongst themselves.

"Is she really Mr. Valentine’s daughter?"

"Ethan just made her sit at the third table…"

Someone pulled Penelope Valentine aside. "Your brothers don't like her?"

Penelope’s eyes darted. "Yes, my brothers really don't care for her…"

"She's my real cousin!"

He rushed to Evelyn. "Cousin, it was all a misunderstanding! Here's my allowance money. Take it!"

A card appeared in Evelyn’s hand.

Ethan awkwardly approached as well.

He looked at her as if she were a precious treasure.

"Little cousin…" He scratched his head. "You want to learn Cortez Sect martial arts, right? I'll take you to the inner sect tomorrow! If the sect leader won't accept you, I'll make my master do it!"

It was both an apology and a peace offering.

Evelyn thought of their initial misunderstanding.

This third brother wasn't a bad person.

She nodded. "Thank you, Third Brother."

Ethan breathed a sigh of relief.

The other Valentine brothers crowded around.

The second brother gifted her a hospital. "For convenient medical care."

The fifth brother gave her an entertainment company, complete with a famous actor.

The sixth brother presented a jewelry store.

The gifts grew more extravagant.

The guests watched in astonishment.

The girls at the third table were green with envy.

Someone tugged Penelope’s sleeve. "And you said they didn't like her?"

Penelope’s face was stormy.

Weren't they just disliking her?

What changed?

Where did she miscalculate?

She clenched her fists tightly.

These gifts were worth more than her entire dowry!

The Valentines were so biased!

She looked at Ethan. "Third Brother, didn't you say she kept a boy toy?"

Ethan raised an eyebrow. "So what if she does? He can marry into the family. Saves her from hardship after marriage."

Penelope could hardly believe her ears.

That wasn't what he said earlier!

The guests understood perfectly now how the Valentines valued their daughter.

Penelope felt all eyes on her.

With the real daughter back, her status as the adopted one was awkward.

Not wanting the attention, she suddenly looked at Mina Sherwood.

"Sister-in-law, will Grace be moving back to the Valentine estate? Will you be returning too?"

This successfully diverted everyone's focus.

All eyes turned to Mina.

"That was just the Valentines saving face! I heard… Mina’s child isn't Marcus’s!"

"Really? No wonder they had a falling out…"

Mina instinctively protected her stomach.

Three more days until the third month.

Then she could have the amniocentesis…

She looked at Marcus Valentine beside her.
###Chapter 405

###Chapter 406

News of the Xu and Vance alliance through marriage had long spread throughout New York.

Rumors had circulated years ago, with everyone assuming Fiona Valentine would marry into the Vance family.

Unfortunately, the Vances never gave their approval, so the engagement was shelved.

The Vances were the wealthiest family in San Francisco. Their assets were no less than the Xus, perhaps even greater!

This incident should divert attention, allowing his daughter to escape this crisis.

Xu Chong, furious, moved to cover her mouth.

The Third Master spoke coldly. "Let her speak!"

He picked up his wine glass, his gaze icy. "I want to hear exactly how my daughter is 'disgraceful'!"

A chill ran down Xu Chong's spine.

He knew this daughter was beyond saving now…

He bowed his head dejectedly, wiping cold sweat from his brow.

Evelyn listened to these words, her expression calm.

She glanced at the Third Master. The cold aura around him intensified, a murderous glint flashing in his eyes.
###Chapter 407

Kelsey Lopez was all sweetness before the divorce.
Afterward, her true colors showed.

She fought with Mrs. Davis constantly.

Grace did all the housework.
She never had to worry about the child.

Kelsey withheld their allowance.

Elder Valentine saw everything.
