###Chapter 408

Zack Davis stared intently at Evelyn Ross.

Unemployed for days, he had been drowning his sorrows in alcohol, looking utterly disheveled.

Upon learning that NanTech Ltd. had miraculously recovered and even partnered with the Ashcroft and Sherwood families, he had repeatedly tried to reconcile.

Unfortunately, security always stopped him at the gate.

A few times, he had sneaked into the neighborhood late at night, only to be knocked unconscious and thrown out just as he approached Grace's residence.

Fortunately, the weather had turned warmer, or he would have frozen to death on the streets.

From that moment, he realized Grace had someone incredibly powerful backing her.

Until a few days ago, when someone approached him, claiming Grace was actually a love child of the Ashcroft family!

He was speechless with shock.

How was that possible?

Wasn't she an orphan? How did she suddenly become a wealthy heiress?

The person gave him an address and told him to see for himself today.

He and his mother were quietly led into the banquet by a waiter.

Finally, he saw Grace again.

She was still radiant and stunningly beautiful, her face so captivating it made him regret his past actions deeply.

How could he have ever found such a woman boring and been swayed by wildflowers outside?

He must have been completely blind!

So when his mother rushed forward, he didn't stop her. He wanted to reconcile too.

Unexpectedly, before his mother could even touch Grace's clothes, bodyguards suddenly appeared and restrained her.

Two bodyguards stared coldly at him, as if daring him to make a move.

These intimidating bodyguards, whom he wouldn't have dared to look at in the past, were now protecting Grace.

Zack forced an ingratiating smile. "Grace, it's me. Let me come over and talk, okay?"

Evelyn only responded with a cold laugh.

Zack's mother shouted again, "Grace, you can't abandon me! I'm starving and freezing at Kelsey Lopez's place. I'm your mother!"

Evelyn replied coldly, "I'm divorced from Zack. What kind of mother are you to me?"

Mrs. Davis immediately shrieked, "Now that you've climbed the social ladder, you deny us? Then we'll find Chloe. I'm her grandmother, and this is her father. She has an obligation to support us!"

Evelyn found this woman utterly shameless.

She sneered, "Chloe is only three years old. You want her to support you? Fine. Live until she's eighteen and can support herself first!"

Mrs. Davis trembled with rage.

She struggled against the bodyguards. "Grace, don't be ungrateful! Tell them to let us go immediately and allow us to join the banquet!"

She then shouted at Mr. Valentine, "In-law, we're family!"

Mr. Valentine: "..."

He found this utterly humiliating.

He didn't know how Grace had endured these two in the past.

He lowered his eyes to conceal his fury.

The real Grace was already dead. He didn't know the exact details.

But these scumbags had bullied his deceased daughter!

They had even made her live such a卑微 life.

They were still Chloe's grandmother and father. They would try to drain every last drop from Chloe in the future.

In that case, they didn't need to live beyond today.

A flash of murderous intent crossed Mr. Valentine's eyes.

The Davis母子, however, couldn't read the room. They thought this important figure was tacitly approving their actions.

Zack immediately said to Evelyn, "Grace, I know I was wrong. I'll break up with Kelsey Lopez. Let me come home!"

Evelyn found it laughable.

Penelope Valentine spoke softly, "Grace, they are, after all, Chloe's—"

"Shut up!"

Ethan Valentine and Charles Valentine both jumped up and glared at her angrily.

Penelope immediately pretended to be wronged. "Third Brother, Seventh Brother, I just believe in reconciling couples rather than separating them. It's better to preserve a marriage than destroy it—"

"That applies to others. Does this scumbag deserve it?"

Ethan Valentine interrupted sharply.

His cousin had finally come to her senses. How could she jump back into that fire?

Penelope didn't dare say more, afraid of being too obvious and getting caught.

Fiona Valentine, however, sneered, "Grace, Zack just made a mistake any man could make. If he cheated, you should look at yourself for reasons. Were you not good enough? Did you not keep your husband in check?"

Evelyn: "..."

Zack fell to his knees with a thud. "Grace, I truly know I was wrong. Please forgive me! Let our family of three live happily like before. I promise I'll treat you and Chloe well after we remarry..."

A faint glimmer flashed in Evelyn's eyes.

Sister, do you hear this?

This scumbag admits his mistake.

If it were you, would you forgive him?

Evelyn suddenly smiled, feeling a sense of release.

Grace wouldn't.

She was never a love-struck fool. She only used humility to disguise herself, trading it for freedom.

Evelyn uttered only one word: "Go!"

Mr. Valentine waved his hand. The bodyguards immediately stepped forward to drag Zack and his mother away.

The two screamed frantically:

"Grace, if you drive us away, who do you think will marry you?"

"Hahaha, everyone here, listen well! Grace is used goods I've slept with. I'm such trash. Are you really going to take my leftovers?"

Zack had completely lost his mind, causing a scene regardless of the occasion.

He knew that if Grace remarried, he would have no chance left.

By acting despicable now, he could still manipulate Chloe later.

Fiona Valentine, her face still swollen, also sneered, "Grace, you should just remarry him! No one else will want you except Zack!"

As soon as she finished speaking, a clear, resonant voice suddenly came from the entrance: "Who says no one will marry Miss Ashcroft?!"

The Vance family's dowry procession had finally arrived!
###Chapter 409

Everyone turned their heads in unison.

The Vance family's dowry gifts were carried in one by one.

A dozen bodyguards in black suits.

Each carried a heavy case.

The cases were opened.

Brilliant jewels glittered dazzlingly.

Property deeds were neatly arranged.

Each item was worth a fortune.

The elite guests widened their eyes.

They couldn't help but gasp.

"The Vance family is truly extravagant!"

"This dowry is incredibly generous!"

People craned their necks to look behind.

They all wanted to catch a glimpse of Mr. Vance himself.

At the end of the dowry procession.

A man in a suit stepped forward.

He was tall and well-built.

With a sunny, handsome face.

A roguish smile played on his lips.

"Is this Mr. Vance?"

"He looks quite young…"

"Doesn't quite match the legends…"

Evelyn's mouth twitched.

The person who arrived wasn't Alexander Vance at all.

It was Thomas Watson.

Such a grand display.

Yet the man himself didn't show up?

She pursed her lips.

Thomas walked up to Third Master Valentine.

He bowed respectfully. "Third Master."

"Mr. Vance is occupied with urgent matters."

"He specifically sent me to deliver the dowry."

Third Master Valentine glanced at Evelyn.

He knew exactly what Alexander was busy with.

Being Evelyn's chauffeur.

His lip twitched.

He said calmly, "How thoughtful."

"Marrying a Valentine daughter requires sincere effort."

Thomas smiled ingratiatingly.

He winked at Evelyn.

Evelyn rolled her eyes in response.

Thomas grinned even wider.

He was genuinely happy.

When Evelyn was presumed dead, Mr. Vance almost followed.

Thankfully, Madame was still alive.

The surrounding crowd finally understood.

So this wasn't Mr. Vance.

Just his assistant.

The dowry gifts were displayed one by one.

The guests stopped eating.

They crowded around for a closer look.

Someone called out to Penelope Valentine. "Miss Valentine, come see your dowry!"

Penelope blushed and stood up.

She felt she had finally regained her footing.

Earlier, when Grace was recognized, her seven brothers gave extravagant gifts.

Now the Vance family's dowry surpassed all of those combined.

"My God! Fifteen percent of Vance Group shares!"

"Mr. Vance is truly sincere in his proposal!"

"Though he's been married before, he treats Miss Valentine so well!"

Penelope was stunned by the shares.

She never expected Alexander to be so determined.

When the Vance family didn't respond earlier, she thought the engagement was off.

Now she couldn't help but smile.

People around her rushed to flatter:

"This dowry is absolutely unmatched in New York!"

"The Valentine family's bridal gifts must be equally impressive."

"Miss Valentine benefits the most!"

A daughter of the Xu branch family sneered at Grace. "See? This is how the Valentine family marries off daughters! What did Zack Davis give you when you married?"

Penelope pretended to be kind. "Don't say that to Grace."

"She was treated poorly because she wasn't recognized back then."

"Third Uncle, if Grace remarries, we must provide a proper dowry…"

Evelyn scoffed.

Third Master Valentine raised an eyebrow.

Marcus Valentine detected the hypocrisy.

But Ethan Valentine took it seriously. "Yes, yes! We must set up assets for Grace!"

Charles Valentine nodded. "Sister, don't reconcile with him. There are plenty of good men in New York. We'll contribute to your trousseau!"

Evelyn was speechless.

Penelope said insincerely, "Grace, why aren't you speaking? Are you unhappy? How about I share half the Vance dowry with you…"

The Xu branch daughter immediately objected. "This is the Vance dowry! How can it be shared? Miss Penelope, you spoil her too much!"

Thomas also frowned. "What right do you have to share half?"

Penelope explained. "My sister just returned to the family. I'm giving her a welcome gift…"

Thomas laughed scornfully. "Then give from your own possessions! Sharing from Miss Grace's dowry? That's just taking credit for someone else's generosity!"

Penelope froze. "What do you mean… Miss Grace's dowry?"

The Xu branch daughter pointed at the dowry. "Isn't this the engagement gift for the Valentine family? The unmarried Valentine daughter is Penelope!"

Thomas was bewildered.

He pointed at Evelyn. "You've misunderstood. The Vance family is proposing to Miss Evelyn Ross!"

After Grace returned to her roots, she was called Evelyn Ross.

Everyone understood now.

Penelope's voice turned shrill. "What did you say?"

The Xu branch daughter couldn't believe it. "Grace is married and has a daughter! You've made a mistake!"

Thomas finally understood.

He glanced at Evelyn.

Now he knew why his boss made such a grand proposal.

He was here to secure Madame's position.

He sneered. "Listen carefully!"

"Mr. Vance is also remarried!"

"He and Miss Evelyn Ross are a perfect match!"

"Today, he specifically came to propose!"

"The position of Madame Vance belongs only to Third Master's biological daughter!"

"What right does an adopted daughter have to marry Mr. Vance?"
###Chapter 410

Thomas Watson's voice echoed through the entire banquet hall.

Everyone stared in shock, their eyes wide.

William Ashcroft's daughter thought she must have misheard.

Penelope Valentine was also stunned. The situation had developed completely beyond her expectations.

How could this be?

She looked at Evelyn Ross in disbelief. How could Alexander Vance be interested in this discarded woman?

Was it just because she was the legitimate daughter?

She clenched her fists tightly, her nails digging deep into her palms.

After a brief silence, the crowd immediately erupted into heated discussion.

Those who had previously surrounded her now all flocked to Evelyn Ross, their faces filled with ingratiating smiles.

"Miss Ross, congratulations!"

"Mr. Vance values you so much, proposing at the family reunion gala. A double celebration!"

"Congratulations!"

Charles Valentine agreed. "Exactly. If he ever dares to bully you, we seven will back you up!"

Marcus Valentine glared at them. "Keep your comments to yourselves."

Ethan Valentine glanced secretly at Evelyn Ross, hesitating to speak.

He wanted to advise his sister to break up with that boy toy.

After all, her fiancé was Alexander Vance now. Keeping a boy toy was really inappropriate.

But now was not the time. He would remind her privately later.

Evelyn Ross looked at the seven brothers, a slight smile touching her lips.

Although she didn't need their protection, having family felt... not bad.

Penelope Valentine was pushed aside. She wanted to step forward but couldn't squeeze in, forced to stand resentfully on the periphery.

She took a deep breath and suddenly stepped forward, approaching Evelyn Ross with a concerned expression. "Sister, you're engaged to Mr. Vance now. What about that boyfriend of yours?"

The room instantly fell silent.

Someone asked curiously, "What boyfriend?"

Ethan Valentine urgently signaled Penelope Valentine with his eyes, telling her not to speak nonsense.

Penelope Valentine pretended not to see, continuing with worry. "That college student from the last banquet. Exceptionally handsome, better looking than a celebrity. But... sister, shouldn't you break up with him first?"

Ethan Valentine became anxious, quickly looking at Thomas Watson, wanting to explain something.

William Ashcroft's daughter shouted loudly, "Yes! She has a boyfriend! Assistant Watson, tell Mr. Vance not to be deceived by her! She keeps a boy toy after her divorce. How can such a promiscuous woman become Mrs. Vance?"

Ethan Valentine immediately retorted, "What nonsense are you talking? That's just my sister's driver! Definitely not a boy toy!"

Evelyn Ross watched him lie through his teeth, barely suppressing a laugh.

A few days ago, he had stormed in angrily demanding she break up. Now he was speaking up for her in public.

This brother, though previously annoying, was quite cute now.

William Ashcroft's daughter sneered, "What driver? She admitted he was her boyfriend at the last banquet! Assistant Watson, don't be fooled!"

Hearing this, Thomas Watson's face darkened immediately.

How dare these people call the boss a boy toy?!

Seeing his unpleasant expression, Penelope Valentine felt the situation was secured.

This marriage would definitely not happen!

No man could tolerate his fiancée fooling around outside.

But just as she thought this, she heard Thomas Watson sigh and slowly say, "Mr. Vance knows about this."

Everyone: "?"

Thomas Watson continued, "But he doesn't mind. As long as Miss Ross is willing to marry him."

Everyone: "???"

Did they hear correctly?

Was the world turning surreal?

The richest man in San Francisco, the head of the Vance family, the decisive and ruthless Alexander Vance, was actually a lovesick fool?

For Evelyn, he would go to such lengths?

William Ashcroft's daughter couldn't help asking, "Why? Just because she is the Third Master's biological daughter? I've seen many arranged marriages among the wealthy, but never one so undignified!"

Wealthy marriages are mostly based on利益结合.

Many couples play around separately.

But at least they maintain superficial harmony, not flaunting their lovers openly.

Evelyn's boyfriend was known to everyone. How could a man of Alexander Vance's status not mind?

Thomas Watson raised his head, spouting nonsense with a straight face. "Of course not. Mr. Vance fell in love with Miss Ross at first sight, vowing to marry no one but her. Even if Miss Ross has someone else in her heart, Mr. Vance doesn't mind two men serving one woman! Mr. Vance admires Miss Ross immensely, deeply in love, his affection as deep as the sea, his love..."

Evelyn Ross shot Thomas Watson a look, telling him to shut up quickly.

Thomas Watson immediately stopped, summarizing, "In short, Mr. Vance recognizes only Miss Evelyn Ross!"

Everyone: "..."

All were stunned.

They must have attended the banquet the wrong way tonight.

Ethan Valentine believed it, examining Evelyn. "Little sister is quite good-looking, but not enough to make a man so obsessed, right?"

Charles Valentine was also bewildered. "If I were so lovesick, Third Uncle would definitely break my legs!"

Only Marcus Valentine couldn't help rolling his eyes.

That scheming Alexander Vance really had a theatrical secretary.

But these words made his sister look good. He was satisfied.

Even the Third Master couldn't help twitching the corner of his mouth.

After a long pause, people began to awkwardly force compliments.

"Mr., Mr. Vance is truly a romantic."

"Miss, Miss Ross is impressive..."

"Heh, tonight truly witnesses a match made in heaven!"

"..."

Amid the forced compliments, William Ashcroft's daughter realized she had caused trouble. She glanced at Penelope Valentine and tried to sneak away quietly.

Just as she was about to slip out of the banquet hall, a cold voice suddenly rang out. "Where do you think you're going?"
###Chapter 411

Xu Chong's daughter spun around.

Xu Chiye stood behind her.

His gaze was icy.

A cold smirk played on his lips.

Xu Chong's daughter stumbled backward in fear.

"I... I need to go..."

He pointed at Zhang Hao and his mother.

"You brought them here, didn't you?"

"If you're leaving, take them with you."

His eyes burned with anger.

His daughter frantically waved her hands.

"It wasn't me..."

A server stepped forward with a camera.

The video clearly showed it.

Xu Chong's daughter bribing the server.

She led Zhang Hao and his mother in through the staff entrance.

"She told me to do it!"

Without proof.

He couldn't move against Xu Panjiao yet.

Xu Chiye frowned.

"Panjiao, I believe you."

He turned to Xu Chong.

"Uncle Xu, your daughter plotted against the Xu family like this."

"Does she have no respect for Third Uncle?"

Cold sweat dripped down Xu Chong's forehead.

He knew the situation was grave.

He rushed to his daughter.

And slapped her hard across the face.

"Shut up!"

The slap was vicious.

The girl spat out two bloody teeth.

Xu Chong bowed to Mr.Valentine.

"Third Master, I failed to teach my daughter properly."

"I will send her abroad immediately for... experience."

Mr.Valentine rubbed his jade thumb ring.

His tone was flat.

"She is still young. Experience is indeed needed."

"GMR is a good choice. She can study there."

Xu Chong's pupils constricted.

GMR was a war zone.

Sending her there was a death sentence.

But he didn't dare object.

"You are absolutely right."

"I will arrange the paperwork immediately."

Mr.Valentine smiled faintly.

"I will have someone assist with the visa."

A chill ran down Xu Chong's spine.

This cut off all his escape routes.

He dragged his daughter away hurriedly.

Mr.Valentine looked at Zhang Hao and his mother.

His voice turned frigid.

"Since you are family to Zhizhi."

"The Xu family will extend you every hospitality."

Zhang Hao wet his pants in fear.

His mother, however, beamed with joy.

"That's more like it, we're all family now..."

They were led away.

Mr.Valentine raised his glass.

"Everyone, a toast to my daughter's return."

The hall fell silent.

No one dared utter another word of dissent.

Evelyn Ross looked at Mr.Valentine.

Her expression was complex.

This man.

He held the power of life and death so effortlessly.

The banquet concluded smoothly.

Guests began to depart.

Xu Chiye ran up to Evelyn.

"Little sister, ride home with me?"

Evelyn shook her head.

"I'm returning to the Nan residence."

Xu Chiye said urgently.

"Cortez Sect matters?"

"I'll take you to meet the Sect Leader tomorrow."

"Alright."

Elsewhere.

Marcus Valentine looked at Mina Sherwood.

"Still not coming home tonight?"

Mina lowered her eyes.

"I'll return after the baby is three months old."

"How many days left?"

"Three or four."

Mina looked up.

"Marcus, see you at the hospital in four days."
###Chapter 412

Marcus Valentine's lips curled into a bitter smile at her words.

He truly couldn't understand it.

Why was Mina Sherwood so insistent on having an amniocentesis?

Three separate test results all showed oligospermia.

He couldn't possibly father a child…

Evelyn Ross was preparing to leave.

Mr. Valentine instructed his seven sons to escort her and Mina to the parking lot.

On the way to the parking lot.

Charles Valentine sidled up to Evelyn. "Little sister, want to know what happened to Zack Davis and his mother?"

Evelyn glanced at him.

This was the youngest Valentine boy, the seventh son.

He had been calling her 'little sister' repeatedly.

Evelyn hadn't corrected him before.

But now…

Evelyn cleared her throat softly. "How old are you?"

Charles grinned. "Little sister, I'm almost twenty-one. I study at Harvard~"

Harvard was a top-tier institution.

Wealthy heirs might buy their way into schools abroad.

But Harvard required genuine academic excellence.

Charles getting in proved he was truly a top student.

Evelyn's mouth twitched. "I'm almost twenty-three."

Charles still didn't catch on. "Oh, and? Does little sister want a birthday gift?"

Evelyn was speechless.

She pressed her lips together. "I mean, you should call me older sister."

Charles froze.

He was stunned!

Evelyn looked at the sixth Valentine brother. "How old are you?"

The sixth brother smiled. "I'm older than you. Twenty-three years and one month!"

Evelyn understood. "So I have six older brothers and one younger brother."

Charles' eyes instantly reddened.

He thought he finally had a younger sister.

Turns out this 'little sister' was actually an older cousin!

Wah… He wasn't happy!

But it didn't dampen his enthusiasm for Evelyn.

He leaned in closer. "Older cousin sister, want to know what happened to Zack Davis and his mother?"

Evelyn: …What kind of weird address was that?

He had the face of an innocent young man.

His features, similar to Marcus and Ethan Valentine, held a certain naivety.

His eyes held the clear simplicity unique to college students.

He was quite pleasant to look at.

She asked, "What happened to them?"

Charles chuckled. "Just heard Uncle order the bodyguards to deliver them to a pyramid scheme organization. That group specializes in human trafficking, sending people to Eastern countries. Their fate from now on is to have their organs harvested!"

Evelyn fell silent.

She couldn't help but twitch her mouth. "Our family has that kind of business?"

Ethan immediately explained. "Of course not! Don't misunderstand our family! Our family has extensive connections, but everything else is completely legal!"

Evelyn nodded in relief.

After all, this was the United States!

She didn't notice Ethan's slight cough of guilt after saying 'legal,' or the mocking glance Marcus shot his way.

Ethan caught his older brother's sarcastic look and coughed again to cover his unease.

They chatted as they walked to the parking lot.

The car Evelyn drove in New York was a black Chery.

A domestic brand, fitting for Evelyn's economic situation.

Charles noticed the car and immediately said, "Older cousin sister, let me get you a better car! This piece of junk isn't worthy of you!"

Evelyn smiled. "I'm used to it. It's fine."

This car had long been modified by Alexander Vance.

The engine was replaced.

The windows were swapped for bulletproof glass.

The metal body was replaced with aircraft-grade composite material.

All changes Alexander made fearing she might encounter danger again…

It looked modest, but its performance and safety far exceeded that of a regular car.

Charles wanted to say more.

Ethan lowered his voice. "Little sister, Mr. Vance made his stance clear today, but don't push it. Your boy toy is good-looking, but you need to consider Mr. Vance. A man like that is true talent. This kind of boy toy who latches onto a wealthy woman right after graduation only cares about your money. Don't get fooled emotionally!"

Evelyn was speechless.

Since when did Alexander Vance resemble a freshly graduated college student?!

He was twenty-eight!!

She twitched her mouth, nodded goodbye to her seven brothers, and got into the car with Mina.

Evelyn took the passenger seat.

Mina sat in the back, never once looking at Marcus Valentine.

The car started.

Mina sighed, not wanting her worries to affect Evelyn's good mood, and made small talk. "Grace, what's really going on with Alexander Vance? Are you really going to marry that secondhand older man?"

Evelyn was silent.

She glanced at Alexander.

The man looked ahead, a sharp light flashing in his deep eyes.

She cleared her throat and looked at Mina. "Actually, Alexander is quite good."

Mina frowned. "He is good, his family background is impeccable. But you have to choose between the boy toy and Alexander Vance, right?"

Hearing this, Alexander glanced sideways at Evelyn out of the corner of his eye.

The woman blinked and answered slowly, "Children make choices. Adults, like me, want it all!"

Mina was shocked!

Alexander's lips curved into a slight smile.

He remembered when he first met Evelyn Ross, she found his grandmother and called to say, "I have your elderly relative…"

He thought then that this girl was really cheeky!

Now she was regaining some of that playfulness, no longer so burdened.

The Valentine family returned home.

Ethan hung his head, gloomy. "Uncle, big brother, little sister refuses to move back. Does she hate me?"

Marcus couldn't be bothered with him.

Mr. Valentine also remained silent.

Charles comforted him. "I think it's okay. Older cousin sister had a smile in her eyes when she talked to you."

"Really?" Ethan's eyes lit up, then he wondered, "Then why won't she come back? Is there someone she dislikes in the family?"

As soon as he said it, everyone paused and looked at Penelope Valentine.

Penelope: "…"

Under the gaze of her seven brothers, she felt extremely uncomfortable and quickly said, "Before I knew Grace's identity, I did have conflicts with her, but we've made up…"

"Really?" Ethan stroked his chin. "Did little sister forgive you?"

Penelope: "…"

She twitched her mouth, lowering her head to hide her hatred.

Her seven brothers were definitely doing this on purpose!

They were forcing her to apologize to Evelyn.

She sighed and suddenly said, "It's my fault. If she won't come home because of me, then I'll move out first…"

She pretended to be on the verge of tears, slowly looking up.

She expected to be persuaded to stay.

Instead, what greeted her was…
###Chapter 413

###Chapter 414

Ethan Valentine kept chattering nonstop.

"Little sister, those three moves you used to dodge me last time were impressive... Of course, I was going easy on you! I'll teach you properly another day and test your real skills..."

"Oh, my fiancée Kiki Cortez asked to meet today. Perfect chance to introduce you. We'll all be family soon!"

"Just to be clear, I'm not marrying into her family! The Valentines aren't so poor that our sons need to be taken in... It's just the Cortez family lacks capable fighters in this generation, alas!"

"Not that they have no people. Kiki has plenty of siblings and cousins. But too few are willing to endure the hardship of martial arts training... Young people these days lack patience!"

"Like me, sent here by Third Uncle at age three, training hard every day... First Senior Sister is even more amazing. She only started at fifteen, two hours a day, sometimes less... She's truly remarkable!"

Evelyn Ross listened quietly.

This third brother was practically a chatterbox machine.

His mouth hadn't stopped since they met.

They entered the Cortez compound, heading towards the rear courtyards.

A slender girl suddenly rushed over from the outer disciples' rest area.

The girl grabbed Evelyn's arm.

Evelyn froze.

The girl urgently made hand gestures.

Realizing something, she quickly pulled out a notepad and wrote.

Her handwriting was neat and elegant.

"Hello, I'm Jane Evans, Justin's sister. My brother was beaten by Jason. He's bedridden now. He asked me to warn you to be careful of Jason's revenge."

Evelyn looked at the girl.

Her features were delicate, like a fragile little white flower.

She couldn't speak?

Evelyn pressed her lips together, taking the pen and paper to write.

The girl snatched back the notepad, shaking her head and pointing to her own ears.

Indicating she could hear.

Evelyn spoke. "Did your brother go to the hospital? Is he badly hurt?"

The girl wrote quickly. "Yes. His leg is broken. He needs bed rest for a month."

Evelyn asked another question. "Do you have enough for the medical bills?"

The girl nodded vigorously, waving to leave.

Evelyn stopped her. "Give me your address. I want to visit Justin."

Justin got beaten because he stood up for Evelyn.

She felt responsible.

The girl wrote down the address, tore off the paper, and handed it over.

She gave Evelyn a sweet smile, revealing two dimples.

Then she turned and ran off.

Evelyn tucked away the address, looking at Ethan Valentine.

She was about to speak when a sharp cry rang out.

"Grace Nan!"

A brisk girl strode over quickly.

"Come on, let's fight!"

Evelyn was stunned.

Ethan spoke up urgently. "Kiki? Perfect timing, let me introdu—"

Jason Evans suddenly jumped out. "Senior Sister Cortez! See? I told you! Second Senior Brother is bewitched by Grace Nan! She uses his backing to bully everyone in the outer sect! You must uphold justice for us!"

Kiki Cortez glared furiously at Ethan Valentine.

Her eyes instantly reddened.

"You said you only liked me!"

Ethan was frantic. "Let me explain, she's—"

"I don't care!" Kiki turned to Evelyn. "Settle martial matters with martial ways! Whoever wins today gets Ethan Valentine!"

Her eyes were red, full of both grievance and stubbornness.

Evelyn was speechless.

Someone actually wanted this silly third brother?

She thought her brother was marrying above his station into the Cortez family.

Turns out they had genuine feelings.

So easily swayed by provocation...

Were the Cortez family's standards for raising children too low?

Her lip twitched. She wanted to explain.

But Kiki had already thrown down her whip and unwrapped the bandages on her legs.

"You're an outer disciple. I'll give you three free moves. No weapons, your choice! A fair fight!"

Evelyn waved her hand. "I am—"

"Take this!"

Kiki was impatient and didn't listen.

Her fist shot straight towards Evelyn's face.

Evelyn stepped back, not yet moving.

Ethan Valentine rushed forward and caught Kiki's fist.

Kiki froze.

Jason shouted again. "Senior Sister Cortez! He protects Grace Nan right in front of you! Who would believe there's nothing between them!"

Tears fell from Kiki's eyes with a patter.

"Ethan Valentine! You raised your hand against me for her!"

Ethan panicked, reaching to wipe her tears. "No, she is my—"

"Move! I will duel her! If I win, you must come back to me!"

Kiki pushed him away, throwing another punch at Evelyn.

Jason Evans crossed his arms, enjoying the show.

"Senior Sister Cortez, teach Grace Nan a hard lesson! She flirts with everyone in the Cortez sect, using her looks to bewitch Second Senior Brother! Show her what happens!"

Kiki suddenly turned, kicking up the whip from the ground.

Evelyn frowned.

Jason laughed triumphantly. "Yes! Use the whip! No mercy for this kind of tramp!"

The whip cracked through the air.

It landed hard on Jason's body!

Jason screamed and jumped.

Kiki gave him a cold look.

"Whatever is between Grace Nan and Ethan Valentine, it takes two to tango! Don't you dare talk about a woman like that!"

She looked at Evelyn with tear-filled eyes.

Evelyn spoke calmly. "Don't get excited. I'm his sister."
###Chapter 415

Cortez Kiki froze.

Tears welled in her eyes.

Her entire body went rigid.

She swallowed hard.

She looked at Ethan Valentine.

Ethan immediately nodded. "I told you yesterday. My third uncle has a daughter. It's her. This is my sister!"

Cortez Kiki fell completely silent.

She was utterly petrified.

She looked at Evelyn Ross again.

Suddenly, she spoke. "Impossible. I don't believe it!"

Ethan grew anxious.

He wanted to explain further.

But Cortez Kiki spoke again. "How could a rough man like you have such a stunningly beautiful sister?!"

Ethan was baffled.

Evelyn was equally confused.

She was slightly taken aback.

Cortez Kiki stole a glance at her.

Her lively eyes held a mischievous and ingratiating glint.

Evelyn understood.

Cortez Kiki was trying to save face.

She chuckled. "I really am his sister."

Cortez Kiki declared, "Then Ethan must have had a genetic mutation."

She put away her whip.

She hurried straight to Evelyn.

She linked arms with her. "You're Grace, right? I felt threatened because you're so beautiful. Please don't mind me, okay?"

There was nothing to mind!

Cortez Kiki had been very measured earlier.

Knowing she was an outer disciple, she had conceded many points.

When Jason Evans insulted her, Cortez Kiki had defended her.

She said it takes two to tango.

Combined with her playful demeanor now.

Evelyn couldn't bring herself to dislike her.

She smiled. "Hello, future sister-in-law."

The term "future sister-in-law" made Cortez Kiki's cheeks flush pink.

She shyly turned her head away.

She fumbled at her waist.

She pulled out a dagger and offered it. "A gift for meeting you, little sister!"

Evelyn accepted the dagger. "Thank you."

Ethan couldn't help exclaiming, "Kiki, isn't that the dagger Master gave you? It's incredibly sharp! I've coveted it for years, and you never gave it to me. Now you're giving it to my sister?"

Cortez Kiki glared at him. "You're big and brawny. A club suits you fine. Our sister is delicate. A dagger is perfect for self-defense. Why are you competing?"

Ethan grumbled, "...Fine."

The three of them started chatting comfortably.

Jason Evans suddenly gasped. "Grace, you're the daughter Mr. Xu the Third recognized and brought home yesterday?"

The family recognition banquet yesterday only invited Xu family relatives.

It wasn't a grand public affair.

But Alexander Vance's delivery of betrothal gifts and his speech at the banquet.

Had become a hot topic of conversation in New York.

Jason had come to the Cortez Sect early today to cause trouble for Grace.

He hadn't clearly heard who the daughter was.

He only knew such a person existed.

Now he understood.

This seemingly soft person was actually Mr. Xu the Third's daughter!

No wonder she dared to charge into the house and beat Justin Evans!

Justin was the third young master of the Evans family's main line.

The most beloved youngest son of the Evans family matriarch!!

Panic seized Jason.

Ethan looked at him. "Yes, remember this. Grace is the eldest daughter of our Xu family's main line. Try bullying her again in the future!!"

He grabbed Cortez Kiki's hand. "Kiki, let's go find Master. Master agreed to accept my sister as a registered disciple!"

Cortez Kiki's eyes lit up. "Then she'll be my junior sister! Junior sister, come with us!"

Cortez Kiki held Evelyn's hand.

Ethan couldn't help feeling jealous. "Hey, your fiancé is right here!"

"Get lost, you big, brawny, smelly man! How can you compare to how sweet our sister is!"

Cortez Kiki pressed close to Evelyn.

If not for Cortez Kiki fighting desperately for Ethan earlier.

Evelyn might have suspected Cortez Kiki didn't like Ethan.

Seeing Ethan's reaction, he seemed used to it.

He scratched his head and explained. "Kiki likes pretty girls."

This kind of liking wasn't about sexual orientation.

It was simply appreciating beauty.

Evelyn expressed her understanding.

Cortez Kiki sighed. "Since childhood, I've been surrounded by a bunch of smelly men. Training martial arts every day makes bodies hard. So I like sweet, soft girls."

She added, "Little sister, you're so soft. I really like it."

She gently squeezed Evelyn's arm.

Evelyn was speechless.

Ethan said, "That's because our sister hasn't trained much. More training builds harder muscles. Just wait until First Senior Sister arrives. You can squeeze her arm then."

Cortez Kiki pouted. "Our sister is still sweeter. I definitely prefer our sister more."

Ethan was baffled.

He grew urgent. "Kiki, even though Evelyn is my sister, and I think so too, First Senior Sister is not to be disrespected! Don't say things like that outside in the future!"

Cortez Kiki snorted and looked at Evelyn. "Little sister, ignore him. He's a devoted fan of First Senior Sister. He talks about her every day. People who don't know might think he has a secret crush on her!"

Ethan grinned. "If it weren't for you, that might be possible. But since I have you, my feelings for First Senior Sister can only be pure admiration~"

Cortez Kiki teased, "Oh, what a pity, right? Did I ruin your great romantic destiny?"

Ethan immediately waved his hands. "No, no! First Senior Sister is not to be disrespected, I just said that! No more jokes like this!"

Cortez Kiki pouted.

But she didn't continue the joke.

She just said, "Okay, okay, I know. I admire First Senior Sister too, you know!"

Their dynamic made Evelyn marvel.

Ethan, this simpleton, seemed to listen to Cortez Kiki.

But whenever he stated a principle, Cortez Kiki would abide by it.

For example, not joking about First Senior Sister.

Cortez Kiki would shut up.

It was quite interesting.

As they talked.

They arrived at the small courtyard where Cortez Kiki's father, Ethan's master, the Cortez Sect Leader's second brother, Ethan Valentine, resided.

Ethan Valentine was ranked second in the Cortez family.

He was also ranked second in martial arts training.

After establishing his own school, he was again second.

He took a disciple who became the Cortez Sect's Second Senior Brother.

His whole life was intertwined with the number two.

He was waiting for them in his room.

Today, to welcome his beloved disciple's sister.

He had specially changed into new training clothes.

The white training jacket's short front was tucked into the belt.

It looked neat and efficient.

After they entered.

His gaze fell on Evelyn.

He was slightly taken aback.
###Chapter 416

Evelyn Ross entered and observed the current second highest master of the Cortez Sect.

Charles Cortez sat cross-legged on a meditation cushion.

His face looked remarkably young.

He didn't appear to have a daughter as old as Kiki Cortez.

He should be nearly fifty, yet he looked only in his early thirties.

Martial artists truly knew the secret to preserving youth.

She followed Ethan Valentine and Kiki Cortez inside.

She immediately felt Charles Cortez's gaze pass over the other two and land precisely on her.

Then, his eyes lit up slightly.

Ethan had said he wanted his sister to become a Registered Disciple.

Charles Cortez had been reluctant.

He wasn't as particular as the Sect Leader, who only took one personal disciple.

But his own disciples were few in number.

Each disciple slot was precious.

But Ethan was his most accomplished senior disciple.

He'd also heard this sister was William Ashcroft's daughter…

William Ashcroft had personally called him last night!

He had to give the Ashcroft family this face.

He had been somewhat reluctant.

He didn't understand why a young lady from a wealthy family would want to learn martial arts.

But when he saw Evelyn's steps as she entered, he unconsciously straightened his posture.

They were all martial artists.

Light steps were a fundamental skill.

Ordinary people had heavy footsteps when they walked.

Right now, both Ethan and Kiki's footsteps were very light.

But he didn't hear a third set of heavy footsteps.

He instinctively looked at the feet of the three.

He discovered each of Evelyn's steps was exceptionally light.

That posture… somewhat resembled the Sect Leader's.

It was like a peerless master hiding their true capabilities.

This suddenly piqued Charles Cortez's interest.

He examined Evelyn's face carefully and smiled slightly.

"I heard your name is Grace Nan?"

"You've been in the outer sect all these years?"

Evelyn nodded.

Grace Nan it was…

After her sister was recognized by the family, she would probably take the Ashcroft name too.

Charles Cortez asked again, "How long did you practice daily in the outer sect?"

Evelyn thought for a moment.

"One or two hours."

Charles Cortez frowned.

"So short?"

"Yes. I had other matters to attend to."

Evelyn replied calmly.

Charles Cortez felt somewhat disappointed.

He wondered if he had misjudged.

He then remembered this "Evelyn" seemed to have been pregnant and given birth in recent years.

This was why martial arts were harder for women than men.

After men achieved success in training, their bones and muscles were largely set.

After women gave birth, their bone seams opened, and their physique changed significantly.

Many skilled female martial artists were powerful before having children.

But after childbirth, they were no longer the same…

Of course, there were exceptions.

Some were average before having children but transformed completely afterward.

He had already inquired about this Evelyn before she came.

As a child, she had her bones assessed by Elder Xavier several times but was never chosen.

She wasn't famous for her martial arts in the outer sect either.

Those disciples who trained well were all renowned.

But Evelyn was unknown.

Could her excellent physique be due to a sudden change after childbirth?

Charles Cortez suddenly wanted to test her current martial arts level.

He immediately stood up from his cushion.

"Grace Nan, come. Let's go outside and exchange a few moves."

Upon hearing this, Ethan and Kiki immediately stepped in front of Evelyn.

They objected in unison.

"Master, didn't we agree she would just be a Registered Disciple? Why bother testing her?"

"Dad! You don't know your own strength. What if you hurt her?"

They spoke simultaneously.

They glanced at each other, then looked back at Charles Cortez.

"Master, I think Kiki is right!"

"Dad, Second Senior Brother is right!"

Charles Cortez: "…"

The corner of his mouth twitched.

He said helplessly, "Do you really distrust me so much?"

"I think Evelyn has talent in martial arts."

"Her demeanor suggests she has some foundation."

"I just can't gauge her depth, so I want to test her."

"How can I know how to teach her without testing?"

Ethan said, "She's just a Registered Disciple. No need to be so serious!"

"Exactly! Second Senior Brother is right!"

"If you train her until she's all hard and muscular, I won't like touching her anymore!"

"…"

Charles Cortez and Ethan simultaneously looked at Kiki.

She quickly covered her mouth.

She had accidentally spoken her thoughts aloud.

Charles Cortez ignored them and looked directly at Evelyn.

"Do you dare?"

Evelyn nodded.

"Alright."

Since she was going to acknowledge him as master, she should learn seriously.

But…

She suddenly spoke.

"Senior, there's something I should mention."

"I've already acknowledged a master before."

She already had a master.

So even if she acknowledged another, her first master would always come first.

Hearing this, Charles Cortez smiled.

"I knew you must have a master."

"That's fine. Martial arts are one family."

"Your master will only be happy for you knowing you've acknowledged me."

"After all, no one in the industry has a higher status than me."

"Well, except the Sect Leader."

Ethan also said, "Right. Who is your master?"

"After you acknowledge my master, your master's status in the industry will also rise."

Evelyn: "…Well, I'll tell him when I see him next time."

The key was she didn't even know her master's name!

The group walked into the courtyard as they spoke.

Charles Cortez stood in the center, tucking the hem of his robe into his waist.

He looked at Evelyn.

Evelyn assumed an offensive stance.

This stance was taught by her master.

It happened to be the most basic starting stance of the Cortez Sect.

Outer disciples had all learned it.

So no one found it unusual.

Charles Cortez smiled.

"Young lady, attack me with all your strength."

"Let me see your current level."

Evelyn nodded seriously.

"Alright."

Ethan on the side couldn't help shouting.

"Master, you must go easy on her!"

"I only have one sister!"

"My uncle only has one daughter!"

Kiki also said worriedly, "Dad, aren't you ashamed to fight a girl?"

"If you want to practice, I'll spar with you. Don't pick on her!"

Charles Cortez smiled.

"What do you know?"

"The Sect Leader will also attend today's acknowledgment ceremony."

"There are still ten minutes until the agreed time."

"Exchanging a few moves is better than sitting here waiting for him."

He waved to Evelyn.

"Come on, young lady!"

Evelyn nodded solemnly.

Since Ethan's master wanted to test her, and this was the top martial arts family in the country…

She would use her true skills!

Evelyn gathered all her strength.

She threw a powerful punch directly at Charles Cortez!
###Chapter 417

This punch contained all of Evelyn Ross's strength.

Charles Cortez opened his palm, ready to block it head-on.

A relaxed smile was on his face as he met the blow.

"Thud!"

A dull sound echoed from the impact.

Charles's expression changed instantly.

The sheer force of the punch made him sense danger.

He instinctively used his full strength to block, but was still forced back five steps.

Charles was stunned.

He looked down at his numb palm, then back at Evelyn.

Surprise and delight instantly flashed across his face.

He was about to speak when a disciple rushed in and interrupted.

"Second Uncle! Something bad has happened!"

Charles frowned. "What is it?"

The newcomer was a lower-ranked inner disciple.

He gasped for breath. "The Evans family has come to the Discipline Hall to make trouble."

"They claim the Cortez Sect is condoning an outer disciple's violent actions outside."

"They said Justin Evans, the third young master, was severely beaten!"

The disciple shot a furtive glance at Evelyn.

Charles was still processing. "Justin? That playboy?"

"If an outer disciple did it, just handle it according to sect rules."

The disciple looked helplessly at Ethan Valentine and Evelyn. "But it was Grace who beat him!"

Charles immediately turned to Evelyn. "You did this?"

Evelyn admitted frankly. "Yes."

Rumors about Mina Sherwood had to be stopped at the source.

Justin was stubborn and refused to back down, so she had to use more force.

It was only after Alexander Vance stepped in that Justin finally apologized.

She hadn't expected them to cause trouble now.

Evelyn's face was dark. "He slandered my friend, Mina Sherwood."

"Mina is now my sister-in-law, so she's family."

"The Cortez Sect rules don't say we can't fight back when outsiders bully us, right?"

Charles smiled wryly and nodded. "There is indeed no such rule."

Evelyn continued. "Bullying isn't just physical harm."

"Spreading malicious rumors is also bullying."

"What he did is known everywhere online. The evidence is solid."

"Was I wrong to hit him?"

Charles sighed. "If he were an outsider, it would be fine."

"But he is the fifth uncle's registered disciple!"

Evelyn was stunned. "What?"

Charles explained. "The five major families in New York have intermarried for generations."

"Each family sends their children to the Cortez Sect to learn martial arts."

"Outstanding ones like your third brother become inner disciples."

"Those without talent choose a direct descendant to be a registered disciple."

He shook his head helplessly. "Justin is your fifth uncle's registered disciple."

"The Cortez Sect rules state you cannot attack seniors outside of formal sparring."

"He is your senior brother. Hitting him is a violation."

Evelyn was speechless.

Did the Cortez Sect accept disciples so casually?

Ethan said urgently, "Master, as of today, my sister is your registered disciple."

"Her status is higher than Justin's. Doesn't that settle it?"

Charles shook his head. "The Cortez Sect is strict about disciple registration. It must be recorded."

"I only gave verbal approval today. It hasn't been registered yet."

"She is not an official disciple."

Cortez Sect disciples have three levels.

Outer disciples are the lowest. Registered disciples and inner disciples have similar status.

But inner disciples are the core of the Cortez Sect.

Evelyn hitting Justin did violate sect rules.

Unless she was already an inner disciple and enrolled before Justin.

But that was clearly impossible.

Ethan said anxiously, "We can't hand my sister over!"

"Second Senior Brother, just say you did it!"

He turned to Evelyn. "Sister, tell me how you hit him that day."

"I'll take all the blame later."

"As the second senior brother, disciplining a junior is justified."

Evelyn shook her head. "I did it openly."

She and Alexander Vance stormed into the club to get him. The whole thing was on camera.

Ethan gritted his teeth. "Then say I dressed as a woman and did it!"

Everyone's eyes moved between their faces.

They all showed expressions of disbelief.

There was no way anyone could mistake those two faces.

Just as Evelyn was about to speak, a sharp shout came from outside.

"Is this how the Cortez Sect fools our Evans family?"

Everyone turned to see a middle-aged woman striding in.

Justin followed beside her, his face bruised and swollen.

When he saw Evelyn, his eyes turned vicious. He looked ready to pounce.

He pointed at Evelyn and said to the woman, "Mom, she's the one who hit me!"

"She almost ruined me! You might not have grandchildren because of her!"

Mrs. Evans immediately glared at Evelyn. "You're the one who bullied my son?"

She then looked at Charles. "Charles Cortez, are you shielding this outer disciple?"

"The Cortez Sect is known for its strict rules. Do you not care about your reputation? Or the sect's?"

She sneered and looked outside. "Call your sect leader!"

"You must give me an answer today!"
###Chapter 418

Justin Evans immediately sent a disciple to fetch the sect leader.

Ethan Valentine was sweating under Mrs. Evans' stare.
He forced a smile.
"Madam, let's discuss this calmly. Please, don't be angry... By the way, is Mr. Zhao not here today?"

Mrs. Evans snorted coldly.
"He's too embarrassed to confront you all. I told him to rest at home."

She gestured with her hand.
Someone immediately brought a chair.

Mrs. Evans sat down directly.
"I'm waiting right here today. I want to see how the Cortez Sect plans to give me an explanation!"

Ethan Valentine fell silent.

He took two steps forward, trying to ease the tension.

Evelyn Ross stood to the side, frowning slightly.

This Mrs. Evans appeared to be nearly fifty, yet she remained decisive and formidable, her face filled with anger.

Evelyn looked at Ethan Valentine and asked quietly, "Is Mrs. Evans very influential in the Evans family?"

Ethan lowered his voice.
"The Evans family appears to be led by her husband, but everyone actually listens to her. Her status is practically equal to the family head. She has three sons and dotes especially on the youngest..."

Evelyn was thoughtful.

This Mrs. Evans clearly was a strong, capable woman.

This matter probably wouldn't be easily resolved today.

She lowered her gaze.

Ethan whispered again, "I heard Mrs. Evans had feelings for Third Uncle when she was young. Should we call Third Uncle over now? Use his charm?"

Evelyn was speechless.

Does your Third Uncle know how badly you want a beating?

Kiki Cortez nodded beside them.
"Good idea! Good idea!"

Evelyn thought... These two were quite the pair.

She looked away and asked again, "According to sect rules, how is this matter handled?"

Ethan answered, "According to sect rules, martial arts cultivation is to be abolished, and the person is expelled from the Cortez Sect."

Evelyn frowned.

She was about to say her martial arts weren't learned from the Cortez Sect but suddenly remembered she was currently Grace Nan.

Grace Nan was an outer disciple of the Cortez Sect and had practiced Cortez Sect martial arts.

She didn't want to implicate Ethan Valentine or damage the Cortez Sect's reputation.

Ethan added, "But don't worry, we're all part of the Five Great Families. You're Third Uncle's newly recognized daughter. Mrs. Evans wouldn't dare punish you so severely. The Cortez Sect will also consider your standing in the Valentine family before deciding any punishment!"

Ethan's tone was firm.
"At most, you'll leave the Cortez Sect. If we want a clean exit, our family has countless ways. Let's see who dares to lay a finger on you!"

Evelyn remained silent.

But she couldn't back down!

She had to be accepted as an inner disciple to secure cooperation with the Cortez family.

Ethan Valentine spoke, "...The incident at the Valentine family yesterday caused quite a stir. This is Mr. Valentine's biological daughter, just recognized after being lost for many years. Mr. Valentine dotes on her greatly and specifically called to ask me to accept her as an inner disciple. You and Mr. Valentine are old acquaintances. This is just children fooling around. Perhaps we can let this matter go?"

Mrs. Evans shot a sharp glance over.

Seeing Evelyn's face, she frowned slightly.
After a moment, she suddenly asked, "Is your mother Sophia Ashcroft?"

Evelyn did not deny it.

Mrs. Evans sneered.
"No wonder you dare to hit my son. You're that woman's child! Back then, she led Mr. Valentine on. If she wanted to be with him, then she should have stayed! Instead, she disappeared, leaving Mr. Valentine unmarried all these years! I never thought she hid away and secretly gave birth to you!"

Evelyn sensed resentment towards her mother in Mrs. Evans' words.

Did she have feelings for Mr. Valentine and was taking it out on her mother?

Mrs. Evans suddenly said, "Since you're his daughter, I'll show you some consideration. Here's what we'll do: Kowtow to my son, and this matter ends here!"

Evelyn frowned.

Ethan stepped forward.
"Mrs. Evans, don't go too far!"

"Too far?"

Mrs. Evans pointed at Justin Evans.
"Look what she did to my son! There isn't a patch of good skin on him! I'm only asking her to kowtow. I'm not having her beaten in return only because she's a girl!"

Ethan Valentine also frowned.
"Mrs. Evans, this is a bit excessive towards a junior..."

Mrs. Evans scoffed.
"I'm just dealing with the matter at hand!"

Evelyn prepared to reason with her.
"Mrs. Evans, do you know why I hit your son?"

Mrs. Evans laughed coldly.
"Isn't it just about some romantic affair? If Mina Sherwood did it, can't my son talk about it?! Since she seduced my son and took the cooperation deal from our Evans family, she must pay the price! My son wasn't spouting nonsense. How dare you attack him? Do you think the Evans family is weak?"

Evelyn immediately retorted, "Mina did not seduce your son! He was lying! He admitted it himself!"

"Hah, he only admitted it under your coercion and torture. I heard you had an assistant? Bring him here too. I want to see who is so bold as to beat my son like this in New York!"

The Evans family was powerful, on par with the Valentine family.

Justin Evans was Mrs. Evans' beloved son, thoroughly spoiled.

This was also why Justin Evans remained defiant when Marcus Valentine was interrogating him.

Because Justin Evans was certain Marcus Valentine wouldn't actually kill him.

Until he encountered Alexander Vance.

Evelyn looked at Justin Evans.

She now understood Mrs. Evans was somewhat reasonable, just blinded by her son's lies.

But Mrs. Evans trusted her son. Where could Evelyn find proof now that Justin Evans and Mina were innocent, that it was all his slander?!

Evelyn felt trapped in a dead end.

How could she break this impasse?

Just then, a hoarse voice, cracked from too much crying, arrived.
"Mrs. Evans, how can you be so unsympathetic towards a junior?"

"A little girl beat your boy. You should look at yourself. How could a boy be beaten until he wailed by a girl? Don't you think so?"

The person hadn't entered the courtyard, but the voice arrived first.

Ethan's eyes lit up.
"It's the Sect Leader!"
###Chapter 419

Evelyn Ross's gaze immediately shifted to the doorway.

The sect leader's voice was still hoarse, just like the last time she heard it.

But the mischievous tone felt strangely familiar.

Especially that careless, flippant manner... it was exactly like a playful old man.

It was identical to her own master's demeanor.

As she pondered this, a familiar figure appeared at the door.

The sect leader wore a gray training uniform.
His short hair was messy and unruly.
He stood with his back to everyone, hands clasped behind him.

His posture was that of a reclusive master.

Evelyn pressed her lips together in silent exasperation.

Ethan Valentine leaned close to her ear, whispering softly.
"The sect leader is putting on airs again."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

Seeing this, Mrs. Evans immediately sneered.
"Sect Leader Cortez, I've been waiting for quite some time. You're certainly not in a hurry!"

Though it was clearly sarcasm, the sect leader seemed oblivious.
He chuckled good-naturedly.
"Ah, I'm getting old. But I am a martial artist, so my legs are still quicker than most."

Mrs. Evans felt like she had punched a cushion.
Frustration welled up inside her.

She took a deep breath and pointed at Evelyn.
"Sect Leader, what did you mean by that earlier? Are you planning to protect this Outer Disciple?"

"Absolutely not!"

The sect leader denied it immediately.
"Cortez Sect affairs are always handled according to our rules! How could we show favoritism?"

Mrs. Evans' expression softened slightly.

But then the sect leader's tone shifted.
"I'm thinking of Justin Evans's reputation. He's a Registered Disciple of the Fifth Uncle. He's quite successful in New York. Everyone says his martial arts are exceptional. If we punish this Outer Disciple girl today, wouldn't that be announcing to the world that he was beaten by an Outer Disciple? Justin, do you really not care about your reputation?"

Justin Evans was taken aback.

His entire social circle relied on his reputation!

That's why he insisted on spreading rumors about Mina Sherwood—to show off.

The fact that he got beaten up hadn't spread yet...

If it became common knowledge, how could he ever show his face again?

Justin hesitated, but Mrs. Evans already snapped sharply.
"Sect Leader Cortez, you're quite the smooth talker! Clearly, it's your Outer Disciple who violated the sect rules and assaulted a Registered Disciple. Yet you're blaming the victim? Are you seriously asking my son why Evelyn Ross didn't hit anyone else but him?"

The sect leader immediately seized the opportunity.
"Yes, that's exactly what I'm asking. Why did she only hit you? Justin, shouldn't you reflect on yourself... maybe you just have a punchable face? Or perhaps your face says 'come hit me'?"

Mrs. Evans shot to her feet, enraged.
"Joseph Cortez, you...!!"

"Now, now, don't get angry, Mrs. Evans. Anger causes wrinkles!" Joseph Cortez said with a cheeky grin.
"Besides, children must find their own path. Our Cortez Sect has its rules, but if my son got beaten up, I wouldn't fight his battles for him. I'd tell him to train harder and get his revenge later..."

Mrs. Evans sneered.
"You are you, and I am me! The Cortez family are martial artists who believe in proving themselves through combat. We, the Evans family, are merchants. We only take legal routes! Sect Leader Cortez, if you insist on not punishing this Outer Disciple according to the sect rules, then don't blame me for calling the police!"

"There's no need for that, no need..."

Joseph Cortez waved his hands repeatedly, still keeping his back to everyone.
"We're all reasonable people. How about this: I'll ask the Third Master Valentine to come apologize to you in person?"

Mrs. Evans' eyes lit up for a moment before she scoffed.
"Don't try your handsome man tricks on me! My husband is still at home. That won't work on me!"

Joseph Cortez coughed dryly.
"What handsome man tricks? We're all old friends... The Third Master Valentine's daughter hit your son. It's only right that he apologizes... If you're worried your husband will get jealous and don't want him coming to your home, then he can come here. We can discuss it properly."

Mrs. Evans said sarcastically.
"Stop talking nonsense, Joseph Cortez! You've had your back to me since you arrived. What does that mean? Do you find me unworthy of your gaze? Or is my appearance so offensive it would give you a sty?"

"Not at all, not at all!" Joseph Cortez said with a smile.
"Mrs. Evans, you are a great beauty. You were famously beautiful in New York when you were young. Didn't we just see each other recently? It's just... well, I'm a bit inconvenienced. I'm afraid my current appearance might damage the Cortez Sect's image!"

Mrs. Evans was furious.
"Stop making excuses! I think you just look down on women! Joseph Cortez, are you planning to settle this today with your back to me the entire time?"

"Not exactly..." Joseph Cortez sighed softly.
"If you insist on facing me, Mrs. Evans, it's not impossible..."

Evelyn stared intently at Joseph Cortez, a faint anticipation growing in her heart.

She saw Joseph Cortez's body pause slightly.
Then, slowly and very reluctantly, he began to turn around...
###Chapter 420

Joseph Cortez slowly turned around.

Everyone gasped in unison the moment they saw his face.

His eyes were swollen and red like walnuts, bulging prominently.

Though he still stood with his hands behind his back, trying to maintain an air of dignity, his swollen, distorted face was almost unrecognizable.

Evelyn Ross was also stunned.

Though disfigured, the face held a faint sense of familiarity.

It looked very much like her master.

No, that couldn't be...

Charles Cortez cried out in shock. "B-big brother? Sect Leader?"

Mrs. Evans was also taken aback, her tone softening unconsciously. "What happened to you...?"

Sect Leader Cortez touched his own face.

Not only were his eyes swollen, but prolonged crying had also caused red rashes to cover his cheeks. His entire face was a dreadful sight.

Sect Leader Cortez cleared his throat softly. "Mrs. Evans, I am indeed unwell. My apologies for the neglect."

Mrs. Evans was momentarily speechless, finding it hard to press about his earlier attitude. She only asked, "What's wrong with your eyes?"

"It's nothing. Just an inflammation."

Joseph Cortez sighed quietly.

Charles Cortez said urgently, "Sect Leader, you must take care of your health! How could you let it get this bad without seeing a doctor? I'll accompany you to the hospital later!"

"Not necessary." Joseph Cortez knew perfectly well his eyes were swollen from crying. A few days of warm compresses would help.

Provided... he stopped crying.

But how was that possible?

Like right now...

Justin Evans smirked. "Mom, even if we handle this quietly, at least make Grace apologize to me! I need to feel her sincere remorse!"

Sect Leader Cortez immediately agreed. "Not too much, not too much at all..."

The Cortez family had several sets of fist techniques, plus sword forms and other martial moves. Practicing them all would take the entire day without rest!

###Chapter 421

The sect leader returned after three years abroad.

Her master had said the exact same thing.

Cooperating with Cortez Sect would be incredibly easy now.

Evelyn becoming an Inner Disciple would inevitably cause discontent.

A flood of challenge petitions would follow.

Would she accept them or not?

###Chapter 426

Memories from fifteen years ago suddenly flooded his mind.

He was five years old then.

She refused to let go no matter how hard the trafficker hit her.

A neighbor heard the commotion and was about to come out.

He forcibly took his sister away.

He had lived with guilt all these years.

They knew about his unresolved guilt.

That was also why Mrs. Evans told him the news immediately.

Justin's voice shook with excitement. "Having an address makes it easier to find her! Mom, send more people! We must find my sister even if we have to turn New York upside down!"

Mrs. Evans reassured him gently. "Don't worry, I've already arranged it."

Justin rushed upstairs and brought down a box.

He ran to Mrs. Evans. "Mom, these are all gifts I prepared for my sister. My New Year's money, company shares... My life was saved by her. I'll give everything to her!"

Mrs. Evans' eyes welled up. "Okay, your sister won't blame you."

Justin wiped his tears. "My sister was closest to me. She won't blame me. I just don't know... how has she been all these years?"

Mrs. Evans sighed softly. "I hope kind people adopted her."

Justin nodded heavily.

His phone suddenly rang.

He answered to hear Jason's shrill wailing. "Justin! You have to stand up for me! That Evelyn... she kicked and destroyed my family jewels!"

Justin instinctively clenched his legs.

He felt a chill down below.

Thankfully, Evelyn had gone easy on him last time.

He didn't know.

Evelyn always knew her limits.

Jason's actions had crossed her line.

Justin swallowed hard. "Which hospital are you at? I'll come see you."

Jason gave the hospital name.

Justin hung up and looked at Mrs. Evans. "Mom! Evelyn has gone too far! She completely disregards the Evans family! I just went to Cortez Sect to see her today, and she beat up Jason again!"

Mrs. Evans frowned. "Jason causes trouble all the time. He deserved to be beaten."

Justin said urgently, "But his family jewels were destroyed!"

Mrs. Evans stood up abruptly. "That is no small matter! Evelyn truly doesn't know her limits! The Evans family must hold her accountable!"

Justin nodded repeatedly. "Mom, if we let her off lightly again, I might be next!"

Mrs. Evans looked at her son's cowardly appearance and shook her head helplessly. "Don't worry, we won't go easy on her this time! Even if she's William's daughter, she needs to be taught a lesson!"

Justin felt relieved. "Mom, you continue looking for my sister. I'll go to the hospital to see Jason."

…

Justin quickly arrived at the hospital.

Jason had just finished surgery in the ward.

The anesthesia hadn't worn off yet, but the doctor said he was completely ruined.

Justin entered and asked immediately, "Are you really a eunuch now?"

Hatred filled Jason's eyes. "Justin! My life is over! You must avenge me!"

Justin felt a chill down his spine. "What exactly did you do to make her go so far?"

Jason gritted his teeth. "I was just playing with a little girl and she caught me... Justin! Take me to Cortez Sect tomorrow! I want her to pay in blood!"

Jason had gone completely mad.

Justin nodded. "Mom has already agreed. We'll go to Cortez Sect tomorrow. Cortez Sect might ignore my injury, but your situation is too serious! They must give the Evans family an explanation!"

Jason screamed through tears. "I also want her to marry me! To be responsible for me for life! I'll torment her in bed every day!"

Justin frowned.

He thought Jason had lost his mind.

A nurse entered with changed clothes. "The surgical gown has been changed. Your clothes are here."

The nurse placed the clothes on the sofa and left.

Justin glanced at the bloody pants.

He felt pity.

His gaze suddenly fixed on a crescent moon-shaped jade pendant.

Justin froze completely.

This... wasn't this his sister's jade pendant?

He grabbed Jason and demanded, "Where did this jade come from?!"
###Chapter 427

Jason Evans's pupils contracted sharply.

His heart sank like a stone.

The entire Evans family knew how much the main family valued their lost daughter.

If they found out what he did to his own sister...

He would be expelled from the Evans family!

A cripple cast out would face certain death!

John's residence.

This mute girl possessed maturity beyond her years.

Early next morning.

###Chapter 428

It was true Jason had bullied others first.

Jason can no longer have offspring.
So Grace should lose her fertility too.

###Chapter 432

"First, explain clearly. Where did this jade come from?!"

Jason racked his brain to fabricate a lie. "Aunt, a friend acquired it. The seller was a vegetable vendor. It seems they were struggling and pawned it. They left no contact information."

Mrs. Evans immediately issued an order. "Investigate all vegetable vendors! Look for children around my daughter's age!"

The bodyguard responded, "Yes."

…

Cortez Sect.
Joseph Cortez and Mr. Valentine concluded their brief conversation and said their goodbyes.

Evelyn Ross escorted Mr. Valentine to the parking lot.

The father and daughter, separated since childhood, felt a slight awkwardness together.

Not knowing their relationship had felt more natural. Now, with their identities clear, there was a certain stiffness.

Evelyn was searching for a topic when Mr. Valentine suddenly spoke. "Don't worry. The Evans family wouldn't dare move against the Valentines."

Evelyn nodded.

Mr. Valentine continued, "Evelyn, you can tell me anything. You have family now. I can help you carry the burden."

Evelyn paused, looking at him.

Warmth surged in her heart.

She nodded. "I want to cooperate with the Evans family."

Mr. Valentine was taken aback. "Why?"

To find her mother, she needed to complete the Nan family's task quickly. Though the reason was unknown, having offended Mrs. Evans, cooperation now relied on Mr. Valentine finding a way.

She didn't explain. Mr. Valentine didn't press further. He simply said, "I'll think of a way."

"Thank you."

"Between father and daughter, no need for formalities. Shall I take you home?"

"No need. My people are here."

Evelyn pointed to the low-profile black car by the roadside.

Mr. Valentine looked over, pursing his lips slightly in displeasure. "Doesn't he work? Driving for you every day?"

Evelyn nodded. "He's scared after what happened last time."

Mr. Valentine was silent for a moment. "The Valentine home will always be your home."

"Okay."

Mr. Valentine got into his car under Evelyn's gaze.

The motorcade started. Through the rearview mirror, Mr. Valentine watched Evelyn walk briskly towards that car.

The corner of his mouth lifted. After a long moment, he muttered, "That boy is getting off too easy!"

His secretary didn't catch it clearly. "Mr. Valentine, what did you say?"

Mr. Valentine's face darkened. "Where is Linda Lowell now?"

The secretary immediately replied, "I'll send someone to investigate!"

"Find her and make her suffer! Force a confession. Make her pay tenfold for everything she did to my daughter!"

The secretary was about to respond.

Mr. Valentine suddenly thought of Alexander Vance…
###Chapter 433
That man wasn't known for his generosity. If he learned of Evelyn's suffering, he would certainly act…

He added, "Even if she's dead, dig her up and scatter her ashes!"

"Yes."

…

Evans Family.
Besides Justin, the Evans family had two other sons.

The two hurriedly took the jade and left.

Justin caught up. "I'm coming with you!"

After everyone left, Mrs. Evans sat on the sofa, taking a deep breath.

…
Produce Market.
The three Evans brothers showed a photo around. "Have you seen this jade?"

Passersby shook their heads.

Jane Evans was shopping at the market.

Her brother was sick. She needed to buy some ribs for soup.

She walked to the meat counter, pulling out money to pay. Suddenly, she heard someone nearby ask, "Little miss, have you seen this jade?"

Jane paused, turning to see a gentle, jade-like big brother.

He looked exceptionally kind.
Jane Evans froze for a moment.

A memory seemed to stir deep within her mind.

But it was blocked by a hazy, fog-like barrier.

She instinctively tried to see the screen of his phone.

But the girl buying vegetables spoke first. "Haven't seen it."

The older brother quickly put his phone away and kept walking.

Jane Evans couldn't make out what was on the screen.

She frowned.

Compelled by some unknown force, she began to follow him...

This was Dylan Evans, the eldest son of the Evans family.

He didn't notice the girl trailing him at all.

He continued asking around the market.

Two young men in sharp suits appeared in the distance.

They looked completely out of place in the shabby market.

Jane Evans stared at them, lost in thought.

A sharp, impatient voice suddenly barked behind her. "Move! Are you deaf?"

Jane Evans turned around in a fluster.

It was a vendor pushing a cart.

She immediately bowed and apologized.

The commotion caught the attention of the three Evans brothers.

All three froze the moment they saw Jane Evans.

Justin Evans stared intently at her. "Big brother, second brother, don't you think..."

"Her eyes look just like Dad's."

"There's an indescribable familiarity."

The three exchanged glances.

They suddenly looked at Jane Evans in unison. "Could she be...?"

Jane Evans quickly waved her hands.

She pulled out a small notebook she always carried.

She neatly wrote the words "I'm sorry."

The vendor glanced at the writing and smirked. "Oh, a mute."

He pushed his cart away without another look.

Jane Evans stood still for a moment, slightly dazed.

She sighed softly and walked toward the vegetable stall.

The three men in the distance immediately looked disappointed.

Justin Evans spoke first. "Our little sister was always so articulate."

"There's no way she's a mute."

They left the market, their hopes dashed.

Justin Evans was eager to rush to the next market.

Dylan Evans suddenly stopped him. "This isn't the way."

"There are hundreds of markets in New York."

"Not to mention countless street vendors."

Justin Evans grew agitated. "You don't want to find her anymore?"

The second Evans brother quickly mediated. "Big brother must have a plan."

Dylan Evans rubbed his temples. "When will you ever grow up?"

"Always causing trouble."

"I heard you took Mom to offend the Xus and the Cortez Sect?"

"The Five Great Families have never had a falling out."

"Are you determined to ruin the Evans family?"

Justin Evans stiffened his neck. "They started it!"

"We're talking about finding our sister now!"

Dylan Evans took a deep breath. "I'm suggesting we place an ad."

Justin Evans was stunned. "An ad?"

"Yes," Dylan Evans explained. "Back then, we were afraid the traffickers would harm her."

"That's why we never searched publicly."

"Now we know she's in New York."

"It's the perfect time to launch a full-scale search."

Justin Evans's eyes lit up. "You're a genius!"

Dylan Evans immediately started planning. "Get an ad made."

"Run it city-wide... no, nationwide."

"Everyone needs to see this!"

The second Evans brother turned to leave. "I'll handle it at the office."

Dylan Evans looked at Justin Evans.

He felt helpless about his spoiled younger brother.

He patted Justin Evans's shoulder. "Behave yourself lately."

"Don't provoke Marcus Xu again."

Justin Evans curled his lip. "Fine."

He waited until Dylan Evans was out of earshot before sneering. "I'll provoke him if I want."

"Let's see what he can do."

In just half a day.

The Evans family's missing person ads were everywhere.

But promotions need time to gain traction.

It wasn't until she was cooking dinner that evening.

That Jane Evans saw the ad notification on her phone.

She stared at the crescent moon jade on the screen, stunned.

She quickly tapped Justin Evansan's shoulder.

She handed him her phone.

Justin Evansan was also stunned by the ad.

"Jane, isn't this your jade?"

"Why is it on TV?"

"Let me see what this is about!"
###Chapter 434

Justin Evansan stared at the television screen.

A missing person advertisement was playing.

It showed a jade pendant shaped like a crescent moon.

Someone was searching for the owner of this pendant.

A contact number was displayed below.

Justin Evansan immediately pulled out his phone.

He noted down the phone number.

He wanted to help Jane Evans contact them.

He wanted to know who they were.

If suitable, he hoped Jane Evans could meet her family.

Jane Evans reached out and stopped him.

She shook her head.

She signed in response: I will contact them myself.

Justin Evansan was stunned. "How will you contact them? You can't speak."

Jane Evans glared at him.

She stubbornly pouted.

She signed: I can send a text message.

Justin Evansan fell silent.

He knew his sister's personality.

She appeared fragile, but was stubborn inside.

Once she decided something, no one could change her mind.

He sighed. "Then send a message and ask now."

Jane Evans nodded.

She took her phone and walked out of the room.

She sat on the steps by the door.

The courtyard was lively.

Neighbors were strolling and chatting.

They had all watched her and her brother grow up.

Jane Evans watched them, her feelings complicated.

She had known she was adopted since childhood.

She also wanted to know who her biological parents were.

She wanted to know why they abandoned her.

Was it because she couldn't speak?

Her brother said she had a fever when he found her.

After she woke up, she couldn't speak anymore.

Was that the reason?

Or was it because the family was too poor?

She had considered many possibilities.

She never expected to find a clue on TV today.

She picked up her phone and hesitated for a long time.

Finally, she sent a text message.

[Hello, I am the owner of that jade pendant. May I ask who you are?]

There was no immediate reply.

Jane Evans stared at her phone screen.

The hope in her heart gradually faded.

Perhaps her family didn't want to acknowledge her.

She smiled bitterly.

Just then, someone walked through the gate.

Jane Evans's eyes lit up, and she stood.

The person was Evelyn Ross.

She walked up to Jane Evans. "Did Jason Evans's people return your pendant yesterday?"

Jane Evans shook her head.

Evelyn's face darkened. "I'll help you get it back now."

Jane Evans hesitated.

She took out paper and pen to write: I want to go with you.

That jade was on the news.

It means it came from Jason Evans.

Maybe her relative is at the hospital.

Evelyn nodded.

She went inside and greeted Justin Evansan.

Justin Evansan's leg was still injured and he couldn't walk.

Evelyn told him to rest well.

She led Jane Evans out of the alley.

A black car was parked by the roadside.

Alexander Vance leaned against the car, waiting for them.

He wore casual clothes, his posture relaxed.

His deep gaze fell on Evelyn.

His eyes were so tender they could pull you in.

Sunlight streamed from behind him.

Gilding him in a golden light.

Evelyn was momentarily mesmerized.

Alexander had been accompanying her recently.

He had put all his work aside.

Willingly acting as her driver.

She lowered her eyes.

She had Jane Evans sit in the back.

She herself took the passenger seat.

It was Jane Evans's first time in a luxury car.

She looked uneasily at the clean seats.

She clutched her clothes, head bowed.

Afraid of dirtying the car.

Evelyn noticed her discomfort.

She broke the silence. "Jane Evans, is that pendant very important?"

Jane Evans handed her a piece of paper.

It read: [My parents left it for me. My brother adopted me.]

Evelyn was stunned. "I will definitely help you get it back!"

Jane Evans wrote another line: [Thank you, sister.]

Evelyn looked at her through the rearview mirror.

The young girl sat quietly.

Her clothes were old but clean, very likable.

Evelyn smiled slightly.

Jason Evans's hospital address wasn't hard to find.

Alexander quickly found the place.

The car stopped at the hospital entrance.

Evelyn asked Alexander to wait in the car.

She took Jane Evans upstairs.

The VIP Suite was quiet.

The hallway was nearly empty.

Jane Evans followed nervously behind Evelyn.

They arrived at Jason Evans's room.

Justin Evans was inside, talking to Jason Evans.

Justin was anxious. "Jason Evans, where did that jade really come from?"

He scrolled through his phone. "After the ad went out, so many scammers showed up!"

Jason Evans sighed. "They're all just after money."

Justin didn't care. "Finding my sister is worth any cost!"

He continued replying to messages.

Evelyn entered with Jane Evans.
