Jane Evans took out paper and pen to write: It's a jade pendant shaped like a crescent moon...

###Chapter 435

###Chapter 436

What happened?

Marcus didn't want her, so she climbed into his bed!

Now, the Valentine stock was crashing. The whole Sherwood family would be buried with her!

At a time like this, they only cared about the family's reputation.

###Chapter 437

Mina Sherwood listened to her parents, a cold smirk curling her lips.

We thought Marcus Valentine didn't want to take responsibility, but it turns out we were the ones scamming them! Mina, have you no shame?!"

She looked at William and her parents, thinking of the only person in the Sherwood family who had ever been kind to her: her uncle from another branch, Kevin Sherwood.

Back then, Kevin had staged that incident at the hotel to force Marcus to marry her… Even though he used her, he never used her looks to get what he wanted.

She smiled bitterly. "Fine. Everything from the Sherwood family has nothing to do with me!"

William breathed a sigh of relief. Mr. and Mrs. Sherwood frowned but ultimately remained silent.

Several cars suddenly pulled up.

Mr. and Mrs. Valentine and Marcus stepped out of the lead car.

Kevin got out of the car behind them.

Kevin was apologizing to the Valentines. "This is my fault for not investigating thoroughly enough… But we still need to get the full story from the children. Mina is a Sherwood. The Sherwoods will not shirk responsibility. Let's work together to minimize the impact of this matter."

The Valentines' faces were cold.

Both were reasonable people, but they had never been deceived like this before.

Mrs. Valentine thought back to the previous rumor about Marcus bringing abortion pills to Mina. So that's what it was!

She shot a glare at Marcus. This foolish son of hers, wearing a cuckold's horns and not saying a word, even trying to cover it up stupidly.

If there was a medical condition, they could treat it. If not, it was fine. Before, their third son wasn't married and had no children. With many brothers in the family, having a nephew provide for him in old age was acceptable.

But for things to have reached this point… What was this!

Mrs. Valentine felt wronged on her son's behalf. Her expression was dark as she walked up to Mina.

She looked directly at Mina. "Mina, we've treated you well since you married into our family, haven't we? How could you deceive my son like this? Whose child is it in your womb? You must give us an explanation today!"
###Chapter 438

Mrs. Valentine's gaze was sharp as a blade.
She was certain this child was not of Valentine blood.

After the oligospermia report, she personally escorted her son to three different hospitals for re-examination.
The results were shockingly consistent.
Four diagnostic reports, in black and white, left no room for doubt.

Mr. Valentine's face was dark with fury.
He paced the living room with his hands behind his back. "The Valentine bloodline cannot be tainted! Mina, you must give us an explanation!"
His voice was cold and hard as iron.

Mina found it utterly absurd.
She knew better than anyone that she had never been unfaithful.
But no one believed her.

She looked at Marcus Valentine.
The man radiated a chilling coldness, his thin lips pressed into a tight line.
Online mockery flooded in like a tide.
Labels like "Cuckold King" and "Impotent Man" stabbed painfully at his pride.

Marcus had been groomed by the Third Elder since childhood, pride etched into his very bones.
When had he ever suffered such humiliation?
He should be furious. He should make her suffer.
But meeting her clear gaze, his heart unexpectedly softened for a moment.

Seeing his silence, Mina's lips curled into a bitter smile.
Her voice was calm. "What explanation do you want, Mom and Dad? A divorce? I agree."

Her casual, dismissive attitude instantly ignited the conflict.

William Sherwood flew into a rage. "Kneel! Kowtow and apologize to the Valentines!"
He reached out to grab her arm.

Mr. Sherwood hurriedly tried to mediate. "Mina, apologize quickly! Don't bring down the family!"

Mrs. Sherwood dropped to her knees with a thud, her forehead hitting the floor with loud bangs. "In-laws, the Sherwoods forced her to marry up... She made a momentary mistake!"

Mrs. Valentine trembled with rage. "Forgive her? Who will bear the gossip and rumors for my son!"
She turned sharply to Kevin Sherwood. "Is this the Sherwood family's sincerity?"

William Sherwood interjected urgently. "Elder Brother! Just disown her and cast her out!"

Kevin Sherwood rebuked sharply. "Foolish!"
He turned to Mina, his gaze profound. "Mina, tell the truth—is the child really Marcus Valentine's?"

Mina's eyes suddenly grew hot.
Finally, someone was willing to hear her out.

She stepped forward and faced William directly. "Uncle, shouldn't you know better than anyone if I've ever slept around?"

William's eyes shifted uneasily.
He had indeed kept this piece on the board for a higher price, never letting her lose her virtue easily.
But he could never admit it now!

He sneered. "How would I know about your private affairs? If you've done nothing wrong, where did this child come from?"

Mina clenched her fists, her knuckles turning white.
She took a deep breath and looked at Kevin. "Marcus is the only man I've ever been with."
Her gaze shifted to Marcus, her voice trembling. "It's been three full months today. We can do an amniocentesis—do you dare to test it?"

Mrs. Sherwood clung to this lifeline. "Yes! A DNA test! Clear my daughter's name!"
###Chapter 439

Marcus's words silenced the entire room.

Mrs. Valentine frowned at Mina. "You would really dare to do an amniocentesis?"

Marcus cut her off sharply.

He strode over to Mina and grabbed her chin.

He said coldly, "Option one is the best choice for both families!"

Mr. and Mrs. Valentine spoke up. "Marcus, you don't have to sacrifice yourself for the family. If there are unresolved issues, it's better to separate."

Mr. Valentine disapproved. "Marriage is not a game. Don't make decisions out of spite."

Mrs. Valentine nodded. "Let each other go."

Marcus gritted his teeth. "She humiliated me. She must stay!"

He looked at Mina.

The thought of her leaving filled him with burning rage.

He realized he had fallen for Mina…

But this woman was so heartless.

Mina lowered her head with a bitter smile, then looked up again. "I want the amniocentesis."

Marcus said, "Why subject yourself to this humiliation!"

"Whether it's humiliation or not, we'll know after the test."

Mina's voice was calm, long accustomed to doubt.

Just as Marcus was about to speak, a crowd of reporters suddenly swarmed in.

Cameras were pointed at them.

Microphones were shoved in their faces. "Mr. Valentine, Ms. Sherwood, is this about the azoospermia?"

"If Mr. Valentine has azoospermia, how did the pregnancy happen?"

"Did Marcus marry Ms. Sherwood willingly?"

"Was Ms. Sherwood pregnant before the marriage?"

"The Valentine stock price has plummeted. Any comments, Marcus?"

"Is the azoospermia due to horseback riding? Will it affect your position as heir?"

"Marcus, does azoospermia mean you're… impotent?"
###Chapter 440

Reporters swarmed the Nan family villa.

Footage of Marcus Valentine and the Sherwoods trapped at the gate quickly became a trending topic.

Inside a hospital room.

Justin Evans lounged on a sofa, scrolling through his phone.

He suddenly leapt to his feet.

"Did you arrange for those reporters?"

Jason Evans shook his head from his hospital bed.

"Not me."

Justin frowned.

"Then who?"

Very few people knew Mina Sherwood was staying at the Nan residence.

Yet reporters found the exact location.

Someone had to be behind it.

Jason suddenly remembered something.

"Do you still have that number that sent the report?"

Justin found the number and dialed.

A cold automated voice answered.

"The number is not in service."

He immediately ordered an investigation.

The results came back quickly.

It was a prepaid burner phone.

Justin burst into laughter.

"Someone hates Marcus Valentine more than we do!"

"Azoospermia exposed to a media siege."

"What a humiliation!"

Jason's face instantly darkened.

His gaze dropped to his lower body.

There was nothing there anymore.

Compared to azoospermia.

His situation was far worse.

But he didn't dare show his anger.

Justin was the eldest son of the main family line.

He was just a son from a secondary branch.

His father had warned him not to offend the main family.

Jason clenched the bedsheet tightly.

His nails dug into his palms.

...

Outside the Nan villa.

Marcus Valentine's face was like stormy water.

The usually carefree young master appeared unusually composed.

There was a reason Third Master Valentine chose him as heir.

The reporters' questions grew more aggressive.

"Mr. Valentine, was the azoospermia caused by horseback riding?"

"Or is it hereditary? Third Master Valentine also has no children..."

Marcus's eyes turned icy.

"Which newspaper are you from?"

He glanced at his assistant.

"Note it down."

"Sue him for defaming my uncle's reputation."

The reporter paled.

"I was just making a reasonable assumption..."

Mr. and Mrs. Valentine started to speak.

"The boy I loved most is already dead."

She covered her abdomen.

"I will never forgive the person who killed my child."

"Let's get a divorce."

She leaned on Evelyn's shoulder.

"Evelyn, take me away from here."

"Okay."

Evelyn supported her as they walked step by step out the main door.

Once in the car, Mina finally broke down crying.

"My baby..."

Evelyn comforted her softly. "Don't cry. Actually..."
###Chapter 447

Mina Sherwood's heart felt as if it were being shredded.

The months of her pregnancy had been pure torment.

No one believed her.

She had endured until the third month, when she could finally undergo an amniocentesis with Marcus Valentine.

The reporters had ruined everything.

She knew Marcus had been provoked.

She could understand his actions.

But she could not forgive the pain he caused.

Mina smiled bitterly.

A heavy, dragging sensation settled in her lower abdomen.

It had been two hours since she took the pill.

Was the baby leaving her now?

Evelyn Ross spoke suddenly. "The baby is actually fine."

Mina was stunned. "What?"

She smiled mockingly. "Grace, you don't need to comfort me. After taking an abortion pill, even if it doesn't work, the baby won't be healthy."

Evelyn cleared her throat softly. "I switched the abortion pill for a vitamin."

Mina froze.

Alexander Vance strode over. "I discovered Marcus had bought the abortion pill and told Evelyn. She found an opportunity to go home and switch them."

Mina was bewildered. "How did you discover he bought the pill?"

Alexander paused.

It was when Evelyn had forced him to go get Marcus to sign the divorce agreement.

He hadn't wanted to interfere in a couple's affairs.

But Evelyn had been insistent.

He had people track Marcus's movements and accidentally discovered the purchase.

Alexander gave a light cough. "I happened to see it."

Mina frowned. "But my stomach hurts."

"Psychosomatic."

Evelyn's words were direct and precise.

Mina was silent for a moment.

She focused on the sensation. "It... really doesn't seem to hurt anymore."

Evelyn supported her arm. "The choice is yours now. Tell Marcus, or don't tell him?"

Mina looked down.

If she told him, he definitely wouldn't want the divorce.

Better not to say anything.

She didn't want to use Marcus.

Her husband's lack of trust had exhausted her.

She truly wanted a divorce.

If not for Evelyn, he would have killed their child.

She wasn't a saint.

The baby being okay didn't absolve his despicable behavior.

Mina made her decision. "I want to go somewhere no one knows me. Raise the baby in peace. Live my life."

"Stay at the Nan family home," Evelyn said directly. "I'd worry about you alone in a strange place."

Marcus's oligospermia still needed to be investigated.

If someone was targeting Marcus, would they harm Mina if they knew her baby wasn't terminated?

Evelyn felt the situation was becoming complex.

She helped Mina into the car, and Alexander joined them.

Alexander took the driver's seat.

Evelyn and Mina sat in the back.

During the drive, Mina bowed her head, tears falling.

Evelyn didn't know how to comfort her, just gently patted her hand.

Mina wiped her tears. "Grace, I'm okay."

She looked at Alexander in the driver's seat. "Boy Toy, thank you this time."

Alexander was about to offer a polite response.

Mina continued. "Evelyn, I think you should just be with this Boy Toy. That Alexander Vance isn't right for you... His wife just died, and he went to the Ashcroft family reunion gala with a dowry. He's clearly not a good man."

Alexander: "..."

He suddenly felt a twinge of regret for helping her.

...

At the Evans residence.

Justin Evans stormed into the house and found Mrs. Evans.

"Mom, I was played!"

Mrs. Evans looked at him coldly. "You're a fool. Who else is to blame?"

Justin threw his phone in front of her. "Look! They set me up! They talked about oligospermia, so I believed it! Now look!"

He pointed angrily at the news on the TV. "The child is his! It was all a farce! He used the chance to promote a new product, staged that scene in the car refusing to get out, let people try to smash it without success. Acted like it was real! The stock price shot up instantly!"

Mrs. Evans's face was dark.

She looked at Justin. "I heard Evelyn Ross wanted to cooperate with our family, made inquiries before. Whatever her goal was, cooperation is out of the question now!"

She picked up Justin's phone. "This report, didn't you verify it before sending it?"

Justin was even more aggrieved. "I did verify it! He went for the check-up, and it just happened to be at our family's hospital! Would our own doctor lie to me? Who knew there'd be this kind of twist!"

Mrs. Evans gave the butler a look.

The butler immediately went to investigate.

He returned five minutes later. "The doctor is indeed ours. No issues."

Justin was puzzled. "Then what happened?"

Mrs. Evans was exasperated. "He has his own hospital. Why go to ours? He must have switched the samples! He was setting this up a month ago! Devious!"

Justin fumed. "The Valentines are too cunning! They seem honest, but they're like this!"

Mrs. Evans sighed.

She exited Justin's text messages, about to return the phone, when her eyes caught an unread message.

Unknown Sender: [Hello, I am the owner of that jade piece. May I ask who you are?]

Mrs. Evans froze.

She sharply turned to Justin. "This message, why haven't you read it?!"

Justin cared most about his sister's situation.

When placing the external advertisement, they had used his number.

Justin was also stunned. "I've been busy with the Valentine matter recently, missed a lot of messages. Could this be... sister?"

Mrs. Evans urged. "Go contact them immediately!"

Justin quickly nodded. "Right, it must be sister! Technology is advanced now, no one would falsely claim this!"
###Chapter 448

The Evans family set up a DNA verification requirement after posting the missing person ad.

This prevented any imposters from coming forward.

Occasionally, someone would provide a vague lead, claiming to have seen the jade pendant.

The Evans family would purchase all such information and verify each one meticulously.

Finding their lost daughter became the family's top priority.

Even the business war with the Ashcrofts was left for Justin Evans to handle alone.

This led to frequent oversights and mistakes.

The eldest and second Evans brothers had no time to spare for other matters.

Justin Evans made a phone call.

The call was disconnected.

He stared blankly at his mother.

As he was about to redial, a message arrived: [Can I help you?]

Justin called again.

It was disconnected once more.

Another message popped up: [I can't talk right now. Who is this?]

Justin stamped his foot in frustration. "Why won't our sister answer the call?"

Mrs. Evans tapped his head. "Your sister should be in college now. She might be in class. Send a text."

Justin immediately replied: [Sister, it's your brother!]

...

Hospital corridor.

Jane Evans and her classmates listened to their supervisor's instructions.

She was a medical student in her final year of internship.

Outstanding performance could secure her a permanent position at the hospital.

Her phone vibrated.

Seeing the message, her fingers trembled slightly.

The other party's high-profile search suggested immense wealth and influence.

Why would such a family lose a daughter?

Whether she was abandoned or not, she needed answers.

Jane lowered her head to type a reply.

The supervisor suddenly called out. "Jane Evans, Peng Hui. You'll take the third-floor VIP suites. Mind your manners."

Jane nodded.

Peng Hui acknowledged.

They headed toward the elevator.

Someone muttered under their breath. "Why assign a mute to the VIP floor?"

The supervisor frowned. "No questions."

He watched Jane's retreating figure with a complex expression.

Though mute, Jane was brilliant. She should have focused on research.

Unfortunately, she had offended the second branch of the Evans family.

Young Master Jason Evans had specifically requested she be "taken care of."

The hospital was Evans property. The supervisor was powerless to intervene.

He could only sigh inwardly at Jane's misfortune.

...

Jane was filled with hope.

Peng Hui encouraged her. "You're top of your class. You'll definitely get to stay."

Jane nodded emphatically.

After graduation, she could earn money to support her brother.

Her brother had endured countless hardships for her education.

She was determined to succeed.

They made their rounds through the VIP suites.

Peng Hui whispered a reminder. "These clients are wealthy and powerful. Impress them, and you'll secure your spot."

Jane nodded again.

They pushed open the door to room 306.

Jane met Jason Evans's cold gaze.

She turned to flee.

"Stop!"

Jason barked.

Peng Hui offered an apologetic smile. "Is something wrong, sir?"

Jason pointed to his abdomen. "My wound might have reopened. Check it for me."

Peng Hui stepped forward to lift the covers.

Jason pointed at Jane. "I want her to do it."

Peng Hui froze.

Jason sneered. "Do doctors have genders? Can't handle it? Then get out!"

Jane clenched her fists.

Jason roared. "Get over here!"

Jane approached with trembling steps.

She uncovered the bandage.

The wound was perfectly intact.

Jason suddenly kicked her hard in the chest. "You bitch! Trying to kill me with pain?"

Jane staggered backward.

She hurriedly pulled out paper and pen. [I didn't touch you!]

Jason laughed scornfully. "A mute doctor? Diagnosing with gestures? If you don't satisfy me today, I'll make sure you never practice medicine again!"
###Chapter 449

Jane Evans' pupils constricted sharply.
She stared fixedly at Jason Evans.

Her eyes reddened.
Her nails dug into her palms.

After a long moment, she took a deep breath.
She crossed out the words she had just written and started again.
[How may I assist you?]

Jason Evans let out a derisive laugh when he saw the line.
"You know what's good for you. You understand that pleasing me is your only way out!"

He pointed to the fruit platter.
"Go wash the fruit and cut it into small pieces. Remove every single strand of pith from the oranges. I hate that stuff."

Peng Hui couldn't help speaking up.
"That's not an intern's job…"

Jason sneered.
"You know it's an internship? Do you want to get hired full-time or not?"

A bodyguard barked sharply.
"Open your damn eyes! This is the Evans family young master! The entire hospital belongs to the Evans family! The young master can make you disappear with one word! Shut your mouth!"

Peng Hui was frightened into silence.

Jason stared at Jane.
"Are you going or not?"

Jane bit her lip and nodded.
She picked up the fruit platter and walked out of the room.

She wiped her tears at the corridor corner.
She turned on the faucet and began washing the fruit.

A familiar voice suddenly came from behind her.
"Jane Evans?"

Jane turned around and saw Evelyn Ross standing not far away.
Her eyes instantly lit up.
She hurriedly pulled out her pen and paper, but before she could write anything, Evelyn spoke.
"I'm here to take care of something. What are you doing here?"

Evelyn had come for Marcus Valentine's azoospermia report.

Marcus clearly had normal fertility with high sperm motility.
Why did three hospitals all issue azoospermia diagnoses?

She had come today to confront the attending physician.
But she was told he had suddenly gone abroad for an exchange program last night.
It was a wasted trip.

Just as she was feeling disappointed, she bumped into a familiar figure.

Jane wrote on the paper: I am interning here.

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.
"Medical major?"

Jane nodded.

Evelyn was about to speak when she suddenly remembered Jason was on this floor.
Her brow furrowed tightly.
"Jason hasn't given you trouble? Do you need me to help you transfer departments?"

Jane quickly waved her hands.

She didn't want to trouble Miss Evelyn again.
Getting the heirloom back last time was already an enormous favor.

Thanks to that pendant, she could return to the Evans family…

She unconsciously touched the pendant at her neck.
She felt its outline through the fabric of her clothes.

Evelyn wanted to say something more, but footsteps suddenly sounded from the end of the corridor.

Justin Evans and Mrs. Evans walked side by side.

Justin swiped his phone irritably.
"Mom, my sister still isn't replying to messages. Her phone's GPS is on this floor. Which one is her?"

He looked up and saw Evelyn and Jane.
His face instantly darkened.
"What are you doing here?"

Evelyn pulled Jane behind her.
"Did your family open this hospital? Can I not come?"

Justin sneered.
"I'll hang a sign right now: The Ashcrofts and dogs are not allowed!"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.
"Does the hospital pick its patients?"

"So what if it does? The Evans family's hospital. I call the shots!"

Evelyn scoffed.
"Dare to say that publicly?"

Justin choked.
"You…!"

Mrs. Evans pulled her son back.
She gave Evelyn a cold look.
"Miss Evelyn, you're carrying a voice recorder, aren't you? Deliberately provoking my son to get material to use against the Evans family?"

Evelyn rolled her eyes.
"I'm not that bored. I'm here to check my brother's azoospermia report."

Justin flew into a rage.
"Stop pretending! Wasn't it your brother who switched the samples? Used azoospermic semen for the test and then framed us? How could the Evans family hospital's report be fake!"

Evelyn narrowed her eyes.
"Are you sure the report is real?"

"Of course! This is a hospital, not a joke!"

Justin glared at her.
"Trying to bring down the Evans family this way? Dream on!"

Evelyn retorted coldly.
"Then why did the attending physician suddenly go abroad last night? His phone is unreachable."

Justin was stunned.
"What?"

"The doctor who examined Marcus Valentine flew abroad last night. Completely lost contact."

Justin frowned.
"More of your tricks? Bribed the doctor, then shifted the blame to the Evans family? The Ashcrofts are truly vicious!"

Evelyn: "…"

She curled her lip.
"Paranoia is an illness. You should get it treated."

Justin: ?

Evelyn couldn't be bothered to argue.
She pulled Jane forward.
"Don't act so high and mighty. Arranging her internship on the third floor—isn't it just to make it convenient for Jason to get revenge?"

Justin smirked.
"I don't have time to manage such a minor character. Just some stray girl. You crippled Jason for her. What's wrong with the Evans family venting some anger?"

Evelyn was about to retort when Mrs. Evans cut her son off.
"Don't waste time on trivial matters."

Justin suddenly remembered.

He was here to find his sister!

Why was he wasting words with Evelyn!

He shot Evelyn a fierce glare.
His eyes swept over Jane.

Jane慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头慌乱低头 ###Chapter 450

The call was disconnected after just one ring.

A cold, automated voice sounded from the receiver.
"Hello, the number you have dialed is unavailable."

Justin Evans frowned deeply.

Where was his sister?

He sent another message.
[Sis, Mom and I are here. Which floor are you on? We'll come find you!]

The message went unanswered.

Justin grew increasingly anxious.

Mrs. Evans frowned.
"What's wrong?"

Justin sighed.
"No reply. She's not answering her calls."

Mrs. Evans took a deep breath.
"Secure the entire building immediately. Screen everyone who appears to be around her age."

Justin nodded.
"Alright."

He immediately instructed his subordinates to act.

While waiting for the bodyguards to arrive, Mrs. Evans spoke.
"I'll guard the entrance. Since you're here, go check on Jason."

Justin agreed.

Mrs. Evans turned and headed downstairs.

Justin walked toward Jason's room.
He suddenly turned back to look at Jane, his expression dark.
"It just occurred to me that Jason arranged for you to be a medical intern on this floor, correct?"
"Since that's the case, come here immediately and attend to Jason properly!"
"Otherwise…"

Jane clenched her fists, her eyes reddening.

Evelyn Ross stopped her.
"Jane, you don't have to subject yourself to this."

Jane shook her head.

She had to become a doctor.

She had studied medicine for so many years precisely for this goal.

Both her brother and she had been accepted into university back then.
Their family was too poor.
Her brother had given his spot to her.

Her brother had said that even though she couldn't speak, she could definitely become a great doctor.
Perhaps even develop a medicine to treat her vocal cords, allowing her to call him "brother" just once…

For her brother's dream, she worked part-time jobs and studied hard.
She had always been the top student in her department.

She couldn't throw it all away now in a moment of impulse.

She picked up the fruit tray and entered Jason's room.

She hadn't forgotten her original purpose for coming to the hospital.
She didn't follow Jane immediately.

"What is there to talk about?"

He looked at Evelyn with disgust.
"There's nothing to say between you and me."

"Shouldn't we ask for clarification?"

###Chapter 451

Mina won't see you."

Marcus was the perpetrator.
There was no excuse.

Mina would never forgive him.

"I reminded you before.
The child is yours."

Marcus smiled bitterly, offering no explanation.

Evelyn stopped the conversation in time, saving Alexander.

But Marcus was still standing there, perfectly fine.

###Chapter 458

If Mina hadn't been raised well since childhood and been in good health, anyone else might have miscarried.

At Jane Evans's home.

Anthony Castillo.

Back in San Francisco.

Not noticing them.

Mason Evans?

When she reached the spot.

Back in San Francisco.

When she needed monthly iron infusions.

When she moved out of the Ashcroft family.

Near her rental apartment.

During her first episode of fainting.

She didn't know she had iron deficiency anemia.

When she woke up.

About this man.

###Chapter 470
