Jane was the daughter Mrs. Evans had been desperately searching for!

Evelyn felt a deep sense of irony.

Mrs. Evans had indulged Jason Evans in every way for her daughter's sake.

She even went so far as to oppose the Ashcroft family.

But she probably had no idea what Jason had done to Jane.

What a colossal joke!

She took a deep breath to calm herself.

John Evans pressed her urgently. "You know Jane's family? She went to find them with the pendant last time, but came back very depressed… She never wanted to talk about it again…"

Evelyn sighed softly.

She gave him the answer directly. "The missing person notice was posted by the Evans family."

John was stunned. "Which Evans family?"

He didn't dare to think in that direction.

Evelyn remained silent.

John gradually understood. "Could it be… that Evans family?"

Evelyn nodded in confirmation.

John was completely dumbfounded.

He looked at Evelyn in disbelief. "Grace, this is no joking matter! How could Jane possibly be the lost young lady of the main Evans family?"

As a branch member of the Evans family, he had heard about the abduction of the main family's daughter.

That incident changed the fate of all branch families.

The Evans family had originally established a minimum living allowance for branch members.

Many impoverished relatives relied on that money to survive.

But after the young lady was lost, Mrs. Evans directly canceled the allowance.

She publicly stated that no amount of charity would bring her daughter back.

The poor should strive to be self-reliant, not beg.

After losing the allowance, John's family life became even harder.

Adopting Jane forced them to tighten their belts further.

Later, when his parents fell seriously ill, John took Jane to seek help from the clan leader.

The clan originally had medical subsidies and interest-free loans.

But the clan leader regretfully informed him the subsidies had been canceled.

His parents passed away one after another, unable to afford surgery.

John dropped out of school to work and support Jane's education.

Remembering this, John suddenly laughed.

Fate truly enjoyed playing cruel tricks.

His eyes reddened as he looked at Evelyn, countless words stuck in his throat.

He finally understood where Jane's guilt came from.

He understood why she hid news about her family.

Jane felt she had wronged him!

John urgently grabbed Evelyn's arm. "Come on, let's go find Jane at the market!"

She should have been the esteemed young lady of the Evans family.

She shouldn't have suffered hardships with him.

Evelyn nodded and followed.

They had only taken a few steps when they bumped into a neighbor running over in a panic.

The neighbor gasped for breath, shouting. "John! Hurry to the market! Something happened to Jane!"

…

At the market.

Jane was happily selecting ingredients.

Successfully joining the Nora Research Institute had delighted her.

The high internship stipend was an unexpected bonus.

She walked briskly toward the poultry section.

But she ran into an unwelcome guest—Justin Evans.

Justin stood blocking the path with several burly men.

He sucked on a lollipop, a rogue's smile on his face.

Jane instinctively stepped back, wanting to escape.

Justin waved his hand.

His lackeys immediately surrounded her, blocking her retreat.

Jane turned stubbornly, glaring angrily at this so-called brother.

Justin sneered. "Jane, come with me quietly."

Jane shook her head vigorously.

Justin lost patience. "You refuse a toast only to be forced to drink a forfeit!"

He signaled to his men. "Grab her!"

Two strong men seized Jane by both arms.

They roughly dragged her toward a van parked roadside.

Jane struggled desperately, but it was useless.

Vendors around whispered among themselves, but no one dared intervene.

Firmly restrained, Jane couldn't use sign language to communicate.

In desperation, she used her hoarse voice to ask word by word. "Why?"

Justin laughed coldly.

Why?

Because Jason had finally revealed news about his sister.

But his condition was that Jane must be taught a lesson to vent his anger.

To find his biological sister.

He could only sacrifice this mute!
###Chapter 471

Justin Evans couldn't be bothered to explain.

He looked down at Jane with disdain. "Want to know why?"

He scoffed. "You're not worthy!"

Justin Evans turned and got into the car.

The door slammed shut.

He sat in the passenger seat. "Drive."

The engine started.

Evelyn Ross and John Evans rushed to the scene.

They searched anxiously for Jane.

They hurried through the market.

But missed Justin Evans's car by seconds.

Inside the vehicle, Jane struggled desperately.

She tried to bang on the window.

She tried to get someone's attention.

A bodyguard pinned her arms down.

He slapped her hard across the face.

"Keep struggling and I'll beat you to death!"

Jane's vision went dark.

She collapsed weakly into the seat.

Justin Evans laughed coldly.

The car sped away.

Evelyn and John reached the vegetable stall.

Vegetables lay scattered on the ground.

Jane's basket sat alone and abandoned.

John picked up the basket.

His voice trembled. "Where's the girl who sells vegetables?"

A passerby whispered, "Some people grabbed her and took her in a car."

John's legs went weak.

He grabbed Evelyn's arm. "Evelyn, we have to save Jane!"

Evelyn scanned the area.

She asked calmly, "What did the people who took her look like?"

"Well-dressed. She wasn't hurt."

Evelyn immediately concluded, "It was Justin Evans."

She pulled John along. "They're going to the hospital."

They rushed out of the market.

Evelyn regretted not driving.

She flagged down a taxi.

John was sweating with anxiety. "What will they do to Jane?"

Evelyn gripped her phone tightly. "I'll contact Mrs. Evans."

She dialed the number.

The phone rang and rang. No one answered.

...

On the hospital rooftop.

Mrs. Evans sat across from Jason.

Her eyes were desperate. "Have you really found Belle?"

Jason's expression was dark.

He stared at Mrs. Evans. "Help me deal with this person."

His voice was icy. "Then I'll tell you where your daughter is."

Jason clenched his fists.

The taunts on the Campus Network stung.

—The second branch is just incompetent!

—Nora Research Institute really showed them, huh?

—Bullied Jane? Now they're getting what they deserved!

Every comment felt like a whip.

It lashed at Jason, twisting his features.

Why could Jane get into Nora?

He would make everyone see.

The consequences of crossing him!

Jason glanced toward the entrance.

Justin Evans should be back soon.

He wanted to torture Jane himself.

There was a stand-in to take the fall anyway.

If she died, someone else would clean up the mess.

Mrs. Evans's phone rang.

She saw Evelyn's name.

Remembering the cold shoulder she received earlier.

She hung up immediately.

It was too late for apologies now.

The rooftop door swung open.

Justin Evans strode in.

A bodyguard dragged Jane.

She was thrown roughly to the ground.

A red handprint marked Jane's face.

She scrambled up, trying to escape.

The bodyguard blocked her path.

Justin Evans smiled. "Trying to leave?"

Jane nodded frantically.

Justin Evans pointed to the edge of the roof. "Jump from there."

His smile turned cruel. "No one will stop you."
###Chapter 472

Jane Evans's pupils contracted sharply.

She stared at Justin in disbelief.

Her gaze then fell on Mrs. Evans.

She dropped to her knees with a thud.

Tears streamed down her face.

She shook her head desperately at Mrs. Evans.

This was a matter of life and death.

She could no longer afford to be stubborn.

Her hands trembled as she signed: [I am your daughter! I am your daughter!]

Mrs. Evans did not understand sign language.

She looked down coldly at Jane Evans.

She watched the girl frantically point at her own chest and then at her.

But she completely failed to grasp the meaning.

For some reason.

Mrs. Evans suddenly remembered what Second Aunt Amelia had said today: "...Have you ever considered that your Belle might have encountered someone like Jane Evans?"

Looking at the young girl, who was the same age as her daughter.

A tiny crack appeared in Mrs. Evans' hardened heart.

She clenched her jaw and looked at Jason. "...What exactly do you intend to do to her?"

Jason spoke with feigned grievance. "Auntie, what could I possibly do? I just want a proper apology from her. I need to vent my anger."

He glanced at Jane Evans and suddenly smiled. "Auntie, I helped you find Belle. Is this small request really too much?"

Belle...

Jane Evans's eyes widened in shock as she stared at Jason!

He knew everything!

He had been fully aware from the moment he stole the jade pendant!

He even looked directly at her while saying it.

He was doing this on purpose...

Jane Evans trembled with rage.

She continued signing: [It's me! I am her!]

Unfortunately, Mrs. Evans and Justin couldn't understand.

Mrs. Evans seemed about to say something more.

But Justin had already stepped forward and kicked Jane Evans in the back.

She fell forward onto the ground.

Her hands were now pinned, unable to sign.

Justin said coldly, "Mom, we're about to find out where sister is. Don't go soft now."

Mrs. Evans averted her gaze and said harshly, "Don't worry. I won't."

Jane Evans felt utterly hopeless.

She opened her mouth but no sound came out.

She could only struggle weakly, pointing at herself.

Trying to tell them she was Zhao Bao'er!

Jason, because he understood her signs, actually laughed. "Jane Evans, do you have something to say? What is it you want to say?"

Jane Evans stared at him with pure hatred.

Justin impatiently urged, "Jason, whatever you're going to do, do it quickly! Then tell us about Belle!"

Jane Evans looked at Justin, stunned.

Her eyes filled with terror and despair.

Justin grabbed her hair. "Thinking we'll let you go? Impossible."

He crouched down, forcing her to look at him. "Cooperate nicely with Jason. Make him happy! Then he'll tell us where my sister is, and you can leave sooner... Otherwise, don't blame me for helping him teach you a lesson!"

He released her.

Jane Evans's forehead hit the concrete, scraping it bloody.

She stared blankly at Justin, then at Mrs. Evans.

Mrs. Evans said icily, "Little girl, you'd best cooperate and please Jason. Let him vent his anger so he helps us find our daughter!"

Justin chimed in, "Exactly! Someone as lowly as you being connected to my sister is an honor! Hurry up... Jason, what exactly do you want to do?!"

Jason gave a vicious grin. "What do I want to do? She ruined my ability to carry on the family line. Today, I'm going to ruin her!"

He pointed at Jane Evans. "I want her to strip naked right here! Let me have a good look!!"

Jane Evans's pupils constricted violently!

Mrs. Evans frowned slightly.

Justin placed a hand on her shoulder. "Mom, if you can't bear to watch, don't look..."

Mrs. Evans stared at Jane Evans.

If it weren't for her daughter, she would never treat a young girl like this...

Thinking of Belle...

She turned away resolutely and issued a cold command. "Make it quick! Don't cause a death."

"Yes."

Jason smiled triumphantly.

He ordered the bodyguards. "What are you waiting for? Get on with it! Strip her clothes off! Not a single piece left! Hahahaha..."

Several bodyguards immediately stepped forward.

Jane Evans trembled in fear, scrambling backward.

She looked at them in horror, tears welling in her eyes.

No... don't do this to her...

Please don't!

Jane Evans wept bitterly, frantically waving her hands, but it was useless.

Her sign language, her pleas for help... Justin and Mrs. Evans couldn't see them...

In this moment, she regretted it.

She shouldn't have been so stubborn, refusing to reveal herself, driving herself into a corner...

She backed into a wall.

The bodyguards closed in with lecherous smiles.

Someone grabbed her arm, yanking her up.

Other hands groped at her body.

Jane Evans struggled desperately...

But she was no match for their strength...

She cried in despair, a metallic taste of blood rising in her throat...

No!

Mom! Brother!

No!

Jane Evans screamed inside her heart.

As the sound of fabric tearing echoed—her sleeve ripped apart.

She finally found her voice:

"Mom, I—I am Belle!"
###Chapter 473

The rooftop fell into dead silence.

The bodyguards froze mid-action.

Mrs. Evans whirled around. "What did you just shout?"

Justin Evans narrowed his eyes. "Who is Belle?"

Jane Evans' throat tightened. Pain tore at her vocal cords. She opened her mouth. "Brother, I am…"

Jason Evans cut her off sharply. "A mere branch family member dares claim relation? How dare you call him 'brother'!"

Jane's words were brutally cut off.

She glared at Jason with fury.

Mrs. Evans frowned. "Jason, stripping her is too far. Choose another method."

Jason's face darkened, but he didn't dare argue. He lowered his voice. "Aunt, I can't swallow this anger! I lost my manhood! So many people saw in that operating room! My face is completely ruined! I want her to know the shame of being exposed too!"

Mrs. Evans tried to persuade him, but Justin spoke directly. "Mom, stay out of it."

He turned and barked at the bodyguards. "Why are you just standing there? Does the Evans family feed you for nothing? Can't you even handle one woman? Strip her now!"

The bodyguards immediately straightened their backs. "Yes, young master!"

Orders were everything.

The bodyguards advanced on Jane again.

Jane closed her eyes in despair.

She suddenly stood straight. She ripped off her jacket and threw it to the ground.

The bodyguards halted. "Young master, she's taking it off herself!"

Jason stared at her greedily, a lewd smile spreading across his face.

Justin sneered. "If you had been this obedient earlier, this could have been over. Stop stalling. Keep going!"

Jason excitedly raised his phone, ready to take nude photos.

Jane clenched her fists.

Her hands went to the hem of her short-sleeved shirt, as if she would pull it up the next second.

Beneath the shirt was only her underwear.

The onlookers wore triumphant, lustful smiles. Their vigilance slipped for just an instant.

At that moment!

Jane shoved the bodyguard in front of her and dashed toward the corner!

Justin roared in fury. "She's playing us! Grab her!"

The bodyguards rushed after her, but suddenly stopped.

Jane had picked up a rusty iron rod. She held it tightly with both hands, holding it horizontally in front of herself. She let out a hoarse cry, her eyes filled with resolve.

The iron rod was heavy and covered in rust. A direct hit would cause serious injury. Even a scrape could lead to a tetanus infection.

The bodyguards didn't dare approach.

Jane's hands trembled slightly, but she still held the weapon firmly.

Jason cursed loudly. "Useless! All of you are useless!"

Justin laughed coldly. "What's the hurry? Your third brother has your back."

He pushed through the crowd and walked forward, facing Jane directly.

He stared hard at her. "That rod is heavy, isn't it? But I'm not afraid! Go on, hit me here if you have the guts!"

Justin pointed at his own forehead. "Hit me right here. One blow will shatter my skull. Do you dare?"

Jane's pupils constricted. She hadn't expected Justin to be this crazy.

She aimed the iron rod at him.

Malice rose within her. She would hit anyone who came near! She would accept the consequences later! She would not be humiliated!

Jane swung the iron rod. It hit Justin's arm. Excruciating pain made his face change.

But he didn't stop advancing.

He had to subdue Jane today. He had to satisfy Jason. He needed to find his sister quickly!

Justin took another step closer. "Hit me! Aim for the head! If you don't have the nerve, then strip obediently!"

He slapped his own forehead hard again.

Mrs. Evans cried out in alarm. "Justin, stop this nonsense!"

"I'm not being nonsensical! She doesn't dare to hit me!" Justin yelled, taking another step.

Jane retreated in terror. She swung the rod hard toward Justin's head! She thought he would dodge.

Justin didn't even try to avoid it!

"Thud!"

The rod connected with his forehead.

Jane froze.

Blood slowly trickled from the corner of Justin's forehead.

At the last moment, her heart had softened, and she had diverted the blow…

Mrs. Evans screamed in furious rage. "You wretched thing! How dare you lay a hand on him!"
###Chapter 474

Jane Evans's pupils constricted.

She instinctively tried to pull the iron rod back.

But Justin Evans was faster.

He grabbed the other end of the rod.

His eyes glared at her with pure malice.

He yanked it hard.

The rod was instantly wrenched from her grasp.

Justin Evans tossed it to a bodyguard behind him.

Disarmed, Jane Evans was vulnerable.

The bodyguards swarmed her.

They pinned her down forcefully.

Mrs. Evans rushed to Justin Evans's side.

She anxiously checked his forehead.

"Is it bad?"

Justin Evans shook his head.

"Mom, it's fine."

Mrs. Evans immediately turned.

Her gaze locked onto Jane Evans with venom.

"How dare you hurt my son?!"

Jane Evans bit her lip.

Why did she do it?

Didn't they know the reason?

She was only defending herself.

At the very last moment.

She had even held back.

If she hadn't.

That blow would have been fatal.

Justin Evans wouldn't have survived.

Jane Evans clutched her head in agony.

She hated her own weakness.

Her mother and brother treated her like this.

Yet she couldn't bring herself to strike a killing blow.

Her heart felt like cold ashes.

Mrs. Evans's fury only grew.

She motioned to Justin Evans.

"Go get that bandaged."

Justin Evans refused.

"Deal with her first."

Mrs. Evans took a deep breath.

She stepped forward.

She pointed at the bodyguards holding Jane Evans.

"Step aside."

The bodyguards retreated.

Mrs. Evans looked coldly at Jane Evans.

Jane Evans lifted her head.

Her eyes burned with hatred.

Mrs. Evans was unmoved.

She pointed toward the edge of the rooftop.

Her voice was icy.

"Two choices."

"One, strip naked."

"Two, jump."

Jane Evans stared in disbelief.

Mrs. Evans spat the words.

"You dared to hurt my son."

"You won't get away with this today."

"Ten seconds to decide."

"When time's up."

"Don't blame me for being merciless."

She began the countdown.

"Ten…"

"Nine…"

…

Downstairs at the hospital.

Evelyn Ross and John Evans arrived.

John pushed her.

"Go up first!"

His leg injury wasn't healed.

He couldn't run.

Evelyn nodded.

She sprinted toward the inpatient building.

She burst into Jason's room.

It was empty.

She grabbed a passing doctor.

"Where is Jason?"

The doctor paused.

"I think he's on the rooftop."

The rooftop…

Evelyn's heart clenched.

She ran full speed toward the stairs.

Hold on, Jane.

Wait for me.

On the rooftop.

Mrs. Evans coldly watched Jane Evans.

The countdown continued.

"Five…"

"Four…"

In the stairwell.

Evelyn raced upward.

Hearing the countdown.

Her dread intensified.

She reached the stairwell door.

Just in time to hear the final number.

"One!"

Evelyn's heart stopped.

On the rooftop.

Jane Evans looked at Mrs. Evans.

Her eyes were utterly lifeless.

This woman.

Was no longer the mother she remembered.

Consumed by hatred.

She was unrecognizable.

Jane Evans's gaze swept over Justin Evans.

Over the bodyguards.

Finally landing on Jason's face.

Jason was smirking triumphantly.

Jane Evans closed her eyes.

How did she end up with such a family?

Forcing their own daughter to strip in public.

She would rather die than undress.

Jane Evans gave a bitter smile.

Mrs. Evans's voice cut through.

"Time's up."

"Since you won't choose."

"Do it."

"Rip her clothes off!"

A memory flashed.

A gentler time.

Mrs. Evans's soft voice.

"My precious."

"All the most beautiful clothes in the world are for you."

Jane Evans smiled.

She suddenly broke free from the bodyguards.

She dashed toward the rooftop edge.

She took one last look at Mrs. Evans and Justin Evans.

Mom.

Brother.

Goodbye.

Never again in this life.

She leaped.

Mrs. Evans was stung by her final glance.

But quickly regained her cold composure.

Justin Evans panicked.

"Mom, she jumped!"

Mrs. Evans snorted.

"The fourth floor won't kill her."

She looked at Jason.

"Feel better now?"

Jason nodded.

Mrs. Evans pressed immediately.

"Where is my daughter?"

Justin Evans also urged anxiously.

"She jumped."

"Now tell us where my sister is!"

Before he finished.

Evelyn's shout came from the rooftop entrance.

"Mrs. Evans, stop!"

"Jane is your real daughter!"
###Chapter 475

Evelyn Ross broke through the security guards and bodyguards, her voice urgent.

She fought off those blocking her way, worried she was running out of time.

After shouting, she kicked the person in front of her aside.

That person flew backward, clearing a path for her.

She seized the opportunity and rushed in.

Mrs. Evans and Justin Evans stared at her in shock.

Mrs. Evans frowned and asked, "What did you say?"

Evelyn quickly explained, "Jane is your biological daughter! The one you've been searching for!"

Justin gasped in disbelief, angrily retorting, "Impossible! Grace, don't you dare lie to us!"

Evelyn's tone was firm. "I'm not lying. Weren't you looking for a jade pendant? That pendant belongs to Jane! Jason stole it from her!"

Justin's legs weakened, and he staggered back. "What did you say?!"

Mrs. Evans also widened her eyes. "That can't be!"

Jason shouted, "Grace! You're spouting nonsense! Even to save someone, there's no need to tell such a lie!"

Evelyn took a deep breath. "If you don't believe me, you can do a DNA test..."

She didn't bother saying more and scanned the rooftop, but she didn't see Jane.

She couldn't help asking, "Where is Jane?"

Mrs. Evans and Justin just stared at her blankly, not answering.

The surrounding bodyguards glanced toward the edge of the rooftop.

Evelyn's heart sank.

No, it can't be...

Suddenly, shouts came from below. "Someone jumped!"

"Quick, to the ER!"

Evelyn rushed to the edge of the rooftop.

She saw the girl in a white T-shirt and jeans, lying on the ground like a broken doll.

There was blood all around her... But the angle of the jump was relatively fortunate. She hit a tree first, cushioning her fall before landing on the ground.

So she was still breathing.

Doctors and nurses below surrounded her, placed her on a stretcher, and hurried toward the operating room!

John was a step too slow. He had just reached the next floor when he saw his sister falling through the stairwell window!

He stood frozen in horror, then ran down frantically, rushing to Jane's side.

Limping, he followed the doctors and nurses, sobbing and pleading, "Please, Doctor, you have to save my sister! She's only twenty!"

John's cries echoed from a distance.

Evelyn's heart ached. She immediately straightened up and prepared to run downstairs.

Mrs. Evans grabbed her arm, her voice trembling. "What did you just say?!"

Evelyn pushed her away, her expression cold and disappointed. "I said, the girl you pushed and drove to jump is your biological daughter! Belle Winston!"

"That's impossible!" Mrs. Evans's face instantly turned pale.

"Yes, it's impossible! My sister is a princess! How could she be someone like that?!" Justin roared angrily beside her, unable to accept this reality.

Evelyn looked at them coldly, a sarcastic and icy smile on her lips. "Someone like what? Someone poor? But it's that poor John you speak of who saved her! Without John, your sister would have died years ago!"

Justin took a step back. "No, this isn't true. You must be lying! I don't believe it!"

"Believe what you want!" Evelyn threw these words aside and anxiously ran toward the operating room.

She was worried about Jane's condition, afraid she wouldn't make it.

After Evelyn left the rooftop, Mrs. Evans's legs gave way, and she nearly collapsed, saved by a bodyguard.

She stared intently in the direction Evelyn had left, then looked at Jason, pursed her lips, and asked, "Jason, what is going on?"

Justin also spoke up. "Jason, she's lying to us, right?! That jade pendant, where did you really get it?"

Jason immediately replied, "Aunt, Third Brother, don't believe her nonsense! I bought that jade pendant at an antique market, and I've already found your sister for you!"

He took out a DNA comparison report and handed it to Justin. "Third Brother, look. This is the DNA comparison I had done for you. I've already confirmed her identity..."

Justin stepped forward, took the report, glanced at it, sighed in relief, and handed it to Mrs. Evans. "Mom, look. This is the real sister! Jane is not our sister! Not our sister!"

Justin was emotionally agitated, repeating it over and over.

Mrs. Evans coldly looked down at the DNA comparison report.

She then looked at Jason.

Jason gave an awkward smile. "Aunt, don't believe her nonsense. She just wants to make you feel guilty! The sister I found is already on her way to the hospital! I've already done the DNA test for you. You can be reunited soon!"

Mrs. Evans looked at the comparison report in her hand. It showed a sibling match between the subject and Justin.

Mrs. Evans clenched her jaw, remained silent for a moment, then looked at Jason again.

She turned and strode out.

Justin followed her. "Mom, where are you going?"

Mrs. Evans answered directly, "To see the girl who just jumped!"

Justin said, "Mom, why go see her? You don't actually believe Grace's nonsense, do you?"

Mrs. Evans stopped in her tracks.

"I'm going to find her and do a DNA test!"
###Chapter 476

Mrs. Evans threw down those words and strode away without a backward glance.

Justin's heart sank like a stone.

He stared fixedly at her retreating back, his fists clenched so tightly his knuckles turned white.

In the depths of his eyes was stunned disbelief.

He couldn't believe it.

The sister he had desperately searched for over a decade had been driven to this point… by his own hand.

He glanced back at Jason.

Jason was being supported by someone, standing with his legs spread in an awkward stance.

Panic was written all over his face. "Third Brother, you have to believe me!"

Justin rushed at him, grabbing his collar. "Tell me she's not my sister!"

"Yes, yes! She's not!"

Justin stared at Jason, his fingers trembling slightly.

After a long moment, he released his grip and patted Jason's shoulder. "Good. Remember what you said."

He turned and charged out the door, running in the direction Mrs. Evans had gone.

Outside the hospital operating room.

Mrs. Evans arrived with her entourage in a hurry.

Seeing the lit surgery sign, her steps suddenly slowed.

She didn't dare go closer.

She was even afraid to verify it.

Was the girl she drove to jump really her Belle?

Mrs. Evans trembled from head to toe.

John spotted her immediately, fury igniting in his eyes.

He rushed over and grabbed her arm. "What did you do to Jane?! Why did she jump!"

There had been many witnesses when Jane fell.

Everyone confirmed she had jumped on her own.

The hospital hadn't called the police because of this.

But John knew Jane had just received her acceptance letter from the Nora Research Institute. She would never commit suicide.

They must have driven her to it…

Mrs. Evans looked at John, inexplicably guilty.

She opened her mouth, but no words came out.

Justin rushed over and shoved John away. "Get away from my mother!"

John staggered back, nearly falling.

He glared viciously at both of them and charged forward again. "You were on the rooftop too? Tell me what you did to Jane!"

Justin shouted sharply, "Don't go too far! Come any closer and I won't hold back! You're no match for me!"

John stood opposite them, staring them down.

After a long pause, he suddenly spoke. "Do you even know Jane is the daughter you've been searching for for seventeen years! How could you treat her like this? Won't you regret it!"

Before Mrs. Evans could speak, Justin roared, "I said she's not! Stop spouting nonsense!"

He simply couldn't accept this fact!

John charged forward emotionally. "No wonder she was so upset after going to see you that day! It was because you rejected her! Fine! If you won't acknowledge her, then she's my real sister! You hurt her today, I'll fight you to the death!"

John raised his crutch and swung it hard at Justin.

Justin meant to push him away, but suddenly remembered Jane raising a stick against him on the rooftop.

She hadn't even been afraid to die then.

If she had used force, his head would have been smashed.

But she had held back in the end.

This thought made Justin pause. He turned and took the blow squarely on his back without resisting.

Consumed by rage, John raised his crutch again after the first strike!

"You bullied Jane! If you didn't want to acknowledge her, why look for her? Because she doesn't know your hypocritical etiquette? Or because she's mute and you look down on her?"

"Even if you didn't want to reunite, you didn't have to drive her to her death! How could you be so cruel!"

As he hit and shouted, John suddenly threw down his crutch, crouched on the ground, and wept bitterly. "When I found Jane, she had a high fever that ruined her voice. In her delirium, she kept crying for her brother to run… Justin, you bastard, you're her brother!"

Justin was agitated by his crying.

His heart felt like it was being squeezed by an invisible hand!

He yelled at John, "I told you, she's not my sister! Jason already helped us find our sister!"

"Not her?"

John looked up sharply.

He stretched out his hand, covered in Jane's blood.

He thrust it toward them. "This is Jane's blood. If you don't believe me, go test it!"

Justin was frightened by his demeanor.

He looked at Mrs. Evans pleadingly.

Mrs. Evans pressed her lips together, then suddenly spoke. "Fine. We'll test it now."
###Chapter 477

Mrs. Evans’s eyes were resolute as she gave the butler a slight nod.

The butler immediately found a nurse to collect Justin’s blood sample with a cotton swab.

He then looked at Justin.

Justin was about to speak when Mrs. Evans pulled out one of her own hairs and handed it over. "Do it now."

"Yes."

The butler hurried away.

Justin grabbed Mrs. Evans’s hand. "Mom, how can you believe such lies?"

His voice trembled. "How could I not recognize my own sister? I adored her when we were kids… She was kidnapped trying to save me… If she were standing right in front of me, I would know her instantly."

"So it can't be her… It's absolutely impossible… Mom, don't be fooled!"

Evelyn Ross looked at him coldly. "Justin, are you scared?"

Justin froze.

Evelyn stepped closer. "You're afraid that Jane, fighting for her life in that operating room, is the little sister you personally condemned to death! Isn't that right?!"

Justin staggered back.

His face flushed red. "What nonsense are you talking?! I told you, it can't be her!"

Crack—

A jade pendant was thrown to the ground by John.

John’s eyes were bloodshot. "Haven't you been searching for this pendant? Our sister wore it since she was a baby! How can you still not believe it?!"

Jane had once tried to discard the pendant when she didn't want to be recognized. John had retrieved it from the trash.

Justin picked up the pendant, examined it closely, and sneered. "John, you're pathetic! Knowing we're looking for the owner, you bring us a fake?!"

John was stunned. "A fake?"

His sister had worn this pendant since they found her. Even when their parents were gravely ill and they had no money for treatment, they never considered selling it.

But now, it was a fake?

John frowned. "Impossible! She's worn it since she was little!"

"Still lying?" Justin seized the excuse. "Mom, see? They're deceiving us! The person in that operating room cannot be our sister!"

Mrs. Evans remained silent.

Evelyn suddenly laughed coldly. "So the pendant is a fake. No wonder…"

Justin whirled to face her. "What do you mean?"

Evelyn said calmly, "Jason took Jane's pendant and told me to find him. This pendant was returned to Jane by Jason… No wonder it's a fake. The real one was given to you long ago! He knew all along that Jane was your sister!"

"You're lying!" Justin roared. "I don't believe it! Not a single word!"

Evelyn curled her lips in a mocking smile and said nothing more.

Justin felt himself crumbling under her gaze. "You can't fool me! The DNA test will take thirty minutes at most. Let's see what story you spin then!"

He guided Mrs. Evans to the side, but his eyes remained fixed on the operating room doors.

Memories of his first meeting with Jane surfaced—that inexplicable sense of familiarity…

Justin shook his head violently.

Impossible…

Jane absolutely could not be his sister!!

But he didn't notice his own red-rimmed eyes.

His gaze was glued to the operating room doors.

Bullying her had meant nothing to him then. Now, all he wanted was for her to survive…

Thirty minutes later, hurried footsteps approached.

The butler returned swiftly with the DNA test report, his expression grave.

Justin spoke first. "The results are in? Tell me! They were lying, right?!"

The butler hesitated for a moment before handing the report to Mrs. Evans.

Mrs. Evans’s hands trembled, as if this single sheet of paper held her destiny…

Justin snatched it away. "Mom, there's nothing to see! I told you it couldn't be her!"

He ripped open the report, his eyes darting straight to the results column—

He stood frozen, as if struck by lightning.

…99.99% probability of a biological mother-daughter relationship.
###Chapter 478

Justin Evans stared at the report.

His pupils contracted sharply.

His fingers trembled slightly.

"Impossible…"

Mrs. Evans snatched the report.

The moment she saw the result, she staggered backward.

Her gaze snapped toward the operating room.

She threw herself at the door, pounding on it frantically.

"Belle! It's my Belle!"

"Save her! You must save her!"

The Evans family butler urgently summoned specialists.

Doctors and nurses rushed into the operating room.

The hospital director hurried over.

"Mrs. Evans?"

Mrs. Evans grabbed the director's arm.

"That's my daughter!"

"My daughter I've been searching for seventeen years!"

"Save her at any cost!"

The director nodded gravely.

"I'll go in and check."

The atmosphere inside the operating room was tense.

The lead surgeon's forehead beaded with sweat.

"We can't locate the internal bleeding points."

"The technique required is too advanced."

"None of us can do it."

A nurse suddenly cried out.

"Blood pressure dropping!"

"Heart rate slowing!"

The lead surgeon's hands shook more violently.

"The blood transfused just flows right out."

"Four transfusion ports can't keep up."

The director strode out of the operating room.

Mrs. Evans and Justin immediately surrounded him.

"How is my daughter?"

The director shook his head heavily.

"She's losing blood too fast."

"We can't find the bleeding points."

Mrs. Evans screamed hoarsely.

"Use blood! However much is needed!"

Justin thrust out his arm.

"Take mine! I'm type B!"

The director stopped him.

"Siblings can't donate blood."

"It causes hemolytic disease."

Mrs. Evans grabbed the director.

"What can we do then?"

The director took a deep breath.

"We can only attempt blind sutures."

"The risk is very high."

Justin slapped himself hard.

"It's all my fault!"

"I forced my sister to jump!"

He slid down the wall.

His eyes rimmed red.

Mrs. Evans clutched the director's arm.

"You must save her!"

The director sighed helplessly.

"Unless Nora is here."

"Otherwise…"

Mrs. Evans' eyes filled with despair.

"Nora is abroad…"

The director shook his head.

"There's no time."

"Miss Evans can't wait."
###Chapter 479

Evelyn Ross was taken aback by this.

How could it be her?

She wasn't specialized in surgery.

She had only acquired some medical knowledge while researching pharmaceuticals...

As she was puzzling over this, Justin Evans voiced the same question. "Isn't Researcher Nora specialized in pharmaceuticals? How could she be the one to save my sister?"

The hospital director immediately explained. "Miss Evans's bleeding point is deep within her internal organs. We've identified a probable location based on conjecture. The suturing process carries extremely high risk. All the doctors are under immense psychological pressure. In this situation, only Researcher Nora can maintain absolutely stable hands."

He continued, "From what I know, Researcher Nora participated in a highly complex surgery overseas. The lead surgeon became too nervous to continue, and she took over and completed it. Her mental fortitude and operational stability are practically made for surgery!"

Evelyn Ross: "..."

So that was why.

Her steady hands were purely a result of all the odd jobs she worked in San Francisco.

After leaving the Ashcroft family, she had taken on many types of work.

She delivered packages, eventually founding her own delivery company.

She worked as a chef, later taking over her mentor's restaurant.

She had also worked at an equestrian club, even dabbled in computing...

She was young then, unable to find formal employment, so she took on miscellaneous jobs.

To keep those jobs, she perfected her skills in every field.

Even for something as simple as tightening screws, she earned the title of "Master Mechanic" at the steel plant.

It all came down to these hands.

She stepped forward directly. "My hands are very steady. Let me do it."

Everyone looked at her simultaneously.

The hospital director frowned. "This..."

Mrs. Evans scrutinized her.

Justin Evans immediately objected. "Grace, stop joking around! This is my biological sister!"

Evelyn ignored him and walked straight to the director. "My hands are very steady. I've observed surgeries. You guide me, I can complete it."

The director studied her for a long moment before hesitantly saying, "Miss Ross, the steadiness of an average person is not the same as the steadiness required for surgery..."

Evelyn walked directly toward the operating room. "Stop wasting time. The patient can't wait."

The director was stunned.

Mrs. Evans frowned. "Miss Ross..."

Evelyn looked at her. "Mrs. Evans, you don't trust me either?"

Mrs. Evans bit her lip.

A moment later, she ordered the director. "Let her try!"

She then said to Justin, "Contact Researcher Nora immediately! See if she can get here!"

Justin replied, "Yes!"

The director, helpless, led Evelyn into the operating room.

While changing and sterilizing, the director said quietly, "Miss Ross, it's not too late to back out now. Seeing the bloody scene and performing precise operations isn't something everyone can handle..."

Evelyn didn't bother with words, quickly changing into surgical scrubs.

She walked to the operating table. The lead surgeon was sweating profusely, his hands trembling slightly.

On the monitor, Jane Evans's vital signs continued to decline.

A slender hand reached over and took the instrument from him.

A calm female voice spoke. "What's next? You direct, I operate."

...

Outside the operating room.

Mrs. Evans and Justin paced anxiously.

Justin couldn't help complaining. "Mom, how could you let Grace mess around? She doesn't know anything about medicine! What if something happens to my sister..."

Mrs. Evans was distraught.

Earlier, meeting Evelyn's gaze, she had inexplicably felt a sense of trust.

Now, she was filled with regret.

She asked John Evans, "Do you know about Miss Ross's medical skills?"

John stared intently at the operating room door and shook his head. "But I believe she wouldn't act recklessly. Grace always knows her limits."

Justin said angrily, "What good is your belief! Why didn't you say something earlier!"

He then said to Mrs. Evans, "Mom, she's definitely doing this on purpose! She knows Jane is my sister and wants me to feel guilty for the rest of my life! That woman is pure evil!"

Mrs. Evans scolded, "Be quiet! The doctors were already out of options! Even if something really happens... you are not to blame Miss Ross!"

Justin kicked the wall in frustration, squatting down and clutching his head. "She's getting back at me! Making sure I never get my sister's forgiveness!"

Mrs. Evans ignored him.

John swallowed nervously, not in the mood to explain.

The three of them stared fixedly at the operating room door.

After what felt like an eternity, the door finally opened.

They instantly tensed up.
###Chapter 480

Their bodies tensed.

Their eyes remained locked on the operating room doors.

The Chief of Surgery emerged first.

He wiped cold sweat from his forehead, his face as pale as paper.

His expression made their hearts stop.

Justin Evans rushed forward.

"Chief, how is my sister?"

The Chief's voice trembled. "We did our best... but we failed to stop the bleeding..."

Justin froze instantly.

Mrs. Evans staggered backward.

She braced herself against the wall to stay upright.

The light in John Evans' eyes completely vanished.

Evelyn Ross followed them out.

She removed her surgical cap and mask, smoothing her disheveled hair.

Justin immediately confronted her. "Did you kill my sister?"

Evelyn raised an eyebrow but remained silent.

The Chief quickly intervened. "Justin, you misunderstand..."

"What else could it be? It was her!"

Justin was nearly out of control.

The Chief hurried to explain. "Miss Evans is out of danger!"

Justin stared blankly. "What?"

The Chief said awkwardly, "We couldn't locate the bleeding point. Miss Ross took over and successfully performed blind sutures. The bleeding has stopped! She saved the situation entirely."

Evelyn spoke calmly. "It was nothing. Your guidance was helpful."

The Chief fell silent.

Theory was one thing.

But difficult surgeries required steady, precise, and decisive skill.

The hospital bed was wheeled out.

Jane Evans lay quietly on it.

The Chief continued his report. "Miss Evans has multiple fractures, including her lower leg, arm, and ribs... Internal bleeding is under control. No brain injury. She needs ICU observation."

Mrs. Evans asked urgently, "When will she wake up?"

The Chief shook his head. "It's uncertain. Her body is weak. She should wake after anesthesia, but she's been under extreme stress recently. She might remain unconscious longer."

Mrs. Evans looked at her daughter with heartache.

She and Justin followed the bed to the ICU doors.

Through the glass window.

Jane's limbs were suspended and immobilized.

Her pale, small face lacked vitality, like a porcelain doll shattered and hastily reassembled.

Justin suddenly slapped himself hard.

"It's all my fault! I'm a complete bastard!"

Mrs. Evans wept. "I spoiled you too much. I let you hurt your own sister!"

"It's my mistake!"

Justin looked devastated. "Mom, I finally understand... What if my sister encountered someone like me out there? I deserve to die!"

He slapped himself again.

His swollen cheeks couldn't ease his regret.

He recalled how he had treated his sister.

He ruined her internship opportunity.

He drove her to jump...

Justin raised his hand to punish himself again.

Mrs. Evans clutched her chest, gasping. "I failed as a mother. My mistake is greater!"

Evelyn and John exchanged a silent glance.

Evelyn reminded them softly. "Instead of blaming yourselves, consider how to deal with Jason Evans."

Her words woke them.

Mrs. Evans and Justin's expressions changed instantly.

Justin gritted his teeth. "You're right! Jason not only hid the jade pendant's origin, he encouraged us to bully Jane knowing she was our sister! What was his intention?"

Mrs. Evans clenched her fists. "He was toying with us!"

Justin turned to leave.

Mrs. Evans asked urgently, "Where are you going?"

"To avenge my sister! Mom, let us young people handle our own problems."

Justin strode away.

Mrs. Evans hesitated, but ultimately did not follow.

She looked at Evelyn, her lips trembling for a long time before she managed two words. "Thank you."

"Don't mention it."

Evelyn responded indifferently. "I saved my friend's sister. But the Evans family owes me a favor. How do you plan to repay it?"

Mrs. Evans stated immediately. "As long as it's reasonable, the Evans family will repay it!"

She had already vowed privately that if someone helped her find her daughter, she would give half her family fortune.

Evelyn said, "I just want to complete our cooperation with the Evans family."

Mrs. Evans was stunned. "That's all?"

"Yes."

Evelyn thought for a moment. "I own a medical research institute. I hope to sign a ten-year supply contract. Your hospitals will distribute our medications."

Nora Research Institute's products were highly sought after by major hospitals.

She didn't want to take advantage of the Evans family. Mutual benefit was the best option.

But Mrs. Evans misunderstood.

There were thousands of medical research institutes in the country.

Apart from Nora Research Institute, they all begged to cooperate with the Evans family.

Since their benefactor asked, purchasing some insignificant medications was fine.

It could be considered an annual fund to repay the favor.

Mrs. Evans nodded faintly. "What's the name of your institute?"
###Chapter 481

Evelyn Ross was momentarily stunned by Mrs. Evans's question.

Her research institute was publicly known as the Nora Research Institute.

But that wasn't its full name.

It was merely an abbreviation.

As for the full name…

Evelyn paused, unable to recall it immediately.

She owned far too many businesses.

Courier companies, restaurants, equestrian clubs, racetracks…

After a moment of thought, she spoke. "If you have no objections, I'll draft the contract first. I'll inform you of the full name upon signing."

Mrs. Evans nodded readily. "Alright."

They had reached an agreement.

Evelyn brought up another matter. "Regarding my elder brother's oligospermia diagnosis, I hope you can assist with the investigation."

Mrs. Evans's expression turned grave. "I've already looked into it. Our attending physician went abroad for a seminar and never returned. He only sent a resignation email."

Evelyn's gaze sharpened.

The doctor who treated Marcus Valentine at the Valentine private hospital had done the same.

Three doctors had resigned simultaneously.

All were headhunted by foreign medical institutions.

All lost contact.

This was no coincidence.

Someone was targeting Marcus Valentine.

Evelyn frowned.

Her first thought was of that mysterious Nan family.

…

Meanwhile.

In Jason Evans's hospital room.

Jason was restless with anxiety.

He abruptly threw off the covers and got out of bed.

His legs were spread wide in an awkward stance.

"Hurry, pack our things. We're going home!"

He needed to leave this place immediately!

The hospital room door was suddenly kicked open!

Jason had just turned around when a heavy punch slammed into his face!

He was sent flying and landed heavily on the floor.

Justin Evans stormed in, kicking and punching him!

"My sister broke three ribs. Today, I'll break three of yours!"

Justin struck with ruthless precision, directly breaking three of Jason's ribs.

Jason spat blood and begged for mercy. "Third Brother, spare me!"

"Spare you?" Justin's eyes were icy. "How many years did I search for my sister? Why did you deceive us?!"

Jason suddenly erupted. "Were you ever good to me?!"

Justin's movements halted.

Jason screamed hoarsely. "You either hit or scolded me! Treated me like a lackey! When did you ever treat me like a person?! I deceived you all to watch you be made a fool! To watch you bully Jane! Bully your own sister!"

"Your branch of the family acts so high and mighty! After she was lost, she was just some poor wretch! Crushed beneath my feet! What gives you the right to be so arrogant?!"

Tears streamed down Jason's face; he looked deranged.

Justin's expression darkened.

He looked down at Jason, his voice cold. "No excuse can hide your twisted psychology! Jason, you must pay the price!"

He viciously kicked Jason's shin.

The sound of bone cracking was clearly audible.

Then he broke his arm.

Every injury his sister suffered, he returned to Jason one by one!

Piercing screams filled the hospital room.

After venting his rage, Justin looked at the barely conscious Jason and finally stopped.

He turned and left, returning to the ICU.

Mrs. Evans looked at him. "Settled?"

Justin took a deep breath. "He's unconscious. Once his injuries heal, I'll force him to jump off a rooftop."

Mrs. Evans nodded.

She looked at Evelyn. "Miss Ross saved Jane. She is our family's benefactor. I've agreed to cooperate with her research institute. All our affiliated hospitals will import her pharmaceuticals."

Justin was surprised. "You have a medical research institute too?"

"Yes."

Evelyn was having someone draft the contract. "The contract will be sent shortly. We can sign if there are no issues."

Mrs. Evans asked, "How much import volume do you think is appropriate?"

Evelyn pondered.

The Nan family wanted her to cooperate with the five major families.

The deal with the Evans family couldn't be too small.

She looked up. "Fifty million per month. How does that sound?"

Mrs. Evans was taken aback.

Justin's face instantly turned cold.

Fifty million!

Fifty million monthly meant six hundred million annually!

Six billion over ten years!

This price had only ever been offered to the Nora Research Institute.

To compete for the rights to Nora's new products.

This woman seemed indifferent, but her asking price was ruthlessly steep!

Justin wanted to object.

Mrs. Evans stopped him.

Justin was displeased. "Mother!"

Mrs. Evans shook her head. "Is Jane's life not worth six billion?"

Six billion was less than half the Evans family's wealth.

Justin fell silent.

Mrs. Evans said, "Justin, you will handle the contract. Nora's new product is launching next month. This is a good opportunity for you to learn how to cooperate and strive for the new product."

Justin reluctantly nodded.

"Ding—"

Evelyn's phone chimed.

She raised an eyebrow. "The contract is here. Would you like to review it first, Young Master Evans?"

"Fine."

Justin opened the contract and glanced at it.

The next moment, he saw the full name of the Party A company and froze on the spot.
###Chapter 482

Justin Evans stared at the name of Party A on the contract, his brow furrowed tightly.

He suspected his eyes were playing tricks on him.

He rubbed his eyes and looked again.

The Zhao Group had always been Party A, but this time they were listed as Party B.

The Party A field clearly stated: Watson Biopharma Inc.

The name seemed familiar.

Yet he couldn't immediately recall where he had seen it.

Irritated, Justin Evans looked at Evelyn Ross. "Grace, we're the buyers. We should be Party A."

Evelyn's tone was calm. "Our institute's rule is that the seller is always Party A."

Justin Evans was about to retort when Mrs. Evans stopped him. "Justin, watch your attitude!"

Justin Evans snorted coldly and turned away.

Evelyn had achieved her goal, and Jane was now out of danger.

She nodded to John Evans and turned to leave the hospital.

Just outside the main entrance, Justin Evans couldn't hold back his complaint. "Mom! Besides the Nora Research Institute, who dares to be Party A in front of the Evans family?!"

Mrs. Evans glared at him. "Is your sister's life unimportant?"

Justin Evans was left speechless.

After a long moment, he muttered, "I'll stamp and mail the contract tomorrow."

"Mm."

Mrs. Evans' gaze returned to the ICU ward.

Her only concern now was when her daughter would wake up.

Justin Evans followed her gaze, guilt gradually appearing on his face.

He knew he was also responsible for his sister's condition.

Beating up Jason Evans was just him venting his powerlessness.

He finally understood what karma meant.

Spoiled excessively since childhood, he had developed a lawless personality.

Seeking thrills, bragging, bullying others...

Even when forcing Jane to jump, he had felt impatient.

How could he treat a girl like that?

Even if she wasn't his biological sister, he shouldn't have!

Overwhelmed with regret, Justin Evans fell to his knees before the ICU doors.

Mrs. Evans clenched her fingers but did not stop him.

John Evans glared at them viciously.

He believed Justin Evans deserved to kneel in atonement.

Without Justin Evans's indulgence and the Evans family's protection, Jason would never have driven his sister to despair.

The ones who forced his sister to jump were not just Jason.

But also Mrs. Evans and Justin Evans!

They were all culprits!

John clenched his fists, charged over, and struck Justin Evans's back with a stick. "Get out! My sister doesn't want to see you! All of you, get out!"

Justin Evans didn't dare fight back, silently kneeling with his head bowed.

His body trembled with each blow.

After five strikes, he finally couldn't bear it and tried to dodge.

John continued hitting him. "Get out! She won't wake up with you here!"

Justin Evans froze.

John then pointed at Mrs. Evans. "And you, get out too!"

Mrs. Evans' eyes reddened. "Fine, we'll leave."

She wiped her tears and turned to leave.

Justin Evans staggered after her.

They walked down the corridor one after the other and sat on a bench around the corner.

Mrs. Evans wouldn't leave, and neither would Justin Evans.

If Jane didn't want to see them, they would keep their distance.

But they couldn't abandon Jane.

She was family they had searched for over seventeen years...

Justin Evans suddenly looked up. "Mom, do you think..."

He choked up. "...will Sister still acknowledge us?"

Mrs. Evans pressed her lips together, silent.

They sat in silence.

Justin Evans buried his head and wept.

After a long while, he lifted his head. "Mom, you're right. Grace saved Sister. I'd give her the entire Evans family, not just the contract!"

Mrs. Evans sighed.

Just then, the butler rushed over. "Madam, Young Master, it's terrible!"

"What is it?"

Mrs. Evans asked sharply.

The butler gasped for breath. "We just received news. A foreign entity has issued a blockade order against us. They want to acquire the Zhao Group at a low price!"

Mrs. Evans stood up abruptly. "What?"

Justin Evans was also stunned. "Who have we offended?"

Mother and son exchanged a look.

Justin Evans said hesitantly, "Among those we've recently offended, the only one with the power to retaliate is Evelyn Ross, right?"

Mrs. Evans shook her head. "If it were her, there'd be no need for cooperation. The Xu family doesn't have that much influence abroad."

Justin Evans asked the butler, "Which specific entity is it?"

The butler wiped his sweat. "It's still unclear, but the other party is very powerful. Several partner companies have already terminated cooperation since the news broke..."

"So mysterious?"

Justin Evans panicked.

Mrs. Evans took a deep breath. "What is there to fear? We'll meet force with force! Transfer $850 million to Miss Evelyn immediately. Pay all cooperation fees in full."

Justin Evans was taken aback. "Why?"

Mrs. Evans lowered her eyes. "Miss Evelyn saved your sister's life. We owe her this money. Even if the Zhao Group goes bankrupt, we cannot owe her!"

Justin Evans nodded heavily. "You're right, Mom."

Amid the chaos in the Evans family, in the study of the Nan family villa.

Alexander Vance was in an international meeting.

Thomas Watson reported, "The overseas campaign has been successful. Boss, are you sure about acquiring the Zhao Group? The disadvantages outweigh the advantages!"

Evelyn had just taken a taxi back to the Nan residence.
###Chapter 483

Thomas Watson was in the study, trying to reason with him.

"Boss, forcing them to cooperate with Miss Ross is simple enough."

"There's really no need to go down the acquisition route…"

"When we established our overseas operations, you personally stated we wouldn't target any Chinese enterprises."

"Besides, these domestic assets are insignificant to you."

"Why engage in a fight where we lose as much as we gain?"

Alexander Vance's expression remained cold and detached.

"The Vance Group's entry into New York has already disrupted the balance among the five major families."

"Eliminating the Evans family will allow the Vances to smoothly join the ranks of the five major families."

Thomas smirked.

"Stop making excuses! Wouldn't six major families be just fine?"

Alexander glanced at his computer screen.

"I find five more pleasing than six."

Thomas was baffled.

This was clearly all for Miss Ross!

And here he was, pretending otherwise. How infuriating!

The original plan was a gradual acquisition.

But then Mrs. Amelia suddenly went to the Nan residence today.

She took Miss Ross out to apologize.

That's when the Boss completely lost his composure!

Just as Thomas was about to continue persuading him, the study door suddenly opened.

Evelyn Ross walked in.

Alexander's previously stern face softened instantly the moment she entered.

He stood up and asked gently, "Why are you back so late? You must be exhausted today."

His tone then turned sharp. "Did the Evans family give you any trouble?"

Evelyn waved her hand dismissively. "No."

"They're probably grateful to me right now. Why would they cause trouble?"

Alexander was slightly taken aback. "What?"

Evelyn recounted the day's events in detail.

She told him about discovering Jane's jade pendant might be connected to the Evans family.

She mentioned seeking out John Evans for information.

Then going to find Jane.

Finally, facilitating the reunion between Mrs. Evans and Jane on the hospital rooftop.

She concluded with a sigh. "Mrs. Evans and Justin both looked terrible."

"Of course. The daughter and sister they've cherished since childhood…"

"To realize they've been mistreating her all along…"

"They must be filled with regret now."

Alexander was silent for a moment. "So… the cooperation with the Evans family is settled?"

"Yes, I gave them the contract. They'll probably sign it in a day or two…"

Before she could finish, her phone rang.

It was the director of the Nora Research Institute.

"Director Nora, the Evans Group just transferred six billion in advance payment to our company."

Evelyn was puzzled.

After a moment's pause, she replied, "Just accept it for now. They were going to pay eventually anyway."

"Alright. I'll go to the Evans Group tomorrow to expedite the contract."

"What kind of situation is this? The contract isn't even signed, and the money's already here…"

After the director hung up, Evelyn shook her head and stretched.

"I'm going to take a shower."

She had been running around all day and was utterly exhausted.

After Evelyn entered the bedroom, Thomas's voice came from the computer.

"Boss, do you find six more pleasing than five now?"

Alexander's response was curt. "Get lost."

"Understood. I'll notify them to lift the sanctions on the Evans Group then~"

Without hesitation, Alexander closed his laptop, cutting off the call.

—

At the Evans residence.

The eldest and second sons of the Evans family had rushed back from out of town.

They bombarded with questions as soon as they entered.

"What's going on? Why was the Evans Group suddenly sanctioned?"

"Who did we offend? Several overseas partners have suspended cooperation with us!"

"There's no news domestically yet, but friends abroad said someone started shorting our stock tonight!"

"They're also trying to acquire our company!"

"Who is behind this? They're so elusive…"

"Where's Mom? And the third brother? Why aren't they home?"

Amid the chaos, the eldest Evans's phone rang.

It was a partner, speaking urgently. "Mr. Evans, it's a misunderstanding! A huge misunderstanding!"

The eldest Evans was stunned. "What?"

The other party quickly explained. "We made a mistake."

"It turns out that influential figure wasn't targeting you after all."

"Haha, it was all a misunderstanding! They've verbally called off the sanctions against you!"

"Our cooperation continues as usual!"

The Evans family members were speechless.

What was this?!

They were being toyed with!

…

How the Evans family experienced their world collapsing and then being saved was of no concern to Alexander.

Scaring them a little was nothing.

Who told them to give Evelyn a hard time before?

That night, Alexander held Evelyn from behind as they lay in bed.

They spoke in hushed tones. "So, Marcus was truly set up?"

Evelyn nodded. "Yes."

"But I've investigated. The three doctors who examined him have no connection to each other."

"He specifically chose three people who couldn't possibly all give wrong results."

"Yet they were all wrong."

"Don't you find it strange? Why would everyone in the medical industry lie?"

Alexander's eyes darkened. "You know technological monopolies exist in this world."

Evelyn looked at him, surprised.

Alexander spoke calmly. "There are many unknown organizations and clubs in this world."

"Their goal is to monopolize global technology."

"As long as they control the top technology in any industry, they are the kings of that industry!"

Evelyn pursed her lips. "Are you saying there's such an organization in the medical industry?"

"That their king ordered Marcus must be diagnosed with azoospermia?"

"So no doctor in the world dares to disobey?"

Alexander nodded. "It's possible."

Evelyn said softly, "I think you've hit on the truth."

The Nan family was so mysterious.

She and Mason had been searching globally for so long without any leads.

This proved there were indeed many capable people hidden in the shadows.

While ordinary people lived their daily lives…

How many geniuses were secretly manipulating global economic development and technological progress…

As Evelyn and Alexander lost in their respective thoughts, Alexander's phone suddenly rang.

It was Eleanor Vance. "You brat, I'm already in New York. When are you coming to see me?"

Alexander smiled, looking tenderly at Evelyn. "Grandmother, I'll find time to visit."

Eleanor hummed in response. "Brat, I heard you're engaged again?"

"This time to that Ashcroft family's eldest daughter?"

"How could you? It hasn't even been half a year since my granddaughter-in-law passed."

"You're engaged to someone else. Aren't you afraid she'll be sad?"

Alexander was momentarily stunned, then chuckled.

After a while, he said, "Grandmother, she won't be sad."

"Because she wants me to be engaged to the Ashcroft family's eldest daughter more than anyone else."

As he spoke, he moved closer to Evelyn's ear.

Evelyn glared at him fiercely.

Eleanor sighed. "Brat, I'm sure my granddaughter-in-law wants you to move on quickly."

"But not like this."

"I heard you've been clinging to that eldest daughter all day?"

"Even working as her driver…"

"To me, there's only one granddaughter-in-law!"

Eleanor's tone was petulant.

Alexander suddenly laughed. "Grandmother, how about I let the Ashcroft family's eldest daughter say hello to you?"

He moved the phone to Evelyn's ear.
###Chapter 484

Evelyn Ross was about to speak to Eleanor Vance.

But the line suddenly went dead.

She paused, slightly startled.

She realized Eleanor had hung up on her.

Evelyn Ross: "..."

Evelyn sighed softly.

Alexander did not call back.

Discussing these matters over the phone was too risky.

If the phone was tapped, Evelyn's identity could be exposed.

She was still using her sister's identity to rescue their mother.

Alexander would never reveal her outside.

Suddenly, he turned his back to her. "No particular pretext. Just a regular gala."

It was actually being held for his birthday!

But this woman didn't care about him at all.

She probably didn't even remember his birthday!

Alexander felt a pang of irritation.

He understood she was under immense pressure lately.

She carried heavy responsibilities.

But not remembering was simply not remembering...

He knew he shouldn't be upset over such a small thing.

Yet, he couldn't help it.

Seeing him turn away, Evelyn didn't think much of it.

Then, she fell silent.

Alexander: ??

He slowly turned around.

He found Evelyn was already asleep.

Her face was buried deep in the pillow, her long hair splayed out.

With her eyes closed, she looked exceptionally docile.

The red teardrop mole at the corner of her eye revealed an innocent yet alluring charm.

Alexander was utterly captivated.

He watched her quietly, finally sighing in resignation before closing his eyes to sleep.

That night, his sleep was light.

He even dreamed that Evelyn took him shopping.

She said she was buying clothes for Chloe Davis.

But once they reached the mall, she suddenly handed him a gift.

"Here. Happy Birthday."

In the dream, Alexander was deeply moved, smiling continuously.

Unfortunately, when the morning sun shone in, he woke up faintly.

He realized it was just a beautiful dream.

He lay quietly, staring at the ceiling.

He turned to look at the woman still deep in sleep.

Suddenly, he smiled with acceptance.

Evelyn had been so busy lately.

Her mind was completely occupied with completing the Nan family's tasks and rescuing her mother, Sophia Ashcroft.

How could she possibly remember his birthday?

He was asking for too much.

How could he be angry with her over such a trivial matter?

The man who had been sulking all night managed to console himself.

...

After breakfast.

Evelyn suddenly spoke. "The collaboration with the Evans family is complete."

"We're just waiting for feedback after signing."

"Instead of waiting idly at home, why don't we go shopping?"

Alexander's pupils lit up slightly.

He looked up sharply at Evelyn.

But her calm face revealed nothing.

Did she remember?

Or did she just want to go shopping?

Alexander coughed. "Shopping for what?"

Evelyn replied, "Not necessarily for anything specific. Just browsing."

So it was just casual browsing!

Alexander smiled. "Alright!"

After breakfast, they asked the nanny to look after Chloe Davis.

They told Mina Sherwood to rest well.

Then, they left.

Marcus Valentine was not at the door.

But when leaving the residential area, Evelyn vaguely saw Marcus's car again.

It was hidden in an inconspicuous corner.

The windows were tinted with anti-peep film.

It was impossible to see if anyone was inside.

Evelyn asked, "Has big brother's fever subsided?"

Marcus had stood in the rain all night and developed a fever that afternoon.

Ethan Valentine had come to see her about it.

Alexander said, "It broke last night."

He also glanced at Marcus's car.

He wondered if Mina would eventually forgive Marcus.

Lost in thought, they arrived at the mall.

It was New York's largest luxury mall, located within the Third Ring Road.

The underground parking lot was filled with luxury cars.

After parking, they went upstairs together.

Alexander kept observing Evelyn.

He tried to discern from her words and actions if she remembered his birthday was the day after tomorrow.

Unfortunately, she seemed bored while shopping.

There was no special reaction.

Alexander simply stopped guessing.

He would wait quietly for the final surprise.

Evelyn shopped without any purpose.

She entered any store that caught her interest.

After a full round, she only bought two hair clips for Chloe Davis.

She didn't find anything she wanted for herself.

They wandered into a Cartier boutique...

There should be suitable accessories for him here, like cufflinks...

Finding it boring, she was about to leave.

Alexander: ?

So Evelyn really had no intention of buying anything for him?

Her shopping trip today was genuinely just for shopping!

He looked unwilling.

His hands were empty.

Justin immediately nodded.

The manager immediately said, "Young women definitely love jewelry."

Justin nodded. "Right, she'll definitely like them."

Meeting Alexander's solemn face, Justin recalled the pain of being dominated by him.

Evelyn raised an eyebrow.

Alexander didn't speak, just looked at him.

Under his gaze, Justin coughed.

"Miss Ross, since you saved my sister, I'll give you some advice."

"Then do you know what his name is?"
###Chapter 485

The Vance family gala was approaching. Alexander's identity would soon be public knowledge.

The entire Zhao Corporation was prioritizing the Nora collaboration.

Inside Zhao Corporation's legal department.

Back in the boutique.

###Chapter 486

Nora's current status truly didn't require an arranged marriage.

But that boy toy was clearly after her money!

###Chapter 487

High society in New York values reputation above all else.

Even in arranged marriages where both parties lead separate lives, a facade of mutual respect must be maintained in public.

Everyone knew about Evelyn Ross and Alexander Vance's engagement.

Her blatant public appearances with a young boy toy were a direct slap to the Vance family's face.

Evelyn was dining with Alexander.

Marcus's condition was highly confidential.

Anyone who could obtain such information must have come from within the Ashcroft family.

This was a hint at internal strife within the Ashcroft family.

Power struggles among the wealthy were never uncommon.

The seventh was simple-minded. Ethan Valentine was determined to marry into another family. These two were the least likely.

The remaining four…

###Chapter 488

###Chapter 489

Evelyn Ross stood quietly behind him, dressed in soft silk pajamas.

She held an exquisite birthday cake in her hands, a smile gracing her lips.

A new day had begun.

Her voice was gentle, her eyes sparkling with tiny lights.

Today was just about picking it up.

She just wanted to cut him off.

…

The Vance family had been in New York for three months.

The head of the household's continued absence had started rumors.

If he didn't show up soon, rumors of Julian Evans staging a coup would spread.

Evelyn nodded.

"I'll return to the Ashcroft residence. I'll go with the Ashcrofts tonight."

"I'll drive you."

Evelyn…

After breakfast, Alexander drove her to the Ashcroft estate.

As she got out of the car, Evelyn waved goodbye.

Sunlight fell on her smiling face, making it especially bright.

Alexander's eyes darkened.

Evelyn's cheeks flushed under his gaze, and she quickly turned to enter the Ashcroft home.

In the Ashcroft living room, all seven brothers were gathered.

Marcus Valentine, recently recovered from his illness, sat alone in a corner, looking despondent.

The other six huddled together, discussing in low voices.

"Everyone's gossiping about our little sister. What should we do?"

"Wherever she goes, she brings that driver. All of New York knows she's keeping a boy toy."

"If Alexander Vance finds out, he'll be furious."

"I heard Mrs. Eleanor Vance is very displeased. These rumors are coming from the Vances."

"We need to advise our little sister to break it off with that driver quickly."

"Maybe our little sister doesn't want the arranged marriage? She didn't grow up with the Ashcrofts. She shouldn't have to sacrifice for the family."

Ethan Valentine jumped up.

"If our little sister doesn't like Alexander Vance, she should call off the engagement tonight!"

The youngest, Charles Valentine, interjected softly.

"Should we wait and see? What if our little sister and Alexander Vance hit it off tonight?"

As they debated, Evelyn walked in.

Ethan Valentine cleared his throat.

"Little sister, First Senior Sister, your boy toy is gone?"

"He went to prepare."

"Prepare for what?"

"Tonight's banquet."

"What?!"

Ethan Valentine was stunned.

"He's going too?"

This was practically a slap in the face to the Vance family!
###Chapter 490

Evelyn saw Ethan Valentine nearly jump out of his skin.

She raised an eyebrow. "I can't go?"

Ethan nodded immediately.

But Marcus Valentine suddenly approached.

He looked at Evelyn. "Grace, a word?"

Evelyn followed him to the side.

Marcus asked, "How is Mina?"

"She's fine."

Marcus's expression faltered.

The child really was his.

He remembered Mina desperately fighting to keep the pregnancy.

She loved this child.

She loved him too.

But she was doing fine now.

Did that mean she didn't love him anymore?

Marcus lowered his gaze.

He smiled bitterly.

He deserved this.

He brought it upon himself.

He patted Evelyn's shoulder. "Is she attending the banquet tonight?"

"No."

Mina was on bed rest.

Her large belly made it inconvenient.

She had cut ties with the Sherwood family.

She was divorced from Marcus.

Her presence would be awkward.

Marcus nodded. "Then I won't go either."

"Wish Alexander a happy birthday for me."

He turned and left.

He was heading toward the Nan family home.

Evelyn didn't stop him.

Marcus and Mina's issues were their own to resolve.

She had been angry he didn't claim the child.

Now she knew the Nan family had schemed against him.

She couldn't hold a grudge anymore.

She even felt guilty.

The Nan family targeted him.

Probably because of her.

Evelyn was lost in thought when Mr.Valentine, along with the eldest and second uncle couples, emerged.

Mr.Valentine looked at her. "Ready? Let's go."

Evelyn nodded.

A procession of six cars set off.

Evelyn rode with Mr.Valentine.

She glanced at the driver.

She wanted to ask if he was trustworthy.

Mr.Valentine spoke first. "The drivers are all trusted aides."

"Our lives are in their hands."

Evelyn understood.

She said directly, "There's a mole in the Valentine family."

She shared her suspicions.

Finally, she asked, "Who do you think it is?"

She had ruled out Ethan Valentine and Charles Valentine.

She knew them well from their interactions.

Mr.Valentine glanced at her. "The other four wouldn't do it either."

Evelyn paused. "That much trust?"

"Yes."

Mr.Valentine said, "The Valentine family doesn't have internal strife."

"There never has been, and there never will be."

"The seven brothers grew up together."

"They trust each other with their lives."

"You don't need to worry about it."

"Plenty of people had access to that medical report."

Evelyn nodded.

She trusted him.

They fell silent for a moment.

Mr.Valentine raised an eyebrow. "Who?"

Evelyn said a name.

Mr.Valentine sneered. "I'll look into it immediately."

…

The motorcade arrived at the Vance estate.

The Vance Manor was located on the Fifth Avenue.

It was imposing and grand.

It rivaled the old Vance mansion in San Francisco.

When the Valentine cars arrived, the Vance estate was already full of guests.

Alexander Vance wasn't present.

Julian Evans was greeting guests at the door.

He looked terrible.

After coming to New York, he had visited Isabella at the psychiatric hospital.

She had gone insane.

She rushed over, begging for mercy.

She knelt, admitting her mistakes.

She said she would never dare deceive him again.

She looked worse than death.

Julian felt no sympathy.

He thought of what she had done to Evelyn.

He thought about how she always knew she was a love child.

She had deceived him.

She made him lose Evelyn.

He wished he could kill her.

If he hadn't cared about social status back then.

If he hadn't given up on Evelyn.

Would he be happy now?

Julian looked desolate.

He had truly loved Evelyn.

He pursued her for four years in college.

She had been gone for four months.

He couldn't forget her.

His love was no less than his uncle's.

Thinking about his uncle's fiancée arriving, Julian felt displeased.

Evelyn had only been gone for four months.

His uncle had already moved on.

How could he betray her like this?

Julian clenched his fists.

He had no good feelings toward the Valentine heiress.

The gatekeeper announced the Valentine family's arrival.

Julian intentionally did not announce them properly.

A family like the Valentines required his parents, or his uncle, to greet them.

Perhaps even Elder Eleanor Vance herself.

That would be面子 enough.

He didn't want to give them that.

Penelope Valentine suddenly walked over.

She smiled. "You're Julian Evans?"

Penelope said, "I'm a adopted daughter of the Valentine family."

"You must have heard of me."

Julian nodded.

Penelope continued, "I'd like to talk to you."

"It's about Grace Nan having a boy toy on the side."

Julian was stunned. "Who did you say?!"

Evelyn?
###Chapter 491

Julian Evans was still processing the information when Penelope Valentine spoke.

"It's Nange. Third Uncle's biological daughter."

Penelope lowered her gaze.

"Correct. To be precise, she should be Grace Valentine."

Julian's expression grew even more dazed.

"Not the 'Ge' for song. It's the 'Ge' as in 'Gege'. She was originally named Nange. The Valentine surname was added after she was recognized. So it's Grace Valentine now."

Grace Valentine… Grace Valentine…

Julian let out a bitter laugh.

The name was truly ironic.

Was this the reason his uncle agreed to marry her?

"I don't know if you're aware, but Nange was married before. She encountered a scumbag. She's divorced now and has a daughter. After the divorce, she suffered some psychological trauma. So she started dating a young boy toy. My third uncle has been trying to get her to break up with him. Don't worry, once Nange marries into the Vance family, she will definitely break up…"

Penelope was carefully setting a trap for Grace.

Unfortunately, she was targeting the wrong person.

Julian spoke coldly.

"Good."

"What?"

Penelope: ??

She frowned.

Shouldn't Julian be furious about Grace's lack of virtue?

The Vance family members were all inside. No one came out to greet the Valentines.

This was enough to show their dissatisfaction with this marriage!

She gently swirled the red wine in her hand.

Grace Valentine… So what if you stole my position as the Valentine heiress?

You will still be the one humiliated at today's banquet!

…

"What is going on?! Is this how the Vances treat their guests?!"

The Valentine family gathered in the parking lot.

They deliberately slowed their pace before heading towards the Vance banquet hall.

They thought even if Alexander Vance wasn't present, the eldest branch or Mrs. Eleanor Vance would personally come out to greet them.

But surprisingly, there was no one at the entrance?

Ethan Valentine couldn't hold back and immediately complained.

Charles Valentine, being more naive, said directly.

"Maybe there are many guests today, and they haven't arrived yet? Let's walk slower. Give them some time."

Ethan immediately agreed.

"Right. The Vances have just established themselves in New York. They lack experience. It's normal for mistakes to happen at such banquets. Let's walk slower and wait a bit longer."

The group intentionally walked slowly, pointing at the surrounding scenery.

They seemed to be commenting on the Vance family's garden.

"Are they out yet?"

"Have the Vances come?"

"My smile is frozen! What should I do?"

"Do you think the Vances know about little sister's boy toy boyfriend? After all, the matter has been spreading like wildfire…"

The words silenced everyone.

Grace Valentine looked at the speaker.

It was actually her fifth brother, Preston Valentine.

Grace hadn't had much contact with Preston.

But from their limited interactions, she knew this fifth brother was a man of few words, the quiet type.

He was also a bit stubborn.

Saying this now meant he thought Grace was in the wrong.

Preston: "…"

Only a Vance family member could park there.

###Chapter 492

Alexander Vance's father, Robert Vance?

He savored the lingering sensation on his fingertips.

It had been years since he'd touched such smooth skin.

Her voice trembled with fake tears. "I've moved out now."

Penelope's eyes reddened. "My uncle found his biological daughter. Naturally, I should move out... But it's fine. I'm doing well."
