Since arriving in New York, Alexander was never around.

Rumor had it he was chasing after his fiancée.

All that deep affection he showed in San Francisco was clearly an act!

It was all just to climb the social ladder by attaching himself to the Valentines and secure his position.

###Chapter 493

But someone else got there first... After my sister came home, she probably couldn't stand me..."

The hall was filled with guests.

Eleanor Vance was resting upstairs due to her illness and did not attend.

Richard Evans was entertaining guests with Julian.

Alexander relied on the Valentines' support.

###Chapter 496

She nodded firmly. "Grandmother, my name is Grace Valentine."

"Grace, as in elegance."

Tears welled in Eleanor's eyes. "A beautiful name. It sounds just right for my grandson's wife!"

She clutched Evelyn's hand tightly. "My dear child, why haven't you visited me since the engagement?"

Evelyn's voice was slightly hoarse. "I've been too busy lately. I'm sorry."

"Don't apologize. Just visit more often."

Eleanor called out with a trembling voice. "Fiona, quickly bring the family heirloom jade bracelet!"

She led Evelyn toward the seating area. "Keep your grandmother company for a while."

Evelyn carefully supported the old woman, feeling her slightly shaking hand.

A pang of guilt struck her heart.

The Vance family had been in New York for so long, yet she hadn't visited even once.

She had been terribly unfilial.

Ethan stared in disbelief. "Madam, you're... satisfied with my sister?"

Eleanor smiled through her tears. "Satisfied! This girl is exactly what I hoped for. She is meant to be Alexander's wife!"

Penelope Valentine couldn't resist interrupting. "But she has a boyfriend outside!"

The Ashcroft brothers glared at her.

Eleanor understood instantly.

So the driver her grandson played every day was the rumored "boy toy"?

She shot Penelope a sharp look. "So what? As long as my granddaughter-in-law agrees to marry him, I'd be happy if she kept ten or eight!"

The room erupted in murmurs.

Ethan was dumbfounded. "Did little sister give Madam some kind of love potion?"

Charles Valentine muttered, "She must have mistaken her for someone else."

Penelope sneered. "I heard Madam Vance has Alzheimer's. She must be confused!"

The guests suddenly understood.

"So that's it. At least it resolved the awkward situation."

"The banquet can start now. But why hasn't Mr. Vance arrived yet?"

"This fiancé is way too mysterious..."

Penelope stared intently at the corner sofa.

Eleanor was wiping her tears while Evelyn gently dabbed them for her.

They looked as close as real grandmother and granddaughter.

Penelope clenched her fists.

Out of the corner of her eye, she noticed a figure entering through the back door.

Dressed in a tailored suit, strikingly handsome—

It was that boy toy driver!

Penelope immediately rushed over to block him. "How did you get in here?"

She deliberately raised her voice. "Is this the Vance family banquet? Did Evelyn bring you here?"

Nearby guests turned to look.

Penelope announced loudly. "This is the boy toy Evelyn keeps!"

"She actually brought him to this event. It's a total slap to the Vance family!"

"Get out of here now!"
###Chapter 497

Evelyn Ross was speaking with Eleanor Vance.

The elderly woman clutched her hand tightly.
Her eyes were red-rimmed, and her voice trembled.
"My dear granddaughter-in-law, it's so wonderful to see you again."

Evelyn's heart ached.
She responded softly, "Grandmother, it's my fault. I should have visited you sooner."

"You must have had your reasons," Eleanor said, dabbing her eyes.
"I heard you have a new identity now, and a child? What happened exactly?"

She paused, then quickly added, "If it's inconvenient to talk about, you don't have to say anything."

Evelyn felt a warm glow.
She explained simply, "It was my sister, Grace Nan, who fell into the sea that day. I woke up as her."
"As for why I had to live using my sister's identity... that's a long story."

"Then don't say it!" Eleanor said, smiling through her tears.
"It's enough that you're back. What about your status as the Ashcroft family's eldest daughter..."

"It's true. The third Ashcroft brother is my father."

Eleanor was taken aback.
"And your mother-in-law?"

Evelyn's expression dimmed.
"My mother is missing."

"How could she disappear?" Eleanor frowned.

"She was taken by the Nan family," Evelyn said firmly.
"I'm searching for the Nan family's whereabouts. I must rescue my mother."

"The Nan family?" Eleanor seemed thoughtful.
"Your mother is Sophia Ashcroft... so she was from the Nan family!"

Evelyn eagerly grasped her hand.
"You know about the Nan family?"

Eleanor nodded.
"It's a very ancient family. It's normal that your generation wouldn't know about them."

Evelyn's eyes lit up.
"Grandmother, can you tell me everything you know?"

"Of course," Eleanor said, glancing around.
"But there are too many eyes and ears here. Let's talk after the banquet ends."

"Alright."

Evelyn asked with concern, "How has your health been lately?"

"Thanks to your medicine, I've remembered many things," Eleanor sighed.
"Unfortunately, I'm old. Even with a clear mind, I don't have many years left."
"I'll leave that troublesome boy to you."

"Don't say that, Grandmother. You will live a long life. You still need to help us raise our children!"

As Evelyn spoke, she noticed a commotion not far away.

She paused, then stood up.
"Grandmother, something seems to be happening with the Ashcrofts. I'll go take a look."
"Fiona, please look after Grandmother."

Fiona Vance quickly nodded.
"Don't worry, Madam. Go ahead."

Fiona's eyes were also red.

Evelyn patted her hand and walked toward the source of the argument.

On the way, she was stopped by Ethan Valentine.
"Little sister, First Senior Sister, come quickly and meet Preston's girlfriend!"

Ethan and Preston were similar in age and usually called each other by their first names.

Evelyn glanced into the distance, then first approached the Ashcroft group.

Preston was happily introducing his girlfriend to his family, looking very pleased.

Ethan curled his lip.
"Who knows where he found this woman? He treats her like a treasure."
"Earlier, she even hinted that you should watch your image and not offend the Vances too much, for the Ashcroft family's sake."
"She's already giving orders before even entering the family. She'll definitely be a troublemaker in the future!"

Evelyn couldn't help but laugh.

The Ashcroft brothers were all good people.
Though Preston was a bit old-fashioned, he treated her well.

She remembered the Family Reunion Gala, where Preston had given her an expensive jade bracelet.

He always advised her to break up with her "boy toy" to avoid upsetting Alexander Vance, but it was out of good intentions.

As the two approached, Preston enthusiastically introduced them.
"Susan, this is my little sister, Grace Valentine!"

He then said to Evelyn, "Little sister, this is my girlfriend, Susan Langley."

The girl turned around.

The moment Evelyn saw her face, she stopped in her tracks.

What a coincidence.

Wasn't this the woman having an affair with another man in the shopping mall parking lot yesterday?

Evelyn's expression became complicated.

She recalled the conversation between this woman and the young man in the parking lot.

Something about "using my boyfriend's money to support you"...

So that unlucky boyfriend was Preston?

Evelyn gave a wry smile.

When Susan Langley enthusiastically extended her hand, Evelyn looked down at it and made no move.

Susan was taken aback and looked at Preston with hurt feelings.
"Preston, does your sister not like me?"

Preston looked at Evelyn.

Evelyn cleared her throat lightly.
"I have mysophobia."

Mainly, the thought of these hands groping in a man's pants yesterday made her feel sick.

If it were a normal relationship, it would be one thing, but this was clearly an affair.

Susan's face instantly turned ugly.

She was about to say something when Evelyn spoke with a faint smile.
"Miss Langley, did you go to S Mall yesterday?"

Susan's face changed.

Preston, completely unaware, said, "Yes, she went to buy a bag yesterday. How did you know, little sister?"

Evelyn looked at him meaningfully.
"I went to buy a gift for Alexander Vance yesterday and ran into someone who looked very much like Miss Langley."

Susan forced a smile.
"Miss Valentine must have mistaken me for someone else."

"Really?" Preston became interested.
"Maybe you really did see each other. Little sister, what was she wearing yesterday?"

Evelyn said with a smile, "A yellow dress, right?"

"Yes, yes!" Preston's eyes lit up.
"I drove her there, but I had work and left first. Where did you see Susan?"

Evelyn's lips curved slightly.
"The parking lot."
###Chapter 498

"Parking lot?"

Preston Valentine was momentarily stunned.

Susan Langley immediately spoke up. "She must be mistaken! I didn't have a car yesterday. How could I have gone to a parking lot?"

Evelyn Ross's tone was calm. "Then perhaps I was mistaken. After all, that girl was getting intimate with another man in the parking lot."

Preston's face darkened instantly as he looked at Susan.

Susan frantically waved her hands. "Preston, it absolutely wasn't me! I never went there!"

"Really?" Evelyn chuckled lightly. "I even overheard someone saying they were going to give a watch to their boy toy. By the way, did you buy a watch?"

Susan's face instantly turned pale.

She had used Preston's card yesterday. The transaction record was clear as day…

Preston's expression indeed grew even more unpleasant.

Susan shot Evelyn a resentful glare before sighing. "Preston, I did buy a men's watch. But it was for you! Have you forgotten what tomorrow is?"

Preston was taken aback. "Our one-year anniversary?"

"Yes! So I specially prepared a gift for you…"

Susan glanced at Evelyn. "It's a pity that with little sister's misunderstanding, I have to reveal it now… The surprise is ruined!"

Preston immediately turned to Evelyn. "Little sister, you must be mistaken! Susan is a good girl. She has strict upbringing. She wouldn't fool around."

Susan lowered her head. "Miss Ross, speaking of which, I saw you at the mall yesterday too. There was a boy in athletic wear with you? Were you really buying a gift for Mr. Vance? I saw you buying something for that boy."

The expressions of the Ashcroft family members changed instantly. They all began coughing uncomfortably.

Susan continued. "Miss Ross, Preston told me you've been keeping a young boyfriend even after your engagement to Mr. Vance. My family has very strict rules. My parents are both university professors. I even have a curfew. I must be home by ten… Why did you just slander me like that?"

She looked at Preston with委屈. "Miss Ross has the Ashcroft family backing her. That's why she doesn't know how to cherish a fiancé like Mr. Vance. I would never do that… Preston, you are the best man I've ever met. How could I possibly be interested in anyone else?"

Preston, flattered by her praise and trusting her after a year together, immediately chose to believe her. "Don't be upset, Susan. Little sister must be mistaken. I don't doubt you! Tomorrow is our one-year anniversary. What do you want? I'll get you anything."

Susan lowered her eyes. "I don't want anything. I'm not a materialistic person. I just want you to spend time with me…"

Preston nodded eagerly. "Okay, okay, no problem!"

Evelyn scoffed. "We could always check the surveillance footage…"

Before she could finish, Susan's eyes welled up with tears. "Preston, does your little sister really dislike me that much?"

Preston's face turned serious. "Little sister, that's enough. I believe Susan! Susan is nothing like you! And you…"

He took a deep breath. "I've told you before, one should be faithful. You shouldn't fool around outside. How can you do this to Mr. Vance?"

Evelyn frowned.

Ethan Valentine beside her couldn't hold back. "Preston, that's enough! Little sister, our senior sister, had a hard life growing up. The marriage alliance with the Vance family was always a family matter. The Ashcroft family never raised her. What right do they have to make her marry for the family's benefit? So what if she has someone she likes? At worst, call off the engagement!"

Preston immediately retorted. "Then call it off! But she isn't calling it off, yet she's still fooling around outside. When we came in just now, people were pointing and whispering. How many are doubting the Ashcroft family's integrity now? Didn't you hear them?"

Ethan was furious but didn't know how to rebut.

Evelyn was his sister. Of course he would be biased.

Even if she did something wrong, as her brother, he would help her make amends. Why lecture her like this?

Ethan looked at Evelyn. "Little sister, this matter needs to be resolved. Otherwise, it's bad enough that outsiders talk about you, but now even our own family thinks you're in the wrong! That boy toy outside and Alexander Vance, which one do you choose? Once you decide, be devoted to that one!"

Evelyn was speechless.

She found it both funny and exasperating!

Just as she was about to speak, Susan chimed in again. "Little sister, you're not trying to enjoy the warmth of another man outside while still clinging to the prestigious status of Mrs. Vance, are you? One shouldn't be so greedy!"

Evelyn rolled her eyes.

The commotion in the distance grew louder. Charles Valentine ran over. "It's bad! Cousin, your boyfriend is at the party! He's surrounded by people being criticized! Go take a look and tell him to leave quickly. This is going too far!"

Evelyn was baffled.

The person being surrounded and argued with in the distance was Alexander Vance?

She suddenly found it hilarious.

Alexander Vance was about to be kicked out of his own home?

Susan immediately said. "Little sister, this is too much! Today is Mr. Vance's birthday. How could you bring your side piece here? It's so inappropriate!"

Preston also frowned. "Yes, little sister, tell him to leave quickly. Don't let this become too ugly! The Ashcroft and Vance families still need to save face!"

Susan added. "Preston, I understand now why little sister framed me! She was afraid I would tell everyone about her shopping with that boy toy yesterday, so she tried to set me up first! Little sister, how could you do this? I'm Preston's girlfriend. I wouldn't have said anything about this originally."

She turned to Preston. "But Preston, the Ashcroft family's upbringing really needs to be stricter. My parents taught me not to fool around outside… Now she's even brought her boy toy here. The Vance family will definitely be angry this time!"

Preston frowned deeply. "Little sister, what exactly is going on? Did you invite him? If you did, make him leave immediately! If you didn't, I'll make him leave! In any case, he shouldn't be here!"
###Chapter 499

Susan Langley nestled into Preston Valentine's arms.

She looked at Evelyn Ross with a challenging gaze.

"Little sister, it's your choice."

Evelyn's lips curved into a faint smile.

She didn't speak, simply turned around.

"I'll go see."

Ethan Valentine immediately followed.

"I'll go with you."

"If anyone dares to touch you, they'll regret it."

Charles Valentine quickly caught up.

"And me."

The other Valentine brothers followed suit.

Preston was about to step forward.

But Susan held onto his arm.

"Preston, I'm scared."

"Will there be a fight later?"

"Is this really appropriate for your sister?"

"She was in the wrong to begin with."

Preston frowned.

"I need to go watch. I can't let her get hurt."

Susan spoke softly, persuading him.

"The other brothers are all going."

"She won't get hurt."

"This is the Vance estate. No one would dare start anything."

"I think we should inform the elders."

Preston paused.

"Inform the elders?"

Things would be hard to control if they escalated.

Susan's eyes darted.

"At least tell the Valentine elders."

"They still don't know about this."

Preston looked into the distance.

The Vances hadn't come out to greet the Valentines.

But the Valentines were still the top of New York's five great families.

People only dared to gossip behind their backs.

When they saw the Third Mr. Valentine, they still rushed to curry favor.

The Third Mr. Valentine was currently surrounded by a crowd.

As Preston hesitated, Susan pulled him forward.

"Preston, hurry and tell your uncle."

They approached the Third Mr. Valentine.

He was in the middle of a conversation.

Richard Evans was restless.

The Vances had been so disrespectful, yet the Valentines still entered.

The matriarch even personally took the Valentine daughter aside to chat.

Was this marriage alliance really that solid?

No, he had to sabotage the union.

If Alexander Vance married into the Valentines, his position would be secure.

Richard helped Robert Vance to his feet.

"Father, let's go greet the Third Mr. Valentine."

Robert nodded.

They walked toward the Third Mr. Valentine.

The Third Mr. Valentine was proud, but he observed proper etiquette.

He ignored Richard.

But Robert was Alexander's father. He had to give face.

He gave Robert a slight nod.

Robert cleared his throat.

He had intended to find fault and mock.

But facing the Third Mr. Valentine, the words stuck in his throat.

The Third Mr. Valentine's presence was too imposing.

The Third Mr. Valentine asked.

"Is there something, in-law?"

Robert quickly replied.

"Nothing. Alexander is delayed. He'll arrive later."

The Third Mr. Valentine nodded.

"Understood."

Robert added.

"Something came up earlier. We couldn't come out to greet you. Please don't take offense."

"Of course not. We'll be family soon. No need for formalities."
The Third Mr. Valentine smiled and patted Richard's shoulder.

Two pats that nearly shattered Richard's shoulder bone.

He endured the pain and forced himself to ask.

"Sir, I heard you found your biological daughter. And that she was previously married, with a child?"

The Third Mr. Valentine smiled.

"Yes. The child is called Chloe. The Vances will treat her as their own, won't they?"

Richard had meant to sneer, but was instead put on the spot.

He didn't know how to respond.

Preston and Susan approached.

Preston didn't recognize Robert and Richard.

He cautiously paused.

"Uncle, there's something I need to tell you. Could you step aside for a moment?"

Susan glanced at their attire, then looked toward the main seat.

She had an idea but pretended ignorance.

"Uncle, Evelyn's boy toy is at the banquet. He's making a scene after being stopped at the entrance. You should go see quickly! It'll be terrible if the Vances find out!"

The Third Mr. Valentine remained silent.

Preston was stunned and hurriedly pulled Susan.

"Susan, shut up!"

Susan looked at him innocently.

"Preston, what's wrong? Didn't we come to tell Uncle? Uncle should handle this quickly. This is the Vance banquet. It would be bad if they discovered."

The Third Mr. Valentine glanced briefly at Susan, then looked at Preston.

He turned to Robert with a faint smile.

"Do you know who these two are?"

Preston's heart sank.

The Third Mr. Valentine said.

"This is Elder Vance. Next to him is Mr. Richard Evans, Alexander's older brother."

Preston froze.

He sharply turned to look at Susan.

Susan panicked.

"Preston, I... I didn't know..."

Preston took a deep breath and forced a smile at Richard and Robert.

Before he could speak, Robert angrily said.

"Third Mr. Valentine, your Valentine daughter has gone too far!"

He stamped his foot and looked at Richard.

Richard immediately chimed in.

"Yes, brazenly bringing a boy toy to the banquet. This is a slap to Alexander's face! Father, this marriage should be called off! It's too humiliating!"

Robert shouted.

"Call it off! It must be called off!"

The Third Mr. Valentine remained calm and composed.

He lightly placed his wine glass on the table.

The "tap" sound made both men immediately fall silent.

Everyone knew the Third Mr. Valentine was bloodthirsty and killed without blinking.

The Third Mr. Valentine smiled faintly.

"Mr. Vance, Nephew Evans, shall we go take a look? See what this boy toy of my daughter actually looks like!"
###Chapter 500

Robert Vance and Richard Evans froze simultaneously.

What did Mr. Valentine mean by that?

Was he deliberately provoking them?

Their faces turned ashen, but they didn't dare disobey.

They could only force themselves to agree. "Alright, let's go take a look."

The three men turned and walked toward the noisy banquet hall.

Preston Valentine and Susan Langley stood frozen in place.

Susan swallowed hard. "Preston, what is your uncle trying to do?"

"Does he think showing them how handsome that boy toy is will make them forgive your sister for cheating?"

Preston was equally confused.

He had seen that boy toy before. The man was exceptionally handsome, more dazzling than a top celebrity.

But what did his uncle want them to see?

Was he preparing to call off the engagement?

He must be...

Preston immediately grabbed Susan's hand and followed them.

Evelyn Ross had already reached the center of the crowd.

She saw Alexander Vance standing at the entrance, his expression dark.

Penelope Valentine was pointing at him and shouting angrily. "You boy toy, get out of here now!"

"Don't you dare ruin Mr. Vance's birthday gala!"

Guests around them whispered among themselves.

"The Valentines are going too far. How could they bring a boy toy to Mr. Vance's birthday gala?"

"They're completely disregarding the Vances!"

"Will the Vances just take this? This is a huge humiliation!"

"The Valentines are powerful. The Vances probably don't dare to offend them easily."

"But they're the richest family in San Francisco. They can't just let the Valentines humiliate them like this..."

"This Ms. Valentine has no class. Keeping a boy toy in private is one thing, but bringing him out in public?"

"I heard Mr. Vance loved his first wife deeply. Ms. Valentine was also hurt by his first wife."

"Their marriage is just a business alliance. But she still shouldn't be so brazen!"

"She has no shame!"

Amid the accusations, Evelyn walked forward and raised an eyebrow. "Why aren't you coming in?"

With Alexander Vance's status, these people couldn't possibly stop him.

Alexander glanced coldly at Penelope. "I was waiting for you to come get me."

Evelyn almost laughed.

Penelope immediately shrieked. "Grace, have you no shame? You've disgraced the entire Valentine family!"

"If you dare let him in, the Vances will not let this go!"

Evelyn extended her hand to Alexander. "Then step through this door. Let's see what the Vances dare to do."

Alexander took her hand, the corner of his mouth lifting. "I wouldn't dare."

Evelyn raised an eyebrow. "That's more like it."

Penelope trembled with rage. "What are you two doing?"

Ethan Valentine rushed over. "Little sister, this really isn't the right occasion."

"Let me take your friend out for a walk first? I promise I'll treat him well!"

Evelyn shook her head. "No need."

Ethan was almost frantic with worry. "Little sister, you don't understand."

"I heard that Alexander Vance is extremely formidable. Even Uncle is cautious around him."

"He learned martial arts from Uncle. What if he shows up later and beats up your friend..."

Evelyn didn't know whether to laugh or cry.

Alexander struggled to suppress a smile.

Penelope said angrily, "You're still laughing? You think this is funny?"

Charles Valentine suddenly had an epiphany. "I get it now!"

Ethan asked urgently, "Get what?"

"Little sister is here to call off the engagement, right?"

Ethan froze.

Penelope's eyes lit up. "Is that it? Then you should explain it properly to Mr. Vance. Maybe he'll understand."

"But the alliance between the Valentine and Vance families can't be broken..."

This way, she would have a chance!

Penelope was still scheming when a loud voice interrupted. "Who wants to call off the engagement?"

The crowd automatically parted.

Mr. Valentine walked over with the Vance family's patriarch and Richard Evans.

Richard spoke as he walked. "Mr. Valentine, if you're here to call off the engagement, we can discuss it."

"Since both children have someone else in their hearts, there's no need to force them together..."

Robert Vance nodded repeatedly. "Yes, there's no need."

Mr. Valentine scoffed. "The Valentines are not calling off the engagement."

Their expressions immediately darkened.

Robert said angrily, "Mr. Valentine, if you're not calling off the engagement, why did you let your daughter bring a boy toy to provoke us?"

"This is going too far!"

Richard immediately chimed in. "That's right! Bullying North like this? The Vances will not stand for it!"

"You must give us an explanation today! No matter how powerful the Valentines are, you still have to be reasonable, right?"

"Everyone, don't you agree?"

The three men made their way to the front of the crowd.

Penelope spoke first. "Uncle, since little sister and this boy toy are in love, why not cancel her engagement with Mr. Vance?"

Mr. Valentine looked at her deeply. "So you can represent the Valentines and marry into the family?"

Penelope quickly nodded. "I can do it. The Valentines have raised me for so many years. It's only right that I marry for the family's alliance..."

Mr. Valentine sneered. "You? Are you worthy?"

Penelope's face instantly turned deathly pale.

Being kicked out of the Valentine family hadn't been this humiliating.

Mr. Valentine's single sentence made her want to crawl into a hole.

She pointed angrily at Evelyn. "Then is she worthy? Old Mr. Vance, Mr. Richard Evans, look at her!"

"Bringing a boy toy to provoke you like this. Can the Vances really tolerate this?"

Richard followed her pointing finger.

He saw Alexander and Evelyn standing together like a perfect couple.

He was completely stunned.

Robert was also dumbfounded. "Boy toy?"

"Yes, it's him!"

As soon as Penelope finished speaking, the "boy toy" she referred to stepped forward.

Alexander's face was dark. "This farce has gone on long enough!"

"Someone, throw this troublemaking woman out!"

Penelope was incredulous. "What did you say? How dare you throw me out?"

"Who do you think you are? How dare you cause trouble at the Vances!"

Before she could finish, Thomas Watson immediately stepped up to Alexander and bowed. "Yes, Mr. Vance!"

Penelope was completely bewildered.

She stared at Alexander in disbelief. "You... you are..."

Alexander's gaze swept over Ethan Valentine and Charles Valentine.

Finally, it landed on Mr. Valentine. "Uncle, thank you for taking the time to attend my birthday gala."
